sid_journal_description_E03_MQ06_Descrip: Trusting Varan was a mistake. He was gonna screw me over all along, and his captive will never talk again. Nevertheless, I did uncover something useful after all: the captive had an unusual PDA that the bandits overlooked. I need to show it to Diode. As a tech, perhaps he can tell me who in the Zone might be using these things.
sid_journal_description_E03_SQ02_Descrip: Star’s men were looking around Nestor’s hideout when the Ward arrived, cutting off all escape routes. The stalkers found themselves trapped, along with the kingpin’s archive. To locate Nestor, I need to get to the Factory’s underground section – and I need to do it as quietly as possible.

Star’s men were looking around Nestor’s hideout when the Ward arrived, cutting off all escape routes. The stalkers found themselves trapped, along with the kingpin’s archive. To locate Nestor, I need to get to the Factory’s underground section – and I need to do it as quietly as possible.
sid_journal_description_E04_MQ01_Descrip: After a close encounter with Dr. Dalin and Scar, I managed to find out that the unusual PDA recovered from Varan’s prisoner is a device used years ago by a Monolith faction. The trail leads to Wild Island, the headquarters of Noontide – a group made up of former Monolithians. My attackers apparently have some ties to Noontide. I need to find a way there and have a serious talk with their leader.
sid_journal_description_E04_MQ02_Descrip: Strider is confident that Noontide played no part in the attacks – they’ve renounced their Monolithian past and embraced a new way. I’m inclined to believe him, especially since Strider has offered to help me intercept communications from the Monolith PDA to identify the perpetrators. I’ll have to activate Azimuth Station, which is currently occupied by the Ward, and replace the Inductor there. The Wardens should have the access keys for the station.
sid_journal_description_E04_SQ01_Descrip: Getting to Wild Island turned out to be far more difficult than I anticipated as the Noontiders are extremely suspicious of outsiders. The only way to get to the Island entails retrieving and delivering an important shipment, since the Noontiders assigned to transport it have gone missing.
sid_journal_description_E04_SQ02_Descrip: A Noontider called Kvashka inexplicably activated the antenna on the base’s rooftop and succumbed to psi influence. Prof. Lodochka wants me to shut it down and put the zombified Noontider out of his misery – he’s past saving now.
sid_journal_description_E05_MQ01_Descrip: Now I’m certain that Faust is responsible for orchestrating the attack against me. With the help of Strider, the leader of Noontide, I found out that the preacher has an ally within the Ward’s Camp Icarus. If I can interrogate this man, maybe I can discover Faust’s whereabouts and get my Scanner back.
sid_journal_description_E05_MQ02_Descrip: Shterev no longer needs Faust’s guidance, but I certainly do. I need to locate the ship he mentioned and find a guide who can show me the way to the Clear Sky Base. I don’t know what the blind zealot is up to, but I’m going to confront him and put an end to his schemes.
sid_journal_description_E05_MQ03_Descrip: Now that I’ve got Prof. Kalancha’s map, I’m ready to confront Faust and reclaim my Scanner. However, I first need to deactivate the psi-beacons in the Swamps to open the path to Clear Sky. It won’t be a walk in the park – some specialized equipment could come in handy.
sid_journal_description_E05_MQ04_Descrip: I’ve retrieved the Scanner, but Faust managed to get away again. I still don’t know what he’s after. Is it revenge? I should probably talk to Strider about this.
sid_journal_description_E05_MQ05_Descrip: Camp Icarus is a restricted facility, which means that Faust’s associate is affiliated with the Ward. I’m sure the men in charge of Icarus will help me root out the traitor in their midst.
sid_journal_description_E05_SQ01_Descrip: Batalov, the medic from Icarus, asked to find his runaway patients. Looks like their brains got seriously messed up, and the doctor wants to save them from experimental treatment. I need to track them down and wound them just enough for the doctor to invalid them out and send them to the Mainland.
sid_journal_description_E05_SQ03_Descrip: People are saying that some rare parts have been sighted in Zaton. Securing them would give me an opportunity to upgrade standard suits.
sid_journal_description_E05_SQ04_Descrip: Captain Senkevych believes that Khoteyev sells Ward equipment on the side. We should check this out. Senkevych wants me to take part in the transaction and find out who’s at the end buying the goods.
sid_journal_description_E06_MQ01_C01_Des: There’s been some kind of emergency at the Cryptobiology and Psi-Medicine Lab. I’ve got to figure out the cause and deal with it. If I don’t get this sorted out quickly, Dr. Dalin’s experiment and the testing of the Alpha artifact could be in jeopardy. I should check with Dr. Kryvenko, the scientist in charge of the lab, to get the lowdown on the situation.
sid_journal_description_E06_MQ01_C02_Des: Strider thinks that the Monolith has been transferred to SIRCAA – the same Monolith that stalkers used to call the Wish Granter. He’s concerned that those eggheads might try to activate it. I need to find out if he’s right about this.
sid_journal_description_E06_MQ01_Descrip: Now that I’ve got the Scanner and the artifact back, I’m relying on Dalin to come through. Hopefully that weird rock is worth something to him. More importantly, I’m about to find out the truth about my home and who’s behind it all. I’m sure I’ll find the answers at SIRCAA.
sid_journal_description_E06_MQ02_Descrip: Turns out Hermann was the one behind the mayhem at the Cryptobiology and Psi-Medicine Lab – the very same Hermann who led me into the Zone and gave me the Scanner. He’s alive, and this device is his invention. As for Dalin… it was his unbridled ambition that left me without a home. Well, now I know who’s gonna pay the price.
sid_journal_description_E06_MQ03_Descrip: Dalin’s experiment has gone horribly wrong. Instead of linking with the Generators and gaining control over the Zone, he’s unleashed a slew of new issues – including the re-emergence of Monolith. Hopefully Korshunov and Dalin can figure out how to clean this mess up, but that’s a problem for another day. My immediate concern is escaping from the ruins of SIRCAA.
sid_journal_description_E06_MQ04_Descrip: You were right all along, Strider! Dalin and his cronies are only good at one thing: destruction. Now their experiment has cost you your life, my friend. But maybe there’s still hope… First things first – I need to find a way out of this damned SIRCAA facility. Then I’ll figure out what to do next.
sid_journal_description_E07_MQ01_C02_Des: The scientists have detected a sudden spike in psi radiation at the Sphere. Colonel Korshunov wants to know what’s going on there. I need to get to the source and investigate it.
Prof. Lodochka has detected a sudden spike in psi radiation at the Sphere. I need to figure out what’s going on there.
sid_journal_description_E07_MQ01_Descrip: Monolith is back, awakened by some kind of signal. I’ve got to locate the source of this signal and find out what’s going on.
sid_journal_description_E07_MQ02_Descrip: The Signal seems to be originating from the Emitter. It’s hard to believe a device so small can bend people’s will so effectively. I need to find someone who knows what to do with it.
sid_journal_description_E07_MQ03_Descrip: The fact that I found the Emitter on Wild Island didn’t surprise Scar at all. I don’t like that he’s keeping me in the dark, but he’s got a plan to prevent the Ward from destroying the Zone, and the Emitter will help make it happen.
sid_journal_description_E07_MQ05_Descrip:
Dalin suspects outside interference in his experiment. He kept talking about routers and security protocols, but the bottom line is that this whole debacle has rebooted the Monolith’s programming. So there we have it.
Korshunov plans to attack the Duga captured by the Monolithians. I need to rendezvous with Senkevych’s group. It’s time to gear up for the assault.
sid_journal_description_E07_MQ06_Descrip: Scar wanted me to connect the Emitter to some installation at the Promin CMD Factory. It’s done. All that’s left to do now is to strike at the Ward.
sid_journal_description_E07_SQ01_Descrip:
The Gaffer is concerned about the ongoing threat to Zalissya – their former guide Uncle Lyonya turned out to be a Monolithian. He led the squad that attacked Zalissya.
Since all of the Gaffer’s seasoned veterans are tied up, I’ve stepped forward to help. It’s time to confront Uncle Lyonya.
Even though Uncle Lyonya is dead and we've fought off another Monolith attack, Zalissya is still in danger.
sid_journal_description_E08_MQ01_Descrip: That freak Faust is full of surprises! The assault failed. We’ll have to find a way to get through those phantoms of his. Prof. Ozersky from Malachite might know something. I’d better talk to him.
sid_journal_description_E08_MQ03_Descrip: A mysterious professor collecting mutant collars and running his own moonshine operation... not too long ago, I wouldn’t have given Richter the time of day, but now it seems like he’s our only lead. If what Ozersky said about the Controller Project turns out to be true, this could be the key to neutralizing Faust.
sid_journal_description_E08_MQ04_Descrip: The Ward is ready to get even with Faust, and they’re counting on me to make it happen. I hope this much-vaunted Suppressor will deliver as promised, otherwise this entire mission will go down in history as a meticulously planned suicide rather than a reckoning for the Duga. But we’ve got no choice – the future of more than just the Zone is at stake.
sid_journal_description_E08_MQ05_Descrip: I’ve finally got an opportunity to go mano a mano with Faust. Whatever the Spark’s agenda is, Scar knows one thing: if Faust isn’t stopped now, the repercussions could extend far beyond the Zone. Besides, who in their right mind would willingly become one of these zombies? It’s time to make a move!
sid_journal_description_E08_SQ01_Descrip: It looks like Scherba’s pressing on with his research. He’s after some working collars, and he wants me to procure them. I wonder what he’s cooking up…
sid_journal_description_E09_MQ01_Descrip: Faust has fallen, and the Wardens are thrilled. Sure, the victory came at a great cost, but they’ve avenged their comrades and the fiasco at the Duga while showing the Zone they’re still a force to be reckoned with. But I’ve got a nagging feeling that Faust’s legacy isn’t done with us yet, and it won’t be all sunshine and rainbows…

Faust is no more, and the Sparkers are free to pursue their own agenda. But what’s Scar’s next move? I’ve got a nagging feeling that Faust was something more than a mere nuisance, and his death will raise new questions – and cause plenty of trouble.
sid_journal_description_E09_MQ02_Descrip: To help my allies get to the Center of the Zone, I need Strelok. So far he’s the only man to not only reach the heart of the Zone, but also make it back in once piece. Rumor has it that the legendary stalker has recently resurfaced in the Zone.
sid_journal_description_E10_MQ01_Descrip: According to the Representative of C-Consciousness, eternal life exists in the Noosphere. Whether this is true or not, you can only find out for sure by reaching the Center of the Zone. So far, only Strelok has succeeded, so he must know the way. But to reach him, I first need to find another legend of the Zone – the Doctor. He was last seen in the Red Forest… I hope these are not just fairy tales.
sid_journal_description_E10_MQ02_Descrip: I found the Doctor. He’s preoccupied with saving Strider and won’t talk about Strelok until the Noontider is stable. Strider can be revived using a special device used by the Monolithians. I might be able to get one from the Monolith infirmary.
sid_journal_description_E10_SQ01_Descrip: Arkhyp Lichen believes there’s an unusual mutant in the forest. Not only has it scattered the local wildlife, but it also sent a group of Freedom members packing. A stalker called Gray tried to deal with the mutant, but disappeared without a trace. This job won’t be a walk in the park, but at least it gives me an opportunity to hunt some serious game.
sid_journal_description_E11_MQ01_Descrip: It turns out the Doctor is none other than Dr. Kaimanov, who was slated to enter the eighth pod alongside the other members of C-Consciousness, but backed out at the eleventh hour. He’s willing to help me test the Subtle Matter theory and get to the truth. To do this, I need to find the entrance to Lab X3 somewhere at the Cordon and retrieve the cartridge from the Signature Station.
sid_journal_description_E11_MQ02_Descrip: With the cartridge from X3 in my possession, I’m now heading to Orbita Station. Beneath it is the underground Laboratory X15, where Kaimanov once tried to establish a connection to the Noosphere. According to the Doctor, what I’ll find there could either prove or disprove the Representative’s claims about Subtle Matter. Although Kaimanov himself saw nothing resembling life in the Noosphere during his tenure in X15, the emergence of the Zone could have changed everything.
sid_journal_description_E11_MQ03_Descrip: Strelok has figured out how to pinpoint the location of the STALKER Program Control Center. Simply activating the neuroprogrammers in Lab X3 should do the trick. The problem is, the lab’s controlled by the Ward. Naturally, I’m the one who has to make it happen, not him.
sid_journal_description_E11_MQ04_Descrip: Seems like I’ve stumbled across some secrets the Ward would rather keep hidden. My run-in with Korshunov at the TCS ended with me getting knocked out, stripped of all my gear, and tossed into a cage. I doubt they’re keeping me alive out of the goodness of their hearts. I need to bust out before Korshunov comes back looking for answers – if he gets them, he won’t have any use for me.
sid_journal_description_E11_SQ01_Descrip: In exchange for the key to the cell, the Sparker told me where they stashed my stuff. It’s gonna be a tough job to get my gear back from right under the Wardens’ noses.
sid_journal_description_E11_SQ02_Descrip: Like many things in the Zone, the Oasis wasn’t what it seemed to be. I didn’t find the so-called “Klondike of Artifacts” there. Still, it might be a good idea to have a chat with Sidorovich. Dark’s beacon with the coordinates for the entrance to Lab X3 could fetch a pretty penny.
sid_journal_description_E12_MQ01_Descrip: Looks like the main route to Prypiat goes through Yaniv Station, which is controlled by the Corps. I’ll try going that way, and if they don’t let me through, I’ll track down a stalker called Spirit. He might be able to help me.
sid_journal_description_E14_MQ01_Descrip: I need to get to the STALKER Program Control Center. The facility can be accessed through the underground tunnels, but, according to Korshunov, only Degtyarev knows the coordinates for the entrance. It’s high time I paid him a visit.

I need to get to the STALKER Program Control Center. The facility can be accessed through the underground tunnels, but Scar says Degtyarev is the only one who knows the coordinates for the entrance. I guess it’s about time I tracked him down.

I need to get to the STALKER Program Control Center. The facility can be accessed through the underground tunnels, but, according to Strelok, only Degtyarev knows the coordinates for the entrance. I guess it’s about time I tracked him down.
sid_journal_description_E14_MQ02_Descrip: Now that I have the coordinates, all I have to do is get inside the Foundation control center. Once we’re done with Spark and Degtyarev, nothing will stop us from wiping the Zone off the face of the Earth.

Now that I have the coordinates, all I have to do is get inside the Foundation’s control center. Once I’m there, we’ll finally find out what’s needed to successfully complete the Shining plan – and cleanse the Zone of all darkness.
Now that I have the coordinates, all I have to do is get inside the Foundation control center. Once the Ward and Spark cease to be a problem, the Zone can finally be free.
sid_journal_description_E15_MQ01_Descrip: I’m about to venture into the very heart of the Zone, and the only path forward goes through the Dead Valley. My goal is within reach. I need to get to the Generators alive, at any cost, and restore the broken connection with the Foundation.
sid_journal_description_E15_MQ02_Descrip: I’m on the verge of reaching my goal. According to the Representative, the realization of the Shining plan requires a stable connection to the Noosphere. Now I need to navigate through the Dead Valley to reach the Generators and restore connection with the Foundation.
sid_journal_description_E15_MQ03_Descrip: The STALKER program is over. Strelok believes that this is not enough and we need to deal with everyone who has plans to use the Zone in their own interests. And it seems that Kaimanov is the last of them. I’ve got to talk to him.
sid_journal_description_E16_MQ01_Descrip: All bets are off. I’ve reached the Generators. Now it’s time to set up the teleport device in order to transfer Ward’s expedition squad, reach the X7 lab, and help Korshunov enter the Eighth Pod. The Zone will disappear, but I will get the promised new home in a new world where humanity can use the anomalous energy effectively and safely.
sid_journal_description_E16_MQ02_Descrip:
I made it. All I need to do now is take care of the teleport device, bring Spark’s expedition squad to the Generators, get to the X7 lab, and help Scar enter the Eighth Pod to initiate the Shining – eternal life in the Noosphere. 
I hope to finally find my bright and peaceful new home out there.
sid_journal_description_E16_MQ03_Descrip: I managed to get into the Generators without crossing the Dead Valley. Now I just need to reach the X7 lab’s Eighth pod. The Zone is my new home, and my future is inextricably linked with her, but only the owner of the Heart of Chornobyl will change the fate of the Zone forever.
sid_journal_description_EmissionQuest_De: Wait out the Emission in the shelter.
sid_journal_description_RRQ_Description_: I’ve stirred up trouble with one of the factions in the Zone. It’s best to lay low until things settle down, then discuss terms with their leader.
sid_journal_description_RSQ01_Descriptio: It sounds like an easy job – almost too easy, in fact. Still, Warlock seems trustworthy, and a few extra coupons can’t hurt.
sid_journal_description_RSQ04_Descriptio: Boozer tossed a gig my way. I just hope he’s got the coupons to cover it. If I do it quick and clean, he won’t have much of a choice.
sid_journal_description_RSQ05_Descriptio: Owl’s jobs often seem kinda sketchy – as does Owl himself. I figure it’s smart to keep our partnership under wraps just in case.
sid_journal_description_RSQ06_Descriptio: Sidorovich doesn’t pretend to be noble, and that’s fine by me. The only healthy relationships in the Zone are based on fair pay for honest work.
sid_journal_description_RSQ07_Descriptio: The source of Duty’s funding remains one of the Zone’s mysteries, but who am I to complain? Especially when some of their money ends up in my pocket in exchange for completing relatively simple tasks.
sid_journal_description_RSQ08_Descriptio: The Barkeep’s been in the Zone for longer than most of the regulars who frequent his place. And if he’s managed to survive this long, he’s probably someone I can count on – as long as he doesn’t try to repay me with booze instead of coupons…
sid_journal_description_RSQ09_Descriptio: Needle seems kinda suspicious, but in the Zone, that’s par for the course – especially for a scientist. As long as he doesn’t try any crazy experiments on me and pays well, I’m fine with working with him.
sid_journal_description_RSQ10_Descriptio: Harpy’s got a serious job lined up for me, but I’ve got to watch my step, especially around Prypiat. Here’s hoping the payoff is worth the trouble.
sid_journal_description_SQ01_Description: Warlock, the bartender over at Zalissya, wants me to do a bit of debt collecting for him. There’s a hothead called Valik Lummox who owes him money. He was last seen north of Zalissya near the Water Tower. If I can manage to squeeze the debt out of him, I’ll get twenty percent.
sid_journal_description_SQ02_Description: An Orthodox icon for the price of an artifact! Mityay, a stalker who used to live here before the disaster of ’86, wants to get his family heirloom back. He left it in an old root cellar somewhere in the Poppy Field. That place has a knack for putting folks to sleep, he says, so retrieving the icon won’t be easy.
sid_journal_description_SQ03_Description: Lens, the technician from Zalissya, asked me to search for a group of diggers led by Mastiff. He sent them to investigate some old stashes found in PDAs belonging to deceased stalkers. Lens is worried that the diggers might have been ambushed at one of these spots. He suggested starting the search for Mastiff and his team at the location of their last run.
sid_journal_description_SQ04_1_Descripti: I made a bet with a hunter known as Nightingale. We’re competing to hunt blind dogs near Zalissya. Whoever bags the most mutants gets all the coupons for the job.
sid_journal_description_SQ04_2_Descripti: Nightingale offered to split a hunting job with me. He wants me to collect all the collars from mutants in the Lesser Zone that he’s aware of. Hamster’s willing to buy any collars I can get my hands on. I can’t help but wonder if someone actually tried to tame those freaks…
sid_journal_description_SQ05_Description: Huron’s lost a courier carrying an important shipment for the Brood – well, for their nutjob leader, the Shah. Huron doesn’t want any trouble with the kingpin, so he’s asking for help finding the missing goods.
sid_journal_description_SQ06_Description: I need to find an old Monolith gizmo to help start up a trading venture. Ideally, it should be operational.
sid_journal_description_SQ103_Descriptio: Diode asked me to bring him the Emitter from the X18 laboratory. Some scientist used to work on that Emitter, following Nestor's order. The project was called the Aggression Suppressor, and I do not like the name one bit.
sid_journal_description_SQ10_Description: In the Zone, a distress call is a sign of complete desperation. This Jumper at the waste processing station must be in dire straits if he’s broadcasting openly, risking being overheard by bandits or worse. I can’t let my guard down, though – a call for help like this could easily be a trap.
sid_journal_description_SQ13_mat_Descrip: The Warden guard at the Chemical Plant, Mateus, asked me to bring him an artifact from the Chemical Tanks. He said no one would match the pay he offers for it.
sid_journal_description_SQ18_Description: Beard asked me for help. His men went into the Swamps and got ambushed. Vitko Nasal was the only one who made it back. He says the Sultan’s goons have taken the other guys captive. Looks like I’ll have to rescue them.
sid_journal_description_SQ20_Description: Rostok loves bread and circuses. The coveted title of Master of the Arena is on the line, along with respect and a fat reward. I might as well indulge the crowd, especially since their champ hasn’t faced much competition in quite some time.
sid_journal_description_SQ20_R1_Descript: To figure out the rules of the Arena, I’ve got to step into the ring for the first fight. For now, the odds are stacked against me.
sid_journal_description_SQ20_R2_Descript: My first Arena fight is done, and I clinched the win! Arnie even upgraded my status from “rotten piece of meat” to “ordinary piece of meat.” Not exactly a glowing review, but let’s see what he says after the next bout.
sid_journal_description_SQ20_R3_Descript: Things are heating up in the Arena. The mutants were just a warmup. Now Arnie’s ready to offer me a bigger challenge.
sid_journal_description_SQ20_R4_Descript: Looks like Arnie didn’t even expect me to make it this far. Now he’s dangling a real surprise in front of me. But the prize for winning is awfully enticing.
sid_journal_description_SQ20_R5_Descript: Well, I made it to the finals. It’s all or nothing now.
sid_journal_description_SQ25_Description: Cutting ties with certain friends is easier said than done, even when you’re aiming for a fresh start. But in the Zone, you don’t get to pick your pals – you stick with them till the bitter end, even if they’re dragging you down. I just hope Panas knew what he was getting himself into when he asked for their help.
sid_journal_description_SQ47_01_Descript: That scumbag Zakhar from the Slag Heap promised me a big score, but it was all just a ploy to set me and Akopyan up for a bandit ambush. Zakhar’s cronies got the drop on Akopyan, and now he’s out for blood — and looking to me for help to settle the score. Stirring this pot might not be the smartest move in the world, but letting Zakhar off the hook just feels wrong.
sid_journal_description_SQ47_Description: The bar over at the Slag Heap isn’t just for kicking back, it’s also a hotspot for finding gigs. Taking on a brief stint as a debt collector for Zakhar and his crew to score a share of the debtor’s cash? Sounds like a breeze! Having money and connections can make all the difference in the Zone.
sid_journal_description_SQ50_Description: The Sultan has given me a job. After the Emission, he found himself unable to reach one of his old stashes – the entire area is teeming with mutants and anomalies now. His goons can’t get anywhere near it, so he wants me to get to the stash and retrieve a briefcase for him. I can’t help but wonder what makes it so valuable…
sid_journal_description_SQ81_Description: Sonya Kalyna hired me to take out both the Sultan and Beard. Their never-ending feud is dragging Zaton down, and neither of them can secure a better future for the Sultansk. At the moment, they’re gearing up for a showdown at the Harbor Cranes. Once they’re out of the picture, Kalina will take the helm of the Sultansk and put things back on track.
sid_journal_description_SQ86_Description: Mytryk Genius asked me to recover a gun he lost in the underground area beneath the Concrete Forest. Seems straightforward enough, but something doesn’t add up about the stalker’s tale. A weapon slipping from his grasp as if on its own, strange noises echoing in the dark… I gotta keep my eyes peeled when I go down there. There’s something creepy lurking underground, and I’d rather see it in my crosshairs than have it sneak up on me from behind.
sid_journal_description_SQ87_Description: I ran into a bunch of bandits in the midst of a confrontation that quickly escalated into a full-blown shootout. Then I stumbled across a nearby building with a group of loner captives inside. They’ve been locked up there for two days, deprived of food and water. Zhenya Hulk, one of the prisoners, said the key is on the gang leader Adik Pedal, AKA “the scumbag with scars all over his face.” I need to hurry if I want to rescue them.

Back at the Fish Farm, I found that all the captives except for Zhenya Hulk had starved to death. Zhenya himself is barely clinging to life – if I’m going to rescue him, the time is now. I need the key from Adik Pedal, AKA “the bandit with scars all over his face.”
sid_journal_description_SQ89_Description: Sidorovich asked me to deal with a stalker called Wisp. This guy had the audacity to go behind Sidor’s back and reach out to his suppliers, even roping in some village loners without clearing it with Sidorovich first. Sidorovich isn’t one to let disrespect like this slide, so he’s willing to pay good money to make Wisp and any associates he might have disappear.
sid_journal_description_SQ90_Description: Barkeep offered me a serious job – to deal with the bandits holed up at the Container Warehouse in the Red Forest. They've become a real thorn in Freedom's side, and now there's a hefty reward on their heads. It's worth the risk, but I need to decide how to play it: go for a bold assault, or take them out quietly. Maybe I should start by looking for the Freedomers that Barkeep mentioned. I'll scout out the area around the base on the way.

Carl Clinch asked me to deal with the bandits holed up at the Containers in the Red Forest. His group tried to reach the bandit nest through an underground tunnel, only to get jumped along the way. Somebody must have ratted them out. The story sounds fishy, but the reward’s fat enough to make it worth the risk. Now do I sneak through the tunnel, or charge in guns blazing?
sid_journal_description_SQ91_Description: Duty’s bracing for another Monolith assault on the Cooling Towers. The odds aren’t pretty, but Major Rodetsky’s refusing to abandon the wounded or give up their position. If I want to lend a hand, there are critically injured soldiers on the second floor who urgently need medkits. And if I head up to the roof, there’s a sniper rifle and a panoramic view of the battlefield just waiting for me.
sid_journal_description_SQ92_Description: I ran into a Sparker called Mongoose who needs a hand activating two devices simultaneously for a science mission. They’re in different locations, and with the Ward base nearby, he’s willing to pay good money for my help. He claims it’s gonna be a walk in the park. The whole thing sounds fishy, but I’m inclined to give it a shot.
sid_journal_description_SQ93_Description: I didn’t expect to find a scientist standing behind the counter, but even in this capacity, Prof. Dorozhnyuk shows her commitment to Malachite’s cause. She asked me to find some stalkers who went missing while collecting research data, and I agreed to help. Her concern for others appears genuine, but even if she’s only helping those stalkers to resolve her issues with Ozersky, it’s still better than leaving them in the lurch.
sid_journal_description_SQ94_Description: Saw Honta in Zaton – the guy started a hunting school, sharing his knowledge. Weirdly, it's free, and he's paying for mutant trophies. He seemed smart, and he accepts all students, even the less capable. I wonder if he can actually teach me something, or, as the Zone has taught me, if I should expect a catch?
sid_journal_description_TEQ01_Descriptio: !!!ATTENTION!!! THAT'S A TEST QUEST. ALL THE ASSETS WILL BE DELETED BEFORE FEB.20, 2025. Khorundzhy asks Skif to find his friend Honta, who has decided to go to the ruins nearby to find the notes of Prof. Nesterenko.
sid_journal_description_WhoLetPetyrOut_D: That is such a sweet building out there. What kind of adventures await you there?
sid_journal_description_supack_ANCQ_27_D: Received SOS signal. Someone named Kartaush is requesting immediate assistance. Worth investigating.
sid_journal_description_supack_SQ100_Des: Banzai's equipment is not strong enough to record and decipher the whole transmission that's making people crazy. If I manage to find the right amplifier, the group will finally be able to solve this mystery.
sid_journal_description_supack_SQ101_Des: I don't believe for a second that the bandit encounter at Volkhov was a coincidence. Someone from Kazkovyi ratted me out to Skull. I need to find the rat before the mercenaries figure out where the camp is.
sid_journal_description_supack_SQ102_Des: Turns out Banzai ratted me out to the mercenaries to get their frequencies. He also turned the amplifier on, so now Skull knows where the camp is. Our only option now is his crazy plan: Medulin creates an interfering signal to protect us from the brain-frying transmission, Banzai transmits it to the mercenaries, while Scoutmaster and I defend the camp, hoping they can pull it off before we run out of ammunition.
sid_journal_description_supack_SQ95_Desc: I found Banzai, the technician, captured by the bandits in the Old Mine. He says their group was attacked, and he doesn't know what happened to the others. He asked me to free him from the bandits and get his things back – Banzai refuses to run away without them. I wonder what could be so precious.
sid_journal_description_supack_SQ96_C01_: I saved Banzai, the technician, from the bandits, but it turns out his troubles were far from over. There's a manhunt for him and three others: Rascal, Scoutmaster, and Medulin. Banzai asked me to get his tools so he could study some strange radio signal, and promised to tell me more if I bring them to him.
sid_journal_description_supack_SQ96_C02_: To repair gear and equipment, Banzai from Kazkovyi camp needs calibrating tools. He gave me the coordinates to his colleague's stashes, and the tools should be in one of them.
sid_journal_description_supack_SQ96_C03_: Banzai, the technician from Kazkovyi, needs precision tools, for more complex gear repairs. He's trying to prove himself useful after what he pulled. He gave me the coordinates to the stash in Boiler Room C-2. Hope there are no mysteries this time.
sid_journal_description_supack_SQ96_Desc: Banzai has some work back at the hub, and he needs tools. He managed to locate a couple of radio beacons in the stashes that were made by someone who owes him from way back. Now I need to use the spectrum analyzer, find the stashes, and take whatever's inside.
sid_journal_description_supack_SQ97_Desc: I brought the tools to Kazkovyi to give them to Banzai, like we agreed, and found his wounded friend Scoutmaster. While the two of us are waiting for Banzai, Scoutmaster asked me to help around the camp.
sid_journal_description_supack_SQ98_99_D: Banzai and Scoutmaster told me they were part of a group of researchers. They were studying a strange radio frequency and got ambushed by bandits. Scoutmaster asked me to find the two other group members – Medulin, the scientist, and Rascal, the quartermaster. Maybe together they can figure out why they're being hunted down.
sid_journal_description_supack_SQ98_Desc: I need to find the medic called Medulin, he's one of the researchers. According to Scoutmaster, he might be hiding in the railway depots of Malachite.
sid_journal_stage_ALTQuest01_CheckBodies: Check stalkers bodies for any information
sid_journal_stage_ALTQuest01_Comeback: Come back to Thought and Memory
sid_journal_stage_ALTQuest01_GoBack: Go back to the workshop and pick up Thought's gun
sid_journal_stage_DLC00_ItemHunt_Journal: Take a look inside your stash
sid_journal_stage_E01_MQ01_FindArt: Find the artifact in the anomalous field
sid_journal_stage_E01_MQ01_Hide: Move out of the Scanner’s range
sid_journal_stage_E01_MQ01_PowerLineTake: Take the Scanner and review the readings
sid_journal_stage_E01_MQ01_PutTheScanner: Set up and activate the Scanner
sid_journal_stage_E01_MQ01_TakePripoyPoi: Get information about the scanning points
sid_journal_stage_E01_MQ01_Wait: Wait for the Scanner to finish scanning
sid_journal_stage_E02_MQ01_EscapeAnomaly: Get out of the anomalous field
sid_journal_stage_E02_MQ01_PochtovoeItem: Help the stalkers at the Post Office
sid_journal_stage_E02_MQ02_BS: Explore the warehouse with the radiation hazard signs
sid_journal_stage_E02_MQ02_Batya: Take the Ward Sensors to the Gaffer
sid_journal_stage_E02_MQ02_C01_Antenna: Activate the antenna on the tower in Zalissya
sid_journal_stage_E02_MQ02_C03_Back: Return to Squint at the mill
sid_journal_stage_E02_MQ02_C03_Cave: Find the entrance to the cave
sid_journal_stage_E02_MQ02_Harya: Examine Mug’s body for clues about Squint’s whereabouts
sid_journal_stage_E02_MQ02_Hytor: Check the farmstead where Squint might be hiding
sid_journal_stage_E02_MQ02_Items: Retrieve the Ward Sensors from Squint
sid_journal_stage_E02_MQ02_Rihter: Give the Ward Sensors to Richter
sid_journal_stage_E02_MQ02_Zotov: Give the Ward Sensors to Captain Zotov
sid_journal_stage_E02_MQ03_GoPostNorth: Leave the Lesser Zone via the Northern Checkpoint
sid_journal_stage_E02_MQ03_GoPostWest: Leave the Lesser Zone via the Pontoon Bridge Checkpoint
sid_journal_stage_E02_MQ03_RebraEntrance: Get into the Sphere’s abandoned communication hub
sid_journal_stage_E02_MQ03_RebraSearch: Search the bodies of IPSF soldiers
sid_journal_stage_E02_MQ03_RihterKey: Take the key to the Northern Checkpoint
sid_journal_stage_E02_MQ03_WarlockPripoy: Ask Warlock the bartender about Solder
sid_journal_stage_E03_MQ01_FindVodka: Bring Hosha Mohyla’s ‘magic’ vodka to Vetos
sid_journal_stage_E03_MQ01_FollowFireTea: Begin the assault on the Depot
sid_journal_stage_E03_MQ01_KillVarta: Fight off the Ward’s assault on the Depot
sid_journal_stage_E03_MQ01_Korshunov_Ava: Meet up with Colonel Korshunov at the Outpost
sid_journal_stage_E03_MQ01_MeetFireTeam: Join up with the assault squad
sid_journal_stage_E03_MQ01_StealthPath: Sneak into the Depot and find Chornozem
sid_journal_stage_E03_MQ03_BringRoosevel: Bring the Shah’s and Roosevelt’s PDAs to Varan
sid_journal_stage_E03_MQ03_BringShahPDA: Bring the Shah’s PDA to Varan
sid_journal_stage_E03_MQ03_EQ57Done: Find the secret passage to the Commissary
sid_journal_stage_E03_MQ04_HelpVaran: Deal with the Shah or Roosevelt for Varan
sid_journal_stage_E03_MQ04_KillBoth: Deal with the Shah and Roosevelt on Varan’s behalf
sid_journal_stage_E03_MQ04_PayForInfo: Get information about the attackers from Varan
sid_journal_stage_E03_MQ05_Cave: Find any clues about the Scanner scheme
sid_journal_stage_E03_MQ05_LeaveKonderSh: Leave the workshop using the key card
sid_journal_stage_E03_MQ05_SearchKonderS: Examine the workshop for any clues
sid_journal_stage_E03_MQ06_FindSomeone: Learn more about the unusual PDA
sid_journal_stage_E03_MQ06_GoToOffice: Get up to the second floor
sid_journal_stage_E03_MQ06_TalkToScar: Show the unusual PDA to Scar
sid_journal_stage_E03_SQ02_BringBackDocu: Bring the specifications for Product 81 to the Sparkers
sid_journal_stage_E03_SQ02_Documents: Find the documents from Nestor’s archive
sid_journal_stage_E04_MQ01_FindKey: Take the key from North on the WPS roof
sid_journal_stage_E04_MQ01_FindPDAOwner: Learn more about the unusual PDA
sid_journal_stage_E04_MQ01_ReachStation: Go down to the underground level of the station
sid_journal_stage_E04_MQ02_ActivateAzimu: Give Strider access to Azimuth’s systems
sid_journal_stage_E04_MQ02_Varta: Retrieve the Azimuth access key from the Ward
sid_journal_stage_E04_SQ01_BackToNooners: Take the cargo to Wild Island
sid_journal_stage_E04_SQ01_BringContaine: Deliver the container to Prof. Lodochka
sid_journal_stage_E04_SQ01_FindAmbushPla: Find the spot where the Noontiders made contact for the last time
sid_journal_stage_E04_SQ01_TakePackage: Pick up the cargo for Noontide
sid_journal_stage_E04_SQ02_KillZombieNoo: Kill the zombie Noontider on the roof

sid_locations_archanomaly_wandering_ligh: A mysterious swamp anomaly that suppresses light sources.
sid_locations_hub_asulym_desc: This is the base of Noontide, a group comprised of ex-Monolith soldiers who found new purpose under the leadership of Strider following the events of 2012. Given the general aversion towards former Monolithians in the rest of the Zone, this stronghold on Wild Island has truly become their shelter.
sid_locations_hub_chemplant_desc: The Chemical Plant serves as the primary headquarters for the Ward. Accessible only with special passes or permission from a high-ranking officer, this area is off limits to outsiders. From this strategic location, the Ward seeks to assert control over the Zone and other factions.
sid_locations_hub_concreteplant_desc: The Cement Factory functions as Duty’s command center. This is where they train new recruits, guard the passage to the Cooling Towers, and outfit groups assigned to clear the Zone of mutants.
sid_locations_hub_enerhetyk_desc: A remote Corps ourtpost in Prypiat, the last little safe haven in the town the Monolith has returned to. No matter what comes next, the Corps will guard this place with their lives: surrendering it means giving up all of Prypiat.
sid_locations_hub_ikar_desc: Occupying an abandoned Young Pioneer camp in the Swamps, Icarus is a Ward base originally established to safeguard SIRCAA from external threats. As SIRCAA implemented an independent security system of its own, Icarus gradually lost its strategic importance and fell into a state of visible decline.
sid_locations_hub_malahit_desc: The Malachite Science and Technology Center was launched as a Zone research facility under the UNA, marking the next phase after field laboratories. After the establishment of SIRCAA, Malachite lost its former prominence, but gained increased autonomy and room for experiments, including those viewed as pseudoscientific by the international community.
sid_locations_hub_rookievillage_desc: Once a haven for newcomers, this village has now fallen into disrepair. Despite its proximity to the Zone’s border and well-preserved buildings and basements, rookie stalkers opt for Zalissya nowadays. Nevertheless, Sidorovich, the local dealer in equipment and information, refuses to accept this state of affairs and remains determined to restore the Cordon to its former prominence.
sid_locations_hub_rostok_desc: The Rostok Factory acts as Freedom’s central hub, embodying the most cherished dreams of all traders throughout the Zone. Here, the biggest deals are struck and the most fascinating curiosities change hands. Even a brief rest here is a delightful experience, for, apart from their trading expertise, Freedom members are renowned for their remarkable ability to enjoy themselves.
sid_locations_hub_shevchenko_desc: The forlorn and deteriorating remains of a cargo ship that has miraculously withstood the ravages of Emissions. While the Shevchenko may provide a refuge to wait out inclement weather or pick up odd jobs, anything of importance in Zaton happens aboard the Sultansk.
sid_locations_hub_sultansk_desc: Once known as the Skadovsk and now rechristened the Sultansk, this old cargo ship stands as a beacon for all those conducting business in the Swamps and Zaton. Regardless of whether bandits or Stalkers are at the helm, this vessel offers a place to rest and numerous work opportunities for everyone. Despite her name changes, she remains a fixture that will never sail away.
sid_locations_hub_terricon_desc: Sheltered by a landfill on one side and a mound of industrial debris on the other, the Slag Heap stands as the sole neutral ground in Garbage. It attracts members from rival gangs, loners, and all manner of Zone inhabitants, including diggers scavenging for valuables amid the mountains of discarded technology, all converging here to rest and trade.
sid_locations_hub_yanov_desc: This former train station is a gateway to the Boundary, a railcar barricade constructed by Degtyarev’s Corps to cut Prypiat off from the broader Zone to the south. It serves as a resting spot for stalkers willing to brave the deserted city.
sid_locations_hub_zalesie_desc: Serving as the heart of the Lesser Zone, Zalissya offers plenty of residential buildings and bunkers that double as shelters against Emissions. It’s the prime location for newcomers to purchase weapons and equipment upon entering the Zone for the first time. Zalissya is the place from which rookies venture deeper into the Zone, while seasoned stalkers pass it on their way back home.
sid_locations_loc_01_transformer_box_at_: Transformer Box at the Old Wall
sid_locations_loca1_desc: The Test Location is said to be a new, uncharted area within the Zone. No one has ever explored it and lived to tell the tale. Regardless, it’s no more substantial than other booze-fueled tall tales like the vanished city of Lymansk or the mythical Wish Granter.
sid_locations_region_CNPP_desc: In the aftermath of the 1986 catastrophe, the Chornobyl Nuclear Power Plant garnered global attention, and the Second Disaster transformed it into the unreachable center of the Zone. Rumor has it that the legendary stalker Strelok managed to explore the station at least once. Nevertheless, even Strelok remains ignorant of the present conditions within the CNPP.
sid_locations_region_cement_factory_desc: Constructed to help deal with the aftermath of the first Chornobyl disaster, the Cement Factory and its surroundings now fall under the jurisdiction of Duty. Although its influence has diminished, the group remains steadfast in its struggle against the Zone and everything it begets.
sid_locations_region_chemical_plant_desc: Formerly the headquarters of the renowned Spark group, the Chemical Plant is now the central hub of the Ward’s activities in the Zone. Korshunov has the entire area under control, and stalkers and bandits are a rare sight here nowadays, having been replaced by Warden patrols and the occasional Duty soldier.
sid_locations_region_cooling_towers_desc: These cooling towers and new power units once embodied the promise of the third stage of the CNPP, a symbol of hope from a bygone era. Now, only echoes resonate within the lofty concrete evaporator towers. Instead of a new reactor, the rusty walls of the Red Fortress bear witness to the last war waged between the stalkers and Monolith.
sid_locations_region_cordon_desc: In the past, all roads into the Zone passed through the Cordon. However, with most rookies now opting for Zalissya as their starting point, the Cordon has become a retreat for veterans who are stuck in the past. Although thoroughly explored by stalkers, the Cordon still harbors plenty of dark secrets yet to be uncovered.
sid_locations_region_dead_valley_desc: The way to the Generators is blocked by the forbidding Dead Valley, a desolate expanse haunted by phantoms, constantly shrouded in an enduring grayness. The landscape is strewn with the remnants of destroyed equipment and the corpses of those who have dared to traverse it. Venturing here means signing your own death warrant.
sid_locations_region_duga_desc: Persistent rumors about the old over-the-horizon radar station include stories of experiments on the population, bizarre radio interference, and brain-scorching radiation. Now that the route is no longer hindered by anomalous fields, certain daredevils frequently try to explore the enormous antenna. Their attempts are usually thwarted not by mysterious signals but by ordinary bullets fired by IPSF snipers. The military diligently guards the Duga, preventing anyone from discovering its purpose.
sid_locations_region_garbage_desc: This old graveyard for radioactive equipment is a hotspot for diggers and bandits alike. Diggers sift through the mounds of discarded junk, hoping to uncover valuable parts and artifacts, while bandits exploit those fortunate enough to make a discovery.
sid_locations_region_generators_desc: The original purpose of this facility remains shrouded in mystery, but the Generators have now become the beating heart of the Zone, unleashing devastating Emissions.
sid_locations_region_industrial_zone_des: The Industrial Zone is teeming with production facilities, military installations, and old labs. This region is a prime destination for stalkers who eagerly delve into the abandoned buildings, scavenging for valuable parts and looking for stashes left by their peers.
sid_locations_region_iron_forest_desc: Full of anomalies, downed power lines, and wires that still carry electricity seemingly from nowhere, the Iron Forest was once a relatively popular place. However, after one of the Emissions, it became impossible to get into its territory. Maybe the Zone is preparing something special there, or maybe it’s simply luring overly curious stalkers to certain death. Be that as it may, one thing is clear: there is no way there now.
sid_locations_region_jupiter_desc: Being the entrance to Prypiat, the vicinity of Jupiter has always attracted stalkers. A labyrinth of stone walls and ruined roads leads to the now inaccessible plant. No one knows what the Zone decided to hide in it after the last Emmission, but individuals and even entire groups of stalkers, fueled by curiosity, are trying to get inside. To this day, they’ve still been unsuccessful .
sid_locations_region_lesser_zone_desc: Nestled on the outskirts of the Zone, the Lesser Zone is one of its quieter regions. It often serves as a starting point for rookies making their first forays into the Zone from Zalissya. The military tends to overlook their presence as long as the stalkers keep their distance.
sid_locations_region_malachite_desc: The area around STC Malachite is the domain of scientists. Unlike their SIRCAA counterparts, who maintain no direct link to the Zone, Malachite scientists eagerly employ the services of stalkers, purchasing artifacts and enlisting their assistance in field experiments.
sid_locations_region_orbita_desc: Peering through the recently-grown trees of the Burnt Forest reveals massive military structures and the remnants of deserted residential buildings. A lingering scent of burning perpetually hangs in the air.
sid_locations_region_prypiat_desc: Renowned as one of the most perilous areas in the Zone, Prypiat remains under constant guard by the Corps for a reason: the abandoned city harbors a multitude of dangerous secrets. Yet, despite the immense danger, every real stalker dreams of braving the place and uncovering its mysteries.
sid_locations_region_red_forest_desc: As radiation enveloped the Lymansk Nature Preserve, it gave rise to the Red Forest. Half of it is bathed in perpetual sunlight, while the other half is cloaked in fog and rain. Nevertheless, both halves of the dead forest are filled with deadly radiation and vicious mutants, making it an almost insurmountable obstacle on the journey to the center of the Zone.
sid_locations_region_rostok_desc: Following the signing of the treaty, Freedom claimed the Rostok Factory as its headquarters. The factory’s workshops have evolved into a free trade zone, with the iconic 100 Rads Bar and Arena drawing in gamblers from all over the Zone.
sid_locations_region_sircaa_desc: Behind impregnable walls and robust security, SIRCAA is an imposing giant of glass and concrete. Within its confines, the most brilliant minds seek to uncover the Zone’s mysteries for the betterment of humanity’s future. But one question lingers: what kind of future do they have in mind?
sid_locations_region_swamp_desc: This ominous, seemingly endless marsh rife with dangerous mutants and anomalies both frightens and attracts stalkers. Some dream of finding a new Klondike of Artifacts there, while others want to unravel the mystery of Clear Sky, a local group that vanished without a trace.
sid_locations_region_wild_island_desc: In the center of the region stands a former toxic waste storage facility that is now claimed by Noontide. Lone stalkers avoid routes that go through the island – not because of its toxic fumes or radioactive water, but because of unsettling rumors surrounding its inhabitants.
sid_locations_region_yanov_desc: Yaniv Railroad Station is a refuge for Degtyarev’s Corps, the protectors of the Boundary, a barricade of railcars blocking the way to Prypiat. The Corps tirelessly patrols the area, hunting and apprehending any stalkers who attempt to reach the Center of the Zone.
sid_locations_region_yantar_desc: Standing on the shore of the lake, the Yantar Factory may appear scenic and tranquil. However, stalkers prefer to keep their distance, considering the place highly hazardous, with practically nothing of value left. Even the few scientists who were still keen on investigating the mysterious radiation at the factory eventually left the site, leaving only an empty bunker behind.
sid_locations_region_zaton_desc: In its prime, Zaton was a bustling shipping center. After the disaster, the water receded, leaving ships and boats stranded on sandy shores to rust. Today, new businesses and arrangements have taken root, with the Sultan’s bandits and Beard’s stalkers vying for control of the area.
sid_locations_skadovsk_desc: The Skadovsk, an old cargo ship grounded since 1986, is the unofficial hub of Zaton. Beard, her captain of sorts, makes sure there’s a spot for everyone aboard. The Skadovsk is the perfect place to take a breather, enjoy a drink, and gear up for the next adventure.
sid_locations_supack_Kazkovy_Hub2_desc: The newest camp in the Zone. No one thought there would be madmen crazy enough to settle Kazkovyi, but so far, they're doing well.
sid_locations_supack_Kazkovy_Hub_desc: Someone decided to seek shelter in a camp where several scientific teams have already disappeared. Not the best idea.
sid_locations_zone_desc: Since 2006, the Chornobyl Anomalous Zone has been a magnet for mystery lovers. Some see it as a fragile fairytale to preserve and safeguard, others — a living nightmare meant to be tamed or destroyed.
sid_lscreen_Loading_screen_01_desc: Following the treaty, Rostok fell into Freedom’s hands, becoming their stronghold and the Zone’s largest market. Whether you’re looking for relaxation, thrills, or trade, Rostok is the place to be.
sid_lscreen_Loading_screen_02_desc: If you’re going to be sneaking around, do it on a rainy day – it reduces visibility and muffles your footsteps. And another piece of advice: remember to turn that flashlight off, unlike some smartasses from my past...
sid_lscreen_Loading_screen_03_desc: You can’t spot an artifact without a detector, no matter what other people might say. Invest in a top-notch detector for better loot. Don’t be stingy if you’re going on a serious run.
sid_lscreen_Loading_screen_04_desc: Every artifact comes with its own set of pros and cons. A seasoned stalker picks their artifacts wisely, ensuring that the positives outweigh the negatives. Be careful when experimenting, though. Or, at the very least, stay close to a medic.
sid_lscreen_Loading_screen_05_desc: Discovering an anomaly doesn’t guarantee that you’re going to find an artifact. The Zone is a capricious mistress – if you come up empty-handed in one anomaly, try your luck in the next one.
sid_lscreen_Loading_screen_06_desc: “Specific anomalies tend to cough up specific artifacts,” some know-it-all once said. He might have a point about the ordinary stuff, but what about the truly rare finds? You’ll only stumble across those if the Zone really loves you. No amount of scientific insight can help you there.
sid_lscreen_Loading_screen_07_desc: The better your armor, the more artifact slots you’ll have. Once you’re accustomed to the Zone, the first thing on your shopping list should be armor. It’ll shield you from bullets, and your artifacts won’t just gather dust in your backpack.
sid_lscreen_Loading_screen_08_desc: Putting random artifacts on your armor might make your life thrilling, but it’ll also make it ruefully short. Study each artifact, understand how their properties mesh, then incorporate them into your loadout.
sid_lscreen_Loading_screen_09_desc: You can’t pinpoint an artifact without a detector, but even with one, you might end up chasing your tail. Artifacts are crafty little swines – they won’t just drop into your lap. If it were that easy, everyone would be a stalker.
sid_lscreen_Loading_screen_10_desc: Artifacts come in all kinds of flavors. Some shield you from bullets, others from psi effects. Some might boost your strength, or even help you heal faster. But tread lightly around them, or no amount of anti-rads will save your ass.
sid_lscreen_Loading_screen_11_desc: Don’t offload all your artifacts – keep a few for yourself. Coupons won’t protect you from radiation or bullets, but artifacts just might.
sid_lscreen_Loading_screen_12_desc: You can actually hear artifacts if you listen closely enough. But take it from an old man – it’s more important to listen for a branch snapping under a bandit’s boot... or a mutant's foot.
sid_lscreen_Loading_screen_13_desc: There’s one rule everyone follows in the Zone: toss a bolt in there first, then make your move. Anomalies don’t discriminate – discharging into a piece of iron or ripping you apart, it’s all the same to them.
sid_lscreen_Loading_screen_14_desc: More and more stalkers are staying in Zalissya, and fewer at the Cordon. Can't say who's to blame – the new Perimeter, IPSF instead of the regular military, or that shameless Sidorovich. So if you're a greenhorn, head straight to Zalissya. Stay alive.
sid_lscreen_Loading_screen_15_desc: Lost your way in the Lesser Zone? Head to the balloon. It used to float over the CNPP Sarcophagus, but now it serves as a beacon for newbies. No one knows how it got dragged all the way to Zalissya, and if anyone does know, they aren't talking.
sid_lscreen_Loading_screen_16_desc: Korshunov came to the Zone and told everyone they either sign the D4 non-aggresion pact, or there'll be no one left to sign it. But don't think it's all rainbows and roses here. There may not be any big fights, but if you're with Freedom and wander off into Duty territory, you might go missing. Same the other way around.
sid_lscreen_Loading_screen_17_desc: We've been getting fewer mercenaries ever since the Ward came. Some even say Wardens forced them out of the Zone. But the ones left aren't getting any weaker or kinder: you get in their way - you get shot. If you see their colors, better stay away.
sid_lscreen_Loading_screen_18_desc: Clear Sky used to be a faction. Decent guys, I met some of them way back. But there aren't any left, word is, they all went to the Center of the Zone. So if anyone tries to talk crap about meeting them, don't fall for it.
sid_lscreen_Loading_screen_19_desc: Noontiders are former Monolithians, building their new life on an island. Their boss, Strider, is a good guy. I still wouldn't get too close if I were you. What if they glitch again?
sid_lscreen_Loading_screen_20_desc: Hell knows who Monolithians were before. They used to pray to the Wish Granter and protect the Center of the Zone. Now they're not crazy anymore, they're living in the Zone like normal stalkers. So be extra careful around the weird ones – you never know which of them used to be a walking nightmare.
sid_lscreen_Loading_screen_21_desc: Spark are stalkers who used to only work for scientists. Their dream was to unveil the Zone's mysteries, until the Ward attacked them. Now they've got the biggest grudge against Wardens I've ever seen. Keep that in mind if you decide to work with Korshunov, and watch your back.
sid_lscreen_Loading_screen_22_desc: The Perimeter used to be guarded by the military. They've now sent the International Perimeter Security Force, or IPSF for short. Stay away from them, as they don't care which patch you wear. Loner, bandit, or Monolithian are all the same to them – intruders. You're just another target.
sid_lscreen_Loading_screen_23_desc: Freedom believes the Zone will make people better. Their version of 'better' is pretty unconventional, but they always have plenty of goods and coupons. If you need to buy or sell something in bulk, they're your guys.
sid_lscreen_Loading_screen_24_desc: Duty is trying to rid humanity of the Zone. Noble, of course, but the Zone is still here, and Duty… has seen better days. But don't push it. If you get too chummy with Freedom, they can still make your life difficult, even now.
sid_lscreen_Loading_screen_25_desc: The Zone has rules of its own. No wonder it attracts those in trouble with the law on the Mainland. There are about as many bandits here as mutants, so when you see them, shoot without thinking twice. 'Cos that's what they'll do.
sid_lscreen_Loading_screen_26_desc: Loners are the largest group here in the Zone – stalkers who haven't joined any of the factions, and only reporting to their conscience. Except not everybody has one, so think twice before making any deals. This is the Zone, and there's no such thing as friendship here.
sid_lscreen_Loading_screen_27_desc: If you need some kinda special gizmo, go to the scientists. We've got SIRCAA, STC Malachite, and even occasional loners, all hustling to neatly organize our miracles into their filing cabinets. But something tells me those cabinets ain't gonna hold up forever.
sid_lscreen_Loading_screen_28_desc: Help a guy out, and he'll probably tell his buddies about it. Same goes for hurting a guy. If you wanna stay under the radar, just keep on walking. And keep your finger off the trigger.
sid_lscreen_Loading_screen_29_desc: Life in the Zone is simple and straightforward. If you’ve botched your reputation or unintentionally taken someone out, keep some coupons handy… or be prepared to face off against the entire faction.
sid_lscreen_Loading_screen_30_desc: When an Emission hits, the worst thing you can do is get into a fight. All that matters is finding shelter and riding it out; everything else can wait. But don’t expect any help either – in these situations, it’s every man for himself.
sid_lscreen_Loading_screen_31_desc: The enemy of your enemy might not be your friend, but they might be a buddy of sorts. Kill someone, and their rivals might treat you better. Their friends, though… not so much.
sid_lscreen_Loading_screen_32_desc: If you’ve wrecked your reputation with a faction and have no intention of greasing the leader’s palms, gear up for war. Just don’t plan on surviving too long.
sid_lscreen_Loading_screen_33_desc: Pointing a gun at someone’s face is a bad call. At best, they might not get it. At worst, you’ll lose both your face and your gun.
sid_lscreen_Loading_screen_34_desc: PDAs are handy, but don’t forget to keep an eye on your surroundings. The world around you doesn’t stand still while you’re staring at a map.
sid_lscreen_Loading_screen_35_desc: Watch your step, and not just because of anomalies. Folks set traps near their stashes… or worse.
sid_lscreen_Loading_screen_36_desc: When the sky turns blood-red, bolt for the nearest shelter like your life’s on the line – ’cuz it is. An Emission spares no one, and there’s no escape out in the open.
sid_lscreen_Loading_screen_37_desc: Plenty of stalkers carry info about their stashes around just in case. So if you come across someone who won’t be needing his stuff anymore, give his body a once-over – if you ain’t too squeamish, of course.
sid_lscreen_Loading_screen_38_desc: You can’t cram everything you find in the Zone into a single suit. Just like with artifacts, pick your upgrades wisely. Otherwise you’ll be lugging around a ton of dead weight, and before long, you’ll wind up dead.
sid_lscreen_Loading_screen_39_desc: The Gilka may not be the most accurate detector, but it can sniff out an artifact on the other side of the Zone. Some say it makes artifact hunting even more exciting.
sid_lscreen_Loading_screen_40_desc: Capable technicians, the ones with the right set of skills, can both install and remove upgrades. However, some modifications are irreversible, no matter how hard you try. So think twice before taking a hacksaw to a gun barrel.
sid_lscreen_Loading_screen_41_desc: The Veles is the best detector out there. It’s not just about artifacts, though – it also sniffs out anomalies. So if you don’t want to end up in the wrong place after dark, get ready to fork over some serious cash.
sid_lscreen_Loading_screen_42_desc: A good technician is like a doctor – they’ve all got their specializations. If you want to add something unique, you’ll have to run around looking for a specialist.
sid_lscreen_Loading_screen_43_desc: You can track any location tag on your PDA, or even add your own markers. Don’t overdo it, though, or you might end up with a busted PDA… or a busted noggin.
sid_lscreen_Loading_screen_44_desc: Your compass doesn’t just display targets, it can also steer you toward folks who have already met their end. What you do with this information is your call, but the bandits are right about one thing: a dead stalker is as lucrative as a top-tier anomaly. So why let an opportunity go to waste?
sid_lscreen_Loading_screen_45_desc: You can always check your compass to see how much farther you have to go, but it doesn’t factor in obstacles. There’s no telling if you’ll need to take a detour around a hill, river, or the whole goddamn Slag Heap.
sid_lscreen_Loading_screen_46_desc: Some of the markers on your PDA point above or below you. When that happens, the compass will have an arrow next to the marker. If it points up, look up, and if it points down, look under your feet. And if it doesn’t point anywhere, just go straight ahead.
sid_lscreen_Loading_screen_47_desc: Radiation is more insidious than mutants and anomalies – mutants, you can spot, but radiation is invisible. So pay attention to your Geiger counter, have same anti-rad or vodka on hand, and keep an eye out, as lots of old radiation zones are marked with signs.
sid_lscreen_Loading_screen_48_desc: Bleeding isn’t “just a flesh wound” – it’s a serious, life-threatening condition. Remember to pack bandages and quality medkits.
sid_lscreen_Loading_screen_49_desc: Wild dashes, erratic jumps, and haphazard knife-waving might impress frightened rookies, but seasoned fighters know the importance of precision and restraint. They understand that conserving your energy is vital for survival.
sid_lscreen_Loading_screen_50_desc: To avoid becoming a mutant’s breakfast, make sure to grab a bite when you’re hungry. A starved stalker won’t last if he has to run, and the sound of your stomach growling can give away your position.
sid_lscreen_Loading_screen_51_desc: You know what kills folks more surely than any anomaly, mutant, or bandit? A backpack so crammed with loot that walking around with it becomes a struggle. Don’t get too greedy – stashes are there for a reason.
sid_lscreen_Loading_screen_52_desc: More than anything, mutants love sleepy stalkers – they’re so easy to catch! Make sure you always get enough sleep.
sid_lscreen_Loading_screen_53_desc: You gotta know your gun inside and out. The ammo it uses, its firing modes, compatible attachments – the whole megillah. You might forget your birthday, but always remember your gun’s caliber.
sid_lscreen_Loading_screen_54_desc: A real stalker has a steady hand, a cool head, and a spare mag. Once you’ve got that covered, the rest will follow.
sid_lscreen_Loading_screen_56_desc: Rumor has it that Strelok – THE Strelok – was spotted in the Zone again. The most desperate ones tried looking for him, and paid for it. Some lacked gear, others didn't have the skills, or simply ran out of luck. So unless you want to join them, prepare like never before.
sid_lscreen_Loading_screen_57_desc: I heard a story about that smartass who thought it was a good idea to toss a grenade at stashes tucked away in tricky spots. The loot stayed intact, but I’m willing to bet the mutants in the area appreciated the dinner bell.
sid_lscreen_Loading_screen_58_desc: Humans rely on sight, but mutants typically depend on smell. So don’t assume you’re the sharpest tool in the shed and the first guy to come up with the idea of sneaking past a pack of boars. Boars eat smartasses like that for breakfast.
sid_lscreen_Loading_screen_59_desc: In the old days, large packs of mutant rats were limited to the northern part of the Zone. However, their numbers have surged in recent years, and you can now run into them even in the areas that were previously considered “safe.”
sid_lscreen_Loading_screen_60_desc: Normally, if you fall into a stupor in the middle of a fight, you’re toast. But when all sorts of debris starts flying around, the best move is to freeze. Stop, duck, and crawl away nice and slow. If luck’s on your side, you might just make it out in one piece.
sid_lscreen_Loading_screen_61_desc: If you’re going on a hunt, make sure you’re packing the right piece. For dogs or Fleshes, a regular AK will do. If you’re after a boar or a deer, bring along a shotgun. And if you spot a chimera or a pseudogiant, ditch everything and run for it.
sid_notes_ANCQ01_HvorostPDANote_body:
$J0L0Vadya Kindling: Ever notice how there’s always a fuck-ton of blind dogs at the station?
Mykha Tur: They’re all over the place, man. That’s why I stick close to Zalissya.
Vadya Kindling: It’s like they’re drawn here. Maybe the equipment emits some kind of signal? Something that pulls them in?
Mykha Tur: Nah, all that equipment’s been fried a hundred times over! Folks hang out at the station, get loaded, pig out, and leave scraps everywhere – no wonder mutants come sniffing around.
Mykha Tur: You better scram before they overrun the place.
sid_notes_ANCQ01_HvorostPDANote_hint: A chat from the Radiation Monitoring Station.
sid_notes_ANCQ01_HvorostPDANote_name: Vadya Kindling’s Chat with Mykha Tur
sid_notes_ANCQ04_Lokot_PDA_Note_body:
$J0L0Elbow: Howdy, Vitko. A little birdie told me Leshy is somewhere on your turf. If you guys see him, drop me a message.
Vitko Handbrake: Should we grab him?
Elbow: I need the asshole alive. He went after me, if you can believe it. I’m not that easy to get, of course, and now I want him to rat out his employer.
Vitko Handbrake: I see. You got it.
sid_notes_ANCQ04_Lokot_PDA_Note_hint: Taken from his body near the POL Depot
sid_notes_ANCQ07_RabinaNote_body: $J0L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault> I got the coupons. Get me the gear. </NoteDefault></NoteBody>$J0L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault> I’ll be waiting at Baptist Hill. </NoteDefault></NoteBody>
sid_notes_ANCQ07_RabinaNote_hint: PDA from the body of Rowan, killed at Baptist Hill
sid_notes_ANCQ07_RabinaNote_name: A stalker’s chat with a dealer
sid_notes_ANCQ08_NoteFromSolovej_body: $J0L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault> I thought you never strayed far from your burrow, Hamster. But look at you, finding a cozy spot like this! Is it for vodka or fishing? Hopefully vodka, because all the fish kicked the bucket here back in ’06. I left you some sausage. Help yourself. </NoteDefault></NoteBody>
sid_notes_ANCQ08_NoteFromSolovej_hint: A note found in the Fisherman’s House next to some sausage
sid_notes_ANCQ10_NoteMedicText_body:
$J0L0Day 52
Patients reported difficulty breathing, fatigue, and headaches. High blood pressure noted in nearly all cases. Weather changes may be a factor, as standard tests came back with normal results…
Day 53
At first, I thought it was the cyclone, but it turned out to be an Emission that caused the Perimeter to expand! This expansion promises more chances to save lives. Perhaps, new artifacts will emerge. This event will undoubtedly lead to a better future for all of us!
Day 54
IPSF officials are expected shortly. They’ll terminate our research and enforce nondisclosure agreements. As if the signing of those papers could cover up the immense power of the Zone! I’m hoping this expansion of the Perimeter won’t be the last – further expansions could allow me to explore new ways to aid our patients.
sid_notes_ANCQ10_NoteMedicText_hint: A page from a doctor’s journal discovered at the elevator
sid_notes_ANCQ12_NoteFromDobrovolsky_bod: $J0L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault> It is my obligation to report that the unit commander has been engaging in the illicit trade of government supplies with loners and stalkers affiliated with various factions. Furthermore, he is pressuring his subordinates to confiscate coupons and personal belongings from individuals passing the checkpoint for his personal gain. Given the severity of these allegations, I hereby petition for an investigation into this matter. </NoteDefault></NoteBody>$J2L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault> Pvt. Dobrovolsky </NoteDefault></NoteBody>$J0L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault> Decision: have a discussion with Cpt. Sukhin and evaluate Pvt. Dobrovolsky’s professional competence </NoteDefault></NoteBody>$J2L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault> O. K. Sukhin </NoteDefault></NoteBody>
sid_notes_ANCQ12_NoteFromDobrovolsky_hea: $J0L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault> Report to Maj. Harmata regarding the misconduct of IPSF Sphere unit commander Cpt. Sukhin </NoteDefault></NoteBody>
sid_notes_ANCQ12_NoteFromDobrovolsky_hin: Found in one of the facilities at the Perimeter decontamination station
sid_notes_ANCQ15_NoteRat_body:
$J0L0Vadya Profit: You rat.
Mytko Pear: Were you even there? No? Then shut your mouth!
Danya Izbach: You rat..
Mytko Pear: And you, stay the fuck outta it! We’ll handle this without you.
Timokha Bleach: You rat.
Vasyan Sniffy: You rat.
Borya Feint: You rat.
Mytko Pear: Guys, what the fuck? Guys?
sid_notes_ANCQ15_NoteRat_hint: Taken from a bandit killed near the Railway Bridge
sid_notes_ANCQ16_AboutRoosevelt_body:
$J0L0Vadya Flyer: Nah, I’m sick of your crap.
Danylo Kepa: Oh yeah? And what’s that supposed to mean?
Vadya Flyer: You’re all actin’ like a pack of trained mutts, dancin’ to Roosevelt’s tune. He’s a fuckin’ cop, I’m tellin’ ya.
Danylo Kepa: Okay, I heard you.
Danylo Kepa: And when I come around to break your legs for flapping your gums, everyone else will hear you too.
sid_notes_ANCQ16_AboutRoosevelt_hint: Taken from a bandit killed near the Hydroelectric Station
sid_notes_ANCQ17_AboutVaran_body:
$J0L0Vitya Bun: Hey there! How’s it going, Mr. Creepy-Crawly?
Roma Sipuha: Not great, man. Stay outta this place. Varan’s one mean son of a gun. Not as nuts as the Shah, but I’d rather not share a bunk with him. Went on a job with my buddy once and ran into an ambush. They were really laying it on, and my buddy started blabbering about Varan. They let us off, but sent Varan the tab.
Vitya Bun: Oh, fuck.
Roma Sipuha: Yeah. We made it back to the Plant, but Varan’s not one to let a debt slide. He hands me a piece and says, “Debts are meant to be paid.” Then he points at my buddy.
Vitya Bun: What’d you do?
Roma Sipuha: Shot him dead. What else could I do? If I hadn’t, that bastard would’ve had us both whacked, damn it! That’s how it goes, bro.
sid_notes_ANCQ17_AboutVaran_hint: From a PDA found in Varan’s camp
sid_notes_ANCQ20_LostStalkerNote_body:
$J0L0Seryoha Siegmund: So? Did you get out?
Vovka Cottager: No. I didn’t.
Seryoha Siegmund: Why? What happened?
Vovka Cottager: It wouldn’t let me out.
Seryoha Siegmund: I see. Yeah, bro, there’s an entrance to the Zone, but there’s no exit. We just don’t belong anywhere anymore… not after what we’ve been through here.
sid_notes_ANCQ20_LostStalkerNote_hint: From a PDA found in a fishing cabin
sid_notes_ANCQ20_LostStalkerNote_name: The Zone Never Lets Anyone Out
sid_notes_ANCQ21_WaterNote_body:
$J0L0 The water here used to smell like diesel. There were so many ships. God, I hated that smell, and I know why.
It was my first day on the towboat when the XO found out I couldn’t swim after all. Well, what was I supposed to do? I’d never have gotten the job otherwise, so I had to lie. Anyway, he tossed me overboard, just like that. I kicked and thrashed and sputtered desperately, begging him for a lifeline, but he just stood there watching me. “You can lie your way onto a boat,” he told me. “But now it’s just you and the water. Let’s see how you lie your way out of this one.”
Now I look down and all I see is a dry riverbed. No water. Just me.
sid_notes_ANCQ21_WaterNote_hint: Diary from the destroyed Preobrazhensky Bridge
sid_notes_ANCQ22_ClearSkyDiary_body:
$J0L0 We’re closing in on the objective of our expedition. The professor insists on proceeding slowly and cautiously… which is funny, considering that we’ve entrusted our lives to the first scumbag we met – some stalker who goes by “Scar.”
But I have to admit, I find him a lot more reliable than our professor, who’s been getting more and more erratic every day. He keeps repeating how absolutely sure he is that there won’t be any Emissions. Like we need any reassurance from him! I just hope he doesn’t drop his gun if we get attacked.
sid_notes_ANCQ23_AtasNote_body:
$J0L0Kolya Atas: If he changes the spot one more time, I’ll blow his fucking head off.
Seva Squirm: Hold on, guys.
Seva Squirm: Huh?
Seva Squirm: New coordinates. That Ward asshole just sent them.
Kolya Atas: Fuck!
sid_notes_ANCQ23_AtasNote_hint: Taken from a bandit killed at the Hydroelectric Station near Icarus
sid_notes_ANCQ24_VenyaNotesText_body:
$J0L0 It’s done! You finished the job, and I’m grateful for that. I don’t think you’re ever going to read this, but I’m just writing it to get some closure. Although… we both knew you’d come find me one day. Am I right?
I’ve made my decision. I see no point in staying here. I mean, I did see a point – this whole business was the only point, the only incentive for me to go on. I have to write this down because I’m scared. It’s a terrifying thing, facing the end of your life. I always thought I could never muster up the nerve to… you know, pull the trigger.
sid_notes_ANCQ24_VenyaNotesText_hint: A letter found in a Prypiat apartment
sid_notes_ANCQ_19_BanditDiary_body:
$J0L011th. My second week among the Brood. The Shah’s not as nuts as folks make him out to be. Sure, he flies off the handle sometimes, but that’s par for the course for a big shot.$J0L019th. There was a rumor this morning that Goon stashed some loot from the gang. That same night, the Shah had rounded us up at the Poisonous Hole. He grabbed Goon and dragged him to the vent. “Live like a rat, die like a rat,” he said, and shoved him in.
The anomaly turned Goon inside out, and the boss forced us all to look at the mess. Holy shit.

20th. I’m fucked. Found a gun in my stuff this morning. Looks like some scared shithead dumped it there after last night’s execution. I don’t know what to do. If I bail, they’ll think I snitched and find me. Gotta figure out how to get rid of it. Toss it in the swamp? Slip it into the group stash and play dumb? Fuck, fuck, fuck.$J0L023th Three nights straight, I get nightmares about the Shah. He takes the gun from my stash and shoots me in the chest. I fall to the ground, then I end up in the anomaly. It tears me apart while the Shah cackles. The rest of the guys are fed up with my screams waking ’em up at night. They’re eyeing me, I can feel it. Are they onto me?$J0L0Can’t sleep anymore. Yesterday, I buried the gun near the camp. And now they’re saying the Shah wants a word with me. Do they know? No, no, they ain’t gonna get me. I’m not going down like Goon. I’d rather do it myself.
sid_notes_ANCQ_19_BanditDiary_hint: A bandit’s diary found in the Brood barracks
sid_notes_ANC_18_IOUNote_body: $J0L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault> I, Myshko Seagull, for the losses caused by my careless actions, hereby agree to compensate the group with: </NoteDefault></NoteBody>$J0L1<NoteBody><ListItem><NoteDefault>One Rat King artifact</NoteDefault></ListItem><ListItem><NoteDefault>One flashlight</NoteDefault></ListItem><ListItem><NoteDefault>One Veles detector</NoteDefault></ListItem><ListItem><NoteDefault>One work jacket and its contents</NoteDefault></ListItem><ListItem><NoteDefault>Coupons for the pain and suffering endured by the group that had to rescue my ass.</NoteDefault></ListItem></NoteBody>$J0L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault> I pledge to refrain from any thoughtless behavior that not only prevents the group from finding new artifacts, but also results in the loss of previously acquired ones. </NoteDefault></NoteBody>
sid_notes_BorodulinPDANote_body:
$J0L03/20 [20:55]
Roosevelt: I’ve got just the job for you, Colonel. I need to whip my new recruits into shape. Smart kids, but shitty shooters.
Borodulin: Training your rabble up to Duty standards? Not my cup of tea.
Roosevelt: Tea? More like vodka, huh?
Borodulin: Fuck off. I’m laying off the booze.
Roosevelt: We’ll see about that… $J0L03/26 [15:47]
Roosevelt: It’s a date, then? I’ve got an interesting piece to show you, by the way. Zulu brought it over from our old stash. Swing by the shooting range with my guys and check it out.
Borodulin: So Zulu’s riding high, huh? Don’t forget to remind him about that Rostok delivery. He’s only getting cozy at the Cement Factory because I chose not to spill the beans to the Ward or Voronin about what went down with his Freedom guards. You can shove your “interesting piece” where Zulu won’t enjoy it. Along with your goons. $J0L04/02 [17:20]
