Borodulin: Sorry, got carried away for a moment there. All right, I’ll show your boys a couple of Duty moves.
Roosevelt: Deal. You’ve got the shooting range tomorrow. Just don’t leave a bunch of empty bottles there. We’ve got plenty of targets already.
Borodulin: I told you, I’m laying off the booze. $J0L04/04 [09:32]
Roosevelt: Borodulin, you bald sack of shit! What the hell did you do at the shooting range yesterday?! My medic had to yank bullets and glass out of my boys till his pliers snapped!
Borodulin: I told you, those clowns can’t hit the broad side of a barn.
Roosevelt: Clowns? You were sloshed! That’s it – no more allowance for you. You’ll spend it all on booze anyway.
Borodulin: Fuck you! Think you’re the next Voronin, huh? Still pretending to be a big shot in Duty? You’re nobody to him!
sid_notes_BorodulinPDANote_hint: Chat with Roosevelt, the Commissary kingpin
sid_notes_Cemzavod_Note_Eger_Alensky_bod: $J0L0<NoteBody><NoteBold>Jager: </NoteBold><NoteDefault>What's up with the newbies? The whole base is gossiping.</NoteDefault></NoteBody>$J0L0<NoteBody><NoteBold>Alensky:</NoteBold><NoteDefault> I couldn't save them.</NoteDefault></NoteBody>$J0L0<NoteBody><NoteBold>Jager: </NoteBold><NoteDefault>Shit! They went to take that bastard down, like real Duty. Back in the day, they would've gotten medals, postmortem, but still, and now they had to do it all hush-hush. Nowadays, Duty only awards cowardly dipshits who sit around with their traps shut.</NoteDefault></NoteBody>$J0L0<NoteBody><NoteBold>Alensky: </NoteBold><NoteDefault>It is a real shame it was just the two of them. If there were more, they could've stood a chance.</NoteDefault></NoteBody>$J0L0<NoteBody><NoteBold>Jager:</NoteBold><NoteDefault> And where do you think you can find a whole team of brave folks in this dump? I wish I'd known those guys were about to go there...</NoteDefault></NoteBody>
sid_notes_Cemzavod_Note_VoroninDecree_bo: $J0L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault>Having evaluated the situation with the special-class bloodsucker in the Cement Factory underground, I hereby order: isolate the location of the mutant, cut its food supply routes. Permission to eliminate is not granted. Utilizing the resource of experienced soldiers to take out a bloodsucker is irrational, if there is a possibility to eliminate it without risk. Failure to comply with this order, as well as vigilantism, will be reprimanded accordingly.</NoteDefault></NoteBody>
sid_notes_Cemzavod_Note_Zulus_body: $J0L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault>If you're going stir crazy sitting on your ass at the barracks and embarrassing Duty, we have something in common. I'm putting together a team of real fighters, who are ready to go to war against the Zone, because that's why we joined Duty.</NoteDefault></NoteBody>
sid_notes_Cemzavod_Note_Zulus_hint: Found at the bar at the Duty base
sid_notes_DLC00_KorshunovVartaNote_body: $J0L0<NoteBody><NoteItalic>Korshunov:</NoteItalic><NoteDefault> Stay where you are, journalist. Don't do anything foolish, again. You have no idea what you are about to unleash.</NoteDefault></NoteBody>$J0L0<NoteBody><NoteItalic>Korshunov:</NoteItalic><NoteDefault> If the information about the other Generators gets out, it will be a catastrophe. Worldwide riots and wars for Noosphere access would be just the beginning.</NoteDefault></NoteBody>$J0L0<NoteBody><NoteItalic>Korshunov:</NoteItalic><NoteDefault> We cannot let that happen. So I strongly advise you to change your mind, I'll ask nicely this time.</NoteDefault></NoteBody>$J0L0<NoteBody><NoteItalic>Korshunov: </NoteItalic><NoteDefault>I promise you, we will forget about all the damage you have done to the Institute. But this is your last chance to make it right, Veniamin. </NoteDefault></NoteBody>
sid_notes_DLC00_KorshunovVartaNote_hint: The last inbox messages on the Journalist's PDA
sid_notes_DLC00_ScarSparkNote_body:
$J0L0Scar: Venia, Venia. Why do you have to run so fast?$J0L0Scar: I only had your best interest at heart... Have you forgotten who helped you get away from SIRCAA? $J0L0Scar: Tell me, journalist, why do you want to tell people this "truth" of yours so badly? $J0L0Scar: Why does it matter how many other Generators are hidden on the Mainland? Why do you care what the Regulatory Board will do with them?$J0L0Scar: We will be the first ones, Venia. The Shining Zone will begin here, and that is the only thing that matters.$J0L0Scar: Or is the truth more important to you than the happiness of all humanity?$J0L0
$J0L0
$J0L0
$J0L0
$J0L0
sid_notes_DLC00_ScarSparkNote_hint: The last inbox messages on the Journalist's PDA
sid_notes_DLC00_StrelokNote_body:
$J0L0Strelok: I've always liked you, Veniamin. I don't think you can comprehend what you are about to set in motion.$J0L0Strelok: If the public finds out about the other Generators, about what they are capable of, it will lead to a world war. $J0L0Strelok: Governments, international corporations - all of them will try to grab the Noosphere for themselves, with their dirty hands. There will be even more of those who want to take advantage of the Zone.

Strelok: I will not let that happen. She must be free. $J0L0Strelok: So, if you don't change your mind... You are pretty good at hiding, journalist, but this is my territory.$J0L0Strelok: I already know how to find you.
sid_notes_DLC00_StrelokNote_hint: The last inbox messages on the Journalist's PDA
sid_notes_DevatyjPDANote_body: $J0L0<NoteBody><NoteBold>Sickle:</NoteBold><NoteDefault> Damn... Ninth. Monolith bless you. We had to leave quickly. You've come such a long way... </NoteDefault></NoteBody>$J0L0<NoteBody><NoteBold>Nine:</NoteBold><NoteDefault> And I'll do it again. To return. </NoteDefault></NoteBody>$J0L0<NoteBody><NoteBold>Sickle:</NoteBold><NoteDefault> For what? Don't be stupid. Wherever you hide, they'll find you sooner or later. Everything's changed. </NoteDefault></NoteBody>$J0L0<NoteBody><NoteBold>Sickle:</NoteBold><NoteDefault> Now you either go the way of lies and self-pity, live as a stalker, among strangers. Or you come to us, brother. </NoteDefault></NoteBody>$J0L0<NoteBody><NoteBold>Nine: </NoteBold><NoteDefault>Then tell me: What do I do? </NoteDefault></NoteBody>$J0L0<NoteBody><NoteBold>Sickle:</NoteBold><NoteDefault> Put on our old armor. The gifts of the Monolith will help you pass where no one else can. Trust me. I will send a route. </NoteDefault></NoteBody>$J0L0<NoteBody><NoteBold>Sickle:</NoteBold><NoteDefault> May the Monolith light your way. </NoteDefault></NoteBody>
sid_notes_E01_GermanNote_body: $J0L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault> Step-by-step to get the artifact: </NoteDefault></NoteBody>$J0L2<NoteBody><ListItem><NoteDefault>Take the Scanner off your shoulder.</NoteDefault></ListItem><ListItem><NoteDefault>Place the Scanner on the ground.</NoteDefault></ListItem><ListItem><NoteDefault>Activate the Scanner.</NoteDefault></ListItem><ListItem><NoteDefault>Step back.</NoteDefault></ListItem><ListItem><NoteStrike>Yes, these instructions are for morons.</NoteStrike></ListItem></NoteBody>
sid_notes_E01_MQ01_NedogaNote_body:
$J0L0Solder: Got it?
Dr. Nehoda: Yes. It’s all on my laptop. How long will these coordinates be up to date?
Solder: Until the next Emission. So that guy of yours should move his ass.
Dr. Nehoda: There’s someone at the bunker. That must be the man Hermann sent…
sid_notes_E01_MQ01_NegodaNote_body:
$J0L0Solder: Got it?
Dr. Nehoda: Yes. It’s all on my laptop. How long will these coordinates be up to date?
Solder: Until the next Emission. So that guy of yours should move his ass.
Dr. Nehoda: There’s someone at the bunker. That must be the man Hermann sent…
sid_notes_E01_MQ02_Memo_to_Voloshin_body: $J0L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault> Voloshyn! As project leader, I’m ordering you to remain in the bunker until we receive the suits from the Institute. Safety comes first, regardless of any psi radiation coefficients you might have detected. If you venture into that cave one more time, I will kick you out personally – first out of the cave, then out of this project. </NoteDefault></NoteBody>
sid_notes_E01_MQ02_Memo_to_Voloshin_hint: A note from the mobile lab
sid_notes_E02_MQ01_ScanerNote_body:
$J0L0Activation date and time: DD/TT
Event log:
[00:00:001]: Initiating procedure protocol.
[00:01:247]: Background anomalous radiation detected. Saturation: 0.47 ef.
[00:03:589]: Forming transition channel.
[00:04:761]: Initiating scan procedure.
[00:05:115]: Phase transition.
[00:06:247]: Saturation: 0.99 ca.
[00:07:081]: Object reactivated.
[00:07:821]: Approbating using the Knyazhinsky formula.
[00:07:921]: Detecting auto-emission in the upper tau range.
[00:08:001]: Checking for sync phase.
[00:09:111]: Kaymanov’s sync phase detected.
[00:10:001]: Procedure complete.$J0L0WARNING! Kaymanov’s sync phase detected! Operator, follow special protocol PS-07! Make sure the procedure is complete! Do not try to extract the container on site! The device must be safely transported to the ARL in the Special Field Research Unit.
sid_notes_E02_MQ02_BS_Note_body: $J0L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault> Hey, Elbow – quick update. We had some sketchy stalker snooping around. Sent him packing back to the farmstead. Don’t sweat it: the loot’s gonna be top-notch. </NoteDefault></NoteBody>
sid_notes_E02_MQ02_BS_Note_hint: From a PDA found at the radiation hazard signs warehouse
sid_notes_E02_MQ03_MSOP_Note_body:
$J0L0Sgt. Petrushenko: All set. The tunnel codes have been updated: it’s 2765 now.
Base: Is everything sorted at the Ribs?
Sgt. Petrushenko: Affirmative, sir. All squared away.
Base: Copy that. Now make your way back, and keep this hush-hush.
Sgt. Petrushenko: My lips are sealed, sir.
sid_notes_E02_MQ03_MSOP_Note_hint: Chat from the dead IPSF soldier’s PDA found at the Ribs
sid_notes_E02_MQ03_Note_for_Sidor_body: $J0L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault> This is the second and last shipment without advance payment. I didn’t screw Hamster over just to have you bullshit me. Either cough up what you owe, or I’ll close up shop and tip the Gaffer off. </NoteDefault></NoteBody>
sid_notes_E02_MQ03_PripoyNote_body: $J0L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault> We’ve got an emergency here, Nestor. The Ward knows about the scanners. </NoteDefault></NoteBody>
sid_notes_E02_NoteAbotKosoy_body: $J0L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault> Hey Elbow, here's a quick update: we had some sketchy stalker snooping around. We sent him packing toward the farmstead. Don’t sweat it, the loot’s gonna be top-notch. </NoteDefault></NoteBody>
sid_notes_E02_NoteAbotKosoy_hint: Found at the radiation hazard signs warehouse
sid_notes_E02_SQ01_ZorikNote_body: $J0L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault> Gloomy, in case I don’t make it, I stowed the goods in the chopper. Here are the coordinates. </NoteDefault></NoteBody>
sid_notes_E02_SQ01_ZorikNote_hint: Found on the PDA of a stalker from the Post Office
sid_notes_E02_SQ01_banditPDA_Note_body:
$J0L0Silencer: Varan, we tracked down those assholes. One made a run for the Post Office, Valve’s got the other. Should we off ’em both?
Varan: First, find out where the money is, then you can do them in. Not like last time.
sid_notes_E02_SQ01_banditPDA_Note_hint: Found on a dead bandit’s body
sid_notes_E03_MQ01_ChernozemNote_body:
$J0L0Diode: Need some help here, Chornozem. I had a close call today. Think it was some thugs. Any leads on who might be gunning for me?
Chornozem: I could use a hand myself. The Ward’s making moves – the place is swarming with those clowns. Doubt it’s all for show.
Chornozem: Why isn’t Nestor handling your shit? You’re his pet tech, after all.
Diode: The damn scanners, that’s why. You’re lucky you’re not in this mess with us.
sid_notes_E03_MQ01_GoshaMogilaNote_body:
$J0L0Hosha Mohyla: I saw you packing up today. Getting ready to bail, huh?
Vetos Balaban: You’re sharper than you look. But not sharp enough.
Hosha Mohyla: Heard Chornozem sent Cucumber to chat with the Ward. Maybe they’ll sort things out.
Vetos Balaban: I don’t give a shit about Chornozem’s games. Pigs are pigs. They’ll screw you over, put a bullet in your skull, and dump you in the nearest burner.
Vetos Balaban: You think they just let Cucumber go, and now he’s sipping cocktails on the Mainland? He’s six feet under already! And I ain’t planning to join him for those shitheads.
Hosha Mohyla: Bolting on your guys is fucked up. The boys ain’t gonna like it. But take me with you, and I’ll keep my mouth shut.
Vetos Balaban: Fuck. Guess I got no choice!
Vetos Balaban: But if we end up in a jam because of you, you’re as good as dead.
sid_notes_E03_MQ01_GoshaMogilaNote_hint: From the PDA of a bandit who tried to escape from the Depot
sid_notes_E03_MQ01_GoshaMogilaNote_name: Vetos Balaban’s Chat with Hosha Mohyla
sid_notes_E03_MQ02_DeviceNote_hint: A document salvaged from lab Х18
sid_notes_E03_MQ02_IskraPDANote_body: $J0L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault> Keep an eye out for the device specs, guys – they’re in a regular file folder. And watch your step in there. Who knows what might have creeped in from the lab to the warehouse. </NoteDefault></NoteBody>
sid_notes_E03_MQ02_IskraPDANote_name: Last Message from a Sparker’s PDA
sid_notes_E03_MQ02_ZmeevikPDANote_body: $J0L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault> You need to get to the lab, Serpentine. Nestor stashed loads of gear there, but the device blueprints are top priority. Look for an entrance on the lower levels. I’ll send you the passwords so you won’t have to crack anything. And be quick about it. The Ward’s been scouring Garbage ever since Nestor went missing, so watch your back. </NoteDefault></NoteBody>
sid_notes_E03_MQ02_ZmeevikPDANote_name: Last Message to Serpentine from Unknown
sid_notes_E03_MQ03_Note1_body:
$J0L0[10:00] Roosevelt: Mind checking out an old stash for me?
[10:00] Roosevelt: I sent you the coordinates.
[10:01] Roosevelt: Got a bad feeling someone might’ve cleaned it out.
[10:05] Zulu: Sure thing, I can do it for you.
[18:00] Zulu: All good, even that vodka Borodulin left for you.
[18:01] Zulu: How’s he holding up, by the way? Think he might come back to your side?
[18:15] Roosevelt: Even if he’s having second thoughts, it’s too late. You know how many chances I gave him? There’s only so much patience a man can have.
[18:16] Roosevelt: If he wants to get drunk as a skunk every day, that’s his choice. I ain’t his mamma.
sid_notes_E03_MQ03_Note1_hint: From a PDA belonging to Roosevelt, the Commissary kingpin
sid_notes_E03_MQ03_Note2_body:
$J0L0Anonymous: You’ve been holed up in your bar for too long. Crack a window open and get some fresh air. Can you smell it?
That’s the wind of change, Shah.
The Bazaar’s on the brink of a new era. More cash rolling in, more players at the table, and less chatter about that “free trade zone” crap.
But if we want to make it happen, Myklukha has to go.
Do you agree?
Check the stash. There’s bound to be something that’ll put an end to our problem.
sid_notes_E03_MQ03_Note2_hint: From a PDA belonging to the Shah, the Brood kingpin
sid_notes_E03_MQ04_VaranPDANote_body:
$J0L0Varan: What the fuck, Nestor?!
Nestor: What’s wrong?
Varan: We gave your fucking scanner a ride yesterday, and some assholes jumped my boys!
Nestor: Who?
Varan: Loner gear, but it looked off. Iron thinks they’re not from around here. We invited one of them over for a chat.
Nestor: I have no idea, Varan.
Varan: Nestor, you son of a bitch! My boys had a look at your scanner. Are you trying to screw me over?! It’s rigged! What, did you want those hitmen to raid my Plant?!
Varan: You must be real busy. Whatever. We’ll sort this out face-to-face soon enough.
sid_notes_E03_MQ04_VaranPDANote_hint: From the brazen kingpin Varan’s PDA
sid_notes_E03_MQ05_NestorNotes_body:
$J0L0 You might be an egghead, Hermann, but your plan sucks. Let’s do this the smart way, okay?

1. You throw the scanners away with the SIRCAA trash. Make sure nobody sees you.
2. Diggers get them and bring them to the Labyrinth for radiation cleanup. I’ll get artifacts to absorb the radiation. Swiping a few from loners ain’t a problem.
3. Once they’re clean, I’ll send Diode to tinker with them just like we talked about.
4. Then we scatter them around the Dig Site, and I’ll send the coordinates to our buyers. Just keep your freaking head down, you got it?

You better. You’re a smart one.
sid_notes_E03_MQ05_NestorNotes_hint: A draft message to Hermann found among Nestor’s papers
sid_notes_E03_MQ05_PahanOrder_body: $J0L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault> The info is solid. Go to the mark. Tomorrow, I’ll be waiting at the Factory for the results. </NoteDefault></NoteBody>
sid_notes_E03_MQ05_PahanOrder_hint: PDA from a bandit who needed a Scanner, apparently
sid_notes_E04_Note_DubnyjToBrodyaga_body:
$J0L0 Look, I know what’s on your mind, and I swore not to speak of it again. But this is a note, so I’m not breaking my fuckin’ word, okay?
Faust is really pushing it. He went off today about how our resistance to Emissions and toxic fumes has diminished because the Monolith is gone. And folks are eating it up.
Some of them are starting to fall for it, Strider. I’ve seen it in their eyes. Even if it’s not total bullshit, we gotta do something about this!
sid_notes_E04_Note_KillingMachine_body:
$J0L0Zamir Thunder: So, what do you think?
Shell: We’re all in agreement that stalking is a dangerous business.
Zamir Thunder: I thought you weren’t afraid to remember your past.
Shell: What’s that got to do with anything? You just need some extra firepower.
Zamir Thunder: Exactly! You’re going to do what you’re good at. And that means awakening your muscle memory! The rest will come back over time.
Shell: Are you serious?
Zamir Thunder: Absolutely! That’s what the scientists are saying.
Shell: All right. I’ll give it some thought.
sid_notes_E04_Note_KillingMachine_hint: Chat from a missing PDA found at the Noontide Base
sid_notes_E04_Note_Legends_body:
$J0L0Tall: Can you believe it? They’ve got canned Flesh over on the Mainland!
Comet: How do you know?
Tall: Found one in a warehouse. The label says “Pork,” but there’s a Flesh on it! Just a tad pinker and with ears.
Comet: I always thought the Mainland was nothing like the Zone. Turns out, all they’ve got on us is Fleshes with ears.
sid_notes_E04_Note_RememberUs_body:
$J0L0Vector: Think any of those stalkers remember us from way back? Someone who knows who we are and where we came from?
Six-Fingers: If there’s any loner who might have known us, he’s conveniently forgotten it.
Six-Fingers: Don’t worry about it. Strider’s already told us everything we need to know. Unlike those stalkers, he’s actually looking out for us.
sid_notes_E04_Note_RememberUs_hint: Found on Vector’s PDA on Wild Island
sid_notes_E04_Note_Unity_body:
$J0L0 That was a special moment. I want to hold on to it. The guys and I were chatting by the fire when silence suddenly fell upon us, and we all just watched the flames dance. And yet it felt like our conversation carried on, and we understood each other without saying a word. There was a strange familiarity to it. A sense of unity…
The brothers say they experience the same feeling during their gatherings with Faust. Maybe I should join them…
sid_notes_E04_Note_Unity_hint: A page from a diary found on Wild Island
sid_notes_E04_SQ01_PDABanditNote_body:
$J0L0Karim Cyclops: Roosevelt wants Mute and Sava taken care of. But all I see is Sava and a couple of shady guys in Noontide gear.
Sprain: So what? Let’s take them out anyways.
Karim Cyclops: Some stalkers recognized Mute and Sava from Prypiat. They left a trail of dead loners there. Why bother with the rest?
Sprain: All the damn Monolithians slaughtered folks left and right. We’ll just tell the client to fork over the dough for the other two. Then we’ll hit the Commissary and celebrate.
sid_notes_E04_SQ01_PDABanditNote_hint: A chat between two bandits behind a hit on Noontiders
sid_notes_E05_MQ01_EchoReportNote_body:
$J0L2Psi-radiation hotspots with significant fluctuations in range have been detected across the Zaton region.
Source: unknown.
Research priority: high.Psi-radiation hotspots with consistent exposure levels identified in the Swamps.
Source: presumably the equipment that used to protect the Clear Sky base. Access to faction archives required for further investigation.
Research priority: low.Intense surge in psi activity detected near the long-abandoned Clear Sky base.
Source: likely a malfunction of antiquated scientific equipment.
Research priority: very low – remote areas are of minimal interest to the expedition.
sid_notes_E05_MQ01_EchoReportNote_hint: Found at the scene of the scientist killings at Echo Station
sid_notes_E05_MQ01_Shterev_Diary_body:
$J0L0 Ha! I’ve never come across a Zone “miracle” quite like this one. How on earth did he manage to survive if he’s blind? He keeps going on about how there’s always someone ready to lend a hand. Where’d he come across anyone that stupid around here? That voice of his, though…

That’s it. I’m taking the next patrol bound for the Claw. It’s time to put an end to these endless debates in my mind and confront him face to face. Sure, he knows the Zone like no other. Sometimes, I can’t help but feel he’s not the one who’s been blind. $J0L0 It’s time for the Ward to wake up to the truth: we’ve been protecting the deceivers who dread his true power. But I know how to make them see the light. I’ll do what he’s taught me, and then they’ll listen. They’ll come to believe in him, just like I have. $J0L0 Just as he anticipated: SIRCAA doesn’t need truth – only obedient soldiers. One of their agents showed up at Icarus the moment I began my mission. Faust believes this presents an opportunity nonetheless. We can always leverage others to further our own agenda, but first, we need to determine exactly what they intend to do at Icarus and Echo Station.
sid_notes_E05_MQ01_Shterev_Diary_hint: Found on Shterev’s body, in his breast pocket
sid_notes_E05_MQ02_KalanchaMap_body: $J0L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault> A map of the Swamps. Received from Nimble. </NoteDefault></NoteBody>
sid_notes_E05_MQ02_KalanchaMap_hint: A map of beacons in the Swamps, provided by Nimble
sid_notes_E05_MQ02_ZveroboyTask_body:
$J0L0 Here’s the deal, Trapper. The mutants in Zaton are chomping through my boys like candy. You handle them, and I’ll make it worth your while.
You won’t be flying solo on this one – you’ll be teamed up with Nimble, a seasoned loner who knows his way around. Check every spot and take out every critter. There’s a cave swarming with snorks – Nimble’s got that covered, and he’ll blast it to kingdom come. Meanwhile, you handle that hairy bastard I told you about.
If Nimble pisses off or bites it in that cave, you’re on your own.
sid_notes_E05_MQ03_KalanchaCommsSessionN:
$J0L0Message from 9/03/2011
V. D.: In response to your earlier inquiry: As of September 2011, the anomalous energy emanating from the Noosphere remains unstable. The so-called Emissions are a consequence of these ongoing fluctuations. Continue monitoring and collecting data.
Kalancha: Understood. We’ll continue our observations. $J0L0 Message from 9/10/2011
V. D.: To ensure the successful execution of the Cutter Initiative, you are required to accept Agent A:MS-017 into the group and continuously monitor his activity. Should any instability arise in his neuroprogramming, the agent must be compelled to seek out and terminate stalker A:LS-013.
Lebedev: Acknowledged. Initiating monitoring.
PDA found at the Clear Sky base
sid_notes_E05_MQ03_LebedevOrder_body: $J0L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault>MDST is done. The Ancile scientist protection project has been compromised. I have one last job for you: find any information on two stalkers who go by Fang and Ghost while Scar is looking for Strelok. If you find them, eliminate them. Good luck. </NoteDefault></NoteBody>
sid_notes_E05_MQ03_NimbleDiaryNote_body: $J0L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault> I got a little present for you. Ran into this stalker named Scar right after the Emission. Don’t ask me how, but the guy made it through. Lebedev’s all hyped about him, says we gotta rescue him at all costs. Keep an eye on him, would you? It’d be a shame if he bites the dust now. I’ll see if I can rustle up some meds for you. </NoteDefault></NoteBody>
sid_notes_E05_MQ03_NimbleDiaryNote_hint: Found in the ruins of the Clear Sky base
sid_notes_E05_MQ03_NimbleNote_body:
$J0L0Nimble: Nope, I’m out. Had my fill of this. Killing fellow stalkers wasn’t in the job description.
Sickle: But you did not kill him. And now he has lots of questions. Some of them involve you.
Nimble: All the more reason to get the hell outta the Swamps. I’ll leave you the map of Kalancha’s beacons. Tune them to yourself, starting with the first, and they’ll guide you to Clear Sky’s doorstep. Or should I say, your thugs. You managed without maps just fine.
Sickle: The Monolith guides me. Also, there is no need to insult my brothers.
Nimble: Are you reading your own lines for once, Sickle? Or is he pulling the strings like always? Eh, whatevs. You’ve got to recalibrate at least four beacons for this to pan out. Then you can waltz through the ruins all the way to Clear Sky.
Sickle: You said you would guide my men there.
Nimble: And you said I wouldn’t have any problems, Faust.
Sickle: All of your problems are of your own making.
Nimble: I’ve heard enough. You’ve got the coordinates. Let’s hope we never cross paths again.
Sickle: Of that I am certain.
sid_notes_E05_SQ01_VartaPatrolNote_body:
$J0L0 1. Icarus – Izumrudne – Skadovsk – Canning Factory – Icarus.
2. Icarus – Canning Factory – Maintenance Facility – Icarus.
3. Icarus – Burnt Farmstead – Gas Station – Canning Factory – Icarus.
sid_notes_E05_SQ01_VartaPatrolNote_hint: Found among the belongings of runaway Wardens
sid_notes_E05_SQ03_NoteAboutX11Equipment:
$J0L0 Pursuant to the directive to modernize the monorail station, I hereby issue the following orders:
– organize a detail to accompany the scientists transporting the first shipment to the station
– set up a hangar to accommodate the mobile field laboratory
– implement measures to ensure the safety and security of the expedition
$J2L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault> Sgt. Shavruk has been briefed on the mission objectives </NoteDefault></NoteBody>
From a Warden’s body found in the depot near Icarus
sid_notes_E05_SQ04_ArturLapaPDANote_body:
$J0L0Sultan: I got that Warden in line, Paw. He’s moving the goods today.
Sultan: Pick up my stuff from that quartermaster guy. Keep it quiet.
Sultan: And keep your head down.
Artur Paw: Will do.
sid_notes_E05_SQ04_ArturLapaPDANote_hint: PDA from a dead customer’s body beneath Icarus
sid_notes_E05_SQ04_ArturLapaPDANote_name: Order from the Kingpin for Artur Paw
sid_notes_E06_MQ01_AgathaNote_body: $J0L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault> You’re on the verge of surpassing your father’s accomplishments. </NoteDefault></NoteBody>
sid_notes_E06_MQ01_DalinWindow_Note_body: $J0L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault> With the latest breakthroughs in Emission protection tech, this window now offers a clear view of your aspirations and humanity’s future. </NoteDefault></NoteBody>$J0L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault> Happy birthday, Dr. Dalin! </NoteDefault></NoteBody>
sid_notes_E06_MQ01_DalinWindow_Note_hint: Written on the window of Dalin’s suite
sid_notes_E06_MQ01_ExperimentRoom_Note_b:
$J0L0Dr. Leshkiv: If the intensity of radiation is kept at the same level, there will be irreversible changes.
Dr. Kryvenko: All the changes so far remain within acceptable parameters.
Dr. Leshkiv: Does Dalin know about all this?
Dr. Kryvenko: Believe me, all interested parties are in the loop. I never expected you of all people to be concerned about the ethical side of the matter.
Dr. Leshkiv: I’m concerned about the “my career” side of the matter. I had to abandon my own research for this. And now I’m facing the risk of being left with nothing.
Dr. Kryvenko: All our sacrifices will bear fruit when fifth-level protection is created. Keep up the good work.
sid_notes_E06_MQ01_SciMagazine_body: $J0L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault> “I know this might not sit well with everyone, but I do not see 2006 as a tragedy. It was a blessing for humanity! The Zone presents endless opportunities that cannot be confined behind walls. As Director of SIRCAA, I am committed to ensuring that in the coming years…” </NoteDefault></NoteBody>
sid_notes_E06_MQ01_SciMagazine_header: $J0L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault> Dmytro Dalin, the young scientist now at the helm of SIRCAA, shares his thoughts on his appointment: </NoteDefault></NoteBody>
sid_notes_E06_MQ01_SciMagazine_hint: A clipping from an article in a prominent scientific journal.
sid_notes_E06_MQ01_VartaOrder_body: $J0L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault>I hereby order: </NoteDefault></NoteBody>$J0L2<NoteBody><ListItem><NoteDefault>The following detachments (list provided in Annex А) to report to SIRCAA immediately.</NoteDefault></ListItem><ListItem><NoteDefault>Increase institute security comprehensively, enforce enhanced safety procedures (the Warden program).</NoteDefault></ListItem><ListItem><NoteDefault>Ensure evacuation of scientists (list of names provided in Annex B) from the institute.</NoteDefault></ListItem><ListItem><NoteDefault>Secure SIRCAA and ensure safety of the personnel left on the premises (list of names provided in Annex B) until further instructions.</NoteDefault></ListItem></NoteBody>
sid_notes_E06_MQ01_Zombie_Note_body:
$J0L0Observation time: 74 days
Ratio: 000.98
Conclusion: No potential. Should be disposed of.
sid_notes_E06_MQ01_Zombie_Note_hint: Taken from a zombie at the Cryptobiology Lab
sid_notes_E07_MQ01_HorsNotes_body:
$J0L0Khors: The valves are all set. What about those fuses? I just need to replace them, and we’ll be good to go.
Quiet: Still waiting on those. Should be here tomorrow. How’s my gun, by the way?
Khors: Almost done. I gotta finish one for Skif first.
Quiet: Hey, we had a deal! I’ll stash everything in the usual place. Swing by when you’re done with my stuff. Just leave the gun and take the fuses. Fair and square.
sid_notes_E07_MQ01_HorsNotes_hint: A note about vent repair parts
sid_notes_E07_MQ01_HorsPlan_body:
$J0L0The brothers have been complaining about the ventilation. It’s struggling under the strain. Stink from the waste is seeping into the living quarters.

Shelter ventilation system repair plan:
1. Identify cause of malfunction and rectify it (Valves are worn out. Tried swapping them out. Didn’t work.)
2. Insufficient power output from generator. Fuses? (Checked. Found a few blown.)
3. Swap out the fuse.
sid_notes_E07_MQ01_SkiperNote_body:
$J0L0 Client: Wild Island, Skipper. It’s time. Get moving. Then hand the goods over to Degtyarev. I’ll send the coordinates.
Skipper: Wasn’t planning on risking my neck. How about a bonus?
Client: It’ll be a walk in the park. The Monolith have no stake in the island. They’ll move on.
Skipper: I’ve heard different.
Client: And I’ve got history. I dealt with the Monolith long before Noontide. I know their ways.
sid_notes_E07_MQ01_SkiperNote_hint: A chat about the Wild Island job
sid_notes_E07_MQ01_TihiyNote_body:
$J0L0Sidorovich
1. Courier for ongoing deliveries from Rostok (found, discussed price, reached an agreement).
2. Batch of acid artifacts (all packed and ready for transportation. Once the shipment gets there, Sidorovich will hand over some interesting guns).
3. Got an order for something science-y. Still exploring my options (The price tag is pretty sweet!). $J0L0Kalyna
She sent me some sardines. A whole can. How’d she know? Did Sidorovich spill the beans? I should definitely catch up with her. Feels like she’s got more than just canned fish to offer.
sid_notes_E07_MQ02_SyhinBody_Note_body: $J0L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault> The hour has come for all your questions to be answered, brother. Have faith. You will become what you have always been: a man among his own kind. </NoteDefault></NoteBody>
sid_notes_E07_MQ02_SyhinOffice_Note_body: $J0L2<NoteBody><ListItem><NoteDefault>Voluntary transfer request of First Lieutenant O. K. Sukhin to the Zone is hereby granted.</NoteDefault></ListItem><ListItem><NoteDefault>First Lieutenant O. K. Sukhin is to be promoted to the rank of Captain.</NoteDefault></ListItem><ListItem><NoteDefault>Captain O. K. Sukhin is to assume command of the Sphere garrison, paygrade to be established in accordance with respective procedures.</NoteDefault></ListItem></NoteBody>$J2L0<NoteBody><NoteBold>7/6/2018</NoteBold></NoteBody>
sid_notes_E07_MQ02_SyhinOffice_Note_foot:
$J2L0 Lieutenant Colonel G. S. Kozhedub,
Commander of the IPSF
sid_notes_E07_MQ02_SyhinOffice_Note_hint: Papers found in Cpt. Sukhin’s office
sid_notes_E07_MQ02_Syhin_Note_body:
$J1L0 MEDICAL HISTORY $J0L0 Name: Oleksiy Kyrylovych Sukhin
Age: 26
Rank: Lieutenant
Date of admission: 2/17/2014.
Diagnosis: gunshot wounds to the left forearm and thigh, shrapnel wounds to facial soft tissue, partial retrograde amnesia attributed to concussion. $J1L0 ANAMNESIS $J0L0 According to the patient, he was leading a patrol near the Perimeter walls when his squad was ambushed by Monolith forces. During combat, he sustained injuries and lost consciousness following a grenade explosion. Upon regaining consciousness, he found himself the sole survivor of the engagement. He managed to call for assistance and was subsequently evacuated.
The patient’s memory is clear only for the hours immediately preceding the incident. Memories preceding the traumatic event are fragmented.
There are no apparent cognitive impairments apart from memory loss. $J1L0 TREATMENT PLAN $J0L0 1. Surgical intervention
2. ECG, MRI of the brain
3. Consultation with neurosurgeon
4. Reconstructive surgery
sid_notes_E07_MQ02_Syhin_Note_footer: $J2L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault> Col. O. Mendel, Head of Medical Services </NoteDefault></NoteBody>
sid_notes_E07_SQ01_Note_MonolithOrders_h: Found on the M-PDA from Uncle Lyonya’s corpse
sid_notes_E07_SQ01_Note_ThanksForDadyaLe: $J0L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault> I really owe you for yanking me out of that Fruit Punch and helping me get my head on straight. I brought you some supplies from my first haul. Stay safe, and see you soon. </NoteDefault></NoteBody>
Found at Uncle Lyonya’s house near Zalissya
sid_notes_E08_EQ02_DenyaAndVenyaPDANote_:
$J0L0Denya Veteran: You hear what the fellas were saying about that mutant wearing a collar?
Venya Kalych: Yeah. Folks said it looked weird, like… twitchy or some shit.
Denya Veteran: They’re dumbasses, and so are you. Damn critter’s trained, for sure! Let’s bring it to Boozer, like a gift for the bar. Maybe he’ll let our tabs slide. Or even toss us some coupons!
Venya Kalych: Better to hand it over to the eggheads. Heard some prick’s collecting them collars. We could make a pretty penny off this one.
Denya Veteran: Dope! Tomorrow, we’ll put it down.
sid_notes_E08_EQ03_KrikPDA_Note_body:
$J0L0Viktoria Dorozhnyuk: There’s a bloodsucker in a collar running around near the Scorcher. My scientists can’t work like that! Kill it.
Yevhen Mamay: Hello, Viktoria. For jobs like these, we usually talk about payment upfront.
Viktoria Dorozhnyuk: Bring me the collar, and then we’ll talk money. End of story.
Yevhen Mamay: I can’t say no to you. Anything else you need? Shooting some blind dogs, maybe?
Yevhen Mamay: Viktoria?
sid_notes_E08_EQ03_KrikPDA_Note_name: Order to Eliminate a Mutant in the Area
sid_notes_E08_MQ01_ActivationInstruction: $J0L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault> Always verify that the emergency safety system is disengaged before activating the equipment! </NoteDefault></NoteBody>
sid_notes_E08_MQ01_ControllerList_body:
$J0L0Project title: Controller
Sections: Induced illusions, psi exposure, phantoms.
Description: classified. A-8 clearance required for viewing.
List of project participants:$J0L2I. VishnevskyA. GalitskyV. DvupalovA. YeliseevaI. KornienkoY. KravchenkoN. KurkubetL. MalyshevaA. MelnikovaD. RussS. SmetaninP. StrizhA. FomichyovM. CherikovV. ChubkoT. ShugaevaN. YusupovE. Yastrebenko
sid_notes_E08_MQ01_Desiatov_body:
$J0L0 I, Mykola Desyatov, hereby request a transfer from Prof. Razumovsky’s research team due to personal conflict and an unhealthy work environment.
Furthermore, I bring to your attention Prof. Razumovsky’s increasingly erratic reports, which often stray from the experiments’ primary objectives. His prolonged research into induced illusions may have adversely impacted his mental well-being.
sid_notes_E08_MQ01_Desiatov_hint: Request found in the Mirror VPC
sid_notes_E08_MQ01_PhantomProtocol_body: $J0L2<NoteBody><ListItem><NoteDefault>Take a few deep breaths to calm down.</NoteDefault></ListItem><ListItem><NoteDefault>Close your eyes and open them, repeat several times. This is often enough to get rid of any remaining residue from the induced illusion.</NoteDefault></ListItem><ListItem><NoteDefault>If the illusion persists, do not attempt to interact with it. Ignore it.</NoteDefault></ListItem><ListItem><NoteDefault>Ask a coworker to accompany you to the medical ward, where a qualified staff member will assist you.</NoteDefault></ListItem><ListItem><NoteDefault>Do not panic. Remember, induced illusions are a harmless side effect of excess psi-exposure. There will be no consequences to your health, as long as you get immediate medical assistance. </NoteDefault></ListItem></NoteBody>
sid_notes_E08_MQ01_PhantomProtocol_heade: $J0L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault> Protocol for Treating Induced Illusions Residue </NoteDefault></NoteBody>
sid_notes_E08_MQ01_PhantomProtocol_name: Protocol for Dealing with Induced Illusions
sid_notes_E08_MQ01_TruePhantomProtocol_b: $J0L2<NoteBody><ListItem><NoteDefault>Do not breathe too loudly.</NoteDefault></ListItem><ListItem><NoteDefault>Do not turn your back to them or take your eyes off them.</NoteDefault></ListItem><ListItem><NoteDefault>If you do what they say, they might leave.</NoteDefault></ListItem><ListItem><NoteDefault>Do not ask coworkers for help – it could be a trap.</NoteDefault></ListItem><ListItem><NoteDefault>FEAR THE ILLUSIONS. They can and will harm you. If they persist, do your best to sneak into the medical ward and get your hands on some sleeping pills…</NoteDefault></ListItem></NoteBody>
sid_notes_E08_MQ01_TruePhantomProtocol_h: $J0L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault> Real Protocol for Dealing with Induced Illusions </NoteDefault></NoteBody>
sid_notes_E08_MQ01_TruePhantomProtocol_n: Protocol for Cases in Which Meds Do Not Work
sid_notes_E08_MQ01_TuningInstructionNote: $J0L2<NoteBody><ListItem><NoteDefault>Connect the station to the power source and ensure access to the X-Network.</NoteDefault></ListItem><ListItem><NoteDefault>Clear the rails of any obstructions to facilitate movement.</NoteDefault></ListItem><ListItem><NoteDefault>Use the main console to initiate and stabilize connection.</NoteDefault></ListItem><ListItem><NoteDefault>Closely monitor movement along the rails to mitigate potential emergencies.</NoteDefault></ListItem><ListItem><NoteDefault>Once a connection has been established, save the data in the appropriate archive section based on its category.</NoteDefault></ListItem></NoteBody>
$J0L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault> Instructions for connecting to X-Network facilities </NoteDefault></NoteBody>
Instructions found at the Mirror VPC
sid_notes_E08_MQ03_CollarsTests_body:
$J0L0 The data is derived from an experiment involving blind dogs.
10 m range: specimen mortality recorded at 0.01 sec.
50 m range: specimen mortality recorded at 0.08 sec.
200 m range: specimen mortality recorded at 1.1 sec.
500 m range: specimen mortality recorded at 2.0 sec.
700 m range: specimen mortality recorded at 2.8 sec.
1 km range: specimen mortality recorded at 4.06 sec.
Findings derived from experiment with a single mutant species are considered unrepresentative.
sid_notes_E08_MQ03_CollarsTests_footer: $J2L0<NoteBody><NoteItalic>Permission is required to proceed with an extended experiment involving different specimens</NoteItalic></NoteBody>
sid_notes_E08_MQ03_CollarsTests_hint: What little has survived in the labs beneath Ahroprom
sid_notes_E08_MQ03_DocsProjectController: $J0L0<NoteBody><NoteBold>Prototype 8. </NoteBold><NoteDefault>High post-implantation mortality; survivors show no telekinetic abilities. Outbreaks of self-harm. </NoteDefault></NoteBody>$J0L0<NoteBody><NoteBold>Prototype 11. </NoteBold><NoteDefault>Deemed unsuccessful at the development phase. </NoteDefault></NoteBody>$J0L0<NoteBody><NoteBold>Prototype 17. </NoteBold><NoteDefault>Displays extreme aggression, prohibited from reproduction. Final specimens sent to reclamation lab for preservation. </NoteDefault></NoteBody>
From the depths of Lab X5 beneath Ahroprom
sid_notes_E08_MQ03_DvupalovLastWord_body: $J0L0<NoteBody><NoteBold>Day one post-implementation.</NoteBold><NoteDefault> Medical parameters remain within expected ranges. I’m certain I made the correct choice this time. </NoteDefault></NoteBody>$J0L0<NoteBody><NoteBold>Day ten.</NoteBold><NoteDefault> Subject is awake, and conditioned reflexes have returned. No apparent disorientation. Tests indicate that his brain is capable of processing human speech – a promising sign despite our inability to establish direct contact. </NoteDefault></NoteBody>$J0L0<NoteBody><NoteBold>Three months.</NoteBold><NoteDefault>  Every attempt to indirectly stimulate the subject’s cognitive processes or Controller abilities has been futile. It’s like he isn’t even here. </NoteDefault></NoteBody>$J0L0<NoteBody><NoteBold>Five (six?) months.</NoteBold><NoteDefault> Physical stimulation has also proven ineffective. The subject has not used his abilities once. I’m beginning to suspect that his brain may be damaged after all. </NoteDefault></NoteBody>$J0L0<NoteBody><NoteBold>Hell if I know how long. </NoteBold><NoteDefault> Now they’re cutting our funding! </NoteDefault></NoteBody>
sid_notes_E08_MQ03_DvupalovLastWord_hint: Miraculously preserved on the lower level of Lab X5
sid_notes_E08_MQ03_NoteWarning_body: $J0L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault> Any documents in the control room should be off limits to lab assistants. Last week, someone took the Suppressor manual yet again, jeopardizing a scheduled experiment! This is not the first case of negligent handling of crucial Project X documentation. This matter needs to be addressed at once. </NoteDefault></NoteBody>
sid_notes_E08_MQ03_NoteWarning_header: $J0L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault> To Prof. Chubko from Prof. Dvupalov </NoteDefault></NoteBody>
sid_notes_E08_MQ03_NoteWarning_hint: A note from Dvupalov’s desk at X5
sid_notes_E08_MQ03_ReceptionNote_body:
$J0L0 The new batch of samples is coming in earlier than expected, Valentyna. They’re sedated, but keep an eye out for any issues.
And send someone downstairs to inspect the central control panel.
sid_notes_E08_MQ03_ReceptionNote_hint: Left at the entrance to X5 beneath Ahroprom
sid_notes_E08_MQ03_SherbaNote_body:
$J0L0 Dear Prof. Dvupalov,

I have successfully defended four theses in biotechnology. I delivered the printouts along with the comments, but unfortunately, you seem to have used them as kindling…

No, that won’t do. I must refrain from negativity.

Dear Prof. Dvupalov, I concur with your assessment of Project X’s initial failures in mutant research. However, your genius can change everything. Please find attached, once again, the printouts of my theses in biotechnology, which I brilliantly…

No, I shouldn’t brag too much… $J0L0Dear Prof. Dvupalov, How could you…

I need to take a breath.
sid_notes_E08_MQ03_SherbaNote_hint: Found among Shcherba’s personal belongings at Ahroprom
sid_notes_E08_MQ03_SherbaNote_name: Draft of a Message to Dvupalov
sid_notes_E08_MQ03_X5SciArticle_body: $J0L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault> …say with certainty that Prof. Dvupalov was mostly correct. The main flaw in the Universal Soldier program lay in the Project X scientists’ failure to preserve the test subjects’ reason and humanity during brain manipulation, prioritizing basic… personality is also a factor. For example, prisoners with type C7 exhibit less promising results than stalkers with type… </NoteDefault></NoteBody>
sid_notes_E08_MQ03_X5SciArticle_hint: What little has survived in the labs beneath Ahroprom
sid_notes_E08_MQ03_X5_NewEra_body:
$J0L0 In light of the escalating risks posed by the Chornobyl Exclusion Zone, Project X has been terminated. All activities within your laboratory have officially been canceled.

Unofficially, Prof. Chubko, Laboratory X5 now falls under the jurisdiction of MDST – our jurisdiction. Those interested in advancing the mission entrusted to us by C-Consciousness and continuing to work on Project X may join MDST and maintain their positions.
More information regarding the lab’s new priorities will be provided later. Until then, please continue your work as usual.
sid_notes_E08_MQ03_X5_NewEra_hint: What little has survived in the labs beneath Ahroprom
sid_notes_E08_SQ01_KartezhnykPDA_Note_bo:
$J0L0Caps: Hey, Mr. Scientist. That thing damn near took me out! Either you cough up double the cash, or I’ll fetch you some Fleshes or psi dogs instead.
Shcherba: Absolutely not. We had a deal.
Shcherba: I’ve had my fill of Fleshes and psi dogs. I need larger specimens.
sid_notes_E10_MQ01_ArhipPDA_Note_body:
$J0L0Myklukha: Great work last time, dude.
We gotta do it again. The route to Yaniv’s getting too risky – I’m burning through more ammo than I’m selling. It ain’t good for business, ya know?
Myklukha: Well, mutants are your problem, and coupons are mine. Business as usual.
Myklukha: Do it fast, and I’ll toss in a bonus. Freedom appreciates reliable folks like you.
sid_notes_E10_MQ01_LeaderPDA_Note_body:
$J0L0 The doctor is currently in the Red Forest, but his exact whereabouts are unknown.
Your job is to locate and deal with him swiftly. Ask the locals, explore anomaly clusters, and investigate abandoned settlements, as these may serve as his hideouts.
You’ll get paid double. Stick to the deadline.
Keep me updated, especially if you discover his lair.
Get started.
sid_notes_E10_MQ01_LeaderPDA_Note_name: A Hit on the Doctor from an Unknown Client
sid_notes_E10_MQ01_SiviyPDA_Audiolog_bod:
$J0L0 Can you hear me, Arkhyp? Arkhyp! It’s Gray! Fucking anomaly!
Tundra got shot! We’d just set up shop at the same place the doctor was spotted. I figured that if we saw him, we’d hand him over to the mercs. But now Tundra’s guts are hanging out! I couldn’t even see where the shot came from! We’re wide open out here. Tundra’s a goner! You gotta alert… *gunshot, followed by the sound of a body falling* Please, don’t… *another gunshot*
sid_notes_E10_MQ01_YarlPDA_Note_body:
$J0L0Wolfhound: Jarl’s team, get to the village. We’ll hit the cemetery. Stay frosty, he’s gotta be close by. The doctor’s been around the Zone longer than us, so this won’t be a walk in the park. Report back every three hours.
Wolfhound: Let’s go.
sid_notes_E10_MQ01_YarlPDA_Note_hint: From Jarl’s PDA in the abandoned village
sid_notes_E10_MQ02_Monolith_Note_body:
$J0L2Injured: 4.Seriously injured: 1.Gone to the Monolith: 2 (1 as a resource-saving measure).$J0L0 Regenerators running low, need fresh supply.
Other equipment sufficient.
sid_notes_E10_MQ02_Monolith_Note_hint: A note found at the Monolith infirmary
sid_notes_E10_MQ02_OldPDA_Note_body:
$J0L0Tailor: What made you think I could be a doctor?
Strider: You stepped up when your brother needed help. You didn’t flinch. You fixed him up without hesitation, like it was second nature.
Tailor: But I don’t remember a thing…
Strider: None of us do, but we can learn everything again. We’ve got a future to build, Tailor. Don’t let fear hold you back.
sid_notes_E10_MQ02_RegeneratorNotes_body: $J0L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault> If the Regenerator is not configured properly, the brothers start to have nightmares and vivid hallucinations. It is important to tune the exposure level very carefully lest these temptations test their faith. </NoteDefault></NoteBody>
sid_notes_E10_MQ02_RegeneratorNotes_foot: $J2L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault> Guide your protector along the path, o Monolith! </NoteDefault></NoteBody>
sid_notes_E11_MQ01_OasisNote_body:
$J0L0[11:57]: I found it! I found the Oasis! You didn’t believe I would, huh? And now I’ve already run outta containers for artifacts!
[11.59]: So, Sidor – you ready to pay for the coordinates? Fifty percent of every transaction. Sixty, if you try haggling. You mull it over, and I’ll take a little break.
[12:10]: It’s so beautiful here, you know. So peaceful. Just… just right.
[12:13]: I can see it as it really is. I feel so…
[12:14]: …grt
sid_notes_E11_MQ01_OasisNote_hint: From an unknown stalker’s PDA, found in X3
sid_notes_E11_MQ01_OasisNote_name: Last Messages from Someone Who Found the Oasis
sid_notes_E11_MQ02_3rdKaymanovDiaryNote_:
$J0L0 Currently pursuing the first option at Orbita Station (X-15). Setting up a Pod for myself. In theory, everything should work. Producing a signature imprint at the station in Lab X3 is quite the ordeal, of course… I’d have to bend the rules and request additional bio samples for the incubator down in the mine from lab assistant Burnov. The thought of spending two hours in a dry aquarium myself isn’t exactly thrilling.

One thing is certain: the prototype Pod offers more than just observation of the Noosphere. These sessions feel more like lucid dreams, where I sense the potential to influence those entities, albeit limited to one psynapse. Alas, “potential” is the keyword here: we’re clearly lacking sufficient energy to reach full interaction. It would take way more than one doctor’s mind and a single Pod to achieve that level of engagement.

Upon hearing my thoughts, Valentyn Dalin crunched the numbers and designed the specifications for an eight-pod system. That means we’re about to take a leap of faith and explore my second option to contact the Noosphere. What will come of it? Only time will tell.
Found among the papers at Orbita Station
sid_notes_E11_MQ02_CodeToBunkerDoor_body:
$J0L0 Following three recent incidents involving biological psi threats, floor B2 is now closed until further notice. Entry to this area is strictly prohibited except for emergencies. In the event of an emergency, use the code 4221 for access.
For further inquiries, contact First Assistant Dorosh.
sid_notes_E11_MQ02_CodeToBunkerDoor_hint: Found among the papers at Orbita Station
sid_notes_E11_MQ02_InstAnt_body:
$J0L0 In the event of my absence, follow these instructions.
Reminder: the antenna is a powerful psi conductor. Being in its immediate vicinity while it is in operation is strictly prohibited. $J0L2Activate the antenna on the rooftop.Ensure that there is no biological activity nearby – any interference could damage the antenna. If the equipment detects such activity, the fuses will blow, cutting off the power supply.Stand by. The settings are configured to activate the antenna automatically if the aforementioned conditions are met. No manual input required!
sid_notes_E11_MQ02_KaymanovDiaryNote_bod: $J0L0<NoteBody> <NoteDefault>Obtained the Alpha, a product of the Caribbean experiment. This remarkable artifact possesses the unique ability to convert biological alpha-psi radiation into electromagnetic beta-psi and vice versa. This discovery significantly expands our technological capabilities, allowing us to utilize existing equipment safely and paving the way for groundbreaking innovations. Are we on the threshold of accessing the Noosphere?</NoteDefault> </NoteBody>$J0L0<NoteBody> <NoteDefault>Nope, no way. Creating a stable channel for data reception is turning out to be a real challenge, mainly because the ionosphere-noosphere barrier turned out to be way more energetically stable than I expected. I’m considering three options:</NoteDefault> </NoteBody>$J0L2<NoteBody><ListItem><NoteDefault>First, I could crank up the intensity until it fries the brain.</NoteDefault></ListItem><ListItem><NoteDefault>Second, I could explore the possibility of linking several consciousnesses together.</NoteDefault></ListItem><ListItem><NoteDefault>The third option, which is a bit out there, involves using the signature imprint not just as a channel key, but as a way to create a Mediator within the Noosphere itself. Data would flow directly to the Mediator, eliminating the need to penetrate the barrier and drastically cutting down on energy consumption – sounds pretty slick, right? Problem is, how do we retrieve the data from the Mediator? If we can figure out how to set up the Source-Mediator system as a superposition, thus getting around the barrier, then we'll be rolling in it.</NoteDefault></ListItem></NoteBody>$J0L0<NoteBody> <NoteDefault>As enticing as it sounds, it’s implausible for the time being. I’ll focus on the first two options for now.</NoteDefault> </NoteBody>
sid_notes_E11_MQ02_KaymanovDiaryNote_hin: Found among the papers at Orbita Station
sid_notes_E11_MQ03_PTSthreadNote_body:
$J0L0 Since its inception in 1966 by the young and gifted Associate Prof. Sakharov, Project IRCD-1 (Individual Remote Communication Device), also known as unit “FIBER-1P,” has encountered persistent challenges in advancing beyond rudimentary experimental stages until present.
Despite the project’s official suspension due to technical constraints, such as limited range and susceptibility to interference, Professor Kalancha succeeded in making notable progress by refining and calibrating the equipment to establish a link with the STALKER Program Control Center. This breakthrough revitalized efforts in exploring the transmission and reception of cognitive information over extended distances by leveraging principles of biological radio wave propagation.
sid_notes_E11_MQ04_LetterFromKrivenko_No:
$J0L0 To Col. Korshunov
From Head of Joint Cryptobiology and Psi-Medicine Laboratory, S.A. Kryvenko $J0L0 The HVS coils have been installed on the exoskeleton as you ordered.
I understand the emergency but feel obliged to warn you:
1. The exoskeleton has been in development for quite some time. I am ready to guarantee its reliability, although your body will need some time to adapt and synchronize motor functions.
2. The stability of the intrusion protection coils cannot be guaranteed: the technology (based on the evolution of Ruhmkorff coils) has not yet passed all stages of testing, therefore, it is recommended for activation only in extreme cases (if there is a direct threat to life).
sid_notes_E11_MQ04_PDA_Chernozem_body: $J0L2<NoteBody><ListItem><NoteDefault>Find potential couriers among stalkers.</NoteDefault></ListItem><ListItem><NoteDefault>Memorize requirements for potential candidates and destroy these instructions immediately.</NoteDefault></ListItem><ListItem><NoteDefault>Get in touch with Nestor.</NoteDefault></ListItem><ListItem><NoteDefault>Provide couriers with scanners on a rolling basis.</NoteDefault></ListItem></NoteBody>
sid_notes_E11_MQ04_PDA_Chernozem_hint: Chornozem’s PDA, found in a warehouse at the Chemical Plant
sid_notes_E11_MQ04_PDA_Nestor_body: $J0L2<NoteBody><ListItem><NoteDefault>Find a contact among SIRCAA employees.</NoteDefault></ListItem><ListItem><NoteDefault>Gain their trust and establish mutually beneficial cooperation.</NoteDefault></ListItem><ListItem><NoteDefault>Obtain scanners.</NoteDefault></ListItem><ListItem><NoteDefault>Deliver all obtained equipment to Chornozem.</NoteDefault></ListItem></NoteBody>
sid_notes_E11_MQ04_PDA_Nestor_hint: Nestor’s PDA, found in a warehouse at the Chemical Plant
sid_notes_E11_MQ04_Protocol78_Instructio: $J0L2<NoteBody><ListItem><NoteDefault>Following an investigation conducted in the Depot and at the Slag Heap, two personal digital assistants belonging to the subjects were located and confiscated.</NoteDefault></ListItem><ListItem><NoteDefault>Biological samples were dispatched to the Mainland in accordance with transportation protocol Cargo 900X.</NoteDefault></ListItem><ListItem><NoteDefault>All material evidence has been moved to the Chemical Plant warehouse for safekeeping.</NoteDefault></ListItem><ListItem><NoteDefault>Access code: 8506.</NoteDefault></ListItem></NoteBody>
Found in the Chemical Plant infirmary
sid_notes_E12_MO01_AboutDalin_body:
$J0L0Ozersky: Dalin! Can you believe it? Dalin! He’s never set foot in the Zone! He’s only seen fieldwork from his cushy penthouse!
Hermann: Well, that might work in our favor, right? Sounds like he won’t be sticking his nose where it doesn’t belong. And with his connections, we might get some serious funding…
Ozersky: I came to the Zone to push the boundaries of science, not to push pencils! And now they want me to take orders from some kid? No way in hell! I’m out!
sid_notes_E12_MO01_AboutDalin_hint: Prof. Ozersky’s PDA found inside Spirit’s bunker
sid_notes_E12_MO01_DuhToRihter_body: $J0L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault> Stay put until the heat is down, Richter. The Corps doesn’t know about this spot. You’ll be safe here. If I don’t reach out in two days, head through the tunnel to Lymansk and wait for me at the base. </NoteDefault></NoteBody>
sid_notes_E12_MO01_DuhToRihter_hint: A message on a sheet of paper found in the cave
sid_notes_E12_MO01_Groups_body:
$J0L0Group 1: Danya Bylaw & Sanya Marine.
(Crossed without problems.)
Group 2: Roma Winger & Bohdan Hook.
(I took them through, then they refused to pay. Only upfront from now on.)
Group 3: Avanes Mountain.
(Sanya Marine sent him my way. Everything’s fine.)
Group 4: Vitya Copper, Lyova Lout, Vitya Brash.
(Lout couldn’t keep up and ended up a crispy critter in the anomaly. Why’d he even tag along if he was in such shitty shape?)
Group 5: Fly & Richie.
(Richie got cold feet about crossing the Boundary. I still got paid for both.)
sid_notes_E12_MO01_Groups_name: List of People Crossing the Boundary
sid_notes_E12_MO01_Instruction_body:
$J0L0 I’ve tinkered with the scanner as requested and added a few tweaks. You can deactivate anomalous fields now. Not all of them, mind you, or I’d be richer than Myklukha by now. But maybe it’ll work in that tunnel under the Offloading Terminal.
Using the scanner is easy, but I’ll break it down so you don’t bug me about it.
Scanner-M Manual:
1. Set the scanner up by a stable anomaly.
2. Press the button once to activate it.
3. That’s it. Now you can go through the anomalous field. $J0L0 Note 1: Don’t drag your feet after switching it on – the effect doesn’t last long.
Note 2: Remember to throw a bolt to make sure the anomaly is really off.
sid_notes_E12_MO01_Photo_body: $J0L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault> Spark is no more. Those who claim to be them now are just fakes. The original founders of Spark were killed by the Ward. And if it wasn’t for Marshal, I wouldn’t be here either. </NoteDefault></NoteBody>
sid_notes_E12_MO01_Photo_header: $J0L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault> Spark Scientific Research and Investigation Group. Topol, Spirit, Snake, Mace. </NoteDefault></NoteBody>
sid_notes_E12_MQ01_KPKDuh_body:
$J0L0 The key card to the bunker is in my pocket. The scanner I used to get to Prypiat is inside. It’s not too hard, you’ll figure it out. I left the manual there, too.
Sorry for delivering the message like this. We can’t be sure who’s listening in. A delayed one is safer.
When you search my body, don’t be shy – take everything you need. Just make sure you bury me deep, okay? I think I deserve some rest.
sid_notes_E14_MQ01_Helicopter_Note_body:
$J0L0Squad Name: Stingray-7
Assignment: Locate all underground entrances to the Foundation facility and register their coordinates.
Sitrep: Lost contact with the helicopter crew. The last communication and coordinates are encrypted.
sid_notes_E14_MQ01_Helicopter_Note_hint: Information from a Stingray-7 flash drive
sid_notes_E14_MQ01_LaundromatDocs_Note_b: $J0L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault> Due to the Failure of Operation Fairway, Stingray survivors are to be evacuated from Site B28. </NoteDefault></NoteBody>$J0L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault> Group list: </NoteDefault></NoteBody>$J0L2<NoteBody><ListItem><NoteDefault>Col. Kovalsky (Stingray-1).</NoteDefault></ListItem><ListItem><NoteDefault>Cpt. Tarasov (Stingray-3).</NoteDefault></ListItem><ListItem><NoteDefault>Lt. Rogovets (Stingray-5).</NoteDefault></ListItem><ListItem><NoteDefault>Lt. Kirillov (Stingray-3).</NoteDefault></ListItem><ListItem><NoteDefault>Lt. Podorozhny (Stingray-3).</NoteDefault></ListItem><ListItem><NoteDefault>Lt. Sokolov (Stingray-4).</NoteDefault></ListItem><ListItem><NoteDefault>WO Valentyr (Stingray-5).</NoteDefault></ListItem></NoteBody>$J0L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault> For assistance in rescuing our men, the stalker known as Strelok is to be evacuated with the squad. </NoteDefault></NoteBody>
sid_notes_E14_MQ01_LaundromatDocs_Note_f: $J2L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault> Maj. Degtyarev, officer in charge of the evacuation </NoteDefault></NoteBody>
sid_notes_E14_MQ01_LaundromatDocs_Note_n: Evacuating Stingray Group out of the Zone
sid_notes_E14_MQ01_Prometey_Spark_Note_b: $J0L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault> Rustle, we’re retreating to our fallback position. If anything goes wrong, take cover at these coordinates. </NoteDefault></NoteBody>
sid_notes_E14_MQ01_Prometey_Varta_Note_b: $J0L0<NoteBody><NoteBold>Maj. Dvoryankov: </NoteBold><NoteDefault>Delta Team, rendezvous at the exact coordinates for points B or C. </NoteDefault></NoteBody>
sid_notes_E15_MQ03_2ndDoctorDiaryNote_bo: $J0L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault> A group of these new explorers of the Zone wandered into my swamp the other day. These two, who go by the names Fang and Ghost, find the local miracles quite enough for them, but this Strelok guy wants more. He wants to go north, to the CNPP, and even further, to the very Center. I don’t have the slightest idea what’s going on there now. I’ve heard rumors that after becoming C-Consciousness with the help of the MDST engineers, my former colleagues are doing their best to cover up their tracks. Their futile attempts to bring order to chaos are making things even worse. But to hell with them. My duty here is to save lives. </NoteDefault></NoteBody>
sid_notes_E15_MQ03_2ndDoctorDiaryNote_hi: Found in the basement of the doctor’s cabin
sid_notes_E15_MQ03_3rdDoctorDiaryNote_bo:
$J0L0 Strelok left an artifact with me for safekeeping: the Heart of Chornobyl. He brought it back from the north.
I did some tests and came to a surprising conclusion: apparently the Zone has created an Altered Variometer, an anomalous version of Suslov’s electromagnetic psi-amplifier, and it’s at least eight times more powerful!
If I had something like this back in 2006, I would have been able to connect to the Noosphere by myself!
sid_notes_E15_MQ03_3rdDoctorDiaryNote_hi: Found in the basement of the doctor’s cabin
sid_notes_E15_MQ03_4thDoctorDiaryNote_bo:
$J0L0 Fang and Ghost brought in Strelok. He was half-dead. They say they came across some “Monolith” inside the CNPP Sarcophagus. Supposedly, it’s a kind of mystical wish-granting rock… what kind of nonsense is that? Either my former colleagues have gone completely nuts, or they still fancy themselves a force of destiny. Should I intervene, or remain on the sidelines?
How grateful I am to find a source of joy in all this gloom. Strelok has become like a son to me – not by blood, but by the ways of the Zone. He is strong. He will heal. And when he does, he’ll go north again.
Do I have the right to hold him back?
sid_notes_E15_MQ03_4thDoctorDiaryNote_hi: Found in the basement of the doctor’s cabin
sid_notes_E15_MQ03_DoctorDiaryNote_body: $J0L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault> Kaymanov the scientist is done: forever infamous. By trying to prevent a disaster, I may have brought forth another, more terrifying one. There is no return to the Mainland for me. I will stay in the Red Forest, contemplating the consequences of my actions. The Zone is a monster from the darkest realm of creation. I unleashed it upon us all, and this can hardly be undone. The Zone kills and maims, so my atonement must be to devote the rest of my days to helping its countless victims. Kaymanov the scientist is dead. I hope that the doctor will serve this world better. </NoteDefault></NoteBody>
sid_notes_E15_MQ03_DoctorDiaryNote_hint: Found in the basement of the doctor’s cabin
sid_notes_E15_MQ03_ProjectYNote_body:
$J0L0 You are capable of more than you think – enough to take on the hardships that will inevitably befall us in the New World. You will need something more, however, to face the unknown. Much more!
May the Zone aid us if we find ourselves lacking courage in our future trials!
You were all courageous enough to get here – every single one of you! You had the will and the faith to make it through, and you are now worthy to become liberators.
Now go to the Mainland. Wait for the right time.
Something beautiful is about to happen.
sid_notes_E15_MQ03_ProjectYNote_name: Draft of a Speech to an Unknown Audience
sid_notes_E15_MQ03_ProjectY_body: $J0L2<NoteBody><ListItem><NoteDefault>Couriers (Chornozem, destroy the list).</NoteDefault></ListItem><ListItem><NoteDefault>Lab X3 (destroy all the documents, as well as any evidence of reframing agents and using couriers).</NoteDefault></ListItem><ListItem><NoteDefault>Provide scanners (Nestor).</NoteDefault></ListItem><ListItem><NoteDefault>Provide Alpha Artifacts (we’ve got a number of faded ones – more than enough).</NoteDefault></ListItem><ListItem><NoteDefault>Duga Comms Center (replace the microchips with external action modules).</NoteDefault></ListItem></NoteBody>
sid_notes_E15_MQ03_ProjectY_hint: A document from the secret unit in Kaymanov’s Laboratory
sid_notes_E16_AgentSFile_Chernozem_body:
$J0L0Name: Timur Tairov
Nickname: Chornozem
YOB: 1988
Profile: Career criminal, wanted murder suspect. Upon his arrival in the Zone, he promptly formed a gang and garnered favor from prominent underworld figures. Prone to violence. Psychological assessment indicates no risk of suicide and zero resistance to programming.
Programming results: Fully successful.
Programming objective: Elimination of individuals identified as potential threats.
sid_notes_E16_AgentSFile_Chernozem_hint: C-Consciousness Agent Profile from the Neurolaboratory Archive
sid_notes_E16_AgentSFile_Strelok_body:
$J0L0Name: N/A.
Nickname: N/A.
YOB: N/A.
Profile: N/A.
Programming results: Program error.
Programming objective: Kill Strelok / A:LS-013.
sid_notes_E16_AgentSFile_Strelok_hint: C-Consciousness Agent Profile from the Neurolaboratory Archive
sid_notes_E16_AgentSFile_Temnyj_body:
$J0L0Name: Ihor Bykov
Nickname: Dark
YOB: 1976
Profile: Former children’s hiking instructor and mountain rescuer. His professional background facilitated rapid adaptation to the Zone, where he garnered respect among stalkers assisting field researchers. Known for his strategic acumen. Cool-headed, strong communication skills. Psychological assessment indicates zero resistance to programming.
Programming results: Fully successful.
Programming objective: Kill Strelok.
sid_notes_E16_AgentSFile_Temnyj_hint: C-Consciousness Agent Profile from the Neurolaboratory Archive
sid_notes_E16_AgentsFile_Nestor_body:
$J0L0Name: Oleksandr Kuchko
Nickname: Nestor
YOB: 1959
Profile: Behavioral psychologist, one of the founders of Ukrainian criminal psychology. Captured by bandits while conducting sociological research in the Zone. Low level of resistance to programming due to problems with alcohol.
Programming results: Fully successful.
Programming objective: Select candidates for future programming.
sid_notes_E16_AgentsFile_Nestor_hint: C-Consciousness Agent Profile from the Neurolaboratory Archive
sid_notes_E16_AgentsFile_Scar_body:
$J0L0Nickname: Scar / A:MS-017
YOB: 1979
Profile: A mercenary. Has a natural resistance to anomalous energy and can survive Emissions without any means of psi-protection (5th class psi-resistance assumed).
Intrinsic traits: Stubborn, reckless, works well under pressure. Had Marshal’s key memories implanted during programming. [EVENT REFRAME: The Chemical Plant/death of the Spark group members].
Obtained traits: Strong interpersonal skills, exuberance, hatred for the Ward.
Warning: On the verge of nervous breakdown. Could become unstable.
Programming objective: Reflection Program.
sid_notes_E16_AgentsFile_Scar_hint: C-Consciousness Agent Profile from the Neurolaboratory Archive
sid_notes_E16_MQ01_PDA_Scar_body: $J0L2<NoteBody><ListItem><NoteDefault>Locate an active Alpha Artifact or recruit a person to bring it to SIRCAA and keep them under control.</NoteDefault></ListItem><ListItem><NoteDefault>An Alpha Artifact is needed in X11 to restore the functionality of the X-Network.</NoteDefault></ListItem><ListItem><NoteDefault>Use Orbita Station to recruit scientists.</NoteDefault></ListItem><ListItem><NoteDefault>Assemble a working Visiograph at STC Malachite (X17) using modules found in the Zone.</NoteDefault></ListItem><ListItem><NoteDefault>Activate the Visiograph.</NoteDefault></ListItem><ListItem><NoteDefault>Await further instructions.</NoteDefault></ListItem></NoteBody>
sid_notes_E16_MQ01_PDA_Scar_hint: Scar’s PDA taken from his body
sid_notes_E16_MQ02_MarshalNote_body:
$J0L0Marshal: That night at the Chemical Plant… It keeps coming back to me, Spirit. Spark and our boys. Why did it have to happen? We ran to them as soon as we heard the first gunshot. Topol was the first to die, and I can still see it. Mace tried to warn us, but all he could do was wheeze. That fucking second assault squad… That workshop, packed with barrels of chemicals…
Marshal: We wouldn’t be talking right now if it weren’t for Snake.
Marshal: Thieves rule, Wardens drool. That’s what he always said, remember?
Spirit: Of course I do.
Spirit: Just be careful. Don’t defy Korshunov openly.
Marshal: Don’t worry. We’ve got other ways to give him hell.
Spirit: I just don’t want them to find you too.
Marshal: Don’t worry, Spirit.
Marshal: I’ll go north. The Ward won’t get me there.
sid_notes_E16_MQ02_MarshalNote_hint: PDA taken from Marshal’s body in the Neurolaboratory
sid_notes_E16_MQ03_1stNoosphereDocumenta: $J0L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault> According to a report from Lab X-18, alpha-psi radiation of a biological nature has been detected while studying the brain of sentient species. I have no idea where Pyotr Kalugin gets his test subjects from, and I have no intention of finding out. I want no part in these studies after what I’ve heard from my colleagues, even though their twisted “in vivo” methods do yield quite spectacular results. </NoteDefault></NoteBody>$J0L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault> They performed a successful test of IRCD-1 (Individual Remote Communication Device) – the “FIBER-1P” unit – at the Troposphere Comm Station (TCS). As a side effect, they obtained data indicating that psi-radiation might somehow be linked to a powerful external information field. </NoteDefault></NoteBody>$J0L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault> Existing issues: </NoteDefault></NoteBody>$J0L1<NoteBody><ListItem><NoteDefault>Very limited range (no more than 50 meters)</NoteDefault></ListItem><ListItem><NoteDefault>Susceptibility to interference</NoteDefault></ListItem><ListItem><NoteDefault>Interference from a far more powerful psi-source of unknown nature</NoteDefault></ListItem></NoteBody>
sid_notes_E16_MQ03_2ndNoosphereDocumenta: $J0L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault>  Biological psi-radiation has some intrinsic, fundamental constraints that can hardly be overcome in the foreseeable future. The only apparent option is to artificially grow a brain of enormous size. X16 does have a number of volunteers for this project, including Prof. Prykhodko, but this is not the path that will take us to the Noosphere. For now, while alternative approaches are yet to be found, we’ll focus our efforts on the electromagnetic generation of psi-radiation. Objectives:  </NoteDefault></NoteBody>$J0L1<NoteBody><ListItem><NoteDefault>Increase the capacity of the transmitting channel</NoteDefault></ListItem><ListItem><NoteDefault>Develop a “tunnel” to limit interference</NoteDefault></ListItem><ListItem><NoteDefault>Increase range</NoteDefault></ListItem></NoteBody>$J0L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault>  The group’s work on the electromagnetic simulation of psi-radiation has resulted in the discovery of beta-psi radiation. А physical prototype is to be presented shortly.  </NoteDefault></NoteBody>
sid_notes_E16_MQ03_3rdNoosphereDocumenta:
$J0L0 Part of the group led by Prof. Chubko is working in Lab X10. So far they have tested “Raduga,” an omnidirectional antenna for a psi-emitter with electromagnetic excitation. $J0L0 Known issue:
The dispersion field provided by the modulator is quite small (approx. 1 km). The modulator is poorly isolated from the surrounding psi-environment, and the level of interference is high, resulting in significant damage to anything alive and sentient in the vicinity. $J0L0 Documented results have been sent to Lab X8, to Lebedev. Suslov says that they plan to add some necessary modifications and will try to use Raduga as an aggression-suppressing device. Kalancha has always been a dreamer. There’s no changing him. He should bring his ideas to Pyotr Strizh at the Ahroprom Research Institute – they’re definitely both on the same page. $J0L0 Unexpected discovery: (un?)fortunately, the reason for all of our technical hurdles might be due to the existence of a greater information field engulfing the entire planet. I can hardly believe it myself. The Noosphere? $J0L0 Raduga has been built and tested, but to test this incredible hypothesis, we need something smaller and equipped with a beam-focusing feature.
sid_notes_E16_MQ03_4thKaymanovDiaryNote_:
$J0L0 Progress is underway as we scout for a site to set up the installation. We’ll need to be patient until the stations for probing the barrier’s density are operational:
– Azimuth router station
– Sphere station
– Krug antenna complex $J0L0 My colleagues are enthusiastic about the idea, and so am I. But the more I think about it, the less I like their fervor. I’ve been hearing a lot of talk lately about connecting to the Noosphere and changing it to get rid of the darkness humanity has brought upon itself – to create a more perfect world… $J0L0 At one point, I had to step in and remind everyone of the unwritten rules of scientific ethics and the individual mission of every scientist in particular.
Found in Kaymanov’s office in Lab X7
sid_notes_E16_MQ03_5thKaymanovDiaryNote_: $J0L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault>  We’ve secured an optimal location north of the CNPP. Rapid preparations are underway for the Northern Plateau. The Generator units are arriving – we’re having significant progress. Dalin is currently assembling the O-KTA. He intends to integrate eight Pods, which should suffice. Thanks to our benefactors, Lab X7 has been deployed in no time. Additionally, Lab X11 is under construction southeast of the cooling pond and equipped with a podium for the Alpha. It will serve as a backup location for debugging, test runs, and maintaining a secure remote connection to the Center.  </NoteDefault></NoteBody>$J0L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault>  That unhealthy gleam in their eyes troubles me. What does “getting rid of the darkness” even mean? A general amnesty? But what about human nature, the Ecce Homo? At the end of the day, we humans will remain the same, with all our inherent flaws. Even if some of us would die happy, what about the generations to come? No, their plan offers only a momentary respite. Ultimately, these measures lead nowhere.  </NoteDefault></NoteBody>
Found in Kaymanov’s office in Lab X7
sid_notes_E16_MQ03_6thKaymanovDiaryNote_: $J0L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault> Looks like they’ve made up their minds. They’re keeping up professional appearances, sure, yet they’re avoiding eye contact. What does the Noosphere mean to you, Kaymanov? Will you give in to your desires, or will you protect the thought-forms within its confines? This is the crux of the matter. Should we take a risk? It might pay off, but if it doesn’t… We could be facing a disaster of unthinkable, even global magnitude. </NoteDefault></NoteBody>$J0L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault> It seems Dalin intends to swap out my (empty) Pod with Suslov’s apparatus from the Caribbean Experiment. I’m surprised they haven’t thought about constructing a Variometer large enough to replace all eight of us. But then who would claim the accolades? </NoteDefault></NoteBody>
Found in Kaymanov’s office in Lab X7
sid_notes_E16_MQ03_CaribbeanDoc_body: $J0L0<NoteBody> <NoteDefault>Suslov has built an interesting contraption he calls the Variometer. It is an electromagnetic psi-amplifier with a modulation unit. Its output power is actually similar to that of a human brain in active state. It allowed us to perform the Caribbean Experiment, which was aimed at testing the hypothesis of the Noosphere’s existence.</NoteDefault> </NoteBody>$J0L0<NoteBody> <NoteDefault>The report is attached.</NoteDefault> </NoteBody>$J0L0<NoteBody> <NoteDefault>Location: Island in the CNPP cooling pond, Laboratory X-6.</NoteDefault> </NoteBody>$J0L0<NoteBody> <NoteDefault>Emission source: RV OROL Long-Range Communication Vessel, equipped with Suslov’s Variometer. The OTH range was reached via electromagnetic psi-beam reflection at the lower ionosphere layers.</NoteDefault> </NoteBody>$J0L0<NoteBody> <NoteDefault>Emission receiver: Orchila Island, Caribbean Sea, RV AGAR.</NoteDefault></NoteBody> $J0L0<NoteBody> <NoteDefault>Results:</NoteDefault> </NoteBody>$J0L2<NoteBody><ListItem><NoteDefault>Processing the obtained data and analyzing the registered distortions has provided experimental proof of the Noosphere’s existence.</NoteDefault> </ListItem><ListItem><NoteDefault>Not only was the psi-beam reflected at the ionosphere, it also “grazed” the Noosphere itself. As a result, a cluster of unknown matter coagulated on board the receiving vessel in the Caribbean Sea. This cluster has received the code name “Object Alpha.”</NoteDefault></ListItem></NoteBody>$J0L0<NoteBody> <NoteDefault>The Artifact was discretely delivered to the Chornobyl Exclusion Zone for in-depth study by the Project X special task force.</NoteDefault> </NoteBody>
sid_notes_E16_MQ03_InstructionOKTA_body:
$J0L0 NOOSPHERE EXPERIMENT PROTOCOL $J0L0 Instruction for technical personnel operating O-СTA equipment $J0L0Laboratory power supply:
Engage the power switch to power up the facility.
Needle antenna:
Place the psi-amplifier into the container on the central stand.
Main control panel:
1. Insert Cartridges containing personal signatures into the slots in the receiving panel.
2. O-СTA: Set the primary mode lever to the “INITIAL” position and wait for the Pods to become operational.
3. Press the “SERVICE: EXPOSE” button and wait for the Pods to assume vertical position.
4. All experiment participants should enter their designated Pods.
5. Set the primary mode lever to the “EXPERIMENT” position. $J0L0*Note:Pod #1 is equipped with a remote launch button. It can be unlocked from the console in Room #1.$J0L0 6. Technical staff should continuously monitor telemetry and nominal modes. Close your eyes and go to your happy place!
sid_notes_E16_MQ03_InstructionOKTA_hint: Found in Kaymanov’s office in Lab X7
sid_notes_E16_MQ03_StrelokPDANoteOnArtif: $J0L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault>  To think that I had the Heart of Chornobyl on me when I first reached the Pods in X7, at the Generators… Protecting the Zone wasn’t on my mind at the time, however. But if the doctor’s findings regarding this artifact are true, I can succeed now. This time, there’s no doubt.  </NoteDefault></NoteBody>
From Strelok’s PDA taken from his body
Note on the Heart of Chornobyl
sid_notes_E16_MQ03_StrelokPDANote_body:
$J0L0 I had to copy the instructions by hand. The original is pretty much unreadable. $J0L0 “The Signature must be obtained in the Marking Neurolaboratory.
The process is entirely automated.
Staff must ensure the subject is seated and remains still throughout the procedure.
Once the Signature has been obtained, retrieve the Cartridge and archive the recording following established protocol.”
sid_notes_E16_MQ03_StrelokPDANote_hint: A note from Strelok’s PDA taken from his body
sid_notes_EQ109_MarikTopotNote_body:
$J0L0 Marik Stomper: I packed my bags and headed out. Moved to the Burnt Forest.
