I heard he left the Zone for good.</NoteDefault></NoteBody>$J0L0<NoteBody><NoteBold>Whale:</NoteBold><NoteDefault> Let's make it the last time you're bothering me, okay?</NoteDefault></NoteBody>$J0L0<NoteBody><NoteBold>Dragonfly:</NoteBold><NoteDefault> You're a tough one to crack.</NoteDefault></NoteBody>$J0L0<NoteBody><NoteBold>Dragonfly:</NoteBold><NoteDefault> I know where I can make some more money. I'll be back in a month.</NoteDefault></NoteBody>$J0L0<NoteBody><NoteBold>Whale:</NoteBold><NoteDefault> Did I stutter?</NoteDefault></NoteBody>
sid_notes_RookieVillage_Hub_Note_Agent_h: Found at an abandoned house at Rookie Village
sid_notes_RookieVillage_Hub_Note_Oasis_b:
$J0L0Sanya Goose: Take this, all of you who didn't believe me - I found it!$J0L0Dimka Spook: Oasis, or what? Come on, give me a break.$J0L0Dimka Spook: Where?$J0L0Sanya Goose: Just like they said, at the Cordon. Won't tell you no more, I'm not stupid. $J0L0Dimka Spook: You going in alone?$J0L0Sanya Goose: I did all the legwork looking for it, so all the loot is mine. I'm not cutting you in.$J0L0
Dimka Spook: Long time, no text. How's Oasis?$J0L0
Dimka Spook: Sanya, you there?
sid_notes_Rostok_Hub_MessageNote_body: $J0L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault>So.</NoteDefault></NoteBody>$J0L2<NoteBody><ListItem><NoteDefault>Myklukha didn't approve. Fuck him. I didn't join Freedom to sit on my ass and sell boots. If you don't like it, just say it already, I'll find another partner.</NoteDefault></ListItem><ListItem><NoteDefault>We'll split it like always - I get the docs, you get whatever else we find.</NoteDefault></ListItem><ListItem><NoteDefault>Don't go around blabbing about it. Better shut it.</NoteDefault></ListItem><ListItem><NoteDefault>Same meet spot. I bring the keycard, you bring the gear. If the gear's shit, you're going in first.</NoteDefault></ListItem></NoteBody>
sid_notes_Rostok_Hub_MessageNote_hint: Found on a PDA forgotten at Rostok
sid_notes_Rostok_Hub_MessageNote_name: A note to a Freedom member
sid_notes_Rostok_Note_Agreement_body:
$J0L0 The following terms and conditions have been mutually accepted:
1. The mercenaries agree not to accept contracts from any third party without prior notification to the employer regarding any changes in their rate
2. Bogey, in his capacity as coordinator, shall maintain a consistent presence in Rostok and guarantee that the mercenaries will not interfere in the employer’s affairs.
sid_notes_Rostok_Note_SalesToShevchenko_:
$J0L0 Shipment for Beard:
1. AR416 x4
2. Mark I EMR x1
3. Sawed-off shotgun x5
A list found among Myklukha’s personal belongings
sid_notes_Rostok_Note_SalesToSultansk_bo:
$J0L0 Shipment for Kalyna:
1. Sawed-off shotgun x14
2. AKM-74S x20
3. Sunrise Suit x1
sid_notes_Rostok_Note_SalesToSultansk_fo: $J2L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault> Offer at a grossly inflated price, then let her negotiate it down to half </NoteDefault></NoteBody>
sid_notes_Rostok_Note_SalesToSultansk_hi: A list found among Myklukha’s personal belongings
sid_notes_Rostok_PDA_Bet_body:
$J0L0Leva Starter: Pay up. Frog sat down.
Yaryk Chill: He was just picking up a smoke. You bet he’d sit there and have a heart-to-heart chat.
sid_notes_Rostok_PDA_Bet_hint: A PDA left unattended in Rostok
sid_notes_Rostok_PDA_Polovin_body:
$J0L0Molotov: Enjoying yourself in Rostok, huh? The Freedom scumbags just hit one of our patrols!
Polovyn: Where’d you hear that?
Molotov: From the higher-ups. They’re itching to know who leaked the patrol schedules. We all are.
sid_notes_Rostok_PDA_Polovin_hint: PDA from a Duty member’s body in Rostok
sid_notes_SEQ01_Note_body:
$J0L0 So you weren’t shitting me, Varan. This thing is real. No more shaking down chumps – this gizmo’s gonna do all the work for us! Ain’t that sweet?
I’m gonna try and get it working now. One of those eggheads told me everything about it. Didn’t even need much persuasion, heh heh. Man, life is gonna be sweet!
sid_notes_SEQ02_Note_body: $J0L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault> I used this fucking thing five times already and got shit! I’m just hauling it around the Zone like an idiot, catching more and more psi along the way! You promised the coordinates were accurate! I’m trying one more time, and if it doesn’t work, I’m heading right back to you and stuffing this damn thing up your ass! </NoteDefault></NoteBody>
sid_notes_SEQ02_Note_hint: From the body of a zombified stalker
sid_notes_SEQ04_Note_body:
$J0L0 I guess the mercenaries are starting to realize that all the money got spent on this “scanner.” That was a bit reckless of me, but if the experiment is successful, I’ll be able to afford a proper expedition, instead of this farce… Then I’ll finally be able to pay all my debts!
I hope it works…
sid_notes_SEQ04_Note_hint: From the body of a dead scientist
sid_notes_SEQ05_Note_body:
$J0L0 It’s gonna be all right, I promise.
I know it wasn’t the right thing to do, but we had no choice! We needed the money, and it was their idea from the start… I bought this scanner, and it’s gonna work. It has to work!
It’s gonna be all right.
sid_notes_SEQ06_Note_body:
$J0L0[16.46]: Are you sure these coordinates are accurate? There’s nothing here! Where am I supposed to put this shit?
[16.57]: Hey! Are you ignoring me?
[17.03]: If you fucked me over, I’ll turn the whole Sphere upside down, you hear me? $J0L0[22.01]: :)
sid_notes_SEQ06_Note_hint: From the body of a dead stalker
sid_notes_SEQ08_Note_body: $J0L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault> How could you sign me up for this? You know I’m afraid of heights. No way he could give us some other coordinates? If I was into crawling up construction cranes, I’d become a climber, not a stalker! Good thing there aren’t any mutants here. </NoteDefault></NoteBody>
sid_notes_SEQ08_Note_hint: From a dead stalker’s body near a construction crane
sid_notes_SEQ08_Note_name: Message from a Stalker Who’s Afraid of Heights
sid_notes_SEQ09_Note_body:
$J0L0 Sorry, man. I admit, I screwed up. Those coordinates I sent you… well, they aren’t exactly right. I mean, they’re supposed to be right, but only under certain conditions. Maybe they’re right at a certain time, and for the rest of the day they’re wrong… I don’t know, man. It’s complicated!
Anyway, I’m sorry. I’ll give you a discount next time. My bad.
sid_notes_SEQ09_Note_hint: From a PDA found in a backpack
sid_notes_SEQ10_Note_body:
$J0L0[11.34]: Deggy’s gonna kill us for this.
[11.40]: Deggy’ll kill us anyway as soon as he finds out about our new pals, so it’s kinda too late to get cold feet now. You gotta do it quickly – get in, deploy it, and get out before the Monolithians spot you. Okay? Now go.
sid_notes_SQ01_ValenokPDA_Note_body:
$J0L0Valik Lummox: Can you believe it? Those knuckleheads asked for a showdown by the water tower. Ha ha! They’re in for a surprise up there.
Hunchback: What’s gotten into you, Valya? Trying to dig yourself into an even deeper hole?
Valik Lummox: Take it easy. I’ve got a plan to sort this out!
Hunchback: You’re gonna end up dead, you idiot…
sid_notes_SQ01_ValenokPDA_Note_hint: The PDA taken from Valik Lummox’s body
sid_notes_SQ02_PomorNote_body:
$J0L0Ragman: New order’s in. Same deal, but I need double the ammo. They’re selling like hotcakes. We’ve got rodents all over the place.
Pomor: I’ll have it sorted in a week.
Ragman: Don’t nod off over there! You’re my top supplier.
Pomor: Not gonna happen. I’ve got a map.
sid_notes_SQ02_PomorNote_hint: Found on Pomor’s PDA at the Poppy Field
sid_notes_SQ04_SoloveiPDANote_body:
$J0L0Hamster: The professor sent the money for the collars.
Nightingale: Oh! Hey, Hamster. How come burers don’t have any ID?
Hamster: Spare me your jokes. You out hunting dogs again?
Nightingale: What else is there? The routes are sealed and there’s nothing else to do.
Hamster: You could school the newbies.
Nightingale: Nah. It’ll be slick as a pseudodog’s fur. I’ll return shortly with the haul. Have those coupons ready.
sid_notes_SQ04_SoloveiPDANote_hint: The last message before his death
sid_notes_SQ06_TransitWaybill_Note_body:
$J0L0 To V. Konstantinov, Lab X11 Security Chief

Your request #117 dated 3/15 has been partially fulfilled.
We are currently experiencing a shortage of weapons. I am sending all available items, with the remainder to be included in the upcoming shipment once our supply issues are resolved. The equipment and necessary biological samples have been provided in full, as the research conducted in your laboratory is considered a priority.

Attaching the shipping list:
1. AKM-74S x7
2. AKM-74U x11
3. Portable Psi Installation (PPI) x1
4. Horizon-2 suppressor capsule x1
5. Biological samples.
sid_notes_SQ06_TransitWaybill_Note_hint: An old form found in the railroad tunnel at the Cordon
sid_notes_SQ103_DeadDiggerNote_body: $J0L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault>Kostyk, found the code, thought I wrote it down – no dice. The place where I saw it is still there, but that's it. Maybe you can take a look? And pick me up on the way back. I'll wait here. I've got a Jello brain, and I'm walking in circles.</NoteDefault></NoteBody>
sid_notes_SQ103_DeaddropInstructions_Tex: $J0L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault>There's a safe next to you. Enter the code, put the Emitter inside, and you'll get what's yours when the system recognizes the device. Next steps according to plan.</NoteDefault></NoteBody>
sid_notes_SQ103_MapNote_Text_body: $J0L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault>Memorize the safe code. Need the Emitter ASAP.</NoteDefault></NoteBody>
sid_notes_SQ103_MiheyevNestorNote_body: $J0L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault>The Emitter from the last shipment works, but standard psi-blockers are no good against hallucinations. I'd like to ask to reduce my working hours to four, and to six hours for Assistant Kovalenko.</NoteDefault></NoteBody>
sid_notes_SQ103_MiheyevNestorNote_footer: $J0L0<NoteBody><NoteItalic>Tough it out. Cut the union crap. Nestor.</NoteItalic></NoteBody>
sid_notes_SQ103_PersonalCorrespondance_T:
$J0L0[20:48] Mavka: Are you having a midlife crisis, or what? "There are things I need to do on my own" – and off he went. I had to put up with Myklukha's bullshit, and the Arena, you think I can't handle your secrets?!$J0L0Piece of shit. Mavka out.

[00:17] Lukash: I'll respond. I know you can handle it, but there are things I can't risk, and people I can't sacrifice. Don't go looking for me, I'll get in touch.$J0L0Lukash.
sid_notes_SQ103_ZombieDocNote2_body: $J0L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault>If you have the Emitter, go to the Plant, the old place. You'll get your instructions there.</NoteDefault></NoteBody>$J0L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault>– Lukash.</NoteDefault></NoteBody>
sid_notes_SQ103_ZombieDocNote2_footer: $J0L0<NoteBody><NoteItalic>(map has two places marked: one near the Plant, with a handwritten note saying "instructions here", the other near the Electric field and "bring Emitter here")</NoteItalic></NoteBody>
sid_notes_SQ103_ZombieDocNote_body: $J0L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault>Kovalenko, always double-check if the room with the electrical system emergency reboot control is closed! Nestor's courier saw a big red button and couldn't resist. The door unlocked, the equipment powered down, and the zombies all scattered – half my work day went out the window!</NoteDefault></NoteBody>
sid_notes_SQ103_ZombieStalkerNote_body: $J0L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault>Checked my part of the lab, there's no damn code there. Check yours out. If there's nothing, let's get out of here.</NoteDefault></NoteBody>$J0L0<NoteBody><NoteItalic>Below is a photograph of a basic sketch of the laboratory with some notes on it.</NoteItalic></NoteBody>
sid_notes_SQ10_DubnyjPDANote_body:
$J0L0Dubny: How are Bluish and Jumper holding up?
Quiet: What do you expect?
Quiet: They’re fine.
Quiet: This is the place for them. Seriously.
sid_notes_SQ10_DubnyjPDANote_hint: From Dubny’s PDA, found on Wild Island
sid_notes_SQ10_HogDubniiTalk_body:
$J0L0Dubny: We’ve got a controller at the WPS. It’s urgent. Can you handle it?
Hog: Standard fee, 50% upfront.
Dubny: Payment’s on the way.
Hog: We’ll take care of it. Though it’s a solid half-day’s walk from Rostok to your neck of the woods.
Dubny: Hurry. Please.
Dubny: The order covers the controller, its zombies, and any potential witnesses.
Dubny: If any of our crew are unaffected, help them. Eliminate the others.
Hog: Understood. Your Noontide secrets are safe with us. We’re on our way.
sid_notes_SQ10_JumperSinjaiTalk_body:
$J0L0Jumper: How about a smoke break?
Bluish: What about the safety rules?
Jumper: Screw ’em! North’s our boss. He’s got all the keys. And he still keeps running up to the roof to get some fresh air.
Bluish: But I don’t want to run. I’m fed up with this work, and you know it.
Jumper: Not this again! We’re not abandoning the guys. Not gonna happen. We’re better than that.
Jumper: All right. Enough griping. Meet you in five.
sid_notes_SQ10_MonolithStashNote_body: $J0L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault> I’m taking my share and leaving. I know you won’t be happy about me dealing in relics of the past, Strider, but for me, this marks the beginning of a better future! I swear I’ll make it up to you big time once I strike it rich – ten times over, in coupons! Don’t hold it against me. </NoteDefault></NoteBody>
sid_notes_SQ10_MonolithStashNote_hint: A note found inside the Monolith stash at the WPS
sid_notes_SQ10_SeverPDANote_body: $J0L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault> Rumor has it that Faust’s followers may have smuggled a Monolith object onto the station in violation of established protocols. No hard evidence of the object being installed or venerated on-site so far. Running discreet inquiries into the veracity of these claims to avoid alerting the perpetrators prematurely. </NoteDefault></NoteBody>
sid_notes_SQ10_SeverPDANote_hint: Report from the shift supervisor, found at the WPS
sid_notes_SQ18_KamysDeadbodyPDANote_body:
$J0L0Sultan: It’s been a while, Ringmaster. Gotta sit down and talk shop.
Ringmaster: Word is, you’re dealing with some heat. I heard about Miss Kalyna.
Sultan: So what? My issues with Kalyna are my business, not yours. We got our own.
Sultan: I’ll send the coordinates. Don’t keep me waiting.
sid_notes_SQ18_KamysDeadbodyPDANote_name: Invitation for a Meeting with the Sultan
sid_notes_SQ18_KamysPDANote_body: $J0L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault> Hello, Ringmaster. I need a favor from you. Arrange a little show for someone special. Four stalkers will be passing through tomorrow, loaded with weapons and gear. The poor bastards are gonna be real tired. You take them in, prep them for the show, and send a ticket to Beard. And remember, keep this hush-hush, just between you and me. </NoteDefault></NoteBody>
sid_notes_SQ18_KamysPDANote_hint: Note from the Ringmaster’s bandit camp
sid_notes_SQ18_KamysPDANote_name: Request to the Ringmaster from Unknown
sid_notes_SQ20_Note_body:
$J0L02011 Oleh Fiend (KIA)
2012 Marked One (This one valued North more than Arena)
2013 Barin (Good fighter, Freedom got him.)
2014 –
2015 –
2016 Bes
2017 Bes (Lost)
2018 Sanya Kugelblitz
2019 Tyrant (He did the previous champ beautifully! Boom! Headshot.)
2020 Tyrant
2021 Tyrant?! (Honestly, get lost already!)
sid_notes_SQ47_Note_DavidAkopan_PDA_body:
$J0L0[12:30] Hertz: Same time at the stash tonight?
[12:33] David: Let’s do it tomorrow. There’s this gig at the Slag Heap from a guy named Zakhar Babay. I’ll take care of it and report back. $J0L0[17:15] Hertz: You still doing that Babay gig, David?
[17:17] Hertz: Folks say he’s up to some sketchy stuff. Watch out for an ambush. Check in when you can. $J0L0[19:01] Hertz: You didn’t walk into that shit-show, did you?
sid_notes_SQ47_Note_DavidAkopan_PDA_hint: PDA found on a stalker’s body
sid_notes_SQ47_Note_DavidAkopan_PDA_name: David Akopyan’s Chat with His Buddy
sid_notes_SQ47_Note_LevaBorzoj_PDA_body: $J0L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault> I’m passing a dry law just for you, you prick. You blabbed about my stash at the bar again! I’m fed up with shifting it every damn month. You wag your tongue one more time, and I’ll cut it off! </NoteDefault></NoteBody>
sid_notes_SQ47_Note_LevaBorzoj_PDA_hint: PDA found on the body of Zakhar Babay’s thug
sid_notes_SQ87_AdikPedalPDANote_body:
$J0L0 Yo, listen the fuck up – keep tabs on them stalkers, and don’t hand them any grub. I stowed the key up on the roof, but it’s off-limits, capisce?
Now, about our gig: I had a chitchat with some folks, now we play the waiting game. While they’re dukin’ it out in Zaton, we’re gonna be runnin’ the whole damn show. Big things comin’, ya dig?
Oh, and stay the hell away from the pier at the Fish Farm. That nasty anomaly turned Hoary into mush. Ain’t fuckin pretty.
sid_notes_SQ87_AdikPedalPDANote_hint: PDA from a bandit’s body at the Fish Farm
sid_notes_SQ87_ZenaGlybaPDANote_Alternat:
$J0L0Zhenya Hulk: I need help, Illyukha.
Zhenya Hulk: Some thugs got us locked up. No food, no water. Can’t hold out much longer.
Zhenya Hulk: Sent you the coordinates.
Illyukha Redhead: I’ll be there in two days. Can you hang on for that long?
Zhenya Hulk: Not like I got a choice. Can’t reach anyone on the radio. Some loner iced the bandits, and then he just took off, the bastard. Now we’re stuck here, and there’s nobody to let us out.
Illyukha Redhead: Gotcha. On my way. $J0L0Zhenya Hulk: The guys are out cold. I’m alone. Where you at?
Illyukha Redhead: Fuck, I took a shortcut and bumped into some mutants. Gotta fix myself up and press on.
Zhenya Hulk: Hurry up. It’s pretty bad over here. $J0L0Zhenya Hulk: Illyukha?
PDA from a stalker’s body at the Fish Farm

$J0L0Zhenya Hulk: Got a problem. We’re running late.
Illyukha Redhead: What happened?
Zhenya Hulk: Some fly-by goons jumped us. Got us holed up, demanding cash.
Illyukha Redhead: Need backup?
Zhenya Hulk: Nah, we’re covered. Help’s on the way. They’ll show these punks who’s boss around here.
Illyukha Redhead: Covered? Ha! Those Zaton crooks played you. One thug ain’t gonna duke it out with another over some loner.
Zhenya Hulk: Guess we’ll find out.
PDA taken from a Stalker’s body at the Fish Farm
sid_notes_SQ87_ZenaGlybaPDANote_body:
$J0L0Zhenya Hulk: We got jumped, Sultan. Make sure your boys know we’re under your protection.
Sultan: Shit happens. Fine, I’ll sort it out. Who did it?
Zhenya Hulk: Adik Pedal and his goons. They’re shaking us down for coupons.
Sultan: Doesn’t ring a bell. So… tourists, huh? That’s against the rules. Okay, I’ll send my guys. Give me your coordinates.
sid_notes_SQ87_ZenaGlybaPDANote_hint: PDA from a stalker’s body at the Fish Farm
sid_notes_SQ88_GrebenNoteText_body:
$J0L0A couple more weeks to go. I treated Machek and Poluyanov to the good stuff today. They stared at my burned ugly face, unable to recognize the man they’d condemned to a radioactive tomb in Chornobyl for a measly couple thousand coupons. $J0L0 Those scumbags knew it all. They knew the place was empty – there was nothing there, only death. Yet they sold us the key, laughing at us. Today, I returned the favor, grinning right back. It was the last thing they ever saw. $J0L0Just a week left. I can’t sleep. Every time I close my eyes, I hear a gunshot echoing. Sledgehammer grabs the anti-rad and walks away. I can’t move, just like back then in Chornobyl. The same feeling, over and over again. $J0L0Only a couple of days left. I awoke to a violent cough, spitting blood all across the floor. All I want is to hold on until the soldiers put a bullet in Sledgehammer’s forehead. I’d love to watch him suffer from radiation sickness like I am, but time is running out. $J0L0Pvt. Machek
Pvt. Poluyanov
Pvt. Jason
Lt. Novak
Sledgehammer
sid_notes_SQ88_GrebenNoteText_hint: PDA from a stalker’s body in Chornobyl-2
sid_notes_SQ88_KuvaldaDiaryText_body: $J0L0<NoteBody><NoteBold>September 10. </NoteBold><NoteDefault>Jason and Novak – two artifacts (payment received). We’d agreed on a new shipment, but they’ve been reassigned from external patrol duty to guard the warehouse under the Duga. Machek and Poluyanov will fill in for them. </NoteDefault></NoteBody>$J0L0<NoteBody><NoteBold>October 3. </NoteBold><NoteDefault>The clients didn’t show up for the meeting. Novak says they never made it back to base either. On my way home, I heard someone strumming a guitar. I followed the sound, but there was no one there. Better check the tripwires. </NoteDefault></NoteBody>
sid_notes_SQ88_KuvaldaDiaryText_hint: Diary of a dead stalker in Chornobyl-2
sid_notes_SQ88_MacekPDANote_body:
$J0L0Machek: I got a few days leave. We’re heading out. But let’s move our meeting place away from the kindergarten.
Guitarist: Any complications?
Machek: We’re supposed to meet Sledgehammer, too. If he spots us, we’ll have some explaining to do. Let’s keep it under wraps for now.
Guitarist: Fine by me. Novak’s still in the loop?
Machek: Yeah, no changes there. His patrol’s rolling through the kindergarten, so he’s gonna check in with you.
sid_notes_SQ88_MacekPDANote_hint: PDA from a soldier’s body in Chornobyl-2
sid_notes_SQ88_NimbleToBeardNote_body:
$J0L0 Beard, I got my boys to spill the beans. Sledgehammer pissed off to Chornobyl-2 with that freaking buddy of his. Folks say he's done fetching you artifacts for next to nothing. So I reckon that's it, you lost your golden goose.
Eh, one more thing. That vodka you gave me to "break the ice" wasn't enough. Had to share my own. So you owe me two more of 'em bottles.
sid_notes_SQ89_ZhgutNote_body:
$J0L0Wisp: Listen, man… been thinkin’ about your offer. Count me in.
Swedish: Hell yeah, that’s what I’m talkin’ about! Together, we’ll show Sidor who’s boss!
Wisp: All right. Where’s your trading post? Where do I drop off the goods?
Swedish: Head over to the western railroad tunnel. You know the spot?
Wisp: Yeah, I think so. I’ll check my old stashes first and see if there’s anything else worth selling. How’s security? I hope you’re ready for anything?
Swedish: Yeah, we’ve got three volunteers already. Couple more on their way. Need any backup? We can send a guy.
Wisp: That’s not too much. You better have ’em on standby – the post has to be secure at all times. Need more manpower, too, that’s for sure. I’ll ask around the village.
sid_notes_SQ89_ZhgutNote_hint: Wisp and Swedish discuss shipments and security
sid_notes_SQ90_PlanOfTheAttack_body: $J0L0<NoteBody><NoteBold>Freedom member: </NoteBold><NoteDefault>We’re almost there. I’ve got the key. The door on the second floor opens up to the tunnels that’ll take us right to the bandits’ den. We’ll slip in quiet and take ’em out before they even know what hit ’em. Don’t worry. They’ll never see it coming. </NoteDefault></NoteBody>
sid_notes_SQ90_PlanOfTheAttack_hint: PDA from a Freedom member’s body at the Parking Lot
sid_notes_SQ90_PlanOfTheAttack_name: Plan for a Freedom Assault on a Bandit Base
sid_notes_SQ90_TalkAboutAnomalyUnderBase:
$J0L0 Wig: How come you keep pokin’ around that tunnel near the anomaly? I warned you to stay the hell away.
Bustler: There’s an artifact bouncin’ around in there, man. It’s practically callin’ my name. Shit, wish I could snag it somehow. A little insurance for the future, you get me?
Wig: Getting mixed up in this Zone crap ain’t a thief’s game. But I like where your head’s at. We gotta figure out how to work this.
Bustler: But how? I’m not setting foot in there. No way!
Wig: You won’t have to. We’ll grab some schmuck, apply a little pressure, and tell him to fetch us a couple artifacts if he wants to keep breathing. He’ll keep bringing ’em until he keels over. Then we’ll find another one. We’ll split the loot… and stash half for the Boss, of course.
Bustler: Damn, that’s slick, man. Now we just need the Boss to sign off on it.
Wig: Long as we butter him up nicely, he won’t have a problem with it.
PDA from a bandit’s body at the Containers
sid_notes_SQ90_TalkAboutPseudogiant_body:
$J0L0Mushroom: We got a bit of a problem, Boss.
Boss: What’d ya do this time?
Mushroom: Last night, the guys at the gate got plastered and passed out. One of ’em must’ve left some munchies out, and now we’ve got a real huge critter rummaging around out there.
Boss: Then blow it the hell away.
Mushroom: We tried! The fucking freak’s built like a tank. Ran us ragged until we herded it into a container. That’s where we’ve got it trapped.
Boss: Fine, then leave it there to rot. What’s all this got to do with me?
Mushroom: It went after the new guy. Can’t remember his name. So, uh… the kid’s a goner.
Boss: Tough break. Let those idiots know that if they’re ever caught drunk on duty again, they’ll wake up in those containers themselves. And they won’t come out alive.
sid_notes_SQ90_TalkAboutPseudogiant_hint: PDA from a bandit’s body at the Containers
sid_notes_SQ90_TalkAboutRewardForBanditH:
$J0L0Boss: Listen up. Got word that Myklukha’s gunning for us. The bounty’s pretty hefty, so expect some visitors. Double up the patrols, make sure everyone’s strapped, and stay on your toes.
Tyoma Wig: Guess we roughed his boys up real nice. Your “good friend” has been a real asset. Mind telling us who he is? Wouldn’t wanna off him along with those Freedom guys on a whoopsie.
Boss: Don’t sweat it. He’s from the Cement Factory, so he’s in the clear. Just keep your trap shut about it.
PDA from the body of a gang leader’s lieutenant
sid_notes_SQ90_TalkWithSnitch_body:
$J0L0Hera: Got something for you. Myklukha sent a team after you. They’re planning to sneak into your base through the caves.
Boss: Gotcha. We’ll set up an ambush. How much do I owe you for this?
Hera: Nothing, like always. Just make sure you handle that Freedom scum.
Boss: Ah, come on. I’m not one for handouts. If you don’t want coupons, I can send a couple of my boys over to the Cement Factory posing as loners. They’ll get you a case of vodka or something.
Hera: If they set foot in here, I’ll put ’em down. That’s all I gotta say. I’ll give you a heads-up if I hear anything else.
sid_notes_SQ90_TalkWithSnitch_hint: PDA from a gang leader’s body at the Containers
sid_notes_SQ92_MangustPDAText_body:
$J0L0 Dr. Almatov: Hello, Mongoose. Let me remind you of your assignment. You are to set-up two anomalous activity sensors and start them simultaneously. It won’t be too tricky – just make sure you don’t place them anywhere near mutants, otherwise there might be unintended consequences.
Mongoose: The fuck you talkin’ ’bout?! What “unintended consequences?”
Dr. Almatov: There is a chance that activating the devices could attract mutants in the vicinity and make them more aggressive. Since the devices’ range is quite significant, well… there may be a lot of mutants. The person who activates the second device is taking most of the risk. But still, this is merely a possibility.
Mongoose: And where do you suggest I find a place in the Zone without any mutants? How about we activate the devices inside Malachite? Dr. Almatov: No, I need data obtained in the field. I don’t expect any problems. Just have your partner close by to cover you. That’s all you need.
Mongoose: Dimon’s dead. Killed by Wardens. Whatever. I’ll think of somethin’.
sid_notes_SQ92_MangustPDAText_hint: From a dead Sparker’s PDA found near the Chemical Plant
sid_notes_SQ92_VolkovPDAText_body:
$J0L0Sgt. Volkov: Hey, Myron. Could you cover my next patrol shift? The one to the Elevator?
Sgt. Myronov: Why?
Sgt. Volkov: I don’t know, man, that place just gives me the creeps. We shot a stalker there the other day, but we didn’t get his partner. And I’ve got a weird feeling somebody’s been watching me there ever since.
Sgt. Myronov: Oh, come on. Who’d dare to attack you, with all your gear and combat experience? But whatever – I’ll cover for you for three grand if you want.
Sgt. Volkov: Seriously? I’m asking you as a friend!
Sgt. Myronov: That’s where you’re wrong. I’m not your friend, I’m just a fellow soldier. And money is exactly why I joined the Ward in the first place. So you can send me the coupons, or you can say hi to the Elevator for me. Your choice.
sid_notes_SQ92_VolkovPDAText_hint: From a dead Warden’s PDA found near the Chemical Plant
sid_notes_SQ94_DogmasterDairy_body:
$J0L0Day one. Had to climb up boxes, so the pack wouldn't get me. They can sniff canned grub if you open it. Pal was careful, but took a bite.
Day two. Apparently, I smell tastier than sausage. Barely made it out.
Day three. Took a whole ruck of canned meat, enough for a week – I'll try to lure Pal out. Got a couple of military-grade first aid kits, just in case.
sid_notes_SQ94_DogmasterPDA_body:
$J0L0Alabai: Honta, I can't do this job. I made a friend here.
Honta: Meaning? How does that make sense?
Alabai: Heard of Noah? I'm almost like him now – a dog saved me from a rodent. Can't bring myself to kill blind dogs any more. If I tame him, he'll make a good hunter!
Honta: You were lucky, stupid. There's a whole pack, and you won't tame them all. Don't be dumb. Come back.
sid_notes_SQ94_GontaPDA_body: $J0L0<NoteBody><NoteBold>Viktoria Dorozhnyuk: </NoteBold><NoteDefault>Honta, this is no good. Time is of the essence. I'm counting on you.</NoteDefault></NoteBody>$J0L0<NoteBody><NoteBold>Honta:</NoteBold><NoteDefault> I'll get it to you tomorrow morning, maybe even tonight. It won't be just me hunting from now on, so it'll be easier.</NoteDefault></NoteBody>$J0L0<NoteBody><NoteBold>Viktoria Dorozhnyuk: </NoteBold><NoteDefault>Are you sure? I don't have any field operatives I can send out to collect material without risk. If I don't have the organs tomorrow, I'll have to send whoever I can.</NoteDefault></NoteBody>$J0L0<NoteBody><NoteBold>Honta: </NoteBold><NoteDefault>Don't put your people at risk. I'll get it for you.</NoteDefault></NoteBody>
sid_notes_SQ94_ZuzaPDA_body: $J0L0<NoteBody><NoteBold>Private Illienko: </NoteBold><NoteDefault>The package is in the usual spot, but I can't promise I'll be able to get your meds next time.</NoteDefault></NoteBody>$J0L0<NoteBody><NoteBold>Yaryk Zhuzha: </NoteBold><NoteDefault>Thank you. There won't be a next time.</NoteDefault></NoteBody>$J0L0<NoteBody><NoteBold>Private Illienko: </NoteBold><NoteDefault>“Won't be”? Haven't you considered that maybe your doctors made a mistake? And this “you only got six months” is bullshit?</NoteDefault></NoteBody>$J0L0<NoteBody><NoteBold>Yaryk Zhuzha: </NoteBold><NoteDefault>Sasha, don't bother. How much for the meds?</NoteDefault></NoteBody>$J0L0<NoteBody><NoteBold>Private Illienko: </NoteBold><NoteDefault>You offer me money again and I'll punch you in the face. I'm deleting this chat.</NoteDefault></NoteBody>
sid_notes_SQ94_ZuzaPDA_name: Yaryk Zhuzha's chat with an IPSF soldier
sid_notes_Shevchenko_Hub_BeardSpartacus_:
$J0L0Spartak: Welcome home, I guess. I heard you made it all the way to Prypiat. No place like Zaton in the whole Zone, ha?
Beard: Zone gossip spreads like radiation. Gets everywhere.
Spartak: Ah, come on. How long has it been since Sultan stole Skadovsk? So, you here because of old memories or old grudges?
Beard: Home is supposed to be the place you come back to.
Spartak: Watch out, those bandits are still in charge. You cross them, you're good as dead.
Beard: Maybe, that's what I wanna be. Good.
Messages from an old PDA found at the Shevchenko
sid_notes_Shevchenko_Hub_RepairNote_body: $J0L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault>Bobber, stand down! Can you believe it, didn't need to ask nobody, folks scraped it up on their own.</NoteDefault></NoteBody>$J0L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault>Shaggy, thank the sweet fuck, managed to get rad-free APC plates. Logger, that sneaky little shit, nicked some IPSF sealant. And I am the proud bastard who put all that crap on. So, no more shipwrecks when it rains!</NoteDefault></NoteBody>
sid_notes_Shevchenko_Hub_RepairNote_hint: A note to the bartender from Beard's ship
sid_notes_Shevchenko_NoteBoroda_body: $J0L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault>List of essentials:</NoteDefault></NoteBody>$J0L2<NoteBody><ListItem><NoteStrike>PTM (29 out of 29).</NoteStrike></ListItem><ListItem><NoteDefault>Sawed-off shotgun (11 out of 15). </NoteDefault><NoteItalic>Hamster promised delivery by the end of the week.</NoteItalic></ListItem><ListItem><NoteDefault>AKM-74U (8 out of 11).</NoteDefault></ListItem><ListItem><NoteDefault>SVDM-2 (1 out of 4).</NoteDefault></ListItem><ListItem><NoteDefault>RGD-5 Grenades (33 out of 60). </NoteDefault><NoteItalic>Negotiate with Sidorovich.</NoteItalic></ListItem></NoteBody>
sid_notes_Shevchenko_NoteBoroda_name: List of Equipment for the Shevchenko
sid_notes_Shevchenko_NoteDiary_body:
$J0L0Day 47. Found a few promising trails today. Not many artifacts, but the paths seem clear of bandits for now.
Day 53. There was a major Emission a couple of days back. Checked an old route today, and the anomaly there seems to have vanished. Need to update my map. $J0L0Day 84. The Sultan asked me to guide his thugs. Well, more like demanded, given the debt I owe him. This is my chance to settle my tab. He said he’ll make it worth my while. Let’s hope for a smooth ride this time.
sid_notes_Shevchenko_NoteDiary_hint: Notes on a sheet of paper found on the Shevchenko
sid_notes_Sultansk_NoteAgreement_body:
$J0L0 1. Effective immediately, the Skadovsk will be rechristened the Sultansk. With the Sultan as the kingpin, the vessel will function as an assembly point and a place of recreation for his buddies.
2. Beard’s crew is permitted to visit the Sultansk and conduct business aboard her, albeit at a premium of 20% above bandit rates. Should any stalker fail to meet his obligations to the Sultan, Beard can assume his debts and pay the coupons he owes. Late payments accrue an additional 5% charge per day.
3. The Sultan commits to addressing unwarranted attacks by his buddies against loners. Any unauthorized showdowns will result in punishment for the participants.
4. The Sultan permits Beard to depart unharmed and establish a new base for his associates. Both parties commit to respecting each other’s turf and conducting business honestly. Should they team up against a common enemy, any resulting profits will be shared at a ratio of 70% for the Sultan and 30% for Beard.

Both parties pledge to comply with these rules and uphold the agreements outlined above.
sid_notes_Sultansk_NoteAgreement_header: $J0L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault> Spheres of Influence in the Swamps </NoteDefault></NoteBody>
sid_notes_Sultansk_NoteAgreement_hint: A paper from the Sultan’s personal belongings
sid_notes_Sultansk_NoteAgreement_name: Contract between Beard and the Sultan
sid_notes_Sultansk_NoteKalinaDebt_body: $J0L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault> I, Oleksandr Budko, promise to settle my outstanding debt to Ms. Sofiya Avramenko within three months. </NoteDefault></NoteBody>
sid_notes_Sultansk_NoteKalinaDebt_footer: $J2L0<NoteBody><NoteItalic>He’s past due. Tell Chisel to have a word with him. And give him some ammo, just in case.</NoteItalic></NoteBody>
sid_notes_Sultansk_NoteKalinaList_body:
$J0L0 Monday:
1. Secure an alternate vodka supplier (three poisoning incidents, people refuse to drink it, down 13k coupons).
2. Talk prices with Quiet (he loves sardines – don’t forget to bring some).
3. Talk to the Ringmaster (it’s about time).
4. Send a present to Sidor.
5. Wash the handkerchiefs.
sid_notes_Sultansk_NoteKalinaList_hint: Papers found at the bar on the Sultansk
sid_notes_Sultansk_NoteKalinaSituation_b:
$J0L0 Food for thought:
1. Situation on Icarus – find out what’s going on, dispatch stalkers for reconnaissance.
2. New bilateral treaty with Myklukha – draft a plan.
3. Cold Island – ?
4. Add one more group of troublemakers to the Slag Heap – last push.
5. Loyalty checks – identify troublemakers.
sid_notes_Sultansk_NoteKalinaSituation_h: A sheet of paper found on the Sultansk
sid_notes_Sultansk_NoteKalinaSituation_n: Sonya Kalyna’s Plans for the Future
sid_notes_Sultansk_NoteParty_body: $J0L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault> For the celebration: </NoteDefault></NoteBody>$J0L1<NoteBody><ListItem><NoteDefault>Vodka: </NoteDefault><NoteStrike>1</NoteStrike><NoteDefault> 2 bottles per person</NoteDefault></ListItem><ListItem><NoteDefault>Beer: 3 bottles per person</NoteDefault></ListItem><ListItem><NoteDefault>Sausage: 1 per person</NoteDefault></ListItem><ListItem><NoteDefault>Canned food: 2 cans per person</NoteDefault></ListItem><ListItem><NoteDefault>Bread: 2 loaves per person</NoteDefault></ListItem><ListItem><NoteDefault>Condensed milk: 1 can per person</NoteDefault></ListItem><ListItem><NoteDefault>Shared food stash: canned food and vegetables</NoteDefault></ListItem></NoteBody>
sid_notes_Sultansk_NoteParty_hint: A list found at the bar on the Sultansk
sid_notes_Sultansk_NoteZlotar_body: $J0L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault> So I figured… sticking around here is a waste of time. The Sultan’s out of the picture, and for real, he never did much for us anyway. I say we hit the road for Rostok tomorrow and take it from there. </NoteDefault></NoteBody>
sid_notes_Sultansk_NoteZlotar_hint: A note found on the Sultansk
sid_notes_Sultansk_PDABanditStalker_body:
$J0L0Vitya Chilik: Guess what? I’ve teamed up with a loner.
Vitya Chilik: Mutants give me the heebie-jeebies, and he’s got zero nose for loot. He could stroll past a stash a hundred times and still not catch a whiff of it.
Vitya Chilik: So we cooked up a plan: I find the goodies, and he takes care of the mutants lurking around. Then we divvy up the loot fifty-fifty.
Vitya Chilik: Who knew these loners would ever come in handy!
sid_notes_Sultansk_PDABanditStalker_hint: A bandit’s PDA found at the bar on the Sultansk
sid_notes_Sultansk_PDABandit_body:
$J0L0Valik Lax: Are you heading to the bar soon?
Ashot Fugitive: Why? You gonna pour me a cold one?
Valik Lax: I’m about to turn cold myself. The stalkers I mugged yesterday are here. The fuckers know it was me.
Valik Lax: Get the hell over here!
sid_notes_Sultansk_PDABandit_hint: From a dead bandit’s PDA near the Sultansk
sid_notes_Sultansk_PDABandit_name: Chat between Two Bandits from Zaton
sid_notes_Sultansk_PDAKalina_body:
$J0L0Rubenchik: Myklukha’s asking about the shipment. When’s the next one due?
Sonya Kalyna: I’ve already told him — by the end of the week.
Sonya Kalyna: Seems like he’s losing it. Or maybe he’s just antsy?
Rubenchik: The local fences keep bothering him. Like, we’re out to screw him over or something.
Sonya Kalyna: We haven’t double-crossed him in two years, and they think we’re going to now?
Sonya Kalyna: Fine. I need info on those fences. They need mending.
sid_notes_Sultansk_PDAKalina_hint: From Sonya Kalyna’s PDA on the Sultansk
sid_notes_Sultansk_PDASultanBoroda_body:
$J0L0[17:45] Beard: We need to talk, Sultan.
[1:46]Beard: This can’t wait.
[18:05]Beard: Talk to me. This is urgent.
[22:08] Sultan: What’s going on? The Shevchenko’s on fire?
[22:11]Sultan: Well? Did you put it out, or what?
[23:10]Sultan: What’s the matter? Cat got your tongue?
sid_notes_Sultansk_PDASultanBoroda_name: The Sultan’s Chat with an Old Rival
sid_notes_Sultansk_PDASultanSych_body:
$J0L0Sultan: Well?
Owl: He’s still twiddling his thumbs aboard the Shevchenko.
Owl: You better mind your own ship.
Owl: Lyokha Corps-de-ballet is poaching your men for the Slag Heap.
Sultan: Where the Ward struts around like they own the place?
Sultan: Let him have ’em. I won’t shed a tear for those deadbeats.
sid_notes_Terricon_NestorPDANote_body: $J0L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault> This freak thinks he can take me for a ride like this? It's been a long time since we've had a real shooter in the Garbage This time I don't even have to call the Benefactor, I'll just take ten guys. No, five will do, or that punk will think I'm afraid to talk to him. </NoteDefault></NoteBody>
sid_notes_Terricon_PDAAboutFriend_body: $J0L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault> It seems your buddy’s in a bit of a pickle. If you’re up for sorting it out, swing by the Sultansk, and we’ll have a chat. </NoteDefault></NoteBody>
sid_notes_Terricon_PDAAboutFriend_hint: From a PDA left unattended at the Slag Heap
sid_notes_YanovDeggyToNor_body: $J0L0Nor,$J0L0You listen up: I don't buy the story about Monolith never coming back. It is more than just stupid, it is dangerous. In my absence, I forbid to reduce the number of patrol teams. We have to be ready. Always. $J0L0
sid_notes_Yanov_Note_Hana_body:
$J0L0 This week’s shopping list:
1. RP-74M: 2
2. Dnipro: 8
3. 5.45x39mm: 3 boxes of 7N6, 1 box of 7N10
4. Energy drinks: 4 boxes
5. Armor: remind Myklukha about PSZ-12
6. Grenades: 2 boxes of RGD, 1 box of F-1
sid_notes_Yanov_Note_Hana_hint: From the Yaniv trader’s personal belongings
sid_notes_Yanov_Note_Order_body:
$J0L0 Permission granted for entry into Prypiat for a group of five stalkers:
1. Valya Hook
2. Senya Smoke
3. Leva Roundelay
4. Mykola Marksman
5. Vitya Silent $J0L0 Any changes to the list are prohibited.
sid_notes_Yanov_Note_ToDoList_body:
$J0L01. Get the gun fixed.
2. Buy some ammo. Negotiate for a better price?
3. Buy some vodka. Note: No drinking until back from Prypiat!
4. Buy anti-rad.
sid_notes_Yanov_PDA_AboutGarik_body:
$J0L0Vitya Rudko: You ever think Garry might be losing it, Kostya? The dude keeps saying “we” when he’s all by himself.
Kostya Bandura: You flunked the rookie test for Yaniv! He’s always taking someone around the Zone. That’s why he talks like he’s not alone. That’s my theory anyway.
sid_notes_Yanov_PDA_Lost_body:
$J0L0[17:40] Senya Smoke: Set the table! I’ve got some artifacts that even Wreck would pay a fortune for!
[17:42] Mykola Marksman: Whoa, man! Can’t wait for ya to get back!
[19:01] Mykola Marksman: So how long do we have to wait?
sid_notes_Yanov_PDA_Lost_hint: PDA from a stalker’s body in Yaniv
sid_notes_Zalesie_BatyaPDANotes_body:
$J0L0Lens: Sure, Hamster’s a prick, I’ll give you that. But kicking him out means we’ll be screwed for at least a month. No spare parts, no food… or meds. Yet another week without meds.
Lens: Look, I ain’t backin’ him up. Heard every damn word myself. But before we boot his sorry ass, we need someone else lined up to fill his shoes.
sid_notes_Zalesie_BatyaPDANotes_hint: PDA found among Gaffer’s personal belongings
sid_notes_Zalesie_ConderPDANote_body:
$J0L0Chapa Grumpy: Too many Wardens sniffing around. All our diggers are shitting their pants. Asking if they really need to stay here.
Diode: Once we finish our job around Slag Heap, we can sit on our asses anywhere in the Zone and have anything we want.
Diode: If you wanna piss of, be my guest. Won't have to cut you in.
sid_notes_Zalesie_ConderPDANote_hint: Messages on a lost digger's PDA
sid_notes_Zalesie_HomakPDANote2_body:
$J0L0[14:55] Unknown: The coupons have been sent, as promised. Don’t drag this out, or there won’t be an advance next time.
[15:16] Hamster: No advance, no deal. The pills oughtta be here this week.
[15:17] Unknown: You said Monday!
[15:45] Hamster: Not everything’s up to me, bud. Don’t push it.
sid_notes_Zalesie_KoldunNote1_body:
$J0L0 I’ve chosen not to speak openly about his, but hear me out.
I’ve had enough.
Bring this up in public one more time, and we’ll have a different kind of conversation. We stand by our own. Always have, always will.
I hope that got through to you.
sid_notes_Zalesie_KoldunNote1_hint: A note found in Warlock’s bar
sid_notes_Zalesie_KoldunPDANote2_body:
$J0L0[22:11] Unknown: Having someone who grasps the real power dynamics in charge of Zalissya would make things easier.
[22:12] Warlock: I’m not going to talk about it.
[23:45] Unknown: You don’t have to talk about it.
[23:45] Unknown: But maybe you ought to mull it over.
sid_notes_Zalesie_LinzaPDANote_body:
$J0L0 Isn’t it ridiculous that we’re writing to each other when we live right next door? Like some fucking lovebirds or something…
Look, I know you feel guilty about my… condition. That’s why you never visit me here. But I chose to go into that lab on my own. It was my decision, you get it? You were just being a good guide, doing your job. Stop blaming yourself. You should be proud you got me out of there, you old coot.
Anyhow, drop by sometime. Let’s catch up like we used to. It’s lonely as hell here. At least tell me what’s happening on the other side of that fence.
sid_notes_Zalesie_NikolaichNote_body:
$J0L0 This shit actually works! Put it on a fresh wound, and it closes up right then and there! I tested it on myself and the bleeding stopped in seconds – two hours later, there wasn’t even a scar left!
Now to see if it works on old injuries.
If it does, Lens’ll be all over me!
If he can catch me, that is!
sid_notes_Zalesie_NikolaichNote_hint: Notes found among the personal belongings of Zalissya’s medic
sid_notes_Zalesie_NoteClosedDoor_body:
$J0L0DO NOT ENTER
5 Curiosity killed the 8 guys before you
sid_notes_Zalesie_NoteClosedDoor_hint: A sign written on a crumpled piece of paper
sid_notes_Zalesie_NoteClosedDoor_name: A piece of paper on a restricted door at Slag Heap
sid_notes_Zalesie_NoteGravestone_body:
$J0L0Another anniversary, brother.
Don't even think about it, we haven't forgotten how you jumped at those Monolithians and saved us all. And I haven't forgotten it should have been me.
I've seen enough of all this. And you were so young, you would've liked our new Zalissya.
sid_notes_Zalesie_NoteGravestone_hint: Found at the graveyard near Zalissya
sid_notes_Zalesie_NoteGravestone_name: A note from the burial cross
sid_notes_e01_mq01_ssp_memo_body:
$J0L0 All personnel employed by the International Perimeter Security Force (IPSF) must comply with the following standards outlined in the Code of Professional Ethics: $J0L2Diligently perform service and combat duties within the scope of their authority, including:
- ensuring the safety and security of the Perimeter from threats posed by hostile fauna (referred to as “mutants”) and armed groups
- preventing the unauthorized crossing of the Perimeter by individuals and vehicles
- providing assistance to approved scientific expeditions
- assisting adjacent IPSF units within the area of responsibility
- carrying out any additional duties as directed by IPSF Command.Adhere to the principles of impartiality, prioritizing the mission and principles of the IPSF over personal objectives (whether material, ethical, or otherwise) or the interests of third parties.Refrain from disclosing any classified information obtained in the line of duty.Understand that individual conduct reflects directly on the reputation and credibility of the IPSF.$J0L0 IPSF personnel are not permitted to: $J0L2Enter areas of anomalous activity (referred to as “anomalies”) except when required by official duty.Possess, acquire, or otherwise engage with anomalous formations (referred to as “artifacts”) except when required by official duty.Receive any form of compensation from any third party, including artifacts, unofficial electronic currency (referred to as “coupons”), currency, services, loans, etc.Transfer IPSF-owned property to any third party (see IPSF Statute pp. 108-115 for detailed regulations).
sid_notes_e01_mq01_ssp_memo_hint: Found on a soldier’s body in the tunnel
sid_notes_mutpartspack_AboutHunt_body:
$J0L0Anonymous: Howdy! How are you, man? Getting those burns treated?Anonymous: A little birdie told me merchants are paying for mutant "spares" again. Thinking of taking up hunting.
Anonymous: Why not? Take a small mutant apart, sell what you can. You'll get enough for a can of grub and a bottle of vodka. Beats jumping headfirst into anomalies and spending your pay on meds.
Anonymous: What I mean is, once you're up and running again, join me. Don't be a stranger, bro.
sid_notes_nesterenko_jokes_body: $J0L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault>Joke 1: Who is the best friend in the Zone? Those one, who will send the absolute stranger to find their commrade.</NoteDefault></NoteBody>$J0L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault>Joke 2: What the real circus is? It's when two bandits can't beat the loner enough to make him tell them the location of the stash.</NoteDefault></NoteBody>$J0L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault>Joke 3: And after all, who is the real zombie? those ones, whose brains were melted down, or the one, who are ready to go to the absolute asshole to find just nothing but the notes with some dad jokes</NoteDefault></NoteBody>
sid_notes_nesterenko_jokes_footer: $J0L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault>That's for your expensive and low-quality work, Khorundzhy.</NoteDefault></NoteBody>$J0L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault>Best regards,</NoteDefault></NoteBody>$J0L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault>Prof. Nesterenko</NoteDefault></NoteBody>
sid_notes_nesterenko_jokes_header: $J1L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault>The List of Stalker jokes</NoteDefault></NoteBody>$J1L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault>Gathered by Prof. Serhii Nesterenko</NoteDefault></NoteBody>
sid_notes_nvgpack_ACNQ27_BanditNote_body:
$J0L0Boys, word from Pickaxe. Some new smartasses are coming down to the tunnel to replace the dead ones. Round them all up, don’t let them through. Client wants a chat with them, so the less busted up they are, the fatter the payday.
For the slowpokes: no shooting. Not even in the knees! If that’s unclear – I’ll come knock it into you myself.
sid_notes_nvgpack_ACNQ27_BanditNote_name: Bandit's PDA from the tunnel at Yantar
sid_notes_nvgpack_supack_ANCQ25_BanzaiUn: $J0L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault>I've been chasing this frequency for a couple of weeks now, and I'm sure you'll be interested. Transmissions on it are able to negatively impact the listener's brain. If you're skeptical and </NoteDefault><NoteStrike>you think it's bullshit,</NoteStrike><NoteDefault> – I attached a recording fragment.</NoteDefault></NoteBody>$J0L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault>Haven't been able to listen for more than five seconds as I keep getting a nosebleed. There's gossip about others who listened to it, </NoteDefault><NoteStrike>all weird as hell.</NoteStrike><NoteDefault> Too weird to write about.</NoteDefault></NoteBody>$J0L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault>I've got some quality equipment here. The frequency characteristics </NoteDefault><NoteStrike>sure are fucking something – </NoteStrike><NoteDefault>nothing like I've ever seen. If you have brain impact experts, I'm ready to meet them. Give them the whole recording, graphs and everything. For free, 'cause I'm curious myself. </NoteDefault></NoteBody>$J0L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault>What do you think?</NoteDefault></NoteBody>
A scribbled letter found at Rostok
sid_notes_nvgpack_supack_ANCQ_26_Medulin:
$J0L0PROGRAM TYPE: Scheduled data transfer$J0L0STATUS:
Alpha package: researcher data archive – TRANSFERED
Beta package: technical documents – TRANSFERED
Gamma package: ХХХХХХХХХХ – PENDING TRANSFER$J0L0LOG IN TO VIEW DETAILS
LOG IN TO RESUME SESSION$J0L0USERNAME: DocMedulin
PASSWORD: _ _ _ _
Program launch report from Medulin's computer
sid_notes_nvgpack_supack_ANCQ_26_Scoutma:
$J0L0Name/nickname: refused to provide
Relevant experience: over 10 years
Psychological profile: extremely stable
Competence self-assessment (citing):"Radio transmission? Guys, if this turns out to be scarier than what I heard on my radio back in '88, I'll buy you all a drink".$J0L0Recommended to join NK-4 research group$J0L0Notes:
After the RG NK-4 incident, all potential surviving participants are STRONGLY NOT RECOMMENDED for independent field work. Close observation required.
A dead group survivor's personal file
sid_notes_return_clock_loot_note1_hint: Loot on one of the Pseudodogs
sid_notes_scientists_task_body: $J0L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault>Malakhit scientific community asks Khorundzhy's squad to bring us notes of prof. Nesterenko. The exact coordinates will be provided additionally.</NoteDefault></NoteBody>
sid_notes_supack_SQ100_Note_FinalWarning: $J0L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault>Sending the coordinates with the reward for your cooperation. You have to forget everything you saw at Kazkovyi – for your own safety.</NoteDefault></NoteBody>
A message about a strange radio wave story.
sid_notes_supack_SQ101_ShelmaBuh_body: $J0L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault>A neat pencil-drawn spreadsheet with columns "Customer name", "Item", "Price", "Skull's cut". The spreadsheet is filled with names of scientists and military-grade radio equipment items.</NoteDefault></NoteBody>
sid_notes_supack_SQ101_ShelmaBuh_hint: Business correspondence about radio spare parts.
sid_notes_supack_SQ95_Note_GroupList_bod: $J0L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault>NK-5 research group. Intercept and deliver to location alive, if not possible – eliminate them. Group members:</NoteDefault></NoteBody>$J0L1<NoteBody><ListItem><NoteDefault>Medulin – scientist, leader of the group;</NoteDefault></ListItem><ListItem><NoteDefault>Banzai – technician;</NoteDefault></ListItem><ListItem><NoteDefault>Scoutmaster – guide, only combat-ready member;</NoteDefault></ListItem><ListItem><NoteDefault>Rascal – quartermaster.</NoteDefault></ListItem></NoteBody>$J0L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault>Coordinates and advance sent. Rest of the money when I get results.</NoteDefault></NoteBody>
sid_notes_supack_SQ95_Note_GroupList_hin: Order for Pickaxe to eliminate a research group
sid_notes_supack_SQ96_C01_Clue1_body: $J0L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault>House with the white mark. An old safe in the room. Not a mechanical lock, code – soldering.</NoteDefault></NoteBody>
sid_notes_supack_SQ96_C01_Clue1_hint: Clue to the tools stash location
sid_notes_supack_SQ96_C01_Clue2_body: $J0L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault>Code – not the marking, temperature with threshold around one hundred and eighty. Standard, 60%</NoteDefault></NoteBody>
sid_notes_supack_SQ96_C01_Clue3_body: $J0L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault>Fucking code locks! None of them have three digits. Ignore the first box.</NoteDefault></NoteBody>
sid_notes_supack_SQ96_C01_Clue3_hint: Clue to the order of the numbers on the code lock
sid_notes_supack_SQ96_C03_DiggerNote_bod: $J0L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault>Damn merchant and his damn riddles. Couldn't just sell me the tools, no, he sends me off to look for them! I hope I won't have to get into any trouble.</NoteDefault></NoteBody>
sid_notes_supack_SQ96_C03_DiggerNote_hin: Found in a basement of an old boiler house
sid_notes_supack_SQ97_DeadExpedition_Not: $J0L0<NoteBody><NoteBold>Day 5</NoteBold></NoteBody>$J0L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault>Measuring temperature. Medulin contacted me three times for radio checks. Assistant professor, if you're reading this, thanks for caring, but we had to carry all your "just in case" equipment on our backs.</NoteDefault></NoteBody>$J0L0<NoteBody><NoteBold>Day 6</NoteBold></NoteBody>$J0L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault>Some interference on the radio. Structured, not just noise. As if someone is trying to speak.</NoteDefault></NoteBody>$J0L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault>Ihor demands we cancel the mission because of that. Paranoid.</NoteDefault></NoteBody>$J0L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault>Sanya tried to burn the generator. Don't know what got into him, but he can't be sent to Malachite alone like that. Shoved it into the room just in case, keeping the key to the roof on me.</NoteDefault></NoteBody>$J0L0<NoteBody><NoteBold>Day 10</NoteBold></NoteBody>$J0L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault>Arguing for no reason. First fistfight. The transmission is on more often. I'm starting to make out words.</NoteDefault></NoteBody>$J0L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault>Don't touch the radio. DO NOT TOUCH THE RADIO.</NoteDefault></NoteBody>$J0L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault>It's singing. The static is. I'll record it.</NoteDefault></NoteBody>$J0L0<NoteBody><NoteItalic>The next page is covered in symbols resembling letters and dried bloodstains.</NoteItalic></NoteBody>
A log from the expedition that died in the Burnt Forest
sid_notes_supack_SQ97_GeneratorNoteText_: $J0L0<NoteBody><NoteDefault>Fuel – only if I give you a voucher. NOT OVER RADIO, in person. So that I can be sure no one's trying to burn us down because he went crazy.</NoteDefault></NoteBody>
sid_notes_supack_SQ97_LostExpeditionMess:
$J0L0[22:47] Ihor: Assistant professor, I'm done. This wasn't in our contract, and sure as shit wasn't in our paycheck.
[07:12] Assistant Professor: Ihor, stay calm! What's happening?
[07:13] Ihor: Can't you fucking figure it out?! All this shit – the ambush, the mutants, Compass almost getting killed by a tree – you think we're just out of luck? More like cursed! And the radio… you told us to keep it on so you could watch that frequency fry our brains?!
[09:24] Assistant Professor: For the love of god, calm down! This isn't SIRCAA, I can't deal with this! If you're under the effects of psi-radiation, I'll send a field operative with some anti-psi as soon as I can.
[09:25] Ihor: Screw you and your anti-psi! I'm telling you, it's not the psi making people crazy, it's the fre-quen-cy! And there's no way anyone can be this unlucky in a normal place! Can't you read, or are you just dumb?!
[18:36] Assistant Professor: Watch your tone. I'm messaging the NK-3 head researcher about your situation. We'll get you all the necessary assistance.
NK-3 member's chat with the assitant professor
sid_notes_supack_SQ97_PelengatorRoomNote:
$J0L0All NK groups: high-frequency resistors within the system are regularly failing. If you need new ones, go to Maksym "Rascal" Shelomyn in Rostok, and I will compensate you. Please submit replacement applications to STC Malachite only if absolutely necessary!

Assistant Professor K. Medulin.
sid_phrase_ANCQ02_Comment_Fight_angry_FF: Stay outta here, you bastards! You ain’t takin’ me alive!
sid_phrase_ANCQ03_Comment_AboutMoneyAlt_: Just hand over the coupons already. I’ve got better things to do than mess around with you.
sid_phrase_ANCQ03_Comment_AboutMoney_Abo: No one ordered you to speak. I could bust you for trespassing near a secure military site!
So what’s it gonna be? Do I follow the regulations, or would you rather work something out?
sid_phrase_ANCQ03_Comment_MoveAlong_Move: Back off, stalker! Stay out of this.
Did I stutter? Hit the road, or I’ll shoot to kill!
sid_phrase_ANCQ03_Comment_PhraseAfterSol: You got coupons to spare too? No? Then get lost.
sid_phrase_ANCQ03_Comment_PraseAfterStal: Those uniform-wearing Bloodsuckers… why the hell did I even take this route?
sid_phrase_ANCQ03_Comment_Thanks_thanks_: Thanks. Nice work dealing with these thugs. We’re all sick of their “fines.”
sid_phrase_ANCQ03_cernysov_1_InfoTopic_1: All those loners hanging around here — what's the point? They only make trouble.
One joy left to me — to catch a greenhorn on not knowing the regs and make a coupon or two.
sid_phrase_ANCQ03_sana_stopor_0_InfoTopi: Those assholes act all tough when they’re waving their guns in your face. But the moment the mutants show up, poof! They vanish into thin air.
You wander all over the Zone, diving into anomalies and mutant nests… only to have your hard-earned money end up in the pockets of those camo-wearing thugs.
sid_phrase_ANCQ06_Audiolog_ScaredMan_sca: I’ll be at Zalissya later. Need to catch my breath.
sid_phrase_ANCQ13_Audiolog_PsyField_1_57: Either I’ve lost too much blood, or… the statue’s, like, moving.
sid_phrase_ANCQ13_Audiolog_PsyField_1_FF: I’m getting a little winded over here, Misha. Keep going without me. I’ll patch myself up by the statue, then catch up with you.
sid_phrase_ANCQ26_AL_StrangeRecording_Su: (a recording of a weird and unpleasant sound)
sid_phrase_ANCQ27_AL_KlucUpdated_NK4_802: Check, one-two… yeah, it's working. This is Wrench, NK-4 research group. Day three. We made it out. Recorded that famous transmission of yours — no big deal… Don't forget, you owe me one.
The guys are a little shaken, but we pulled through. Bit of puking, but we're fine now. And here I thought I'd miss out on all the action stuck in the "control group". Okay… Deep breath…
Hey, man! What's up?! Hey… Hey! What the hell?! Drop the gun! Drop—
sid_phrase_ANCQ27_AL_Kluc_NK4_78152: This is Wrench, NK-4 research group. Day three.
sid_phrase_ANCQ27_AL_Kluc_NK4_78154: Recorded that famous transmission of yours — no big deal…
sid_phrase_ANCQ27_AL_Kluc_NK4_78155: Don't forget, you owe me one.
sid_phrase_ANCQ27_AL_Kluc_NK4_78156: The guys are a little shaken, but we pulled through. Bit of puking, but we're fine now.
sid_phrase_ANCQ27_AL_Kluc_NK4_78157: And here I thought I'd miss out on all the action stuck in the "control group".
sid_phrase_Batya_Generic_ByeFriend_Bye_3: Feel free to drop by any time.
sid_phrase_Batya_Generic_HelloDefault_St: Another one? Great timing. Sorry, kid, now's not the time. Come back later.
sid_phrase_Batya_Generic_HelloEnemy_Hell: You just had to show up, didn't you? What is it?
sid_phrase_Batya_Generic_HelloFriend_Hel: Ah, Skif! Finally a face I'm glad to see. How can I help you?
sid_phrase_C_E01_MQ01_Assault_Dialog_1_4: Skif, come in! Come in! Solder sold us out, you hear me? That greedy son of a bitсh sold us out! I…
sid_phrase_C_E01_MQ01_Assault_Dialog_1_5: What's that? Some kind of anomaly?
Situation unclear. You: inside. You: to the roof. Move out!
Finish him and let's make tracks!
sid_phrase_C_E01_MQ01_Assault_Dialog_1_7: Quiet! I see the Scanner… Tracing for anomalous activity and radio signals…
sid_phrase_C_E01_MQ01_Intro_Dialog_1_488: The Zone is really raging today.
See that? It was a hell of a blast! Can't remember one, so fierce.
The Central Checkpoint could be on alert after the Emission, but there's another way in: a crack in the wall, to the left of the Fault.
Take the tunnel straight to the Zone. Jump over the wall and the sewers will take you right in.
Easy now, Hermann. Steady, real steady…
What the hell is this? I’ve seen artifacts taken from the Zone before,  they always burn out and fall apart real fast. But this one…
This one’s not from the Zone.
Oh, I bet! Where'd you find it?
Who do you think you're talking to, huh? Do you have any idea what this Scanner cost me?
This stone owes me a new home. We gotta move.
Get to the scientists’ bunker. Nehoda will explain how to proceed with the Scanner. He’s waiting for you. Good luck!
sid_phrase_C_E01_MQ01_Intro_Dialog_1_497: If it's not an artifact, it's gonna blow us all to hell! I won't let my hard work come to nothing because of you. I won't let you flush it down the shitter!
sid_phrase_C_E01_MQ01_Intro_Dialog_1_703: …Sector 9, visual detection of the Fault, I say again, sector 9, visual detection of the Fault…
sid_phrase_C_E01_MQ01_Intro_Dialog_1_705: Hope you didn't lose your “secret artifact” along the way? If it was outside the Zone for too long, it would've lost its anomalous energy,
sid_phrase_C_E01_MQ01_Intro_Dialog_1_706: One lucky activation and we have a goldmine here!
but I’ve got this shiny metal thing to recharge it.
sid_phrase_C_E02_MQ01_Bar_Dialog_1_50422: Nope. Talk to Gaffer. That’s him over there.
sid_phrase_C_E02_MQ01_Bar_Dialog_1_50424: Sit down and stay out of sight.
sid_phrase_C_E02_MQ01_Bar_Dialog_1_50426: Three of my men never reported back from their overnight patrol around the Lesser Zone.
sid_phrase_C_E02_MQ01_Bar_Dialog_1_50427: We found their bodies by the Old Bridge.
sid_phrase_C_E02_MQ01_Bar_Dialog_1_50428: Hey, soldier. Over here! What do you call three dead Wardens up the Zone’s ass? A good start.
sid_phrase_C_E02_MQ01_Bar_Dialog_1_50429: A trader who goes by Hamster was peddling this service weapon that belonged to one of those Wardens. He got it from Squint.
sid_phrase_C_E02_MQ01_Bar_Dialog_1_50430: Apologies, Captain, but Zalissya is a free trade zone. We have an agreement with Korshunov.
sid_phrase_C_E02_MQ01_Bar_Dialog_1_50432: Zone has its share of secrets. Who are we to meddle in its designs?
sid_phrase_C_E02_MQ01_Bar_Dialog_1_50433: Playing a wizened stalker sage, are you? Fine.
sid_phrase_C_E02_MQ01_Bar_Dialog_1_50434: Squint was there for sure. He finished off the wounded and looted their bodies. Is that the stalker way now?
sid_phrase_C_E02_MQ01_Bar_WFR_1_A_50435: I suppose it’s not that simple.
sid_phrase_C_E02_MQ01_Bar_WFR_1_A_57542: All right. If you won’t tell me, others will. Seize them!
sid_phrase_C_E02_MQ01_Bar_WFR_1_A_57545: If you don’t want the innocent to suffer, find me the culprit.
sid_phrase_C_E02_MQ01_Bar_WFR_1_A_57546: Well then. In times of war…
sid_phrase_C_E02_MQ01_Bar_WFR_1_B_57536: If you won’t tell me, others will. Seize them!
sid_phrase_C_E02_MQ01_Bar_WFR_1_B_57539: If you don’t want the innocent to suffer, find me the culprit.
sid_phrase_C_E02_MQ01_Bar_WFR_1_B_57540: Well then. In times of war…
sid_phrase_C_E02_MQ01_Bar_WFR_1_C_57530: If you won’t tell me, others will. Seize them!
sid_phrase_C_E02_MQ01_Bar_WFR_1_C_57533: If you don’t want the innocent to suffer, find me the culprit.
sid_phrase_C_E02_MQ01_Bar_WFR_1_C_57534: Well then. In times of war…
sid_phrase_C_E02_MQ01_ToughAwakening_Dia: Whoa, whoa, whoa! Some fireworks, huh?
I'm your guardian angel. Here's a bolt.
Why the hell would I need a bolt?
Yep. Toss it into the anomaly, then while it resets, run like hell. Got it? Now go!
sid_phrase_C_E02_MQ03_Pripoy_Dialog_1_45: Oi! Drop the gun. Who the fuck are you?
Ha! You've got some nerve, mate. Breaking into a military base, raising hell, and now you’re pestering me with questions? Shit. I should shoot you right here and be done with it.
When was the last time you cleaned that thing? PMs tend to misfire a lot if you don’t take care of them.
sid_phrase_C_E02_MQ03_Pripoy_Dialog_1_48: Who’d you sell Hermann out to?
PMs tend to misfire a lot if you don’t take care of them.
He’s the one you should be talking to. The kingpin of Garbage. That's huge pile of trash, you can't miss it. It’s north of…
Listen. I didn’t betray Hermann. I’ve sold some people out in my day, but not you guys.
sid_phrase_C_E02_MQ03_Pripoy_Dialog_1_50: Look, I’m a nobody. I just put anomalies on a map. Sure, I’ve sold stuff on the side to guys like you. But that’s it. Nestor runs the Scanner business.
Ah. Hang on. Did you get busted?
sid_phrase_C_E02_MQ03_Pripoy_WFR_1_A_459: That’s one less pile of trash to deal with.
sid_phrase_C_E03_MQ02_PsiBomb_Dialog_1_4: Gimme a hand! Bring that shit over here!
sid_phrase_C_E03_MQ02_PsiBomb_WFR_1_A_49: Easy. We’ve got an infinite amount of time ahead…
You planning to hang around and catch some rays?
sid_phrase_C_E03_MQ05_TerriconePeak_Dial: Richter?!
Spell-bound, a grove, all enchantedly radiant, Sleeping or dreaming, as if…
Skif?
What are you doing here?
Just living a little. I mean, take a look at that view!
Whoa, stay away from the edge!
Isn’t it beautiful?
Just a load of trash. Food waste, human waste, you name it.
You could find anything you wanted here, Skif: a friend, an enemy, money, death, miracles.  Even yourself.
You ever been to the… Zone’s center?
Not to the plant, no – you can’t get in.
I mean, you won’t find anything there now. It’s like a plundered pyramid. Word is, even the Wish Granter was stolen.
But I have been to Prypiat.
What?
We got hammered once and went on the Ferris wheel. It was a pretty shitty ride, but we had a hell of a time!
If crows are nesting in the landfill now, that means… Skif. Have you ever lost something you’ve loved?
What happened?
Fine. So, what do you think of the Zone, then?
Why are the stalkers so obsessed with the CNPP?
Because the Plant is the heart of it all. It's the very essence of the Zone.
And this… will pay for one ride.
sid_phrase_C_E03_MQ05_TerriconePeak_WFR_: It’s a chance… to turn my life around. Although, it looks like it’s going straight to hell right now.
It may seem so. But that’s the will of the Zone. It knows what’s best for us all.
If you ever go to Prypiat, the first ride on the Ferris wheel is on me!
White is the night, bright the moon shining down on it...
I get it. It takes time to hear it. But keep your eyes and ears open. The Zone might just help you.
If you ever go to Prypiat, the first ride on the Ferris wheel is on me!
White is the night, bright the moon shining down on it...
Of all the shitty places I’ve been to, this one’s the worst.
It just hasn’t dawned on you yet. Give it some time, and you’ll become a changed man. For better or worse, I can’t say. But rest assured, you will.
If you ever go to Prypiat, the first ride on the Ferris Wheel is on me!
White is the night, bright the moon shining down on it...
sid_phrase_C_E03_MQ06_ScarMeeting_I_1_Di: A good man is always on time for lunch!
Who said I’m a good man?
Richter. And the guys from the detention center. And me.
Scar.
Quite a trick you played there. I’d say you’ve had some practice!
What happened at the factory?
As they say, you gotta use whatever is close at hand. And there are plenty of things close at hand in the Zone… if you know where to look.
Like a Scanner.
I was told it was invented at the Institute.
Who told you that? Dalin? Nah, kiddo. All their gadgets are just revamped Project X designs that were found in the old labs.
Project what?
The Zone harbors many secrets, scattered among the old bones. There were scientists here way before the Institute, even before the Zone itself came into being.
Ah, so they’re the ones that left us the goodies that we give to the Ward. Take a seat.
The Ward have no desire to conquer the Zone. They want to extend the Institute up to its borders.
They want to turn the Zone into a testing ground to meet mankind’s insatiable needs. Dalin says, it’s all for the greater good of the Mainland. But what does that really mean?
It’s perfect as is. People come to the Zone to witness miracles!
One of your “miracles” wrecked my home.
Aha! Things just got interesting. So you’ve seen an Anomaly? Outside the Zone?
That’s why I’m here. Someone’s gotta pay.
Ooh! Shit’s gonna go down at the Institute!
I’ve heard the rumors, but never first-hand. And I’ve got a funny feeling there’s more trouble to come…
As long as SIRCAA keeps going, none of this is gonna end well. For any of us.
What do you want to achieve?
Zombies!
Relax.
This is the dark side of the Zone, but there’s another – the Shining side. It’s here, all around us, but we cannot see it…
I wonder what’s out there… Eternal Spring? The Klondike of artifacts? A new home? Or…  our heart's desires. But most importantly… No Ward.
We want the Shining Zone to reveal itself.
Would you care to elaborate?
For now, as far as we’re concerned – shit – you’re just a good guy. That’s all I’m going to say. Or, rather, I think that’s all YOU are ready to hear. Beauty is in the eye of the beholder.
I get it, you’re angry. That’s why you’re not hungry… and couldn’t care less about the troubles of others.
You, uh, don’t like the Ward.
Would you like some toast?
I’m gonna check the surroundings.
What happened at the factory?
As they say, you gotta use whatever is close at hand. And there are plenty of things close at hand in the Zone, if you know where to look…
Like a Scanner.
A good man is always on time for lunch!
Who said I’m a good man? ?
Richter. And the guys from the detention center. And me.
Scar.
Don’t worry, this time you won’t have to lift anything heavier than a bottle.

sid_phrase_C_E03_MQ06_ScarMeeting_WFR_1_: Hey, easy now. Do you need anything?
Thanks but it’s my shit so I’ll sort it out by myself.
Fair enough. Good luck, stalker. See you around.
I’ve got enough troubles of my own, thanks. But good luck, I hope it works out for you.
Hey, easy now. Do you need anything?
Nah. It’s my shit so I’ll sort it out by myself.
Fair enough. Thanks, stalker. Spark has a good memory. See you around.
I’ve got enough troubles of my own, thanks. But good luck, I hope it works out for you.
I’ve got enough troubles of my own, thanks. But good luck, I hope it works out for you.
Well, it was nice to get to know you.
Hold on to your badge, Mr. Fixer.
I’ve got enough troubles of my own, thanks. But good luck, I hope it works out for you.
Well, it was nice to get to know you.
Thanks for your help. Spark has a good memory.
The SIRCAA facility stands on top of an old lab. It used to be locked shut, but the Ward has managed to break it open. That’s when the troubles on the Mainland began.
We need to find out what they’re up to there, but the PDAs taken from the dead Wardens are pretty useless.
I can get inside once I have my Scanner back. I’ve already found something..
Really? What is it?
An early model. Looks like Monolith tech. Where’d you get it?
One of my attackers had it on him.
Sounds like Strider is in trouble… You must head over to Wild Island. Normally they don’t let loners in, but we’ll figure something out.
Can I help?
How does the Ward’s badge feel in your pocket, you snitch?
I have a plan. And I’m sticking to it.
Do I hear the Colonel speaking?
I've got a mind of my own.
Well, do as you please, but watch your head.
Can I help?
sid_phrase_C_E03_MQ06_TerriconeVarta_I_1: Where’s Diode? You know, the technician?
You like asking questions, don’t you?
Diode said you asked him about some Scanners.
The thing about the Scanner…
Hold on, Dalin…
Take a seat. Let’s talk.
Colonel, if I may… It’s Skif, right?
I need you…
I need all of us to understand the urgency of this situation given the extreme importance of our mission.
That goes for you too, Skif.
I hear you’ve managed to activate one of our devices, the Institute’s most valuable invention.
You got it to work at the Old Bridge, didn’t you?
And the artifact? There was a faded artifact in the Scanner. What happened to it?
I see the Scanner... Tracing for anomalous activity and radio signals…
Stalker PDA detected! Someone here!
I was there.
I was attacked, just like your men.
The Scanner was stolen. And I’m looking for the scumbags who took it.
Fuck it. We’re all in the same boat.
Help me find those bastards, Skif. Maybe you know something I don’t…
Search him!
Dima.
Dima, you okay?
sid_phrase_C_E03_MQ06_TerriconeVarta_I_2: Who the hell are you?
Decided to play soldiers with me?
Dmytro and I were just discussing a certain puzzle…
…we’ve found in the Zone…
But we can’t quite seem to get all the pieces to fit…
Maybe you can figure it out?
Good. Only I can’t decide…

I was there.

The Scanner was stolen. I’m looking for the scumbags who took it.
Good. Okay. Maybe you know something I don’t…
Help me find those bastards, Skif.

My men died there, Skif. I’m here to find those bastards.

A friend of the late Chornozem, and a Warden in the making!

Sit.
sid_phrase_C_E03_MQ06_TerriconeVarta_V_2: Are you crazy, or just fucking stupid? Fuck off. You wanna play soldiers with me?
You picked the wrong side, stalker!
And while you lie here, trying to hold your guts in, somewhere in a better life, a highly respected young Warden soldier is looking back at this, bewildered.
Because he racked his brains real good and realized that he learned something about that nighttime attack at the Old Bridge!
Don’t be so hard on him, Colonel. A stalker’s life is all about survival.
You can't imagine what he’s been through.
Isn't that right, Skif?
We’re already on the same side here. All of us. Bound by the extreme importance of this mission.
And you play an instrumental part in it, Skif.
I hear you've managed to activate one of our devices, the Institute’s most valuable invention.
You got it to work, didn't you?
Kind of…
