Dalin, you’ve got the Sensor readings…
I was attacked, and the Scanner was stolen. I’m looking for the scumbags who took it.
Good. Those bastards killed my men.
You can help us, and we can find them together.
Son of a bitch!
Sit.
As I was saying. We are already bound by the extreme importance of this mission.
Colonel, give me a second. Please.
Wait.
Continue.
sid_phrase_C_E03_MQ06_TerriconeVarta_WFR: Something similar happened to Varan, a bandit from Garbage. He had a Scanner, and—
What?
Another goddamn Scanner?! Why are the bandits running around Garbage with your precious invention?!
Another Scanner?!
Don’t play dumb with me! At this point, there are more Scanners in the Zone than there are fucking stalkers!
I don’t know about that…
Well, I don’t know if—
Holy shit! A Monolith PDA!
Now I can finally burn their fucking nest down!
Wild Island. Fortifications...
Fortifications… two. No, three… They’re pretty heavily armed.
Zotov, come in!
The Scanner and the artifact, out there, in the hands of lunatics. Among the mud and the toxic waste!
Colonel…
What?!
Please, what if the Scanner gets damaged in the fighting? These are not stalkers, but former Monolithians! No, this is out of the question. I can’t bear to think about it!
Maybe we should send some neutral person to the island, someone who’s already helped you… I mean us.
Come on, think about the risk! We could lose the very thing we’ve been striving to obtain!
Colonel, this is Zotov. I don’t copy. Please repeat. Over. Colonel?
Just checking in. Over and out.
Unfortunately, I haven’t had a chance to introduce myself.
My name is Dmytro Dalin, and I’m the head of SIRCAA.
A temple of Science, a haven for geniuses, a place where the Holy Grail is not sought, but invented…
The Scanner is the Institute’s greatest achievement, but right now, all that really matters is the revived artifact at the heart of it.
If you get it back, our dream can become a reality. Mankind will be transformed – we’ll open the way for future technology.
And it will all be thanks to you — thanks to your courage and dedication to progress. You’ll be leading the way!
The brink of a new scientific era!
That’s wonderful, man.
But I’m here because the Anomaly burned down my home on the Mainland, and someone’s got to pay for it.
This cannot be…
Another of your miscalculations, eh, Einstein?
Skif, head over to Wild Island and find their leader, Strider.
He’ll know where the gang hangs out. And if you can persuade him, he just may tell you what kind of shady business they’ve gone and gotten themselves into.
I’ll get you a new one.
Take this. It’ll come in handy if someone gives you the silent treatment.
Good luck, soldier. Keep in touch.
Whoa, whoa! Guys! Varan’s men were attacked. But one of the attackers had this.
Wild Island.
Colonel!
Thank you.
But it did.
Where are they getting their equipment? Give me the radio.
Nope. Nothing so far.
You stalkers are a strange breed. Do you have to be so stubborn?
You put your secrets before reason, and for what? To snuff it in the first anomaly you stumble into. Just for some make-believe miracles of the Zone.
Unfortunately, I wasn’t given a chance to introduce myself.
My name is Dmytro Dalin, and I’m the head of SIRCAA.
The Scanner is the Institute’s greatest achievement, but right now, what really matters is the revived artifact at the heart of it.
If you get it back, our dream can become a reality. Mankind will be transformed – we’ll open the way for future technology.
And it will all be thanks to you – thanks to your courage and perseverance.
And rest assured, all your hard work and efforts will be rewarded handsomely.
Wasting your time, Dalin…
Nice to know, Head of SIRCAA.
Aha.
sid_phrase_C_E04_MQ01_StriderAndFaust_Di: “He’s dead,” they say. “Destroyed!”
You can go and hide from him and cower away in some musty hole in the ground, but that won’t stop the wind from blowing or the rain from pouring!
“Brave warriors,” I tell you! His worthy children!
“Lies,” I tell you. They’re all filthy, pathetic lies.
You can’t kill God, just like you can’t get rid of the sky up above.
“Crazy fanatics,” they say. “Murderers!”
Stop brainwashing my men, Faust. You’re only making it worse.
What could be worse than… this? What have we become? Are you happy, Strider?
My boots are fine for the road I walk. There are more important things than happiness.
And what’s that then? Backbreaking work? Hardship? That enduring sense of guilt…? It didn’t use to be like this. We were different – part of something much greater…
I was the Word, and you carried the banner of the Monolith!
We brought death. Against our will.
Well now we’re dying ourselves. The fumes are killing us, as are the bullets of our vengeful enemies!
How many brothers roam the Zone to this day, lost and forgotten, forced to hide their past? We have to find them. We have to bring them to the safety…
By force? We all know about Wild Island. Everyone must make the choice for themselves.
And what will they find here? The meaning of life? Or a similar bunch of miserable wretches – just like them, who don’t know what they want?
Strider saved us!
And now he’s killing us…
Once, we were His children. And what are we now?
Men of free will, who choose their own way. You’re living in the past, Faust. The Monolith is dead. It’s time to grow up!
You can’t kill God, just like you can’t get rid of the sky up above.
Think again!
I am blind, but it’s you who cannot see. Go cower in your hole in the ground! Hide from the past, hide from His light… Hide from yourself
sid_phrase_C_E05_MQ02_ThatNimble_Dialog_: I don’t know who you are, stalker, but you best run!
Shit! Fuck! That was a little too close. I thought it was game over, for sure.
Two close calls in a minute. Not cool.
You were asking about Clear Sky. What do you wanna know?
You... That night, back at the Old Bridge. You stole my Scanner and gave me a hell of a headache!
That’s horseshit, man. I ain’t been back at the Old Bridge in a long time!
It was all Faust, man! Shi-i-i-t. I’m just a guide! I was only showing the way, all right?
It was a run-of-the-mill job, where the customer’s kinda shady, all obsessed with the Monolith and shit. But I’m not in the business of killing stalkers.
He ain’t that bad. Maybe a couple of screws loose, but here in the Zone, that don’t exactly make him unique.
You good now, bro? That’s what I thought.
But you are in the business with Faust!
Although, that whole blind thing? I think it’s a crock of shit.
sid_phrase_C_E05_MQ02_ThatNimble_WFR_1_A: That’s a pretty stupid take. Any others?
Oh yeah, lots. You patch me up and I’ll share everything.
sid_phrase_C_E05_MQ02_ThatNimble_WFR_1_B: Back at the Old Bridge. I got lucky. But others? They could've been killed. So…
sid_phrase_C_E05_MQ03_ClearSky_Dialog_1_: Shot by Loners. Died in an anomaly.
Found with a stone around his neck. Thrown off a cliff.
Torn apart by dogs. Operation Monolith.
Wait just a second, Skif, and the Scanner is yours. I need to… say my goodbyes.
Farewell to our great past. Enemies? Yes. But united by faith in one idea. For your kind, it was the Wish Granter. For us, the Monolith.
It spoke in many tongues, but everyone heard only what they wanted to hear. Repentance? Forgiveness? Not a chance. After a decade of silence, only hatred remains.
The Monolith has abandoned us to make us understand…
…that being alive does not mean to be happy. Nothing else matters.
Shot by Loners. Found with a stone around his neck.
Thrown off a cliff. Operation Monolith.
Hanged. Torn apart by dogs. Killed.
sid_phrase_C_E06_MQ01_TemplesDoorstep_Di: We need to run compatibility tests. Check everything is all right.
Oh, yes. Of course. You must be tired.
Oh, here. Here’s the key to my room. I’m sleeping in my study either way. Don’t worry about your reward. You’ll get it!
Have some rest and get yourself cleaned up.
My colleagues promised me a surprise for my birthday, I wish I knew what it was…
Guess you’ll have to tell me. Enjoy it!
In fact, someone would like to talk to you as soon as we’ve completed all the necessary tests.
sid_phrase_C_E06_MQ02_MeetingAgatha_Dial: Skif! Skif! Thank God you’re here! If only Dalin had listened to me, I wouldn’t have to cheat! You know that’s true! Tell her, I’m begging you!
Your friend is a strange kind of scientist, Skif. He loves secrets more than discoveries.
That makes sense. People need more than just a couple of days to really become friends. You need to trust one another, share your secrets…
Like the fact that someone stole the Scanners from SIRCAA. Just because he was so envious of Dr. Dalin.
And guided by his co-conspirator, Faust – he insisted on murdering every last witness. Including you, of course.
There is no need to repeat yourself, Professor.
This man almost got you killed. So it’s up to you to decide his fate.
sid_phrase_C_E06_MQ02_MeetingAgatha_WFR_: Let him go.
Professor Hermann, you may go.
Skif… Thank you. Thank you!
Professor! You forgot something.
Goodbye…
Yevhen Martynenko, twenty-five years old. Born in a small town, loved a good book but preferred a good fight.
Served in the army. Upon returning home was engaged in risky part-time jobs.
No real friends, no family – just lonely evenings in front of the TV. A while ago he came home to find his place in ruins.
You’ve been through a lot, Yevhen.
Cigarette?
After the fire at those apartments, everyone rushed to seek help from officials.
But not you, Yevhen. In your search for the truth, you had the courage to enter the Zone.
Perseverance is an admirable quality, and you’ve got heaps of it. That’s why we can thank you and you alone that the Scanner is back at SIRCAA.
The Zone is extremely dangerous to the outside world, but, thanks to you, we will soon have its power under control.
And in the meantime, you can return to the Mainland and move into a beautiful new house.
Sounds… great.
You deserve it.
But I want to be there for the experiment. Bread was promised – now it’s time for circuses.
Dr. Dalin speaks highly of you, but the experiment requires top-level security. Although, perhaps you could help us in this matter?
Someone’s planning to stop us, right? Stop us getting what we deserve…
He has to die.
Fair enough.
Yevhen Martynenko, twenty-five years old. Born in a small town, loved a good book but preferred a good fight.
Served in the army, upon returning home was engaged in risky part-time jobs.
No real friends, no family – just lonely evenings in front of the TV. A while ago he came home to find his place in ruins.

Someone’s planning to stop us, right? Stop us getting what we deserve…
Skif!
Why should I care?
Professor Hermann, you may go.
Professor! You forgot something.

Perseverance is an admirable quality, and you’ve got heaps of it. That’s why we can thank you and you alone that the Scanner is back at SIRCAA.
The Zone is extremely dangerous to the outside world, but, thanks to you, we will soon have its power under control.
And in the meantime, you can return to the Mainland and move into a beautiful new house.
Sounds… great.
You deserve it.
But I want to be there for the experiment. Bread was promised – now it’s time for circuses.
Dr. Dalin speaks highly of you, but the experiment requires top-level security. Although, perhaps you could help us in this matter?
Someone’s planning to stop us, right? To stop us getting what we deserve…
Strider, the leader of Noontide, intends to break into SIRCAA. They might be armed.
I knew I could count on you. Meet with Strider, convince him not to do anything stupid. I don’t want our new world to begin with bloodshed. And neither do you.
And if he doesn’t change his mind?
You’re an experienced guy, Yevhen. I’m sure you can handle it… one way or another.
I can’t help you. Sorry.
Skif, come in! Are you there yet? See if you can get to the labs and find out about the Monolith. I’m counting on you, stalker.
Someone’s taking advantage of you again, Yevhen…
Yeah, and you aren’t?
Strider has nothing to offer you in return – unlike us. Find him. Ask him to lay down his arms.
No worthy cause should begin with bloodshed.
I can’t help you. Sorry.
Skif, come in! Are you there yet? Scar says the Monolith is at the Institute! Can you get to the labs and confirm it?
Someone’s taking advantage of you again, Yevhen…
Yeah, and you aren’t?
Strider has nothing to offer you in return – unlike us. Find him. Ask him to lay down his arms.
No worthy cause should begin with bloodshed.
sid_phrase_C_E06_MQ02_TheCaribbeanExperi: What do you want from me? Get to the point!
It’ll become clear soon enough, Colonel. And no, you won’t have to send your men anywhere or put them in danger. For that, we’ve got—
Speaking of danger.
As I was saying, we need someone who’s already got certain… qualities.
Courage. Persistence. Endurance.
Any of my men will do.
Not quite. We need someone who’s already had a… particular kind of experience in the Zone. Basically, we need a stalker.
The readings all match up, but to see it with my own eyes – that’s something else... A scientific miracle!
Alpha. Quarter of a century ago. Maybe even longer. The artifact that started it all.
It’s still going strong. And not just in the Zone.
You stalkers with your tall tales… You need to lay off the booze.
The Second Caribbean Experiment, you asshole. Tall tales, huh? Look at me! I lost everything back on the Mainland! And you tell me to lay off the booze?!
Colonel?!
To the victor go the spoils.
This Experiment isn’t gonna conduct itself… Stand down!
We had a simple choice: sit back and just wait for another Super-Emission, or take a chance and get control of the Anomalous energy!
And look – our gamble paid off!
As for your home… You can blame the Zone, not me!
Cool it, soldier. The Second Caribbean Experiment… didn’t go as planned. During the experiment, the group was attacked by Scar’s saboteurs. They’re local stalkers – a thorn in my side.
And since they achieved what they set out to back then, you can be sure they’ll keep trying to get in our way.  You’re blaming the wrong people, Skif.
Let’s hope that, this time, security will be your top priority.
sid_phrase_C_E06_MQ02_VoiceOfMonolith_Di: Your objective is here. Come to me. Your path is coming to end. Come to me. Only one will be rewarded. Your wish will soon be granted. Come to me.
The time has come. I see your wish. Your path has come to an end, human. Come to me. Come to me. You will get what you deserve.
sid_phrase_C_E06_MQ02_VoiceOfMonolith_en: Humanity is corrupt. Mankind must be controlled.
I want the Zone to disappear.
sid_phrase_C_E06_MQ02_VoiceOfMonolith_ru: I want to rule the world.
sid_phrase_C_E06_MQ03_StriderSignal_stri: Where the hell have you been? Help me out with these, will ya?
One on each hinge should do the trick.
Performing visual analysis. No damage detected.
SKALA here. Request for Sync Phase received.
Center! X11! The signal… The Sphere… Slag Heap!
sid_phrase_C_E06_MQ03_VartaSignal_signal: Zero coulombs on the Knyazhinsky scale.
Performing visual analysis. No damage detected.
Keep away from the door! Hands up! Keep your hands up! Where I can see them!
SKALA here. Request for Sync Phase received.
Are you nuts? Put it away!
This is the last one. The threat has been eliminated!
I barely slept during all those years of preparation. Maybe perhaps just a couple hours here and there on that awful couch. Thank you again for your help, Skif.
As of today, the Zone will be gone along with all our fears. You’ll go back to your new home. Strider will return to his island. And I’ll finally be able to sleep in a bed. With a real pillow.
Peacefully, with no dreams. Because all the dreams have come true.
Yes! I was right! I knew it!
Center! X11! The signal… The Sphere… Slag Heap!
sid_phrase_C_E06_MQ04_TheZoneIsAlive_Dia: Skif, glad to see you, buddy! Let’s get outta here!
Hey, would you look at that!
My, my motherland, so great a land of Cheremosh and Prut...
You’re my only love, my native land, my motherland…
It’s alive! The Zone is alive!
You fucking crazy! I almost died at that fucking Institute!
What happened over there, Skif? Where is Strider?
Why are they shooting at us?! Why are you shooting at us?! We’re with Strider!
…just like you can’t get rid of the sky up above…
They say the sun’s always shining in Prypiat…
sid_phrase_C_E07_MQ02_PsiImmunity_Dialog: The Signal has restored the programming of Noontiders and other ex-Monolithians, but their psi-immunity has been compromised ever since Project X.
I can’t yet say how dangerous it is for regular people.
But it is safe to say you’re a fucking failure, Dalin! Well, I guess it runs in the family.
Remind me – why did you save this little sack of shit from X11?
And you really think all this technobabble is gonna save your ass now? I should’ve kicked Dalin’s bullshit out of you a long time ago!
So you want me to dumb it down for you? Fine.
Here. See this Mesh? The Emitter is like the Duga, but in miniature.
The Duga can be used to amplify psi-radiation. It’s powerful enough to transmit another Signal and destroy the psi-immunity of everything and everyone in the Zone.
What, some kinda Brain Scorcher?!
In theory, yes. Only ten times as powerful. But that’s just a rough estimate, of course…
Son of a bitch… you just wanna do another experiment, don’t you?
Here we go again with your daddy issues. He can’t stand to live in his father's shadow.
Poor boy! Well, your old man also went to great lengths and ended up betraying the Board and destroying everything! Oh, he was really pleased with himself. Until he got shot.
You were hired to fix the problems on this Project, but now the Institute lies in ruins and my men are dead! What are you gonna fuck up next, Dalin?
But I succeeded!
Doctor is right, Colonel.
He did succeed. The Alpha Artifact has been found. Project X's network is restored and functioning. The Institute’s mission has been accomplished.
We may have underestimated Strider, but that’s not Dalin’s fault. Something else is going on here, something we don’t know about… yet.
But I’m sure the Ward is perfectly capable of dealing with these… issues.
Everyone needs to be on top of their game right now.
Our goal is within reach.
sid_phrase_C_E07_MQ03_ExtremeMeasures_Di: There you are my good man!
I’ve got what you asked for.
It’s amazing this lamp is still intact…
Okay. Business first. I get it.
I’ve seen what this piece of shit is capable of.
The Ward is about to attack Duga. Their best men are going to be there. This “piece of shit” would be very helpful in thinning out their ranks.
So we're back to the same old bloodthirsty plan, huh?
That’s how the Zone works. Some people have to suffer so other people can stop suffering.
You knew all along, you son of a bitch!
Tell me. Do you actually have someplace to go back to? Or have you found what you were looking for?
Too many words, too few answers.
Man! A long time ago, I woke up in the scientists’ mobile lab at Yaniv Station. They were good men. They fed me and took care of me, all while huddled together in a cramped little iron box.
I wanted to help them, to find them a better place to live. During one of my trips, I stumbled upon the Chemical Plant. Yes, sure, another brick monstrosity, but…
So those good men moved there — it was the first Spark group.
We kept to ourselves, minded our own… business, even during the new faction war. Until one day…
BAM! BAM! Bam!… Topol… he was the first to die.
Mace tried to warn us…
“Thieves rule and Wardens drool!”
And now tell me, who holds the Chemical Plant now?
The Ward…
Yes, the Ward!
I've kept away from any ruins ever since… except for the ruins of the Institute. Sure, you can take my “bloodthirsty plan” as paying off a debt. But this war isn't just mine war.
Yes, you… Him.
You came to the Zone looking for justice too, didn’t you?
What’s the point of all this sacrifice, Scar? All these victims?
What’s the point?
We’re all to stand upon the sea. Through death, through darkness, thus.
I choose the nets that swallow me. This mortal coil failed us.
Do what must be done, and the mystery will reveal itself. That I know for sure. As for you — just have faith.
Back then, it was like an Oasis to me.
Over here!
But all he could do was wheeze. Spirit and I tried to escape through the workshop, and then the second assault squad burst in! Boom! But Snake had a grenade ready for them.
sid_phrase_C_E08_MQ01_Infirmary_Dialog_1: “Rest and recuperate?” My men died getting your asses out of SIRCAA, I damn near died at the Duga, and now you’re telling me to rest?!
I’m afraid there’s nothing else I can do for you right now.
You’ve been promising me class 5 psi-protection for years!
Colonel, sir, no one expected the Small Duga to—
No one, huh? My men are still lying out there and I can’t even bury them!
At least you didn’t share their fate.
We… selected soldiers and studied them in order to understand how psi-immunity works. But, this procedure had a side effect we weren’t expecting: loss of protection against targeted psi-exposure.
What the hell does that mean?
We have created class 5 protection as promised, but… it only works for you, Colonel.
Those Monolithians. They fight like it’s their last stand and die happy. Faust could be envied, huh?
sid_phrase_C_E08_MQ06_DeathofFaust_Dialo: Take a seat, Skif. We have a lot to talk about.
What time of day would you choose for an intimate conversation?
A mortal dies, yet the soul endures.
sid_phrase_C_E08_MQ06_DeathofFaust_WFR_1: I've always liked dawn.
What a… fascinating show.
Much of the Zone is not what it seems.
What the hell… did you unleash upon us?
You know more than I do, Envoy of the Monolith.
What?
You stood by His side, carrying out His will, and now you’ve come for me. I have angered Him, and that is why you are here – seeking revenge and the reward.
I don’t get it. I just want to reclaim what I’ve lost.
That makes two of us.
Is that so?
Some come to the Zone to make money. Others come looking for miracles. Many just want to find happiness, you know? A way to feel real and alive again.
But have you ever met anyone who was truly happy?
And what do they look for, if the miracles don’t satisfy them? The Monolith brought joy to a chosen few. But I've decided that all stalkers in the Zone deserve better…
How generous of you…
I’ve been here before. Once, a long time ago, when I was a stalker looking for an adventure. I took just one awkward step, and my whole world plunged into darkness.
But, in the end, it was I who gained true sight, while the others kept stumbling around in the dark, searching for invisible wonders.
The Monolith showed me the Zone for what it truly is.
I prefer dusk.
What a… fascinating show.
Much of the Zone is not what it seems.
What the hell… did you unleash upon us?
You know more than I do, Envoy of the Monolith.
What?
You stood by His side, carrying out His will, and now you’ve come for me. I have angered Him, and that is why you are here – seeking revenge and the reward.

But, in the end, it was I who gained true sight, while the others kept stumbling around in the dark, searching for invisible wonders.
The Monolith showed me the Zone for what it truly is.
Does it matter?
sid_phrase_C_E08_MQ06_DeathofFaust_WFR_2: The Monolith has nothing to do with this…
You know more than I do, Envoy.
There is no Monolith, Faust. You acquired Controller abilities in one of the X-Labs so you could guide the Monolithians and serve C-Consciousness. So long as they were alive…
Well, you would say that. I’m not surprised. You need to stay here a little longer.  There is nothing you can keep secret that’ll not come into the open. It’s just a matter of time.
Such is the will of the Monolith.
Define reality. The Zone is what you consider it to be. I've shown you how I see it. Walk your own path and you will come to perceive the Zone in your own way.
Such is the will of the Monolith.
sid_phrase_C_E09_MQ01_TheEnemyUnseen_Dia: All right, Ozersky! Do I look like an idiot to you?
That’s Professor Ozersky, if you don’t mind. And I think you know my answer to that question. As for the Visiograph, we haven’t actually tested it yet.
What? You discovered a device from Project X and you haven’t even tried to activate it?!

We followed your instructions to the letter, Dr. Dalin! We used the technical drawings to assemble the components, then waited for the green light from the Institute. I sent you several requests, and I—
If I didn't know you as well as I do, I might think you did this on purpose!
I don’t remember any requests about X17! When did you file it?!
I guess it's buried under that giant pile of reports no one ever bothered to read.
sid_phrase_C_E10_MQ01_Kaymanov_Dialog_1_: How long did you know each other?
Not long. I met him on Wild Island. Back when he was still… human.
He was someone who had everything taken away from him — his past, his ability to fight back, even his name.
We go to great lengths to cover up our mistakes.
After years of work, a group of scientists managed to tap into… the Noosphere, the Earth’s informational field, an achievement comparable at best to the conquest of space! It was a turning point…
However, as with all people, each of my colleagues was truly interested in only one person — himself! They began to dream of worldwide fame, of laurels crowning their bald shiny heads!
Naturally, the truth couldn’t come out about the secret laboratories, brutal experiments, and other such dubious undertakings, it was out of the question.
And so, they got all carried away with their dream of a “perfect world.” You know how the saying goes: great minds think alike — though fools seldom differ.
And, lo and behold, what do we have decades later? The Zone. In our image, after our likeness.
Not just for science, but for the fate of the human race…
You were there, right? For the C-Consciousness project?
I was once as deluded as they were, but my doubts soon outweighed their confidence. And so when the experiment took place, one of the pods was left empty.
The eighth one. Or, rather… The first. Dr. Kaymanov's pod.
sid_phrase_C_E10_MQ02_OrbitaStrider_I_1_: Skif? What’s going on? Where are we?
But the Experiment… Tell me we made it in time!
sid_phrase_C_E10_MQ02_OrbitaStrider_I_2_: We were too late.
What happened to my men?
They’re under  control of The Monolith.
I’m sorry, Strider.
May 2012. The military is storming the CNPP. Lun and Sova Teams are in my sector. The primary objective is Quartet.
Southeast, 180. Landmark: Inspection Tower.
Target: Sova, 345. Low lateral.
Southwest, 195. Landmark: Pumping Station.
Target: Quartet, 400. Moderate lateral.
Windage. Breathing. Three… two… one.
That was the day the psi fields were turned off.  Suddenly, the voice of the Monolith was gone. I felt as if I was awakening from a years long coma.
He was not my enemy, Skif! I just hope that he has survived and has forgiven us.
You asked me about my good luck charm, remember? It’s an empty cartridge casing, a reminder from that day. I genuinely believed that I would never be a puppet again.
I am sorry, brothers. We have failed. Forgive me. And forgive me for everything you must do now.
It was a worthy try, Skif. Thank you. Stay strong.
And through the scope I saw the body of an unknown soldier fall.
Yes, we did.
Really? Good… Were any of my men hurt?
No. Just… Just you.
Well, we protected our new life. That’s all that matters.
You asked me about my good luck charm, remember? It’s an empty cartridge casing, nothing special. A reminder from the day the psi fields were turned off.
May 2012. The military is storming the CNPP. Lun and Sova Teams are in my sector. The primary objective is Quartet.
Southeast, 180. Landmark: Inspection Tower.
Target: Sova, 345. Low lateral.
Southwest, 195. Landmark: Pumping Station.
Target: Quartet, 400. Moderate lateral.
Windage. Breathing. Three… two… one.
Suddenly, the voice of the Monolith was gone. I opened my eyes, as if after years of coma. And through the scope I saw the body of an unknown soldier fall.
It’s the empty casing from that cartridge. It reminded me of what it meant to not be in charge of your own life… but now we are free, masters of our own destiny.
Thank you, Skif. Noontide will remember what you’ve done.
sid_phrase_C_E10_MQ02_OrbitaStrider_V_1_: You see anything, Skif? Enabling the visualizer…
Skif? What’s going on? Where are we?
But the Experiment… We were too late. So you sold me out, huh? What did they promise you?
…the Zone will be gone along with all our fears.
sid_phrase_C_E10_MQ02_OrbitaStrider_V_2_: I was dealing with my own problems, and I couldn't let you get in the way.
You lost. The Monolith is once again controlling you all.
You asked me about my good luck charm, remember? It’s an empty shell, nothing special. A reminder from the day the psi fields were turned off.
May 2012. The military is storming the CNPP. Lun and Sova Teams are in my sector. The primary objective is Quartet.
And then the voice of the Monolith was gone. And through the scope I saw the body of an unknown soldier fall.
That empty shell… it reminded me that we used to be puppets. Only I didn’t choose that path.
I wanted to sort out my own problems, and I didn’t expect it to go down that way.
You asked about my good luck charm, remember? It’s an empty shell, nothing special. A reminder from the day the psi fields were turned off.
May 2012. The military is storming the CNPP. Lun and Sova Teams are in my sector. The primary objective is Quartet.
Suddenly, the voice of the Monolith was gone. I opened my eyes, as if awakening after a long coma. And through the scope I saw the body of an unknown soldier fall.
He was not my enemy. I hope he has survived and forgiven us.
Farewell, Skif. Forgive me for what we did… and must do again.
sid_phrase_C_E10_MQ03_OrbitaScar_I_1_Dia: Well, well… What a meeting! A good man in a tight spot. You don’t give up easy, huh? Keep sniffing around…
Hey, is it just me, or were you talking to somebody?
Strider? How'd he get here? Strider! Strider? Have you boys seen Strider around here? Strider! Stri-der!!!
Turns out there's life in the Noosphere. Can you believe it?
This rocket ain’t gonna take you into the Noosphere!
sid_phrase_C_E10_MQ03_OrbitaScar_I_2_Dia: I guess so. It's hard to digest at first.
But this time it's all different, right? Let me guess — it wasn’t the most pleasant conversation in your life?
The Shining! We’ll get rid of the darkness that imprisons us, preventing us from hearing each other. Imagine an Eternal spring, Skif! No pain, no regret, no suffering!
If you ask me, it looks more like a mind-prison.
You should have listened better to the Representative. Quit concentrating on the here and now, think instead about what we might achieve!
The Shining! To get rid of the darkness that imprisons us, preventing us from hearing each other. Imagine an Eternal spring, Skif! No pain, no regret, no suffering!
sid_phrase_C_E10_MQ03_OrbitaScar_I_3_Dia: I know, Scar. I just wanted to make sure.
Wonderful, I hope you didn’t waste your time and that you figured out something interesting. So, now it’s time to share!
I won’t be a part of this. Life is for the living, Scar. If the dead start talking to us, we’ll end up slaughtering each other. And then there won’t be peace for us – not even in death.
You know, all this time you’ve been a talisman to us, someone truly inspiring! I believed in you, but you turned out to be a forgery.
That’s why you didn’t get it.
sid_phrase_C_E10_MQ03_OrbitaScar_V_1_Dia: I thought you were one of the good guys. Turns out you’re with the Ward.  Keep sniffing around, doing their bidding. I told you, it’s safer to sniff around for us — and way more fun.
Hey… is it just me, or were you talking to somebody?
Strider? Is he here? Strider? Strider! Have you seen Strider around here, boys? Strider!  Stride-e-e-er!
I get it. You were sent here to check if there’s life in the Noosphere, right?
This rocket ain’t gonna take you into the Noosphere!
sid_phrase_C_E10_MQ03_OrbitaScar_V_2_Dia: If you ask me, it looks more like a mind-prison.
You should have listened better to the Representative. Quit concentrating on the here and now. Think instead about what we might achieve!
The Shining! To get rid of the darkness that imprisons us, preventing us from hearing each other. Imagine an eternal spring, Skif! No pain, no regret, no suffering!
It was hard to digest at first.
Ah, I see. I take it you’ve met the Representative? Let me guess – the Wardens couldn’t make heads or tails of it, could they?
It has to be done, Skif. The Shining! We can get rid of the darkness that's imprisoning us, preventing us from hearing each other. Imagine an eternal spring! No pain, no regret, no suffering!
sid_phrase_C_E10_MQ03_OrbitaScar_V_3_Dia: Life is for the living, Scar. If the dead start talking to us, we’ll end up slaughtering each other. And then there won’t be peace for us – not even in death.
You know, I made some inquiries on my way here. I wanted to help you learn more about the people you’re mixed up with. But now I realize there’s no point in explaining myself. You’re just not worthy.
Death doesn’t mean “goodbye”, it means “until we meet again”! See you later, Skif!
Don’t push it, Scar. I need some time to digest all this.
Fine, all time is ours now, at least while it still means something. Let’s talk – I’m here to help with your… digestion.
sid_phrase_C_E11_MQ02_StrelokX3_Dialog_1: I'm not looking to make friends.
Hm, Skif. You’ve got some curious friends here.
sid_phrase_C_E11_MQ03_KorshunovPTS_I_1_D: What’s wrong with you, soldier? You’re a constant thorn in my side. You helped Strider, got mixed up with Spark—
Look, I’d love to gather all you stalkers around the campfire and explain exactly what we’re guarding and why, but I have no desire whatsoever to trip over your soiled mattresses while on patrol.
I’ve been ordered to keep the aftermath of Project X a secret from the likes of you. It’s too bad you trusted those dreamers. You’ve got a knack for it you know – taking people at their word…
You trusted Strelok? An agent of C-Consciousness?
The Dark One, Scar, Strelok. C-Consciousness bred them like cockroaches.  This installation will help us find every last agent, but only within the Perimeter.
Unfortunately, many of them have already left the Zone. And if we do nothing, houses on the Mainland will continue to burn and collapse!
This is reality! Miracles and fantasies are for those who can’t face up to the real world.
sid_phrase_C_E11_MQ03_KorshunovPTS_V_1_D: What are you doing here, Skif? You could’ve just asked.
Well, I’m asking now. What is this installation, Colonel?
There’s too much stuff left over from Project X that we need to keep quiet about. Otherwise, we’ll end up with stalker bonfires and soiled mattresses piled up in every corner.
But… you’ve been a great help to the Ward, and I value your tenacity. So we can talk openly.
Not all knowledge is equally beneficial. Some of it can be outright dangerous in the wrong hands.
You trusted Strelok? An agent of C-Consciousness?
The Dark One, Scar, Strelok. C-Consciousness bred them like cockroaches. This installation will help us find every last agent, but only within the Perimeter.
Unfortunately, many of them have already left the Zone. And if we do nothing, houses on the Mainland will continue to burn and collapse!
If you fight us, soldier, you’re only fighting yourself.
sid_phrase_C_E11_MQ03_StrelokPTS_Dialog_: Screens. A whole wall of them, all static. And a creepy chair.
That’s his eyes you’re looking through. Wave to the man, Dark One.
Hello, Skif! It’s Dark One! Remember how I tried to kill Strelok? I’ve changed my mind!
Holy shit! How is this possible?

Well, C-Consciousness used to have very… advanced technology. Now we need to figure out what the Ward wants to do with it. Anyway, it’s time to wrap this up. It won't be long before they sniff you out.
I’ve got an idea. Talk to you later.
sid_phrase_C_E14_MQ02_FoundationSpark_Pa: Dalin! Don't act up! This ragtag bunch can’t get to the Generators on foot. So what was the point of opening up X11?
Just do this, and soon the name “Dmytro” will echo 'round the world.
We need someone we can all trust to get those fucking hoops spinning. And we both know that the Representative isn't an option.
But my father…
Your father’s dead! He’s gone! I saw them boiling in those Pods with my own eyes!
The goddamn Visiograph is just a fucking talking TV set!
It’s hard to believe, but Subtle Matter is real, Colonel! I know it!
You’re not a scientist, you’re just a lousy piece of shit.
Why the hell are you stalling? You just need to disconnect one stupid device and connect another in its place. It's no more difficult than changing batteries!
Don't push me, Colonel! This equipment hasn't been maintained in a long time. We need to be extremely careful, otherwise...
Shh!
You got it? Here.
Who are you?
We are the result of an experiment ended creating a super consciousness called "C-Consciousness".
Father?
According to our calculations, Earth is surrounded by a special informational field, the so-called Noosphere.
What’s the true scientist's rule?
It links all the sentient inhabitants of the planet and stores their mental images.
Our main goal was to make small adjustments to the Noosphere, allowing us to remove things like anger, cruelty, greed, and other negative factors from the planet.
Dad? Like you said… What’s the true rule of a scientist?
Individuals are unable to affect the Noosphere…
Be quiet.
…but C-Consciousness could.
Shut up!  Shut the fuck up!
It must be erased! Destroyed! Eradicated! I want it wiped out forever! Without a trace, without pity!!!
SKIF!!!
Dig deeper, Dalin! Dig deeper, son…
sid_phrase_C_E14_MQ02_FoundationStrelok_: Dalin! Don’t act up!  This ragtag bunch can't get to the Generators on foot. So what was the point of opening up X11?
Just do this, and soon the name “Dmytro” will echo 'round the world. We need someone we can all trust to get those fucking hoops spinning. And we both know that the Representative isn't an option.
But my father…
Your father’s dead! He’s gone! I saw them boiling in those Pods with my own eyes! The goddamn Visiograph is just a fucking talking TV set!
There's no way to know for sure. But if there's even the slightest chance…
You’re not a scientist, you’re just a lousy piece of shit.
Oh yeah? Fuck you!!!
Let’s go on three. One, two, don’t fuck this up…Three!
Why the hell are you stalling? You just need to disconnect one stupid device and connect another in its place.
Don't push me, Colonel! This equipment hasn't been maintained in a long time. We need to be extremely careful...
Who are you?
We are the result of an experiment that ended creating a super consciousness called "C-Consciousness".
Father?
According to our calculations, Earth is surrounded by a special informational field, the so-called Noosphere… It links all the sentient inhabitants of the planet and stores their mental images.
What’s the true scientist's rule?
Our main goal was to make small adjustments to the Noosphere, allowing us to remove things like anger, cruelty, greed, and other negative factors from the planet.
Dad?
Like you said… What’s the true rule of a scientist?
Individuals are unable to affect the Noosphere…
Be quiet!
…but C-Consciousness could.
Shut up! Shut the fuck up!
It must be erased! Destroyed! Eradicated! I want it wiped out forever! Without a trace, without pity!!!
Skif! Help me with this!
sid_phrase_C_E14_MQ02_FoundationVarta_Pa: Dalin! Don't act up! This ragtag bunch can't get to the Generators on foot. So, what was the point of opening up X-11?
We need someone we can trust to get those fucking hoops spinning. And we both know that the Representative isn't an option.
But my father…
Your father’s dead! He’s gone! I saw them boiling in those Pods with my own eyes!
The goddamn Visiograph is just a fucking talking TV set!
What happened on the Orbita station, Skif? What did you see there?
I talked to Strider.
Just do this, and soon the name “Dmytro” will echo 'round the world.
It was just something that looked and sounded like him.
The Shining is real. Colonel, Subtle Matter is real!
You’re not a scientist, you’re just a lousy piece of shit…
Fuck you!
Whether it was Subtle Matter or Induced illusion, it felt like a mind-prison…
In every experiment, there is a probability of error.
You’re not a scientist, you’re just a piece of shit…
Fuck you!
Hermann?  What the hell?
I'm sorry about that show Skif, at the Institute. You had so much anger in you, I just wanted to help you to get rid of it.
A cold mind is what you need for our mutual success. Now, everyone is alive and well. Isn’t that wonderful?
He sold me out.
And he's atoned for it. You can trust him now. Don't be angry. A great cause needs a noble spirit. You don’t want to ruin our little party, do you?
Relax.
Get the Control unit! I’ll make this carousel move.
Hermann?
I'm sorry about that show at the Institute, Skif. You had so much anger in you, but you still did the right thing.
I don't know if you forgave Professor Hermann with your cold mind or warm heart, but… I followed your lead. I followed your example. Now, everybody is alive and well. Isn’t that wonderful?
Besides, Hermann is a great scientist. He’s every bit as good as… late Professor Dalin.
He’s the one who gave us something to celebrate!
Hello Skif.
Get the Control unit! I’ll make this carousel move.
sid_phrase_C_E15_MQ03_MemoryCube_Dialog_: If you only knew where I’ve just been and what I saw!
One day you’ll finish badly, Strelok…
sid_phrase_C_E15_MQ03_TheHardestChoise_D: The Monolith is no more, yet its cause lives on…
The greatest temptation - is trying to control something we do not fully understand. But what do we do when faced with the truly inexplicable?
Some tear it up to make souvenirs. Others keep it chained up inside the Perimeter cage.
Perhaps all the Zone really needs is our compassion. Perhaps the time has come…
…to let her choose her own destiny.
A tragedy most horrible, or a joy most profound. No one knows what the Zone truly desires.
And yet has a big heart. One that craves freedom… and the death of her tormentors.
He believes that he has been reborn here. So he will protect the Zone. That’s for sure. But how does one know the value of freedom if one has never been free?
sid_phrase_C_E15_MQ03_TheHardestChoise_K: We can't just unleash an unfathomable monster on the world!
Do what you must. Whatever will be, will be.
sid_phrase_C_E15_MQ03_TheHardestChoise_L: That means the Zone will never be free?
Do what you must. Whatever will be, will be.
Take the logs down to the basement. Behind the screen with the fireplace…
Just do what I said, please.
sid_phrase_C_E15_MQ04_CouldNotBeFree_Dia: And the bullet was well deserved? It’s a shame you treated him like that.
Tell me about it. You think I didn’t try to spare his life? But the Zone couldn’t be free unless he died. And it was worth it – just look at it now! No, Skif, I won’t let anyone have the Zone.
She gave me a new life, one that I’m ready to give back if I have to. I'll always protect Her!
You think I didn’t try to spare his life? But the Zone couldn’t be free unless he died. And it was worth it – just look at it now! No, Skif, I will not let anyone have the Zone.
She gave me a new life, a life that I’m willing to give back if I have to. And I’ll always protect Her!
The Zone doesn’t need your protection. She can take care of herself. Just let her go.
Your lips are moving, but all I can hear is the “good doctor” talking… He knew what he’d done. He would've done anything to free himself from his past.
I trusted him. We were this close to unveil the real secret of the Zone. Turns out he was just using me to atone for his own sins…
He saved your life, you son of a bitch! And you never even thanked him. Every time you went to the North, you were just thinking about yourself.
sid_phrase_C_E16_MQ03_DogOnALeash_Dialog: Stalkers call them “Death Trucks.” They were used to deploy Agents all over the Zone.
Agents infiltrated the stalkers and carried out whatever tasks they were programmed for.
They were totally unaware of what they were doing.
The hands and eyes of C-Consciousness. I’ve been looking for them all over the Zone… Now it seems they’re on the Mainland.
You know, I used to have this hunting dog. And… he'd run round and round in circles, chasing his own tail.
Back then, I thought it was hilarious. Now I wonder: what was going through that dog’s mind? One day he’d get lucky and catch the damn thing.
But what then? Bite it? Sniff it? Lick it? Forever drag it around between his teeth? And why… Why was he chasing it in the first place?
Maybe it’s not about the tail… Just the chase.
’Cause once a dog’s got his tail, what then? What’s the fucking point?
sid_phrase_C_E16_MQ03_Final_Skif_Dialog_: The greatest temptation – is trying to control something we do not fully understand. But what do we do when faced with the truly inexplicable?
Some tear it up to make souvenirs. Others keep it chained up inside the Perimeter cage. Perhaps all the Zone really needs is our compassion.
Perhaps the time has come… to let her choose her own destiny.
There is nothing you can keep secret that’ll not come into the open. It’s just a matter of time.
I used to serve the Monolith blindly, until she opened my eyes.  To keep life's adventure going, one must embrace freedom. Such is the gift of the Zone.
We are receiving numerous reports of unidentified anomalous entities appearing on the streets of our city.
Authorities are urgently forming a special commission to manage the situation.
Military officials are urging citizens not to leave their homes until further instructions from the special commission.
If you are outdoors or in an open area, please proceed to the nearest shelter or enclosed space.
If you are outdoors or in an open area, please proceed to the nearest shelter or enclosed space.
An address from the government to the nation is expected shortly. Please remain in a secure location and monitor government updates closely.
An address from the government to the nation is expected shortly. Please remain in a secure location and monitor government updates closely.
Do not approach or make contact with the anomalous objects.
sid_phrase_C_E16_MQ03_Final_Strelok_Dial: Fallen cog returns to the machine…
“It seems that this is the only way to unveil the Zone’s real secret – the one you’ve been trying to figure out…” Come.
Hope the old man wasn’t lying this time.
You. Slide that damn thing back up, on my signal.
…Anyone… attempting to enter or leave… eliminated on the spot…
Attention!… checkpoints are closed. Crossing… is strictly prohibited… Attention!
Attention! All Exclusion Zone checkpoints are closed. Crossing the Zone’s Perimeter is strictly prohibited. Anyone attempting to enter or leave the Zone will be eliminated on the spot. Attention…
Lower your weapons. Let this stalker pass.
sid_phrase_C_E16_MQ03_Final_TheSpark_Dia:
Wow! 
That's it. Stairway to Heaven.

Very nice.
My entry ticket… 
Come on.

Looking for O-CTA…
O-CTA… O-CTA… Oh there!

I’m going to go there. You're going to stay here. On my command, push that thing up. 
Don’t let me down!
Reality… is whatever you think is real.
Oh god, he plays so well!
Many stalkers owe you their lives, thank you.
sid_phrase_C_E16_MQ03_Final_TheWard_Dial: All this technology… Pods, wires, antennas and all that jazz…
Just so you can question less and serve more.
A tuft of hair… from a mangy old mare.
Thanks for the crutches, you old bastard.

Look for “O-KTA”.
Set it to “INITIAL” mode.
One last step for a spent man,

Thanks to you, we’re going to live in a new world with a bright future… 
I keep my promises.
You can stay with us, or…
You look tired. I’ll show you out.

Someone has to keep an eye on all of this. And “Colonel Martynenko” does have a nice ring to it. 
That’s your bird.
Condor-One. Whatever this stalker wants - this stalker gets.
sid_phrase_C_E16_MQ03_TotalRecall_Dialog: Poor Scar!
I knew him, Skif. A fellow of infinite jest, of most excellent fancy… and now, how abhorred in my imagination it is! My gorge rises at it.
Where be your gibes now? Your gambols? Your verses?
Prithee, Skif, tell me one thing.
What’s that?
Do you think the Representative looked o' this fashion in the earth?
I guess…
And smelt so? Pah!
What a miserable end, Skif! The Representative dies, the Representative is buried. The Representative turns to dust, the dust turns to soil, the soil to clay.
And who's to say that clay won't end up a stopper for a rusty rain barrel? And just to think, once he determined the destinies of all these stalkers.
So this is why in our own dreams sometimes we see their memories?
But the Representative is alive, only our enemies claim there's no life after death. If that’s the case, then there's no Shining Zone.
Tagan-tagan-tagan-ding-ding! If there's no Shining Zone, then all these lives are wasted in vain, Skif…
It’s not true.
Come on kids, the Shining Zone ain’t gonna find itself!
Poor stalker lost his arm, lost his head for this cause. Along with everything in it…
Jesus...
Scar?
Poor Scar!
I knew him, Skif. A fellow of infinite jest, of most excellent fancy… and now, how abhorred in my imagination it is! My gorge rises at it.
Where be your gibes now? Your gambols? Your verses?
Prithee, Skif, tell me one thing.
What’s that?
Do you think the Representative looked o’ this fashion in the earth?
I guess…
And smelt so? Pah!
What a miserable end, Skif! The Representative dies, the Representative is buried. The Representative turns to dust, the dust turns to soil, the soil to clay.
And who’s to say that clay won’t end up a stopper for a rusty rain barrel? And just to think, once, he determined the destinies of all these stalkers.
So this is why, in our own dreams, sometimes we see their memories?
But the Representative is alive. Yeah! Only the enemy claims there is no life after death. Argh! If that is the case then… there is no Shining Zone?
And if there is no Shining Zone, then all of these lives are wasted in vain, Skif!
No, we know it’s NOT TRUE!
I’ve lost my head for this cause! Along with all in it!
Jesus...
Scar?
sid_phrase_ConcretePlant_Hub_GCA_1_1_611: Why were you screaming in the middle of the night?
Well, I... I had a nightmare. A Rodent went up my pants, headed straight at my junk, and it was so damn fast... and so real!
I can still feel its little claws tearing at my skin...
Don’t worry, private, once you’ve killed a couple dozen of those little shits, they'll stop torturing you at night, I promise.
sid_phrase_ConcretePlant_Hub_GCA_2_1_611: Your warrant officer refuses to sell me more than twenty rounds unless I fill out a bunch of paperwork. What the fuck is that about? What am I, his fucking secretary?
That’s the rule. If you don't like it, take it to the general.
You just gotta make simple shit complicated? Hello! We’re not on the Mainland!
sid_phrase_ConcretePlant_Hub_GCA_3_1_611: Zulu is gathering a team to retrieve some documents. He says they might have information on how to take down all the mutants in the Zone at once.
We’re waiting for the right moment, but when it comes, we need to be ready. So you’ve got some time to think.
sid_phrase_ConcretePlant_Hub_PropTopic_1: Our duty is to protect the world from the ravenous Zone. Join us and become a hero!
The Ward isn’t going to help us anymore, soldiers! We’re on our own now, but we’re up to the task! This is our duty!
Now is the time for action! Duty will put an end to the Zone and eliminate anyone who stands in our way!
Join your brothers in arms and show the mutants what you’re willing to do to protect humanity!
They fed us lies for too long, but enough is enough! We will not falter, we will not retreat, we will do our duty even if it’s the last thing we do.
Join Duty! The innocent civilians behind the Perimeter need you now like never before. Protect them from the Zone!
We have a huge responsibility on our shoulders: protecting the world from the sprawling Zone. Do your duty and join our ranks!
sid_phrase_ConcretePlant_Hub_PropTopic_2: Stalker! Protect the world from the Zone! Join Duty!
sid_phrase_ConcretePlant_Hub_PropTopic_3: Everyone who joins Duty gets weapons and the latest equipment!
sid_phrase_ConcretePlant_Hub_PropTopic_4: We know that victory is at hand! We know that we will destroy the Zone! If we don’t, who will?
sid_phrase_ConcretePlant_Hub_PropTopic_5: Free stalkers, veterans, brave hunters — join Duty today! Save the world from the Zone plague! Save our planet and humanity!
sid_phrase_ConcretePlant_Hub_PropTopic_6: Join Duty, and help innocent civilians! Don’t let the Zone devour the planet!
sid_phrase_ConcretePlant_Hub_PropTopic_7: We are Duty! We are all that stands between the Zone and our loved ones!
sid_phrase_ConcretePlant_Hub_PropTopic_8: The Zone must be destroyed. Don’t let mutants and anomalies tear your hometowns apart!
sid_phrase_ConcretePlant_Hub_PropTopic_9: We’re closer to our goal now thanks to the Ward support! We’re unstoppable!
sid_phrase_ConcretePlant_Hub_closing_1_6: Attention, stalker. You’re entering Duty territory. Put your gun down, or we'll have to take action according to protocol.
sid_phrase_ConcretePlant_Hub_doktor_alen: Can’t you see I’m busy?
Yeah, go already.
You take care, all right?
See ya.
Quit whining. You’ll live.
I’ll still help you, don’t worry. Doctor's Oath.
Let’s get you patched up. You’ll be as good as new.
You did the right thing coming to me! It’s dangerous to neglect a condition like yours.
Well, speak up. What do you want?
Do you need medical assistance, or just dropping by for a chat?
Well, hello.
Here’s a little friendly heads-up: don’t go anywhere with Jager. He always takes the most mutant-ridden routes. 'Cause he's freaking bored, like that's an excuse!
It’s all a big pissing contest for those idiots, and I have to patch up what’s left of you. Damn savages!
The worst wounds I’ve seen here were inflicted by another human. Makes you wonder who you should really be afraid of in the Zone.
See what the Zone does to people? It’ll have us all at each other’s throats soon enough. It’s already started.
I can’t imagine how many boys will get killed in worthless skirmishes now.
Okay, are you gonna look around, or do you just need meds?
Need to get patched up? I’m having a hell of a busy shift today. Let me guess — stab wound? Sit down, please. I’ve had four of these today already...
Why would you ever leave the Mainland in the first place? Can't you see what the Zone’s doing to people? It makes you go feral. Always hunting for loot and fighting over it like wild animals.
Or maybe you’ve just got a death wish. Like Jager, a guide now, of all things... so? Cat got your tongue? What's bothering you?
I won’t sell you much, of course — I’ve got my own men to take care of.
Okay, let’s see what you might find useful.
Good decision. Make sure to restock your meds before heading out there.
sid_phrase_ConcretePlant_Hub_fila_ohotsk: Go get yourself some good loot.
Hera, the bartender here, is an awesome dude. You can't tell, with that sour puss of his.
I guess it’s time to get out of here. I have a feeling it's gonna get ugly...
Fucking pencil pushers and their fucking papers...
I could sure go for some potatoes right about now... fried to crisp, with salt and garlic...
sid_phrase_ConcretePlant_Hub_g_hyphen_l_: I should have you locked-up, not chat with you.
Glad to see you’re still in one piece.
Tell me about the Cement Factory.
It’s our main base now, after we signed the Treaty and had to leave Rostok to Freedom.
It’s a good spot. Not exactly comfy, that’s for sure, but it has ample storage, and it’s easy to defend.
Loners are allowed to trade here, if they’re interested, and we have a bar. We have a doctor with us too. Go find him if you need him.
If you want my advice, don’t rush it. Every decision I’ve ever made in a hurry led to a disaster.
Those Freedom guys can taunt me all they want, I’m not taking the bait. It'll sort itself out. You’ll see.
That was unexpected, huh? We’ll have to see how it goes...
We need to keep observing and stay put. We can still pursue our goal, even without the Treaty. Proceeding slowly but steadily...
General Voronin, in charge of Duty. You should know right away, loner, that we follow military regulations here, and there are no exceptions.
If you want to join us, you’re always welcome. We need more men.
The enemy is falling back. Good job!
Attention! Emission in progress. Stay in shelter.
Attention, Duty! Emission underway. Take cover.
Remove the body from the premises.
We seem to have gotten off on the wrong foot. How about we start over? Clean slate?
Oh, you want a clean slate now, huh? You do realize how much trouble you’ve caused us, don’t you?
How about fair compensation first? If you want to make amends, bring my men something useful.
I need to think it over.
You’re on thin ice here, Skif.
Okay, we can start over. But I’ll be watching you, Skif.
sid_phrase_ConcretePlant_Hub_gera_0_ByeT: If you need anything, stop by.
sid_phrase_ConcretePlant_Hub_gera_0_Hell: I’ll tell you straight away, I won’t let you cause any trouble here.
sid_phrase_ConcretePlant_Hub_gera_0_Info: I used to go out there, too, you know... shooting mutants, doing my duty, all that jazz. Until those mutants tore me up. Pretty bad, too. I almost spilled my guts crawling back.
But our doc here brought me back from the dead. Patched me up real good. Now I don’t even have a half-decent scar to show for it. He's a gem!
Still... after that, I decided I was done. No more running around.
Komisarov has really been helping me deal with this mess we’ve gotten ourselves into. He’s a nice guy. Always stays positive. It’s pretty inspiring.
sid_phrase_ConcretePlant_Hub_gera_0_Meet: So, the Treaty’s over. That’s a bitter pill... I need some vodka to wash it down with. You in?
Hey, a new face! That’s always good. Welcome to The Roadside. First drink’s on the house. But all the rest, you gotta pay for.
Name’s Hera. I'm here if you need a drinking buddy. And if you don’t mind listening, I can spin you a few yarns.
How do you like my joint? I remodeled it myself.
sid_phrase_ConcretePlant_Hub_gera_0_Trad: I save all the good stuff for my boys, so don’t get your hopes up.
If you really like something, I might give you a discount.
Are you looking to buy or sell?
sid_phrase_ConcretePlant_Hub_marsal_0_Bu: Got nothing else to do, eh?
sid_phrase_ConcretePlant_Hub_marsal_0_By: Beat it while you still can.
If you need anything, stop by.
sid_phrase_ConcretePlant_Hub_marsal_0_He: I heard you’ve been busy, Skif. Good for you.
sid_phrase_ConcretePlant_Hub_marsal_0_In: Duty ain’t what it used to be. No more raids, no solo hunting allowed. I didn’t join up to waste my time on patrols! I’m a hunter!
My only hope is the Ward. Maybe one day they’ll finally clear out the whole Zone. It’s long overdue.
To tell you the truth, I might be leaving soon. I came here to fight the Zone, not shoot stalkers. Maybe I’ll take up hunting, I dunno.
sid_phrase_ConcretePlant_Hub_marsal_0_Me: I don't think I’ll live long enough to see the Zone cleared out. It’s too bad, but it doesn’t really change anything for me.
And, as always, I’m ready to take you wherever you want.
Well, hello, stalker. I’m Jager. You might have heard of me. I can guide you through the most dangerous routes. If you’re up for it, of course.
I can handle myself all right. You wouldn’t believe how many mutants I’ve killed. More than anyone in Duty! No, really. If you don’t believe me, ask the boys, they’ll tell ya!
Okay, how about we show the Zone who’s boss?
sid_phrase_ConcretePlant_Hub_marsal_0_Tr: Tell me where you need to go.
You’re badass. I’m sure you can handle a shortcut.
I can’t guarantee your safety, but I can promise you some fun. So, where to?
sid_phrase_ConcretePlant_Hub_praporsik_k: Well, you screwed things up all right… What do you need now?
What are you looking for, soldier? Maybe I can give you some advice.
I might just be a cog in a huge machine, but I’ve got Duty and the Ward watching my back. We will destroy the Zone. It’s just a matter of time.
Our general is smart. Always thinking ten steps ahead. No enemy will be able to stand against us now.
Everyone should just follow orders, and the commanding officers will take care of the rest. We all need to do our duty, and then everything is gonna be just fine.
My orders haven't changed, so you know the drill.
Name’s Komisarov. Quartermaster. Authorized to trade on behalf of Duty.
There have been some disruptions in our supply chain, so not much to choose from. But if you really need something, I might be able to get it. I know how the system works.
Do you know what you're looking for?
I haven’t been ordered to ban you from the warehouse, so you can have a look.
Okay, you don’t need to bother with the papers.
I run a tight ship here. If you want to get something, you have to fill out all the paperwork.
sid_phrase_ConcretePlant_Hub_rad_molotov: So you want to join Duty, loner?
Zulu knows what he’s talking about. Duty has to take action. If we don’t, who will? But then, he was also the one who lost his whole squad in the field last time. Hmm...
I heard Freedom attacked our patrol, point blank, just like that! They definitely started it.
I wish Voronin never gave up Rostok. Now those Freedom bastards are all nice and warm in there, and we’re freezing our balls off in this concrete box.
Can’t wait to go on patrol... I feel like I’m gonna turn all gray and cold, like this damn concrete. I want to go outside, see the faded grass and the blue sky...
Fucking allergies. I can barely breathe.
sid_phrase_ConcretePlant_Hub_serz_bykovn: I’m not in the mood to chat.
Our doctor's been frequenting the bar lately... I mean, no wonder. If I was getting free drinks, I’d never leave the place either!
Freedom were the ones who broke the Treaty. Those sons of bitches ambushed us, stabbed us in the back! We'll make them pay.
This shit wouldn't be happening if Krylov was still in charge.
Why are we sitting on our asses? When was our last decent mutant raid?
sid_phrase_ConcretePlant_Hub_serzant_hma: I’d kill you here and now, and that’d be the right thing to do. But I follow regulations.
Don’t think you can gain my trust by helping Duty out a couple of times.
Voronin might be an asshole, but our boys here are even worse. Half of them go shopping with Freedom now.
They’ve got no values. Duty means nothing to them.
Why are you asking? Maybe it’s none of your damn business.
To hell with the Treaty! Good riddance. Now we can finally remember who our real enemy is.
I know your kind. You loners think you’re free to plunder any place in the Zone like you own it all.
And Voronin is a spineless little bitch for allowing it.
All right. What do you want?
I’ll make sure it doesn’t fall apart. That’s all I can promise you.
Took this from a stiff, I guess?
sid_phrase_DLC00_ItemHunt_Audiolog_III_m: The can of worms I opened, stalker… Now they will never get off my back.
Remember when I said I came here for the truth? Scratch that. I was chasing a hot scoop. It is true what they say: be careful what you wish for - the Zone can hear everything.
I'm going back to the Mainland, hoping to publish everything I've uncovered. It's the only way this information won't become a weapon in the wrong hands.
As for you, remember: do not trust anyone. Even those who say they are on your side.
sid_phrase_DLC00_ItemHunt_Audiolog_II_ma: Hello, stalker. I'm afraid we can't communicate directly. They're tracing my calls, which means they're watching me again.
My investigation has become a real pain in the neck for both my old enemies and former allies. But, since I rose from the dead, these trifles seldom bother me. Or you, I hope.
Our meeting will have to wait for now. I can only hope my stashes have made your journey a bit easier.
sid_phrase_DLC00_ItemHunt_Audiolog_IV_ma: My escape failed. They won't let me out of the Zone. And there is no place on Earth where I can be safe anyway.
Your story is a perfect example: it disappeared from every archive outside the Perimeter.
Maybe, it is a good thing I'm at the center of all these events. I have another way of communicating with the Mainland.
If you, like me, are not willing to give up, just like me, head over to Prypiat. The door code is 2021, you don't have to knock.
sid_phrase_DLC00_ItemHunt_Audiolog_I_mai: Hello, stalker! I'll keep it brief: I know who you are, and I can guess why you came here. My occupational hazard — always reading the news from the Mainland and making acquaintances that might prove useful.
Once upon a time, I came to the Zone to find the truth, too. So, I want to help you.

I will transfer to you the coordinates for my stashes. There, you will find the things and information I managed to gather over the years. The dead have no use for it, and I've been dead for a while, according to my paperwork.
I hope we will meet soon. And as for my stuff, it’s nothing compared to all I’ve received from the Zone.
sid_phrase_E01_MQ01_Comment_SkifNearPolt: Time to plant the Scanner and get out of here.
sid_phrase_E01_MQ01_Dialog_2_German_afte: I knew it! I told you to get out of there! Hurry back, or I’ll leave without you.
Calm down. I’ve still got some time left. Heading for the next spot.
sid_phrase_E01_MQ01_Dialog_FirstHelliFly: Perimeter breached! Extensive damage caused by… fuck knows what!
Copy that. Dispatching a response team.
sid_phrase_E01_MQ01_Dialog_German_after_: I’m okay. Is charging always this… noisy?
No, but that rock of yours is pretty unusual. Check the Scanner, quick! Is it charged?
sid_phrase_E01_MQ01_Dialog_HermanInsideB: Well, what is it? Any updates?
Nehoda’s dead. So are the other scientists.
Dead? Oh god... We're screwed! Get out of there immediately!
Stop freaking out and tell me where the data is stored.
Find his laptop. The coordinates should be in there.
sid_phrase_E01_MQ01_Dialog_HermanNearBun: Have you reached the bunker? Find Dr. Nehoda. He’s agreed to give you the coordinates.
I don’t think it’ll be that easy, but I’ll figure it out.
sid_phrase_E01_MQ01_Dialog_Herman_ArtOrD: Do you have the coordinates? I hope you do. Things are about to get a little more complicated from here…
Good thing it’s been a walk in the park until now…
You’ll need to acquire an artifact to shield yourself from the Scanner’s radiation. If you don’t, you might end up joining Nehoda.
First, find the detector. This is a Mobile lab. The scientists ought to have at least one.
Then head over to an anomaly – there ought to be plenty of those around. Use the detector’s signal to navigate it. That’s how the stalkers do it, so I’m sure you can figure it out.
After that, attach the artifact to your belt and go plant the Scanner. Just try not to get killed in the anomaly. That would be rather embarrassing, wouldn’t it?
Really? Impressive. Are you sure this is your first time in the Zone? Not that it’s any of my business. Attach the artifact to your belt and go plant the Scanner.
Find an anomaly – after the Emission, it shouldn’t be that difficult. Then use the detector’s signal to navigate it. If the stalkers have figured it out, I’m sure you can too.
After that, attach the artifact to your belt and go plant the Scanner. Just try not to get killed in the anomaly. That would be rather embarrassing.
sid_phrase_E01_MQ01_Dialog_Pripoy_in_Bun: Hey, Nehoda. What’s up? Have you met Hermann’s client yet? Tell him a new Emission means new coordinates. And new coordinates means a new bill, heh-heh.
Hold on a sec, Mr. Client. Did Hermann at least tell you what to do with the coordinates?
Whoa, that’s a hell of a mess you got there, boys! Well, screw that smart-ass bastard. I never liked him anyway.
Holy fucking cow, will you let me eat in peace for once?!
All right. Follow the coordinates and stick your Scanner into the ground, then back off. When it's all done, you pick it up. Got it?
If not, that’s your problem. I’m not a damn help desk. So long, Mr. Client.
I’ll figure it out, one way or another.
Sounds good to me. Well, the tea pot’s boiling… over and out, Mr. Client.
sid_phrase_E01_MQ01_Dialog_ScamPoints_po: Hermann, Solder's premium coordinates smell like a scam.
His coordinates have always worked like a charm, but the Emission could have thrown a wrench in the works.
sid_phrase_E01_MQ01_Dialog_SecondHelliFl: Where’s your team? I’m on my second lap here, and I’m going blind!
They’re en route, but we’ve lost contact. Falcon-3, investigate the scientists’ bunker.
sid_phrase_E01_MQ01_Dialog_ThirdHelliFly: Falcon-3, we’ve got a breach at the Duga. The bunker’s off. Another ground team is on the way.
South, I still don’t have any maps!
Stick to the Perimeter. Avoid going in. I’ll contact the Sphere.
sid_phrase_E01_MQ01_Dialog_Varyags_near_: Varyag-1 to Checkpoint South. The bunker’s locked, and the scientist isn’t responding.
sid_phrase_E01_MQ01_Dialog_after_2_point: Skif? Are you there? The place is swarming with soldiers! They’re gonna shut everything down, and then you’ll never get out of the Zone!
Did you hear me?! Drop the damn Scanner and come back right now!
I’ve got nowhere to go back to, Hermann. Over and out.
sid_phrase_E02_GamescomEmission_LeaderSt:
Listen up! We've got an Emission going!
Shelter in place, Stalkers!
sid_phrase_E02_GamescomEmission_LeaderWa:
Listen up, Stalkers! An Emission is coming!
Everybody find shelter!
sid_phrase_E02_MQ01_Audiolog_HermanLastW: Skif, come in! Come in! Solder sold us out, you hear me? That greedy son of a bitсh sold us out! I…
sid_phrase_E02_MQ01_Comment_Batya_Messag: Lone stalkers, the bar in Zalissya is open again! The first drink after the Emission is on me!
sid_phrase_E02_MQ01_Comment_RihterExamin: The bastard shot him at point-blank, then took everything, even the sensors. Fuck…
Now the entire Lesser Zone is gonna have to clean up this mess…
sid_phrase_E02_MQ01_Comment_RihterHello_: You handled it like a real stalker! Come over here, and let’s talk.
sid_phrase_E02_MQ01_Comment_RihterInTheH: You wanted to settle some scores, didn’t you?
Shit, the timing couldn’t be any worse…
sid_phrase_E02_MQ01_Comment_Rihter_on_Pl: Why is your ass still here?! Head over to Zalissya and keep your mouth shut – unless you want both of us killed!
sid_phrase_E02_MQ01_Comment_SkifCallsHer: Hermann! Over! Can you hear me? Over!
Fuck! No comms, a total shitfest all around. Hermann, damn in, what did you drag me into?!
Screw you, then. I'll figure it out myself.
sid_phrase_E02_MQ01_Dialog_RihterAfterAn: I knew you could do it! Well, okay, I didn’t really know, but I sure hoped you would. How’d you get here without any gear?
I was attacked while on a job. Got hit in the head. When I woke up, I was in almost nothing but my birthday suit.
You’re lucky they didn’t kill you right away. You should stop by Zalissya and check up with the doc, man. And talk to the bartender while you’re there, see if he knows anything about your attackers.
You know how to keep your mouth shut, good for you. The Zone doesn’t like blabbers. You should drop by Zalissya. The bartender is nice enough to rookies… might give you something.
Long story short – bad luck. By the way, I’m looking for someone nicknamed Solder. Know anywhere I could ask around about him?
If you’re looking for somebody, go to Zalissya. Talk to Warlock, the local bartender. He might know something. Or maybe not. It depends…
sid_phrase_E02_MQ01_Dialog_RihterExposit: If you have any questions, be quick. If anyone spots you here, the whole Ward will come after you.
An ordinary stalker. I used to be a guide, took people all around the Zone. Then I got lost, in every meaning of the word. So it’s probably not a good idea to stick around me for long.
The Ward guards SIRCAA, that big ass institute at the swamps. Or so they say. In truth, they control nearly the entire Zone.
Duty and Freedom couldn’t hold a candle to the Ward. They tried to resist, but Korshunov put them in their place quick enough. He made sure they wouldn’t mess with the “new world order.”
Anyway, stay away from the Ward. And don’t mention this around them, obviously.
Do you help everybody? Or am I somehow special?
To quote my fictional dad, “Son, help if you can, and if you can’t, don’t get in the way”. So, don’t get in the way. Or help, if you will. My men at the post office need a hand.
Bring ’em a couple of medkits. You’ll do good and make some friends, maybe.
A Warden gadget. It records everything around the squad like a black box. We’re lucky not every Warden gets one. If it ends up in the wrong hands, or worse, in the right hands…
What are you doing here, anyway?
Cleaning up a hotheaded moron’s mess. You better get out of here before you get that shit on you.
sid_phrase_E02_MQ01_rihter_0_CallPlayer_: Hey, over here! Let’s have a chat.
sid_phrase_E02_MQ01_rihter_0_DefeatComme: You got a medkit to spare?
sid_phrase_E02_MQ01_rihter_0_InfoTopic_1: Do I look like a radio to you? The best news is the news you uncover on your own. Or, better yet, the news you make. Unless it’s your obituary, of course. Gotta draw a line somewhere.
sid_phrase_E02_MQ01_rihter_0_NPC_Combat_: I’m gonna flank them. Cover me!
Fuck! One of ours is down!
Here’s a little present for you!
You ain’t gettin’ away from me!
Well, well, what do we have here?
sid_phrase_E02_MQ01_rihter_0_ResumeComme: So, what were we talking about?
sid_phrase_E02_MQ02_Audiolog_BS_Note_1_7: Hey Elbow, here’s a quick update: we had some sketchy stalker snooping around. We sent him packing toward the farmstead. Don’t sweat it, the loot’s gonna be top-notch.
sid_phrase_E02_MQ02_Comment_ASKRO_Done_1: Okay, here we go… Don’t even breath over there, got it? If we screw up now, you ain’t gettin’ any coordinates.
Aha, that’s it! Squint’s at the old mill. Oh, and thanks for helping me tune this thing. I know you had your own reasons for doing it, but I still don’t like being in debt. Here you go.
sid_phrase_E02_MQ02_Comment_BCBanditsKil: He’s stealin’ Elbow’s loot! Get the freak!
sid_phrase_E02_MQ02_Comment_BanditsNearB: Why are you loners always so eager to get here? Enough lurking, kiddo. Come here, let’s have a talk.
Do you even realize whose stuff you’re trying to steal? We’re Elbow's crew.
sid_phrase_E02_MQ02_Comment_Interrogatio: Told ya. I don’t know nuthin’.
And yet your friend says you used to talk to Squint all the time.
I talk to a lot of people. I’m an extrovert.
Zotov remembers those who help him.
Other folks remember who’s helping Zotov, too. I don’t want any trouble.
sid_phrase_E02_MQ02_Comment_KosoyWarning: Get up. Slowly. Keep your hands where I can see them.
sid_phrase_E02_MQ02_Comment_Kosoy_after_: Hurry up. The artifact in the cave ain’t gonna retrieve itself.
sid_phrase_E02_MQ02_Comment_Leans_on_lad: Lens here. Look, I created this system a little while ago to keep track of the rookies’ PDAs. You know, in case they go… missing after an Emission.
To locate Squint’s PDA, we need to tune in to three points. I’ve already got two, but the third one is on you. Try not to fall, by the way. The last climber I had took a good month to recuperate.
sid_phrase_E02_MQ02_Comment_MilitaryOnRa: Simmer down, everything’s gonna be all right. Stay low for now. Go mess around with Fleshes at the Cordon or something… Hey, Shuhayev, bring me a bottle. We’re drowning our sorrows tonight!

I wish I could join you. That old prick Sukhin has completely lost it. Says he’s gonna shoot anyone he sees moping around. Can you believe it? All right. I’ll try to figure something out about that ammo. I’ll be in touch.
Shut it down, he said. And I had a deal set up at the Eastern Checkpoint worth two cases of ammo! Bullets don’t grow on trees, ya know!
sid_phrase_E02_MQ02_Comment_PlayerRunFro: Where do you think you’re going, stalker? Halt!
sid_phrase_E02_MQ02_Comment_VartaStopPla: Nobody’s going in without the Captain’s orders.
sid_phrase_E02_MQ02_Comment_WadenAboutRa: Come here. We need to talk.
Stand down, Lieutenant. Let him pass. I’m aware of the situation.
Got nothing else to do, climber? This is Captain Zotov.
Leave the site at once, stalker. The radio tower is under Ward protection. Entry is prohibited.
sid_phrase_E02_MQ02_Dialog_BanditsAboutC: Why the hell are you skulkin’ around here?
Well, if a kingpin needs me, I guess I’ll help him out.
That’s more like it. Come to our base then.
All right, out with it. What do you want?
Not me – our kingpin, Elbow. He’s got an order to fill. You can ask him about Squint too while you’re at it.
He went toward the Farmstead. He’d need a decent hole to hide in case of an Emission. All the houses in the area are totally wrecked, so I guess he’s hangin’ out at the mill.
I’m sick of talking. You guys are boring.
Looks like the stalker’s bored, boys. Let’s entertain him!
’Cuz I feel like it. Do I need your permission or something?
Tough guy, are ya? Get this jackass, boys!
I already helped Elbow out. Guess I did it for free, huh?
Why didn’t you you say right away you were one of us, bro?
All right, I can pay you.
I’m looking for a stalker. Name’s Squint. You seen him?
Well, we saw a loner here. Told him to get lost. Didn’t ask for his handle.
Did you see where he went?
Do I look like an information desk to you?
sid_phrase_E02_MQ02_Dialog_GuideInfo_BS_: I’ve been to the warehouse. All I found was an old hazmat suit.
Well, not many folks get lucky on the road when Uncle Lyonya isn’t around. Squint’s no exception.
sid_phrase_E02_MQ02_Dialog_GuideInfo_Inf: The guy who did the Wardens in? Yeah, I saw him. Before they shut down the Lesser Zone.

He was asking for directions, just like everybody else. I told him to get a decent suit from Hamster first, and then we’d see how to get to the warehouse where they store the radioactive hazard signs.
sid_phrase_E02_MQ02_Dialog_HomyakKosoyTo: I mean, I can understand the guy to some extent… just between you and me, okay? All those riches just lying around, waiting to be taken – how could you refuse? But still, the asshole almost got me killed.
A walking honeypot, that’s Squint for you. If you meet him, give him a black eye for me. To think I got him a whole new suit in exchange for some guns looted from corpses!
You bet I do! He brought me some guns and sensors. How was I supposed to know they were taken from the Ward? At least I was bright enough not to take the sensors off him…
So, do you know where I might be able to find him?
He told me he’s gonna study some arch anomalies with Uncle Lyonya. The bastard!
sid_phrase_E02_MQ02_Dialog_KillOrFreeKos: What kind of artifact? How could it help you?
What do you care? It’s an artifact like any other. I just happen to know how to use it.
What kind of cave?
Just a cave. Nothing special about it.
But it has to be me that goes there. Not you.
All right – there’s an anomaly field there. That’s it, really.
I see. That’s all I need to know.
So, can you help? It’s a matter of life and death.
Got any ideas how to escape the Lesser Zone?
Just one, but I need an artifact. There’s one in the cave nearby.
All right. I’ll get you the artifact.
I know a stalker when I see one. Got a detector?
Who the hell are you? And what are you doing here?
I’m tired of running around. I’d rather just gun you down.
You’re not a stalker. You’re a murderer and a looter.
Easy there. I’m a friend. See, my gun is down.
Or maybe you’re a Warden bitch sniffing around here? Nah, you don’t look the part. Those assholes think they’re too good for stalker gear.
If you’re a friend, care to help a fellow stalker escape?
Nope. Do I need one?
Shit. Fine, take mine. I’ll get myself a new one if I make it through.
Put your gun down, would you? I’ve got one too.
You wanna play quick draw? Okay, relax. You’re not from the Ward, are you? Nah, I’m sure you aren’t. A Warden would never swap his rags for stalker gear.
Well, tough guy… Care to help a fellow stalker escape?
Was it you who attacked them near the Old Bridge at night? Why?
