At night? Nope, I found the bastards at dawn. One was almost dead, so I finished him off. You should’ve seen those assholes pushing us around a couple years back…
So many good stalkers had to die because those bastards wanted to show everyone who’s the boss in the Zone. A blind dog’s got more humanity than any Warden! So, an eye for a fucking eye.
Why can’t you escape on your own?
The pigs from the Ward have everything locked down. But I’ve got my own way – the stalker way.
I sure do. Why do you ask?
I’ve seen stranger things. All right, I’ll wait for your triumphant return.
sid_phrase_E02_MQ02_Dialog_KoldunKosoyTo: Do you know anything about Squint?
Not much, really. He only stopped by once, before this whole… mess. Had a few drinks. He was looking for a guide, so I told him to talk to Uncle Lyonya.
sid_phrase_E02_MQ02_Dialog_KosoyPlayerWi: Why the hell didn’t you tell me about that scary thing in the cave?
Would you have gone in there if I did? That’s why.
Here you go. Now hit the road.

Well ain’t you a peach, stalker! I can’t give you any money, sorry. Don’t got any. No gear either. Mine’s all worn out. Well, except for this Warden stuff. I got no clue what it is, but maybe it’ll come in handy. So long. Zone willing, we’ll meet again.
Yeah, and I’m keeping it. When the Ward gets you, try sending them to the cave too.
sid_phrase_E02_MQ02_Dialog_Kosoy_If_aliv: All right. I'll leave you to it.
Why do you hate the Ward so much? Not everybody’s capable of killing an unarmed person.
Right, you’re green… When the Ward took over the Chemical Plant, they didn’t beat around the bush. Gave us fifteen minutes to grab our stuff and get out. Anyone who didn’t make it caught a bullet.
Those bastards sure are. I used to have a group, back before Richter. We had a proper base… a good, solid place.
So I don’t have any pity for those assholes. Not now, not ever.
Weren’t you going to leave the Zone?
Going somewhere right away is a sure-fire way to get yourself killed, rookie. You gotta get ready for the Zone, gotta know exactly where you’re going and, most importantly, why.
sid_phrase_E02_MQ02_Dialog_LeansAntennaI: There’s another antenna nearby. It’s an ancient piece of junk, but it worked the last time I had it checked. This one’s gonna take at least a week to fix. Get to the other one. I’ve sent you the coordinates.
I know, okay? Goddamn Emission! I’ll have to replace the coils again! Okay, let me think…
Did you turn it on? Yeah, it seems to be working… I’ll reroute more power to the amplifier… Oh, fuck! Damn it to hell!
sid_phrase_E02_MQ02_Dialog_LinzaKosoyTop: I’m looking for information. About Squint.
Everyone’s looking for Squint – especially folks with fancy gear. But I’m a technician, not a search engine.
If you need anything fixed, that’s me. If you’re looking to wag your tongue, talk to Warlock at the bar. He’s a real chatterbox.
And don’t bother me about stolen goods, like Squint did when he brought me those Warden sensors.
sid_phrase_E02_MQ02_Dialog_RihterGotDisk: I told you I never had any doubts about you, didn’t I? He's an IPSF tech. A slippery type. You don’t look like the kind of guy Solder normally deals with.
My part is done. Now spill it about Solder.
And I’m supposed to believe that? If you’ve got skin in the game, speak up. If you don’t, back off.
We have a responsibility to the people we’ve rescued, you know. If you want to go to war against the whole Sphere, be my guest. But first, listen up.
But we’re getting off topic. Taking the Sphere by storm would be even dumber than walking into the Zone without any bolts. I strongly advise against this course of action. But I’ve got an idea.
What I was doing there is no concern of yours. But what you were doing there? Now that’s a good question…

I was checking on a new light anomaly, naturally. You could see it from the post office, clear as day. I looked for the anomaly and found you instead. What an amazing coincidence… One in a million, really.
Tell me more about IPSF.
Another security organization from the Mainland that thinks it can push stalkers around. They always stay behind the Perimeter, though.
I activated a certain device. There was a huge stream of light, the Ward, and three strangers who tried to kill everyone.
Yeah, I remember that little performance. You could see it from the post office, clear as day. I should have known you had a hand in it.
Tell me more about the Sphere.
They say it can be used to draw maps of anomalies. Nobody knows for sure, and anyone who does ain’t telling anyone. It’s a relic from Project X times. I’d stay away from stuff like that if I was you.
Tell me about Sukhin.
He’s the man in charge of the Sphere, and Solder’s commanding officer. If Malachite published a guide to the worst scumbags in the Zone, they’d put his ugly mug right on the cover.
For smuggling stuff he sold them himself, then realized he could get a better deal. If Sukhin met a grisly end, even Gaffer would be grateful – and he’s an honest-to-goodness pacifist.
He ordered his men to ice two stalkers once. You know why?
That’s not much. Is that all you know?
I knew Solder before… in passing. So that motherfucker Sukhin took him under his wing at the Sphere, eh? They say IPSF are mapping anomalies there…
What were you doing at the Old Bridge? Looking for anomalies? Or there was something more to it?
So that’s how you know him!
Just tell me what I have to do.
And who do I look like then?
You look like a man with a remarkable ability to mess with idiots. Which makes us two of a kind, by the way…
Seriously though, Solder is a greedy coward, always willing to stab someone in the back. How he ended up in IPSF is beyond me.
Then again, IPSF is home to all kinds of scumbags. But how do you know Solder?
I learned something interesting. After the Ward started raising hell, Sukhin ordered his men to sweep the backup communications center under the Ribs.
Why? Are there monsters there?
Worse — evidence of Sukhin's illegal activities. Get it?
You might be able to find something useful in there. Something that’ll open door for us.
sid_phrase_E02_MQ02_Dialog_VartaGotDiskD: I knew those sensors would lead me to the guys who killed my men.
And they did. Everything points at you.
Then I’m off to the Sphere.
Sukhin, the commanding officer at the Sphere, always was kind of an asshole. And now that we’ve cracked down on his smuggling operation, he’s gone off the deep end.
Problem is, we aren’t authorized to use force against IPSF. You’ll have to get inside on your own.
So perimeter security is keeping the Ward from questioning a witness?
The only thing in this shithole that can keep me from doing what needs to be done is a direct order from Colonel Korshunov. And I’m not one to question his orders.
What’s IPSF?
International Perimeter Security Force. They’re supposed to protect the Mainland from the ravages of the Zone. In reality, they’re kinda useless. Can’t even keep stalkers like you away.
Roger that. Guess I’m on my own then.
Hold on. We’ve got an insider at the Sphere. He owes the Ward a favor. He can let you in quietly. After that, you’ll just have to figure it out. I’m sure you can handle it.
All right, then. Good hunting. Damn, now I’m saying that too… dismissed.
So that’s why you’re looking for Solder. Did he give you that… ‘device’?
That explains a lot. Solder is an IPSF technician. He works at the Sphere. He must have used it to find the coordinates you followed. Apparently, he wasn’t working with you alone…
He told me where to turn it on.
Now I really want to talk to this guy. I hate scammers.
The Sphere? What’s that?
Only Colonel Korshunov knows for sure. I don’t have the necessary clearance. Rumor has it, IPSF uses it for mapping anomalies – and it looks like they sell those maps on the side.
Don’t you want to know what happened to Squint?
Not really. If he’s not dead yet, he will be soon enough. We’ve already written him off.
Ironclad proof, Captain. I feel like you’re trying to screw me. You don’t know where Solder is.
If I wanted to screw you, stalker, you’d be on your way to the Chemical Plant. Think before you open your mouth.
Let’s give it one more try.
In addition to the shootout, the sensors detected an Anomalous energy emission. Was that your doing?
That was the device I had on me. It was taken by the same strangers who attacked your men.
I was doing my job, and that’s when your men showed up. And then…
They were attacked from behind. Three men, swift and professional. No insignia, one’s got a weird mask on his face. Next thing I knew, I was knocked out.
That doesn’t explain much…
sid_phrase_E02_MQ02_Dialog_ZotovAboutKos: I found Squint. He’s at the mill over by Deaf Meadow.
I don’t need Squint, I need the sensors. Bring them to me.
I wanted to talk about Squint.
I’ve got the sensors. Now for your part of the bargain. What did you learn about Solder?
The warehouse isn’t the only way to find Squint.
We’re all grownups here, regardless of what Gaffer thinks. You know the job, and I don’t care how you get it done. All I care about is results.
Why are you looking for the sensors? Isn’t finding the murderer more important?
He won’t stay at large for long, with or without your help – I can promise you that much. But I suspect he wasn’t alone. These sensors should’ve recorded the guys who murdered my men.
And finding their killers is a matter of honor to me.
You won’t find him here, I’ll tell you that right now. Dismissed.
sid_phrase_E02_MQ02_hara_0_InfoTopic_1_1: I hear some numbskulls in the Zone are thinking about taking on the Ward. If there’s one thing I’ve learned in life, it’s that fighting the pigs is a dumb move. You gotta get away from them, and fast.
sid_phrase_E02_MQ02_korcnoj_0_DefeatComm: I’m hit! I could use a medkit!
sid_phrase_E02_MQ02_korcnoj_0_InfoTopic_: There are worse things out there than mutants, stalker. I’m talking about people. As a rule, the nicer a guy seems at first glance, the more of a monster he turns out to be. Just a side effect of the Zone, I guess.
Gaffer’s a good leader, I’ll give him that. Too bad he lacks discipline. Fortunately, we’ve got a crash course for just that occasion. No one’s complained about the results yet.
I’ve been here a long time, stalker. People like you come to the Zone looking for miracles. Let me tell you something: there ain’t no miracles here. None at all.
sid_phrase_E02_MQ02_kosoj_0_InfoTopic_1_: You wanna chat, huh? A little small talk about the weather, and then you’ll ask about my stash? I know your kind, trying to ride into Prypiat on someone else’s coattails. Well, that dog don’t hunt.
sid_phrase_E02_MQ02_lejtenant_novak_0_Bu: Not now! Can’t you see I’m busy?
sid_phrase_E02_MQ02_lejtenant_novak_0_In: I feel for your kind, stalker. But here in the Ward, we follow orders, not our hearts.
sid_phrase_E02_MQ02_rihter_0_InfoTopic_1: Have you seen the Poppy Field yet? It looks spectacular this time of the year. The sunsets there deserve to be framed and hung on the wall. But don’t get too close, or you’ll need a frame too – and a black ribbon to go with it.
sid_phrase_E02_MQ02_rihter_0_InfoTopic_2: Gaffer’s been getting soft lately. Sure, he’d still tear anyone’s throat out for his people, but… this place is way bigger than the pastures in the Lesser Zone. It’s almost like he doesn’t even wanna see further than his own nose.
sid_phrase_E02_MQ02_rihter_0_InfoTopic_3: Don’t get too enamored with the Ward. They know how to pull the wool over your eyes with their fancy uniforms and gear. They’re so polite, it’s sickening… But take one look inside, and you’ll find so much dirt, no amount of soap could ever wash it away.
sid_phrase_E02_MQ03_Audiolog_E02_MQ03_MS: All set. The tunnel codes have been updated: it’s 2765 now.
Is everything sorted at the Ribs?
Copy that. Now make your way back, and keep this hush-hush.
sid_phrase_E02_MQ03_Audiolog_VartaInfo_a: I see the scanner… Tracing for anomalous activity and radio signals...
sid_phrase_E02_MQ03_Comment_BatyaIntheBa: This place is a mess… I’m real busy right now. Sorry.
sid_phrase_E02_MQ03_Comment_EmisionFin_F: Hear that? It’s over. We can go outside now.
sid_phrase_E02_MQ03_Comment_EmisionScene: So no one in the Zone has ever survived an Emission?
Well… there are the Islanders. Rumor has it they don’t give a damn about the Emissions. But they’re not exactly the sociable sort. I’ve never seen anyone survive an Emission myself.
It’s like wind… Like a red, deadly wind. If it gets you, you’re toast. So if you hear the warning, run like hell to the nearest shelter.
I’ve seen the Emission on my way towards the Zone. Looked more like an explosion to me.
Who gives a shit? One thing’s for sure: if you hear that warning, that means the Zone wants to play hide and seek with you. And if you can’t hide, that ain’t the Zone’s fault.
Get out, man! It’s too crowded already! Go find yourself another shelter!
Who said that? If it’s too crowded for ya, take a hike! We’ve got one rule here: Zalissya gives shelter to every stalker.
But Gaffer, we’re packed like sardines in this basement!
Right now this one’s all I got. The Wardens took the other one, and they won’t let strangers in. Come on in, kid.
sid_phrase_E02_MQ03_Comment_Gate_Sphere_: What took you so long? They could’ve spotted me! I knew I shouldn’t have sold those medkits on the side… Did Zotov send you?
Great. I don’t wanna know why the fuck you want to get to the Sphere, but keep it down. If they see you, they’ll shoot you on the spot.
sid_phrase_E02_MQ03_Comment_Guide_after_: Take care out there, kid. Just in case.
sid_phrase_E02_MQ03_Comment_MSOP_Warning: This site is under IPSF protection. Unauthorized entry into the secure area will be met with deadly force.
sid_phrase_E02_MQ03_Comment_Malar_EastOp: Gaffer, come in. Plaster here. Zotov wasn’t bluffing – the Eastern Checkpoint’s all clear.
So I’m making my move to the Cordon, then onwards to Zaton to meet up with Beard. Let’s just hope he’s not sleeping with the fishes in the swamp. All right, over and out.
sid_phrase_E02_MQ03_Comment_RihterAfterS: Skif, my friend – business comes first. Good luck with Solder.
sid_phrase_E02_MQ03_Comment_RihterBatyaS: I knew this was your doing, Richter!
And who does Squint work for?
You brought that nutjob to my Zalissya, and now you’re making demands of me?!
Me. That’s why I need your help. I have to make sure the Wardens don’t get him.
I’m not demanding anything. I’m just asking. As an old friend.
Screw you. Old friend, my ass…
All right, sit down. I need to think.
sid_phrase_E02_MQ03_Comment_Temp_Emision: Incoming Emission! Take cover, everyone! Stay outside, and you’ll get a lavish funeral, orchestra and all. At your own expense!
sid_phrase_E02_MQ03_Comment_VartaSoldier: My name is Skif. Zotov sent me.
Yes, he told us about you. Get across the bridge.
sid_phrase_E02_MQ03_Comment_Warlock_afte: Hey, stalker! You must be new here. Come over here. Let’s have a chat.
sid_phrase_E02_MQ03_Comment_Zotov_Quaran: Attention all stalkers, this is Captain Zotov. The Ward’s investigation in the Lesser Zone is now concluded.
You have been granted freedom of movement once again, but be warned: any transgressions will have severe consequences. No more second chances.
sid_phrase_E02_MQ03_Dialog_Give_Zotov_Se: I’m a man of my word. Here are your sensors.
sid_phrase_E02_MQ03_Dialog_GuideBeforeBa: I'm trying to find a Solder. You know him?
Maybe I do, maybe I don’t. We guides keep our mouths shut. Ask around at the bar. Somebody there’ll know.
sid_phrase_E02_MQ03_Dialog_HamsterAfterB: If someone else had asked instead of you, I’d charge him through the nose. But you’re my friend, so I’ll tell you for free. Your friend’s name sounds kinda techy, so, I’d wager he’s a tech.
You’re lucky I’m not one to take offense, or you’d be stuck in the middle of the Lesser Zone without my gear. Hey, I’ve got a sale on ammo right now. It used to be a little wet, but I dried it out.
Always eager to help a friend! Remember, if you find something, I’ll give ya a good price! Keep it in mind!
sid_phrase_E02_MQ03_Dialog_HamsterBefore: I’m looking for Solder. Do you know him?
Oh, so you wanna talk, eh? The best talkers are at the bar. Have a few drinks, and they’ll talk your ear off.
sid_phrase_E02_MQ03_Dialog_LeanseAfterBa: Of course I have! I have no idea where to find him, but I’ve heard plenty of stories about Solder. He’s a hell of a tech, but as a man, he’s rotten to the core.
If you’re looking for him, ask around at the bar. If I were you, I’d stay away from that guy. He’d shoot you in the back, then charge you for the bullet.
sid_phrase_E02_MQ03_Dialog_LeanseBeforeB: I’m looking for Solder. You know that name?
I’ve got a bad memory for names. But when it comes to business… well, that’s a different story. A larger mag, a custom attachment – these things I remember. But if you want names, go ask at the bar.
sid_phrase_E02_MQ03_Dialog_RBHK_v2_Batya: Are you eavesdropping or something? I said I’m busy!
sid_phrase_E02_MQ03_Dialog_RBHK_v2_I_wil: Okay. I’ll help you with Squint.
And I’ll help you with Solder. Fixing the tower is easy. I’d have done it ages ago if it wasn’t for the Ward.
Just be careful when you find Squint. He’s never been the calm type, and now he’s totally gone off the rails.
But Squint is still one of my men, regardless of whatever shit he’s gotten himself into. Help him out if you can. And if you can’t, bring me the sensors he stole.
The Ward can’t get their hands on those devices. We’ve still got a mess to clean up.
sid_phrase_E02_MQ03_Dialog_RBHK_v2_Rihte: Hold on, Gaffer. Persistence is a virtue. Maybe the guy’s got something important.
Everybody’s got something important today. Zalissya must be hosting a UN assembly or something. So what do you want?
Hey, good to see you! Sorry, if I knew the Ward would get to Zalissya this fast, I would’ve sent you somewhere else.
I think I might even know where— What do you want?
sid_phrase_E02_MQ03_Dialog_RBHK_v2_Yes_i: Great. Then get the hell outta here, both of you. Better yet, head straight to Zotov. He’s in charge of Zalissya now, not me.
sid_phrase_E02_MQ03_Dialog_RBHK_v2_about: I’m looking for someone. His name’s Solder.
I know him. And I know where you can find him. But I need help too. Help me find Squint, and I’ll tell you about Solder.
We just need someone to climb that tower, Gaffer. It’s no big deal. I’d do it myself…
They’d catch you up there, and then I’d have to explain why there’s a Sparker hanging around Zalissya. Nah, sit tight.
Have you seen the balloon in the middle of Zalissya, stalker? We need someone to climb that tower and tinker with the equipment. I’ll tell the tech to contact you when you get there.
You’re gonna get the rookie involved too?
sid_phrase_E02_MQ03_Dialog_RBHK_v2_i_hav: There’s no need to climb anything. I’ve already got a lead.
Whoa! I told you he was a smart cookie, Gaffer!
I know where that lead of his comes from… I can smell the Ward a mile away.
Just be careful when you find Squint. He’s never been the calm type, and now he’s totally gone off the rails.
But he’s still one of my men, regardless of whatever shit he’s gotten himself into. Help him out if you can. And if you can’t, bring me the sensors he stole.
The Ward can’t get their hands on those devices. We’ve still got a mess to clean up.
sid_phrase_E02_MQ03_Dialog_RBHK_v2_no_il: Go climb that tower yourself. I’ll find some other way to get to Solder.
Are you sure? Because you’ll get no second chances with me. I’m sick of talking already.
sid_phrase_E02_MQ03_Dialog_RBHK_v2_no_th: You sure about that? You have the look of a man who’s got something to say.
sid_phrase_E02_MQ03_Dialog_RihterAfterBa: What happened between you and Gaffer?
The Gaffer can’t see anything beyond his precious Zalissya. You’d think it was his own back yard or something. Everything that happens in the rest of the Zone affects the Lesser Zone too.
Whatever... We’ve known each other a very long time. Let him blow off some steam and have a drink or three. He’ll be all right.
Thanks for the help. You can’t imagine how important it is for us to find him.
Enough chitchat. I need to go.
Squint can go… have a nice life. I don’t give a damn. All I need is the sensors he took from the Ward.
Is Squint a friend of yours?
Having friends... that’s for the Mainland. But sure, we used to be buddies.
He has his reasons for hating the Ward. He used to be an ordinary stalker, you know. He was all right sometimes, but other times... not so much. And then one day he just... snapped.
It happens to all of us sooner or later.
I’ve got what you are looking for.
Why are these sensors more important than Squint?
Because Squint made his choice. And the sensors recorded it – along with plenty of other stuff. If the Ward gets their hands on this data, Korshunov’ll plaster our mugshots all over town.
And then he’ll hang us right beside them.
sid_phrase_E02_MQ03_Dialog_RihterAfterSp: Skif, come in. What the hell are you up to? Actually, don’t tell me – it’ll just make me jealous. Did you find what you were looking for?
Solder doesn’t know shit, but he did mention a guy called Nestor.
Nestor? This is getting interesting now… Well, welcome to the real deal. Head over to the detention center. My, uh… friends there might be able to help you with Nestor.
Use the Northern Checkpoint. Ask the Gaffer for the key. Don’t worry about my men – they’re nice guys, if a little on the wild side.
sid_phrase_E02_MQ03_Dialog_StalkerOnGuar: A rookie, huh? Talk about bad timing.
The Ward happened. They're conducting a “search operation” here. Goddamn pigs.
Get to the bar and take cover. Maybe you can wait it out.
I’m looking for a man named Solder. Do you know him?
Never heard of him. We’ve got plenty of problems of our own. Try asking at the bar, but don’t blame me if the Ward grabs you by the balls.
sid_phrase_E02_MQ03_Dialog_Warlock_in_Ba: That’s how we roll here in Zalissya. These maniacs are gonna drink all my vodka at this rate…
If anyone asks, I’m at home. And busy.
So who should I ask about Solder?
Try Zotov. The Ward can find anyone you want as long as you can cut a deal with them… and you’re okay with their methods.
Or you could ask the Gaffer. Just give him some time to get to his house. He's an easygoing guy. He usually helps rookies out, but he's had to deal with a year's worth of crap today.
A force to be reckoned with, whether we like it or not. Personally, I don’t, but I’m not happy about the Emissions either. But I’m not about to go out there and throw down the gauntlet.
Gaffer needs to realize that sometimes you have to make concessions for the sake of your people. We already had to leave the Cordon. If we leave Zalissya, where are we gonna go?
See ya. Good luck. First drink’s on the house if you need it – and I get the feeling you’re gonna need it.
Don’t buy into that whole dog and pony show, rookie. Gaffer’s being uppity ’cuz Zotov lets him. If Korshunov was here instead of him, we’d be standing against a wall already.
The Captain prefers a more diplomatic approach.
sid_phrase_E02_MQ03_Dialog_ZotovAfterSph: Skif, come in. IPSF is having a meltdown on the airwaves, so I take it you’ve found Solder. What did he say?
Solder was just a pawn. He was working for a guy called Nestor.
Nestor? Hmm… that’s outside the Lesser Zone and beyond my authority. Head over to the Western Checkpoint. My men will let you out.
Go to the Chemical Plant. Tell them Zotov ordered you to talk to Colonel Korshunov.
I hope he likes you. Out.
sid_phrase_E02_MQ03_Dialog_ZotovIdleTalk: I’ve got a couple of questions.
I see. That’s all I needed to know.
Mind telling me more about yourself?
I’m Captain Zotov, and I’m in charge of dealing with “perimeter trespassers” – in other words, stalkers. Don’t even ask why Korshunov likes me so much. I don’t want to know.

First and foremost, the Ward keeps the Zone safe. The stalkers are good people, but they can’t see the forest for the trees. The Zone isn't a junkyard, it’s our shot at a better life and a new future.
And we’re here to make sure humanity doesn’t squander that chance… like it always does.
So, does Uncle Lyonya work for you or something?
The guide? Oh, no. The guides hate our guts. We’re charting their “unknown land of miracles” and taking it apart piece by piece. Why would they like us?
That’s why you’re going to the warehouse, and Uncle Lyonya is staying here with me, where it’s warm and cozy… and I can keep an eye on him.
sid_phrase_E02_MQ03_Dialog_ZotovKosoyTas: I hope you’re here to talk business.
How do I know you’re not gonna screw me?
We’re neither hustlers nor bandits. The Ward keeps its word. Sure, the loners call us… various names, but they’ve never accused us of scamming anyone.
I’m not going to insist, though. If you don’t want the job, somebody else’ll take it.
I want the sensors Squint took from the bodies of my men. If anything happens to him, I won’t be too upset.
Do you really think it was Squint who killed your men near the cabin?
It doesn’t matter what I think. If the loners decide they can kill my men with impunity, things’ll get ugly real quick. Rest assured, we’re going to find the real killer. But in the meantime...
Catching a looter who stole Ward property will suffice.
He’ll spot a squad at once. I’d rather have our friend take a look. In the meantime, I’ll try to find you some answers, stalker.
I think it’d be better to send your own men, Captain. They’re experienced professionals, and—
Easy now, Uncle Lyonya. Let’s just have a seat and wait for news from whatever place you want me to send my men so badly… you’ve got your orders, stalker. Good hunting.
So you’ve made up your mind? I knew the Lesser Zone had its share of reasonable people.
Here’s the deal: I tell you what I know, and you stop harassing our guys. Sounds fair?
We’ll see. Spill it out.
I… asked around. I think he’s nearby, at the warehouse. If you send a squad there…
Fine. What do you want me to do?
Squint is a piece of shit, but as far as the locals are concerned, he’s a stalker first and foremost. And he knows his way around the Lesser Zone…
A stalker like you might have an easier time looking for him.
I’ve heard you can help me find Solder.
You’ll have to help me out first, stalker. You know what I’m talking about.
Squint?
Exactly.
sid_phrase_E02_MQ03_ceba_0_BusyTopic_1_6: What’s wrong with you, man? Not now!
sid_phrase_E02_MQ03_ceba_0_InfoTopic_1_1: If you leave the bunker more than once a year, saving money's not safe. First, your coupons attract all kinds of scumbags.
And second, it’s better to put it to good use: fix up your suit, grab some medkits, buy a drink for a buddy…
sid_phrase_E02_MQ03_ceba_0_ResumeComment: You wanted to talk, so talk.
sid_phrase_E02_MQ03_dada_lena_0_BusyTopi: Hold on. I can’t go yet.
sid_phrase_E02_MQ03_garik_malyj_0_InfoTo: Stay away from the Ward. If you look at them funny, you’ll end up at the Chemical Plant. Some folks say they’re testing new guns on loners. Can you believe that?
sid_phrase_E02_MQ03_grisa_gornyj_0_InfoT: That’s what I like about the Zone. If you head out, no one bothers asking where, and no one waits for you to come back. If you don’t make it, no one’s gonna go looking for you. True freedom.
sid_phrase_E02_MQ03_pripoj_0_ByeTopic_by: See you later… or maybe not.
sid_phrase_E02_MQ03_rad_pilipcuk_0_InfoT: In the Zone, brass changes faster than the weather. Getting away from the fallout can be trickier than dodging an Emission.
sid_phrase_E02_MQ03_radovoj_asenskij_0_I: Listen up, stalker. I don't get paid to chitchat. I get paid to follow my orders.
sid_phrase_E02_MQ03_radovoj_asenskij_0_R: Are you ready to listen now?
sid_phrase_E02_MQ03_sana_bespalyj_0_Busy: Are you blind? This can wait!
sid_phrase_E02_MQ03_sana_bespalyj_0_Defe: Son of a bitch! I need a medkit!
sid_phrase_E02_MQ03_sana_bespalyj_0_Hell: Seems quiet around here. You can talk.
sid_phrase_E02_MQ03_sana_bespalyj_0_Info: I don’t stroll around the Zone at night. I go on my runs in the daylight. At least I’ll see the critter that’s taking a bite out of me before I kick the bucket.
sid_phrase_E02_SQ01_Comment_BanditsCave_: Come on, spill it, chump. Where’s the money?
Hey, Valve. Put a slug in him!
sid_phrase_E02_SQ01_Comment_BitokAgro_ag: Whoa, the chump’s brought backup! Get him, Hoshan!
sid_phrase_E02_SQ01_Comment_BitokWarning: Hey, loner! Keep moving, and don’t try any funny business. Show your ugly mug at the Post Office, and we’ll put you down right then and there.
sid_phrase_E02_SQ01_Comment_GlusakHeIsBa: Guys, the stalker scum want their crib back! Kill the bastards!
sid_phrase_E02_SQ01_Comment_GlushakAfter: Let him lie there and think about our question.
sid_phrase_E02_SQ01_Comment_LowHealth2_G: Get lost, man. Just leave, okay?
sid_phrase_E02_SQ01_Comment_StalkerCall_: Skif, come in. This is Zhorik, from the Post Office. We’re at the Magnetic Cave. If you're ever passing by, pay us a visit.
sid_phrase_E02_SQ01_Comment_ZhorikAndHmu: All right, you stupid asshole. Get over here.
Gloomy! You have no idea how glad I am!
Why don’t you come a little closer? Let me get a better idea.
Uh, I’ve got something I need to take care of, so why don’t you take a breather and cool off? I’ll be right back!
sid_phrase_E02_SQ01_Comment_ZhorikLetsTa: I wasn’t really expecting any help. Come on over. Let’s have a chat.
sid_phrase_E02_SQ01_Comment_ZhorikPostSo: The enemy's attacking from all around, A young machine gunner pinned them all down…
At sunrise, at last, the wearied young lad Fell down, wounded, right on his head…
sid_phrase_E02_SQ01_Comment_ZorikCallToP: Come in. This is Zhorik, from the Post Office. I heard some shooting coming from the Magnetic Cave.
That was me, mopping up your cave.
Had some fun, did ya? Good. Must be quite a mess, huh? Don’t worry, Gloomy and I'll clean it up. Stop by when you get a chance.
sid_phrase_E02_SQ01_Comment_ZorikHmyriyA: Were it not for you, I’d…
Kick the bucket. About five years ago. Now shut up and don’t move while I’m looking for meds.
That’s the last time, Gloomy. I had enough of your shit.
sid_phrase_E02_SQ01_Dialog_BanditsOppres: We’re gonna hunt ya down, chump! Think you can hide there?
Come on in, tough guy. I’ve got a special delivery for ya – a couple of bullets with your name on ’em!
Hoshan, check the windows! I’m gonna figure out how to smoke this jerk out…
sid_phrase_E02_SQ01_Dialog_HmuriyAdditio: So what were you doing here?
Whatever we were doing, it’s all done. What’s there to talk about? Let’s not, okay?
Even at the best of times, I’m not one for small talk. And right now is definitely not the best of times.
How long have you been in the Zone?
Long enough. People don’t end up in the Zone because their life were all sunshine and rainbows. Although it ain’t that great here either, as you can tell.
Still beats the life I had back on the Mainland, though.
sid_phrase_E02_SQ01_Dialog_HmuriySaved_d: Here you go. There’s an anomaly in the cave, appeared right after the Emission. Maybe the detector’ll help you sniff something out.
Well, congrats. Now you’ve got two.
sid_phrase_E02_SQ01_Dialog_HmuriySaved_f: Thanks for the help, stalker. But don’t count on anyone standing up for you if you run into trouble. You’ll be better off that way.
sid_phrase_E02_SQ01_Dialog_HmuriySaved_i: Well, who sent you? Richter, or that asshole Zhorik?
sid_phrase_E02_SQ01_Dialog_HmuriySaved_n: No one sent me. Guess today’s your lucky day.
Did I ask for your help? Or do you just have a death wish?
sid_phrase_E02_SQ01_Dialog_HmuriySaved_r: Whatcha lookin’ at? Expecting a reward? Check the thugs. I’m just glad they left me my suit— Wait a sec…
They didn’t take the detector! Those scumbags think they’re too good to dive into anomalies looking for artifacts. Easier to shake down a loner on his way out. Assholes.
Everyone in the Zone knows Richter. Even the blind dogs. He only shuts his mouth when he’s asleep – and even then, not always. But that’s not the point right now.
sid_phrase_E02_SQ01_Dialog_HmuriySaved_t: Do I look like a department store to you? Fine, fine… I left my backpack in the cave. Go have a look. If you find anything useful, it’s yours.
Unless the bandits beat you to it, of course.
sid_phrase_E02_SQ01_Dialog_HmuriySaved_w: It’s nothing. Those losers from Garbage can’t even throw a decent punch. The Zone’s not the same anymore. Even the damn bandits have gone soft.
sid_phrase_E02_SQ01_Dialog_HmuriySaved_z: You mean Zhorik set you up?
Abso-fucking-lutely he did! Zhorik can miss a whole Emission when he gets into cards. He won’t stop until he loses his shirt and his underpants, too.
It’s no use blaming him, though. You wouldn’t blame a Flesh for grunting, would you? I wouldn’t. But I might give him a good punch in the nose.
Zhorik asked me to get you out of here.
He asked you to wipe his ass, that’s what he did. You think those goons were brought down here by the Emission?
Zhorik lost a wad of cash playing cards in Garbage and figured he could hide out here. Well, the fucker wasn’t completely wrong. They didn’t get him – they got me.
sid_phrase_E02_SQ01_Dialog_QuestTook_ban: How did those bandits catch you off guard?
Easy. We were trying to snag an artifact from the anomaly under the Boiler House and totally didn't see ’em coming. Don’t give me that look. It can happen to anyone.
You better get going and rescue Gloomy.
sid_phrase_E02_SQ01_Dialog_QuestTook_tim: Don’t take your sweet time about it, okay? Gloomy’s not exactly having the time of his life over there.
sid_phrase_E02_SQ01_Dialog_QuestTook_you: Tell me about yourself. Been in the Zone long?
Not really. But, you know, I ain't whining – well, maybe a little. The Zone… there’s just something about it.
Here, it isn’t just “man is wolf to man,” it’s more like “man is chimera to man,” and there’s danger lurking behind every bush. And yet, somehow, it feels better here than back on the Mainland.
sid_phrase_E02_SQ01_Dialog_StalkerIdleTa: What do you know of Richter?
He’s a stalker like you and me. The guy grew up in the Zone, pretty much. I knew him as a kid. He’s a little bonkers. You’ll see.
I want to know more about this place.
It’s a quiet swamp. No decent artifacts to be found, no serious stalkers. If I were you, I’d go further north right away.
You’re gonna die either way, but at least it’ll be quick and painless up there.
I’d like to know more about you.
Are you serious? You gonna ask me out or something? Fine, I came here to serve and protect, many years ago. A lot's changed since then, as you can see.
Thanks, that’s all I need to know.
Hey, Gloomy. I’ve got a couple of questions.
sid_phrase_E02_SQ01_Dialog_ZhorikAfterSt: Did you make it? The mutants didn’t get you, did they?
That’s the Zone for you… Take care out there. And, uh… try not to hit Gloomy, all right? He may be a son of a bitch, but he’s still my bro.
sid_phrase_E02_SQ01_Dialog_ZhorikIntro_a: For real?! Dude, you rock! Don’t worry, I won’t just send you there empty-handed. I’ve got a stash close by.
It’s got everything you need: a gun, some ammo, the whole shebang. Whatever you don’t use, consider it yours! Just make sure you’ve got Gloomy’s back, all right?
I might be pushing my luck here, but could you lend me a hand with something else?
sid_phrase_E02_SQ01_Dialog_ZhorikIntro_b: How many of those guys are there?
About five, give or take. I’ve seen you in action – you can handle ’em easy. And if your conscience bothers you, trust me, they wouldn’t have spared you or Gloomy if the tables were turned.
What did those guys want from you?
Typical thug stuff: my loot, my gun, maybe my boots, if they’re fancy. They’re the scavengers of the Zone. What else can you expect from ’em? So, you’re new, huh?
sid_phrase_E02_SQ01_Dialog_ZhorikIntro_d: What are you doing here, anyway?
I could ask you the same thing, but I won’t. The less we know about each other, the better. Loose lips and all that, you know?
sid_phrase_E02_SQ01_Dialog_ZhorikIntro_h: Not that it’s any of your concern.
Fair point. In the Zone, getting into someone else's business is riskier than stumbling into an anomaly. Thanks for helping me out with those punks.
sid_phrase_E02_SQ01_Dialog_ZhorikIntro_i: You fucked ’em up pretty good, huh? I didn’t expect anyone to step in for me. That’s rarer than Mama’s Beads around here.
Normally folks in the Zone just walk past other people’s troubles. They’ve got their own shit to deal with. You’re new around here, ain’t ya?
sid_phrase_E02_SQ01_Dialog_ZhorikIntro_l: Yeah, figured as much. But you've definitely taken out a few scumbags in your day. Thanks for having my back.
sid_phrase_E02_SQ01_Dialog_ZhorikIntro_n: There's four of us at the Boiler House, not far from here: Squint, Richter, Gloomy, and myself. Squint is the leader, Richter is his right-hand man, and we'd just joined Spa… uh, a group.
Squint and Richter took off somewhere in the morning, and then me and Gloomy got ambushed by those bandits. I, uh… managed to make a tactical retreat, but the bastards took Gloomy.
I don’t know what they’re doing to him, but I doubt they’re throwin’ him a party. We need to rescue him and make those thugs regret it. Gloomy will find a way to return the favor.
sid_phrase_E02_SQ01_Dialog_ZhorikIntro_r: Nah, I’ve got my own stuff to worry about.
I see. Come back if you change your mind, okay? Things ain’t lookin’ good for Gloomy out there.
Nah, I’ve got my own stuff to worry about.
I see. Well, if you change your mind, I’ll be around. Seems safer that way.
Richter sent me here. Do you know him?
Sure I do! He came here with Squint, our leader. He used to be a guide, so he knows the area.
And like any guide, he’s got your back on the road – but don’t count on him jumping into a firefight with you. Best you can hope for is he sends some help your way.
sid_phrase_E02_SQ01_Dialog_ZorikArtifact: Hey, Gloomy! Skif’s got the artifact! Shell out my coupons, man!
I will, if he gives you the detector back.
The hell are you talking about?
He’s gotta give it back. Either the detector, or the artifact. The kid shouldn’t get used to getting stuff for free.
You take the artifact. I can find another.
That’s right, kid. See that, Zhorik? That’s how you raise a good stalker. I’m keeping the bling for my troubles. Don’t you ever forget uncle Gloomy’s lesson.
Lesson my ass… Good luck, Skif.
You can have the detector back. I’ll get a better one.
You’d rather keep the bling? Come on, it’s a piece of trash. Zhorik, your boy’s a street kid, not a stalker.
Shut up, Gloomy, will you? Good luck, Skif.
Fuck off, Gloomy. You’re asking for too much.
What did you say?
Man, I didn’t pull you out of that shit so you could get into another one!
Someone’s got to teach the sucker a lesson.
Do what you want. I’m staying out of this crap.
Hey, Gloomy! Skif’s got the artifact! Shell out the coupons, man!
Wow, a stalker just found an artifact! I’m shocked, positively shocked! The whole Zone’s gonna be abuzz for weeks!
You’re such an asshole, Gloomy. It might be the guy’s first artifact ever.
Pfft. Run along, rook. If you can’t figure out what to do with the artifact, sell it to Hamster.
Don’t go too cheap. Our sweat and blood are worth something, after all.
sid_phrase_E02_SQ01_Dialog_ZorikBanditTa: Remind me — what’s the deal with the Magnetic Cave? Are there bandits inside?
Yeah. The bastards took it over. Nabbed all our stuff too.
How many of them are there? What kinds of weapons did they have?
Sorry, can’t help you there. I've been a little too busy for math. I had to rescue Gloomy’s ass, not to mention my own.
sid_phrase_E02_SQ01_Dialog_ZorikIdleTalk: Got a minute? Let’s have a talk.
That stalker who sent me to you — who is he?
He’s a decent guy. One of the few good ones here. A guy who wouldn’t stab you in the back.
Got any useful advice for a rookie?
You’re lucky you ended up in the Lesser Zone. It's a good place to learn the basics before moving on. And when you get to Zalissya, talk to Gaffer. He takes rookies under his wing sometimes.
How did you become a stalker?

Like many others, I came for the miracles. Not the kind you see on TV — the miracles you can hold in the palm of your hand. Later on, I realized it’s not about the miracles… the Zone... well, it has a soul.
Thanks. That’s all I need to know.
sid_phrase_E02_SQ01_Dialog_ZorikInCave_r: Hey, Skif! We just got the place cleaned up. You did a hell of a job. Here, take this. It’ll come in handy.
So how do I use this thing?
Do we look like a help desk to you?
It’s real simple. The sound will lead you straight to the artifact.
I already have a detector. Don’t need another one.
Well, then you must know what to do with it…
Junk like this? Better sell it to Hamster.
Thanks, but no thanks. I bet it’s just some old junk.
Whoa. This is one spoiled lady we got here, Zhorik.
As you wish, Skif. But I don't have any more presents for you.
Alright, I’ll see what you’ve got in there.
Why give him a kiss on the ass for good luck?
If you know what’s inside the anomaly, why don’t you take it yourself?
Because all the artifacts around here are junk.
We’re not that greedy, Skif. A rookie like you needs it more than we do.
A detector? What am I supposed to do with this?
What rock did you crawl out from under?
Come on, Gloomy, it’s not like you were born a stalker yourself. There’s an anomaly field over there, Skif. There’s a present for you inside. The detector will help you find it.
sid_phrase_E02_SQ01_Dialog_ZorkBack_1_38: What are you doing here? I don’t have anything to give you — all our stuff is in the cave. I’ll drop you a message once we get there.
sid_phrase_E02_SQ01_glusak_0_DefeatTopic: Ouch! You got me, you prick!
sid_phrase_E02_SQ01_glusak_0_InfoTopic_1: Why’d I come here? Oh yeah – no laws, no cops, no jails… until the Ward showed up. Now I’m stuck here. There’s jail time for me back on the Mainland, and the freaks here are breathing down my neck.
sid_phrase_E02_SQ01_hmuryj_0_Combat_Star: I’m gonna strangle you with your own guts!
sid_phrase_E02_SQ01_hmuryj_0_DefeatComme: I’m feelin’ real bad. Got a medkit?
sid_phrase_E02_SQ01_hmuryj_0_InfoTopic_1: Heard folks saying you can juice up spent artifacts now. Prices are about to take a nosedive.
Never thought we’d be talking about this stuff, but now fucking SIRCAA's barged in. Those corporate assholes are gonna leave us all high and dry.
sid_phrase_E02_SQ01_zorik_0_BusyTopic_1_: I’m busy, Skif. Leave me alone.
sid_phrase_E02_SQ01_zorik_0_Combat_Start: You son of a bitch! I’ll get you!
sid_phrase_E02_SQ01_zorik_0_HelloTopic_1: Howdy, stalker. What brings you here?
sid_phrase_E02_SQ01_zorik_0_InfoTopic_1_: There’s too many new groups around, but we’ll manage on our own. I’ve got enough problems to worry about – I don’t need to be dealin’ with other people’s bullshit.
sid_phrase_E02_SQ02_Comment_MalarEnd_end: That’s it, finally! I’ve settled my tab with Gaffer. Now I’m off to the Swamps, where I won’t have to put up with greenhorns anymore.
sid_phrase_E02_SQ02_Dialog_MalarStart_fa: Back on the Mainland, Mom and Dad were the most important people in the world, right? But here in the Zone, it’s the tech and the trader.
And their love comes with a price tag – loot or coupons. Got it?
sid_phrase_E02_SQ02_Dialog_MalarStart_li: Here are the coordinates for our tech, Lens, and Hamster, the trader. Chat them up. If you’ve got coupons, buy something. If not, check in with Warlock for a gig – he's usually got something going.
Anyway, once you’ve mingled with the locals, hit the road. Just don’t bother me again. No one else is gonna help you for free in this neck of the woods, least of all me.

One more thing: if you find yourself in a dust-up with any of the factions, pay their boss a visit. Conflict is a part of life in the Zone, but there’s always a way to resolve it. Always better off with a lousy peace than an awesome firefight, get it?
sid_phrase_E02_SQ02_Dialog_MalarStart_no: Not really. I can figure it out on my own.
Well, suit yourself. Just a heads up – you gotta pay for your own funeral around here.
sid_phrase_E02_SQ02_Dialog_MalarStart_ok: Who could turn down such a generous offer? I’m all ears.
sid_phrase_E02_SQ02_Dialog_MalarStart_st: Here’s the deal: I’m ready to pass some stalker wisdom on to you – right here, right now, and totally free of charge. Interested?
sid_phrase_E02_SQ02_Dialog_MalarStart_te: I take it the trader peddles goods. What about the tech?
In the Zone, the quickest way to figure out how something works is to try it out. An enemy gets a bullet, an anomaly gets a bolt, and a trader gets a little conversation.
sid_phrase_E02_SQ02_Dialog_MalarStart_th: I was hoping for something a little more practical.
You think you’re clever, huh? Fresh off the Perimeter, and you’ve already figured everything out? Well, go ahead. Show us what you’ve got.
sid_phrase_E02_SQ02_Dialog_MalarStart_wh: Maybe. But first I want to know what the catch is.
There’s no catch. I owe Gaffer money, so I’ve been working off my debt by helping rookies with advice. And today’s my last day, so you’ve hit the jackpot.
sid_phrase_E02_SQ02_malar_0_CallPlayer_c: Hey, stalker! Come over here, rookie. We need to talk.
sid_phrase_E02_SQ02_malar_0_InfoTopic_1_: That’s the last time I owe anything to anyone, even to Gaffer. He’s a decent guy, just too strict. Babysitting rookies for three months... I’d take Prypiat over your sorry mugs any day of the week!
sid_phrase_E03_MQ01_Audiolog_ChernozemKo: Need some help here, Chornozem. I had a close call today. Think it was some thugs. Any leads on who might be gunning for me?
I could use a hand myself. The Ward’s making moves — the place is swarming with those clowns. Doubt it's all for show.
Why isn’t Nestor handling your shit? You’re his pet tech, after all.
The damn scanners, that’s why. You're lucky you’re not in this mess with us.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ01_Comment_AssaultBrief: Keep close, but stay out of the way. And try not to get yourself killed. Don’t underestimate these thugs.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ01_Comment_AvanpostFluf: Why did the Colonel come in person?
That’s just how he rolls. Get used to it.
Why would Zotov send us a stalker?
Stand down. It’s none of our business.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ01_Comment_AvanpostGuar: Turn back, stalker. This is Ward turf.
I’m looking for the Colonel. Zotov sent me.
Turn back, stalker. This is Ward turf.
I’m looking for the Colonel. Zotov sent me.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ01_Comment_BanditsAssau: Grab your guns, we’re gonna put those piggies down!
sid_phrase_E03_MQ01_Comment_BanditsWarni: You get any closer to the Depot, and you’ll catch a slug, loner!
sid_phrase_E03_MQ01_Comment_DepoFluff_De: You can have my jacket if the shit hits the fan, Ace.
Writing your will already? Fuck, go get some vodka from the Frenchman. You’ll feel better.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ01_Comment_DepoPatrol_D: I fled the Mainland to get away from the cops. And now boom, here comes the Ward!
Maybe the cops set a bounty on your head, huh?
sid_phrase_E03_MQ01_Comment_Interact_Ban: I don’t have any orders about talking to you.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ01_Comment_Interact_Kon: I won’t talk to you unless I’m ordered to.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ01_Comment_KorshunovAft: You've got a way with words, Skif. Now let’s see if you can shoot.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ01_Comment_KorshunovAsk: I’ve got another request for you, Skif – a very important one. Search Chornozem’s body. Everything else can wait.
This is Colonel Korshunov speaking. And you are?
I see. You're the man Zotov was talking about.
I've got another request for you, Skif — a very important one. Search Chornozem’s body. Start with his PDA.
The Depot is clear… No thanks to you, stalker.
That’s disappointing. But I’ll give you one more chance to find Nestor.
Search Chornozem’s body. Everything else can wait.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ01_Comment_KorshunovIfS: So you couldn’t keep quiet, eh, stalker?
It’s okay. Let’s see if you can shoot.
You'll regret what you've done here, stalker.
You might be good at killing, but wherever you hide, the Ward will find you.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ01_Comment_KorshunovOrd: Come in, Captain. I sent you a stalker. He’s going to take part in the assault.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ01_Comment_KorshunovPro: Initiate protocol 78-A. Seal Chornozem’s body and bring it to the Chemical Plant.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ01_Comment_KorshunovRad: Change of plans. An emission is coming. We just got a warning from SIRCAA. The assault is starting right now. If you want to live, join us. There’s no better shelter around here than the Depot.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ01_Comment_KorshunovSht: We’re commencing the assault with or without you, stalker. And Colonel Korshunov won’t be happy if you decide to opt out.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ01_Comment_SoloDepoReac: Some loner is raising hell at the Depot, Colonel. Should we attack?
Not yet. We'll wait and see.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ01_Dialog_ChernozemAfte: All right, I’ve got what I wanted. Now go to the balcony with your hands up. That way the Wardens will know we made a deal.
He’s some kind of technician. Nestor’s henchman. He’s pretty tough, but you can handle him. I can see it in your face.
That’s all I know. I’m staying away from shit like that. I ain’t messin’ with the Ward, neither.
Calm down. I’m here to talk.
Nestor?! Believe me, I wish I knew where the old prick’s at! He swore he was gonna broker a deal with the Ward, but now…
I knew his shady deals were gonna bite us in the ass!
You’ve got the Ward at your doorstep. Tell me where Nestor is, and Korshunov won’t send his men in.
If you really wanna get involved, go see Diode. He’s at the Slag Heap. Somebody tried to kill him too. He said it was ’cuz of scanners.
I’m already involved. I just need to figure out how. What kinds of deals?
All I know is it's got something to do with scanners. And everyone who’s involved in this business is royally fucked. Ask Diode for the details.
You’re out of luck, buddy. That’s all I got for ya. Can I go now?
sid_phrase_E03_MQ01_Dialog_ChernozemMove: Whoa! Just like the good old days!
Back on the Mainland, there were police sirens and dogs… Man, I must've killed…
sid_phrase_E03_MQ01_Dialog_FinishKorshun: Diode, Nestor’s tech. Maybe he knows where his boss went.
But I lost a lot of good men.
Now I’ve got a request for you. A very important one. Search Chornozem’s body. Everything else can wait.
And you thought you could just come back once the show is over? I don’t like your strategy, stalker. I hope you’re going to make yourself more useful in the future.
Looks like what happened back at the Sphere was neither coincidence nor blind luck. You’re pretty good with a gun.
Then we need to talk to this Diode guy. Head over to the Slag Heap.
It’s in the middle of Garbage, the most bandit-infested part of the Zone. You’ll see what I meant when I said the place needs to change.
I’ll reimburse you for all your expenses. We’ll meet again, Skif.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ01_Dialog_KorshunovAvan: They keep the place secure for SIRCAA.
In a way. But the truth is simpler than that.
That’s the kinds of guys we're fighting. So much for rebels and adventurers.
They only come here because they think no one’s going to be watching them. No one’s going to see them drink and fight… and worse.
Ambushing people. Attacking people. Stealing.
Looks like this place is getting under your skin too.
They’re taking over the Zone. Stealing whatever they want.
Do you really think I care about this lousy pile of junk? The truth is simpler than that.
But why is the Ward looking for Nestor?
Let’s put it this way: as the kingpin of Garbage, he suited my purposes. He knew how to keep the local thugs in check.
I’d like to talk to this Chornozem guy first.
If you don’t mind talking to bandits, I'm not going to interfere. We can delay the assault. But I have one condition.
After Chornozem answers your questions, take him to the balcony. I want him to face us and put his hands up.
Either that, or you can join the assault. It’s up to you.
I’ll join the assault.
I’m Colonel Korshunov, commander of the Ward.
And you must be Skif. I know what you did in the Lesser Zone… It's interesting that you've decided to work with the Ward.
That’s an unusually sensible decision for your kind. You guys usually think we’re ruining your fun. Do you understand what the Ward is really doing here, stalker?
I haven’t thought about it.
How predictable. Some people say we’re the scientists’ lapdogs. They say we want to drive out all the stalkers, hoard all the artifacts… but the truth is simpler than that.
Right now, my purpose is to find Nestor.
Under different circumstances, I could have easily arranged for you to meet him. But Nestor’s missing.
The last person who talked to Nestor was Chornozem, a murderer and a thief. He and his gang are staying at the Depot, and we’re going to assault it and get the answers from him. Are you with us?
So who are you working for, anyway?
Laugh all you want, but we're working for the future of all mankind. That sounds glorious, but in reality, doing this job means wading up to our elbows in shit.
You think you can change the Zone? No one’s ever succeeded at that before.
That’s because no one’s ever really tried. The Zone has its share of weirdness, but it’s nothing a man can’t handle.
We’ve got the resources, stalker. And we’ve got the best soldiers, hand-picked by yours truly. It’s just a matter of time.
Why assault it? The bandits would be more talkative alive.
But would they tell the truth? I don’t think so, stalker.
Besides, the Depot is strategically important to me. It’s on the only western approach to the Slag Heap.
With Nestor gone, I can’t have anyone other than the Ward in charge of the place.
I’ll join the assault.
I agree to your terms.
As you wish.
One more thing: if anybody sees you near the Depot, they’ll shoot you on sight.
We’ve captured one of the thugs. He knows how to get to Chornozem without getting shot. You can interrogate him. Good luck, stalker.
Smart choice.
My men are taking up positions. Report to Captain Shkarlat for your orders.
Once this is over, I’ll contact you again. I’ll have a request for you, and I’ll need you to do everything exactly as I ask. Good luck, soldier.
The Zone has been doing more harm than good for a long time now. It's become a kind of amusement park for scumbags. But the Ward is going put an end to that. The Zone is a valuable resource.
I couldn’t care less about stalkers and bandits, as long as they stay out of my way. But I can tell you’re not like them. You have a purpose.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ01_Dialog_KorshunovDisa: You’re going to regret attacking my men, stalker. The Ward will find you, no matter where you hide. We’ll meet again. I promise.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ01_Dialog_KorshunovRadi: Diode, Nestor’s tech. He might know where his boss went.
Then we need to talk to this Diode guy. Head over to the Slag Heap.
It’s in the middle of Garbage, the most bandit-infested part of the Zone. You’ll see what I meant when I said the place needs to change.
I’ll reimburse you for all your expenses. We’ll meet again, Skif.
You didn’t tell me you were going to shoot Chornozem and leave me alone against his entire gang.
I had to test something out.
I read Zotov’s report, and I was certain you could handle it. And you did. Besides, I knew the bandits would panic. They're just a bunch of thugs. No discipline.
The Depot is secure now, Skif. I've got a request for you – a very important one. Search Chornozem’s body. Everything else can wait.
What about his PDA? See anything strange?
A fu— looks like we’ve hit another snafu.
You did a good job, soldier. If our cooperation continues in the same vein, I might reevaluate my position on having stalkers in the Ward.
Nothing so far. It might help if I knew what I was looking for.
Now, about Nestor — have you learned anything?
sid_phrase_E03_MQ01_Dialog_KorshunovShtu: The assault begins in an hour, stalker. You won’t get another chance.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ01_Dialog_KorshunovSolo: Come to the outpost. We need to talk.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ01_Dialog_Korshunov_Cho: Well done. You saved my men some ammo. But next time, I expect you to stick to the plan.
Well done. You saved my men some ammo. But next time, I expect you to follow orders to the letter.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ01_Dialog_VartaKilled_I: I dunno who the hell you are, but watching you take out those pigs was pure bliss. I’d never have guessed there’s someone crazy enough to go up against the Ward and Korshunov on his own. But why?
I need information about scanners, and I’m looking for a man named Nestor. Do you know him?
Who doesn’t? He’s the kingpin of Garbage! I dunno where the old prick is, though. He promised to broker a deal with the Ward, but...
Hey, I've got an idea. Go see Diode, a tech from the Slag Heap. Somebody tried to kill him too. He said it was 'cuz of scanners.
Oh, and one more thing: sure, you helped us, but now Korshunov’s gonna bury anyone the Ward suspects of being too chummy with you. So stay away from the Depot from now on. You’re not welcome here.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ01_banketnyj_0_DefeatTo: Don’t just stand there, help me!
sid_phrase_E03_MQ01_banketnyj_0_InfoTopi: In the Zone, it's the law of the jungle — only the strong survive. And the strongest force out here is the Ward. So as long as you’re on our side, you’re not really breaking any laws.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ01_bazanuk_0_InfoTopic_: I stopped dreaming when I got here. I just close my eyes, and it’s like flipping a switch. It’s not just me; other guys have the same thing. No one knows why, not even at SIRCAA.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ01_bogdan_mehanik_0_Def: Spare me a medkit, would ya?
sid_phrase_E03_MQ01_bogdan_mehanik_0_Inf: I don’t give a damn what’s going on around here. As long as the Zone is full of chumps, I’ll be fine.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ01_bogdan_mehanik_0_Res: Eh, what were we chattin' about?
sid_phrase_E03_MQ01_bora_galuska_0_Defea: Why the hell are you just standing there? Help me!
sid_phrase_E03_MQ01_bora_galuska_0_InfoT: We shook down some losers who were showin’ off their brand-new guns. They were all like, “it ain’t fair!” and “we’re gonna complain!” Ha! Bunch of clowns. I mean, who are they gonna complain to?!
sid_phrase_E03_MQ01_cernozem_0_InfoTopic: Nestor’s a decent kingpin. A reasonable guy. Hope he didn’t bite the big one. I’m not in the mood to build bridges with some new jerk like the Shah.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ01_cernozem_0_ResumeCom: Well? You wanna keep on chattin’?
sid_phrase_E03_MQ01_denis_vorobev_0_Busy: I haven't got time for you, stalker.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ01_denis_vorobev_0_Info: There’s no commander quite like Colonel Korshunov, I tell you what. He believes in the cause, looks out for his men, and is always right there in the thick of the action.
Ask any soldier — everyone in the Ward is ready to face the worst, come hell, high water, or a damn anomaly, for the colonel.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ01_kapitan_skarlat_0_In: I’ve got my hands full, and I’m not going to waste my free time on chitchat.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ01_kapitan_skarlat_0_Re: Let’s get back to it, loner.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ01_konah_0_DefeatTopic_: What are you waiting for? Gimme a medkit!
sid_phrase_E03_MQ01_konah_0_InfoTopic_1_: For starters, the names of our Captains that everyone in the Zone ought to remember: Shkarlat, Zotov, Senkevych… you fellas can only dream of having commanders like them.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ01_parfenov_0_InfoTopic: The bandits have several areas of the Zone under their thumb, including Garbage and Zaton. In my book, that’s hardly a normal state of affairs.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ01_polkovnik_korsunov_0: There's nothing here but dirt and soiled mattresses. This is how it was, is, and always will be until we’re done eliminating this shithole.
You won’t get me here, you sons of bitches!
What the fuck, stalker? Are you shooting at me?!
There you are, you son of a bitch!
Fall back! We’ll finish them later!
No one's getting out of here alive!
sid_phrase_E03_MQ01_vita_tuz_0_InfoTopic: In the Zone, your handle is like your fate. I had a friend called Alex Burn. Did all kinds of stuff to earn himself a new nickname, even managed to scrounge up a machine gun.
He met his end in a burner, hauling it back from a farmstead. It’s fucking fate, I tell ya…
sid_phrase_E03_MQ02_Audiolog_Nestor_Log_: What's that got to do with me? I'm just a technician.
You're a tech involved with the scanners, Diode.  Everyone who so much as touched one of them is on the hit list. Do what I tell you and toss all the records and papers into the Burner.
Have you seen what's in those files? That's enough to get me killed!
Sure have. Why would you read them though? Calm down, as long as the Slag Heap is mine, you're safe. Come on, be quick about it.
Like I got a choice! Now I'm stuck at the freaking Slag Heap for good, tinkering with this stalker trash!
sid_phrase_E03_MQ02_Comment_KosoyMet_Lab: That you, Skif? Didn’t expect to see you here. Look, I got something for you.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ02_Comment_KunitsaStarW: He’s headed this way after all.
Let him come. I wanna get a look at him.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ02_Comment_StarTakeMyGu: Here, take my gun. If we end up in a firefight...
“The Zone likes it quiet.” That’s what we say.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ02_Comment_VartaBasemen: One, nine, eight, six. Nope. Let’s get out of here.
You seriously think that’s gonna work?
Well, the stalkers cracked it. Must be something simple.
I bet it’s the year somebody was born.
You bet what? You already owe me your salary and a half.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ02_Comment_VartaDrinkin: Have you ever been to SIRCAA?
Nope. I’ve got my hands full with local “miracles” already. I’m not interested in whatever Dalin is cooking up in there.
I’d love to take a peek at it. I wonder what the future holds…
A payday, that’s what. And a couple of days of leave if this guy starts talking.
Man, that’s the kinda of future I like!
sid_phrase_E03_MQ02_Comment_VartaGuardsm: Fuck, I’m sick of these cocky assholes!
Shut your goddamn mouth, or I’ll fucking kill you.
Do you have the Colonel’s permission to do that, you dog?
I’m about to find that out.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ02_Comment_VartaOutside: Are you stupid or something? I told you to stay away!
Halt! This facility is off limits.
This just isn’t your day, stalker. Go back.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ02_Comment_VartaYard1_C: We were lucky to catch that Sparker. The Colonel’s real pissed off.
We all are. Zotov needs to stop coddling them. We should run them down and set them on fire. Remember the mercs?
Don’t ever say that in front of the boys, let alone Zotov. We’re not the same as… those people.
Bullshit. All's fair in love and war.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ02_Comment_VartaYard2_C: How are things in the Lesser Zone?
Business as usual. The walls are full of holes large enough to drive a truck through, and they’re just sitting around a fire trading jokes.
I don’t get them, Koron. I don’t get them at all.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ02_Comment_ZmeevikMoans: Whatever happened to the Geneva Convention?
Can a prisoner get some water here?
How long do I have to wait?
At least tell me a joke or something!
sid_phrase_E03_MQ02_Dialog_KosoyMet_How_: What happened to you on the way here?
Bumped into a bunch of Noontiders. They had some kinda hardware with them. If I didn’t know the Monolith was done for, I’d have thought they were making those totems again.
I hid in the bushes right away, but I was, like, fifteen feet away from them. I dunno how did they missed me.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ02_Dialog_KosoyMet_UHel: I’ve been thinking some things over. But all that matters is that we made it here in one piece, right? And the artifact could have failed…
sid_phrase_E03_MQ02_Dialog_KosoyMet_What: Why were you trying to get here?
I guess I screwed up in the Lesser Zone. Listen, that guy at the Factory… was he pissed off, huh?
Really? Then I guess he’s forgiven me.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ02_Dialog_KunitsaAboutP: What do you know about this place?
It used to be a detention center — a prison, basically. Obviously, an environment like this is an acquired taste, so we don’t get a lot of visitors, not even now.
It’s pretty quiet here, and that’s fine by me. The basement, though… good thing it’s under lock and key. I think there’s something down there.
Serpentine mentioned he found the keys. I think he hid them somewhere on the ground floor.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ02_Dialog_KunitsaBefore: We’ll talk later. Head to Star.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ02_Dialog_StarMainTopic: You’re way too disciplined for a bunch of loners. Who are you?
You’re a sharp one, huh? We’re Spark. We used to help scientists unravel the mysteries of the Zone.
But now the whole Zone is in serious danger. And we're standing against that danger - the Ward.
What do you want Nestor for?
We don’t really need the old man himself. But he’s like a magpie: he steals anything that isn’t nailed down.
And I’d love to see whatever he’s got from the lab under the Factory — documents, specimens, you name it.
Take care, Skif.
I don’t know what the hell you’re doing here, stalker, but you got here just in the nick of time. The question is: can we trust you?
Ha! You just might be our ace in the hole.

We’ve got a certain interest in the basement under the Factory. And we found one of Nestor’s hideouts there too. He’s got quite an archive! And my gut tells me there are some clues down there about his current whereabouts.
But we’ve got a problem: the Ward. They’ve sealed off every exit from the Factory and jammed all our communications. And they did it while my men were inside.
We could really use a good man who can make short work of a problem like this…
You can’t trust anyone. A better question would be: what’s in it for me?
Fair enough. So what do you want? Money?
Nestor.
Do you trust Richter? He’s the one who sent me here. He said you could help me find Nestor.
I’ll see what I can do to help your… people.
You don’t need to go in guns blazing. The building is large and dilapidated. There are plenty of ways to get past the patrols and security checkpoints.
The last time my squad made contact was in the basement. The place you’re looking for is down there too. I’ll send you the lab access codes. They might come in handy.
Why don’t you rescue your men on your own? Am I really that special?
Well, I can name one advantage: you don’t really know us. Worst case scenario, you won’t tell the Wardens too much. And they’re experts at loosening people’s tongues... trust me.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ02_Dialog_StarReturn_Bl: Are you all right? Fucking Wardens. Well, at least someone got out…
sid_phrase_E03_MQ02_Dialog_StarReturn_Lo: Your men are dead. But I guess you already know that, don’t you?
I do. Their sacrifice can’t have been in vain. We have to go back.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ02_Dialog_StarReturn_Sc: Someone contacted me while I was in there. He helped me out. One of your men?
Actually, I’m one of his men… you don’t need to know more than that yet. When the time comes, he’ll find you.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ02_Dialog_StarReturn_Ta: I found something while I was inside.
Product 81! Good job! If not for you, another secret would have been lost to SIRCAA and the Ward.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ02_Dialog_StarReturn_Th: We haven’t discussed payment yet, but I don’t like to be in debt to anyone. Good luck. May you find your path through the Zone.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ02_Dialog_UnknowCaller_: I’d rather introduce myself some other time.
For now? Are you threatening me?
On the contrary. I’m offering you a helping hand.
What kind of game are you playing?
Well, well! The detention center sent word that a stalker is coming to help. So I’m gonna treat you as a friend. For now.
Your death isn’t part of my plans. I suggest you come to me.
Up the stairs. I sent you the code to the door.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ02_Dialog_UnknownLeaves: It was nice meeting you. I’ll see you around, Skif.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ02_Dialog_VartaOutside1: The Ward is currently securing the area.
That’s swell. Can I go in?
No trespassing, stalker. Cross the line, and I’ll shoot to kill.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ02_Dialog_VartaOutside2: I want to talk to your commander.
That’s impossible. All communications with civilians are handled by authorized staff at the center. If you’re up for it, you can go to the Chemical Plant.
Sorry, pal. Can’t let you in.
We’re pursuing a criminal gang. I can’t give you any details, but we don’t want anyone getting in the way in case there’s an armed confrontation.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ02_Dialog_ZmeevikMainTo: Star sent me. Where are the others?
In the tunnels below, two floors down. The key… Star told you, right? Go. I can handle this.
If you find any classified documents downstairs, bring them to the Detention Center. We pay good money for stuff like that.
He’s got an archive of sorts down in the tunnels. You can probably learn more there — more than I know, anyway. I’ll give you the code. You opened my door, so I’ll open yours.
What do you know about Nestor?
The Ward’s been watching this place, and we didn’t realize it until it was too late. I got wounded and stayed behind to cover the others.
I didn’t sell out any of our men, if that’s what you’re worried about.
So you’re gonna kill me? After I told you about Nestor? So much for your word of honor… wait, you’re not one of them.
I’d get up and shake your hand if it wasn’t for my wounds. Are you one of Nestor’s men?
Did you sound the alarm? Don’t worry about me. Finish those dogs off before they call for help.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ02_kunica_0_InfoTopic_1: Here’s a piece of advice: if you even think about setting up a stash in a mutant lair, think again.
For one, mutants can steal stuff, too. And if you need something, you won’t have time to crawl about on your belly.
But, most importantly, you’re not the only smartass out there. Trust me, I got my first good gear from stashes like those.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ02_l_hyphen_t_kostenko_: I don’t keep up with the news, and you shouldn’t either. Talk is cheap. Those who act keep their mouths shut.
It’s quiet now. All right then…
sid_phrase_E03_MQ02_lejtenant_koronov_0_: Is it urgent? Ask me later.
Hey, stalker, did you know that before the Ward came into the picture, Freedom and Duty were constantly at each other’s throats? Always fighting over some dumb ideology.
I’m glad Korshunov made them sign the Treaty, along with Noontide and the Corps. This place has seen enough senseless death as it is.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ02_lejtenant_sebenak_0_: Yesterday, the anomaly was in one spot; today, it’s somewhere else. You can’t count on anything here — except for your fellow Wardens.
I’m in a bit of a hurry, but let's get back to it.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ02_radovoj_cuprik_0_Inf: All you stalkers ever leave behind is a stupid handle. Meanwhile, the Ward’s leaders will be honored with the highest awards for restoring order in the Zone.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ02_radovoj_pilipenko_0_: I don’t have time to talk right now.
Talk to you later... much later.
Just looking at you makes it clear why the Ward doesn’t enlist stalkers, and I don’t see that changing anytime soon.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ02_radovoj_usufov_0_Inf: I’ve only met Colonel Korshunov once, but let me tell you, everyone in the Zone should consider themselves fortunate that he’s still holding back.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ02_serzant_gubin_0_Info: I spotted some zombies while on patrol once. That kind of “dishonorable discharge” is something I wouldn’t wish on my worst enemy.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ02_serzant_gubin_0_Resu: Well, let’s get this over with.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ02_serzant_sivko_0_Defe: Think you could patch me up?
sid_phrase_E03_MQ02_serzant_sivko_0_Info: This one loner tried to buy a SIRCAA pass from me. Now he’s chilling in the detention center at the Chemical Plant. He shouldn’t have insulted my professional pride like that.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ02_serzant_stepanuk_0_B: I don’t have time for this, man.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ02_serzant_vada_0_InfoT: I get what makes people come to the Zone, but what baffles me is why they choose to stick around.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ02_sram_0_CallPlayer_1_: Come over here, stalker. Let’s have a chat.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ02_sram_0_InfoTopic_1_1: The road to the Shining Zone is a long one, and full of hardships. Those who tread it must find their own answers. But for everyone in Spark, one thing is clear: the Ward must be destroyed!
sid_phrase_E03_MQ02_sram_0_NPC_Combat_Ac: I’ll hit them where they least expect it!
That’s one less problem to deal with.
Hit them with everything you've got!
Another one has left for the Shining Zone.
Are you Faust or something? Watch where you shoot!
sid_phrase_E03_MQ02_sram_0_NPC_Combat_En: They’re running away, ha! There you go.
You better come out, or else!
sid_phrase_E03_MQ02_sram_0_NPC_Peaceful_: Made a friend? Find some other place to chat him up.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ02_star_3_InfoTopic_1_1: You can’t demand anything from the Zone or try to seize it by force. You can only earn it.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ02_star_3_ResumeComment: Now’s a good time to talk.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ02_zmeevik_0_InfoTopic_: Mark my words: pretty soon the Wardens will claim the entire Zone as their turf, from the Swamps to Prypiat. Never mind that it's always belonged to us stalkers… they just don’t give a rat’s ass.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ03_Comment_CypaInBar_Go: Move along, chump. Leave me alone.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ03_Comment_FinikOnExit_: How’d you get in here? Using the old passage, huh? Clever.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ03_Comment_FinikSleep1_: Shoo, Granny… let me get some sleep…
sid_phrase_E03_MQ03_Comment_FinikSleep2_: I’m getting up, Granny… just gimme a minute…
sid_phrase_E03_MQ03_Comment_HrusOnExit_S: If Roosevelt’s letting you go, I won’t keep you here. But how’d you get inside? I don’t get it…
sid_phrase_E03_MQ03_Comment_RicoWarn_war: Where the hell are you going, stalker? If you wanna end up six feet under, I’d be happy to oblige. The boss is on the second floor. Head up there while I’m feeling charitable.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ03_Comment_RooseveltAft: I thought we were done here. Now go. I want to be alone.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ03_Comment_RooseveltCal: I see you’re also a man of your word. I respect that.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ03_Comment_RooseveltToH: A loner is leaving my office. Don’t stop him. Let him go.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ03_Comment_SahMikluha_E: And keep your ambitions in check from now on. Do we understand each other?
sid_phrase_E03_MQ03_Comment_VodnijOnAtta: Guys, someone’s killing Shah! Help the boss!
sid_phrase_E03_MQ03_Comment_VodnijOnPass: Are you lost, stalker? Turn right and get the fuck outta here!
sid_phrase_E03_MQ03_Comment_VodnijWhenNe: This is my spot! Fuck off!
sid_phrase_E03_MQ03_Comment_VoentorgGuar: Freeze, motherfucker! I mean halt, stalker. This is the Commissary. You can’t go in without a personal invitation from Roosevelt.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ03_Comment_ZemelaIfBoth: Come on, whatcha waiting for? Second floor.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ03_Comment_ZemelaOnAtta: You can save that motherfucker yourself. Let’s get outta here, boys!
sid_phrase_E03_MQ03_Dialog_FinikWokeUp_A: Come on, Granny… fuck! A trespasser!
sid_phrase_E03_MQ03_Dialog_RooseveltWant: I’ll take your word for it.
Then good luck. Talk to you soon.
Some information I couldn’t get anywhere else.
That’s a high price.
Coupons. A lot of them.
Business as usual.
I need your PDA to convince the client you’re actually dead.
That dog won’t hunt, stalker. My whole life is on that PDA — my deals, my connections, my money… you might as just shoot me.
Guess we can’t make a deal.
Too bad.
We don’t have anything to talk about.
Fucking Garbage... nothing but scum.
That’s none of your business.
Whatever you say.
All right. I’m listening.
Good. Here’s the deal: I’ll tell my guys to let you out, and you’ll leave the Commissary base real quiet-like.
Once you’re out, I’ll contact you about the reward. You have my word as a member of Duty.
Who are you? What are you doing here?
Did the Shah finally hire someone who could slip past my guards? Or was it Varan? Eh, it doesn’t matter. If you want to start shooting, take your chances. As for me, I’d rather talk.
Well, speak up.
First of all, I’d like to know what they offered you for killing me.
There’s just one thing: nobody’s going to pay you. You must be new to the Zone if you don’t know that. You can only trust the bandits as long as you’re holding a gun to their head.
What about you?

Don’t compare me to the likes of Varan and the Shah. I served in Duty for ten years, and I might just go back one day. But first I need to show certain wannabe generals how to take out the trash in the Zone.
The bandits aren’t people like you and me, loner. They’re more like Rodents — dumb, greedy rodents. And unless you deal with them, they breed like rodents. That’s why Garbage is crawling with them.
But that’s my problem. Let’s make a deal. I still have connections in Duty, and I'll pay good money for my life. Let’s settle this like civilized men, okay?
I need something more substantial than just your word.
Too bad. Then we don’t have a deal.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ03_Dialog_SahWantsSkifD: Die!
Docent handed your stash over to me.

Hold on... why’d you give him up so easy? Trying to turn me against my own men, are you? No dice! Docent is a dickhead, but the rest are willing to die for me! I’m in charge here! I'm the Shah, dammit!
I knew I shouldn’t have trusted that asshole! Always walking around with that sour puss on him... fucking rat! I’ll make him shit blood for the rest of his miserable life!
Why should I tell you? You’re already dead.
You bastard!
I wanted to see your face once you realized I’ve got your gun.
Seen enough?
‘Cuz that’s the last thing you’re ever gonna see, asshole!
I want some coupons in exchange for your gun.
That’s one hell of a business plan. You oughtta join the scammers in Rostok.
You really think I’m gonna pay you for my own gun, shitbird? Do you have any idea who you’re fucking with?!
I found it on my own.
Lucky bastard. If I were that lucky, I would've taken over the whole of Garbage in two days.
Except I don’t believe in luck, so cut the crap. You think I’m stupid? Who sold you the stash?!
I need your PDA. I can trade it for the gun, or I can just take it.
So you wanna know about my deals? Use my connections? None of that’s gonna help you, stalker.
Where the Shah succeeds, a loser bleeds.
I got the coordinates from Roosevelt.
Why don’t we start with you? You and Roosevelt must be real chummy if he’s handing other people’s stashes over to you. If I’d known, my guys would have put you in the dirt right away!
That fucking Duty prick! I wonder who the sellout is… who should I toss in the burner?
So why’d you come here? You want money?
Who the fuck are you? Oh, so you brought my gun, eh? Well? Who’s the snitch bitch? Who sold you my stash? Yokel? Bruise?! Spit it out!
sid_phrase_E03_MQ03_Dialog_ZemelaGuard_A: I told you we don’t let loners in.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ03_Dialog_ZemelaGuard_D: I don’t give a damn about your rules.
Are you fucking nuts?! I’ll kill you!
sid_phrase_E03_MQ03_Dialog_ZemelaGuard_G: Got it. Don't get your panties in a bunch, I’ll stay out.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ03_Dialog_ZemelaGuard_H: I’ve got a pass. This is your kingpin’s gun. Recognize it?
Holy shit... you got a death wish? Fine, come on in. Take the stairs to the second floor. The Shah’s gonna want to talk to you personally.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ03_Dialog_ZemelaGuard_W: Where the fuck are you going? This bar is for the Brood. No freaks or loners allowed.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ03_finik_0_InfoTopic_1_: I don’t regret signing up with this crew. Sure, Roosevelt is a bit of a weirdo — he shits on the way we talk, and he’s a bear about booze… but at least he doesn’t steal money from his men, unlike the Shah.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ03_finik_0_ResumeCommen: Weren’t we done shooting the breeze?
sid_phrase_E03_MQ03_hrus_0_InfoTopic_1_w: Our boss is all right, but he’s kinda lousy at making friends. Always keeps to himself.
Then again, if my ex-buddies were throwing vodka bottles at my windows and shouting about what a prick I am, I wouldn’t get along with people either.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ03_riko_0_HelloTopic_1_: Don’t you have anyone else to bother?
