sid_phrase_E03_MQ03_riko_0_InfoTopic_1_1: The obituaries of them chumps who bothered me with that stupid question.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ03_ruzvelt_0_InfoTopic_: The Zone attracts all kinds of scumbags, but I’ve learned to make do with what I’ve got. Some of them can even be molded, if you give them a purpose and a little discipline.
Just wait till you see Garbage turn into a half-decent place.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ03_sah_0_InfoTopic_1_1_: Fuck, those coupons are the real deal. You do a gig, and your account shoots up right away. No prancing around the Zone. But damn, I miss that feel of holding my cash, counting it, sniffing it…
sid_phrase_E03_MQ03_sinak_0_InfoTopic_1_: I heard Roosevelt prohibited booze at the Commissary. Can you believe that? He’s treating his men like dogs.
Why do guys even join him? I wouldn’t put up with his shit for all the coupons in the Zone.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ03_vodnyj_0_InfoTopic_1: You stalkers have it easy. Strolling around, snagging trinkets from anomalies… sure, you might stumble across a mutant, but the real nasty beasts are rare. Here, every kingpin is a fucking Controller.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ03_zemela_0_InfoTopic_1: I’ve been stuck here for a week now. Said something stupid in front of the Shah, and this is my punishment. No raiding, no getting rowdy at the Slag Heap… but now that you’re here, so we can have some fun.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ04_Comment_ApproachingC: You’re on Varan’s turf now, loner.
If you don’t know who that is, you’re about to find out.
Here’s a free tip: Varan doesn’t like to be disturbed.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ04_Comment_DenaLom_Inte: I got nothing to talk to loners about.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ04_Comment_GogaTatarin_: I like to have a shot or two of vodka and shoot at stalkers. Just kidding. I don’t drink.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ04_Comment_Gresnyj_comi: Put your gun down. It won’t help you anyway.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ04_Comment_Hottab_Inter: Don’t piss Varan off. He never forgets anything.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ04_Comment_KolaGastrole: I’ll only talk to you if Varan says so.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ04_Comment_Mokryj_comin: Put down your gun. It won’t help you anyway.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ04_Comment_Mokryj_inter: Guess what’s gonna be left of you if you start fucking around.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ04_Comment_Notarius_Int: Don’t even breathe on me, loner.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ04_Comment_Pastor_Inter: Unless you need absolution before you die, stay away from me.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ04_Comment_SuraPeps_Int: I used to have a dog. He always tried to look scary, but couldn't help pissing himself anyway. I wonder why I’m thinking about him just now…
sid_phrase_E03_MQ04_Comment_SuraPeps_com: Look at that, we’ve got a loner for dinner.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ04_Comment_ValaVagon_In: If you wanna have a staring contest, be prepared to lose.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ04_Comment_VaranEscape_: The stalker’s making a run for it! Git him!
sid_phrase_E03_MQ04_Comment_VaranFight_f: I’ll give you a “memento mori,” shithead!
sid_phrase_E03_MQ04_Comment_Varan_Intera: Head over to the underground garage, loner. That’s where we keep our mystery guest.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ04_Comment_VitaKotelsik: Varan’s the guy to talk to. This ain't my job.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ04_Dialog_DenaLom_Hotta: I heard Iron almost got killed.
Iron, killed? Fuck no, you can’t take that guy down. He’d gnaw the slugs out with his teeth, cauterize the wound with a knife, and move on.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ04_Dialog_Kurales_Grob_: Look at that loner’s face! Must be a tough guy, huh?
sid_phrase_E03_MQ04_Dialog_Utyug_Assault: Varan says you and your men were attacked. Tell me more about that.
It was a good fight. Pure fucking mayhem. Some pricks jumped us. I iced three of 'em, knifed another one.
And the one who tried to get away — I took a rock to his head. Then I brought him here.
Do you know who they are?
I don’t give a fuck. But it was fun taking 'em down.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ04_Dialog_Utyug_Varan_4: What can you tell me about your boss?
You do anything Varan doesn’t like, I’ll squeeze the life outta you.
You can figure out the rest on your own.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ04_Dialog_VaranBetrayal: So I’ll ask you the question I didn’t ask the first time we met: what makes you think you’re gonna get out of here alive?
Well, well... I didn’t expect Iron to bring me such an important guest. If you’re willing to jump through hoops for his sake, what would the others do?
sid_phrase_E03_MQ04_Dialog_VaranGivesQue: The Shah and Roosevelt won’t get in your again. Here’s the proof.
Huh… and I thought the rumors about you were bullshit. Now I see how you fought off those creepy fucks…
Here’s the money. I think it’s a fair price.
A stalker has to work for months to make this kind of money. They pissed you off pretty good, didn’t they?
Roosevelt no longer poses a problem. Here’s the proof.
Nice. Now I see how you fought off those creepy fucks…
I killed the Shah. Here’s the proof.
Somebody finally put that rabid dog down, huh? Now I see how you fought off those creepy fucks…
I want either money, or a PDA from one of the kingpins. Otherwise you’re not welcome here, loner.
You’re taking your sweet-ass time, loner.
Let me make one thing clear: they’re not my competition. Just a couple of lowlifes who got ahead because of their connections with Freedom and Duty. So what do you want?
sid_phrase_E03_MQ04_Dialog_VaranStart_Ou: Do you know how they got you, by the way? The Scanners are bugged. Nestor told me.
All right, now for my part of the deal. One of the attackers is locked up in the underground garage. Here’s the key. Have a nice jazz.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ04_Dialog_VaranStart_al: Your problem is already taken care of.
And you expect me to just take your word for it? I’m not asking for their heads on a platter— a PDA would suffice. Bring me a PDA, and we'll talk.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ04_Dialog_VaranStart_co: What kind of man is that Roosevelt?
I've never had any dealings with Roosevelt. He’s a stuck-up son of a bitch. But he’s got a tough crew and some decent gear, and he’s not stupid.
Why would he kowtow to Duty after they kicked him out? Beats me. The man’s got no self-respect.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ04_Dialog_VaranStart_in: It’s brave of you to come to my camp uninvited. But fortune favors the bold. Let me ask you a question…
Me first. Did your men attack me and take my Scanner?
Now, this is getting interesting. Are you here to kill me? I would, if I were you. When push comes to shove, strike first and strike hard. Watch your tongue, though. We’re on the same side.
Iron and his crew got attacked while they were refueling a spent artifact. Sound familiar? The boys fought them off, but I’m not about to let it slide.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ04_Dialog_VaranStart_iw: Or I could just kill you instead.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ04_Dialog_VaranStart_ne: I have proof it was you who ordered Nestor’s kidnapping.
Sure, I did it. Nestor was the mastermind behind the whole scheme. He was the only one who knew where Iron was attacked.
It looked like the old man had decided to screw me. I guess he figured out who was gonna take his place.

Me and him, we had a little talk. Unfortunately, Nestor didn’t know anything about the attackers. Still, everything worked out just fine in the end. A most convenient opportunity, if I do say so myself.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ04_Dialog_VaranStart_ot: That's a lot of money. Any other options?
Nestor is in the past. Only two men stand in my way of becoming the kingpin of Garbage: Roosevelt, the leader of the Commissary, and the Shah, the boss of the Brood.
Roosevelt is supported by Duty, while the Shah is backed by Freedom. Since the treaty, the factions no longer fight in the open, but they're both eager to put their puppet in charge of the Slag Heap.
I could have wiped them out on my own, but a smart man never misses a good opportunity. Kill either of the bosses and bring me his PDA.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ04_Dialog_VaranStart_pa: I think it’s a fair price.
Looks like they pissed you off pretty good. You’re sparing no expense.
Who knows? You might even get away with it. But if you charge them head-on, you’ll have your work cut out for you. I know of a better way, of course.
Try getting a job from a trader who goes by Huron. He does business with the Shah. As for Roosevelt, his former buddy Borodulin might know something useful. He’s at Garbage, always drunk as a skunk.
By the way, loner – next time you come here, show some proper respect. I won’t forget your insolence.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ04_Dialog_VaranStart_te: Tell me more about this Shah guy.
The Shah is a greedy psycho. Freedom pumps him full of dough, but his men only get the scraps. It’s almost like they prop him up just to spite Duty.
I wish I could tell you the dog from the Brood is all bark and no bite… but I'm afraid that’s not the case.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ04_Dialog_VaranStart_th: By the way, loner… next time you come here, show some proper respect. I won’t forget your insolence.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ04_Dialog_VaranStart_wh: Do you know who attacked your men?
If I knew, I’d have buried the fuckers.
But they left me a gift in the form of one of their men. I can set up a meeting for you. He’s not a talkative guy, but I bet you’ll think of something. But it’ll cost you. That, or…
sid_phrase_E03_MQ04_Dialog_ZenekCirva_Fo: Hey, Pops is in. Shell out the dough, man! You underestimated the old man.
He's lucky Varan didn’t interrogate him first.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ04_utug_0_BusyTopic_bus: Don’t you pester me. Not now.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ04_utug_0_InfoTopic_1_I: Think you can steal a sausage from a loner and get into Varan’s gang right away?
Fuck no. Gutter trash hang out with Roosevelt and the Shah. Varan’s a man of vision.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ04_varan_0_BusyTopic_bu: I got no time for you.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ04_varan_0_InfoTopic_1_: Just like there can’t be two suns in the sky, there can’t be two kingpins in Garbage. Not as long as I’ve got my eye on this dump.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ04_varan_0_ResumeCommen: All right. We can finish up now.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ05_Audiolog_Nestor_1_70: You might be an egghead, Hermann, but your plan sucks. Let’s do this the smart way, OK? You throw the scanners away with the SIRCAA trash. Make sure nobody sees you.
Diggers get them and bring them to the Labyrinth for radiation cleanup. I’ll get artifacts to absorb the radiation. Swiping a few from loners ain't a problem.
Once they’re clean, I’ll send Diode to tinker with them just like we talked about. Then we scatter them around the Dig Site, and I’ll send the coordinates to our buyers.
Just keep your freaking head down, you got it? You better. You’re a smart one.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ05_Audiolog_PahanOrder_: The info is solid. Go to the mark. Tomorrow, I'll be waiting at the Factory for the results.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ05_Comment_AfterCutscen: I know it might seem like the Zone is nothing but darkness to you now, Skif. But trust me, it’s always sunny in Prypiat. You’ll see the other side of the Zone soon enough.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ05_Comment_LabyrinthBan: You’re screwed, stalker! Get that freak!
sid_phrase_E03_MQ05_Comment_VictimRadio1: You’re taking your sweet time, Kiddo. What’s the holdup?
sid_phrase_E03_MQ05_Comment_WitnessTellA: Man, let me catch my breath. I told you everything I know…
sid_phrase_E03_MQ05_Dialog_Key_Key_45483: I found this on the bandits. Look familiar?
sid_phrase_E03_MQ05_Dialog_Key_Key_45484: Of course it does! That’s a key card to my workshop. A spare one. It was Nestor’s. What the hell did you get me into, you old prick?
sid_phrase_E03_MQ05_Dialog_Key_Kidnappin: Do you think whoever sent those men after you has Nestor?
I’d bet on it. And you better believe Nestor’s singing like a canary.
Can’t blame him, though. It’s hard to keep secrets when somebody’s pulling your toenails out with pliers. And believe me, plenty of guys at the Slag Heap own pliers. Not just techs.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ05_Dialog_Key_WhoAreThe: Any idea who they were working for?
None whatsoever. When it comes to the whole Scanner business, I’m just a cog in the machine. I haven’t got a clue who the customer is.
As for Nestor... well, he had everything written down. As far as I know, he kept all his records in a special stash. Why don’t you go check it out? You never know.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ05_Dialog_Key_Yes_45651: All right. I’ll go have a look.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ05_Dialog_Key_Yes_45652: You’ve got the coordinates. Keep me posted.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ05_Dialog_KondjorAboutA: All right. I’ll have a look at your workshop.
Whatever you find there, bring it to me. I want to see it.
You’ve got problems. That’s obvious. I can help you deal with them... in exchange for some information.
Straight to the point, huh? Without Nestor, this place is a mess. I don’t want to get involved. A good tech is worth his weight in gold, but kingpins are expendable.
Where can I find Nestor?
If you think I had a hand in it, you’re barking up the wrong tree. His job brings all sorts of trouble... some of it pretty dangerous.
And with Nestor gone, it’s obvious that whoever came looking for me knew we were working together.
Nestor. The Scanner. Ring a bell? The only right answer is an honest one.
Why hello there. First those thugs, and now I’ve gotta deal with you...
At least you didn’t stab me right off the top. Guess that means we can negotiate. So what do I get for being honest?
Do you have a choice? The bandits have got you by the balls, and I don’t think they’re gonna let you go.
My, aren’t you observant? With Nestor gone, everything’s been falling apart. I just can’t fix things the way he used to, and I don’t want to. That’s not my line of work. So what do you want to know?
You sure know a lot about the Scanners for an ordinary tech. How?
Why shouldn’t I? I’ve been in the Zone for a long time now. I used to tinker with all kinds of different gadgets for Degtyarev back at Yaniv. I’ve looted a few Project X labs with stalkers too.
The Scanners are based on Project X tech. Once you’ve handled their stuff a few times, you know which buttons to press.
Come on, spit it out. I can tell by your face you’re here to talk business, not chat about life.
Do you suspect anybody in particular for the attack?
I’m telling you, Nestor’s business is nothing but trouble. If you’re gonna start getting involved in it, you’d better stock up on ammunition.
You really ought to take a look around my workshop. I figure you might find something useful in those thugs’ pockets.
I'm better off here at the Slag Heap, but if you're one for risks, I'll give you the coordinates.
What happened to you? Don’t spare any details.
I’ve got a workshop not far from here. The only ones who knew about it were Nestor and me... and now the thugs do too, apparently. They showed up without warning, of course.
I’m not that much of a fighter. I’m better with tools than a gun. So I had to get creative. I turned the Scanner on. It created a bunch of anomalies, and I managed to get away.
“Project X?” What's that?
A network of labs. Beats me what the hell they were doing there, but I’ve seen a couple of papers indicating that they used to experiment on people. The labs are crawling with mutants to this day.
You can put two and two together, stalker. As for me, I’d rather not. I’m an engineer, not a mathematician.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ05_Dialog_RichterDocs_G: You’ve earned yourself a little bonus.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ05_Dialog_RichterDocs_I: By the way, I think the guys asked you to grab some papers from the lab.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ05_Dialog_RichterDocs_P: I found the papers, but my services aren’t free.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ05_Dialog_RichterUnknow: I met some weird guy underneath the Factory. He did something to the Wardens.
Congratulations, you just met a Zone legend!
What about the guys from the detention center? Are they legends too?
They wish. Right now, they’re just good people.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ05_Dialog_RihterBusy_1_: No offense Skif, but your face is nowhere as pretty as the view around here.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ05_Dialog_WitnessAboutA: Man, that was rough… easy money, they said… the thugs, they’re one thing, but those little guys, I’ve never seen anything like them before. Shit… you showed up right in the nick of time…
Look, I had a simple job. I was just supposed to get a device. I walked in and saw a bunch of thugs with guns. So I threw a grenade at ‘em.
The explosion collapsed the wall, and then these dudes in cloaks showed up. They took the thugs out in instant. I hid in the corner. I figured I was a goner.
What are you doing here, anyway?
Do you know what kinds of thugs they were?
You think they introduced themselves? They attacked without saying a word. But I heard them talking to some Varan guy about some kind of a plant. I know the Scanner wasn’t theirs…
A plant, you say? Guess I ought to go check it out.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ05_maloj_0_InfoTopic_1_: Those mutants in dusters are no joke. They’ll either hurl something at you or slam you against a wall. Better stay away from 'em.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ05_maloj_0_ResumeCommen: Let’s wrap it up, come on.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ06_Comment_DalinBackOff: This is about more than just the Scanner. What makes the artifact inside it so important?
The artifact could be the key.
I hope you’re not thinking about selling it. No one understands its real value. No one else would beat my offer. Bring it to SIRCAA, and I’ll explain everything.
Don’t take too long, please. I can hardly believe we're about to get it back! My Institute’s greatest invention!
How did the Scanners end up in the Zone, Professor?
I have no idea. Ask Colonel Korshunov. That’s his line of work.
I don’t understand why you need to be so dramatic. Were it not for this, who knows how long it would have taken us to find it...
I’d like to hear more about SIRCAA from the man who's in charge of it.
Wondering what’s hidden inside those walls, are you? I understand. I've never been one to ignore a good mystery either, not since I was a child. It’s a miracle!
Imagine you’ve found the Holy Grail. It can cure the sick, give hope to the despondent… would you hand it to scientists so everyone could benefit from it? I’m not sure I want to know your answer…
Well, my answer is SIRCAA. It's purpose is to channel the power of the Zone for the good of all mankind.
Why do you call the Scanner your greatest invention?
You're already acquainted with its functionality. You’ve seen what happens when it's activated.
It used to be that we could only interact with the Zone’s manifestations, such as anomalies and artifacts.
The scanner makes it possible to manipulate Anomalous energy directly. Everything else is merely a side effect. Do you understand? This invention has already changed the world.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ06_Comment_KonderCall_C: Skif, come in. Diode here. The diggers told me you’ve gotten into some trouble with Varan.
Get your ass over here. I wanna see what you found.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ06_Comment_KorshunovBac: You know the way. Wild Island, Noontide base.
Talk to their leader, Strider. Find out what this Monolithian gadget has to do with my men getting killed and the scanner getting stolen.
What's going to happen to the Slag Heap now?
We'll maintain order here. Nestor was a compromise, but the time for compromises is over.
If the Ward isn't in control, the locals will be at each other’s throats in no time flat. You’ve seen it for yourself… from the front row.
This conversation’s over, stalker. You're dismissed.
I need more information about Noontide.
Noontide is what's left of Monolith. By all accounts, they're a shadow of their former self.
But I still advise caution. Their base on Wild Island is heavily fortified, and it looks like they've got something to hide.
Besides, those combat skills stay with a man forever. Even if your memory gets wiped, your body never forgets.
What can you tell me about Monolith, Colonel?
The Center of the Zone used to be protected by a well-armed and superbly trained group called Monolith. And they did their job extremely effectively.
Of course, their exceptional discipline had more to do with so-called “scientific progress” than any kind of training. It was dirty work, but their effectiveness was still unmatched.
How did the scanners end up in the Zone, Colonel?
The Wardens are doing their job, stalker. SIRCAA has been secured against outside threats.
But our brilliant Professor Dalin here is too obsessed with his experiments to keep an eye on his own staff.
And he leaves his toys all over the place.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ06_Comment_PreCutsceneA: …your equipment, Professor. Not at SIRCAA — out there in the Zone! Who knows who’s got it now…
Do you suspect me of something, Colonel? How exactly could my… I mean, the Institute’s invention has left SIRCAA?
Looks like somebody needs to keep a closer eye on his lab rats…
I think you mean “scientists,” Colonel. Eminent scientists. And what do you mean, “keep a closer eye” on them? Nobody leaves SIRCAA without permission — your permission, mind you.
And what about the lab? Who was against posting security there? Who complained to the top brass that the colonel was making the scientists “uneasy”?
No more! From now on, your people will be escorted wherever they go, even to the bathroom. My men aren't the squeamish type.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ06_Comment_Richter_Call: Skif, come in. You went to see Korshunov, but forgot all about your old friend? That’s not very nice. Why don't you stop by?
sid_phrase_E03_MQ06_Comment_Richter_Hide: I’d like a word with you. In private.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ06_Comment_SawMill1_Sca: Speak to Scar. I can’t talk right now.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ06_Comment_SawMill2_Sca: You want answers, talk to Scar.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ06_Comment_SawMill3_Sca: Are you reaching for the light, stalker? Scar lights this place up real good.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ06_Comment_SawMill4_Sca: Scar’s in charge here. I’m just a grunt standing watch.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ06_Comment_Sawmill_Radi: We’ve got a loner here, Scar.
I’ve been expecting him. Let him in.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ06_Comment_SecondFloorG: Do you need a written invitation or something? Either come in or move along.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ06_Comment_VartaFluff_E: What's going on? Are you taking over the Slag Heap?
The Ward asks the questions around here. The rest of you just smile and nod or keep your mouths shut. About face, stalker!
And stay away from the APC! I know you pack rats'll steal anything that isn’t nailed down!
sid_phrase_E03_MQ06_Comment_VartaFluff_H: You must be tired, little chopper. It’s all right, we’ll be leaving soon. The colonel's gotta eat too.
We've got borscht in the canteen today. Green borscht with ribs, his favorite. So he’s not gonna stick around and sample the local rats on a stick.
My God, those people… living in shit, eating the same kind of shit…
sid_phrase_E03_MQ06_Comment_VartaGetOff_: Go ahead. But don't make any trouble, or I’ll shoot to kill. I've got orders.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ06_Dialog_Kogut_Start_4: So you’re the one we’ve been waiting for? Huh. I’ll never understand Scar…
sid_phrase_E03_MQ06_Dialog_Kogut_Start_5: Then again, you don’t need to understand someone to follow them. All right. Head on in, and make it snappy.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ06_Dialog_KorshunovAbou: Nestor is dead. I’ve read the messages on his PDA. Apparently, he worked for you.
I won’t pretend to be upset. That's one less thug in this dump. I’m running this place solo now, and I’d rather forget some of the compromises I had to make. And so should you.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ06_Dialog_Metlov_LookFo: I’m looking for Diode. Is he here?
Hmm... I figured you’d be taller. Head to the first floor. Diode’s up there. I’ll let him know you're here.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ06_Dialog_Metlov_WhatsG: The colonel’s cleaning up the place. It’s about time you guys got brought to heel.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ06_Dialog_RichterPlaceh: I don’t even want to know what Korshunov wanted from you. “Blah-blah-blah, keeping order, blah-blah-blah, the Zone is the key to the good of mankind…”
He doesn’t give a damn about mankind or the Zone… even though the Ward is already becoming a part of it. Don’t tell him that, though. The news would crush that poor bastard.
You should drop by the Sawmill, Skif. You’ve already seen one side of the Zone, it’s about time you saw the other. You won’t regret it, I promise.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ06_Dialog_RihterAfterSc: Come on, Skif! The Sawmill! No one’s gonna wait for you forever.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ06_Dialog_ShramAfterCut: Sure, Noontide isn’t going anywhere, but you’d better hurry… Just in case.
You mentioned the Shining Zone. Do you really think it exists?

Belief is a complicated thing, and I’m a simple man. I don’t believe it – I know it. I heard about it from a man who’s been walking the Zone for longer than any of us. He’s seen the Shining Zone with his very own eyes.
I’m talking about Strelok.
But we’re not picky. When you don’t have a map, directions will do.
Strelok? You mean that Strelok?
The one and only. He went to the Center of the Zone, and that’s where he saw it. The other Zone. The real one. Unfortunately, he doesn’t want to share any details about it.
What can you tell me about Noontide?
What I can tell you, I won’t, and what I want to tell you, I can’t.
Richter’s probably the only one that knows what kind of people they really are.
But as far as I’m concerned, we’ve got a lot in common. What is “noontide,” after all? The sun shines brightly at noon, but we’ve still got plenty of work to do.
Who’s that?
He’s the legend of the Zone. Strelok went to the Center of the Zone, and that’s where he saw it. The other Zone. The real one. Unfortunately, he doesn’t want to share any details about it.
Well, if I said I understood everything, I’d be lying.
Honesty is good. But paying attention is even better.
Everything that’s happening now, and everything that happened to you before, is the work of SIRCAA. The scanner, your home… and that’s just the beginning.
Using Project X’s tech is always a bad idea. Even if Dalin… improves it. Especially if he does.
All right. You’re looking for Strider, the leader of Noontide. They’ve used PDAs like this one in the past. They’re not exactly welcoming to outsiders, and for a reason. But I’ve got an idea.
I’ve got a guy inside Noontide. His brothers were supposed to deliver an important shipment to their Wild Island base, but they’ve gone missing.
He’ll send you the coordinates to the spot where they made contact last time. Find that cargo, and it’ll be your ticket in.
Why did the zombies just walk right past you?
Maybe we’re the zombies? Always restless, always longing for something…
Our bellies never full.
Ha. I’ll teach you that trick if we end up hitting it off.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ06_Dialog_Stairs_AskPer: I’m looking for Diode. The guard at his workshop told me he’s here.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ06_Dialog_Stairs_Door_3: What’s in there? Why all the secrecy?
I don't report to stalkers. The place is off limits, period.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ06_Dialog_Stairs_Intro_: Are you deaf or something? Keep out.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ06_Dialog_Stairs_Return: You again? Do an about-face and get outta here. You’re not allowed in.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ06_Dialog_Stairs_Varta_: Since when is the Ward in charge of the Slag Heap? This is a neutral territory.
Not anymore. From now on, you and the rest of the local scum need to keep your traps shut and not bark at your masters.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ06_Dialog_Star_Spark_46: I’m glad you’re with us, Skif. Trust me, Scar knows where to find a little light in the Zone.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ06_Dialog_Star_Varta_46: How’s that Warden collar, Skif? Not too tight, I hope?
sid_phrase_E03_MQ06_doktor_dalin_0_BusyT: If you'd excuse me, I'm really busy.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ06_doktor_dalin_0_CallP: Skif, do you have a minute?
sid_phrase_E03_MQ06_doktor_dalin_0_Defea: A bad day. A terrible day.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ06_doktor_dalin_0_InfoT: The Zone today is a phenomenon born from scientific mistakes. But I believe that one day it'll become the key to a bright future for humanity.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ06_doktor_dalin_0_Resum: I hope we won't be interrupted again.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ06_kogut_0_InfoTopic_1_: To survive here, you gotta see who's strongest and stick with them. But strength ain’t just about good gear, like the Ward’s. It’s a gut feeling. Scar's got it. The Zone leads him, and we follow.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ06_kudravyj_0_InfoTopic: You can win the battle, but still lose the war. I can’t quite remember who said that first, but when it comes to the Ward, it’s spot on. The Slag Heap is going to grind them down to nothing.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ06_lejtenant_majstrenko: We need a medkit here! Quick!
Look around. All you see is trash. That includes people, too. Not just here — everywhere. If it wasn’t for us, the Zone would be drowning in shit.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ06_lejtenant_tulub_0_By: Hope you have a soft landing.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ06_lejtenant_tulub_0_De: Hey, grunts, I need a medkit!
sid_phrase_E03_MQ06_lejtenant_tulub_0_In: Sometimes I think maybe it’s the people who are to blame. When you look at the Zone from above, it’s not that bad.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ06_nuhac_0_InfoTopic_1_: Here in the Zone, you have to believe in something. Or someone. I've chosen to believe in Scar’s instincts and experience. As you can see, I’m still alive.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ06_sana_hvoryj_0_InfoTo: Anything the Ward takes will eventually go back to the way it was. Mark my words.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ06_serzant_gricaenko_0_: I need a medic over here!
You want some advice? Keep your gun clean, like your teeth. A properly maintained weapon will prolong your life — especially here.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ06_serzant_metlov_0_Def: We’ve got wounded here! I need a medkit.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ06_serzant_metlov_0_Hel: Keep it short and to the point.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ06_serzant_metlov_0_Inf: I have faith in my men. If things go south, they’ll always cover for me. But you always need to watch your back, because even your own kind will turn against you the moment it suits them. Such is life.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ06_serzant_metlov_0_Res: What were we talking about again? Oh, right…
sid_phrase_E03_MQ06_serzant_safronov_0_B: I don’t have time for this, man.
See you. Stay out of trouble.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ06_serzant_safronov_0_D: Don’t just stand there, gimme a medkit!
sid_phrase_E03_MQ06_serzant_safronov_0_I: We’ve suffered some strange casualties at the Factory. They chalked it up to a stalker raid, of course, but the medic showed me a bullet he pulled from one of the wounds, and... it’s one of ours.
sid_phrase_E03_MQ06_vana_ula_0_DefeatCom: What are you waiting for? I need a medkit!
sid_phrase_E03_MQ06_vana_ula_0_InfoTopic: The Slag Heap used to be bandit turf, but now the Ward is in control. Makes no difference to me.
sid_phrase_E03_SQ01_Comment_GorbachBefor: You can’t be here without orders.
sid_phrase_E03_SQ01_Comment_Gorbach_1_45: If not for Korshunov’s orders, no one would have let you walk around here.
sid_phrase_E03_SQ01_Comment_KruchokBefor: You can’t go in. Don’t push it.
sid_phrase_E03_SQ01_Comment_KrucokIntera: Try to get it done quickly. If he tells you any more tall tales about the east gate, don’t believe him.
I’m honestly surprised. But the Ward is isn’t in the business of running errands for bandits.
You’d better go. You’ve caused me a whole lot of trouble.
sid_phrase_E03_SQ01_Comment_Mogila_1_FF_: Fuck, they got me… you’re gonna die with me!
sid_phrase_E03_SQ01_Comment_VartaAfterTr: He’s bullshitting you. Don’t believe him.
You really wanna do this, stalker? Think about it.
sid_phrase_E03_SQ01_Comment_VartaGuard_1: You can go in. But I’m warning you – it’s a waste of time.
On the contrary. He’s bending over backwards, claiming we can slip through the east gate of the Depot unnoticed.
He must think we're a bunch of idiots. There’s 300 yards of open terrain there – not even a rat could get through. Or whatever passes for rats around here.
In other words, he’s full of shit. It’s no use.
sid_phrase_E03_SQ01_Comment_VartaGuard_2: I’ll talk to him anyway. See what I can do.
Suit yourself, stalker. Bet he ain’t gonna tell you shit.
sid_phrase_E03_SQ01_Comment_VartaGuardsK: Whoa, lucky me! Over here, stalker. Let’s have a chat.
sid_phrase_E03_SQ01_Comment_VetosKilled_: Why? We didn’t have any orders.
sid_phrase_E03_SQ01_Comment_VetosToVarta: Hey, let’s talk some more! I’m all for it!
sid_phrase_E03_SQ01_Comment_Vetos_AfterH: Nice talking to you, loner. Now run along. Head to the east gate.
sid_phrase_E03_SQ01_Comment_Vetos_AfterN: Get moving, man. And say hi to Hoshan for me.
sid_phrase_E03_SQ01_Comment_Vetos_AfterV: Can’t a man have a little peace and quiet? The way to the Depot is through the cave under the radiation, then through the hole in the fence.
sid_phrase_E03_SQ01_Dialog_VetosAfterKil: Hoshan’s dead? Of course he is. No way he would’ve parted with this vodka. Good. Together on the stage, together in the cage. I won’t be putting it off for long…
Let’s get down to brass tacks.
I appreciate your display of unprecedented generosity. But you didn’t help me out of the goodness of your heart, did you, stalker?
Just tell me how I can sneak into the Depot, and we’re even.
No problem. There’s a way through the cave, under the radiation. Get to the hole in the fence, go up the crane, and head straight to the Depot.
sid_phrase_E03_SQ01_Dialog_VetosInTraile: Whoa, what’s this? A stalker? You came for a visit? Why, that's mighty nice of you. I just love talking. Anyone would…
Make it fast. How do I get into the Depot without being seen? I’ve got a couple of questions for Chornozem.
Whoa, whoa! What’s the rush? You gotta assess the situation first, loner. I ran from the Depot, and I ain’t going back. The pigs won’t let me out of this tin can that easy. I’m fucked, man!
I don’t give a shit about Chornozem. He had it coming. But I ain’t helping you neither. Why would I help someone when I’m already screwed six ways from Sunday?
Hoshan's gone, and I’m not going to run after him.
The anomaly hit Hoshan hard, so he can’t be too far from here. Think again, loner. I’ve got nothing to lose. Hoshan’s death is the only joy I’ve got left.
As long as this Hoshan of yours didn’t get too far, I can take care of him.
The anomaly hit Hoshan hard. He won’t be able to get away fast enough. Find the scumbag and kill him. Then I’ll tell you how to get to Chornozem.
One more thing, loner – I won’t believe it unless you bring me the bottle of vodka from Hoshan’s body. It’s a local brew, not from the Mainland. You can’t mistake it for any other booze.
Just what are you getting at? Quit beating around the bush.
You’re such a bore, loner. All right… Hoshan Mohyla and me, we made off from the Depot together. And now I’m sitting here all alone. Got it?
See, we got spotted by a few of these clowns on patrol. So Hoshan puts a slug in my knee and darts off towards the vehicles. The bastard didn’t even squeak when he got into the anomaly!
It’s bugging me, man... There's one thing I learned when I was a kid: when you go down, take everyone else with ya. It’d be a shame if the Ward wiped us out with that fucker still on the loose.
It’ll be even worse for you if you keep wasting my time.
Aw! It hurts! Fuck… I open up to you, and this is how you repay me…
Go to the east gate. Chornozem’s got a blind spot there. That’s how I got away from the Depot. You can do it too. With those fists of yours…
sid_phrase_E03_SQ01_krucok_0_InfoTopic_1: I can’t talk about SIRCAA. We aren’t involved in their research. The Ward’s job is to keep them safe.
sid_phrase_E03_SQ01_vetos_balaban_0_Info: A lot of people here treat the Ward like some kind of police force. I strongly disagree. Cops can be reasoned with, and nothing helps negotiations like good old booze.
But the Ward… they don’t even see us as human beings.
sid_phrase_E03_SQ01_vetos_balaban_0_Resu: Let’s continue our conversation, stalker; shall we?
sid_phrase_E04_MQ01_Comment_DubnyjOnInte: Head to the storage facility already. Strider’s in there.
sid_phrase_E04_MQ01_Comment_FaustGuardia: You don't belong in Faust’s cell, stranger.
sid_phrase_E04_MQ01_Comment_Kremnij_Inte: They shouldn’t have let you in.
sid_phrase_E04_MQ01_Comment_Kruzo_Intera: I ain’t talkin’ to no stalker.
sid_phrase_E04_MQ01_Comment_MashinaInter: Loner. Too bold. I don’t like you.
sid_phrase_E04_MQ01_Comment_MedicDisturb: I’m trying to help my brothers.
sid_phrase_E04_MQ01_Comment_MedicInterac: I have to help my brothers. Can’t you see that?
sid_phrase_E04_MQ01_Comment_MedicWorks_c: Let me get a look at you, Strider.
You could've gotten hit while you were pulling everybody else out.
Make sure they’re going to be all right first.
sid_phrase_E04_MQ01_Comment_NemojGoodLuc: May the Zone be kind to you, stalker.
sid_phrase_E04_MQ01_Comment_NemojOnInter: You’ll find your answers on Wild Island, loner. Bring them the cargo, and they’ll let you in.
sid_phrase_E04_MQ01_Comment_NoonFluff_Ab: I hope he ain’t stayin’ here long.
sid_phrase_E04_MQ01_Comment_NoonFluff_Ch: Quiet. We got a loner here.
sid_phrase_E04_MQ01_Comment_NoonFluff_Fe: I dunno why they let you in, but you’re not welcome here.
sid_phrase_E04_MQ01_Comment_NoonFluff_Me: This is a bad omen, man. Real bad. Remember Richter?
He was a stalker too. That guy freaked me out, man. The way he used to wail... it still gives me goosebumps.
It’s called "singing." Stop whining, okay? I feel bad enough as it is.
sid_phrase_E04_MQ01_Comment_NoonFluff_My: Then stop going to his sermons every night! Get a good night’s sleep for once.
As if we could catch any shuteye here. It’s ready to blow at any moment. Did you hear that loud bang earlier? Faust’s right, you ought to listen to him too.
If you wanna sleep better, work harder. Instead of listening to some headache-inducing gibberish.
I’m just telling you to be on your guard.
Another three men went missing recently.
My brothers… at least the island is safe.
After everything that happened at the warehouse? I wouldn’t be so sure about that.
sid_phrase_E04_MQ01_Comment_NoonFluff_Re: So now they let your kind in. Faust was right. If things keep goin' like this, pretty soon there won't be any place left for us in the Zone.
sid_phrase_E04_MQ01_Comment_NoonPreyInte: It was a mistake to let you in. All Strider’s fault. Faust would never have let this happen.
sid_phrase_E04_MQ01_Comment_NoonPrey_Flu: He says you can't answer, but you can still hear me.
If that’s true, then do something about them! Send them to an anomaly, unleash the mutants… avenge my brother!
sid_phrase_E04_MQ01_Comment_PortnoyCalmD: Shh! Get some rest. I’ll stick around for a bit. They might need my assistance with the wounded from the warehouse.
sid_phrase_E04_MQ01_Comment_RadioWarning: Listen up, everyone! We’ve got a stalker at the base.
I repeat, there’s a stalker at the base.
sid_phrase_E04_MQ01_Comment_StriderAfter: It’s off. Good. Now we can talk.
sid_phrase_E04_MQ01_Comment_StriderBefor: Turn it off first! I don’t want anyone else to get hurt.
sid_phrase_E04_MQ01_Comment_StriderGoFin: Go talk to Professor Lodochka. We’ll figure out who's responsible for this.
sid_phrase_E04_MQ01_Comment_StriderStore: Is that you, stalker? Careful now.
sid_phrase_E04_MQ01_Comment_StriderYoyAr: I’ll let my brothers know you’re no longer a stranger on this island.
sid_phrase_E04_MQ01_Comment_TalantIntera: On Wild Island, one stalker is one too many.
sid_phrase_E04_MQ01_Comment_ZavhozIntera: I’m busy, loner. Go find Dubny.
sid_phrase_E04_MQ01_Dialog_BroughtStride: It looks like Faust is involved in this mess. Are you gonna do something about it?
You think I haven’t tried to stop him, Skif? What I am supposed to do? Silence him? Lock him in his cell?
People blame us because of our Monolithian past. But Faust... he was one of the first people I brought here. We built Noon together. He cares for his brothers. He wants them to find happiness.
Faust’s got his own way of helping them. I might disagree with the path he has chosen, but that’s no excuse to take away his freedom. Otherwise, what’s the point of it all?
So I guess I’m going to Azimuth.
Wait a minute, Skif. This isn't just your concern anymore.
Faust is my brother, that much is true. I still trust him. But if it turns out that he’s going to hurt someone…
Noon is not Monolith. We make our own choices now.
Sure, I have it with me.
There's an old Azimuth comm station not far from here. One day, after we find and mothball all that’s left of Monolith, we’ll destroy it. In the meantime… Khors?
Azimuth was taken over by the Ward a little while ago. They have the access keys. I guess you can figure out what to do about them.
It's a good thing you have that inductor, stalker. It can direct the signal. You'll need it later. But the station needs to be turned on first.
Skif... do you have the inductor?
What did I just see?
The end of one very long argument. I just hope we never find out who was right…
This whole schism looks pretty serious. What are you gonna do now, Strider?
The same thing I always do. As long as there's someone who still believes in Noon, there's a reason to keep going.
You said Noon has rejected its Monolithian past.
Losing our memory was a gift from the fates. I believe that Noon has its own way. Its own future. And we will earn it with the sweat of our brow.
But Faust… he still remembers Monolith... remembers the days when we used to be happy. Unfortunately, there are others who wish they could still remember it too.
So now they’re gone. They're no longer a part of Noon. But if any of them ever changes their mind, Wild Island will always welcome them back.
How did the Ward end up there?
I do a great many things to secure the safety of my people... including negotiations with the Ward.
They wanted Azimuth, and I needed security.
sid_phrase_E04_MQ01_Dialog_DubniyConfron: You’re the only one who could have done it, Faust! Got anything to say? Don’t move!
You knew Strider was gonna go alone!
He was not alone. I sent Foma with him.
Don’t try to mess with my head!
This is your anger talking, brother. You seek enemies amongst your own kind, and yet there is a stranger in your midst.
Strider entrusted the security of this island to you. I will not get in your way.
sid_phrase_E04_MQ01_Dialog_FluffNoonHate: Did you come here to gawk at us? Watch us eat and drink? We’re monsters, right? Worse than any mutant?
Your brothers are killing mine. For reasons we can’t remember. Go away.
sid_phrase_E04_MQ01_Dialog_InjuredNoonBe: Dubny told me about you. Come on in.
The facility is off limits. Dubny’s orders.
The facility is closed on Dubny’s orders.
sid_phrase_E04_MQ01_Dialog_LodochkaNeeds: Any PDA can be traced that way?
Only the Monolithian ones. They had their own comm system based on alternative waveguides. And by “alternative,” I mean anomalous.
It’s tech from the old Project X days. My knowledge of it is pretty superficial, and I only know as much as I do because Strider used to be interested in that stuff. But I do know how to use it.
Khors and I even offered to adapt a station to our needs, but Strider wants nothing to do with anything Monolith-related.
I’ll go get the inductor you mentioned.
Once you have it, take it to Khors. He is even better than me in this kind of thing. He’ll figure it out.
I don’t know what he did in his past life, but the boy's got a remarkable knack for technology. He’ll have to learn to take care of Noon’s equipment on his own…
All right, loner. You know what to do. Good luck.
Sure, it can be done. But you’ll need an inductor, and they're not easy to come by. There could be one lying around somewhere at the WPS, though.
Sure, it can be done. But you’ll need an inductor, and they're not easy to come by. You’re in luck, though — we brought one back from the WPS. We put it in a storage building not far from here.
Strider said there was a way to trace the source of commands being sent to a Monolith PDA. He told me to talk to you.
The local fauna’s not enough for you, so now you're off to chase ghosts, ha? No one’s used those things for a very long time. Or...?
Crap. Strider's got enough problems as it is…
The waste processing station. That’s where Noon is storing toxic waste until I can figure out how to dispose of it on a large scale.
Then they'll use stuff no one else wants to become entirely self-sufficient. They might even sell electricity to other groups. If that's not a utopia, what is?
Strider's got his mind on the future, you know. I wish I could live long enough to see what he sees…
sid_phrase_E04_MQ01_Dialog_Lodocka_WFRQu: The explosion at the storage facility was caused by a Scanner. Do you know anything about them?
I never heard of anything like that back at Malachite, and news about scientific breakthroughs rarely makes it to this neck of the woods.
I wonder what kind of a scientific wonder blew our facility to kingdom come.
Unfortunately, all I can do now is run diagnostics. Field testing is out of the question.
sid_phrase_E04_MQ01_Dialog_MeetDubnyj_Fi: All right. I’ll leave you to it.
I wish you would. Havin’ a stalker around means a fuck-ton of trouble for us.
sid_phrase_E04_MQ01_Dialog_MeetDubnyj_Me: We let ya in, but that don’t mean I approve of it. Whaddya want from us?
I need to talk to Strider.
Forget it. He’s busy dealin’ with a problem at the storage facility — alone, as usual. He told me to back off. It’s too dangerous to get near the anomalies, he says.
But you are a stalker, right? You wander into that crap every day, detector in hand, tradin' artifacts for hooch. Ever dealt with any anomalies emerging without an Emission?
All right, go ahead. I sure hope you ain’t lyin’ – and I don’t regret trustin’ ya.
sid_phrase_E04_MQ01_Dialog_MeetDubnyj_Mo: I thought ya wanted to talk to Strider. So go talk to him.
sid_phrase_E04_MQ01_Dialog_MeetDubnyj_Wh: Tell me about the storage facility. What happened there?
You’ll see for yourself soon enough.
That man you were arguing with – who is he?
What were you and that Faust guy arguing about?
sid_phrase_E04_MQ01_Dialog_MeetHors_Bye_: I have to go. See you around.
It’s not every day stalkers come to the island, and when they do, they never stay here long. If you stick around, stop by again. I’ve got so many questions!
sid_phrase_E04_MQ01_Dialog_MeetHors_Cann: So you’re not allowed to leave Wild Island?
Nah, that’s not it! Everybody here is free to leave or come back whenever they want.
It’s just that Strider asked me to stay here. He says it’s too dangerous out there, and I trust my brother.
sid_phrase_E04_MQ01_Dialog_MeetHors_Firs: Huh? Sorry, I got a little carried away there.
Whoa! Are you a stalker? Do the brothers know you’re here?
They announced my arrival over the radio.
Really? Cool! Look, I’ve got a question. Is it true that there are no Emissions on the Mainland?
sid_phrase_E04_MQ01_Dialog_MeetHors_Neve: You mean you’ve never been to the Mainland?
Dunno. If I have, I can’t remember it.
We don’t remember anything that happened before the Monolith fell silent.
So, is it true? There really aren’t any Emissions on the Mainland?
sid_phrase_E04_MQ01_Dialog_MeetHors_Stri: I need to talk to Strider. Do you know where he is?
When I’m busy doing something, I get really focused on it.
Actually, I need to talk to Strider.
You wouldn’t even stop to chat with me for a second?
“Stalkers don’t care about anything but themselves. That’s why they’re always alone, and always miserable.”
sid_phrase_E04_MQ01_Dialog_MeetHors_Tell: What do you think of this place?
I like it. I’ve got all kinds of tools, and this machine too. Professor Lodochka got it powered up for me.
I wish my brothers would stop arguing all the time, though… and I wish there weren’t so many Emissions. But Strider says things are gonna get better.
sid_phrase_E04_MQ01_Dialog_MeetHors_True: I’ve never seen Emissions outside the Zone.
So Richter wasn’t lying to me! And they say a stalker’s word can't be trusted since they’re constantly lying, even to their own kind…
The Emissions always make me feel kinda weird... like I’m losing myself or something. Guess I should move to the Mainland, huh? Too bad I can’t even leave the base.
sid_phrase_E04_MQ01_Dialog_MeetMashina_F: Not asking you. I just know. You look me straight in the eye. Those who remember Monolith never look me in the eye. They hate me. Fear me.
sid_phrase_E04_MQ01_Dialog_MeetStrider_D: Do you know what this thing is?
The Scanner. We'd better turn it off.
sid_phrase_E04_MQ01_Dialog_MeetZavhoz_By: Like it or not, I’ve got things to do here.
A typical stalker, huh? That won’t fly here. Go talk to Dubny. He’s Strider’s second-in-command. He’ll know what to do with you.
sid_phrase_E04_MQ01_Dialog_MeetZavhoz_Co: Not gonna happen. We don’t use coupons here.
sid_phrase_E04_MQ01_Dialog_MeetZavhoz_Fi: Since you’re here anyway, got any anti-rad? I need a lot of it, and I need it right now. I’ll trade you for it. The only thing I don’t need here is an artifact. You can figure out what it does.
Yes or no? Think quickly. It’s urgent.
sid_phrase_E04_MQ01_Dialog_MeetZavhoz_Ha: So you’ve got a problem with stalkers, do you?
I’ve got but one problem: we’re short of everything, and I have no fucking clue where to find it. But I don’t go into the Zone.
Anytime a Noontider walks out that gate, there’s always some stalker who thinks that killing him is the right thing to do. Or at least robbing him.
Everybody wants revenge on Monolith... even though there’s no Monolith anymore, despite what certain people might say.
sid_phrase_E04_MQ01_Dialog_MeetZavhoz_Me: I happen to have just the right amount with me.
I hope there’s nothing wrong with it. When our doctor gets back, he’ll check it out.
What, you’re gonna keep hanging around the island? Folks here won’t like that one bit.
sid_phrase_E04_MQ01_Dialog_MeetZavhoz_No: I don’t have any anti-rad to spare.
Guess I’ll have to deal with that traitor Quiet again…
Whatever. I’m more worried about a stalker wandering about our base while everyone’s looking the other way.
Go talk to Dubny. He’s Strider’s second-in-command. He’ll know what to do with you.
sid_phrase_E04_MQ01_Dialog_MeetZavhoz_Se: You got my anti-rad? I’ll trade it for an artifact.
sid_phrase_E04_MQ01_Dialog_MeetZavhoz_St: I need to talk to Strider.
He’s got his hands full as it is. There was an explosion at the toxic waste storage facility. He went to deal with it.
But you’re not gonna just walk away, are you? No, of course not.
Go talk to Dubny. He’s Strider’s right-hand man. You aren’t getting into the storage facility until he says so.
sid_phrase_E04_MQ01_Dialog_NemojStopsSki: I used to... take refuge among Varan’s men. I hear you paid through the nose for that PDA.
Where’s the cargo that'll help me get in?
Here’s the spot where my group made contact for the last time. Find the cargo and take it to Wild Island.
Look for Strider. He’s the leader of Noon and the only man there who’d be willing to help you.
If anyone asks, just apologize for me. Tell ’em Mute ain’t never coming back.
What the…
Come on, man, chill out. Scar never taught me his tricks.
And how do you propose I get there?
I'm actually from Wild Island myself. Our group was supposed to deliver some important cargo, but I decided I’m not going back. And the rest of the group never reported back to base either.
Get the cargo... please. They’ll let you in. Professor Lodochka has been waiting for it… for a long time.
I used to... take refuge among Varan’s men. But for some reason they stopped returning my calls after they ran into a guy named Skif.
Anyway… it’s a Monolithian gadget. You’ll find the answers you need on Wild Island. The place is off limits to stalkers, but if you help me, you can get in.
Hold on, stalker. Don’t run away just yet.
I need some help. And so do you, right? So how about a fair trade?
What are you hinting at, exactly?
That strange PDA in your backpack.
Get to the point already.
I can help you with that strange PDA in your backpack.
What’s Wild Island?
You really don’t know? It's Noon's base. That's right, the same Noon that's made up of former Monolithians.
Most of the horror stories they tell about Wild Island are just fiction, but loners still ain’t allowed there.
You say it’s a Monolithian PDA. What does that even mean?
That means it used to belong to the men who protected the center of the Zone. They killed plenty of stalkers back in the day.
But Monolith is gone now. All that’s left is Noon... and an occasional wanderer with no place to call his own.
Why aren’t you ever going back?
I’m ashamed, stalker. First of all, I was the one who volunteered to deliver the package.
And second, because I didn’t tell them right away that I’m not coming back. I ran into Scar, you see… he showed how petty our problems are compared to the wonders of this place.
I’m grateful to Strider, I really am. But Noon just ain't for me no more.
You show up out of nowhere, acting strangely, prying into my business… why should I listen to you?
Because you always do the same. Right, Skif?
So you're with Spark now, huh?
For now. I thought since I’m not gonna have a place of my own anyways, I’d rather get to know the Zone for real. And there's no better teacher than Scar.
I’m not getting my hopes up, though.
sid_phrase_E04_MQ01_Dialog_SavaGratitude: Well, I have business to attend to. I gotta go.
Why don’t you bring it back on your own, if it matters so much?
I can’t. I’m not really much of a fighter. I, uh... I faint at the sight of blood, okay?
I’m sure I didn’t join Monolith of my own free will. But if I ever start firing a gun again… well, what if I... like it?
Look, I’m sorry about what I said at the gate. I shouldn't have told Stone not to let you in.
Thanks for delivering the cargo. It really means a lot to me.
Do you know where I can find Strider?
Right now? I don’t know. I haven’t gone to the base. I'm too ashamed.
Ask around. Maybe some brothers will be willing to talk to you. I gotta get to the infirmary.
Why is that package so important to you?
There were three of us who were supposed to deliver it. I was the only one who made it here.
Strider says we’re doing all this for the sake of a better future, but I still feel like my brothers died in vain. Know what I mean?
It’s not just about the cargo. I just wish there was some kinda meaning to it all, you know? Not in the future, but... right now.
sid_phrase_E04_MQ01_Dialog_StriderAfterS: And this is...?
If you activate it with anything other than an artifact, this is what happens.
Is it safe now?
Yes. Unless whoever is responsible for this comes back to finish you off. That's what happened to me.
What else do I need to know?
Noon is not your enemy. And this is my proof. Keep it, loner.
It’s a good gun. Why would you give it to a stalker, but wear a shell casing that matches it around your neck?
This is indeed a piece of Monolith technology. And I’ll help you solve this puzzle. But first, you need to understand one thing.
Noon is not Monolith. We’ve rejected our past. We do not use our old gear. We never attack our brothers, nor do we attack stalkers.
The gun is a gift. For the future. As for the shell casing… it signifies the past. Do we understand each other?
My attackers had a PDA like this one. Looks like some of your men are involved in this.
Okay. So what does Noon want?
A future. A former Monolith thrall is unlikely to survive on its own. He can't just become a stalker and accept their way of life.
I tried — until I realized I just couldn’t live the way everyone else in the Zone does.
And that’s why I brought my brothers here and formed Noon. We have our own road to walk, loner.
I’m not sure I fully understand you. So what do you think Monolith actually was?
A human shield. A crime against humanity.
Those who wandered too close to the secrets hidden at the center of the Zone ended up brainwashed, stripped of their identity and will. This is how Monolith recruited its fighters.
That control is gone now, but our memories never returned. I spent a long time trying to learn more… and then I decided it was time to move on.
I understand. So what do we do now?
The force that used to control us guarded its tools jealously.
There used to be a database that tracked every Monolithian PDA out there. Hopefully we can take advantage of what’s left of it.
Talk to Professor Lodochka. He’ll know what to do. Report back to me once you're done. Right now, I need to help my men.
I'm here because I was attacked, and a device like this was taken from me. The attackers had Monolithian PDAs. By all accounts, some of your men are to blame.
We get blamed for a lot of things. I’ll need something more solid than that.
sid_phrase_E04_MQ01_azbuka_0_ByeTopic_1_: I just lost my appetite again.
sid_phrase_E04_MQ01_azbuka_0_HelloTopic_: Don’t you have anyone else to talk to?
sid_phrase_E04_MQ01_azbuka_0_InfoTopic_1: You stalkers don’t even trust your own kind. So how should we expect you to treat us?
sid_phrase_E04_MQ01_bas_0_BusyTopic_1_55: I’m not wasting my time on you.
sid_phrase_E04_MQ01_bas_0_HelloTopic_1_5: What do you need me for?
sid_phrase_E04_MQ01_bas_0_InfoTopic_1_1_: You think you’re better than us. But I've got my brothers’ backs, and you’re only here for your own sake. Your stash is all you care about.
sid_phrase_E04_MQ01_bas_0_ResumeComment_: Let’s pick up where we started.
sid_phrase_E04_MQ01_brodaga_0_CallPlayer: Hey, loner. We need to talk.
sid_phrase_E04_MQ01_brodaga_0_InfoTopic_: It's an old Monolithian habit. Our past is unwilling to let us go.
But the word carries a different meaning now. It's become genuine.
Nothing ever stays the same, and sometimes change is for the better. In the old days, there was no one in the Zone we would call a friend, let alone a brother.
sid_phrase_E04_MQ01_brodaga_0_NPC_Combat: I can’t see them. Eh, screw it.
Hold on. Help is on the way.
Looks like someone’s a good shot.
sid_phrase_E04_MQ01_brodaga_0_NPC_Emissi: It’s over. Is everything okay? We can go out now.
sid_phrase_E04_MQ01_brodaga_0_NPC_Intera: A drawn weapon does not make for a good conversation.
sid_phrase_E04_MQ01_brodaga_0_NPC_Peacef: Remove the dead man from our home.
Starting a fight is never a good idea.
I’m sorry. You won’t be needing this anymore.
sid_phrase_E04_MQ01_devatyj_0_BusyTopic_: Time is a priceless resource. I can’t waste mine on you right now.
sid_phrase_E04_MQ01_devatyj_0_InfoTopic_: I don't care who my patients are. If I've got a knack for medicine, I’ll use it to help anyone who needs it.
sid_phrase_E04_MQ01_fila_0_BusyTopic_1_5: You’re wasting your time. I’m busy.
sid_phrase_E04_MQ01_fila_0_InfoTopic_1_1: When I got here, the place was already up and running. Strider found us this spot, Khors and Lodochka set everything up. Fuses blow during a storm sometimes, but a few days without power is nothing.
sid_phrase_E04_MQ01_goncar_0_InfoTopic_1: I can’t wait until I get better. I’m gonna volunteer to clean up toxic waste again. It’s a dangerous job, sure, but somebody's gotta do it. Still beats going out into the Zone.
sid_phrase_E04_MQ01_kometa_0_InfoTopic_1: There's no better place anywhere in the Zone for me and my brothers. And I’ve been to a lot of places. I’m not going back there, and neither should anyone else.
sid_phrase_E04_MQ01_kremnij_0_ByeTopic_1: This is where we part ways.
sid_phrase_E04_MQ01_kremnij_0_HelloTopic: You’ve been here longer than I was on my first day.
sid_phrase_E04_MQ01_kremnij_0_InfoTopic_: They say good things are easy to get used to, but I couldn’t get used to this place at first. I left a week after coming here. And came back the same day. I'm never leaving this island again.
sid_phrase_E04_MQ01_kruzo_0_InfoTopic_1_: You’re only alive because Strider says we can trust you. I don't know what he sees in you, but don’t ever let him down, you hear?
sid_phrase_E04_MQ01_kruzo_0_ResumeCommen: I thought we were done here.
sid_phrase_E04_MQ01_maks_sonnyj_0_BusyTo: I don’t waste my time on stalkers.
sid_phrase_E04_MQ01_maks_sonnyj_0_HelloT: Well, here you are. Speak up.
sid_phrase_E04_MQ01_maks_sonnyj_0_InfoTo: There are worse things than mutants around here. The creatures of the Zone don’t know what they’re doing. But what about you — the bandits, the loners? What’s your excuse?
sid_phrase_E04_MQ01_pancir_0_HelloTopic_: Got nothing else to do, eh?
sid_phrase_E04_MQ01_pancir_0_InfoTopic_1: Some of us don’t even dare to talk about Monolith because they're afraid Strider might hear. Not me! I want to remember everything. It's my past, whatever it is. It belongs to me and me alone.
sid_phrase_E04_MQ01_semen_dubnyj_0_InfoT: I owe my life to Strider. After I woke up in Garbage, I did some real nasty things. But Strider found me and took me under his wing.
And the rest of us only got another chance because of Strider.
I’ve seen the worst shit the Zone has to offer. And I’m not proud of it, unlike you stalkers. You guys don’t care who you kill. A mutant, a former Monolithian, one of your own...
It's better to let the Zone go its own way while we go ours.
sid_phrase_E04_MQ01_sestipalyj_0_BusyTop: Don’t even think about it! I’m busy.
sid_phrase_E04_MQ01_sestipalyj_0_InfoTop: Before I met Strider, I never planned more than a day ahead. What’s the point, when tomorrow might never come?
And I still don’t, but that’s because Strider has a plan of his own. And that's enough for me.
sid_phrase_E04_MQ01_talant_0_BusyTopic_1: I only have time for my brothers.
sid_phrase_E04_MQ01_talant_0_InfoTopic_1: How does it feel when everyone else wants to kill you, loner? Don’t bother answering. I know it all too well.
sid_phrase_E04_MQ01_ucenik_0_HelloTopic_: Since when are we letting loners in?
sid_phrase_E04_MQ01_ucenik_0_InfoTopic_1: Strider says you can be trusted. This is the first time I've heard him say that about a stalker.
sid_phrase_E04_MQ01_urec_rys_0_BusyTopic: Talking to you would be a mistake.
sid_phrase_E04_MQ01_urec_rys_0_InfoTopic: I thought Strider was gonna unite us and fight back against the loners. Instead, we're sitting behind a fence, pretending the rest of the Zone is no concern of ours.
sid_phrase_E04_MQ01_urec_rys_0_ResumeCom: Was there something more to say?
sid_phrase_E04_MQ01_vosmoj_0_InfoTopic_1: To some, this place is the stuff of legend. To me, it’s home. It's that simple.
sid_phrase_E04_MQ01_zavhoz_0_BusyTopic_1: Looking after my brothers is more important than talking to some loner.
sid_phrase_E04_MQ01_zavhoz_0_ByeTopic_1_: Now I can get back to work.
sid_phrase_E04_MQ01_zavhoz_0_InfoTopic_1: I told Strider we need to start trading. Quiet is eating his fill, and I have to cut back on rations every single day.
sid_phrase_E04_MQ01_zavhoz_0_ResumeComme: Speak quickly. I've got things to do.
sid_phrase_E04_MQ02_Audiolog_FaustAzimut: Return to Icarus. Get everything ready.
sid_phrase_E04_MQ02_Audiolog_VartaTechni: Begin recording. Reporting on the status of the router at Azimuth Station, as requested by Dr. Dalin.
All tests have been passed. System configuration is within defined parameters.
Hershevych, what's that noise? Are you done with diagnostic?
What the hell! Is that gunfire over there?! Hershevych! Nikitin! Come in!
sid_phrase_E04_MQ02_Comment_FomaInjured_: You again… I should have killed you when I had the chance.
sid_phrase_E04_MQ02_Comment_FomaInsane_D: Those who are in eternal union with the Monolith can never be stopped.
sid_phrase_E04_MQ02_Comment_FomaPrayer_1: May your light shine down on the souls of the brave soldiers who gave their lives in service to your will.
Reveal the cunning plans of your enemies to us.
Give us the power to avenge our fallen brethren.
sid_phrase_E04_MQ02_Comment_PDATraced_It: Skif, come in. We found the place your Monolith PDA was last contacted from. And we intercepted the sender’s final message.
We can listen to it! There’s got to be a backup storage panel on the antenna near you.
sid_phrase_E04_MQ02_Dialog_FomaInjured_L: It doesn’t matter… I’ll never be alone… not until the very end.
sid_phrase_E04_MQ02_Dialog_FomaInjured_S: You think I’m going to beg for mercy?
It doesn’t matter. You stalkers don't understand anything. The Monolith opened my eyes…
I’m not afraid… not afraid to die for Its glory.
sid_phrase_E04_MQ02_Dialog_FomaInjured_W: Why are you doing all this?
You’re not one of us. You’ll never understand it.
This isn't the way it's supposed to be… the Monolith helped me see that. Sure, a lot of people might die.. but if I don't do this, everyone's going to die.
Are you the one who activated the Scanner on Wild Island?
My brothers are poisoning themselves on that island… and to what end? I just couldn’t watch it anymore…
The immunity that kept us safe has been weakening ever since the Monolith fell silent… they were dying in my arms.
sid_phrase_E04_MQ02_Dialog_RadioBrodagaH: So it was you all along. Oh, Faust…
Skif, if you find him, try to find out what he's up to.
He’s my brother. In the end, all his sins are also mine.
He wasn't the only one. Someone else on Azimuth finished the Ward off before I got there.
Foma was with them. He tried to kill me, Strider.
I need to get to Icarus, and the sooner the better. Faust had a reason to leave the island just now.
sid_phrase_E04_SQ01_Comment_LeaveAlready: I hope you share my brothers’ fate, stalker.
sid_phrase_E04_SQ01_Comment_Lodochka_Hel: So you’re the man everyone’s talking about. Greetings, stalker. I haven’t seen your kind around here in a while.
sid_phrase_E04_SQ01_Comment_NoonChatterN: You need a medic. What are you waiting for?
I came back alone, without the cargo. Nope, I’m not going back in.
I just can’t. I gave Strider my word.
Yeah… please. We’ve lost too many people already.
sid_phrase_E04_SQ01_Comment_SavaNotice_S: And a fucking stalker, too. Here, of all places.
sid_phrase_E04_SQ01_Comment_SavaOnIntera: Thanks again. Sorry I talked shit about you.
sid_phrase_E04_SQ01_Comment_SavaYell_Noo: He’s alone, brother. He’s the one who should be afraid of us.
sid_phrase_E04_SQ01_Comment_YouMayPass_L: I’ve got nothing else to say.
sid_phrase_E04_SQ01_Dialog_LodochkaHorsT: I’ve got a package for you.
Oh, really? About time! A pleasant surprise for once.
Why the hell should I reinvent the bicycle to replace the car?! None of the other methods have anywhere near the capacity I need!
I can't help but ask: why would a loner deliver my cargo? Are you running an errand for somebody, or...?
The group you sent after the cargo got ambushed. I had to get to the island, so I volunteered to help.
Sons of bitches… they just won’t leave us alone. But why did you risk your life to get here?
I have some business on Wild Island. Your cargo just happened to turn up at the right time.
You’re an interesting man, I see. Dangerous, too. Well, I won’t waste any more of your time. I should get to the work.
Mute sent me. He said he’s not coming back. The rest of the group got ambushed.
Sons of bitches… they just won’t leave us alone. I guess I can understand Mute. Is that all you came for?
What’s so important about the cargo I brought?
Wild Island doesn’t trade, doesn’t wage war, doesn’t steal. The question is: how do these wonderful people make a living here?
They have me. And my experimental devices that convert chemicals into electricity to power the base. Their capacity is limited for now, but their potential is not.
The package contains some rare parts I’ve been sorely lacking. And now you can understand why.
I just stumbled across it. I came to the island looking for Strider.
Everyone’s looking for Strider. Everyone needs Strider’s help. I wish someone would help him for a change, but that guy… eh, you’ll see what I mean.
Strider’s at the storage facility. We have an emergency, and he said not to let anybody in without Dubny’s permission until the threat is eliminated. I guess you need to talk to Dubny, then.
Dubny is Strider’s right-hand man. And he’s mighty protective, so keep that in mind. My gut tells me you should look for him there. Good luck.
sid_phrase_E04_SQ01_Dialog_SkifNoonTalk_: All right. I’ll get going.
You shouldn’t have come here in the first place, loner.
Wild Island is off limits to stalkers.
I brought some cargo. I believe it’s yours.
I’m working for the Ward. Here’s my pass.
I don’t really care. But Strider will want to talk to you. Go ahead.
Looks like you’ve got a problem. Maybe I can help.
A stalker, offering to help? Sure.
Bring it back here, and I’ll let you in.
You can’t trust this asshole!
I sent you the coordinates. Our brothers were attacked at that spot. They lost some important cargo.
Let him try it, brother. Better him than you.
Take it to Professor Lodochka. Don’t hang around.
It’s not ours. It belongs to Professor Lodochka. You can enter Wild Island and give it to him.
What else do you need, stalker?
You again? Wild Island is closed to loners.
What is this place, anyway?
This is Noon’s base. Our home. And visitors aren’t welcome.
Why don’t you allow loners on the island?
Why should we? This place is ours.
sid_phrase_E04_SQ01_kamen_0_BusyTopic_1_: You think I don’t have anything else to do?
sid_phrase_E04_SQ01_kamen_0_ByeTopic_1_5: Hopefully we won’t be seeing each other again.
sid_phrase_E04_SQ01_kamen_0_HelloTopic_1: I thought you were gone already.
sid_phrase_E04_SQ01_kamen_0_InfoTopic_1_: Strider says he’s going to do what it takes to keep us safe. But living here can’t be the only way to do it. We can’t hide forever.
Sooner or later, we’ll have to come out and face the Zone... unless it comes to us first.
sid_phrase_E04_SQ01_nemoj_0_ByeTopic_1_5: The Zone will show you the way.
sid_phrase_E04_SQ01_nemoj_0_InfoTopic_1_: I always thought the Zone was hostile. But then Scar told me that it only seems hostile to people who are afraid of it. People who’d sooner kill everything that lives here than try to understand it.
And then I realized: that’s how the stalkers see me too.
sid_phrase_E04_SQ01_nemoj_0_InfoTopic_2_: You know what I did after I woke up? I tried to hide, to blend in with the others. And it worked — for a time.
When I joined Noon, I thought I could finally be myself. But the real me wants to know the Zone, not hide from it like Strider does.
sid_phrase_E04_SQ01_nemoj_0_ResumeCommen: What were we talking about? Oh, right.
sid_phrase_E04_SQ01_professor_lodocka_0_: Don’t just stand there! Give me a hand!
Oh, it’s you. What can I do for you?
This place may not be perfect now, but it used to be a real wasteland. Even before Strider brought the first of his people here.
We settled in over time. I’ll have a waste recycling facility up and running soon. It’s gonna be amazing! Then I can retire in peace. Maybe get my research published…
Back at Malachite, I had a project in mind. To put it simply, it was about anomalous conversion of toxic waste into electricity.
Ozersky turned it down. Said it wasn’t promising enough. The funding went to his lapdogs instead. That’s when I left Malachite for Wild Island.
I didn’t abandon the project, though. Science has to move forward. For the sake of those who truly need it.
Remind me what we were talking about.
sid_phrase_E04_SQ01_sava_0_DefeatComment: I’m hurt! I need a medkit!
sid_phrase_E04_SQ01_sava_0_InfoTopic_1_1: I don’t like the path Mute has chosen. Sure, I don’t agree with Strider about everything either, but at least he listens to us.
Scar... he just likes to talk.
sid_phrase_E04_SQ01_sava_0_InfoTopic_2_1: I used to stay away from the infirmary. You know, too much blood and everything…
Turns out, that’s not the reason at all. I can watch people getting treated just fine. But it hurts to see my brothers suffer.
sid_phrase_E04_SQ02_Audiolog_KvashkaUnkn: On the roof. I’m turning it on. Ugh…
The signal’s too weak. It’s not working. All right, get out of there.
No. I can… turn it… up…
It’s not working! Get out of there! Now!
sid_phrase_E04_SQ02_Comment_HorsAboutZom: Hi there, stalker. This is Khors. I see you’re going up on the roof. Did the Professor send you?
Leave Kvashka alone. Please. If you want to help, just turn the antenna off.
We used to be kinda like zombies ourselves. What if he can be saved too? We’ll find a way, just give us time. You should never give up hope.
sid_phrase_E04_SQ02_Dialog_LodochkaGiveQ: A Noontider named Kvashka went up to the roof and turned on the antenna for some reason. He was blasted by psi-radiation and turned into a zombie. They just left him there.
You should get up there and shut the antenna down. And put the guy out of his misery. I can’t bear to see him like that every day.
You’ve already helped me once, so I guess I can trust you… yeah, there’s something you can do.
I’d rather not get involved right now.
Oh, I almost forgot: I don’t have many coupons, but I’ve got something nice I can give you as payment. If you change your mind, I’ll be here.
What were you and Khors arguing about? Need a hand?
Surprisingly, I’ve got something that’s right up your alley.
The Noontiders don’t seem too upset about the zombie.
They have a peculiar attitude about it. Maybe it’s because they’ve all been reborn once already.
But that thing on the roof… there’s nothing of Kvashka left there. I hope my degrees are enough to convince you, at least.
I’ll see what I can do about it.
When I detected the spike in psi-radiation, Strider had the main entrance to the roof blocked. But there’s an old ladder on the outer wall.
There’s only one switch — you can’t miss it. And please... help Kvashka. It’s wrong to leave him like that out of some foolish hope.
One zombie and a switch? And no one in Noontide is willing to do anything about it?
They can't go there. The psi-radiation is weak, but given their past, it’s too much for them to handle. They could end up just like Kvashka.
Strider sealed off the area and forbade them from going up there. Like that’s gonna solve anything.
I’d do it myself. I’ve shot zombies before. But what would the Noontiders think of me after that?
sid_phrase_E04_SQ02_Dialog_LodochkaHorsD: It won’t affect the functionality of your devices.
That’s beside the… if something like that happened to me, I’d want to have a proper burial, at least!
It’s always something negative with you.
Then give me something positive for once!
sid_phrase_E04_SQ02_Dialog_LodochkaJobAg: Have you dealt with the psi-antenna and the zombie on the roof yet?
No. He’s still up there. Why don’t you take care of it?
sid_phrase_E04_SQ02_Dialog_LodochkaRemin: About the job you asked me to do…
Kvashka went up to the roof and turned on the antenna for some reason. He got blasted by psi-radiation and turned into a zombie. He’s still up there.
Turn the antenna off and release the poor guy from this… perverse form of existence.
All right. I’ll do what I can.
Thank you. It’s for the best. Noontide’s got enough problems as it is.
sid_phrase_E04_SQ02_Dialog_LodochkfHorsE: The antenna is off. I left Kvashka alone, though.
I won’t ask you your reasons. Maybe the madness of this place is contagious. After all, I’m still here.
The antenna is off. You can take Kvashka’s body down.
Khors will never forgive me, but it was the right thing to do.
It’s better than clinging to empty hope. Here’s your reward, stalker.
sid_phrase_E04_SQ02_Dialog_NoonGuard_Abo: I need to get on the roof. I want to help.
We don’t need your help. You can’t get through, and that’s all there is to it.
sid_phrase_E04_SQ02_Dialog_NoonGuard_Sta: The door is closed, loner. For everyone.
sid_phrase_E04_SQ02_Dialog_NoonGuard_Wha: A brother got exposed to psi-radiation. After a lengthy debate, we decided to leave him be. So mind your own business.
sid_phrase_E04_SQ02_Dialog_NoonGuard_Why: It’s dangerous there. My brothers could get hurt. And you might do something stupid.
sid_phrase_E04_SQ02_Dialog_TellHorsAbout: I wanted to talk to you about that zombie on the roof.
His name is Kvashka. Thanks for not killing him. I don’t believe that my brother is gone for good. There’s always hope. That’s all that matters.
Say what you will, but you’re the one who killed him. You couldn’t have known for sure he’s not coming back. You’re just used to shooting at anything that scares you.
I guess there’s no other way for a stalker to survive in the Zone.
sid_phrase_E05_EQ01_Comment_InnerGuardsH: That’s what happens to folks who wander into Warden territory.
Those mutants make me sick! I wasn't expecting any help, thanks.
sid_phrase_E05_EQ01_Dialog_InnerGuardIka: Always prowling around. Worse than any stalker.
At least you know how to hold a gun.
I’m headed to your base, believe it or not. Gotta check something.
I can’t allow strangers into the base given the circumstances. Is Captain Senkevych expecting you?
Hey, stalker, where are you going?
That’s too bad. People disappear easily in these parts. I suggest you be a little more cooperative.
I’m just going for a stroll. Shooting mutants, collecting artifacts…
Stroll somewhere else unless you want to lose your mind... or catch a bullet from somebody who already has.
sid_phrase_E05_EQ02_Comment_KosacAfterTa: We’re leaving soon, stalker. I suggest you do the same.
sid_phrase_E05_EQ02_Comment_KosacCallFor: Eagle, this is Kosach. We need backup, over. Eagle, do you read me? Come in, Eagle!
Shit! Does anyone read me? We need reinforcements!
Requesting backup at Icarus! I repeat, backup at Icarus!
sid_phrase_E05_EQ02_Comment_KosacCallPla: Phew... that’s it, I guess. Hey loner! Got a minute?
sid_phrase_E05_EQ02_Comment_KosacNoHelp_: What do you want, stalker? Looking for some loot? Get lost before you catch a bullet.
sid_phrase_E05_EQ02_Comment_RadioOrder_1: Attention! Attention! Icarus Base is currently under quarantine.
Most of the personnel have been evacuated. Entry is strictly prohibited.
sid_phrase_E05_EQ02_Comment_Varta1_IfHel: Thanks for your help and all, but this is no place for stalkers, you hear me?
sid_phrase_E05_EQ02_Comment_Varta1_NoHel: Shit, a stalker, just what we need! Move along now!
sid_phrase_E05_EQ02_Comment_Varta2_NoHel: Listen, man, I’ve got no time for you right now.
sid_phrase_E05_EQ02_Comment_Varta2_ifHel: Listen, loner, I’ve got enough on my plate as it is.
sid_phrase_E05_EQ02_Dialog_KosacSaved_Go: All right. I should get going.
Yeah, take care now, stalker. Thanks for the assistance.
sid_phrase_E05_EQ02_Dialog_KosacSaved_Mo: You're mistaken. I never risk my ass for free.
Well, there goes my faith in humanity…
Fine, take this. I know ammo's hard for you stalkers to come by.
sid_phrase_E05_EQ02_Dialog_KosacSaved_St: Never thought I’d live to see the day when a stalker helps a Warden out of the goodness of his heart. Thanks, man.
sid_phrase_E05_EQ02_Dialog_KosacSaved_Wh: Fuck if I know. We got the order from above to clear the camp ASAP. The core staff got evacuated to SIRCAA. And we… well, I guess we covered the extraction. With nobody left to cover us.
I’m not the one you should be asking, man.
We heard them talking during the evac. The camp's been compromised, they’re drawing their forces back to SIRCAA, potential whatever-class threat, blah-blah-blah…
Our job is always simple: do what you’re told and don’t ask questions. But the colonel wouldn’t have stirred the pot like this without a damn good reason.
sid_phrase_E05_EQ02_pavlienko_0_BusyTopi: Don’t get in my way. Later.
sid_phrase_E05_EQ02_pavlienko_0_DefeatCo: I need help! I can’t patch myself up.
sid_phrase_E05_EQ02_pavlienko_0_InfoTopi: It’s like we’re doing medical trials here, not military service. Take a purple pill with breakfast, a square one with lunch, a round one with dinner, and half a yellow one before lights out.
I’ve never taken so many pills in my whole life! But at least I’m still sane. Some folks aren't so lucky.
sid_phrase_E05_EQ02_rad_didovec_0_BusyTo: This is gonna have to wait.
sid_phrase_E05_EQ02_rad_didovec_0_Defeat: I caught a bullet! I need a medic over here!
sid_phrase_E05_EQ02_rad_didovec_0_InfoTo: When I heard we were gonna get ordered back soon, I pretty much checked out. I was just counting the days until my next rotation. Couldn’t wait to get the fuck outta Icarus and head back to base.
sid_phrase_E05_EQ02_rad_kosac_0_DefeatCo: Son of a bitch, he nailed me! Fuck, it hurts!
