sid_phrase_E05_EQ02_rad_kosac_0_InfoTopi: We shipped a lot of coffins to the Mainland, most of 'em empty. I've been in the Ward for a few years now, but Icarus is the first place I’ve ever seen the Zone keep the dead for itself.
sid_phrase_E05_EQ03_Comment_KemarikAfter: We’re gonna hit the jackpot, guys! Now let’s think about our equipment and route…
sid_phrase_E05_EQ03_Comment_Kemarik_stop: Hey, stalker! Wait! Come here for a second.
sid_phrase_E05_EQ03_Dialog_Kemarik_fine_: Fine. Your funeral. There's a path there from the Smoldering Farmstead. Here, I'm sending it to your PDA.
Cool! We’re gonna get ourselves some real loot!
sid_phrase_E05_EQ03_Dialog_Kemarik_habar: You can find some artifacts if you bring a detector.
Klondike! I’m telling you, it’s gonna be another Klondike! How do we get there? Come on, tell us!
sid_phrase_E05_EQ03_Dialog_Kemarik_no_51: You think anything scares me after the Swamps? Let’s part ways while I’m still in a good mood.
You got balls of steel, son... and a thick skull.
I’ve got a good gun and a steady hand. That’s all that matters.
sid_phrase_E05_EQ03_Dialog_Kemarik_nothi: There’s nothing there but rundown old shacks and burnt-out ruins. The place is a wasteland.
Yeah, right… you don't think much of me, do you?
sid_phrase_E05_EQ03_Dialog_Kemarik_payme: Fine. You want the route? I'll sell it to you.
Fair enough. Got no coupons on me, though… hey! You want artifacts? We’ve got some. How about it?
sid_phrase_E05_EQ03_Dialog_Kemarik_silen: I’m not telling you. If you try to go there, you'll just get yourself and your buddies killed.
Hey, watch it! I’m a bonafide stalker, man. One of the best! Everybody knows that. We’ve wandered around half the Zone, me and my squad, made it almost all the way to Prypiat!
sid_phrase_E05_EQ03_Dialog_Kemarik_tell_: What can I say? I had motivation. And some survival tips from Nimble.
Nimble? Yeah, he’s a good guide. But even he wouldn’t take people there.
That’s true, he didn’t bring me. Just gave me some advice.
You don’t say! Hey guys, looks like it’s possible to get to Clear Sky after all! What did you see there? Any… uh... decent loot?
sid_phrase_E05_EQ03_Dialog_Kemarik_threa: How about you tell us how to get to the Clear Sky, and we part ways on good terms? So... how are you gonna play this?
sid_phrase_E05_EQ03_Dialog_Kemarik_wow_5: Listen, man, have you really been to the Swamps? How come you’re still alive? I hear nobody makes it there.
But still... how did you manage it?
sid_phrase_E05_EQ03_kemarik_0_InfoTopic_: I should have stayed in Zalissya. Why the fuck did I ever go to the Swamps? There's no good loot in Zalissya, but there's way less mutants too…
sid_phrase_E05_EQ04_Dialog_Bob_GiveAll_5: Hey, here’s another one. I told you, while the kingpin does his thing, we cut down all the smartasses running to Clear Sky.
So, here’s the deal, stalker: take off your backpack, put your piece on the ground, and hand over your PDA. You can leave the rest to us.
On second thought… we wouldn’t want you blabbing about our little enterprise here, now would we? Ice the chump, boys!
sid_phrase_E05_MQ01_Audiolog_EchoSOS_1_5: Attention, everyone! Intruder alert. Repeat, there is an intruder in Echo Station.
sid_phrase_E05_MQ01_Audiolog_EchoSOS_2_5: His voice speaks louder than the rest. He heralds the truth and the coming order…
sid_phrase_E05_MQ01_Audiolog_ShterevPDA_: You haven’t been in touch for a while. I’ve got news. Good news.
I’ve done as you asked. I managed to find out how to get into SIRCAA.
Answer me. I’m at the location right now. I’m going to check it out.
The path is clear! Can you hear me? We can…
Damn! I don't think it's going to work. But I’ll find another way in. I’ll do whatever it takes!
sid_phrase_E05_MQ01_Comment_Alpha_AfterP: I think I’m feeling a little better. But my hands are still shaking.
How am I supposed to sleep now? I’m afraid that shit will come back the moment I close my eyes. Fucking Swamps!
I wish I had a couple grenades with me. Maybe then we could make it through. Damn…
sid_phrase_E05_MQ01_Comment_BravoChatter: Why did we have to come all the way here?! Fucking hell!
And the commander’s like, “I’ve got my orders. The lieutenant has to be here. He's got nowhere else to go…”
It’s all right, I’m good… I’ve got a couple of mags. I’ll shoot at the first sound I hear. And if worst comes to worst, I’ll just pull the pin…
sid_phrase_E05_MQ01_Comment_BravoCommand: Go ahead. No need to wait for me.
I’ve got… casualties to deal with.
Thank you for not abandoning him, stalker.
Here. I’m sure you’ll find some use for this.
I’ll patch this soldier up, and then we’ll head back to base…
It wasn't a Bloodsucker that did this to you… you stalker asshole!
Thanks again. If you want to get out of here, the switch that turns the backup power system on is just behind me. It’ll open the door.
sid_phrase_E05_MQ01_Comment_BravoSoldier: I’ll check the ammo, then go back to base. Thank you.
sid_phrase_E05_MQ01_Comment_BravoSquadRe: Who’d have thought we’d be saved by a stalker, eh?
sid_phrase_E05_MQ01_Comment_Bravo_AfterM: Gimme a second to shake it off, okay?
sid_phrase_E05_MQ01_Comment_EchoGuard_Le: Halt! You’re trespassing in a restricted area!
Leave the hangar at once, or we’ll shoot to kill!
sid_phrase_E05_MQ01_Comment_FaustMessage: You’re a tenacious one, soldier. Ruthless, too. There are no better qualities for those who walk His path. You have earned the right to hear me once more.
On a ship bound for nowhere is a guide who is trying to stay afloat. He will show you the way to Clear Sky. Once you get there, you’ll never be alone again.
sid_phrase_E05_MQ01_Comment_Kudravyj_Aft: How’d it go? Did you find the stash?
Yeah. There was a nice gun in there.
Ha! That’s why I like the Zone. And it looks like the Zone likes you.
sid_phrase_E05_MQ01_Comment_Kudravyj_Bef: I’d look for a stash if I were you. I bet there's one not too far from here.
sid_phrase_E05_MQ01_Comment_Kudravyj_Int: We’re looking for mysteries the Ward wants to keep secret. And we're finding them.
sid_phrase_E05_MQ01_Comment_MeetShterev_: Happiness requires sacrifice. I know you understand this.
It’s all right. We're almost there. I just need to turn it on.
sid_phrase_E05_MQ01_Comment_MetrAboutBra: They didn’t even leave anyone to cover their backs!
Spark sends its regards, Skif. Nice job.
sid_phrase_E05_MQ01_Comment_SparkAboutBr: The Star’s gonna be jealous. What a great hunt!
sid_phrase_E05_MQ01_Comment_SparkIzumrud: You're here to see the Star? Come in, he’s waiting.
sid_phrase_E05_MQ01_Comment_SparkThanksF: I heard your hunt was a success, Skif.
Here’s a commendation. From Spark, and from me.
sid_phrase_E05_MQ01_Comment_StarCallsAbo: This is Star from Spark. Come to Izumrudne, Skif. Good people can always help each other.
But hurry – the Zone doesn’t much care for slowpokes. Unlike the Ward.
They won’t let you in, even if you open fire. We’ve already tried that.
Whoa, you’re good! I bet the Wardens didn’t see that one coming!
sid_phrase_E05_MQ01_Comment_StarReminder: You want to know what’s going on with the Ward? Then bring me those sensors.
A Warden sergeant got trapped in a nearby anomaly. They didn’t even bother to collect the body, which means something interesting must be going on.
sid_phrase_E05_MQ01_Comment_ThanksForSav: One more thing: I want to thank you. The soldiers from Alpha and Bravo Team you saved are almost back to normal.
I’m still gonna get demoted to hell and back, but at least the boys are gonna be all right. So here’s your reward. You earned it.
sid_phrase_E05_MQ01_Comment_VartaAlpha_B: Commander! Are you alive in there?
sid_phrase_E05_MQ01_Comment_VartaAlpha_S: Alpha Zero, I can’t hear you!
sid_phrase_E05_MQ01_Comment_VartaRadioCh: Attention, all patrols! You are to detain Lt. Shterev at once!
Do not negotiate with him. If you encounter resistance… to kill.
Alpha Team, you’re cleared to enter the Claw. Bravo Team, get to the Vent.
sid_phrase_E05_MQ01_Dialog_BravoCommande: That’s too bad, Lieutenant…
Sergeant? Oh, a stalker… so you’re the one who killed that thing, eh? Thanks.
What are you doing here? Looking for a backup power system too? Well, you found it. The switch is right behind me.
How did you end up here, Lieutenant?
Some Bloodsuckers chased me here. I thought I was a goner. Good thing the doors held. But I had to break off from the rest of the squad.
Calm down. Let’s talk.
I don’t discuss my orders with stalkers.
“Titmouse,” Lieutenant. I’m not your enemy.
My men are alive?
At least one.
Shterev, Lieutenant. He’s the one I’m after.
All right. He’s at Echo Station. It was attacked while we were here. I’m sure it was him. Who else? I sent you the coordinates.
We’re on the same side.
Damn. Why didn’t you show me that right away?
Listen up, stalker: something weird’s going on at Echo Station. They called for backup, and then the signal cut-off mid sentence. I think they're under attack.
Shterev?
Who else? I sent you the coordinates. Get over there on the double. We can't let the bastard get away.
Why didn’t you leave? You know how to get to the exit.
How am I supposed to do that? I’ve only got one mag left — enough for the likes of you, but not for a Bloodsucker.
I’m looking for Shterev. Where is he?
Shterev, huh? Well, you’re out of luck, asshole! My orders are to eliminate anyone connected to the lieutenant.
sid_phrase_E05_MQ01_Dialog_BravoEncounte: You’re in my way. Take five, soldier.
Where’s your commander? Is he alive?
I think so. He’s crazy as fuck. Took one of the guys and went to look for the backup power system. I guess it's supposed to open another way out or something.
Gimme a medkit, stalker. I’m out.
I need a medkit, not a conversation.
I don’t even have enough for me.
Easy now. I’m just a stalker. What happened here?
Some kind of creature. An invisible one, I guess. I’ve never seen anything like it before.
I’m a friendly. Captain Senkevych sent me.
The captain sent a stalker? Why?
I dunno. Maybe the commander saw him? He went to look for the backup power system. Said we can get out of here if we get it running.
You got a medkit? I'm fresh out.
So you know about him, too… the commander has all the info. You got a medkit? I’m out.
Where'd you come from? The ladder's broken… keep your hands up!
I’m looking for Shterev. Have you found him?
sid_phrase_E05_MQ01_Dialog_BravoForcedPa: Hey, stalker: “titmouse.” Tell the commander that. He’ll get it.
sid_phrase_E05_MQ01_Dialog_Bravo_Command: Have you found the commander? Is he alive?
He saw me and went straight for the gun. So no.
sid_phrase_E05_MQ01_Dialog_FoundShterev_: I had to do it. He’s right. You’re a scientist, you have to understand me. The small must not stand in the way of the great.
Lieutenant, wake up! I’ve got something that’ll help you come to your senses.
I don’t want to wake up, stalker. I want to go to him. I want to be happy again.
I’m not letting you out of here alive.
If I die here, I’ll die a happy man. I had a purpose. And what do you have? Are you needed?
He went silent! And then those rats from SIRCAA didn’t want to re-tune the antenna. I had to do it, don’t you understand?
I’m almost done, stalker. I’m sorry, but I can’t leave any witnesses.
Wake up, Lieutenant. You must have been under psi-influence.
Why did you turn against your own men? What did they promise you?
Acceptance. Purpose. Not the false purpose of the Ward – a real purpose.
What are you talking about? Who is “he?”
Have you ever felt needed? Like you’re not alone anymore? I never have.
Not until he spoke to me.
sid_phrase_E05_MQ01_Dialog_IkarGuard_Sou: I’ve got a permit from Colonel Korshunov.
The Colonel found out we’re in trouble and he sent a stalker to help? That's just great…
Go talk to Captain Senkevych. He’s in the main building. It’s an infirmary now, though.
I’m looking for someone, and he's in there.
Is he even real? Eh, forget it. It’s none of my business, and I’ve got no desire to help you.
This is Ward turf. We don’t let loners in. Is that clear? It better be, for your sake.
Easy now. Why are you so nervous?
You keep asking questions, and I can’t promise I won’t snap. Anything can happen in the fucking Swamps.
sid_phrase_E05_MQ01_Dialog_IkarGuard_Wes: I’ve got a permit from Colonel Korshunov.
Are we hiring stalkers now too? Eh, whatever... as long as you can help.
Come on in. The commander’s in the main building. We’ve got an infirmary there now.
I’m looking for someone. And he's in there.
There definitely aren't any stalkers here, I'll tell you that much. If it were up to me, I wouldn’t be here either. So get lost.
All right. And I don’t want to see you around here again.
You’re entering Ward territory, stalker. You have no business being here, you hear me?! Turn around and get lost!
Easy now. Why are you so nervous?
The Swamps can make anyone nervous. Can’t you feel it? This place ain’t right, I tell ya. Now move along, the boys are already on on edge.
sid_phrase_E05_MQ01_Dialog_KudravyjGives: Since you’re working with Spark, the Zone’s got a little present for you.
This place might be inconspicuous, but it's also legendary — it's where Degtyarev himself performed with a chimera and got a standing ovation.
Stalkers like to leave stashes in places like this. Look around, maybe you'll find something useful... especially if you’re making a move against the Ward.
sid_phrase_E05_MQ01_Dialog_MedicVartaIka: Here you go. Don't take these unless it’s absolutely necessary, or you can kiss your liver goodbye.
I need some SIRCAA pills. Captain’s orders.
The Captain should be thinking about his men instead. I can barely scrape together enough to treat the sick…
sid_phrase_E05_MQ01_Dialog_SparkNextToBr: So much for the Zone elite.
Ha! Put him down like a dog!
sid_phrase_E05_MQ01_Dialog_StarBringSens: What have you got for me?
You were right. I found some sensors on the Warden’s body.
Some mistakes can’t be undone. I’m glad this isn’t one of them.
Now let’s hear what those Warden dogs are barking about…
sid_phrase_E05_MQ01_Dialog_StarOnIzumrud: We already have them, and it's all thanks to you. Richter gave us your little present from the Lesser Zone.
You did the right thing, Skif. Trust me on that. Now let’s see what those dogs are barking about…
A Warden sergeant got trapped in a nearby anomaly. They're often issued sensors, unlike the privates. Go check it out. Maybe you'll get lucky.
Funny, don’t you think? A little while ago, they turned the entire Lesser Zone upside down after their squad got killed. But here in the Swamps, they don’t even bother to collect their dead.
Scar’s right. There's something interesting going on here.
I think you had different people with you the last time we met.
Spark is more than a bunch of stalker groups. Scar believes that everybody needs to work with everyone else, get to know them, and learn to rely on each other.
Do you agree with that?
Scar knows what he’s doing.
You said you could help me.
Scar says you’re looking for a traitor among the Wardens. I think they’d very much like to find him themselves. Maybe they already have.
In order to make sure, though, we have to hack into their radio frequencies. And that means we need their sensors.
How do you know who I’m looking for?
Because Scar told me. As for how he knows… he isn't sharing that information with me. But as a legend of the Zone, folks are willing to share all sorts of things with him.
Things you handed over to the Ward personally.
Good to see you, Skif. I should have moved my camp a long time ago — we’re sitting ducks out here in the open. But there are some folks I just can’t say no to.
Besides, it feels good to lend a hand to someone the Ward doesn’t like. Means we've got a lot in common.
Who are those “folks you just can’t say no to?”
You’ve met him before. Scar’s got a mind of his own, but if he’s asking you for something, it’s not wise to refuse him.
That sounds like a threat.
If you don’t trust a man, why follow him? And if you don’t want to help him, why should he lead you?
If you want folks to trust you, you have to trust someone else first. Otherwise we'll all stay alone, bereft of purpose or meaning.
So where do I find these sensors?
sid_phrase_E05_MQ01_Dialog_StarTellsAfte: Looks like Lieutenant Shterev of the Ward is the rat you’re looking for, stalker.
The Ward sent out two teams to find him. One went to the Claw, and the other one went to the Vent.
Their orders were to capture the lieutenant. But you can still get to him first. If Shterev’s not there, maybe you can find out where else he might be.
sid_phrase_E05_MQ01_Dialog_StarTellsToGo: The Zone always sides with those who tread softly.
One more thing: Spark pays generously for the heads of those Warden curs. If you eliminate all of their patrols, we’ll appreciate it.
Well, stalker… if the Ward is going after one of their own, and there's Monolith tech involved, this Shterev fella must be the one you're looking for.
I take it you’ll want to find the traitor before the Wardens do. Good hunting.
What about you? What are you going to do?
We’ve got a hunt of our own, stalker. But I reckon we’ll meet again.
And what’s in it for you?
Today we scratch your back, tomorrow you scratch ours... who knows what might happen the day after tomorrow?
Besides, we’ve heard a lot about you. As for me, I expect you to seriously thin out the canine population.
sid_phrase_E05_MQ01_Dialog_VartaAlpha_Br: There are Snorks in there. They must have mauled the commander.
I told him not to go in there! What am I gonna tell the guys now? Fucking hell!
Okay, calm down. Captain Senkevych sent me. I'm here to help.
About fucking time! But there are none of us left... except for me.
Hell no. But he was definitely here. The commander found something just before he… before he went down the hole to those creatures.
Snorks. All over the place. And my head feels heavy, like it’s made of cast iron. Something ain’t right here.
The lieutenant? How do you know about him? Are you working with him?! Come on, asshole, try me! I'll put you down too!
sid_phrase_E05_MQ01_Dialog_VartaAlpha_Cl: You’re from Alpha Team?
Say again, Commander! You're breaking up!
Hey! Are you deaf or something?
Here. Take them.
Oh… stalker?
I… what happened? It's all a blur...
I haven't got much time. Where’s Shterev? Did you find him?
Senkevych gave us the go-ahead. We were supposed to look for Shterev at the bottom of the crevasse, near the breach.
Do you think it’s Shterev who screwed me up like this, stalker? Could he do that to me?
I don’t know, but I’m about to find out.
I should probably put you out of your misery.
Goddamn radio… I could hear you a second ago!
He doesn’t answer. He doesn’t answer. He doesn’t answer! He's not that far away. The radio should be able to reach him!
Come on, Commander! Talk to me, please…
Hey, soldier, I’m talking to you. I’m looking for Shterev. Where is he?
Where's Shterev?
Why did you have to go down there, Commander…
I don’t want to be left alone here!
Your brain got fried. You were rambling.
Really? So that’s how it works… I felt like I was lost, you know? Like a little kid. Where am I going, what am I doing? Who’s gonna help me?
It's a good thing you were there. Thank you.
You’ve got a few screws loose, don’t you?
I was hoping you could tell me.
Oh, hold on… our unit had orders to secure the Claw. I provided cover from above, and Vuychik went to check the gas station.
The commander and Krutov went to look for a way down, and then… I can’t remember. What happened to me?
sid_phrase_E05_MQ01_Dialog_VartaAlpha_Ho: What’s the point of it all? If there’s nothing inside me, there’s nothing, period. The Zone took everything. Every last bit. Maybe there was never anything there to begin with.
Look, stalker... do me a favor, okay? Make it quick. Right in the head.
Take this first. Then we’ll see.
Holy Mother of… what was that? Something from SIRCAA? My head is killing me…
Are you okay now? Where’s Shterev?
Shterev? Right, him... the commander went down to look for him. He sent me here.
Don’t jump right into the chasm — you’ll just fall to your death. Even if you don’t, Zhizhko's standing guard will shoot you for sure.
Okay, if that’s what you want.
No. I’m not gonna shoot you.
Find who? What are you talking about?
I’m looking for Shterev. Are you talking about him?
sid_phrase_E05_MQ01_kudravyj_0_InfoTopic: Do you check the weather report? I always do. You know what my favorite is? The fog. It helps you get real close to the Ward and give 'em a nice box on the ear.
How can you not love the Swamps if that's what you're all about? Where else can you give the Ward what for with so little effort?
sid_phrase_E05_MQ01_l_hyphen_t_kozimkov_: The Vent, man… it broke something inside of me. I used to take the lead in any fight, no questions asked, let's go! But not anymore. I want to live now. And I want to keep others alive too.
How can I explain that to the Captain, though? He already thinks I'm a pussy.
sid_phrase_E05_MQ01_metr_0_BusyTopic_1_5: This can wait, okay? Not right now.
sid_phrase_E05_MQ01_metr_0_DefeatComment: They got me after all, the bastards!
sid_phrase_E05_MQ01_metr_0_InfoTopic_1_1: There are no straight routes through the Zone, stalker. Do you know why? It wants you to take a stroll, see its sights and enjoy its beauty.
Sure, you can try and cut straight across the Zone. Like the Ward — they insist on marching in formation all over the place. And then they wonder why the Zone keeps wrecking their plans…
sid_phrase_E05_MQ01_rad_batejko_0_Defeat: I’ll get over it. And then I’m gonna do you… Oh, fuck! That hurts.
sid_phrase_E05_MQ01_rad_batejko_0_InfoTo: I think I’m gonna get discharged soon. After the Vent, I freeze up whenever I see a basement door. I just hear that growling again...
Sure, Batalov gives me pills, but they don't help for shit.
sid_phrase_E05_MQ01_radovoj_krutov_0_Bus: No, stalker. You’re on your own for now.
sid_phrase_E05_MQ01_radovoj_krutov_0_Def: Hey, I could use some meds right about now.
sid_phrase_E05_MQ01_radovoj_krutov_0_Inf: Got me a new pair of boots from the quartermaster. Must be my third pair this month. The Swamps ain’t good for your boots, I'll tell you that.
In fact, they ain’t good for people either. But we’re tougher than boots. We still fall apart, we just do it a little slower.
sid_phrase_E05_MQ01_radovoj_krutov_0_Res: Remind me, where did we… oh, right.
sid_phrase_E05_MQ01_radovoj_strober_0_De: Will someone get me a fucking medkit already?!
sid_phrase_E05_MQ01_radovoj_strober_0_In: Ever since my trip to the Claw, I’ve got a new rule of thumb: the right bullet for the right beast. Works like a charm.
sid_phrase_E05_MQ01_radovoj_zizko_0_Defe: Son of a bitch! I’m gonna fuck you up!
sid_phrase_E05_MQ01_radovoj_zizko_0_Info: The day after tomorrow, I’m on the night watch again. Guess I’ll go grab some earplugs. The Swamps have been particularly talkative lately.
The Captain says it’s just methane. Everything’s rotting, hence the sounds. Yeah, right... when I hear a baby crying, is that methane too?
sid_phrase_E05_MQ01_star_3_InfoTopic_1_1: A lot of folks steer clear of the Swamps, but I like it here. It's a good testing ground — separates the wheat from the chaff.
Here, you figure out real quick if you're a good man who always does what needs to be done — soaked boots notwithstanding — or a windbag who’s only here to swap stories by the fire.
sid_phrase_E05_MQ01_vana_ula_0_InfoTopic: The only good thing about the Ward is their guns. But what good are they if you’re aiming 'em the wrong way? And the Ward’s always aiming 'em the wrong way.
sid_phrase_E05_MQ02_Comment_RichterComme: Before you can deal with something, you need to deal with yourself first!
sid_phrase_E05_MQ02_Comment_RichterOnShe: Get lost, Beard. Let me drink in peace.
I’m not taking your men there.
sid_phrase_E05_MQ02_Comment_RichterOnSka: Oh, the cranberry bush blooms by the creek in field…
You’re very nice… but I ain't taking you there.
sid_phrase_E05_MQ02_Comment_RichterSearc: Let me finish my drink in peace. Look for Nimble. He’s the man you need.
sid_phrase_E05_MQ02_Comment_ZveroboyGift: Nice shooting, eh? Here’s a little present for you. Hope to see you around. Good hunting!
sid_phrase_E05_MQ02_Comment_ZveroboyHunt: Let’s kill the creature first. Then we can talk.
sid_phrase_E05_MQ02_Comment_Zveroboy_Inv: Stalker! You’re gonna scare all my game away. You're making more noise than a pseudogiant.
Get to the observation post on the double. And quit stomping around like that!
sid_phrase_E05_MQ02_Dialog_AskBorodaAbou: We don't have a guide. But I guess there’s Richter... see him drinking over there? He used to be a guide back in the day. Maybe he can help.
sid_phrase_E05_MQ02_Dialog_KrivenkoCalls: Did you get to Echo, Skif? My colleagues at the station still haven’t contacted me.
They’re all dead. Shterev wasn't messing around.
How unfortunate… It’s a good thing our data transfer is top-notch, so this won’t affect our research in any way.
Thanks for the information. I’ll see you later.
sid_phrase_E05_MQ02_Dialog_RichterOnShip: Wanna hear a story? Nah, don’t answer — I’m gonna tell ya anyways. I’ve had just about enough for one little tale.
I used to be a guide, it's true. I figured I was leading people around the Zone. Turns out, I was lying through my teeth. To myself. I thought I knew it all. I thought I could take anyone anywhere…
What kind of mistakes does a stalker make? You can mess up your detector, lose your loot… With guides, it’s a different story. Especially when the Zone wants to put them in their place. You dig?
And until I become a true guide – someone who knows the Zone for real, someone the Zone loves for real – I’m not taking you anywhere.
You need Nimble. I hear he’s been to Clear Sky a couple of times. He’s a good guide, and he’s not a bad guy either. Don’t ask him about the Snork gas mask he wears on his shoulder, though.
Right now, Nimble is taking Trapper on a hunt. The Sultan splurged on the best hunter to shoot the local fauna. I sent you their coordinates. Maybe you can work something out.
There were three of them. I still remember their names, where they were going, and everything that happened to them. I can’t forget it.
I wish I could, but I just can’t. I had the trust of the Zone – and I betrayed it. Ruined it along with the people who had trusted me.
Don’t you eavesdrop on me!
Fine, head to the Skadovsk then. Get the fuck outta here.
You’re welcome to depart for the Shevchenko.
Nope, not quite.
You’re a lucky one, then. I envy you.
You’re a mean one, Mr. Skif. But at least you’re honest. Guess that’s better than the other way around.
I’m not taking you anywhere. You need Nimble. He’s a little on the cocky side, but he always gets out of trouble alive. You’ll know him by the Snork gas mask on his shoulder. Don’t ask him about it.
The last time I saw him was with Trapper. The Sultan hired the hunter to kill mutants in the Swamps. So Trapper pestered every guide to take him straight up the Swamps’ ass.
I just sent you the coordinates of that ass. Watch yourself.
Yes. I understand.
Yeah. I knew you’d get it.
Well, look what the cat dragged in! We've got guests in our swamp... I'm sorry, I mean "their" swamp! It belongs to those paragons of clean living, of course! What can I do for you?
Do you know anything about Clear Sky?
It used to be a faction. They were searching for the ways of the Zone, but they got lost. They’re gone now.
Their base is still out there, but there’s no way to get inside.
And that’s for the best. The Zone has some secrets that are best left undisturbed.
Is everything all right? Or are you celebrating something?
Do I need an excuse to have a little radiation treatment? I went to the wrong place, and now I’m giving the radionuclides the old heave-ho.
And I mean that literally. I’d rather drink water from the river than the booze they got around here.
Looks like every asshole in the Zone knows who you are.
Hey, don’t be so rude to the noble proprietors of this fine rust bucket! But you’re right, I’m sort of a regular around here. A side effect of living in the Zone for… about ten years, I guess.
And I’ve never ever joined any of the factions. Guess that’s why the locals like me. At the very least, they hate me a little less than they hate each other.
sid_phrase_E05_MQ02_Dialog_ShustriySnork: Why do you have a Snork gas mask on your shoulder?
So you can ask me stupid questions. Works every time!
Every guide has a story about failing to take someone to their destination. We’re like doctors – if you can’t live with your failures, don’t even try.
That is why I wear this piece of shit. It reminds me that even the most experienced guides make mistakes sometimes.
sid_phrase_E05_MQ02_Dialog_Shustriy_Clea: How do I get to Clear Sky?
I saved your life once, and now I just might save it again: don’t go there. Steer clear of that place.
I should’ve shot you after all…
Fine! If you wanna die, be my guest! Here’s the map. Professor Kalancha set up some defenses when he left Clear Sky. You can't get through without this map.
There are beacons on the map. You have to calibrate four of them to your signal. Then you can get through the ruins in the Swamps and into Clear Sky.
Any other questions?
Sure, ask away. I’ll tell you whatever you want, and then I’m outta here. I’m fucking done. Finita la commedia.
Fucking Swamps, man… I gotta deal with some bullshit every single time…
How do those beacons work?
Professor Kalancha mentioned something about them affecting the controlled Anomalous psi-field around the Clear Sky base.
But I never got into that stuff, even though I used to hang out with those Clear Sky scientists. If it works, good. If it doesn’t, screw it.
The point is, you can’t get to Clear Sky without the map, and you have to re-calibrate the beacons. Otherwise, you’ll wander around the ruins until the cows come home. Unless you’re Faust. Screw him.
What is Clear Sky, anyway?

It used to be a faction. They broke up a long time ago. I used to help them out here and there – fetching stuff, running errands… They paid and fed me well, so I never asked them what they were after.
Now Faust has taken over what’s left of their base. He even somehow got around Kalancha’s defenses. His men still need a guide, but he’s walking around like he owns the place!
Don’t look at me like that. I don’t get it either.
Why did you decide to work with Faust?
’Cuz he paid me in artifacts. Some military-grade weapons too – Ward shit! I even got a Gauss rifle. Sold it in the blink of an eye. Although I should’ve auctioned it off…
I don’t know how he got to Clear Sky or why I had to guide his men there and back. That’s none of my business. Curiosity killed the cat, you know.
Who is this Kalancha guy?
A scientist. Very smart guy. He was at Clear Sky too. He knew things were going south. He’s the one who gave me the map and told me about the beacons. Then he took off without even saying goodbye.
I guess he just couldn’t stick around that base anymore. Not after everything that went down. I get it.
Whatever Faust is doing there at Clear Sky, it’s like robbing a grave. A mass grave.
So what happened at the Clear Sky base?
We went to the center of the Zone to stop Strelok… and I was the only one who made it back. You ever go back home just to find the place deserted and destroyed?
Yes.
Then you know there are some things you just can’t fix.
All right. You’re free to go.
I wouldn’t go anywhere near that place if I were you. But I get the feeling you ain’t gonna listen.
I met a guy once who was as stubborn as you. He had a big-ass scar across his whole face. And crazy eyes, just like yours. How does the Zone even find guys like you?
I ain’t gonna wish you luck – you don’t need it. So… I hope we never meet again.
sid_phrase_E05_MQ02_Dialog_ShustryjQuest: I told you: re-calibrate the beacons, and you can get past the ruins and into the Clear Sky. Nothing to it.
sid_phrase_E05_MQ02_Dialog_SkadovskGuide: Clear Sky? I don't have that kind of luck.
See that guy over there? His name’s Richter. He used to be a guide too. Ask him — he might be wasted enough for a crazy job like that.
sid_phrase_E05_MQ02_Dialog_ZotovAboutFau: He’s dead. He lost his shit and shot everybody at Echo Station.
Fuck me... The colonel’s gonna rip me a new one for that. Anyway, thanks for dealing with him. I’ll send some men over to clean up the mess.
Skif, come in. What about Shterev? Did you find him?
sid_phrase_E05_MQ02_Dialog_Zveroboy_Talk: Hey. Tryin’ to ruin my hunt, are you?
I hear you used to work with Nimble. I’m looking for him.
Yeah, I did. He wasn’t much help, though.
I sent him to a Snork cave. All he had to do was hit a button, but I haven’t heard any explosions yet. The bastard sure was glad to get his pay up front… Now I remember why they call him Nimble.
So what kind of job were you working on with Nimble?
Something for the Sultan. He’s a scumbag, of course. But he’s a very thorough scumbag.
He asked us to mop up the local mutants, so his boys wouldn’t be so scared to cross the Swamps.
Here in the Zone, you never know for sure. I haven't seen anything but the Sultan’s boys, and they're scattered all over the Swamps.
Keep your eyes open. That creature’s nearby, I can feel it. My gut never lies.
Can you give me the coordinates to the cave? I’ll try looking for him.
What’s the point? He’s already drinking away the Sultan’s advance somewhere.
Sure, you can have it. Quiet! Did you hear that?
The creature’s here! Come on, grab your biggest gun. This is gonna be one hell of a hunt!
sid_phrase_E05_MQ02_sustryj_0_CallPlayer: Can I have a word with you?
sid_phrase_E05_MQ02_sustryj_0_InfoTopic_: It’s time to get out of here. Not out of the Zone, of course — out of the Swamps. There’s so much to see in the Zone, but I just keep coming back here. Life hasn't taught me anything.
sid_phrase_E05_MQ02_sustryj_0_NPC_Combat: You’re not gonna get me here!
I’ll go around. You're done for!
You gotta be around here someplace…
Ready or not, here I come!
sid_phrase_E05_MQ02_sustryj_0_NPC_Emissi: It’s over! Time to come out and play.
Emission! Time to get out of here.
sid_phrase_E05_MQ02_zveroboj_0_HelloTopi: I heard you coming from a mile away.
sid_phrase_E05_MQ02_zveroboj_0_InfoTopic: I thought about giving up hunting, but when the Sultan asked me to shoot some mutants, I jumped at the chance. Not for the coupons – for the sake of the hunt itself. Shotgun in hand, like I used to!
No, stalker. I lived as a hunter, and I’ll die as a hunter.
sid_phrase_E05_MQ03_Audiolog_FaustDeterm: Strider never understood anything. I just couldn’t make him understand.
What a waste. At one point I thought he was going to lead me out of the swamp. Instead, he led the others into it. For ten years now, my brothers have been following a shepherd lost in the dark.
I will lead my brothers out of the darkness. I will free you from the yoke of fools.
At last, I hear your voice again, Monolith.
sid_phrase_E05_MQ03_Audiolog_FaustFound_: His name, Strider, is unknown to me. But he is one of ours. You are the one who sent him to me, I know it!
He says our brothers are scattered all over the Zone, lost, with no memory of themselves. He, too, did not know who he was.
But that doesn't matter anymore! Strider and I will find the others and help them remember.
Whatever has forced you to remain silent, we shall find the reason and set things right together. This I swear.
sid_phrase_E05_MQ03_Audiolog_FaustLost_L: What have I done wrong? Why have you abandoned me?
What have I done to deserve your silence? Why must I wander in the dark, without my brothers, without purpose?
Forgive me. I know I must not doubt. But how can I know your will if I cannot hear you? Show me the way, let me know I am not… alone.
sid_phrase_E05_MQ03_Audiolog_FaustWakes_: What’s going on? I can’t… I can’t feel anyone! Where are they? Tell me where they are.
Why are you silent?! Say something! Anything!
sid_phrase_E05_MQ03_Audiolog_KalanchaLea: To all Clear Sky personnel – we have been forced to leave the base. It is impossible to determine the exact date of our return at this time.
In order to keep the base secure in the absence of scientific personnel, I have reconfigured five Emission monitoring beacons.
In addition to reading higher-order psi-effects, their functionality has now been supplemented with the emission of psi-radiation into the surrounding environment.
I hope that this will be enough to keep everything intact until our return.
sid_phrase_E05_MQ04_Comment_KotovskiyAll: Check your ammo, we’re moving out soon.
It ain’t over yet. Captain Senkevych sent us reinforcements.
sid_phrase_E05_MQ04_Comment_RewardFromSh: Good work. I’d expect nothing less from you.
But the hard part is still to come.
sid_phrase_E05_MQ04_Comment_RewardFromZo: It's been a while, Skif. Nice work.
It’s too bad Scar got away. We’ll see each other again, so this isn’t goodbye.
sid_phrase_E05_MQ04_Comment_ShramInvites: Skif, come in. If you’re heading to SIRCAA, stop by the Ranger Station. We need to talk to you.
Scar and I. We have news. It’s very important.
sid_phrase_E05_MQ04_DialogIskraSashaChay: One squad. But they’re about to get reinforced.
More reinforcements mean more casualties. We’d appreciate your help.
sid_phrase_E05_MQ04_DialogVartaKotovskiy: How did you know Spark was here?
Zotov’s been after those scumbags for a while now, ever since our guys died in the Lesser Zone. And the captain’s the real deal.
You’re pretty brave for a Sparker.
And you’re pretty dead for a Warden.
Calm down, I’m a friendly. On a… scouting mission.
A Spark camp. Follow me, Sergeant. Time to earn your salary.
sid_phrase_E05_MQ04_Dialog_Alarm_Iskra_5: Scar! We’ve got Wardens here. A lot of them.
Korshunov knows what he’s doing, I’ll give him that. Would you welcome our guests, Skif? Strider and I need some time to get off the stage.
Skif will lend them a hand. He’s a good man. And if not… my men are good soldiers.
sid_phrase_E05_MQ04_Dialog_MamontAppears: I see a loner. He’s got some kind of weird device on his back.
That loner’s a little on the weird side himself. Come on in, Skif. We’ve been waiting for you.
sid_phrase_E05_MQ04_Dialog_StriderAndShr: Can you remind me again what the Wish Granter is?
An atrocity.
It’s a device that was used to protect the Center of the Zone. They had it installed inside the CNPP Sarcophagus. It drew stalkers to it with the promise of granting wishes.
The Wish Granter – or Monolith – drove people insane. Those who survived the ordeal were brainwashed and became Monolithians.
I've always wondered, Strider – what did you wish for when you got there?
Do you think Faust is planning something that involves SIRCAA?
If you put two and two together, you’ll probably get four. Even in the Zone.
If the Monolith really is at SIRCAA like Scar says, then Faust is doing all of this for one reason: to get inside the Institute.
Dalin is hell-bent on making a scientific breakthrough… by any means necessary. If the Wish Granter is activated again—
No. I won’t allow that to happen.
Sometimes good people make bad choices. I trust you to make the right one.
Sometimes bad people make good choices. I trust you to make the right one.
What did you want to talk about?
I found out that the Ward moved the Wish Granter from the CNPP to SIRCAA.
The Monolith.
That toy is way too dangerous, even for Dalin. Especially for Dalin.
The Zone itself could suffer for it. And then the Dark would eclipse whatever Light still remains here.
I’ve seen Faust. He mentioned the Wish Granter as well before he disappeared.
So he’s in on it too. The past will never let us go, will it?
I didn’t expect to see you two here. You’re working together?
Scar’s always eager to help. But he never tells me why.
Why spoil a good surprise?
So what do you want from me?
I see you’ve found what you were looking for. And you’re going to find even more – in the darkest part of the Zone, built from the brightest of intentions.
The Scanner is your chance to get into SIRCAA. Then you’ll be able to find out if that damn machine is really there.
I’ll get my squad together and head for the Institute. We need your help, Skif. Noontide… no, the entire Zone is in danger!
Strider wants to know what Faust is up to. I want to get to the bottom of the whole Scanner business. But what’s in it for you?
See, Strider doesn’t trust me either. But, like I told you before, I just like helping people.
The Zone represents the delicate balance between the Dark and the Light in all of us. The Monolith disrupted that balance once. And it will do it again.
Besides, anything that hurts the Ward is fine by me. We all do what needs to be done, and we risk our lives for what we believe in. That’s the only way, Skif. Otherwise, we’re no better than zombies.
You know what makes a stalker a stalker? Curiosity. I told you he’d come.
You told me a lot… and left out even more. Thank you for coming, Skif.
sid_phrase_E05_MQ04_faust_0_InfoTopic_1_: May Your light shine down on the souls of the brave soldiers who gave their lives in service to Your will. Blessed be their eternal union with the Monolith!
sid_phrase_E05_MQ04_faust_0_NPC_Combat_A: I will strike them from behind.
This is just the beginning of your redemption.
Here’s a gift for the Monolith's enemies!
sid_phrase_E05_MQ04_faust_0_NPC_Combat_E: Enemy of the Monolith, you shall not escape!
sid_phrase_E05_MQ04_faust_0_NPC_Combat_O: I offer their souls to you, o Monolith!
sid_phrase_E05_MQ04_faust_0_NPC_Combat_S: In your name they shall fall, o Monolith!
sid_phrase_E05_MQ04_faust_0_NPC_Peaceful: Let the dead bury the dead.
sid_phrase_E05_MQ04_sasa_cajka_0_DefeatC: They got me! I need some help!
sid_phrase_E05_MQ04_sasa_cajka_0_InfoTop: You know what’s better than a dead Warden? A whole squad of ’em! Go big or go home!
sid_phrase_E05_MQ04_st_kotovskij_0_InfoT: I hope I get transferred to SIRCAA after this mission. I bet it's a whole lot quieter there.
sid_phrase_E05_MQ04_st_kotovskij_0_Resum: So, what were we talking about?
sid_phrase_E05_MQ04_zora_mamont_0_BusyTo: Couldn’t have picked a better time… not now, stalker.
sid_phrase_E05_MQ04_zora_mamont_0_ByeTop: Right. There’s no sense treading water.
sid_phrase_E05_MQ04_zora_mamont_0_HelloT: You got some business here, or you just wanna talk?
sid_phrase_E05_MQ04_zora_mamont_0_InfoTo: A day here, two days there… we'll be long gone tomorrow. We’re like sparks in the wind, you dig?
The Zone doesn’t like stagnation. And trust me, it knows something we don’t. Something that will change everything. Listen to the Zone, and you won’t be able to sit still either.
sid_phrase_E05_MQ04_zora_mamont_0_Resume: So, where was I…? oh, right!
sid_phrase_E05_MQ05_Comment_BatalovOnThe: A loner coming to a Warden base? Why don’t you talk to Captain Senkevych? He’s still in charge here.
sid_phrase_E05_MQ05_Comment_CaptainInRad: I’m in the comms room. Come on over.
sid_phrase_E05_MQ05_Comment_KrivenkoOnTh: I probably ought to stay out of Captain Senkevych’s sight for now.
sid_phrase_E05_MQ05_Comment_Krivenko_Bad: Trying to get on the captain’s good side, are you? I thought you were smarter than that.
sid_phrase_E05_MQ05_Comment_Krivenko_Psy: I’m afraid Captain Senkevych is under-qualified to determine the source of the disturbance. We’ll have to do it on our own.
Follow the coordinates to the underground area and take readings from the device you find there.
sid_phrase_E05_MQ05_Comment_Senkevich_Ba: So you’re in cahoots with science, eh, stalker? Kryvenko’s gonna eat you for breakfast. I guess you had it coming.
sid_phrase_E05_MQ05_Comment_Senkevich_Ps: No time to talk, stalker. My men are sweeping the area as we speak. And you have to follow the coordinates underground.
Destroy whatever’s down there that's preventing us from locating that Monolithian PDA of yours... or understanding what the fuck’s going on at my base.
sid_phrase_E05_MQ05_Comment_SoldPatyaOnT: It’s easier to breathe here. Just don’t tell anybody, okay?
sid_phrase_E05_MQ05_Comment_SoldPatya_In: The air down below is kinda… stuffy and dank. Because of the Swamps, I guess.
Nobody’s noticed I’m on my seventh watch in a row up here. Keep a low profile, okay?
sid_phrase_E05_MQ05_Comment_SoldPatya_Lo: I’m not telling anyone. I don’t want to be taken off this post.
And you don’t tell anyone about this spot either.
sid_phrase_E05_MQ05_Comment_VartaPlaneta: We were told you were coming. You can go in. Just keep your head down. You might see things from time to time.
Turn back. This is a restricted facility. Nobody’s allowed in. That's an order.
sid_phrase_E05_MQ05_Dialog_Krivenko_Data: Excellent, stalker. Remember to monitor your condition… and to check in with tangible reality.
Please hurry up and take the readings from my antennas before Captain Senkevych does something very brave and very stupid.
Without that psi-signature data, there’s no way for me to identify your traitor.
sid_phrase_E05_MQ05_Dialog_Krivenko_Loca: I got all three reports. Laugh if you want, stalker, but it seems like you were right. Icarus may indeed be exposed to the influence from outside.
Not exactly. But I did find an interesting disturbance in the psi-field — here, in the camp. So much for the much-vaunted Ward...
I remember Senkevych puffing out his chest when he said his “most trusted man,” Shterev, had checked everything and that the threat was clearly coming from outside the camp.
Follow these coordinates. I’ll have security let you in. If the thing beneath Icarus is what I think it is, I want you to leave it alone. But make sure you get a reading from it.
sid_phrase_E05_MQ05_Dialog_Krivenko_Meet: How would your data help us figure out what’s going on?
Don’t get me wrong – I could explain everything, of course, but why bother? If you wanted to listen to my lectures, you’d be a scientist, not a stalker.
Just bring the data to me, and I’ll do what I was sent here to do.
Deal. I’ll get your data.
It’s a pleasure working with you, stalker. Here’s a recording device.
Tell me if you start feeling unwell. Remember, I’m here to help.
The captain isn't happy about your antennas. He even asked me to destroy them.
How barbaric! But I can see where the captain is coming from – he wants to look out for his men, even if it costs him his career. I’ve got to hand it to him.
However, his sacrifice will be for naught if we don’t discover what’s going on here and who’s responsible for it.
That’s what I need you for. I’ve been informed about a traitor at Icarus. Can you track him down?
In theory… I need to get up-to-date readings from the scanning antennas before he does anything drastic. Let’s collect the evidence before he destroys it.
If you ask me, Senkevych’s methods inspire more confidence.
Is that so? Well, give my regards to the captain.
And don’t forget to warn him: if anything happens to my equipment, he can say goodbye to his career in the Ward. I’ll make sure of it.
I’ve been informed that there’s a traitor in the Ward. He probably has Monolith connections. Can you help me find him?
Monolith? Impossible. I would have noticed for sure.
But if you insist, let’s run another check. I’ll need up-to-date readings from the scanning antennas in order to find your traitor.
Unless, of course, you’re just imagining him. That’s the order of the day around here.
psi-signature? What’s that?
How can I explain it in a way even a stalker could understand? Let’s say it's like your brain’s fingerprint.
It’s not quite a scientific explanation, but it gets the point across.
A stalker at Icarus? These are desperate times indeed. How are you feeling? Any headaches? Dizziness, perhaps?
I wish I had the proper equipment to read your psi-signature. Given the circumstances, it never hurts to have a, uh... control sample.
So what exactly is going on here, anyway?
psi-exposure of unidentified origin and power. And unidentified consequences.
Unidentified for now. But I’m working on it.
sid_phrase_E05_MQ05_Dialog_Krivenko_PsyL: Get to the point.
It’s pretty simple. Lieutenant Shterev’s psi-signature is nearly identical to those we’ve taken from the former Monolithians. Can you believe it?
So it was altered? How?
Based on what I can see right off the bat, the power has been reduced, but the frequency range has been significantly expanded. And whoever made the modifications knew exactly what they were doing.
Hmm... I’ll have to make some adjustments to my preliminary calculations… Was there anything else, stalker?
How did it go?
Not bad. I found a strange device.
Just as I thought! That, my friend, is a psi beacon. Old Monolith technology... with a few recent modifications.
A psi beacon? What’s that?
To put it simply, it's a device that emits psi-waves in a specific spectrum. Regular exposure can even turn a man into a zombie.
This particular unit works a bit differently, but the principle is the same.
How did that psi beacon end up there?
How does anything end up anywhere? Someone put it there. If the signatures I’ve read with your assistance are to be believed, this one was put there by Lt. Shterev.
The same Shterev who was supposed to check the underground area on Senkevych’s orders. Korshunov’s gonna have a long talk with the captain,  I'm sure…
Do you know where the lieutenant is?
I can take a guess. My colleagues at Echo Station are doing similar research, and they picked up an interesting signature recently.
I didn’t think much of it before – there are lots of people roaming the Swamps, after all. But now I know who it is. It’s a good thing SIRCAA expeditions are always accompanied by a security detail.
I believe Shterev will be detained soon. Still, you’d better hurry up.
From the Ward to Monolith? How is that possible?
Not Monolith itself, of course. That Monolith is long gone. But someone clearly has Shterev under their control. If the lieutenant is taken alive, I’ll find out who it is. There are ways, you know.
sid_phrase_E05_MQ05_Dialog_Krivenko_Wher: Can you remind me where I can find Shterev?
Echo Station. That’s where my colleagues picked up an interesting signature recently.
If you want to intercept Shterev first, you’d better hurry.
sid_phrase_E05_MQ05_Dialog_Senkekevich_D: I would not recommend doubling the dosage. These medications have a cumulative…
This is not the wisest course of action.
Expecting any help from you wasn't the wisest course of action. You’ve been here for two weeks, and my men are only getting worse!
Sometimes we have to let the patient get a little worse in order to figure out what's wrong with him. I have the situation firmly in hand.
That’s what worries me. SIRCAA promised me the best expert in the field. Where is he? Who’s gonna tell me what happened to my men?!
I am. But it will take time. Have some patience, Captain. Your doctor has all the medications we need, and my devices are monitoring the situation. You’ll be the first to know.
Unless I lose my marbles myself first! That’s it, I’m done waiting. You're dismissed, Doc.
sid_phrase_E05_MQ05_Dialog_Senkevich_Bro: Ha, you should’ve seen the look on Kryvenko’s face! You know how to get the job done, stalker. As well as any of my men.
Did you find out anything about the Monolithian PDA? Nothing should get in the way of tracking it now.
I wish. Apparently, the antennas weren’t the only thing jamming the direction finder. There’s also something beneath Icarus itself.
Early on, I sent Shterev to check on the underground area. That’s what we’re guarding here, after all. He said the coast was clear.
I don’t like this. I’ve known Shterev for ten years now. He’s tough as nails. If he’s our rat… Get underground. They’ll let you in. Find the source and smash it to smithereens.
sid_phrase_E05_MQ05_Dialog_Senkevich_Mee: If you want us to get a lock on that Monolithian PDA, do something about Kryvenko’s antennas.
We can’t do shit as long as they’re online. Maybe that’s why he put them up — to keep his schemes under wraps.
One of your men is a rat.
I intercepted a message about a plot to sabotage Icarus. It was sent over an old frequency — a Monolithian one. Just like the PDA the traitor used to communicate.
If it’s old, we can trace it. I bet it’s Kryvenko! He put his shitty gadgets all over the place, thinking we'd never put two and two together.
All right, here’s the plan: I’ll send my soldiers for a walk around the camp, just in case. You get rid of the antennas. They’ll jam any direction finder.
All right. We have a deal.
Make it quick. Who knows what the hell these antennas are supposed to do?
I want to talk to Kryvenko first.
Go ahead. And don’t forget to talk to our quartermaster afterwards — he could spin a whole platoon's worth of briefs out of the wool Kryvenko's gonna pull over your eyes.
Stalker?! How did you get in here?
What’s the purpose of these antennas?
On paper, to monitor the level of anomalous exposure. As for what they really do... your guess is as good as mine.
If not for the papers from Dalin, I’d toss Kryvenko into the nearest anomaly myself. Then he can measure the exposure on his own ass till the cows come home.
Who’s Kryvenko? Why do you suspect him?
You just saw him! That SIRCAA rat!
That bozo showed up as soon as my men started hearing things — with papers signed by Dalin himself. And I never even sent a request to SIRCAA.
He hasn’t done a damn thing in two weeks other than set up his locators to “monitor the situation.” I trust my soldiers, but not those SIRCAA crooks.
sid_phrase_E05_MQ05_Dialog_Senkevich_PDA: Still here? You need to trash the hell out of Kryvenko’s antennas. They’re interfering with our ability to trace the Monolithian PDA. I wouldn’t be surprised if our doctor turned out to be a rat.
Don’t drag this out. I’m counting on Colonel Korshunov being a good judge of character. We don't have much else to go on.
sid_phrase_E05_MQ05_Dialog_Senkevich_Psy: So that’s why my men were losing their shit. Shterev, you piece of shit! I’m gonna bury you!
Some kind of strange device. Had more rads than an anomaly. I didn’t feel right until I smashed it to bits.
Well? What the fuck was underground?
Can we track him? I’d like to talk to him too.
He’s not getting away now! I’ll drain these swamps, dig them up, and look under every rock!
He’s gonna answer all of my questions! Who, why, on whose orders? So many good men had to be discharged back to the Mainland – or worse – because of that asshole.
That’s all that’s left of it.
So what kind of device is it, Captain?
Let’s hope Kryvenko can figure it out. I guess I owe him an apology. It was my men, so I’m responsible for this fuck-up. I’ve got a difficult conversation with the colonel to look forward to…
sid_phrase_E05_MQ05_Dialog_Senkevich_Rad: I sent you the coordinates of the patrols, stalker. Give them some backup. We can’t let Shterev get away.
Also, check in with our medic. Have him give you some SIRCAA pills. They’ll come in handy in the Swamps.
Polansky, get me on the radio on the double!
Yes, sir! On the double, sir!
Attention, all patrols! You are to detain Lt. Shterev at once!
Do not negotiate with him. If you encounter resistance, shoot to kill.
Alpha Team, you’re cleared to enter the Claw. Bravo Team, get to the Vent.
Use extreme caution. The lieutenant may employ Monolith weapons or tech.
sid_phrase_E05_MQ05_Dialog_Senkevich_Whe: Can you remind me where I can find Shterev?
I’ve already sent out two patrols looking for Shterev. One went to the Claw, and the other went to the Vent.
Get a move on, stalker. We can’t let that rat get away.
sid_phrase_E05_MQ05_Dialog_VartaAboutLoc: About time. That thing’s making my head spin.
Well, well... looks like Kryvenko’s got himself a new assistant.
Those slackers from the Institute sure like to ride other people's coattails.
sid_phrase_E05_MQ05_rad_palij_0_InfoTopi: The rain here is only half the problem. But the fog… it’s bad enough to fool our thermal seekers. Yesterday, I was standing duty at the entrance, and I almost missed a patrol coming back.
They weren’t even glowing, I swear.
sid_phrase_E05_MQ05_rad_sergienko_0_Defe: Shit! I’m no good like this. I need a medkit.
sid_phrase_E05_MQ05_rad_sergienko_0_Info: I like it here. No drills, no exercising from dawn to dusk like they do at the Chemical Plant. The only problem here is the mud. And sometimes we handle rags and mops more than our weapons.
sid_phrase_E05_MQ05_rad_sergienko_0_Resu: So, what were we talking about again? Oh, right…
sid_phrase_E05_MQ05_radovoj_polanskij_0_: Don’t distract me from the transmission.
sid_phrase_E05_MQ05_sold_pata_0_BusyTopi: Don’t pester me right now, okay?
sid_phrase_E05_MQ05_sold_pata_0_DefeatCo: Somebody, help! It hurts! It hurts so bad…
sid_phrase_E05_MQ05_sold_pata_0_InfoTopi: I’ve had it with this humidity. Everything gets all moldy. Even the food’s no good. Whatever you eat, it always tastes like mushrooms.
sid_phrase_E05_MQ05_sold_pata_0_ResumeCo: All right, now that that’s over…
sid_phrase_E05_SQ01_Comment_ButWhy_Why_4: Do you realize what you’ve done? Get out of my sight.
sid_phrase_E05_SQ01_Comment_JobsDone_all: You’re right on time. You can send your guys home. Here are the coordinates.
I can’t believe it… thank you, stalker. I won't let your efforts go unrewarded, trust me. I’ll meet you in my office.
I’ve got bad news: none of them made it.
Fuck... why did it have to come to this? Well, maybe it’s for the best. Dying from a bullet isn’t the worst thing that could’ve happened to them.
sid_phrase_E05_SQ01_Comment_JobsDone_som: Your guys really were out of their minds. Some of them didn’t make it. I’m sending you the coordinates.
Damn it… well, at least it’s something.
sid_phrase_E05_SQ01_Comment_JobsDone_tha: Thank you, stalker. Believe me, I won't let your efforts go unrewarded. I’ll meet you in my office.
sid_phrase_E05_SQ01_Comment_JobsDone_you: How’s it going, stalker? The soldiers are getting antsy here. I don’t like it…
sid_phrase_E05_SQ01_Comment_SoldiersAliv: Come in. I’ve got a couple of injured men here. I need you to… what do you mean they’re already being transported? Where to?
Fuck… I’m sorry, guys. I tried. I really did.
What? To SIRCAA?! Stand down! Bring them here. They need my help! Do you hear me? Hello?
sid_phrase_E05_SQ01_Comment_WhatToDo_wha: Goddamn Swamps. Goddamn Senkevych… I’ve gotta do something.
sid_phrase_E05_SQ01_Dialog_Batalov_AskAg: Remind me again what you needed my help with.
sid_phrase_E05_SQ01_Dialog_Batalov_GetRe: Here you go. I hope it’s worth your while. Thanks again, stalker.
Here. I hope it’s worth your while. Either way, thanks.
At least you tried to help.
sid_phrase_E05_SQ01_Dialog_Batalov_Start: What do you want me to do?
When you find them, shoot them in the legs. Then I'll send them back to the Mainland on a medical discharge.
They just might stand a chance out there.
I’ve got my own stuff to worry about.
I understand. I just feel sorry for the guys.
You seem a little jumpy. Need help?
How did your patients manage to escape?
I have no idea. But they managed to do it without setting off any alarms, and I’m not surprised in the least.
Even when they're out of their minds, the Wardens are still pros. And these guys are scouts to boot.
Look, stalker… can you help me with something?
Three of my patients have run away, and I’m afraid their meds won’t help them anymore. Not as long as they stay in the Zone, at least.
What did you need my help with again?
Any idea where I should look for your runaways?
How the fuck should I know?! If I did, I’d find them myself. All I have is their personal belongings — there, in the drawer. You can dig around in there and see if I missed anything.
Maybe go ask the loners on the cargo ship if they saw anyone. They scurry around all over the place like rats… present company excluded, of course. You’re basically one of us now.
By the way… thank you, stalker. There aren't that many people here who actually care.
What about your own people? Why don’t you ask them?
I don’t think Senkevych would listen to me. He’d rather capture these guys and send them out for “experimental treatment.”
I have no clue what guys like Kryvenko would even do to them.
That could get them killed. You sure about this?
I know it’s risky.
But if we don’t take this risk, they’ll end up at SIRCAA. And no one with a diagnosis like that has ever come back from there. They’re better off dead... trust me on that.
sid_phrase_E05_SQ01_Dialog_Boroda_AskAbo: All right, here you go. Now spill it.
I heard the guys talking about them Wardens. They’re weirdos... nutjobs, maybe.
They were last seen here. I’ve sent you the coordinates.
I’m still looking for those Wardens. Can you tell me anything?
Sure... but, as you know, information is worth money.
Have any Wardens stopped by your place lately? They might have been acting weird.
I haven’t seen them, but I might know something… that kind of information has a price, you know.
I’ll find them on my own then.
Well, good luck with that. If you change your mind, you know the price.
sid_phrase_E05_SQ01_Dialog_ReportMedicTo: Batalov’s patients ran away. He didn’t want you to know.
That’s the last thing we need…
The doc’s got some runaways. Check with him for the details. When you’re done, send them straight to SIRCAA.
sid_phrase_E05_SQ03_Comment_Audiolog_1_5: What about you, Sergeant? I can’t do that!
sid_phrase_E05_SQ03_Comment_Audiolog_1_F: Get that thing over to me, Horshyn! To the barn!
When it comes in, close the door! On my order!
Close the door! That’s an order!
sid_phrase_E05_SQ03_Comment_ElectricityO: What just happened? Did we blow a fuse?
Shit. The load must have spiked when you hit the button.
We have to reset the circuit. I’ve sent you the coordinates. Flip the breakers — that should do the trick.
sid_phrase_E05_SQ03_Comment_Eremeev_Ohi_: “You can’t fix it, just throw it out!” Yeah, right. There’s nothing I can’t fix!
sid_phrase_E05_SQ03_Comment_PsyReady_rea: Your psi-suit’s ready, stalker. Come try it on.
sid_phrase_E05_SQ03_Dialog_Eremeev_EndQu: I had to take down a mutant in the hangar to get these parts.
Damn. I thought that thing was long dead!
Sorry for setting you up. I’ll try to get your suit ready as soon as possible.
I’ll call you when it’s done.
Just another day in the Zone.
Let me take a look. Whoa, there’s enough here to make a suit for you too! I’ll call you when it’s done.
Let's just hope I don’t lose my marbles while I’m at it, eh?
As for you, try not to drown in that quagmire out there. Keep your eyes peeled, and remember to check your head every now and then too.
sid_phrase_E05_SQ03_Dialog_Eremeev_Start: Do you have any work for me?
With the equipment and the people here both going haywire, we've got plenty. I know what to do when a machine fails, but if my head goes out of whack… I gotta ask Batalov for a bed by the window.
And those eggheads from SIRCAA give us nothing but pills. Thanks, but no thanks! If I had the parts, I’d make me a psi-suit already. They got some, but they ain’t givin’ us any.
You can get the parts at the Depot, though. Bring 'em to me, and I’ll make you a psi-suit too. A stalker could sure use one of those in the Swamps.
All right. I’ll get you your parts.
Get to the Depot. There’s a huge hangar there, with doors. The parts are inside.
The entrance is electronically locked, but you should be able to get in no problem. Unless my tools deceive me, SIRCAA still keeps the place powered on.
Find some spare parts post-haste and get back to me. No one’s supposed to be in there anymore... I think.
I don’t have time for this right now.
I’d rethink my schedule if I were you. A psi-suit could be a real lifesaver out here.
How did the parts end up outside SIRCAA?
We got orders from the top brass to set up a mobile lab. The previous tech was setting up a power grid there.
But after the first equipment delivery, the project fell apart. They discovered “biological activity in the form of a mutant.”
The eggheads ran back to SIRCAA, and what little they'd brought with them was left here to gather dust. They've got plenty of this stuff back at the Institute.
Why would you need a psi-suit when you’ve got pills?
As with anything that comes from SIRCAA, those pills might solve your problem on the spot, but then you’ll have to deal with the side effects for the rest of your life.
I may be a little bit crazy now too, but I trust myself more than their meds.
sid_phrase_E05_SQ03_Dialog_QuesAgain_Any: Remind me about that job again.
Have you forgotten already? I need you to get some parts for a psi-suit from a nearby hangar. I’ll make you one too. It’ll come in handy here in the Swamps.
Still need help with those psi-suit parts?
Sure — they ain’t gonna just show up on their own. Get me the parts I need, and I’ll make you a psi-suit too. Then your head'll be in tip-top shape!
sid_phrase_E05_SQ03_Dialog_QuesAgain_Ok_: Got it. I’ll get the parts.
Cool. But make it quick, I’ve gotta build it while I can still think straight.
sid_phrase_E05_SQ03_Dialog_TakePsy_Later: I don’t need it right now.
It’s your call, but it’s gonna be tough out there without one.
Come get it whenever you need it. I’ll hold onto it for you.
sid_phrase_E05_SQ03_Dialog_TakePsy_Psy_5: You said I could pick up my psi-suit.
Sure, I’m a man of my word.
Rest assured, the seams won’t give way on you. It’ll fit you like a glove. You want it?
sid_phrase_E05_SQ03_Dialog_TakePsy_Take_: Of course. I’m sure I’ll need it.
Good call. You won’t make it through the Swamps without one. You’ve seen what’s going on around here.
sid_phrase_E05_SQ04_Comment_KyrganIntera: I got nothin’ to say to you. Talk to the Paw.
sid_phrase_E05_SQ04_Comment_SenkevichAbo: Well, file a report about the weapon malfunction and get a replacement from Khoteyev!
Assuming he hasn’t already sold everything on the side…
sid_phrase_E05_SQ04_Comment_SergienkoInt: My orders are not to let anyone in.
sid_phrase_E05_SQ04_Dialog_ArrestHoteev_: Get to the warehouse, Serhiyenko. Don’t let Khoteyev out, and don’t let anybody else in. Get it done.
sid_phrase_E05_SQ04_Dialog_BanditFight_T: I see you brought the goods. I guess your job’s done, then.
sid_phrase_E05_SQ04_Dialog_BanditFight_h: I’m just a courier. He doesn’t report to me.
Well, now you’re gonna have to answer for him.
sid_phrase_E05_SQ04_Dialog_BanditFight_w: Why would a Warden want to have face time with some thugs?
That Warden set up a meeting with these thugs, but he was too much of a pussy to show up in person. So now you’re gonna have to answer for him.
sid_phrase_E05_SQ04_Dialog_BanditFight_y: He ain’t no cop. Who the fuck are you?
You wanna exchange business cards? Or are you gonna take the merchandise?
Cocky one, ain’t ya? All right, tell me this: why didn’t Khoteyev show up himself? He’s the one the Sultan had a deal with.
sid_phrase_E05_SQ04_Dialog_CameToSenkevi: I talked to Khoteyev. He asked me to deliver a package to some buyers.
Then do it! Go see who’s crazy enough to buy equipment from the Ward.
sid_phrase_E05_SQ04_Dialog_HoteevAskAgai: You mentioned a crooked subordinate. Still want me to check up on him?
Yeah. I think he’s trying to run a little side business. Find out if it’s true and who his buyers are.
Good. Then get to the warehouse and look for Khoteyev the quartermaster.
What did you need help with again?
My subordinate set up a deal on the side. Find out who his customers are and report back to me.
sid_phrase_E05_SQ04_Dialog_HoteevFinishQ: How did the meeting go? Tell me!
It wasn’t a meeting, it was an ambush.
Those bastards! Now I see why they agreed to my price right away. They didn’t even try to haggle!
I’m closing up shop. This little business venture didn’t work out, so screw it. I’m gonna run out the clock here, then head back to the Mainland.
Good. At least I don’t have to go pick it up myself.
I didn’t make any money on this deal. That’s all I got. Thanks for doing your part, stalker.
sid_phrase_E05_SQ04_Dialog_HoteevStartQu: You mentioned a package. Do you still need help delivering it?
Yeah, the buyers are waiting. Will you take it to the meeting place?
Do you have any work that needs to be done?
Hmm... a stalker, eh? Maybe I do.
Sure, I’ve got a job — should be real easy, especially for a guy like you. There's a package that needs delivering.
I’m in. Give me that package of yours.
Deliver it, and make sure the payment gets made. If the deal goes well, I’ll have more work for you in the future.
By the way, the commander doesn’t need to know about this. He’s got his hands full already.
What’s in the box, if you don’t mind me asking?
Nothing special — just some surplus gear we’ve written off. I didn’t take the shirts off anyone’s back, if that’s what you’re worried about.
The people I’m delivering your package to. Who are they?
We’ve never met, so I can’t exactly give you a description.
They contacted me of their own volition. All I had to do was name the price and choose a place to meet.
I’m too busy at the moment.
Isn’t courier work your specialty? Think it over and come back.
sid_phrase_E05_SQ04_Dialog_SenkevichFini: I’ve got news about that Khoteyev guy we talked about.
The sergeant was supplying equipment to the Sultan’s bandits.
Bandits?! Son of a bitch! He’s gonna get it now!
Good job, stalker! I know it’s not much, but here’s your payment.
Khoteyev’s gonna have to answer to the colonel now. That’s one less problem for me!
Did you get the package back?
Yeah, and now I’m returning it.
sid_phrase_E05_SQ04_Dialog_SenkevichStar: Why don’t you just discharge him?
Do we look like IPSF to you? That’s not our way. We need proof first.
Besides, once we figure out who the buyers are, the Ward will deal with them once and for all. That’ll send a message to everyone else.
Do you need any help, Captain?
Perhaps. I need to check on a subordinate. I suspect he’s been making deals on the side... or intends to.
If you find out who’s trying to trade with the Ward through him, I’ll reward you handsomely.
I’ll be back later. Maybe you’ll have something more serious for me then.
Things will start getting serious once Ward property is being sold all over the Zone.
So think it over and get back to me.
Fine. What do you need me to do?
The subordinate in question is Khoteyev, the quartermaster. He’s got the keys to the warehouse, and he’s been too fucking quiet over the past two weeks. I think he’s moving the goods sometime soon.
Ask him if he needs any help. I bet he’d be happy to hand this job over to a stalker — Khoteyev never leaves Icarus. Come back once you know who the buyer is.
Hurry up, before everyone in the Zone starts showing off their Warden gear.
sid_phrase_E05_SQ04_artur_lapa_0_InfoTop: I’m in good standing with the kingpin. He didn’t just send anybody to talk business; he called for me specifically. That’s respect for ya!
sid_phrase_E05_SQ04_bronevoj_0_InfoTopic: Looks like I was wrong about Khoteyev. I checked the inventory in the warehouse, and everything is where it should be. At least somebody’s got their shit together around here.
sid_phrase_E05_SQ04_serzdot_hoteev_0_Inf: The Zone only knows how to take. ’Cuz humans don’t belong in this place. I wish I had something to show for my time in the service, but no such luck. I can’t wait to get out of here.
sid_phrase_E05_SQ05_Comment_Noah_AfterQu: I gotta pull myself together. This is my second chance! Go, Skif. We can talk later.
I'll never tame another mutant. They can’t breed. It was all for nothing.
sid_phrase_E05_SQ05_Comment_Noah_Greetin: There you are again, stalker. You’re really starting to get on my nerves. I’ll ask you nicely: just go away!
sid_phrase_E05_SQ05_Dialog_AboutPlace_Ab: No one’s ever told you about the nutcase on the Ark before?
I used to offer stalkers a helping hand! Salvation from the coming Wave of mutants!
I even sheltered two of them here… but the men I rescued didn’t deserve it.
sid_phrase_E05_SQ05_Dialog_AboutPlace_al: Why do you live here all alone?
No matter how much you help the stalkers, they’ll never repay you in kind.
And I wouldn’t be alone here if I hadn’t helped those Duty members back then…
No. I don’t want to talk about it anymore.
sid_phrase_E05_SQ05_Dialog_AboutPlace_by: So why did you come? Enough loitering!
sid_phrase_E05_SQ05_Dialog_FinishQuest_A: Remember how you asked me to bring you back your bait? Well, here it is.
sid_phrase_E05_SQ05_Dialog_FinishQuest_a: Oh, right. That’s all of it. Here, take your coupons.
sid_phrase_E05_SQ05_Dialog_FinishQuest_d: Did you kill all the psi-dogs? Did you, uh... see anything out there? Maybe you heard something unusual?
sid_phrase_E05_SQ05_Dialog_FinishQuest_n: There was nothing out of the ordinary.
Nothing, then. I was hoping the Zone would offer me another opportunity to bond with a mutant, to make a friend again…
It was all for nothing! I did everything the Zone asked me to do, and this is what I get? I’ve saved other people, but I couldn’t save what matters most…
Go away, stalker. I need some time alone.
Yeah. But where's the rest of it? I’m not paying you until you deliver all of it.
sid_phrase_E05_SQ05_Dialog_FinishQuest_y: There were puppies in the dog's den. They were crammed into a hole.
You’re not trying to trick me, are you, stalker? So mutants can still reproduce! There’s still hope!
What did you say your name was?
Thank you, Skif. The Zone itself sent you to me! You’ve given me a second chance to make a friend.
Maybe we’ll meet again. But right now I need to pull myself together. This is very important!
sid_phrase_E05_SQ05_Dialog_FirstMeet_Ill: Remember to bring all the bait back to me. You won't get any coupons otherwise. And remember what I told you: if you see anything odd, tell me.
sid_phrase_E05_SQ05_Dialog_FirstMeet_giv: I hear you have Kalancha’s map. Is it for sale?
sid_phrase_E05_SQ05_Dialog_FirstMeet_jus: My bait near the Ark — bring it all back to me and deal with any psi-dogs you run into. And if you see or hear anything out of the ordinary, steer clear of it. Just let me know instead.
sid_phrase_E05_SQ05_Dialog_FirstMeet_now: If you want the thing so badly, you gotta help me first. Take it or leave it.
What do you want me to do?
sid_phrase_E05_SQ05_Dialog_FirstMeet_tel: Why did you leave bait for psi-dogs?
I had my reasons. It didn’t work out. And if it did, you’d be the first to know.
Now stop prying and get to work. I ain't payin' you to chat.
sid_phrase_E05_SQ05_Dialog_FirstMeet_you: There are too many psi-dogs in the area. They may keep stalkers away, but that’s not the reason I left that mutant bait there.
sid_phrase_E05_SQ05_Dialog_IfGetQuestAga: About that bait... you still need help?
Help? Yeah, sure. I’ve got a little job for you.
sid_phrase_E05_SQ05_Dialog_MapFirst_give: Will you give me the map now?
sid_phrase_E05_SQ05_Dialog_MapFirst_labl: There are markings on the map. Know anything about them?
They're psi-beacons. I have no clue what they do. I had no reason to ask.
sid_phrase_E05_SQ05_Dialog_MapFirst_sure: The map? Sure, you can have the damn thing! It ain't mine anyways.
sid_phrase_E05_SQ05_Dialog_MapFirst_whog: How did you end up with the map?
I can’t remember. I think some stalker gave it to me on his way back from the Swamps.
He said something about Clear Sky. Maybe the map came down from heaven. I have no idea what he was talking about.
sid_phrase_E05_SQ05_Dialog_MapFirst_youd: The map? Nope — business first, map second.
sid_phrase_E05_SQ05_Dialog_Noah_EndQuest: The job’s done. I got rid of the bait and the dogs.
Thank you, stalker. My last sin is now atoned for.
Here's your reward, as agreed. May this suit protect you better than the Ark protected mankind.
Actually, there’s one more thing: you were wrong. There were puppies in the dogs' den.
No kidding? So I was right after all? The end is coming?!
Those filthy mutants were trying to trick me! They knew I’d leave the Ark unattended! Thank you, stalker. You’re wiser and stronger than me. Now I have to get everything ready, and fast.
See you. Maybe someday we’ll meet again out there in the Zone. I sure as hell ain’t gonna stay on this rust bucket.
sid_phrase_E05_SQ05_Dialog_SecondMeeting: About your bait. What do you need me to do again?
Oh yeah, I remember now. I’m on it.
sid_phrase_E05_SQ05_Dialog_TakeQuest_IWo: I can’t do it right now.
sid_phrase_E05_SQ05_Dialog_TakeQuest_Jus: I’m not looking for anything. Just passing by.
sid_phrase_E05_SQ05_Dialog_TakeQuest_Loo: I was told you’ve got some kind of treasure map. Do you want to sell it?
You mean that piece of paper is really worth something?
Well, if you want it so badly, let’s help each other out. You fix my mistake, and I’ll give you the map.
Let’s talk about that job of yours again.
It looks like things aren’t going well for you. Need some help?
I do, stalker. I made a mistake, and I’m too frail to fix it myself.
sid_phrase_E05_SQ05_Dialog_TakeQuest_Sto: You left bait somewhere, and now you’re asking another man to bring it back. Why would you do that?
I had a friend once, a bitch named Lassie. She was as loyal and kind as they come. And then she died. A mutant’s life is short and full of pain.
I was hoping the dogs attracted by the bait would have puppies. That's how it was meant to be!
A Wave of mutants will overwhelm the entire Zone, and only the Ark will withstand the onslaught… but the dogs didn’t breed! They don’t reproduce!
How can the Wave come if dogs can’t even have puppies? I was such a fool…
sid_phrase_E05_SQ05_Dialog_TakeQuest_Tas: There’s an old camp near the Ark. I put some psi-dog bait in there.
Get it out of there before it attracts mutants from all over the Swamps. Do that, and I’ll give you the only thing of value left in this wreck.
sid_phrase_E05_SQ05_Dialog_TakeQuest_Wha: Have you decided to help me fix my mistake after all? Or are you looking for something else on this derelict vessel?
What are you looking for? The Ark’s gone. The Wave ain’t coming. There’s no point in taking shelter behind these walls.
The Wave? Never heard of it. What are you talking about?
I... I used to know it was coming. I could feel it in my bones — the earth trembling beneath the tread of thousands of feet... the Wave of mutants coming to devour the world.
Forget it. If you don’t know about the Wave or why the Ark matters, you're better off not knowing. The Wave never came anyway.
sid_phrase_E05_SQ05_Dialog_WhoAreYou_fir: What do you think you’re doing here? Quit snooping around!
You’ve mistaken me for someone else. I’ve never been here before.
Yeah, that’s what you always say. Quit messing with my head!
sid_phrase_E05_SQ05_noj_0_BusyTopic_busy: I’ve got my hands full. Go away!
sid_phrase_E05_SQ05_noj_0_InfoTopic_1_wh: This place has chosen me. This is where I was supposed to find my purpose... and my best friend.
sid_phrase_E05_SQ05_noj_0_InfoTopic_2_wh: The Zone’s given me a second chance. This time, I’ll get everything right!
sid_phrase_E05_SQ05_noj_0_InfoTopic_3_wh: I'll never tame another mutant. They can’t breed. It was all for nothing.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_Comment_Announcement: Attention! Due to Aegis Protocol initiation, groups 21-05 and 22-03 will depart ahead of schedule.
Groups 21-05 and 22-03, please proceed to the rally point immediately. Have a nice day.
Attention: Aegis Protocol has been initiated, all staff are required to follow Ward instructions.
The safety of SIRCAA personnel is our priority. Thank you for your cooperation.
Attention! Anomaly Research Laboratory staff, please proceed to evacuation.
Please leave your work stations within five minutes and proceed to the rally point. Make sure to arrive on time.
Attention, laboratory experiments schedule in sector "C" has been updated.
All activities that have been canceled will be resumed upon return of core staff. Have a nice day.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_Comment_DalinAfterMe: That’s all. You can get back to work now.
That’s all. You can get back to work now.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_Comment_DalinCall_1_: And here’s the man who made this breakthrough possible!
Come here, please. I’d like to talk to you.
