sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_Comment_DalinExperim: We’ve sacrificed our time and our comfort. We’ve toiled tirelessly for this very moment. This will be the day of our triumph.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_Comment_DalinGoGo_ne: I’ll inform Kryvenko that you’ve volunteered to help. Now go!
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_Comment_Emision_1_43: Warning! Emission imminent. All staff and guests of the Institute are to vacate the outer perimeter immediately.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_Comment_FirstAnnounc: Warning! Associate Professor Havrysh and Laboratory Assistant Smychko, proceed to the transportation platform assembly location.
Warning! Science Group 21-04 has finished its shift and is departing.
The arrival of the next group is on hold until the current protocol is lifted.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_Comment_GermanComeHe: Don’t be afraid. Come on over.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_Comment_GermanInAnom: Don’t worry, I won't leave you here. I feel like I owe you for that night.
Besides, it looks like you’ve actually brought us the Alpha. This is my accomplishment, Skif. Not Dalin’s.
The only thing that snot-nosed genius managed to achieve was botching the Second Caribbean Experiment. And that’s precisely when anomalies – and artifacts too – began cropping up outside the Zone.
But that doesn’t matter anymore, and I’m running out of time. I’ve reconfigured the Bubble. It’ll take you back to SIRCAA. Then you can tell Dalin what you think about the price he paid for progress.
My home was destroyed because of Dalin’s experiment? And you knew about it the whole time?!
What? Dalin failed to mention any “outside events.” And Korshunov conveniently left out who was responsible for your wild goose chase around the Zone.
Yes, Skif, I’ve been aware of this all along – and so have they! Every asshole in this place has an agenda of their own! At least I tried to help you. I warned you about the attack, remember?
So you knew my artifact was the Alpha all along? Then why the act?
Do you realize the odds that, out of all the artifacts, yours was the one we were looking for? I wasn’t even hoping for it.
But even if I had, why would I tell you that you held the key to the future of humanity in your hands?
This was supposed to be my triumph, and mine alone! Not Dalin’s, and definitely not yours. Sorry.
The Second Caribbean Experiment? What’s that?
Without the Alpha, Dalin’s work was going nowhere. When he realized he couldn’t replicate the artifact artificially, he resorted to drastic measures.
He tried to repeat the Caribbean Experiment. It was our first contact with the Noosphere, even before the Zone came into being. That’s when the first Alpha Artifact appeared.
I told him it was too risky, but our golden boy was willing to risk the entire world in the name of science! So how did it feel when all that progress came crashing down on your own doorstep?
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_Comment_GettingClose: Warning! You are approaching a restricted area. Unauthorized entry is prohibited.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_Comment_InfostendEkz: Generation 3 skeletons, designed by military professionals after the Zone emerged, have been used for military operations in a highly radioactive, anomalous environment.
However, the current exoskeleton design has been refined and upgraded significantly, using composite materials and compounds developed here, at SIRCAA.
The new exoskeleton is now in field trials, showing great results. Upon completion, the prototype will go into mass production.
This exhibit is a Generation 4 exoskeleton prototype, developed by SIRCAA.
It was designed by V. Dalin, a prominent scientist. The Generation 1 test prototype was assembled in 2001 at the engineering laboratory, led by V.I. Suslov.
After initial testing and improvements, Generation 2 skeletons have proven very helpful for construction workers building the Generators.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_Comment_InnerKPPGuar: Colonel, a loner is here with the Scanner. Initiating Guest Protocol.
Go ahead. They’re waiting for you inside.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_Comment_OkhrimchukHe: A stalker? How did you get in here?
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_Comment_ParkhomenkoC: Here, walk through the metal detector.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_Comment_ParkhomenkoS: All right, you can come in. Follow the protocol and stay out of trouble.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_Comment_SIRCAAGuardN: Turn back. You can’t go in.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_Comment_SIRCAANoScan: Halt! This facility is off limits. Go back to the Zone, stalker.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_Comment_SanitaryCome: Stalker? How did you… I thought the Ward… wait! Please, don’t just leave me here!
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_Comment_SanitaryRewa: I was stuck in the lab when they initiated the Aegis Protocol. Can you imagine? I had to lock myself in a cage just to avoid getting devoured by those monsters!
You’ll deal with them, won’t you? Here, take this. It’s the key card to the storeroom. Maybe you'll find something useful in there.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_Comment_ScientistEmi: Damn, now I’m gonna end up with a migraine again, and I’ve got reports to fill out. It’s about time they cleared everyone out of here…
What else did you expect? Caffeine and Emissions don’t mix. You know that.
How was I supposed to know it was going to happen right away? Unlike you, I don’t consider fortune-telling  a scientific method.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_Comment_ScientistsAb: Dalin’s been holed up in the lab for two days straight, and now he has everyone on red alert! I’m gonna punch him in the jaw one of these days, I swear!
Oh, come on. He just holds himself to a high standard and expects nothing less from the rest of us.
What if the Generators aren't going to work? We have no idea whether they are operational.
We don't. Dalin, on the contrary… Why haven't you left with the others, if you are having second thoughts?
They are not second thoughts, but mere caution… Besides, how could I miss this? I would never forgive myself.
I wonder how Hermann liked that whole scanner shenanigan. He wasn't on the departure list, I checked.
Are you certain he wasn't on the Colonel's list? I haven't seen Hermann ever since that scandal.
A stalker?! They are kicking scientists out, and letting a stalker in!
Easier to hush a stalker than issue a gag order in front of respectable professionals if another product of Dalin’s genius fails. It makes sense.
I’m speechless. What are they up to?
Are we getting ready for an experiment or a siege? Those soldiers are literally everywhere.
What is more, I am getting the feeling Wardens know more about what is about to go down than the likes of us.
As if we switched places, and now it's us having to follow orders without question.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_Comment_ScientistsAf: How are we supposed to run the experiment with so little staff? What are we going to do if things go south?
What I really want to know is whether or not everyone’s getting a raise afterwards.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_Comment_ScientistsIn: A stalker? Why’d they let him in?
First, they get all these Wardens down here, then all staff have to evacuate. Now there’s a stalker poking around.
This is a research facility, not an amusement park!
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_Comment_SkifJumpsOve: You’ve gone totally nuts in that Zone of yours!
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_Comment_StrazhEnd_ex: Program complete. Thank you for participating. Please wait for deactivation to complete.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_Comment_StrazhStart_: Attention! The Guardian Program has been initiated.
Your objective is to find the exit despite any effects of the Anomalous energy.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_Comment_StrazhWarnin: Attention! Preparing to initiate the Guardian Program. The main purpose is to train the participant to face any dangers in the Zone.
You are about to go through a series of challenges based on real combat experience.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_Comment_StriderCall_: Skif, come in! Are you there yet? Scar says the Monolith is at the Institute! Can you get to the labs and confirm it?
The Monolith? You mean THAT Monolith? And how do you know where I am?
I’ve been keeping tabs on your progress. And so have the others. But that’s not important. What matters is that they brought the installation there from the CNPP.
I’ve got people looking for a way into SIRCAA – even Richter is onboard. We can’t let them turn it on again!
Skif, come in! Are you there yet? See if you can get to the labs and find out about the Monolith. I’m counting on you, stalker.
They’ve got some serious security here, Strider.
I know. That’s why it was crucial for you to get there. My men outside are looking for a way in.
The Monolith is an evil no one should ever exploit. Please help us.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_Comment_VartaDalinFa: Do you seriously take notes during Dalin’s lectures?
You don’t get it, do you? Our planet is dying, but SIRCAA’s gonna set things right.
The Earth’s gonna be a better place.
Better for who? Did things get better for you when they assigned you here? I didn't think so.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_Comment_VartaNeedtwo: I need two in the courtyard. Over.
Where am I supposed to find them for you? You think we’re doing any better in here? You ain’t the only one the Colonel’s fucking with.
Take a smoke break, man. It’ll calm your nerves.
Wish I could. The brass are prepping for a siege here. Over and out.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_Comment_VartaNext_ne: I ran into Leshkiv today. I thought she never left her office, but… and you know what? She gave me that look!
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_Comment_VartaSpeaksT: What's taking you so long? Over.
Hold on, I need a moment to catch my breath. That dipshit Vavylov left his file at the facility again. Over.
Quit complaining, it’s not like we got anything else to do anyway. Come on out, let’s grab a smoke before our shift. Over and out.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_Comment_VartaStraj_1: There’s more Wardens than eggheads here! A new Guardian program phase?
I don’t think the Colonel would order us all here ASAP for a drill.
This is something way more serious.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_Comment_VartaWhyHere: Why is Korshunov pulling all units here?
Beats me. We received the order two days ago, no explanation. Got here from the Slag Heap, right away.
Same. We were headed to the Swamps, had to turn around halfway.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_Comment_VavilovLIAMe: Skif, isn’t it? I think we met at Icarus before.
Kryvenko? I didn’t know you were a big wheel around here.
I’m in charge of the joint Cryptobiology and Psi-Medicine Laboratory. Actually, I don’t usually venture out of SIRCAA, but occasionally a special project demands some fieldwork.
Skif? I’m Dr. Kryvenko, head of the joint Cryptobiology and Psi-Medicine Laboratory.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_Comment_VavilovOnThe: A stalker at the Institute… That’s a new one.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_Comment_VavilovTroub: Confinement breach detected in the Cryptobiology Lab. Initiating Aegis Protocol in Sector C.
Please remain calm, and have a nice day.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_Comment_VavilovZubko: By the way, the Emission shielding was also designed by yours truly. It was based on the results of the Caribbean Experiment. As for the Scanners, all credit for them goes to my colleague Hermann.
Oh, I’d love to meet him!
You’re not alone in that, my friend! Unfortunately, he just vanished into thin air…
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_Dialog_DalinAboutSca: I hope you had a good night’s sleep, Skif. We’re on the brink of the most important event in human history, and I want you to be there to witness it up close.
After all, if you hadn’t brought us the Alpha Artifact, our Artifact Farm would still be looking for the key to the Generators.
What makes the Alpha Artifact so special?
It possesses unique properties. The artifact is capable of converting electrical energy into a psi-field.
It’s also incredibly rare. We've never found anything else like it in the Zone… nor have we been able to replicate it. And it’s the only way to connect to the Generators remotely.
It's my primary project here, my pride and joy! We are capable of synthesizing artifacts that are identical to those that occur naturally within the Zone.
Regrettably, we were unable to replicate the Alpha Artifact this way… but that doesn’t matter anymore. We have the Alpha now – and it’s all thanks to you.
The Zone doesn’t just exist on its own. It possesses a center, a source. This source is located within the Generators, an installation to the north of the CNPP.
There’s no way for us to get there – physically, at least. However, we can establish a remote connection to the Generators. That’s where the Alpha comes into play.
We can solve the energy crisis, reach the outer limits of the Solar System, expand our horizons in the study of physics! Your discovery is going to change the world, Skif!
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_Dialog_DalinLabTask_: Damn! “Aegis” means an uncontrolled anomalous exposure. That means a shutdown for all labs. How are we supposed to test the Alpha now? Wait for a “special dispensation” from the colonel?
How long will it take? Even with all their gadgets, the Ward can only handle anomalous phenomena in controlled lab environments. Against something like this, we’re talking days… at least.
At this rate, I’m gonna get bedsores from your penthouse couch. Maybe I can speed this up.
Remember, the things I’ve seen in the Zone were just as baffling and uncontrollable.
Are you suggesting we skip protocol and go full stalker on this? It could work…
Look for Dr. Kryvenko. He’s probably at the ARL now. The Cryptobiology Lab is his domain – he’ll point you in the right direction.
But remember, this anomalous exposure isn’t your typical Zone activity. Our Emission Protection System has never failed us before.
Advanced weapons and ammunition. They’ve already been tested on live subjects.
Seems like a misuse of our funding, if you ask me. Once we successfully establish a link to the Generators, the Zone will disappear, and none of this will be needed anymore.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_Dialog_DalinMeetingP: Soon we will establish a connection to the Generators and gain full control over the Anomalous energy!
Soon we will establish a connection to the Generators and gain full control over the Anomalous energy!
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_Dialog_GermanInAnoma: You’ve got to understand, Skif... I didn’t think I was going to make it out alive either.
I thought the Ward was coming after me and the Scanner. You know what Korshunov does to people he sees as traitors?
If you’re so scared of Korshunov, why'd you steal the Scanner?
Steal?! It was my creation! I invented it, not that no-talent hack Dalin! And you’ve proven that it works!
What else was I supposed to do, stand by and watch the Institute squander enormous resources on that “Artifact Farm” of his?!
But Korshunov doesn’t give a damn about science. When I found out you'd come to SIRCAA, I realized that my scheme was done for, and so was I.
I had no choice but to use an experimental design to create a spatial anomaly. I knew that the Ward wouldn’t risk going through it.
It didn’t work though. We’re inside a mothballed section of X11 now, not outside the Institute as I'd intended.
Skif?! You made it here, too?! I guess I shouldn’t be too surprised, given what you went through to bring my Scanner back to SIRCAA.
I’d say I’m glad you made it through your first night in the Zone, but you probably wouldn’t believe me.
You bastard! I thought you were dead, and you… you just took off!
What else was I supposed to do, fight my way back to you? Things aren't as straightforward as they seem. Come on in, I’ll open the door for you.
I've unlocked the door. Come on in, let’s have a chat. There’s no other way out of here anyway.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_Dialog_InfoLabs_1_57: Please select the specific field of SIRCAA research you would like to learn about.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_Dialog_InfoLabs_Ques: By studying the physics behind the emergence of anomalies, the team at the Anomaly Research Laboratory has greatly improved our understanding of this phenomenon.
Here at the ARL, SIRCAA scientists are able to create and contain anomalies that would bring death and destruction outside the confines of the Institute.
Such is the power of science and the genius of those who advance it.
The Artifact Farm is a production facility within the ARL designed for the purpose of synthesizing an Alpha Artifact.
It is the pride of the Institute and the most promising project launched under Dr. Dalin's guidance. The Farm has already successfully created pure level 3 artifacts.
Soon, SIRCAA will possess the ability to replicate the treasures of the Zone in a secure and controlled environment.
The experiments performed at SIRCAA go beyond research into anomalies and artifacts — we’re also investigating the Zone’s impact on the human body.
A higher access level is required to obtain further information.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_Dialog_MeetDoctorVav: I've asked about everything I wanted to know.
Be careful out there.
Any other questions?
Then how did the anomaly show up despite your security measures?
I have no idea! We have complete control over the appearance and behavior of the anomalies you see behind me.
But that particular one developed outside the testing site. In theory, the Institute’s Emission defenses should have made it impossible. Maybe someone helped it manifest.
Sabotage is the last thing we need right now.
All right. I’ll go see what you've got there.
Here, take my gun. Korshunov issued these to all the lab managers, but I never thought anyone would actually need them.
Professor Hermann once mentioned that anomalies like this one always have an entrance and an exit. The cause of its sudden manifestation must lie on the other side.
Due to the Aegis protocol, the regular entrances to my lab are sealed. The only way in is through the ventilation system. You’ll find the engineering entrance outside, near the ARL building.
Please be careful around the specimens of local fauna in the lab. They’re quite valuable to our research.
What are you working on? I’d like to know what’s in store for me there.
Simply put, we’re studying the Zone's various harmful effects along with ways to protect against them.
I’m afraid you don't have the clearance for me to be any more specific than that.
What kinds of special projects?
That information is restricted to lab staff. Not even Dr. Dalin has the clearance to access it, so you don't have a prayer.
Do you know an anomaly has cropped up in Cryptobiology? No? My colleagues over at ARL reviewed the footage, and they think it might be a spatial anomaly.
How often does this kind of emergency happen here?
Hardly ever. Our security protocols cover every scenario, so everything is always under control. Especially when it comes to special projects.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_Dialog_MeetingScient: Your contribution to science won’t be forgotten. Thank you for recovering the Alpha.
Excuse me, I’m quite swamped right now. We’re short on time, you see.
Don’t distract me! This isn’t the right time.
You may not fully grasp how fortunate you are to be here. You're the first stalker to enter SIRCAA — and hopefully the last.
Dr. Dalin is a genius, no matter what anyone says.
I was certain they’d send me back beyond the Perimeter too. I’m so lucky!
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_Dialog_OkhrimchukTal: I’m taking the Scanner to Doctor Dalin. Where’s the internal checkpoint?
Head back. You’re going the wrong way.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_Dialog_OuterGates_Fo: Stand aside! This is a restricted strategic facility. Unauthorized entry is prohibited.
I have an appointment with Doctor Dalin. I brought the Scanner.
You must be Skif. Head to the internal checkpoint. They’ll direct you from there.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_Dialog_Reception_1_5: Identity confirmed. Greetings, Guest. Welcome to SIRCAA, the gateway to the future!
Please select a category for your inquiry.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_Dialog_Reception_Que: The penthouse is located above. With your access level, you can take the elevator down the hall.
At SIRCAA, we uphold the highest security standards  and prioritize mutual respect.
The Guest access level comes with specific limitations on movement within the Institute. Scientific facilities, engineering rooms, and certain other areas remain inaccessible to Guests.
Only the Director and the Head of Security are authorized to upgrade or downgrade access levels.
The Scientific Institute for Research of the Chornobyl Anomalous Area is a cutting-edge scientific facility that employs the world’s top scientists.
We’re working to transform the Chornobyl Anomalous Area into something that will benefit humanity rather than threaten it.
The Institute’s primary and most important purpose is to halt the expansion of the Chornobyl Anomalous Area.
In addition to our primary goal, we’re also developing a wide range of innovative technologies that will usher humanity into a new era.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_Dialog_VavilovBye_By: Now back to the business at hand.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_Dialog_Vavilov_Start: Desperate times call for desperate measures, I guess. Vavylov, head of the Anomaly Research Lab. What can I do for you, stalker?
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_Dialog_Vavilov_carri: You seem surprisingly well-informed about scientific developments for a stalker.
The second experiment took place in 2019. Some call it a total failure, but I beg to differ. Even a failure can yield results if you know where to find them.
The experiment attempted to affect an object located in the Earth's other hemisphere via a psi-field. It was carried out back in 1995. That’s when the first Alpha Artifact came into being.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_Dialog_Vavilov_gener: What do you know about the Generators?
I know quite a lot, but I can’t tell you everything. What you need to understand is that they make it possible to connect to the Noosphere, the informational field surrounding the Earth.
The energy we call “Anomalous” comes from the Noosphere. Its effect on matter is what created the Zone itself.
Artifacts and anomalies are all the result of Anomalous energy affecting our world. Particularly intense surges of this energy are known as Emissions.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_Dialog_Vavilov_skane: I’d like to know more about the Scanners.
If Hermann were here, he could explain it better than I. After all, it was his project. He was trying to obtain the Alpha by using the Scanners to recharge spent artifacts.
Regrettably, his efforts were in vain, and Dr. Dalin decided to prioritize the more promising Artifact Farm.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_Dialog_Vavilov_vybro: Tell me about the Emission shielding.
See this installation? It's my baby, so to speak. It's based on Project X technology and the early works of Dr. Dalin.
Thanks to this installation, the Institute is perfectly safe during Emissions.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_E06_MQ01_Comment_Dal: We’ve sacrificed our time and our comfort. We’ve toiled tirelessly for this very moment. This will be the day of our triumph.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_aspirant_nazarenko_0: Your sense of timing appears to be lacking, I must say.
Interesting. What could you possibly want from me? I’m listening.
I’ll be frank: Dr. Dalin is an exceptionally talented scientist, but I don’t approve of his methods.
Our mission here is to serve the greater good of humanity, not risk everything for the sake of personal success.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_assistent_zaremba_0_: I can’t talk with you now!
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_assistent_zubko_0_Bu: I’ve got no time to chat.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_assistent_zubko_0_He: What can I do for you?
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_assistent_zubko_0_In: I heard the cryptobiologists nicknamed their pseudogiant specimen “The Professor.” Hilarious, right?
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_assistent_zubko_0_Re: I forgot what we were talking about… of, right!
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_docent_grin_0_BusyTo: You picked the wrong time for this.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_docent_grin_0_InfoTo: I’ve been working with the specimens in Cryptobiology for the past month. I hoped I’d get used to it, but nope. No way.
I ended up having nightmares about those mutants! That’s when I had to request a transfer to a different department.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_docent_grin_0_Resume: How am I supposed to concentrate? Eh, forget it.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_docent_hodovskij_0_B: What? No, I can't talk to you right now.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_docent_hodovskij_0_H: I had no idea stalkers were so sociable.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_docent_hodovskij_0_I: Anomalous energy! That should be our primary focus.
Unfortunately, we can’t fully comprehend Anomalous energy yet, but our determination will ultimately prevail. It’s only a matter of time.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_docent_hodovskij_0_R: Let’s pick up where we left off.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_docent_talberg_0_Bus: As if I wasn’t busy enough!
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_doktor_hananasvili_0: Let’s not take up any more of each other's time.
It hurts so much… help me!
Be quick about it. I don’t have much time.
It’s hard to work with Korshunov’s men constantly peering over my shoulder.
I understand the need for security, but if it means setting my work back a year, I’d rather take my chances.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_doktor_krivenko_0_De: Give me a medkit. I need a goddamn medkit!
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_doktor_krivenko_0_In: At first glance, you might think SIRCAA's management doesn’t exactly see eye to eye. It does seem concerning, but let me put your mind at ease.
Dalin and Korshunov can bicker as much as they like, but they are not the ones making the final call. The Institute is in capable hands.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_doktor_raud_0_BusyTo: Not sure I should talk to you.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_doktor_vavilov_0_Def: Oh, no. I didn’t sign up for this. Help!
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_doktor_vavilov_0_Inf: Have you noticed how few people there are in the hallways? Only the cream of the crop is still here, getting ready to establish the connection.
I’m surprised Hermann isn’t here with us, though. He’s had his spats with Dalin, but his accomplishments speak for themselves. His expertise could be invaluable.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_laborant_tamm_0_Busy: I'm afraid it's not the best time to talk.
I’ll just stay here. Seems safer that way. They can’t chew through the bars, can they? They shouldn’t be able to, from what I’ve read…
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_laborant_tamm_0_Info: We’re not just doing any old research here. We’re literally discovering Physics 2.0!
It’s so thrilling to be a part of this, no matter how small. And who knows? If we succeed, maybe my name will go down in history too.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_lejtenant_korban_0_B: I’m not supposed to talk while I’m on duty.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_lejtenant_korban_0_I: Tell you what, those eggheads are going overboard with that science of theirs.
If it weren’t for the Colonel, those “scientific breakthroughs” and “unexpected outcomes” would’ve eaten us all a long time ago. It’s a good thing we’ve got a reasonable man at the helm.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_lejtenant_ostas_0_In: No matter what anyone says, I believe in science. Heck, I’m wearing my proof right here. This armor's saved me from a bullet three times, and I owe it all to science.
I don’t get how you make it in the Zone wearing those rags of yours, though. It’s a wonder you’re still alive and kicking.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_lejtenant_parhomenko: I believe we’re doing the right thing here. The Zone must be destroyed.
I've seen so many stalkers get themselves killed. You guys are dropping like flies out there. The Zone is worse than any armed conflict, and I’ve seen my share of those too.
Anyway, back to our previous conversation.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_lejtenant_sosnickij_: You couldn’t have picked a worse time, stalker.
Some folks at Icarus won’t stop whining about how tough they’ve got it there. From the way they talk, you’d think Korshunov brought us here for a beach vacation.
I wish he had! If they’d seen the Institute’s underground levels, they’d be singing a different tune. They’re just jealous because they’re no match for us.
So, what I was talking about?
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_professor_badbaar_0_: If you feel like talking, go find somebody else.
Are you sure I’m the one you're looking for?
Ever heard of the Dead Valley? The anomalous activity there is so intense, it triggers frequent and unpredictable local Emissions.
But there’s always that one moron who thinks he’s immortal and tries to strike it rich there. I hope you’re not one of them.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_professor_fursak_0_B: Did you bring my file? No? Then don't disturb me.
I’m sure you have other matters to attend to.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_professor_fursak_0_I: To hell with them! They’re easy to replace — but the bastards burned all my equipment! And I spent a year begging for those funds!
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_professor_fursak_0_R: I hate being interrupted. What were we talking about?
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_professor_german_0_I: Malachite or Dalin’s new building — it’s all the same. The way has been paved for the "‎heirs" to the great minds. No one listens to someone like me. ‎
And no one cares if you were one of the Zone’s pioneers or if your new theory is any good.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_professor_german_0_N: Get that beast away from me!
What’s wrong with you, stalker? This belongs in the morgue.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_professor_german_0_R: We got a little distracted there…
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_professor_visnevskij: I’ve got other things to do, young man.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_serzant_cindaev_0_De: I’m down! I need a medic!
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_serzant_cindaev_0_In: I don’t care about the mutants or the stalkers, but the anomalies sure do creep me out. I hear our eggheads finally have an idea about how to handle them.
They’ve got a whole lab dedicated to studying that shit for a reason. They sure take their sweet time, though... while we’re out there getting ourselves killed for the goddamn glory of science.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_serzant_ionesku_0_Bu: I don’t have time for this.
I’m on guard duty, and you’re distracting me.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_serzant_ionesku_0_In: You think I’ve seen anything new here? Nah. Just the same old human greed you see everywhere else.
On the Mainland, it smells like drugs or oil. Here in the Zone, it’s all about artifacts. And when greed is involved, people tend to turn into animals.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_serzant_lebedev_0_Bu: I’m busy. We can talk later.
Don’t bother me, I’m on guard duty.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_serzant_lebedev_0_In: Before the Ward, I adhered to military regulations. Now I follow the protocols. I don’t regret it one bit. I like it when everything is clear and organized. It makes life easier.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_serzant_ohrimcuk_0_B: Go away, stalker. You’re ruining my smoke break.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_serzant_ohrimcuk_0_I: I got into a serious jam a while back. If we were on the Mainland, I probably would’ve ended up a vegetable and be eating through a straw right now.
But the eggheads here fixed me up right quick. A couple of weeks in the infirmary, and I was already running around on my own. The Zone… it works wonders if you know how to use it.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_serzant_ohrimcuk_0_R: So, what were we talking about?
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_soldat_gorsev_0_Busy: Just what I needed… not now. Later.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_soldat_gorsev_0_Defe: Help! I can’t stop the bleeding!
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_soldat_gorsev_0_Info: Did you know Korshunov served in Afghanistan? He’s always in the thick of things, right there with us grunts.
That’s what makes a real leader.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_soldat_kluev_0_InfoT: What about me? My job here is simple: follow orders and stay alert. The real heroes here are the scientists.
They hold the keys to the future. We’ll be living in a better world soon, mark my words!
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_soldat_kulesov_0_Bus: Whatever you’ve got, it can wait.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_soldat_kulesov_0_Bye: I’ve got things to do too.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_soldat_kulesov_0_Hel: All right. Let’s have a chat.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_soldat_kulesov_0_Inf: I saw the guys from Icarus being brought to SIRCAA. It was terrifying. I never imagined people could scream like that.
The brass is keeping quiet about the incident, but I'm friends with one of them. He couldn’t even recognize me, and I doubt he even knew where he was.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_soldat_litvinuk_0_He: You got some business here, or...?
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_soldat_litvinuk_0_In: Don’t assume you can write us off just because we’re educated. No one here can stand up to the Ward.
We've got top-notch equipment and a proper protocol for every situation. You’ll never catch the Ward off guard.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_soldat_sorokin_0_Bus: Don’t you have anything better to do?
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_soldat_sorokin_0_Def: I’m not gonna last long… help…
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_soldat_sorokin_0_Inf: To be honest, I was sick of running for shelter all the fucking time. It was always either an Emission or some other crap.
But I haven't had to take cover once since I got here. Can you believe it? Accepting the transfer here was a smart move. This place is way better than Icarus.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_soldat_sorokin_0_Res: Back to our previous conversation, then?
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_soldat_zagorski_0_He: Hey there. What can I do for you?
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_soldat_zagorski_0_In: The Institute’s been so quiet since most of the eggheads moved out.
What’s the point of leaving us here to guard empty offices, though? It just doesn’t add up.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ01_soldat_zagorski_0_Re: So, what’s this about? Ah, right.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ02_Comment_AttackReady_: Warning! Theta and Gamma Squad, please proceed to your positions as specified in the new defense plan.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ02_Comment_ClassicMonol: What you want is here, stalker. Come.
Only one can be rewarded. Come to me.
You will receive what you deserve.
Your path ends here. Come to me.
Whatever you wish, it will be granted. Come to me.
The time has come. I see your wish.
The path has ended, human. Come to me.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ02_Comment_ConnectionRe: Warning! Connection sequence initiated. All authorized personnel are to proceed to their positions.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ02_Comment_DalinAfterMo: I think that’s it. He’s out. The level of...
Are you okay now, Skif? Would you come up here please?
sid_phrase_E06_MQ02_Comment_DalinBusy_Sh: You should hurry. Don’t keep her waiting.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ02_Comment_DalinCallToH: The anomaly in Cryptobiology is gone. You took care of it after all.
I wish all my colleagues kept their promises like you do. Please come see me. I have something for you. As a stalker, I'm sure you'll appreciate it.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ02_Comment_DalinIsReady: Everything's ready. Get down to the Monolith, and we'll begin the test.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ02_Comment_DalinMakesAm: I wish great experiments could always be performed without casualties.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ02_Comment_DalnAfterSce: So, now that everyone has blown off some steam and is capable of having a civilized conversation, let’s move on to the one truly important thing: science.
Do you want to know what’s there, behind the glass? It’s the dream of every stalker: the Wish Granter. Also known as the Monolith.
It’s not a myth, nor is it a deity. It’s a psi-installation created by scientists from Project X. Instead of rusting away inside the Sarcophagus, it’s now here, at the Institute.
Why did you bring the Monolith here? Wasn’t one failed experiment enough for you?
Skif, please. Alpha Artifacts are a source of power not only for the Generators, but for other Project X installations as well – including those designed to create illusions. Like the Monolith.
If your Alpha Artifact activates the Monolith now, we’ll know it's genuine and can be used with the Generators.
My people are trained for this. They will handle it.
The hell they will. It’s my artifact. I’ll be the one testing it.
So does this installation really grant wishes?
Well, it can show anyone exactly what they wish to see – a customized illusion of happiness. It’s one of its built-in functions.
But we’re both adults, you and I. We realize that all those who come to the Wish Granter are already in thrall to their own illusions.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ02_Comment_Dubny_1_5866: You should go. Strider is waiting.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ02_Comment_FalseKorsh_1: Look, Dalin – this mousetrap still works on stalkers.
Alpha is real. That’s all that matters. We don’t need this one anymore.
Strider’s buddy isn't walking out of here alive. I'm putting an end to this farce. Kill the stalker!
sid_phrase_E06_MQ02_Comment_InfoStand_1_: Welcome to the X11 Lab, the heart of SIRCAA and the flagship of scientific progress!
The X11 Lab is led by Dmytro Dalin, outstanding scientist and head of SIRCAA.
Here, the best physicists work on unimaginable discoveries for the world's good.
Welcome to the future of humanity.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ02_Comment_Infostand1_1: Warning! Enhanced security protocol is now in place. All personnel are to remain at their workstations.
Your clearance level will be restored once the connection is complete. Have a nice day.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ02_Comment_Infostand2_1: Warning! Sector C is now temporarily closed. We apologize for the inconvenience.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ02_Comment_MonolithDest: I want the Zone to disappear.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ02_Comment_MonolithKill: Humanity is irredeemable. It has to be exterminated.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ02_Comment_MonolithPowe: I want to rule the world!
sid_phrase_E06_MQ02_Comment_MonolithStop: Dalin, for fuck’s sake! Shut it down!
Okay, it’s safe now. Shutting it down.
I want this all to end!
sid_phrase_E06_MQ02_Comment_Mykhailov_Be: You down there, don't fret! Colonel has sent a negotiator. It's an in institute after all, not your dump.
But if you can't act like civilized people... the Ward's always ready to shoot some of the Zone's beasts.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ02_Comment_Mykhailov_Cl: A stalker? On the other hand, who knows the local ways better? But mind you, loner, I have orders for any outcome of this... conversation.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ02_Comment_NoonIsDead_1: You did good, soldier. I’m glad there's someone I can trust in the Zone.
Meet us at X11. Let’s get this over with.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ02_Comment_PleaseApproa: You have to get close to the installation, Skif.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ02_Comment_SignalStarte: Zero coulombs on the Knyazhinsky scale.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ02_Comment_VartaNearNoo: Everyone's in position. We'll begin on your mark.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ02_Comment_ZotovOnInter: Okay, let me catch my breath. They're waiting for you.
Damn, you’re good, soldier. You almost got me there. But it’s okay, I’ll live. And I'll never make the mistake of helping a stalker again.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ02_Dialog_DalinToAgatha: What did you see? What happened in the Monolith Room?
No, you're the one who's gonna tell me what the fuck is going on here. Whose memory did I just see? Why did the Ward attack me? Wish Granter, my ass!
Calm down, Skif. Please. That was just a... minor distortion. An illusion, nothing more. It’s over now.
He’s telling the truth, soldier. The installation has been shut down. You’re safe.
Are you planning to use the Wish Granter again?
Why would we do that? It's a relic of the old Zone, a distraction for those who managed to enter the Sarcophagus. But SIRCAA doesn't need that — we have the Ward to protect us.
Besides, even in its prime, this ancient hunk of junk never functioned as intended. The last thing I need right now is “SIRCAA fanatics,” thank you very much.
Was that another botched experiment, doc?
I wouldn’t call it that. I didn’t foresee those psi-vestiges, that’s true. The Monolith showed you someone else’s memory. I never expected that. Who knew those tall tales had a basis in reality?
But I was sure you’d be fine. It was impossible to calculate the safe level of psi-exposure when the Monolith was first created, but it’s a different story now. I used the minimal configuration, see?
You’re safe and sound, and, despite some unpleasant side effects, everything went well. The Alpha Artifact worked! We can begin the experiment!
Are you sure it’s safe connecting the artifact to the Generators?
There are certain risks, of course. But inaction would be far more dangerous. We must take control of the Zone’s Anomalous energy before disaster strikes.
When we connect to the Generators, the Zone will be eliminated, and the Anomalous energy will finally start serving the needs of mankind.
Yes?
I’m waiting for the stalker, Colonel.
Got it. He’ll be there.
It didn’t seem like an illusion to me. It felt too damn real.
Yeah, illusions don’t usually result in injuries.
Everything you saw was a figment of your imagination, Skif. Your actions, however, had an impact on your environment. That is the reason Zotov was injured, and you were not.
No one would have been injured if the illusions had been stopped sooner.
Shutting the installation down abruptly would have fried Skif’s brain. Irreversibly. I did all I could to avoid that outcome.
What are you going to do if the connection is successful? How are you going to use the Anomalous energy?
SIRCAA will become the very thing you stalkers saw in the Monolith. Except we'll grant wishes for real, and we'll do it for all mankind.
The Earth will have an inexhaustible source of energy. We will reach the outer limits of the Solar System. We will work wonders.
Go to the Projection Room, Skif. She’s waiting for you. Good luck.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ02_Dialog_DubniyTalk_2_: Dalin said SIRCAA isn’t going to use the Monolith anymore. They only needed it to prep for another experiment.
They’re gonna end all this shit right here, right now. They’re gonna destroy the Zone.
And you believe that, Skif? Do you really believe the Monolith is just “an installation” and they actually know what they’re doing?!
They'll write off all their mistakes as collateral damage in pursuit of the greater good! But we're not some by-products of their experiments, Skif. We're human beings!

If their plans have any relation to the Monolith, we’re putting an end to them. These people are not to be trusted. We can’t waste any more time. Tell me now, Skif – are you with the Ward, or with us?
sid_phrase_E06_MQ02_Dialog_DubniyTalk_E_: A hallucination, that’s what it was. Psi-influence, nothing more.
Maybe you're stronger than we were. Or maybe you just don’t realize what's happened to you yet.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ02_Dialog_DubniyTalk_He: So Strider was right? The Monolith, Skif. Is it really here?
sid_phrase_E06_MQ02_Dialog_DubniyTalk_Q_: I saw what it did to the people before me. How it made them believe in lies. It was... wrong.
Then you understand that we have to destroy it.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ02_Dialog_DubniyTalk_Va: Drop your weapons. We don't want bloodshed.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ02_Dialog_KorshAfterMon: In this illusion, you saw the Ward attacking you. So you don’t trust us?
I even let you live after you injured him.
Don’t you dare tell anyone about this, Skif. I won’t let it slide.
The Ward always pays its debts to those who help us. You'll see.
When you started shooting, Captain Zotov volunteered to subdue you. And I allowed him, my most trusted officer, to go down there alone.
When you started shooting, I didn't send a squad to take you out. I sent Zotov, my most trusted officer, to go down there and subdue you.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ02_Dialog_KorshAfterSce: Good job with the Scanner. You know how to get what you want. That's a virtue.
But you aren't able to control your own emotions. That's a vice. If you ever do something like that again, I won’t be so understanding.
Do you have any questions? We should get on with the test.
What do you think of this Monolith test?
Dalin doesn't know anything beyond his precious science. But if he says the test requires a special subject, you can be sure it's true.
If you're so eager to risk your life, I won’t try to stop you. I guess you stalkers just need to experience everything firsthand.
Let’s begin.
Head down there. We'll monitor the situation from up here.
A Monolith fanatic! Project X had such crude methods!
I found the person who killed your men in the Lesser Zone. It was Faust, a fanatic who believes in the Monolith.
I got the report on Shterev. But Clear Sky — that's news to me. Pretty bad news. I’ll look into it.
Thank you. No one should ever be left unavenged.
Thank you for bringing them to justice.
Is that some kind of threat?
It's a warning. A gentle reminder that trying to strangle SIRCAA employees in the presence of the SIRCAA Head of Security is a bad idea.
You didn't mind it that much for the first few seconds!
A moment of weakness. Happens to the best of us.
So my home burned down because of Scar?
Scar will sacrifice anything for his ideas. His goal is to eliminate both SIRCAA and the Ward. Everything else is “acceptable losses”.
Someone once told me that believing someone doesn't mean trusting them. Mull that one over.
We should have prepared better for the experiment. But I learn from my mistakes.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ02_Dialog_ZotovOnIntera: I knew you were trouble back in the Lesser Zone... you sure know how to raise a ruckus.
Did I do that to you?
But I won’t be in a hurry to save your ass next time, just so we’re clear.
Who else? Never mind, I won't hold it against you. I know you weren't yourself.
Who else? Never mind, I won't hold it against you. Orders are orders.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ02_MonolithStop_Phantom: I want this all to end!
sid_phrase_E06_MQ03_Comment_DalinAfterHe: Damn it, Skif, are you alive? Over.
The creature almost got us! This is our last bird. I can’t put Dalin’s life at risk any longer.
Listen to me carefully, soldier. You're going to have to evac on your own. Go through the underground tunnel. I order you not to get killed, is that clear? Korshunov out.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ03_Comment_DalinAfterTa: I hope you have the archive. Haul ass to the helicopter pad. We’re evacuating, and we can't wait much longer. Dalin’s condition is critical.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ03_Comment_DalingHealth: Skif, can you hear me? Skif! You have to get to the Data Center right now! There's a flash drive there with the Institute's entire archive on it. It is crucial that you retrieve it.
I need that flash drive! It contains the telemetry data from the experiment! I have to see it, I have to find out what went wrong!
I... ugh... I can’t trust anyone else with it, Skif. Here are the coordinates. Please, go. Hurry!
sid_phrase_E06_MQ03_Comment_InfoStandCor: Welcome... to the heart... of scientific progress.
The most prominent physicists... extraordinary discoveries... prosperity to the world.
Welcome to the future... of humanity.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ03_Comment_MedicsCheckU: Get me a stretcher. It looks like psi-radiation exposure, severity still unclear.
I need to go to my office... I have to find out what went wrong. Everything is so blurry...
Requesting immediate evac. Alpha-category staff member. Psi-exposure, possible concussion. We need assistance ASAP!
sid_phrase_E06_MQ03_doktor_zajcev_0_Busy: It's not a good time, stalker. Dr. Dalin is in bad shape.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ03_serzant_mihajlov_0_B: Address all your questions to Zotov.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ03_serzant_mihajlov_0_I: Whenever those fanatics follow the Monolith, it leads them to their death.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ04_Comment_DalinAboutDa: That’s enough. Send someone to get the flash drive with the archive and the experimental telemetry on it.
I need to find out what happened here.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ04_Comment_DalinSendsFo: I have to examine you, doctor.
We don’t have time for this! Get Korshunov to send his men to the Data Center. There's a flash drive there with the Institute's entire archive on it. It must not fall into the wrong hands!
It contains the telemetry data from the experiment. I have to see it, I have to find out what went wrong!
sid_phrase_E06_MQ04_Comment_NazarenkoKro: I just have to wait. The A-class always gets evacuated.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ04_Comment_RihterToMole: Skif, do you read me? Skif? Where IS everyone?!
Finally! Strider went dark, and you weren't picking up. You both have to get out of there immediately!
The Mole coordinates are on your PDA. Follow the underground tunnel out of SIRCAA. I hope we all make it through this in one piece... and that the Zone is unharmed.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ04_Comment_VartaSearchD: Keep looking. That crap has to be around here somewhere.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ04_Dialog_NazarenkoEvac: Ah, a stalker. Did they send you for me?
No. I’m trying to get out too.
Oh well. Fair enough. What a mess, right? Take care.
You need to get the hell out of here.
Where am I supposed to go? And, more importantly, why would I leave? They'll come evacuate me any minute now. I should stay put so I'm easy to find.
An explosion, I guess. All the security alarms in the lab went off. Then they started the evacuation...
But I didn’t want to lose another day! I figured it was probably nothing, just an overreaction, like that time in Cryptobiology...
Pretty stupid of me, I know. I hope the Ward won’t be too pissed when they come for me.
Why are you here all by yourself?
Oh, I’m just waiting. The Ward always follows protocol. “A-class personnel are to be evacuated immediately. Please remain calm.”
I’ve been here for five years now, so I know the drill. I know Dalin personally. They'll send someone for me, I know it.
sid_phrase_E06_MQ04_aspirant_nazarenko_0: You don’t have to worry about me — or yourself. The Ward will take care of everything. They work fast. They'll be here soon.
sid_phrase_E07_EQ01_Audiolog_Call_1_6428: A stalker lost it out there! Started calling out to the Monolith, shooting everywhere. I made a run for it. Not sure who else survived.
Those nutjobs are all over the place! They even hit the checkpoint! It’s full of dead bodies…
What the hell’s going on, Uncle Lyonya? What do we do?
sid_phrase_E07_EQ01_Comment_Fight_heyyou: Hah! Nice try! You can't fool me twice!
sid_phrase_E07_EQ01_Comment_Promt_comehe: You Monolith bastards thought you could wipe us all out, huh? Well, guess what — I’m still breathing! And I ain’t gonna hide anymore. Come to the Eastern Checkpoint, and I’ll give you a warm welcome!
sid_phrase_E07_EQ02_Comment_CloseToStalk: No! Don’t shoot! I want to join you. Yes! That’s what I want! Take me in!
O great Monolith! Embrace another son into your ranks…
sid_phrase_E07_EQ02_Comment_NearToStalke: Hold on! Hold on! Guys, I didn’t hurt any of your people, I swear! Please, don’t!
sid_phrase_E07_MQ01_Audiolog_HorsPlan_1_: The ventilation's failing. I need to work something out, otherwise, my brothers can fall ill. So weird. Usually my hands know what to do and I... just trust them.
Tried swapping the valves. It was quite a task to find the new ones. Didn't work. But I've found a couple of blown fuses.
I remembered that they need to be replaced, and then I can restart the generator. This should help.
If this works, maybe, I'll remember more? Maybe, something about myself...
sid_phrase_E07_MQ01_Audiolog_SinaiCallsJ: How’s it going? When are you guys coming back?
Today, I think. Vector almost stumbled into an anomaly. Just a bruise, but he can’t walk on his own. I dunno who’s gonna cover for him for the next few weeks.
You sure about that? I mean, I’m all for it, but… are you sure you’re ready?
Yeah, I'm sure. I don’t want to spend the rest of my life afraid of some mutant. It’s time to venture out into the Zone and become a real stalker.
sid_phrase_E07_MQ01_Comment_BatyaRadioAb: Listen up, guys! There’s something going on over at the Sphere. Gunfire and shit. Don't wanna freak anyone out, but they mentioned Monolith.
Here’s the plan: those with enough Zone experience, meet me at the bar. We’ll figure out how to defend Zalissya if they decide to pay us a visit.
No greenhorns for now. Stay put, and don’t try to be a hero. If you’re scared, Warlock’s got something to calm your nerves.
sid_phrase_E07_MQ01_Comment_Hors_Audiolo: There is hope now, brothers. The Monolith is going to disappear. This time, for good.
Strider knows what he’s doing. If we all help him, however we can, everything will turn out just the way he hopes. I know it. And I have faith in him.
You should have faith too. We’ll be back soon. Everything’s gonna be all right.
sid_phrase_E07_MQ01_Comment_LodochkaAbou: You need to fix the ventilation system in the Shelter first, Skif. Without that, you won’t be able to reach the psi-radiation source. Check Khors’s notes.
sid_phrase_E07_MQ01_Comment_Lodochka_Ask: To anyone out there, this is Lodochka. I need help. I’m at Quiet’s camp.
There’s been some intense psi-activity on the island, they all turned into Monolith again.
sid_phrase_E07_MQ01_Comment_Lodochka_Emi: Skif, come in. Did you get lost out there? I haven’t heard from you since you left.
Some guys tried to take what I found at Faust’s place.
Show me what you’ve got. Maybe we can figure out what caused Noontide to become Monolith again.
sid_phrase_E07_MQ01_Comment_Lodochka_The: Noontide, do you copy? This is Wild Island. Lodochka here.
I’ll be on this frequency every hour. If anyone can hear me, please respond.
sid_phrase_E07_MQ01_Comment_MalahitRadio: Malachite equipment detected a psi-field disturbance of immense power.
Our field teams have already faced highly aggressive behavior from Noontide members.
If they’ve returned to their previous Monolithian state, we need to brace ourselves for the worst. The absolute worst!
sid_phrase_E07_MQ01_Comment_Marauders_Ch: Did you get that guy talking? What’s his name… Lodochka?
Not yet. But I’ve got an idea. It works like a charm on folks like him.
Do whatever it takes. If we go back empty-handed, he’ll throw us to the chimera, no shit.
Copy. I’m on it. Stand by.
sid_phrase_E07_MQ01_Comment_ScarRadioAbo: What’s that loud noise? Huh? Did you hear that?
I think it’s the Zone. It’s hammering nails. In your coffins.
What, you don’t like it? Well, this is just the beginning.
sid_phrase_E07_MQ01_Dialog_Lodochka_Brin: Where did those parts come from?
Honestly? Smuggling. Strider didn’t approve of that sort of thing, so I had to use some old stalker connections.
One of them, a guy named Scar, managed to get what I needed.
You were right. It’s some kind of device. Do you know what it is?
An Emitter. It was used to control the Monolithians.
When linked to the X-Network, it transmits commands on a psi-frequency that no Monolithian can resist.
I’ve never seen one of these intact before, but I’ve come across some of the components. I even repurposed a few for extracting energy from the waste here.
I see. All right. I have to go.
Of course. I’ll be here. I’ll manage.
Only the dead have no hope. That’s what Khors used to say. He was right. He was right all along.
Do you think Noontide ever stood a chance?
Only the dead have no hope. Khors was right about that.
Thanks to you, Skif, I’m not dead yet. Hope is all I've got left.
Mute, come in… Eighth, Alphabet, Sava… Machine, are you there?
They weren’t on the island when the Signal hit. Maybe they weren’t affected… All right. Let’s see what you've got.
What are you gonna do now, Professor? Wild Island isn't safe anymore.
Where would I go? This place is my home. Noontide's home.
Only the dead have no hope. That’s what Khors used to say. He was right. He was right all along.
Do you know how many of those Emitters are out there? Did Faust mention anything?
Good thinking. After Azimuth, Strider asked me to listen to the old Monolithian network chatter again. I did come across a peculiar message.
Faust told his men to reach out to their brother Sukhin about the Sphere mission. I assume he was referring to another Emitter.
But how did he get so many? Then again, if I found the components, nothing stopped him from doing the same. Does that mean I had a hand in this? That Monolith’s return is my doing… and Faust’s?
It's the same kind of thing I saw at the Sphere. Do you know what it is?
sid_phrase_E07_MQ01_Dialog_Lodochka_Talk: You killed him, right? That mercenary... Watt, was it? He left to get some water. Said he’d waterboard me until I told him everything.
Khors is dead.
I had to shoot him. He was about to kill Richter.
The man who attacked you wasn’t Khors. My friend cherished life… Loved his life… Let others live…
If he had to choose between serving the Monolith and taking a bullet, it would be an obvious choice.
Gas isn’t an issue. I’ll figure it out.
Very prudent of you. But right now, you can only get to the underground from outside the base, to the northeast.
What did that Watt guy want from you?
They were looking for the source of the Signal, I guess.
But I have no idea who they’re working for.
What happened to Noontide?
After you left the island? First, Strider showed up. Put together a small team: Dubny, Khors, a few others. And he ordered the rest to defend the island.
He said the Ward would never forgive him for what he was about to do. But if he didn’t, there’d be no more Noontide. So they left. And then there was the Signal, and…
I can’t get their “For the Monolith!” cries out of my head.
How did a Monolithian device end up in the Shelter? Strider always tried to keep that stuff away from Noontide.
I can only think of one person. Faust. You know how much he clung to the past.
I couldn’t stand those sermons of his.
Where did that Signal come from?
Somewhere deep inside the Shelter. I’m sorry I can’t give you an exact location. My equipment didn’t last long under that psi-exposure. Just like the Noontiders...
One thing’s for sure: a blast that powerful doesn’t just happen. There must be a device. Something from the Monolith’s past, probably.
There's a problem, though. The vents at the Shelter don't work. Underground rooms are filled with toxic waste gas. This place is already falling apart without Khors.
So I gotta fix the ventilation system to reach the source?
That’s right. Khors just started working on it. I think he even ordered some parts from Quiet before he… left.
I’ve got his notes. You can have them. Khors had everything planned out, so you’ll have no trouble figuring it out.
One more thing: right now, you can only get underground from outside the base, to the northeast. After you’re done with the vents, head there.
How did you end up here at Quiet’s camp?
After the Signal, they all packed up and left. Didn’t even bother to check my bunker.
So I decided to warn everyone off the island. I needed a powerful radio, I knew Quiet had one.
sid_phrase_E07_MQ01_Dialog_Mercs_PressSk: How about I give you some coupons and we go our separate ways?
You’ll never be able to match what I’m about to get for this thing.
Give it to me, or you die.
Did your men capture Lodochka? Why?
Yeah. He was the only one who knew where to find the Monolithian device.
The Monolithian device. The one I was asked to find.
Found what you were looking for?
Drop your gun and give me the device. Now!
sid_phrase_E07_MQ01_Dialog_RichterAboutS: Damn, they got all soggy after all. I should’ve kicked the habit.
Incoming from Wild Island. Maybe not all of them got hit by that Monolith thingy... right? Come on, chime in.
That means Strider, and Dubny, and… they’re all gone? Right… I guess the Zone didn’t protect them after all.
sid_phrase_E07_MQ01_Dialog_RichterLodoch: Richter, it’s Lodochka. The island… it’s a disaster! Something is happening, and it's—
Calm down, professor. Deep breaths. I need details.
It happened all at once. Like a Signal. Intense psi-activity. Incredibly powerful. Noontide… Richter, they’ve all turned into Monolithians again!
I found out where it started, but I can’t get there. I’m at Quiet’s camp.
Damn! Someone’s here! Richter, I need your help. Can you hear me? Help!
sid_phrase_E07_MQ01_Dialog_RichterWildIs: I didn’t know about the Caribbean Experiment, Skif. I’m not one of Scar’s men, and Spark doesn’t report to me. I don’t have a clue what they’re up to or where they are.
But you’re right. Scar is capable of anything. The question is: will his shortcut lead us to the Zone we want?
Right, here’s the deal: you take care of Wild Island and Lodochka, and I’ll go find Scar.
Sure hope it’s not a Monolithian bullet keeping him quiet.
Maybe, Monolith is back because the Wish Granter was taken to SIRCAA? And Scar couldn’t let it go?
What the hell is happening on that island?
No idea. Where’s Scar when I need him, damn it? I may not be Spark, but it’s high time he showed up.
We need to get the professor out of there, Skif. There’s no way we can sort this shit out without him.
Lodochka mentioned some kind of Signal. What do you think it was?
I bet it was the exact same crap that made poor Khors jump us. He wouldn’t hurt a fly.
Lodochka’s gonna have a heart attack when he hears about this.
Ever thought it might all be Scar’s fault? He and the Ward are all busy with their Zone turf war, and the rest of us gotta deal with the fallout.
Did you know that Scar tried to stop Dalin from replicating the Caribbean Experiment?
Looks even worse than I thought.
sid_phrase_E07_MQ01_Dialog_SphereRadioAb: Perimeter, the Sphere is under attack! I repeat, we’ve got contact and need reinforcements. There’s a shitload of them!
Noontide! They breached our defenses already. Fuck! Sukhin is leading them!
Sphere, please repeat. What’s the status of your commander? Sphere, do you read? Over.
sid_phrase_E07_MQ01_Dialog_VartaRadioAbo: Base, this is Slag Heap. Noontide is storming in here like crazy. Definitely headed for the Duga.
We need immediate extraction. I repeat, we need a chopper in here, now.
We can’t hold them off. I’ve already got a dozen heav—
Sniper, two o’clock! Open fire! Keep that fucker pinned down! Don’t let him get his head up!
sid_phrase_E07_MQ01_Dialog_WildIslandTas: Skif, this is Korshunov. Respond. Over.
You're alive? Good. Now listen up.
The situation here is FUBAR. Monolith is back in full force, determined to take over the Zone.
It started all at once, like on command, — two bursts of psi-radiation from Wild Island and the Sphere in the Lesser Zone rebooted the Monolith program in their heads.
I need to know the source of that Signal, Skif. Track it down and bring it to the Chemical Plant. I’ll be waiting. Over and out.
sid_phrase_E07_MQ01_professor_lodocka_0_: Noontide is still radio silent. For now.
But I won’t give up. Not now, not ever.
“Monolith, Monolith, Monolith…” That’s all I hear in the Zone these days. I never thought of Noontide as murderers, but they've gone ruthless.
sid_phrase_E07_MQ02_Comment_Flaff1_1_565: Only in body bags. A whole bunch of ’em got brought in yesterday. I hear Pickman and Kotz are at the hospital. They’re in pretty bad shape.
sid_phrase_E07_MQ02_Comment_Flaff2_1_565: I emptied half a mag into that fucker, and he didn’t even blink — like those armor-piercing rounds were rubber bullets! I was lucky the colonel had that grenade launcher.
Thanks for the reminder — gotta get some VOGs from Ivaylov. A good old grenade up close always gets the job done, Monolith or not.
sid_phrase_E07_MQ02_Comment_Flaff3_start: First Icarus, and now this… I didn’t sign up for this shit, bro.
Looks like we’re in for a rough ride. I heard they’re calling remote squads back to base. Something big's about to go down.
sid_phrase_E07_MQ02_Comment_HimzavodVart: Where you headed? Keep it short, or I’ll put a bullet in you. After the signal, nobody’ll question it.
I’m a friendly. Here to see the colonel.
Skif, right? Come in. But don't go straight to the colonel. See Major Dvoryankov first. That’s an order.
sid_phrase_E07_MQ02_Comment_PripoyInMono: Get over here, you Monolithian scumbag. I’ll cram that Monolith of yours right up your ass!
sid_phrase_E07_MQ02_Comment_Pripoy_CallF: Hello? Is there anyone still breathing in this fuckin’ Zone?
This is Solder. I’m at the Sphere.
The place is fucked. Those Noon assholes killed everybody.
Anybody out there? Come in! If the bastards catch me, they’ll do me in too.
sid_phrase_E07_MQ02_Comment_TechnicianGr: Come here, Skif. The colonel ordered me to kit you out. I hope the sizes are right.
sid_phrase_E07_MQ02_Comment_VartaNoWay_1: The colonel’s busy. He’ll summon you later.
sid_phrase_E07_MQ02_Comment_VartaNoWay_D: The colonel’s busy. Go see Major Dvoryankov.
sid_phrase_E07_MQ02_Dialog_DalinAboutEmi: Skif, this is Dalin. The colonel says you’re looking for the source of that Signal that launched the Monolith program. Made any progress?
Great. Get it over to the Chemical Plant. We’re waiting for you.
sid_phrase_E07_MQ02_Dialog_DvorenkovAbou: Put the equipment on the table. I’ll make sure it gets to the colonel.
He'll contact you when he’s available.
In the meantime, stop by our technician. He’ll give you some gear to make sure you look the part.
sid_phrase_E07_MQ02_Dialog_Pripoy_TakeIn: How did you make it out alive?
I kept quiet and stayed low.
I thought I'd wait it out, but there was no fucking way. Those damn lunatics were playing by the book — started sweeping the whole place.
What kind of gizmo was it?
Some kind of Emitter. I didn’t ask for the details — just installed it in the Sphere like he told me to. That’s it.
Korshunov sent me. They detected a psi-radiation spike here. Do you know why?
It was all Sukhin! The motherfucker brought me some kind of gizmo. He was like, “Fix it up, it’ll make our maps more accurate.”
Did I get a say? I’m at the bottom of the totem pole here. I do what I’m told. Who’d have thought it’d turn his brain to mush?
I’m looking for the Emitter. Any idea where it is?
It was all Sukhin! The motherfucker brought me some kind of gizmo. He was like, “Fix it up, it’ll make our maps more accurate.”
Did I get a say? I’m at the bottom of the totem pole here. I do what I’m told. Who’d have thought it’d turn his brain to mush?
Oh, fuck, it’s you... Mr. Client. Didn’t expect to see you again.
What the hell are you doing here?
Is Sukhin alive? Where is he?
He’s a fuckin’ rat, and rats don’t die easy. After the psi-radiation hit and we were busy duct-taping our brains back together, the son of a bitch opened the gates for his Noon buddies.
Then he holed up at the Sphere while those fuckers massacred everyone “for the Monolith!”
Is there a way to get to the Sphere unnoticed?
Not really, but you can always cut the power. There’s a breaker nearby. Flip it, and the Sphere goes dark.
And hey, take a look around Sukhin’s office while you’re at it. He was always a shitheel, taking bribes like there was no tomorrow. He ain’t gonna need any of his stuff now.
And thanks, by the way. You saved my ass again.
sid_phrase_E07_MQ02_Dialog_RihterAboutEm: Skif, come in. What’s up with Lodochka?
He’s alive. And he helped me find the thing that scrambled Noontiders’ brains.
Good to hear. I’ve got some news too. I’ll shoot you Scar’s current coordinates.
He knew you wouldn't leave the Shelter empty-handed. Whatever you found, he wants you to bring it to him. He says it’ll come in handy for his future plans.
Keep an eye on him, Skif. I don't like those insights of his.
sid_phrase_E07_MQ02_Dialog_TechnicianAbo: Well, the brass are planning, the boys are trading stories, and I’m here busy restocking our supplies. Top-notch stuff. We're almost fully stocked, making sure there's enough gear for the reinforcements.
What’s the chatter among the troops?
Don’t you know? It’s all Monolith this, Monolith that, Monolith every which way. The big buzz is about the Duga. Word is they massacred the whole IPSF garrison there.
Where are these reinforcements coming from?
All over the place. When Korshunov got back from SIRCAA, he ordered a full-scale mobilization.
That’s what HQ is for, to keep us grunts in the dark until the orders come down the line. But when officers spend day and night briefing, we know some serious shit’s in the works.
sid_phrase_E07_MQ02_Dialog_TechnicianRew: Here, take this. This gear's top-notch, just like the colonel requested. I’ve made a few extra enhancements too... based on our combat experience.
sid_phrase_E07_MQ02_pripoj_0_DefeatComme: You son of a bitch! I’ll rip you to fucking… shit, it hurts!
sid_phrase_E07_MQ02_pripoj_0_InfoTopic_1: I’d rather stay the fuck away and watch the Monolith weed out the Zone from afar. But there's no-fucking-where to lay low! Maybe I should make a run for the Mainland.
sid_phrase_E07_MQ02_sold_cervonenko_0_Bu: I really don’t have time for this.
sid_phrase_E07_MQ02_sold_cervonenko_0_In: Ever since we lost Icarus, we've got no news, just orders. Worst of all, those orders are mostly about pulling back.
sid_phrase_E07_MQ02_sold_kalinin_0_ByeTo: See you later... if we make it out alive.
sid_phrase_E07_MQ02_sold_kalinin_0_InfoT: I’ve been thinking: a Monolithian can’t be much worse to deal with than a Bloodsucker. At least with them, we can see where to shoot.
sid_phrase_E07_MQ03_Audiolog_RAudio01_AR: November 8, 1976. Commencing stage two of the Duga test. Launch in three…
Fascinating! We can consider this test a success. I believe we can proceed to the next stage. Nevertheless…
sid_phrase_E07_MQ03_Audiolog_RAudio02_P1: Arrived at the Promin facility to locate and eliminate any potential records related to Project X and the Team.
I immediately discovered an archived audio recording of Dr. Orlov’s initial successful test.
It was a sheer stroke of luck that this data didn't fall into unauthorized hands, given that it was essentially in plain sight.
Efforts are underway to uncover any other Project X materials, including those pertaining to the modification of the Duga into a psi-installation.
sid_phrase_E07_MQ03_Audiolog_RAudio03_P2: Located and secured fragmentary data related to the experiment to map the Noosphere, including the location and interconnections of its psynapses.
The facility is in a state of advanced disrepair, with many documents lost to natural decay.
Damn it, much of the equipment is literally falling apart in my hands. The scant information I've managed to salvage will be documented only in these reports.
Fuck, am I lost or something?
Ouch! That hurts! Where the hell did this desk come from?!
sid_phrase_E07_MQ03_Audiolog_RAudio04_P3: I’ve located evidence of the facility’s modification to ensure remote control of the Duga from that damned Malachite.
These rooms aren’t even on my map! And the Ministry of Defense and Secret Technologies called it a “comprehensive briefing?”
Fuck, it’s a combination lock… how am I supposed to know if there’s anything related to C-Consciousness in there?! Just great…
Now I need to figure out the code. What the hell was Dr. Orlov thinking?
sid_phrase_E07_MQ03_Comment_AR_GRPnotice: Shit, he’s not one of ours!
sid_phrase_E07_MQ03_Comment_AR_GRPspawn_: Be careful! The fucker might still be around.
Don’t go too far — the last shift didn’t put much effort into patrolling the facility.
Look for any survivors, and keep your eyes peeled!
sid_phrase_E07_MQ03_Comment_AR_SQD_SQDha: Attention, everyone. Watch out for anyone who fits the previous shift's APB.
Lone stalker. Heavily armed. Possibly with military training. Eliminate on sight.
sid_phrase_E07_MQ03_Comment_AR_SQD_SQDst: Assemble a new recon team and dispatch them to Becker at once.
Provide him and his men with all the support they need.
sid_phrase_E07_MQ03_Comment_AR_SQD_SQDwi: Everyone, secure your positions and prepare for engagement.
There’s a good chance the enemy that took out the last shift will strike again.
sid_phrase_E07_MQ03_Comment_ProceedToExi: Target’s inside the facility. Should we continue pursuit?
Stand down! The place has been deserted for ages. There's no telling what’s down there.
sid_phrase_E07_MQ03_Comment_ScarAfterDug: How did you like it, Skif? I, for one, savored every moment. The Zone really tore the Ward asunder today.
Head over to STC Malachite and find Ozersky. He’s a friend of Spark. He’ll help us understand this tech and come up with a plan to tackle those doubles.
You’d better explain why I had to kill the same guy over and over again.
They were illusions. Phantom doubles, like those conjured by certain mutants.
Who knows what other tricks the Preacher has up his sleeve? Right now, he poses a greater threat to our cause than the Ward.
sid_phrase_E07_MQ03_Comment_ScarGoAssaul: Skif, come in! You pulled it off, didn’t you? I expected nothing less.
You did a good job and secured yourself a front-row seat for the show.
We’re right under the Monolith’s nose at the Duga, so get over there quickly. You’re the last one we’re waiting for.
sid_phrase_E07_MQ03_Comment_VartaAlarm_a: The fucker opened fire. We’ve got a few wounded.
We’ve got an intruder inside the perimeter. Apprehend him immediately!
sid_phrase_E07_MQ03_Comment_VartaAlarm_p: He appears to be armed, Lieutenant.
sid_phrase_E07_MQ03_Comment_VartaRadioBa: Man, that was crazy! I was walking past a rupture when I heard Fedorchuk shouting from inside. I peeked in, but there was no one there…
Hey, the same shit happened to me there a few times. But I usually keep quiet about it.
I wouldn’t have cared if that jerk Fedorchuk hadn’t told me the same night that I was the one calling out to him from that crack…
sid_phrase_E07_MQ03_Comment_VartaRadio_a: Just a couple more puffs, and I’ll head back.
sid_phrase_E07_MQ03_Comment_VartaRadio_c: Fall asleep on your smoke break there, Swan?
sid_phrase_E07_MQ03_Comment_VartaTalking: How long are we supposed to stay on patrol here?
Zotov said we need to stay put until we get new orders. So we're gonna keep patrolling until our knees give out.
When the captain needs us, he’ll get in touch. The rest isn't our concern.
sid_phrase_E07_MQ03_Dialog_ScarAfterCutS: You stopped Dalin from replicating the Caribbean Experiment. Why?
Feeling homesick, Skif? You shouldn’t be. If Dalin had gone ahead with it, the entire Zone would've become Cold Island, and you wouldn’t be the only one left homeless.
My only regret is that we acted too late... much like you and Strider now.
What’s the plan, Scar? What’s our next move?
The plan is as simple as it gets. The Ward intends to destroy the Zone. Along with you and me, of course.
But we're going to stop them. How? Easy enough. We’re going to strike back, starting with the Duga for now.
What do you want me to do?
First, head over to our tech, Powerplug. He’ll hand you the control Emitter and give you the 101 on how to use it and where it goes.
Oh, and Powerplug’s got a little present for you too. It's time to stop hiding, Skif. Let the Zone know you’re one of us now.
Is the Signal your doing? You provided Lodochka with the Emitters. Could you have supplied Faust as well?
Lodochka needed the Emitters. Faust needed the Emitters. The professor is a good man. And good men usually help each other.
Even when it comes to essential supplies. Sometimes, they might not even be aware of it.
sid_phrase_E07_MQ03_Dialog_ScarInteract_: You should’ve figured out by now that just asking questions won’t bring you any closer to the answers.
You’re a man of action, Skif. So start acting.
sid_phrase_E07_MQ03_Dialog_Starr_ally_59: Pretty much, but I don’t mind it. The less people know about your plans, the better off you are.
And it’s working for us so far, given that we’re all still alive.
sid_phrase_E07_MQ03_Dialog_Starr_duga_59: What do you know about the Duga?
The Duga is an old military facility. It’s been used as a control and PDA relay station ever since the Zone emerged.
But what’s really fascinating is the scale of the mess that’s about to go down in there.
sid_phrase_E07_MQ03_Dialog_Starr_plan_59: Do you know what Scar’s up to?
I know what Scar told me. He’s the kind of guy who only tells people what they need to know... even if they don't get it.
Now that you’re rolling with us, I’d say kick back and enjoy the ride.
Stick to the plan, Skif. It’s not every day you get promised a legit miracle, not some lab-brewed shit.
sid_phrase_E07_MQ03_Dialog_Starr_start_5: You still here, stalker? Get going. Korshunov’s rounding up his goons at the Duga as we speak.
sid_phrase_E07_MQ03_Dialog_Starr_varta_5: Are you really itching to go after the Ward?
Yup, ever since those assholes showed up in the Zone bleating about the greater good of humanity.
I know their type, filling their pockets with that greater good. I hated that crap back on the Mainland. Here in the Zone, I can finally fight back.
sid_phrase_E07_MQ03_Dialog_TechBase_base: Where's your main base, by the way?
It's essential for our organization’s operations, ya dig?
We’re all about staying mobile and never leaving a trail.
sid_phrase_E07_MQ03_Dialog_TechComplex_w: Tell me more about this complex.
It's an old research facility. Abandoned a long time ago, but I bet there are still plenty of secrets hidden in there. I’ve heard you've got a knack for exploring places like that.
Some folks from Malachite used to go there all the time. Put your big boy pants on, take a look around — you'll figure it out.
sid_phrase_E07_MQ03_Dialog_TechnicianUni: Scar told me to grab some scrap here.
Scrap, you can grab. As for the control Emitter, I’m handing it over as I was told. Though it probably makes no difference to grunts like you.
Don’t mess with it — we’ve had a few accidents already. Now those accidents are either drooling in a corner or doing a little dance under the Controller’s spell.
What do I need to do with the Emitter?
There’s an abandoned complex beneath the Promin factory. Inside, you’ll find the device the Emitter is meant to be used with.
Your job is to hook the Emitter up to the device, then get the heck out of there. Comms might get wonky, but we’ll know when the job’s done, rest assured.
What are all these preparations for?
We’re planning a big surprise for the Ward at the Duga.
But in order for our mousetrap to work, we have to wind it up first. And that’s where the control Emitter comes in.
sid_phrase_E07_MQ03_stepsel_0_BusyTopic_: Take a hike. I’ve got enough on my plate.
sid_phrase_E07_MQ03_stepsel_0_ByeTopic_B: That’s it. The exit is over there.
We’re done here. See you around.
sid_phrase_E07_MQ03_stepsel_0_HelloTopic: What do you want? The sooner I’m done with it, the sooner you’re out of here.
Hey there. I hope it’s nothing too serious.
Let’s cut to the chase. We can always chat later.
sid_phrase_E07_MQ03_stepsel_0_InfoTopic_: Our guys picked up something cool from the SIRCAA ruins. No fucking clue how it works, but I have a hunch it’s gotta be fun.
Once I figure out how to use this thing, we’ll take it for a spin. If it worked for the Ward, it’ll pack twice the punch against them, I tell you what.
sid_phrase_E07_MQ03_stepsel_0_Meet_Meet_: You’ve figured out by now that the road we take leads to a better Zone, right?
I’m here to pave that road. Think about it: we’re pushing the Ward so hard, they’re practically gasping for air. Spark wouldn’t be able to pull this off without the toys I’ve been whipping up.
I can give your gear a makeover too, if you want. You’re one of the good guys now — you gotta look the part.
sid_phrase_E07_MQ03_stepsel_0_UpgradeTop: It’ll do the job. Can’t promise more than that.
Gimme five minutes, and I’ll make it better than new.
I’ll get it working like a charm.
sid_phrase_E07_MQ04_Comment_A_SenkTkachC: Tachenko, eliminate that sniper! We’re pinned down!
Copy that, Captain. I’m on it!
sid_phrase_E07_MQ04_Comment_A_SenkevichG: Let’s get moving. And remember, keep it quiet, team!
sid_phrase_E07_MQ04_Comment_A_Storm2RP_s: Come in! We can’t reach the primary LZ. We need fire support!
sid_phrase_E07_MQ04_Comment_A_TkachDeadS: You’re our only chance, Skif. Those snipers will pick us off one by one. Get to Tachenko’s position and… make those bastards pay.
The commander’s down, Captain. Tachenko… they got the commander! Those sons of bitches!
sid_phrase_E07_MQ04_Comment_BAV_fluff01_: I can’t help but wonder… what if this crap — you know, the Signal or whatever — spreads throughout the entire Zone? Or even worse, makes its way to the Mainland…
It’s tough to wrap your head around, but it’d make all those other “cataclysms” look like a walk in the park.
And that’s precisely why we’re here — to make sure that doesn’t happen.
sid_phrase_E07_MQ04_Comment_BAV_fluff02_: I told you something big was about to go down. This is our chance to get back at Monolith.
And we’re balls-deep in this “something big.” This isn’t what I had in mind when I joined up…
