Eh, come on! Don’t you want to get even for the boys we lost at SIRCAA?
That’s what I'm talking about. Those sons of bitches are about to find out who they’re messing with.
sid_phrase_E07_MQ04_Comment_BAV_fluff03_: You bet. I’ve been itching to deal with the crap that’s been trying to get into our heads ever since Icarus. Once and for all.
You think Faust screwed us at Icarus too?
I got no fucking clue. We’ll kick his sorry ass, and then we’ll get to the bottom of it.
The Zone’s no stranger to all sorts of weird shit, but the Monolith’s sudden awakening sure as hell doesn’t seem like a fucking accident.
If we saw 'em wake up, it’s up to us to put ’em the fuck back to sleep. Who’s better suited for the job than us, anyway?
sid_phrase_E07_MQ04_Comment_BAV_solo_hi1: The commander is waiting for you by the tower.
sid_phrase_E07_MQ04_Comment_BAV_solo_hi2: The captain is waiting for you by the tower.
sid_phrase_E07_MQ04_Comment_BAV_solo_hi3: Come on in. Don’t keep the captain waiting.
sid_phrase_E07_MQ04_Comment_BA_Taran_flu: Should I pack just a couple of mags, or is this the time to go overkill?
If I were you, I’d grab some extra grenades too so you don't have to run around later.
You mean so I won’t have to come back for more ammo?
No, I mean so we can take down the Monolith right here, right now, instead of chasing after ’em all over creation.
Seriously, now’s the time to gear up and be ready for anything.
sid_phrase_E07_MQ04_Comment_DalinAfterDu: I’ve received the reports, Skif. Faust and his doppelgangers — is it true? Did you witness this firsthand? And why has the colonel gone silent?
I think Korshunov’s dead. Lots of others too. And yes, I saw Faust’s doubles with my own eyes.
This is bad. This is very, very bad. I’ll look for any records regarding the incident with Faust in the SIRCAA archives, but that alone might not suffice. I’m going to need your assistance.
Get to STC Malachite. There might be someone there who's heard about a phenomenon like this. We need all the information we can get. I’ll reach out to you if I find anything substantial on my end.
sid_phrase_E07_MQ04_Comment_DalinWantsAn: Well, there was hardly anything useful in our archives. Just a reference to research on so-called phantoms within a specific range of psi radiation.
I suggest you speak with Professor Ozersky, the head of Malachite. He’s known for his forays into pseudoscience, but he’s been studying the Zone for a long time. He might have some insights.
And Skif… Make sure you don’t leave without getting some answers from him.
sid_phrase_E07_MQ04_Comment_FaustRun_1_6: You must not be here. Begone!
sid_phrase_E07_MQ04_Comment_Hold_1_69733: Fuck, it’s like they don’t feel any pain at all!
sid_phrase_E07_MQ04_Comment_KorshInterac: Head to the Small Duga. Captain Senkevych will give you a detailed briefing when you arrive.
sid_phrase_E07_MQ04_Comment_KorshWhats_1: What the fuck’s going on, Senkevych?!
sid_phrase_E07_MQ04_Comment_MonolithSqua: Those who are in eternal union with the Monolith can never be stopped!
sid_phrase_E07_MQ04_Comment_MonolithStar: We have no other life beyond our devotion to Him.
sid_phrase_E07_MQ04_Comment_Senkevich_Ho: We’ll hold this position until reinforcements arrive!
sid_phrase_E07_MQ04_Comment_Storm2_Hit_1: Mayday, mayday! We’re losing altitude. The instruments…
Hand over the controls! Those are my men out there!
sid_phrase_E07_MQ04_Dialog_AgataAfterCut: What about the new home you promised me?
Do you want to live in a world where a fanatical sect tries to mold everyone in their own image? Because that’s what they’re preparing to do at the Duga.
Somehow I thought you had a different vision for the future.
One where every human being will be happier than they were yesterday, not the other way around.
Do you have any more questions, Yevhen?
Why does Colonel Korshunov see the scientists from Project X as traitors?
His statements are often quite harsh.
True, they did conduct a poorly planned and unauthorized experiment that ultimately brought them down.
But at the same time, they’re responsible for the creation of the Zone itself. So no, I wouldn’t label them as traitors. Too independent, too ambitious maybe — but traitors, they were not.
But who do you represent? Project X? The Regulatory Board the colonel mentioned?
You’re quite perceptive, that much is certain. Yes, the Regulatory Board.
As for Project X and its scientific endeavors, we simply ensured its continuity during a period when the government was unable to dedicate itself to the study of anomalous phenomena.
You don’t need to know the details, so let's just say we’re interested in anything that can propel humanity forward along the path of evolution. That includes the Zone.
The plan remains the same: we need to get the Zone’s Anomalous energy under control.
To achieve this, we need to reach the Generators and bring them online.
Then we can activate the network of research centers we've set up across the Mainland. These centers are equipped with more advanced Generator facilities.
sid_phrase_E07_MQ04_Dialog_BA_SenkevichB: How are the guys from Icarus holding up?
Thanks to you, most of them have recovered. It sure felt nice to see Kryvenko scram with that pissed-off expression on his ugly mug.
Why not launch a coordinated assault with all our forces instead of risking a small group?
What should we expect from the Monolithians this time?
Nothing good, as usual. I just hope we can get this down without another load of mystical mumbo-jumbo.
Knock on wood, Captain.
If I had an IPSF helmet handy, I would.
The usual bullshit. I’d advise you to keep your head down, but they can’t fry your brains if you ain’t got any.
Why put our men at risk? Our job is to get the enemy’s attention while the landing party hit them hard at the control points, weakening their positions for the main assault.
I’m glad folks like you ain’t in charge of our military operations. If you wanna die a hero, go ahead and charge in on your own. Either that, or you can follow orders.
The usual bullshit. I’d advise you to keep your head down, but I’ve seen enough of your kind to know that my words will fall on deaf ears.
You managed to avoid getting demoted after Icarus, huh?
No one's more surprised by that than me. On the contrary, the colonel has finally entrusted me and my men with an important assignment!
Good to see you too, Captain. I take it you made it out of Icarus.
I did, but a bunch of my guys ended up walking vegetables... all thanks to a certain fucker who thought he could play scientist.
So what happened to Icarus?
Icarus… the Zone swallowed it up, just as it has so many of my men. But let’s not talk about that now.
Well, it’s good to see you again, stalker! Ready for a new challenge?
Well, well, look who’s here... our junior research assistant. A real sight for sore fuckin’ eyes.
Hello there. I’m Captain Senkevych, the commanding officer of the Icarus garrison… or what’s left of it. I'm currently in charge of the assault operation.
I got it. Let’s go.
That’s the spirit! Here's the gear you'll need for the mission. Give me a heads up when you good to go.
Yeah, right. Here, you gotta gear up first. They’ll let you know when it’s time.
I like your spirit, stalker! Here, take some gear you might need for the mission. Give me a heads up when you good to go.
What’s our mission?
Our main mission is to divert the Monolithians’ attention at the checkpoint, allowing for a safe landing. After that, the main forces will join the battle.
Tachenko and his men will take down the sniper in the tower. That will be our signal to start the operation. Any questions?
sid_phrase_E07_MQ04_Dialog_DalinAfterCut: Tell me about the Duga. Do you think Monolith has any plans for it?
The Duga is a former military facility. Or, at least, that was its original purpose.
Its function has evolved since the creation of the Zone. It now serves as both a control facility and a communication relay station.
Of course, the Monolith isn’t after our comms. Their goal is to use the Duga to tap into the minds of pretty much everyone in the Zone. That’s their plan, I believe.
So your father was a scientist who worked on Project X?
Not just any scientist — he was the head of the team that conducted the experiment to contact the Noosphere. They eventually came to be known as C-Consciousness.
The experiment spiraled out of control. And…
Well, they’re essentially responsible for the emergence of the Zone.
What went wrong at the Institute? Have you figured out what caused the Signal?
The telemetry data on the flash drive you brought will give us a clear picture. However, it’s already evident that external interference was involved.
Someone other than us had access to Azimuth Station’s router and tampered with its operation.
As a result, the attempt to establish a connection to the Generators failed, triggering the defense protocol and restarting the Monolith program.
Thanks for retrieving the flash drive, Skif.
sid_phrase_E07_MQ04_Dialog_KorshAfterCut: And make sure to talk to Sergeant Ivaylov. I’ve arranged a little something for you: a token of our appreciation for your service to the Ward, if you will.
You’re welcome. You earned it. I hope this isn’t the last time.
I’m not like Dalin. I don’t make empty promises. If you’re fighting by my side, you won’t go into battle without the essentials.
So, how are you planning to take the Duga?
This time I’m going to harness the full power of the Ward. My vanguard is already scouting the area.
But you belong with the main force. Captain Senkevych will brief you on the details when you get to your position.
You think Monolith poses a real threat? To the entire Zone?
If they manage to establish a foothold at the Duga, it will pose a grave threat indeed.
However, the Ward has a tactical advantage. I know all about their typical MO, and they don't know anything about mine.
I hope that’s the end of it, Dalin. Let’s get down to business.
You heard everything, Skif. Now let’s not waste any more time…
Wait, Colonel! Skif, the flash drive from the SIRCAA archives — do you have it?
sid_phrase_E07_MQ04_Dialog_Korsh_ImClose: We’re on our way, Senkevych. Make sure the LZ is ready.
All squads, prepare for the assault team.
sid_phrase_E07_MQ04_lejtenant_gorovic_0_: You really think this is a good time to talk?
You’ve got a keen eye, you bastard!
All the armories at the Chemical Plant were emptied. I watched Ivaylov hauling ammo boxes from the back shelves. We’re gonna rain so much lead down on Monolith, they’ll be drowning in it.
sid_phrase_E07_MQ04_lejtenant_korban_0_I: We're lucky these brainwashed nutjobs all gathered in one place. Taking them out in one go beats flushing them out one by one all over the Zone.
Given our resources and training, it shouldn’t take more than an hour, tops.
sid_phrase_E07_MQ04_senkevich_Comment_Br: The psi installations! Skif, we need them gone before we’re toast!
sid_phrase_E07_MQ04_soldat_gersuna_0_Def: I need a bandage! Or a medkit! Anybody got one?
sid_phrase_E07_MQ04_soldat_gersuna_0_Inf: To be honest, this is a little nerve-wracking. I used to get nervous before every mission, even back on the Mainland. But this time, it’s for the best.

I’m at my best with adrenaline pumping, relying on pure reflexes. It’ll help against Monolith. stalkers say all sorts of things about them, but I’ve never heard anyone say Monolith are lousy fighters.
sid_phrase_E07_MQ04_soldat_kluev_0_InfoT: The Monolith's not gonna have another SIRCAA here, mark my words. No surprises — they’re all going down. And hey, the Duga makes a pretty decent tombstone. You can see it from miles away.
sid_phrase_E07_MQ04_soldat_kulesov_0_Inf: Everything’s going as planned, just the way it should. The colonel wrote the book on assault operations.
Folks who've fought alongside him for a while say nobody’s ever beaten his record for minimizing casualties while inflicting maximum damage.
sid_phrase_E07_MQ04_soldat_zagorski_0_In: Too bad about SIRCAA. All those years of hard work, right down Monolith’s drain. Me, I’d rather disarm them and make them build it all back. They did a decent job cleaning up Wild Island, after all.
But we’ve got our orders, and nobody’s in the mood to play nice with them now.
sid_phrase_E07_MQ04_tkacenko_0_DefeatCom: You asshole! I’m gonna make a sieve outta you!
sid_phrase_E07_MQ04_tkacenko_0_HelloTopi: Hey there! Go on, spit it out.
sid_phrase_E07_MQ04_tkacenko_0_InfoTopic: They say the Monolith’s got some damn good snipers. Guess it's time to see for ourselves. You know how folks in the Zone love to spread gossip.
sid_phrase_E07_MQ06_Comment_IskraBattle2: Scar never told us they were goddamn super-soldiers!
Fuck, Weasel, keep your mouth shut and stay focused!
sid_phrase_E07_MQ06_Comment_Iskra_Backgr: They’re swarming us! We’re trapped! They’re closing in on all sides!
Can anyone hear us? We need reinforcements!
sid_phrase_E07_MQ06_Scar_Comment_NextSte: All right, team. Secure the comm center. Skif, it’s time to move on to the next phase of the plan.
We’ve pinpointed the Monolithians’ HQ – if you can call it that. You know the drill.
sid_phrase_E07_MQ06_Scar_Comment_WakeUp_: Rise and shine, Skif. It’s time.
sid_phrase_E07_MQ06_Scar_Dialog_BeforeDu: Why do we need the comm center?
To secure total control over the Duga. We can’t afford to let our advantage slip away at the most crucial moment.
Now, we wait for the Ward. We’ve got some time, so rest up and prepare yourself.
We’ll wake you up when it’s time.
Ah, there you are. Ready for the show?
Depends. What’s the plan this time?
You’ll love it. We’re killing two birds with one stone. The Monolithians are all yours.
They're not your concern. I’ve got a little surprise cooked up for them. Just focus on the task at hand.
While everyone’s busy dealing with the mess at the checkpoint, you’ll take over the underground comm center, then eliminate the leader of the Monolith.
I’ve thought it all through. This way, you’ll come at them from their blind side. Any questions?
A surprise for the Ward? What kind of surprise?
All that matters is that they’re gonna be too busy to get in your way.
sid_phrase_E07_MQ06_Scar_Dialog_KillFaus: Skif, the Monolith's leader is outside. He’s fighting the Ward.
No way. I just killed him.
Whoever you killed, it wasn’t him. Move out, now!
sid_phrase_E07_SQ01_Audialog_LinsaFight_: Gaffer, get the rookies to safety! I’ll hold them off!
sid_phrase_E07_SQ01_Audiolog_Radio_Monol: The Monolith’s will has not been done.
Zalissya shall fall. Few infidels remain.
No one should be left alive. No one should stand in the Monolith’s path. Complete your mission.
It’s time for our final prayer.
sid_phrase_E07_SQ01_Comment_Batya_HelpKo: I told Hamster not everyone’s as nice as me.
I bet Warlock's gonna be cuddling that gun every night.
I hear Lens is gonna make it. Thanks.
sid_phrase_E07_SQ01_Comment_Batya_RadioE: Shooting at your own people at a time like this, Skif? Are you out of your mind? We’ll fight them off one way or another, but don’t even think about showing up here again, you asshole.
sid_phrase_E07_SQ01_Comment_Batya_RadioS: Zalissya is requesting assistance. Our forces are stretched thin, and the fanatics are still advancing… dammit, can anybody hear me?
This is the Gaffer. Monolith is at Zalissya. We can’t hold them back on our own. We need all the help we can get. Stalkers, step up!
sid_phrase_E07_SQ01_Comment_Batya_RadioZ: Get to the assembly hall, Skif.
We did it, folks! We fought ’em off! We beat ’em!
Head over to the assembly hall, everyone. I’ve got something to share.
sid_phrase_E07_SQ01_Comment_Batya_Radio_: To anyone seeking refuge from Monolith — come to Zalissya.
We fought them off. And we’ll keep fighting for as long as it takes.
To anyone seeking refuge from Monolith — Zalissya is holding its ground!
I repeat: if you have nowhere to go, Zalissya is safe!
I’m grateful to everyone who made this happen.
sid_phrase_E07_SQ01_Comment_Bavaria_RIP_: They sacrificed themselves for us. We’ll bury them with honor. So many graves…
sid_phrase_E07_SQ01_Comment_Drakula_Afte: Stay frosty. It’s not over yet.
sid_phrase_E07_SQ01_Comment_Farmacevt_Th: They’re watching us. Up there on the hill. You see?
Wish I could get ’em to scram, but I can’t even come close to hitting ’em.
sid_phrase_E07_SQ01_Comment_Hamster_Begg: Come on, this ain’t right! I’ll double it, okay?!
If you double it, you can tag along. I’ll watch your back out there.
Fuck… All right. have it your way.
sid_phrase_E07_SQ01_Comment_Hamster_Next: You're all a bunch of crooks! Thieves, every single one of ya!
sid_phrase_E07_SQ01_Comment_Inostranec_A: All clear. We pulled it off.
sid_phrase_E07_SQ01_Comment_Inostranec_W: Welcome to Zalissya... or what’s left of it. Come in and talk to Gaffer. He'll give you some of our supplies. We don’t have much, but we’re sharing everything we’ve got.
sid_phrase_E07_SQ01_Comment_KoldunNikola: I heard Lens is going to pull through. How about you?
I feel like I’ve aged beyond my years. I guess I’m a real “Mykolayich” now.
sid_phrase_E07_SQ01_Comment_Koldun_Fluff: One box of ammo at most, a couple of medkits, a couple of new guns, four grenades. No food.
Not a single crust of bread on any of the corpses? What the hell are they eating? All right, give the ammo to the raiding team, and put the rest in the pile. Now take a break. Good work.
sid_phrase_E07_SQ01_Comment_Moloko_After: These fuckers are damn near unkillable!
sid_phrase_E07_SQ01_Comment_MonolithPray: The infidels will fear us. You shall take away their fear.
The infidels will hate us. You shall take away their hatred.
Within the Monolith, all is one. All will return to it.
sid_phrase_E07_SQ01_Comment_MonolitiansA: Fuck, they’re coming... a shit-ton of 'em!
sid_phrase_E07_SQ01_Comment_Nicolaic_Art: Hey, Lens! I've got good news. You hear that, buddy?
sid_phrase_E07_SQ01_Comment_Nicolaic_Tal: All right. We can talk about it later.
You should get some sleep. Warlock’s got your back. You won’t be able to help anyone if you're exhausted.
sid_phrase_E07_SQ01_Comment_Rikoset_Leav: You heard him. Get lost. Now!
sid_phrase_E07_SQ01_Comment_ZalissyaClos: He’s here. Want me to fuck him up through the door?
sid_phrase_E07_SQ01_Dialog_Batya_AskAgai: Can you remind me where can I find Uncle Lyonya?
A little house on the outskirts where he used to meet newcomers. We figured the guy valued his privacy. Turns out he was just there to hide his past.
sid_phrase_E07_SQ01_Dialog_Batya_Done_id: I’ve got everything he asked for.
sid_phrase_E07_SQ01_Dialog_Batya_Done_in: Certainly. You’ve already been a big help.
sid_phrase_E07_SQ01_Dialog_Batya_Done_ki: I knew I could count on you.
sid_phrase_E07_SQ01_Dialog_Batya_Done_no: I gave it a shot, but I couldn’t find anything.
Don’t worry about it. You've done what really matters.
sid_phrase_E07_SQ01_Dialog_Batya_Done_th: The Monolithians follow their preachers. When a hydra loses its head, it takes a little while for it to grow back. It’s gonna be easier for us now.
sid_phrase_E07_SQ01_Dialog_Batya_FirstMe: Thanks for coming to our aid, Skif! Just your luck, right? Every time you show up in Zalissya, the shit hits the fan. Last time it was the Ward, and now it’s Monolith.
Glad to see you’re still alive. There ain’t many of us left — everyone who didn’t run off and didn’t get killed is here. As you can tell, we could really use some extra hands.
How can I help out?
Our supplies are running thin. Warlock is working on it. You can discuss it with him. Lens’s injury is getting worse. But the biggest problem is defending Zalissya.
You didn’t know about his past? How did he end up as a guide here?
I didn’t have many options back then. He didn’t drink much, didn’t steal, kept a low profile. Had a clean reputation. No one remembered anything bad about him…
Now I understand why.
Remember our guide? We called him “Uncle Lyonya?”
He was a Monolithian all along. I couldn’t believe my eyes when I saw him leading their squad. A second later, he ordered them to open fire at us. That’s when I had to accept the truth.
Luckily, I know where the bastard is when he’s not out there killing his own comrades.
Tell me what happened here.
The Monolithians came to Zalissya. An entire army of them… I had no clue there were so many left. I tried to get a defense together, but I couldn’t do much with these rookies.
I took as many folks as I could to safety. Eventually, the fanatics moved on. But as you can see, some of them stuck around.
My stay in Zalissya will be short. I have to get to the Sphere.
You think they managed to hold their ground? I doubt it. I suggest you stock up on gear. Head over to Warlock; he’s still got some stuff left. In return, I’d like to ask a favor of you.
You want me to kill him?
That’s right. I’ll send you the coordinates. It’s the house where he used to meet newcomers to take them to Zalissya. The bastard…
Why didn’t you leave the area?
What’s the point? Trust an old-timer — once a Monolithian is on your tail, the only way to get rid of him is to kill him.
How do you know he’s hiding there?
I warned the other stalkers about him. Told them to hold him at gunpoint if he ever showed up again. They spotted the bastard and reached out to me.
sid_phrase_E07_SQ01_Dialog_Batya_SpeachE: There's still a lot to be done. We have to bury the dead, make a plan… we don’t have much time for small talk. But first, there’s something important I need to say.
Skif, you’re the only one who stepped up and came to our aid. Chances are, everyone here owes their lives to you.
No reward can truly express our gratitude, but still… thank you.
sid_phrase_E07_SQ01_Dialog_Batya_Speach_: Zalissya is safe now, and it's all thanks to you.
This place has been our home during times of peace and our refuge during times of peril. Even if it’s nothing more than a ruin now…
sid_phrase_E07_SQ01_Dialog_Batya_Zalyssa: To all stalkers — this is the end of Zalissya. Do not come here. The only thing you'll find here is death.
sid_phrase_E07_SQ01_Dialog_Bavaria_Waiti: I should’ve stayed home. Screw these adventures.
sid_phrase_E07_SQ01_Dialog_Bob_Speach_Za: If we can laugh, we can get through this.
sid_phrase_E07_SQ01_Dialog_Dracula_First: There’s nothing scary about them. They’re flesh and blood, just like us. So much for the rumors.
sid_phrase_E07_SQ01_Dialog_DrakulaInfo_1: You know what’s odd? These Monolith guys are supposed to be guarding the center of the Zone, right? But now they’re heading the other way…
sid_phrase_E07_SQ01_Dialog_Drakula_Speac: Don’t get all weepy on us now. We’ve got work to do.
sid_phrase_E07_SQ01_Dialog_Farmacevt_Bui: Care to lend us a hand?
sid_phrase_E07_SQ01_Dialog_Farmacevt_Int: Nice shot on that fanatic up the hill.
sid_phrase_E07_SQ01_Dialog_Farmacevt_Wai: This is madness. Look for yourself.
sid_phrase_E07_SQ01_Dialog_Farmacevt_You: Holy cow! Thanks a lot. Here’s some coupons for ya. They’re the last thing I need right now. Ha, I never thought I’d say that…
sid_phrase_E07_SQ01_Dialog_Hamster_InHub: I hope this hole holds up during the next Emission.
sid_phrase_E07_SQ01_Dialog_Hamster_OnApp: The Gaffer needs your supplies, and you’re gonna hand them over.
That’s why I pay for security. You’re staring down the barrel of a gun, so I suggest you think very carefully about what you do next.
I tried to settle this without anyone getting shot.
Hey, you. How much is he paying you for security?
That’s a trade secret!
Why? You wanna offer me more, stalker?
Well, have it your way.
Now that we’re done here, have a good day, and remember to close the door on your way out.
You heard me right. This is for protecting the Gaffer’s house.
You bastard! What about our contract? The fucking mercenary code?! We had an agreement. I paid you an advance!
In times like these, the only code that matters is survival. Your greed is gonna get us both killed way before the Monolith shows up. You’re not a hamster, you’re a fucking rat.
It’s a deal.
This way I can at least help some decent folks. Especially if they’re willing to pay me more.
The Gaffer needs supplies. Can we work something out?
I’ve got security covered for another year. Plenty of food and gear too. You’ve got nothing to offer me!
Why aren’t you with the rest of the Zalissya stalkers?
They’re just pissing those fanatics off! Holed up in that house, shooting back, thinking my goods are gonna help them pull through... nah, I’ll pass. The two of us are better off here.
Keep it down! Who’s there?
Oh, it’s you. Apologies, but at the moment my trading assets are, let's say… frozen. But thanks for stopping by. Take care, and goodbye.
Not so fast.
Freeze! Don’t come any closer!
sid_phrase_E07_SQ01_Dialog_Hamster_Walki: Why did you have to get involved?!
sid_phrase_E07_SQ01_Dialog_Inostranec_Bu: Get out of the line of fire!
sid_phrase_E07_SQ01_Dialog_Inostranec_Wa: If it weren’t for the Gaffer and Warlock, we’d be worm food by now.
sid_phrase_E07_SQ01_Dialog_Koldun_AskAga: Can you remind me what you wanted me to bring again?
I’d rather have you convince him, if you can.
My gun. I handed it over to Lens for maintenance just before all of this started. It’s probably sitting on a shelf somewhere.
Hamster won’t share his supplies. He’s holed up in the Emission shelter, hoping to ride it out. That sounds like betrayal to me.
sid_phrase_E07_SQ01_Dialog_Koldun_GunDon: I think this belongs to you.
You’re a peach! Here, take this. I’ll toss in some extra coupons when I get the chance.
sid_phrase_E07_SQ01_Dialog_Koldun_Hamste: That was pretty extreme. But desperate times call for desperate measures.
I took care of your problem with Hamster.
I wanna know how you managed to persuade him!
I didn’t. I just paid his guard off.
And without his security, a huge rat becomes a scared little mouse! Pretty clever!
I talked to Hamster, but he wouldn’t give an inch.
It’s okay. No big surprise, really.
sid_phrase_E07_SQ01_Dialog_Koldun_Help_h: So what’s the deal with you and Hamster?
He’s a fucking rat. Hamster thinks he can ride this out with that guard of his and keep his stockpile of supplies. He’s living high on the hog while the rest of us are starving.
Gaffer may not see eye to eye with me, but if you ask me, not helping out in a situation like this is just as bad as pointing a gun at us.
Seems like you could use a hand.
I sure could. Gaffer and I are trying to turn this kindergarten into something that can actually stand up to those Monolithians.
Our plan is ambitious, but it won’t mean squat without some gear. And while I’m wrapped up in this mess, there are two other issues on my plate.
First, there’s Hamster. He’s holed up in the Emission shelter, hoarding supplies like they’re gold. Second, my precious gun is lost somewhere in the clutter at Lens’s workshop.
sid_phrase_E07_SQ01_Dialog_Koldun_Help_k: Sounds easy enough. So it’s all here in Zalissya?
If I sent you on some wild goose chase, I know you probably wouldn’t bother coming back, ha.
And what would I have you do out there, anyway? Make a run to the Slag Heap and back? Take on the Ward at the Pontoon Bridge?
sid_phrase_E07_SQ01_Dialog_Koldun_Help_w: What’s so special about your gun?
Well, I’ve had my baby since back when I went to Yantar with the Gaffer. She’s seen a lot.
Besides, when everyone else is lucky to get rusty old junk from Hamster’s stash, a good gun is worth its weight in gold.
sid_phrase_E07_SQ01_Dialog_Koldun_Speach: Let me tell you something, guys: Zalissya is more than a bunch of old shacks falling apart. Zalissya is all of you.
sid_phrase_E07_SQ01_Dialog_Linza_Nicolai: Don’t disturb him. He needs rest.
sid_phrase_E07_SQ01_Dialog_Moloko_Speach: Don’t sweat it, Gaffer. We can rebuild everything.
sid_phrase_E07_SQ01_Dialog_Moloko_Waitin: I’m good. I hope you are too.
sid_phrase_E07_SQ01_Dialog_Nicolaic_Abou: The old boy took the brunt of the attack. When things went haywire, he held the workshop single-handed.
He caught three bullets and some shrapnel from a grenade. Somehow he was still conscious when we finally got there and pulled him out.
sid_phrase_E07_SQ01_Dialog_Nicolaic_Arti: These are the instruments you were talking about?
That’s right, stalker! Thank you! You’ll have to talk to the Gaffer about your reward – I need to get Lens ready for surgery right away.
sid_phrase_E07_SQ01_Dialog_Nicolaic_AskA: Where can I find the surgical instruments you need?
I gave you the coordinates for three of the Gaffer’s stashes. The instruments should be in one of them.
Please hurry. Lens doesn’t have much time left.
sid_phrase_E07_SQ01_Dialog_Nicolaic_Help: I don’t know how Lens is still alive, to be honest. The artifacts have been keeping him on life support, but he’s not going to last much longer.
He's gotta get surgery, but I was so fucking busy with the wonders of the Zone that I didn’t even notice someone stole my kit.
I've a spare stored in one of the Gaffer’s stashes for a rainy day, though. Well, that rainy day is here, but everyone’s too busy to help.
If I come across your instruments, I’ll grab them.
I’ll give you the coordinates of three stashes. One of them should have my package.
Please don’t take too long. Lens is strong, but without surgery... wouldn't expect any miracles.
Got any idea who swiped your kit?
No clue. Not everyone was eager to be a hero when the Monolithians showed up.
So if you try to hunt down the thief among the dozens of refugees, you might run out of time to save Lens.
sid_phrase_E07_SQ01_Dialog_Nikolaich_Spe: When did you get so sentimental, Warlock?
sid_phrase_E07_SQ01_Dialog_ObshakFluff_1: It’s for everyone. If you need it, go ahead and take some. If not, just leave it here.
sid_phrase_E07_SQ01_Dialog_Rikoset_InHub: I made the right call. This place gives us a better shot at holding out.
sid_phrase_E07_SQ01_Dialog_Slava_Speach_: Those fuckers will never take Zalissya!
sid_phrase_E07_SQ01_Dialog_SovenokInfo_1: We’re holding our ground. Warlock got a message from Duty. They said they were ready for this. They’re defending the Slag Heap. Once things settle down, I’ll try to break through and join them.
sid_phrase_E07_SQ01_Dialog_Sovenok_First: I’ve only been in the Zone for a week. Funny, right? I haven’t even had a chance to go artifact hunting yet.
sid_phrase_E07_SQ01_Dialog_Sovenok_Speac: I made it… against all odds. And it’s all thanks to each and every one of you.
sid_phrase_E07_SQ01_Dialog_Speach_Zalyss: Zalissya is no more. We’ve lost…
What the hell are you talking about, Gaffer?!
Take a good look around you, Warlock. We’ve lost too many people.
Exactly! And for what? So you can huddle up in the corner and cry over their sorry fate? We have to rebuild Zalissya for them!
Damn it, Warlock… you’re right. We’ll rebuild Zalissya. And this time, we’ll teach them better. We’ll protect them better.
Thank you, Skif. You’re the only one who came to our aid. No reward can truly express our gratitude, but still…
sid_phrase_E07_SQ01_ales_bavaria_0_Defea: Ouch! Lend me a medkit, guys!
sid_phrase_E07_SQ01_ales_bavaria_0_InfoT: Warlock says Duty is finally doing its job: fighting the worst monsters in the Zone. I tend to agree with him. Mutants are nowhere near as terrifying as those fanatics…
sid_phrase_E07_SQ01_bata_0_HelloTopic_1_: Never trust silence. The Monolithians can remain completely still for hours. You won’t even realize they’re there until it’s too late.
sid_phrase_E07_SQ01_drakula_0_DefeatComm: It’s getting hot over here! I need a medkit!
sid_phrase_E07_SQ01_drakula_0_HelloTopic: You're looking a little pale. So what do you want?
sid_phrase_E07_SQ01_drakula_0_InfoTopic_: I came to the Zone to look for artifacts, but it seems like all I’ve been doing lately is digging graves.
sid_phrase_E07_SQ01_inostranec_bob_0_Inf: After the Signal, Gaffer got a whole lot stricter. He’s pushing us real hard, but we gotta stay tough. What choice do we have? We’ve had to bury so many people already…
sid_phrase_E07_SQ01_moloko_0_InfoTopic_1: I used to take it personally when the Gaffer or Warlock lectured me. I thought I was the smartass. Until I saw a Monolithian’s blank stare for the first time.
Now I’m always gonna listen to what the old-timers have to say — they’ve already defeated that plague once.
sid_phrase_E07_SQ01_rikoset_0_DefeatComm: Hey! Patch me up, would ya?
sid_phrase_E07_SQ01_rikoset_0_InfoTopic_: As I’ve always said, the Ward is a joke. Strutting around in polished boots and pushing newbies around is one thing, but facing real killers is a whole different story.
Have you noticed how all those pompous assholes disappeared the moment the Monolith came back?
sid_phrase_E07_SQ01_slava_farmacevt_0_De: I’m hit! Toss me some meds!
sid_phrase_E07_SQ01_slava_farmacevt_0_In: You learn fast to be cautious around those fanatics. If they get you, it’s game over, man. No bribes, no talking your way out of it.
sid_phrase_E07_SQ01_sovenok_0_InfoTopic_: I haven’t been artifact hunting once. I’m busy doing something way more important right now: helping the Gaffer and the crew rebuild Zalissya. I can always go after artifacts later.
sid_phrase_E07_SQ01_sovenok_0_ResumeComm: Hold on, stalker. Let me finish my thought.
sid_phrase_E08_Bossfight_Faust_Comment_P: Hear the Voice of the Monolith!
sid_phrase_E08_EQ01_Comment_ChernyhFirst: Shhh... Don't scare this beast off.
sid_phrase_E08_EQ01_Comment_NearDuty_flu: Son of a bitch! It ain’t coming out!
Watch out. This one’s a sneaky little prick.
sid_phrase_E08_EQ01_Comment_RomaniukIfKi: Thanks for helping us get rid of that thing, loner. It took down too many of my guys.
At least Somov’s still standing. He’s a rookie — this was his first run.
So much for purging the Zone of mutants… but it’s okay, we’ll manage. Best of luck to you!
sid_phrase_E08_EQ01_Comment_RomaniukIfSh: What the fuck is wrong with you, loner?
sid_phrase_E08_EQ01_Comment_RomaniukIfSo: They’re all gone. My entire squad. All because of a fucking mutant… because of the Zone.
At least we made that mutant pay. Thank you, stalker.
How am I supposed to keep serving now?
sid_phrase_E08_EQ01_Comment_SomovIfRoman: There were four of us in the squad, and now it’s just me. I had a bad feeling before this mission, but how was I supposed to tell the sergeant that?
And now I’ve got nobody to tell nothing…
Thanks for the help. I couldn’t have taken that thing down on my own.
sid_phrase_E08_EQ01_Comment_SomovIfRunAw: Where are you going? Help us!
sid_phrase_E08_EQ01_Comment_SomovSendsSk: Can’t talk right now. Talk to Sarge.
sid_phrase_E08_EQ01_Comment_SomovThanksF: The beast is finally down! Now we can head back to the base with a clear conscience.
Told you if we lured it out, we could handle it.
sid_phrase_E08_EQ01_Comment_SomovWhenCat: Thank you, stalker! When that thing attacked, I thought I was a dead man!
Now we can head back to base. It’s too bad there’s only two of us left…
sid_phrase_E08_EQ01_Dialog_RomaniukAsksT: Hey, stalker. Can you do us a favor? We need your help dealing with a mutant, all right? The damn thing wiped out half my squad. Now it’s hiding and won’t come out.
It’s a crafty little bugger. Probably escaped from some kinda lab. Got a collar around its neck, can you believe it? A metal one. Looks pretty sturdy.
If we take the mutant down, the collar’s all yours. I ain’t claimin' it. You could make a tidy profit selling it to some scientist. So, you in?
You try to flush out the mutant. We got your back.
I’m too busy at the moment.
Guess we’ll have to take care of it on our own. Step aside, then.
Get ready, Somov. On my mark.
sid_phrase_E08_EQ01_radovoj_somov_0_ByeT: Well, break a leg. That's what they say, right?
sid_phrase_E08_EQ01_radovoj_somov_0_Hell: Sure, let’s talk before the mutants show up.
sid_phrase_E08_EQ01_radovoj_somov_0_Info: This thing we do... it ain't what I thought it would be. I’m out there killing mutants, but there’s no thrill in it. Just waiting for my next orders. Where's the fun in that?
sid_phrase_E08_EQ01_serzant_romaniuk_0_C: Hey, loner! Come over here. We need some help.
sid_phrase_E08_EQ01_serzant_romaniuk_0_H: To what do I owe the pleasure?
sid_phrase_E08_EQ01_serzant_romaniuk_0_I: Every critter here requires a unique approach. Some have a great sense of smell; others, acute hearing. And some just have this gut feeling you can’t explain. You gotta develop that sixth sense on your own, I reckon.
sid_phrase_E08_EQ01_serzant_romaniuk_0_R: Let’s wrap this chat up and go about our business.
sid_phrase_E08_EQ03_Comment_HuntersAbout: Those scientists are fucking nuts, man. Putting a collar on a freak like that!
Keep it down. They’re footing the bill.
I’d love to meet the owner of this little pet. He’s gotta be off his damn rocker.
Stay alert. Maybe we’ll get that chance.
sid_phrase_E08_EQ03_Comment_SanchezInter: The Zone has its fair share of weirdness, but mutants wearing collars... that's a new one!
sid_phrase_E08_EQ03_Comment_SanchezMamai: No one’s getting hurt… until Dorozhnyuk catches wind of it!
Catches wind of what, exactly? That I took out a mutant that was getting in the way of their research? And saved on her precious budget to boot?
sid_phrase_E08_EQ03_Comment_SherbaCoordi: Stalker, can you hear me? The coordinates to one of the collars have changed. I’m sending them to you before that thing wanders off again.
sid_phrase_E08_EQ03_Dialog_HuntersTrade_: Looks like you found a collar. I need it.
Turns out you were right, Mamay — that critter does have an owner! He looks kinda normal, though…
Yeah. Listen, stalker... my men and I are under contract to kill a mutant. The job's from Malachite.
We did the job, and that collar’s our ticket to getting paid. Without it, we’re shit out of luck.
Unless you’re willing to pay us more... there are two things I don't like: conflict, and making long trips to see women who are playing hard to get.
No offense, stalker, but we don’t like strangers here.
Give me that collar, or you’ll regret it.
And I was just about to take a break…
I don’t like you poking around here. Have you figured out what you want yet?
All right, I’ll buy it. Even though it’s a fucking rip-off.
And no one’s getting hurt. I’ve come to appreciate that in my time in the Zone.
I don't need anything from you.
I was just passing by. You can keep the collar.
sid_phrase_E08_EQ03_Dialog_TradeAgain_de: Look, why don’t you make up your mind already? You need the collar, or what?
sid_phrase_E08_EQ03_sanchez_0_ByeTopic_1: Watch your back around the scientists. They’re all nuts.
sid_phrase_E08_EQ03_sanchez_0_InfoTopic_: Scientists are the biggest weirdos in the Zone. First they create mutants, one scarier than the next, and now they hire us to kill them. I mean, it’s good for business, sure, but what the actual hell?
sid_phrase_E08_EQ03_sanchez_0_ResumeComm: You wanna talk some more? Sure…
sid_phrase_E08_EQ03_zheka_mamai_0_ByeTop: Not the worst conversation I’ve had in my life.
sid_phrase_E08_EQ03_zheka_mamai_0_Defeat: Fuck! I was already sore all over!
sid_phrase_E08_EQ03_zheka_mamai_0_HelloT: Sure, we can chat. No rush.
sid_phrase_E08_EQ03_zheka_mamai_0_InfoTo: I'm thinking about joining Malachite security when I retire. They’ve got a real samovar there. Plenty of hot tea. And other things, too.
sid_phrase_E08_EQ03_zheka_mamai_0_Resume: All right now. Where were we?
sid_phrase_E08_MQ01_Audiolog_DesatovActi: To all junior research staff: always verify that the emergency safety system is disengaged before activating the equipment!
sid_phrase_E08_MQ01_Audiolog_DesatovSums: My initial hypothesis is confirmed: the psi-influence within the ranges in which we operate at the Center doesn’t entirely cease even after a complete shutdown.
We’ll continue with the experiments on induced illusions, but all participants will receive specialized recovery medications from now on.
Moreover, we’re developing an emergency security system to shut down the station if the psi-influence within its range exceeds acceptable levels.
Professor Razumovsky has been transferred to the Mainland and won’t be participating in further research at this facility. I’m hopeful for his eventual recovery.
sid_phrase_E08_MQ01_Audiolog_DesatovTuni: I can see that everyone here is too busy to read manuals, so listen up! To establish connection with objects in the X-Network you need to follow several steps.
Firstly, connect the station to the power source and ensure access to the X-Network. Secondly, clear the rails of any obstructions to facilitate movement.
Use the main console to initiate and stabilize connection. And closely monitor movement along rails to mitigate potential emergencies.
Once a connection has been established, save the data in the appropriate archive section based on its category. Nothing too complicated!
sid_phrase_E08_MQ01_Audiolog_Razumovskij: We’ve got a real breakthrough here! We've gone from incoherent noises, grainy visuals, and flitting shadows to a repeatable illusion – clear and vivid!
We’ve finally determined the range at which psi-effects can produce distinct induced illusions!
They’re still lacking specific details and features for now, but we can already create projections of both physical objects and images borne from the human subconscious.
I saw Desyatov yesterday. Dead. Ghastly and decomposing — as he walked around the corner. He passed me by, with a blank stare, didn't even look at me.
Then the next morning he strolled past me again, alive and well, and greeted me as if nothing had happened. Not a trace of decay. I checked the Center. It’s powered down. It was off yesterday too.
What does this imply? Do these illusions continue after the VPC is shut down? Have we made them too tangible? Or perhaps the issue lies with me, and I’m seeing things that don’t exist?
sid_phrase_E08_MQ01_Comment_GuardsMeetSp: It’s been a while. Is that new wallpaper? Where’s Ozersky, Sarge?
He did. All right, take it easy. We’ll find the professor ourselves.
sid_phrase_E08_MQ01_Comment_LoudspeakerA: Attention! A malfunction occurred during the launch of the Mirror. Clear the rails of any foreign objects immediately.
Attention! The launch of the Mirror was successful. All research personnel, please proceed to your seats.
sid_phrase_E08_MQ01_Comment_MercenariesF: Raspy, we’re on our way. Gimme a sitrep!
sid_phrase_E08_MQ01_Comment_MercenariesS: Raspy’s gone dark. Do we head in, or wait a little longer?
Just hold on. We’ve got a little time left.
Why does he always want to play the fucking scout? Strelok warned us against going in alone!
Once he gets back, we gotta remind him about how the customer's always right.
sid_phrase_E08_MQ01_Comment_RichterAfter: How was your hunt? Did you get that collar?
I’ve got some business nearby. Swing by my coordinates, and we’ll have a chat.
sid_phrase_E08_MQ01_Comment_RichterGoWit: Well, here we are. And yet nothing’s happened. Nothing at all. Feels kinda… off.
Sorry I can’t join in on the hunt. I’ve got a bad feeling about this, Skif. I’ll end up missing the target and hitting you, or something like that.
I’ll just hang back here. It's better for both of us, really.
sid_phrase_E08_MQ01_Comment_RichterGoodJ: I heard you did it! Drop by, and I’ll tell you where to find Dvupalov.
sid_phrase_E08_MQ01_Comment_RichterInter: You go ahead. I’ll wait here.
sid_phrase_E08_MQ01_Comment_SparkMeetsSc: Howdy, eggheads. Care to tell me where Ozersky is?
Young man, I’d like to ask you…
Nah, don’t bother. Just get Ozersky for us, all right?
Tell him we've got an appointment.
sid_phrase_E08_MQ01_Dialog_OzerskijGives: Fortunately, the X-Network is up and running again. There’s an abandoned Mirror facility nearby. It was used to study induced illusions until 2008.
If you can restart it and reconnect the Mirror to Malachite, I could look for the information you need.
I got them after the Signal. They’re saved on this flash drive.
Oh, that makes it so much easier! However, analyzing these archives will require a considerable amount of time, and I take it you need answers as soon as possible.
Yes?
I want to know more about induced illusions.
I’m looking for information about the phantoms.
A Monolithian called Faust conjured doubles of himself during a fight. I want to know how this is possible.
And I want to never have to hear about this subject again... but I suppose you’re luckier than I am.
The doubles you witnessed are induced illusions caused by psi-influence within the tau range. This Faust fellow appears to be employing older C-Consciousness technology.
C-Consciousness were the ones who extensively researched induced illusions and their impact on humans. Have you heard about the Wish Granter? That’s their handiwork.
I’m not entirely sure we still have any books with enough information here at the Center. The SIRCAA archives would be come in handy here.
Malachite doesn't have any files on this research, but I know of a place where you might be able to find them.
What exactly do I need to do?
Two things: activate the Visual Projection Center and calibrate the communication antenna.
Mind you, configuring the antenna might require specific conditions. The older the equipment, the harder it is to set it up properly.
I advise you to find an operations manual for the facility’s engineering team and follow its guidelines to the letter. Talk to you later.
Where can I meet the C-Consciousness scientists? I need to learn more about their technology.
You’re a few years too late. C-Consciousness ceased to exist in 2012 following the demise of all of its members.
A stalker who goes by Strelok managed to reach the Generators after deactivating the Wish Granter. Then he gunned down C-Consciousness.
Evidently, he didn’t take kindly to being used as a guinea pig.
C-Consciousness? That rings a bell.
It was a faction of idealistic scientists from Project X. They aspired to cleanse the Noosphere of the dark manifestations of human nature. To make the world perfect.
However noble their intentions may have been, their execution was rather poor. In June 2006, a decade after the discovery of the Noosphere, they tried to establish an unauthorized connection to it.
The experiment spiraled out of control, leading to the emergence of the Zone. It now serves as a warning about the perils of impulsive scientific endeavors.
sid_phrase_E08_MQ01_Dialog_OzerskijSends: Thank you for establishing a secure connection. I was able to retrieve the information you requested.
The Controller Project was responsible for the creation of phantoms as part of its research into induced illusions. However, all related data is highly classified and never went public.
Fortunately, we have a list of the scientists involved in the project. If any of them are alive and in the Zone, you may be able to get your information straight from the horse's mouth, as it were.
Do you know anyone on that list?
Regrettably, no. But I know someone who could assist you.
This Richter guy’s all over the place. Seems to know everyone in the Zone. Where do you know him from?
I wouldn’t recommend someone unless I was certain of their competence. Richter knows his way around the Zone. If anyone from this list is still here, he’ll track them down for you.
Richter. He used to be a guide, but now his work mostly revolves around finding people. He’s just the man you need.
If not for him, Scar and I wouldn’t be working together right now. I was interested in meeting him, and Richter arranged it.
He worked with Malachite as a guide for quite a long time. An exceptionally bright young man.
Do you know where can I find Richter?
When he’s not busy with his clients, he usually frequents the 100 Rads Bar in Rostok. Try looking for him there.
You can ask our guide. He’ll help you find your way there.
sid_phrase_E08_MQ01_Dialog_RichterDvupal: Is there anything else I need to know?
Collars aside, Dvupalov’s only been working on one project lately: vodka. Making it and drinking it.
If he offers you a drink, don’t turn it down. Or you won’t get any business done. But don’t try to outdrink him either, or you might find yourself beyond medical help.
Even if I don’t hurt anyone, I’m still a long way from becoming a true guide.
Thanks for the tip. I gotta go.
How does someone become a true guide? What does it take?
The Guide who used to help Strelok shared something with me once.
If you manage to find the rarest artifact, the Heart of Chornobyl, you can learn to understand the Zone as though you were one with it.
Where do I find this Heart of Chornobyl?
The Guide said it’s supposed to be in the Dead Valley. But that’s no place for regular folks. You’d need gear that hasn’t been invented yet — or have the abilities of the Guide himself.
Scar keeps saying only a miracle can help me, but I’m beginning to question whether we’ve got the same kinds of miracles in mind…
Your bad luck seems to be over. Are you gonna guide people again?
We’ll see about that. It’s too risky to rely on a single success.
Are you planning to guide people again, or have you given up on it completely?
I’m not sure, Skif. The risk is too great.
Looks like you made it and got the collar, so you’re good to go to Dvupalov. I’ve tagged the location of his hideout for you. You should be able to get there just fine.
If he asks you for a password, just say “the leash.”
sid_phrase_E08_MQ01_Dialog_RichterReadsL: He doesn't trust or cooperate with anyone. Can you really blame him?
Speaking of which... do you know anyone on this list?
What’s Strelok up to? What’s his plan?
That’s something you gotta ask the man himself. And if he doesn’t trust you enough, you’ll never find out. Strelok is the type who keeps his plans to himself.
I need to find some people. Ozersky mentioned you might be able to help me with that.
You seem pretty popular yourself. Are you some kind of recruiter? Bringing folks to Strider, mingling with scientists, cozying up to Scar…
And you keep popping up wherever I go, too.
You’re making some friends, I see. Ozersky’s a man with a heart of gold – when he’s not being a pain in the ass, anyway.
Then just tell me where to look for those mutants.
I’ll forward you the coordinates of a couple of spots. Choose whichever one you like.
Is this the type of collar Dvupalov collects?
Whoa! You’ve certainly been around the Zone if you've managed to find one. You’re shaping up to be quite the stalker!
Now that you have the collar, you’re good to head to Dvupalov’s hideout. I’ll send you the coordinates. If he asks you for a password, the answer is “leash.”
Remember, if the professor offers you a drink, don’t turn it down. It’ll make the conversation smoother. But don’t try to outdrink him either. You won’t stand a chance.
How can I find this Strelok guy?
Uh, nah. I might’ve said too much already. Either the Zone’s gonna help you, or Strelok will find you himself. But I don't think you two would hit it off right now.
But keep in mind that Dvupalov’s been collecting these collars for a while now. The less dangerous mutants are all gone – only the nasty ones are still out there. So be careful.
Must be the Zone’s will! Nah, there’s plenty of recruiters here without me. By the way, there’s that stalker who showed up after the Signal, Strelok. Ever heard of him? He’s a legend around here!
I hear he’s putting together a new crew. He doesn’t like what’s going on in the Zone, and he wants to shake things up.
I know one of them: Dvupalov. He’s a real grump when he’s sober, which, thankfully, isn’t often. He’s usually holed up in his old hideout and doesn’t entertain visitors.
If you want to talk to him, you’ll need to bring him a collar from a mutant. He used to do some research with the collars, and now he just collects them. Talk about sentimental!
Do you know where I can find mutants with those collars?
Sure. I’ll even share the coordinates.
There are no trustworthy people in the Zone — Strelok’s got that right. Looking for allies is a waste of time.
As long as you and Scar keep looking for the Shining Zone, you’d better stay away from Strelok.
Remember when you led me out of those anomalies when we first met? You saved my life that time. Let’s try it again.
I… hadn’t looked at it that way before.
All right. Let’s give it a shot. I just hope neither of us regrets it.
Can you take me to a place where I can hunt some mutants in collars?
You know I don’t guide anymore – not after I got Joker and Barge killed. I don’t want anyone else to trust me and end up getting hurt.
He wouldn’t appreciate you being chummy with the Ward and trying to make the Zone disappear.
What happened to his old crew?
I could tell you they found lots of rare artifacts, left for the Mainland, and lived happily ever after. But in reality, not everyone made it back from their trip to the Monolith.
I’ve found folks in the Zone I can trust. Strelok just needs to try harder.
You're asking the wrong guy. I’ve found folks I can team up with. I wouldn’t last long without them.
sid_phrase_E08_MQ01_marat_vitaz_0_InfoTo: Maybe the mutants are so angry because we won't leave them alone.
sid_phrase_E08_MQ01_marat_vitaz_0_Resume: What were we talking about again?
sid_phrase_E08_MQ01_nikita_uragan_0_Busy: I’ll talk to you later, okay?
sid_phrase_E08_MQ01_nikita_uragan_0_Defe: I need a hand here! Gimme a medkit!
sid_phrase_E08_MQ01_nikita_uragan_0_Hell: Well, who do we have here?
sid_phrase_E08_MQ01_nikita_uragan_0_Info: Heard there was some trouble at SIRCAA. Looks like the Zone has forced those eggheads out of their ivory tower.
sid_phrase_E08_MQ01_nikita_uragan_0_Resu: I lost my train of thought there.
sid_phrase_E08_MQ01_professor_sulgin_0_D: No! It can't be happening! Help!
sid_phrase_E08_MQ01_professor_sulgin_0_I: Some guests at our Center could do with a bit more courtesy. And Professor Ozersky should be more careful about who he invites to Malachite.
sid_phrase_E08_MQ01_professor_sulgin_0_R: I lost my train of thought there.
sid_phrase_E08_MQ01_serzant_svak_0_InfoT: This Center is turning into a flea market. Those scientists gotta make up their mind whether they want security or if they’re cool with every artifact-toting creep strolling in.
sid_phrase_E08_MQ03_Audilolog_ChubkoAbou: Have you seen the reports? Faust is a work of art! It’s a shame Dvupalov won’t get to appreciate it.
From what I’ve observed, he’s more than a work of art. He hasn't ceased to amaze us since he was connected to the Monolith network.
Faust opts to encourage the fighters, to inspire them, rather than relying on his Controller abilities.
Yes, you make a good point. Remind me... where are the blueprints for the Suppressor?
He’s not just a soldier, he’s a general!
sid_phrase_E08_MQ03_Audilolog_DvupalovTe: You’re having trouble sleeping too, I see. Let’s get started early today.
We’re going to explore how your brain responds to metaphors and imagery. Empathy and abstract thinking — those are the traits that separate us from simple beasts.
And they're what separates you from the other mutants... in theory, at least.
I rummaged through my library and came across something that suits our circumstances perfectly. I hope you won’t mind a little poetry.
Not backwards, forwards is the spirit’s sight; This moment now, alone — is our delight.
sid_phrase_E08_MQ03_AudiologWarning_audi: Any documents in the control room should be off limits to lab assistants.
Last week someone took the Suppressor manual yet again, jeopardizing a scheduled experiment!
This is not the first case of negligent handling of crucial Project X documentation. This matter needs to be addressed at once.
sid_phrase_E08_MQ03_Audiolog_MutantsHowl: I don’t understand. I need a visual examination.
No time for that. I have to figure out what went wrong. Strap him down. Scalpel, quick.
Nothing. Once again, nothing. All right. Get someone to clean up this mess.
sid_phrase_E08_MQ03_Audiolog_StrelokX5_1: My head… fuck. Can’t let history repeat itself.
You’re Strelok. You used to work at SIRCAA, but you quit and tried to sabotage X11. You failed.
Escape to the Mainland. Go into hiding.
Then come back and make sure those bastards can’t complete Project X.
sid_phrase_E08_MQ03_Comment_ConnectionFa: Attention, all staff! Communication with C-Consciousness has been interrupted.
System error. Forced reboot in thirty seconds.
sid_phrase_E08_MQ03_Comment_DvupalovSher: Son of a bitch! Where did you find that key?! Can’t you just move on? When are you going to let it go, huh?
I was worried! Haven't you been following the news? The Zone’s going to hell!
All news has been the same since the Garden of Eden! After that, it’s just humanity repeating itself… hic!
You’re sloshed again, Professor. What about science?
It's pointless! Ever since they invented alcohol distillation.
Quit bugging me, you mopey oaf!
sid_phrase_E08_MQ03_Comment_Dvupalov_Aft: I helped you find the Suppressor. What more do you want? Let a man enjoy his oblivion in peace.
Off you go, soldier. You’ve got your own little war to fight.
sid_phrase_E08_MQ03_Comment_Dvupalov_Art: What are you waiting for? We’ve got an artifact on the run!
sid_phrase_E08_MQ03_Comment_Dvupalov_BeC: Ah, good! There it is, my blissful point of extremum! Here comes oblivion!
You whippersnappers should stay away from all this. Nothing good ever comes of it... hic! And never will.
sid_phrase_E08_MQ03_Comment_Dvupalov_Cat: The artifact just popped out of the still! Grab it, stalker!
Fuck! Where are you off to?!
sid_phrase_E08_MQ03_Comment_Dvupalov_Dru: Piss off, Sherpa… to the Red Forest with ya…
sid_phrase_E08_MQ03_Comment_Dvupalov_Foo: What the hell are you doing? No snacks till the third shot! You’ll ruin the experience.
sid_phrase_E08_MQ03_Comment_Dvupalov_Fou: It’s an elixir you’ll either never forget… or remember!
sid_phrase_E08_MQ03_Comment_Dvupalov_Giv: If you only got one, don’t expect a big payout.
sid_phrase_E08_MQ03_Comment_Dvupalov_Hur: Where are your manners? Where do you think you’re going, stalker?
sid_phrase_E08_MQ03_Comment_Dvupalov_Int: I’m… at the point of… excremum.
Hold on. Lemme give you the key to X5 first.
sid_phrase_E08_MQ03_Comment_Dvupalov_Map: The collars were a crude instrument. The Suppressors, on the other hand… they were designed to… hic! dampen abilities of a finer nature.
Now, where did I put it…
No, that’s just some junk… Ah, look! A pickle! I’ll save it for later.
sid_phrase_E08_MQ03_Comment_Dvupalov_NoV: You couldn’t find it? Bottles tend to vanish into thin air around here. Must be yet another one of the Zone’s little wonders. Come on, take a seat. I’ve got a spare one.
sid_phrase_E08_MQ03_Comment_Dvupalov_Wai: I’m starting to sober up, and when I’m sober, I tend to do things I regret later, might just as well tell you to fuck off. Go get that vodka, stalker, and then we can chat.
sid_phrase_E08_MQ03_Comment_Sherba_Found: Thanks for those papers! They’re a treasure worth all the years I spent at Malachite. We’ll stay in touch. I’ve got grand plans for the future!
sid_phrase_E08_MQ03_Comment_Sherba_NoDoc: The only thing I can do now is to keep pestering Dvupalov, hoping that one day he’ll snap out of it.
sid_phrase_E08_MQ03_Comment_SkifCaughtAr: Whoa, you’re light on your feet, stalker. You can hang on to this one. You’ve earned it! You went after that artifact like a bullet from a gun!
Seems like I gave the key card for X5 to Shcherba after all. That’s why I hate teetotalers! The bastard must’ve caught me in a good mood…
Anyway, if you want the Suppressor, you need to talk to that boring grad student yourself. He’s over in Ahroprom, staying close to X5. And now for the fourth shot – the happiest of them all!
sid_phrase_E08_MQ03_Dialog_DvupalovSherb: The stalker! He’s back! Those papers I mentioned… Do you have them?
Where are your manners, young man?! You won’t even offer a man a seat and pour him a cold one?
There’s nothing exciting in there anyhow. I’ve told you before, young man – if even my own work can end in failure–
Faust wasn’t exactly… a failure. In a way.
Haven’t you heard? He tapped into the X-Network, brought the Monolith back, and caused a bloodbath all over the Zone.
So he’s… your project?! The abilities you incor—
Shut up!
I knew I was right all along. So that bunch of old farts in pods haven’t given up on my research. Hooking him up to the Monolith was a stroke of genius…
And I told them that the only way to create a true Controller is to preserve his capacity to think, to feel, and make decisions!
Sure, right now the choices he’s making don’t align with what the Ward, the MDST, or anyone else had in mind… but I did succeed!
You’re as much of a fool as I am if you think you can set things right. The Controller Project was abandoned, and yet Faust endures. Monolith disappeared, and now it has returned.
Go ahead and kill Faust, stalker. It won’t change a thing. You'll see.
Here. I don’t want to be anywhere near these things.
This is… Yes, this is it! At last!
Who is Faust now? What have you turned him into?
We granted him the abilities of a Controller mutant, but I’m convinced that Faust has retained his humanity.
See, there’s no more Project X, no C-Consciousness, no MDST to protect. He wasn’t being directed by a program; he initiated this of his own free will.
I believe the Monolith became an idea to him. Give a man an idea he can grasp, and he’ll do much more than he would ever do coerced. I’ve always said that it works better than any leash.
I found the Suppressor. Can you install it?
Oh, right. I almost forgot the reason you’re here.
All the work I did... It’s all become meaningless. You’re going to kill Faust.
The MDST? What’s that?
The scientists from Project X, who, unlike me, were eager to continue their research even after it was officially shut down. It appears they were the ones who made the Controller Project a reality.
Officially, they had no funding or approval to proceed. However, C-Consciousness wasn’t content with merely creating the Zone; they were determined to safeguard their pitiful existence.
That’s why they needed the Monolith – and, later on, Faust and his abilities.
I couldn’t find them.
I gave you the only key card, and you squandered this treasure so wantonly!
A lot of people died because of him.
Were they good people? Well, then find other good people to install the Suppressor. I’m not a technician.
A lot of good soldiers died because of him.
Soldiers killing other soldiers... It’s a tale as old as time. Why should I care? I’ve provided you with a weapon to use against Faust – it’s up to you to find someone to install it.
Why did you create Faust?
The mutants turned out to be far too unpredictable, useless, and downright dangerous. The aim was to mold them into obedient soldiers.
The Leash had some success with more primitive tools, but I believed we were on the wrong path. The error, I thought, lay in the very design of the Controllers.
Without consciousness and human empathy, their psi-abilities were pitiful. Faust demonstrated this vividly. But masterful tools necessitate much more precise fine-tuning.
sid_phrase_E08_MQ03_Dialog_Dvupalov_Deli: Ouch. Ouch!
Ouch. Not even a flinch, huh? That sort of bravado isn’t gonna impress me. When you’re drinking, you’ve gotta put your heart into it.
So, where were we? How about another one?
So why did you collect those collars, anyway?
They’re the only thing I have left to remind me of all my futile scientific endeavors.
Sentimentality… a quirk of human nature. Who can really say? Anyway, here’s to leaving sentimentality behind.
I’m not interested in vodka.
A man with no interest in vodka is a man with no interest in life itself. What motivates him then? How can you trust him? And, most importantly, why share a drink with him?
All right. Here’s to our partnership.
Let’s toss another one back.
You’re definitely not a grad student. That’s a good thing.
Okay. Bottoms up, Professor.
I see you’re a man of ambition. Great aspirations, I’d even say. Outdrinking me, for one!
Let’s have another one, sure.
To abstract thinking!
All right, but this is the last one.
Bold words from a young man like you.
Here’s to science making things better, not worse.
Here, here!

That’s what I like about you. Maybe I’d like Shcherba too if he could drink like a man. But he just comes in, asks questions, and fumbles around. What am I supposed to talk to someone like that about?
Let’s take you, for instance. What do you want to talk about?
Look, about those collars… Leave them out next time. The shop’s closed. What’s the point of stocking up on collars if I don’t have a leash?
It was a promising project, sure… but it’s time to bid it farewell. Bottoms up, stalker.
How do you destroy phantoms and defeat someone who can create induced illusions?
Seeing double, eh? You probably just had some bad vodka. Try some of mine – it’s good for more than just keeping radiation at bay.
When I saw the man who created those phantoms, I was stone-cold sober.
Ha ha ha! And I was stone-cold sober when I saw evidence that it was impossible. But who am I to forbid you from living in a world of illusions?
If you’re really so eager, drag your ass to X5. That’s where the whole three-ring circus used to be. Try to find the blueprint for the Suppressor – it ought to help you.
I’m all out of vodka anyway. Come on, I’ll give you the key card for the lab.
You used to work with phantoms. Tell me about the ability to create them.
I used to work on lots of things! Including that one, yeah.
What can I say? It didn’t pan out. Turns out, not a single subject was capable of controlling even a paltry ability like this.
I’m interested in a different project: the Controller. You were part of it.
The last thing I need is another Shcherba pestering me! That grad student is obsessed with the subject. The idea of blissful ignorance is utterly beyond him.
That means I could use another drink. After the third shot of my elixir, even the most hopeless souls can start to grasp the basics of abstract thinking.
Then we can talk for real. So, what do you say? You down for a few more drinks, or maybe just one for the road?
Skip the vodka. I want to talk about the Controller Project. Your name’s on the staff list.
You, too? Was it that… Shill? Schmuck? Sherpa? Whatever his name is… Did he send you? He’s a real pain in the ass – bought a bottle of vodka and didn’t even touch it.
As for my biggest project – it’s right here in this glass.
Bottoms up, stalker. To science, to partnership, to whatever floats your boat.
What project are you talking about?
I told ya – Project Leash! The mutant control project. They cooked up all sorts of freaks, then tried to make gadgets to boss them around – and zap them remotely if things get out of hand.
As you know, they fucked up the later part. When the project got shelved, those collared mutants were supposed to die peacefully in their cages, not run amok in the Zone spooking stalkers.
So much for a promising project. But I suppose promises are made to be broken. How about a drink to that, stalker?
You’re the first Project X scientist I’ve met who’s still alive. I’m curious to hear about it firsthand.
Believe me, those men of great learning would rather not have my tongue tarnish their legacy, so I’m more than willing to do exactly that!
Here’s the scoop: Project X was a bunch of charlatans, and I was the most shameless one of them all. How’s that for a revelation?
If man is the measure of all things, then the measure of a man is three shots of this magic vodka. So go ahead, ask your questions while I’m feeling charitable.
All right. Let’s sample the goods.
Let’s have one, and then we can talk about the what, the where, and the how much.
Here’s to the start of our partnership!
What was the purpose of the Controller Project?
Producing one failure after another. No, really, the project was meant to put the abilities of mutants to good use.
But can we truly blame mindless creatures for their actions?
Those in charge should’ve known right from the start that nothing could be changed. When you try to fix someone else’s mistakes, you only end up making new ones.
sid_phrase_E08_MQ03_Dialog_Dvupalov_Firs: Hand it over. You’re new around here, aren’t you? Or have I just forgotten you?
I had an assistant I’ll never forget, Nightingale. He was a delight to work with and a great drinking buddy. It's a shame he’s gone. Think you can fill his shoes?
I wasn’t planning to. I’m here about—
I know what you're here about. The first one’s on the house, but we’ll enjoy it right here on the spot. You can fetch the bottle from my lab.
sid_phrase_E08_MQ03_Dialog_HimzavodTechn: I was informed that Korshunov gave the go-ahead. Come on quickly.
What is it, stalker? I’ve got enough on my plate as it is.
I need a special upgrade for my suit.
Can’t you see what’s going on? I won't lift a finger without Korshunov’s say-so. He’s in the infirmary at the moment. Maybe you can work something out with him.
sid_phrase_E08_MQ03_Dialog_IvaylovIntera: We’ve got more than enough problems to deal with, so I won’t be helping a stalker without direct approval from Colonel Korshunov.
sid_phrase_E08_MQ03_Dialog_KorshunovInHo: Why? Please, don’t! What’s going on? Tell me!
Shut up. For once in your damn life, just shut the fuck up. Class 5 psi-protection, you little shit? My soldiers’ blood is on your hands!
I… I had no idea. I swear, Colonel, if…
Shut. Your. Mouth. What've you got, Skif?
Ozersky wanted me to get the Mirror facility online. It could be important.
I need to see Ozersky, Colonel. I have to talk to him in person. You can trust…
You’ll go with my men. Now get out of here.
I know how to take Faust down. But I’ll need some help from a tech.
You’ll have all the help and equipment you need. Get it done, stalker. I know you can.
What’s the deal with the Promin factory?
We’ve found a depleted Emitter, Colonel.
Get to Malachite on the double. Find out what’s going on.
sid_phrase_E08_MQ03_Dialog_KorshunovInte: How many more men do I have to lose while we’re here shooting the shit?
sid_phrase_E08_MQ03_Dialog_KrivenkoAbout: The colonel could use some rest. Let’s not disturb him.
What’s the deal with the colonel’s psi-protection?
To put it simply, it’s an immunity to any kind of psi-exposure. That means that the colonel can withstand the direct effects of an Emission for a brief period even without protection.
How did you come up with class 5 psi-protection?
I suppose there’s no point in hiding it now. In the psi-medicine lab, we routinely detoxified the soldiers to counteract the Zone’s harmful influences, such as radiation and chemical poisoning.
At the same time, we assessed their thresholds of tolerance to directed psi-radiation, gradually increasing the exposure.
Regrettably, not all soldiers could tolerate even minor doses well enough. In fact, only Colonel Korshunov was able to adapt to it.
How bad is the colonel’s injury?
His spine has sustained significant damage. The chances of him walking again are pretty slim, but we’re utilizing all the Institute’s know-how to aid him.
sid_phrase_E08_MQ03_Dialog_Sherba_AboutX: Hand me that key card, and I’ll see what I can do about your papers.
All right. It’s not like I’ve got much of a choice.
But keep those papers in mind, stalker. You’ll need to head all the way down, but if you’re willing to do the job, I’ll make it worth your while.
I’ll go check on the professor in the meantime. Seems like every time I look away, he’s got a new drinking buddy.
Why do you want those papers?
I tried to convince Professor Dvupalov that I’m a worthy successor to his great work, but all I received from him was a couple of his drafts. One of which had been used as… a food wrapper.
I’m talking about research that could etch our names into history! Since the professor doesn’t seem to recognize it, I’ll take it upon myself to finish the job for him.
Why do you even bother helping Dvupalov?
How can I stand by and watch a scientist of his caliber kill the few brain cells he has left?!
He has invaluable knowledge and skills – the kind that are not taught at Malachite. Especially nowadays…
The professor has no right to go down this path!
Tell me about Dvupalov. So he’s a great scientist?
Professor Dvupalov is a brilliant mind hailing from the Project X era, the mastermind behind the Controller Project. He used to bring science to new heights!
Now the only thing he can bring is a glass toward his lips. He’s disenchanted with his life’s work, you see. Nothing ever changes!
If only the professor had taken a moment to peruse my notes instead of selling vodka…
Dvupalov sent me to get the key card for X5. I’m looking for the Phantom Suppressor.
It took Dvupalov a whole six months just to remember my name while I served him food every day, and now he tells you about the card right away? Did you drink with him or something?!
It’s tough to avoid having a drink with him.
That stuff is pure poison!

All right, I’ll give you the card. It’s too risky for me in there anyway. But you’ve got to promise me that you’ll search X5 for any papers related to the Controller Project and bring them back to me.
Tell me what you know about the Controller Project.
The Controller Project did involve some research on phantoms, but its main objective was to develop specimens modified with both biological and technological enhancements.
Unlike their mutant predecessors, which were impossible to keep in their cages, these were meant to be trained as soldiers to protect Project X personnel.
Can you imagine? Not even SIRCAA is attempting anything like that nowadays. They haven't even managed to create a single new specimen.
I ask you to vacate the premises! I’m occupying it, and… there’s nothing here to take.
Calm down, Shcherba. Dvupalov sent me.
Is the professor doing okay? He didn’t succumb to intoxication from that poison of his, did he? Its alcohol by volume is nearing pure ethanol, not to mention the anomalous component.
What a fall from grace… A great scientist, resorting to making vodka!
Since I'm headed to X5, tell me about the lab.
Until recently, no one even knew it existed. The research there was classified, and the lab had been mothballed for quite some time.
Then Duty did some cleanup with heavy explosives there and found hidden levels beneath Ahroprom.
I used certain informal channels to reach out to the professor. That’s how I found out it was an old mutant research lab. I can’t even begin to fathom what’s going on in there right now.
sid_phrase_E08_MQ03_Dialog_Sherba_AgainA: You haven’t gone to X5 yet? Don’t forget to look for the Controller Project papers while you are there.
If you’ve got any questions, now’s the time to ask. I need to check on Professor Dvupalov.
sid_phrase_E08_MQ03_Dialog_SkifBroughtLe: Come on in. And don’t drag your feet.
sid_phrase_E08_MQ03_SherbaGoAway_Leave_5: I’ve already handed over the key card. Could you please leave now?
sid_phrase_E08_MQ03_professor_dvupalov_0: Stop by again, we’ll have another.
I don’t bother with the news, and I’d suggest you do the same. It'll turn your brain to mush... and I say that as a scientist.
All right. What were we talking about?
sid_phrase_E08_MQ04_CommentBye_bye_FF_58: Make sure to come back. We’re counting on you.
sid_phrase_E08_MQ04_Comment_Alfa1Interac: Thanks for your help, stalker. We’ve got too many wounded, can’t chat right now.
sid_phrase_E08_MQ04_Comment_DeltaMoveToB: Bravo, this is Delta. First batch is ready to go. We’re heading to the Duga.
Roger that, Delta. We’ve got your six.
sid_phrase_E08_MQ04_Comment_DvoriankovIs: Major Dvoryankov in command now. All units, report!
Charlie Team cleared the barracks. Engaging at the Lesser Duga checkpoint.
sid_phrase_E08_MQ04_Comment_InTheBeginni: Put the gun down already, you’ve been cleaning it for an hour. Wanna hear something funny? Two Monolithians walk into a bar…
Get lost before I shove this gun up your ass.
Calm down. We didn’t know what we were up against last time, but now we do. We’ve got this.
sid_phrase_E08_MQ04_Comment_KPPAboutWin_: Well done! Get the wounded checked out.
sid_phrase_E08_MQ04_Comment_LastWords_by: Soldiers! It’s been an honor fighting alongside you.
sid_phrase_E08_MQ04_Comment_LastWords_la: Alpha, get to cover! I’m going to set it off!
sid_phrase_E08_MQ04_Comment_LiterEDeltaB: Bravo, this is Delta. I can’t hear you! Say again!
They spotted us! We’ve got contact!
sid_phrase_E08_MQ04_Comment_MonolitSpeec: All forces, hold the Duga. We cannot allow the infidels to interfere.
sid_phrase_E08_MQ04_Comment_NearDugaChar: Make it fast! We can’t hold on much—
sid_phrase_E08_MQ04_Comment_NearDugaDelt: Delta, Charlie’s in position. We’re pinning them down!
sid_phrase_E08_MQ04_Comment_NearKPPStart: Alpha Team, the stalker is on approach.
sid_phrase_E08_MQ04_Comment_WhatAHell_wh: What are you waiting for, Skif? Get to the checkpoint and into the tunnel, now!
sid_phrase_E08_MQ04_Comment_ZotovAboutSk: Alpha Team, get ready to attack the checkpoint. Proceed as planned.
sid_phrase_E08_MQ04_Comment_ZotovCallAft: Great job, Skif! Let’s keep it up.
There’s a tunnel near the checkpoint. Find it and get inside.
sid_phrase_E08_MQ04_Comment_ZotovIfRunWr: Turn around, stalker! The tunnel’s the other way!
sid_phrase_E08_MQ04_Comment_Zotov_Call_c: Skif, Zotov here. I'm told you're in posession of a device that can protect from Faust's influence. We must act fast.
We're getting ready to take the Duga under the IPSF's control and get rid of those fanatics once and for all. Your help can increase our chances.
Our forward command post is close to the Duga. Sending over the coordinates. You'll get the details once there.
sid_phrase_E08_MQ04_Dialog_CallVarta_hi_: The guys wanted to thank you, Skif. We couldn’t have made it without your help.
Oleksii gave his life for a better future. His death wasn’t in vain.
sid_phrase_E08_MQ04_Dialog_StartQuest_Ne: Finding the base was definitely no problem.
Failure isn’t an option. We had to mobilize all of our forces. Had to pull Dvoryankov from the Chemical Plant!
We’ve got a lot to do. If you need some time to prepare, let me know. If you’re ready, I’ll bring you up to speed.
sid_phrase_E08_MQ04_Dialog_StartQuest_an: I want to know more about your plan for the attack.
Each team has a specific objective. Alpha Team will start by clearing the checkpoint. Bravo and Charlie will create diversions to distract the enemy.
Meanwhile, Delta Team will plant the explosives to deal with the Duga.
Our main forces will secure and infiltrate the facility and sweep it.
sid_phrase_E08_MQ04_Dialog_StartQuest_he: I’m glad you’re here. Ready for the brief?
sid_phrase_E08_MQ04_Dialog_StartQuest_ho: Just some standard C4 with a remote detonator. Your job is to plant it at the exact coordinates we give you, then take cover.
sid_phrase_E08_MQ04_Dialog_StartQuest_no: I need some time to gear up.
Of course. But make sure you’re here when it’s time to put your skills to use.
And keep your head down. Got snipers out there.
Our objective is the Duga. Judging by our findings at Promin, Faust can activate it – even remotely. We need to destroy this contraption before it scorches the brains of everyone in the Zone.
You’ll take the underground route. Almost zero chance of running into Monolithians there. They know our troops wouldn’t risk going through mutants and anomalies.
Get to the Lesser Duga and plant the explosive device. My men will do the same at the other side of the facility. Any questions?
sid_phrase_E08_MQ04_Dialog_StartQuest_ok: Excellent. Head over to the checkpoint and find the tunnel. I’ll guide you through it over the radio, so stay in touch.
As for Faust, I'll let you know when we track him. We'll take it from there.
Best of luck, Skif. I’m counting on you.
sid_phrase_E08_MQ04_Dialog_ZotovCallAbou: Near the exit, I think. Which way is it?
Through the engineering hallway. The IPSF reports say there's a way through mark “D.”
All right. I’ll look for it.
sid_phrase_E08_MQ04_Dialog_ZotovCallBefo: Stick to the plan. Set them up and find cover.
sid_phrase_E08_MQ04_Dialog_ZotovCallNear: Are you close to the Duga, Skif?
