sid_phrase_E08_MQ04_Dialog_ZovovCallAfte: I think the explosion was only on my end. Why didn’t it go off outside?
I’ve got a new mission for you: find Faust. There’s a group of Monolithians on the roof of the Comm Center. Check it out.
I’ll handle the second batch. Load it up.
sid_phrase_E08_MQ04_korcnoj_0_InfoTopic_: I wish I could tell my men, “It’s okay, we’ve been through shit worse than this.” But damn, this is a whole new level of shit we’ve stepped in.
sid_phrase_E08_MQ04_soldat_litvinuk_0_In: What, this isn't new enough for ya? I’ve never seen this kind of shitfuckery before!
sid_phrase_E08_MQ04_soldat_sorokin_0_Inf: Wherever the Monolith takes them, it’s a one-way ticket.
sid_phrase_E08_MQ05_Audiolog_Faust4_Find: Strider, find the Doctor from Strelok’s group. He has to die before he assists the enemies of the Monolith with their schemes.
sid_phrase_E08_MQ05_Audiolog_FaustGerman: You were going to name your terms, scientist.
I know, okay? Your request is causing me a lot of problems here. You’re not getting out of it easy.
Next time I call, your men need to quickly collect a certain item from a stalker.
If you do it, you’ll get remote access to the Monolith database.
Remember, Faust, you don’t have much choice. No one’s going to work with you. No one wants to get involved in what you’re after.
I remember. It will be taken care of.
Faust, come in. The stalker’s heading for the Old Bridge. Send your men, quick!
He can't be left alive, you understand? Get the Scanner from him and stay out of sight. I’m risking my ass here.
We have the Scanner. Now give us the access, scientist.
About time! Here, that should do the trick. Now take the Scanner over here, and I’ll…
That wasn’t part of the deal. If you want the Scanner, come to the old Clear Sky base.
What the hell? You think you’re clever, do you?!
Remember, Hermann — you don’t have much choice.
sid_phrase_E08_MQ05_Comment_AfterFaustDe: Skif! They’re scattering! We’re pushing them north! What about Faust?
He did it, he blew up the Duga! But he didn’t make it. We’ve lost so many…
It doesn’t matter. Let’s not waste our time mourning. Star wouldn’t want that. He believed in his cause and was willing to do whatever it took.
Besides, he’s a legend now. Not many guys in the Zone go out with a bang like that!
We've still got work to do here. Need to transport the wounded and dead. As for you, return to Malachite. They’re waiting for you.
sid_phrase_E08_MQ05_Comment_AssaultRadio: Star, we’ve got a squad coming in from the northwest. They’re skirting around the checkpoint.
Good. Lead them away from the rest, as far as you can.
Make it quick! We’re getting antsy waiting for them here.
Are you there, stalker? Now you gotta activate all the transformers.
That should overload the grid and temporarily shut down the Duga.
Not yet, Skif! Initiate the transformer overload.
We can’t keep them distracted for much longer.
We’re in position, boss. The garage, 20 to the north. We just spotted a scouting party.
Hold them off, no matter what! Don’t let them catch sight of China!
Copy that, boss.
We welcomed our guests. Took some casualties.
Got it. Mugwort, what about you?
What do you think?! Fuck! Where’s that gunfire coming from?
(screaming)
Got the shooter. Third window, right above you.
Fuck yeah! Comin’ in!
All clear.
Hold on! Another one. Tenth window, one floor down! Can you hear…
Shit, Mugwort’s hit!
Boss, we’ve cleared the garage. Heading north now.
What about ammo?
Running low. Almost out of the grenades we took from the Ward.
I know! These assholes are fucking hard to take down! Should’ve packed a flamethrower.
I’m sending another squad to you. We’re moving to the Duga. Let’s get this done!
Damn it, Cleric! Too soon!
sid_phrase_E08_MQ05_Comment_AzbukaMonoli: Our brothers did not return. The infidels are up to something. Stay alert.
sid_phrase_E08_MQ05_Comment_FaustComm_1_: Come to me. I'll show you the truth.
sid_phrase_E08_MQ05_Comment_InteractCajn: If you think about smoking in here, I swear I’ll shoot you before this place goes up in flames.
sid_phrase_E08_MQ05_Comment_InteractKler: Hey, loner! You’re in! I hear you’re quite the fighter, just like my boys. I’d love to see you in action!
sid_phrase_E08_MQ05_Comment_MachineSpeak: Enemies at the checkpoint. Find and eliminate the threat.
sid_phrase_E08_MQ05_Comment_MartOnIntera: You weren’t followed, were you? We’re already short on time.
sid_phrase_E08_MQ05_Comment_MonolithAtta: He is here! The enemy of the Monolith shall die!
sid_phrase_E08_MQ05_Comment_MonolithDete: The infidels have destroyed the Duga. Death to the enemies of the Monolith!
sid_phrase_E08_MQ05_Comment_PolynAfterMe: We’ll circle around from the northwest. They might be good at stealth, but my men are even better.
Okay. Keep your heads down until the stalker does his thing. Star’s counting on you.
sid_phrase_E08_MQ05_Comment_PolynOnInter: Star’s in charge of the operation. He’s the man to talk to. I need to concentrate.
sid_phrase_E08_MQ05_Comment_PolynWarnsOf: Look how… went flying! We’re ready.
sid_phrase_E08_MQ05_Comment_Radio_StarSu: Fuck, Star! There’s a lot of them right under the Duga. They got a Gauss gun! We’re outnumbered. Five men down.
Stay low. Give us your coordinates. We’re moving in.
sid_phrase_E08_MQ05_Comment_Radio_TNTOnl: Hide the explosives and get outta there. Quick! This is your only chance. I’ve got a backup plan!
sid_phrase_E08_MQ05_Comment_Spark_Surpri: I told you not to smoke in here. Go outside. Not in the building!
Are you trying to ruin my surprise for those fanatics?
sid_phrase_E08_MQ05_Comment_StarBeforeTu: So, what’s the hold-up? We’re good to go. Move it!
sid_phrase_E08_MQ05_Comment_StarCallsAbo: They took the bait! Four large units are heading to the explosion site. We’re falling back.
The path is clear. This is your moment, Skif. The rest of you, let’s keep the fanatics running in circles!
sid_phrase_E08_MQ05_Comment_StarCallsSki: Skif, come in. How’s Scar’s request going? Is there a way to deal with the phantoms of our mad Monolith preacher?
Wow, good news for once! Can’t say the same about the rest. Faust is going to turn everyone in the Zone into fanatics. And the worst is that he’s got a pretty good chance.
But we have a plan. Head over to the Duga. I’ll tell you all about it.
I’ve got the Suppressor. It should shield me from Faust’s influence.
sid_phrase_E08_MQ05_Comment_StarCalls_Ra: Damn, we've got everything ready. How come you're not there?
What’s taking you so long? Hurry up! Move, move!
sid_phrase_E08_MQ05_Comment_StarConfirms: You fucking did it, Skif! The Duga’s not gonna pose a threat for a while.
And now these assholes have no idea where we’re gonna hit ’em! It’s time to move on to the grand finale.
So, you were after Faust, right? There’s a gathering on the roof of the Comm Center, the Duga command post. It seems like the preacher is about to come out and address his flock.
Go finish him off. We’ve got your back. And if you hear an explosion, take a look around – it’s not every day we make the Duga sparkle!
sid_phrase_E08_MQ05_Comment_StarOnKillin: Looks like the fun part’s about to begin!
sid_phrase_E08_MQ05_Comment_StarOnTheFut: You know, Skif, I’ve got this gut feeling – when shit’s about to hit the fan, I can sense it coming a mile away. But if there’s good stuff around the corner, I get pumped up in advance.
And this time, my gut’s telling me it’s gonna be smooth as silk. Just like it’s meant to be. You’ll see.
Who knows? Maybe we’ll see the Shining Zone sooner than we expect.
sid_phrase_E08_MQ05_Comment_StarPolyn_Fi: I’m shooting! Hang in there, stalker!
sid_phrase_E08_MQ05_Comment_StarSaysBye_: Hurry back! The sooner we attack, the quicker we can get to Faust.
sid_phrase_E08_MQ05_Comment_Star_IfSkisM: I hope you’re still alive, stalker. We’re sticking to the plan: get to the transformer room and shut down the Duga.
sid_phrase_E08_MQ05_Comment_Star_LastOne: We made it through! I even snagged a Gauss gun, ha!
Everyone, get ready! Moving on to the last phase of the plan.
sid_phrase_E08_MQ05_Comment_Star_LightOn: Skif, do you read me? I sure hope so. It’s gonna be a shame if you don’t.
If my timing's right, you must be heading towards where my men fixed up the lights. Pretty cool, ha? Now get to the radio room so we can have a proper chat.
sid_phrase_E08_MQ05_Comment_TinyFluff1_A: Ambush on the roof! We need backup, 150 to the—
sid_phrase_E08_MQ05_Comment_TinyFluff2_S: They went around the tripwires! They’ve got some weapons, can’t tell what it is!
Don’t even think about engaging head-on! Let the sniper handle it.
sid_phrase_E08_MQ05_Comment_TinyFluff3_M: I remember now… Mykola… Soborna Street… apartment…
sid_phrase_E08_MQ05_Dialog_StarOnTheTask: Why don’t we just head back to Promin and shut down the Duga?
We’ve tracked synchronizing impulses. It means that Faust’s followers obtained control codes from the Emitter and can activate the Duga whenever they want.
Basically, we’ve lost remote access. The only way is to turn it off manually. And I know how.
Any complications I should be aware of?
The sewers ain’t exactly a walk in the park – they’re crawling with mutants. But I’d rather take on critters than Gauss snipers any day. You’ll manage.
Shutting down the Duga might make a little noise. So be ready for some armed guests to crash your little party at the substation. Stay on your toes.
I was born ready. I live for this stuff.
Look at us – we're having a blast!
Oh, yeah. You guys sure know how to throw a party.
We didn’t plan this when we turned on the Lesser Duga. But we can’t let Faust turn the Zone into Zombieland. The Zone bows to no one!
This is gonna be a real mess. The Monolithians aren't creative, but they're stubborn as hell.
I can’t wait to get rolling, but if you need time to gear up, this is your last chance. If you’re ready, I’ll brief you on the plan, and off we go.
As long as Faust is surrounded by phantoms, we can't get to him. And the fact that he has a small army and the most powerful Brain Scorcher in the Zone is not making it easier.
Any guards patrolling the substation?
Our teams report that Monolithians don’t give a damn about the Transformer Room. Plus, we’ve got a couple of tricks to keep them occupied while you’re there.
As for the Ward – after the hit they took, they won’t be coming anywhere near the Duga again. They got the memo.
I’ll shut down the Duga, and I’ll deal with Faust.
Attaboy! Now get into the tunnel before new Monolithian patrols show up.
The signal is patchy down below, so make sure to get to the radio room — that’s our first communication point.
That’s the gist of it. We’ll figure out the rest as we go. March, Mugwort, China, get to your squads and take positions. Let's roll.
I need some time to get ready.
Sure, but be quick about it. The Monolithians must’ve already figured out someone killed their recce team.
And don’t go too far ahead – their Gauss snipers can take you out with a single shot.
Let’s get down to business. You see the Duga? We can’t storm it head-on. Faust and that psi-radiation will fry our brains before we even get close.

But we haven't been sitting around and we've got blueprints from the underground utilities. Turns out, there’s a sewer line running from the firehouse straight to the Duga. It's a quiet way in, and the patrols won't see us.
You gotta get to the Transformer Room and shut down the Duga. Once you’re done, we’ll take care of the Monolithians while you take on Faust. Any questions?
I’ve got my part sorted. What’s your plan?
We’ve got all our friends gathered. Did you see anyone on your way here? That’s the point. Today, the Monolith is gonna find out how many folks are really with Spark. But why ruin the surprise?
You’re the last piece of the puzzle. Once you get down there, my unit commanders will go about their business.
The Monolithians have us outmanned and outgunned. But if we can distract and divide them, their brute force won’t count for much.
Good to see you. So, you ready for the greatest adventure of your life?
sid_phrase_E08_MQ05_Dialog_Star_RadioRoo: I got here as fast as I could. What’s the latest?
So far, everything’s going smoothly. The fanatics are suspecting something, but that was part of the plan, too.
Time to open the presents. That’ll keep the Monolithians busy while you make a run for the Transformer Room. We’ll get in touch once you’re there.
About time. Let’s get this party started, Commander!
sid_phrase_E08_MQ05_cajnataun_0_InfoTopi: Star sure made a hell of a racket. I hope I go down with fireworks like he did!
sid_phrase_E08_MQ05_cajnataun_0_ResumeCo: What was I saying? Oh, right!
sid_phrase_E08_MQ05_edkij_0_InfoTopic_1_: Before the Signal, I used to fall for excuses like “they didn’t know what they were doing” and “they didn’t choose this way”, all that crap.
Screw it. I won’t rest until we find and kill every “former” Monolithian. Those bastards murdered too many of us!
sid_phrase_E08_MQ05_edkov_0_BusyTopic_1_: What are you, stupid? Not now!
sid_phrase_E08_MQ05_edkov_0_DefeatCommen: Ouch! Nice shooting, you sons of bitches!
sid_phrase_E08_MQ05_edkov_0_InfoTopic_1_: I’ll stick with Scar till the end. Come with us if you’ve got the guts.
sid_phrase_E08_MQ05_edkov_0_ResumeCommen: Now where were we, stalker? Make it snappy.
sid_phrase_E08_MQ05_klerik_0_InfoTopic_1: Remember, stalker, it’s not about how many people you actually have. What matters is keeping the enemy guessing about your numbers.
sid_phrase_E08_MQ05_klerik_0_ResumeComme: Okay, what were we talking about?
sid_phrase_E08_MQ05_sasa_mart_0_BusyTopi: Why are you getting in my way?
sid_phrase_E08_MQ05_sasa_mart_0_InfoTopi: The Zone tells the most to those who talk the least.
sid_phrase_E08_SQ01_Audiolog_KardanAndDv: Cardan, I need you to build a device to jam the signal from the collars!
Shit, Professor, it’s 3:00 in the morning. I ought to jam you so you can’t contact me anymore.
Listen to me! This is about Project Leash. We have to block the signal! We cannot allow anyone to control the mutants!
It has to be built and put somewhere where even I can’t access it. If someone decides to try and control the creatures instead of just destroying them…
Sorry, I didn’t catch a single fucking word. You can explain it all at the lab tomorrow. Sleep tight.
sid_phrase_E08_SQ01_Audiolog_KardanCreat: All right, Professor. I've got that collar jammer you wanted all set up, tucked away where no one can mess with it.
But if it ever takes a hit, the X-Network signal might start getting through again. I could crank the jammer to the max to shut down the collars for good...
I think regular jamming should suffice. I doubt anyone would meddle with them if they can’t pick up the signal.
Thanks for the help. I owe you one. How about stopping by for a drink?
sid_phrase_E08_SQ01_Comment_DvupalovAndS: Pretty much everyone around here wants to change the Zone. And everyone else keeps getting in the way.
I guess you’re fortunate that you haven’t created anything that…
You’re right… I haven’t created anything at all! Does that make me even more of a failure than you? But I’m…
Have a drink — it’ll make you feel better. And grab a bite. The pickles are pretty good.
sid_phrase_E08_SQ01_Comment_DvupalovDrin: The hell are you doin' here? Whatcha gawkin' at?
No way I’m gonna pour you one.
But I might as well have another shot. No more toasts, got it?
sid_phrase_E08_SQ01_Comment_DvupalovGets: I'd like to have a word with you.
sid_phrase_E08_SQ01_Comment_DvupalovIfSe: I heard about the collars, Skif. You shut them off, didn’t you?
Then it's finally over. Cardan suggested I do it a while back, but I…
There’s always a price to pay, and sometimes it’s not measured in coupons alone. Here, take this. Let's hope this is the last time we see each other.
sid_phrase_E08_SQ01_Comment_DvupalovIsDr: You know what’s fun about being sober? Hic… nothing!
sid_phrase_E08_SQ01_Comment_DvupalovMoan: That vodka… you know, the magic stuff…
sid_phrase_E08_SQ01_Comment_SherbaCommen: Drink it, Skif! Drink the vodka!
sid_phrase_E08_SQ01_Comment_SherbaIfSetU: And you couldn’t even deal with a stupid jammer!
And you had to step in and ruin everything!
I figured it was better to deactivate the collars.
You figured WHAT?! Do you realize the extent of the damage you’ve done? My entire career is in ruins!
Why don’t you say something? Fix that thing! My whole career is at stake!
This is the Zone. Things don’t always go according to plan.
I don’t give a damn about the Zone’s plans! All you had to do was shut that thing off! Do you realize the damage you’ve done?
A chance like this comes once in a lifetime! I could’ve… this was my life’s work!
Something’s wrong, stalker! My collars aren’t producing any signal at all now!
Are you close to the jammer? What did you do?
sid_phrase_E08_SQ01_Comment_SherbaInvite: Skif, this is Shcherba. You got me those collars and took care of the jammer, remember?
I’ve made remarkable progress since then, and I’d like to show you the results. I’ve even prepared a little bonus for your contribution.
Drop by my lab when you’ve got a moment. You’ll see.
sid_phrase_E08_SQ01_Comment_SherbaIsKill: When I mentioned a reward for you, Skif, I wasn’t being entirely honest. Stage two of the trial is a reward for me as well. It’s a reward for all the effort I’ve put into this.
I failed to persuade Dvupalov to continue his work, so I decided to take matters into my own hands. I’m going to create my own Faust! And, unlike the professor, I’ll be able to control him.
sid_phrase_E08_SQ01_Comment_SherbaLost_y: According to my calculations, the psi-exposure should have had an effect on him by now. I don’t understand…
I told you, that vodka is pure magic!
You old prick! You’ve ruined your own life, and now you want to ruin mine too!
sid_phrase_E08_SQ01_Comment_SherbaNotEno: Why did you have to come down here and distract me from my work, stalker? I gave you clear instructions to bring all the collars at once.
It’s the same story every single time: they bring one collar, then go back for another, and something out there gets them. And I’m left trying to recall which collars I sent them after and where.
I’m waiting for the entire batch.
sid_phrase_E08_SQ01_Comment_SherbaStillN: Subject 48 failed the test. Terminated via sedatives.
sid_phrase_E08_SQ01_Comment_SherbaThreat: You never appreciated my work! Who gave you the right to interfere?
sid_phrase_E08_SQ01_Comment_SkifDidntDri: Stage two of the trial. Test subject number one is prepared for transportation and surgery.
Where’s the vodka, Dvupalov? We've finally got something to celebrate!
sid_phrase_E08_SQ01_Dialog_DvupalovGives: I don’t need any vodka. You can keep it.
Take it… it might… hic! come in handy…
Here, take it… a little magic for ya...
Why would I need your vodka?
sid_phrase_E08_SQ01_Dialog_DvupalovIsSer: How come Shcherba’s supplying you with vodka all of a sudden?
Don’t you even think about asking him! You might spook the guy.
He does it, and that’s good enough for me. Having Shcherba around is better than dealing with a hangover.
That’s right! You’re not like that bore. He can bring me a drink, but he can’t hold his liquor.
That hit the spot… pure magic!
Your call. More for me then.
And he doesn’t drag his feet like you do. All right, now that you’re here, how about a drop of the true?
Ah, stalker! You wanna ask what’s… hic! new around the Zone again?
I’ll fill you in. Turns out, Shcherba isn’t as hopeless as I thought – the grad student’s been bringing me vodka! I don’t even have to ask him for it.
Care for a drink while you’re still hanging around? My grad student sorted out a delivery for me.
sid_phrase_E08_SQ01_Dialog_DvupalovTells: I should’ve listened to Cardan and shut those goddamn collars off. But I kept putting it off and putting it off...
I even got Shcherba mixed up in the whole mess.
He’s the one who got himself into it.
Yeah, right… he just read my files and did it all in my lab.
His fate weighs heavily on me as well…
Thanks for helping me out, anyhow. Here, take this. Let's hope this is the last time we see each other.
He got off way too easy.
That’s one way of looking at it. He’s gonna have to live with it…
All right, we’ll work something out. Just do us both a favor and don’t come back. I’ve got plenty on my plate without you.
But here, take this. You did help me, after all.
sid_phrase_E08_SQ01_Dialog_GotBackToSher: Hey, stalker! And here I thought I might get some news today. You can’t imagine how many of these collars turned out to be completely useless. All I was getting was scrap metal.
I hope you come bearing gifts. How did it go?
Hand them over. I’ll check them out right away. Don’t wander off now.
sid_phrase_E08_SQ01_Dialog_HunterTellsAb: Hey there! You’ve been doing some hunting for that grad student too, eh? He’s kinda shady, ain’t he?
Last time, I nearly got myself killed, and the jerk didn’t even pay me extra. He was all like, “If you don’t do it, someone else will.”
If he ever asks you to lug a chimera on your back for his collection, just don’t. Ain’t worth it, dude.
Nope, I didn't hunt for him. Just brought him some collars.
I guess he hired me to find mutants to go in those collars. And they had to be alive, too! Pretty weird, huh? The scientists never even asked for shit like that.
Did he ever mention why he’s after mutants?
I didn’t bother asking, and I doubt he would’ve spilled the beans anyway. All I know is I’ve been rounding up every critter in the Zone, and he wanted 'em all alive, not dead.
Anything wrong with that grad student?
He asked for mutants, but insisted I bring ’em alive. And not just the psi-dogs — we're talkin' bigger stuff. Can you believe it?
sid_phrase_E08_SQ01_Dialog_SherbaAboutDe: Do you still need help with that disrupted collar signal?
Absolutely! They’re useless in this state, but I’ve got an idea for a way to get the samples online again.
There’s a certain device that needs to be dealt with. Can you help?
What do you need me to do with that signal jammer again?
Shut it off! And do it quick — it’s making my collars entirely useless and stopping my progress in its tracks.
I’ve already forwarded the device’s location to you, but it’s only approximate. Keep an eye on the radio interference — it’ll get stronger the closer you get.
Once you disable the jammer, my collars will pick up the signal. Does everything make sense now, or was there something else you wanted?
sid_phrase_E08_SQ01_Dialog_SherbaAsksToD: Tell me what I need to do.
Something’s disrupting the connection between the collars and the X-Network. I’ve identified the approximate location of the device.
I don’t know what it looks like, but the radio interference will grow stronger as you approach it.
Once you shut off the jammer, my collars should start working again. You can simply break the thing if that's what it takes to get the signal through.
You still haven’t told me why you need these collars.
For research, of course! The kind of research everyone else has given up on. As if there were more pressing matters to attend to...
You wouldn’t believe how much funding SIRCAA was receiving. Where did it all go? And here I am, a humble graduate student, on the verge of a major breakthrough.
But until the collars are operational, they’re worthless. And that makes all of my work worthless too.
Oh, for goodness’ sake! The signal from these collars is jammed as well. They’re utterly worthless in this state.
I’m glad you’re still here. I've got an idea for a way to get these samples back online. Can you lend me a hand?
I’m too busy at the moment.
I hope you can spare some time for me. This job is a walk in the park for you, and I’ll pay you as much as it takes. Besides, we make a great team, you and I.
My research won’t make any progress with the collars offline. Come back when you’re available, and I’ll explain how you can help me.
sid_phrase_E08_SQ01_Dialog_SherbaMercyOr: You’re looking for understanding from the wrong man.
I couldn’t care less about your goals, but I know you won’t be able to replicate this experiment. Get lost.
I’ve seen what you’re capable of.
Who cares?! We’re creating life here! A new life! If that isn’t the reason we’re here in the Zone, then what is?
You’ve told me about Strelok, Scar, and the other stalkers! They’ve done even worse things to achieve their goals.
And yet everyone knows their names. You must understand me, Skif — you’re no different, after all.
What on Earth do you need him for, Skif? He’s squandered his talent! But I can still achieve so much. I’m capable of…
Countless scientists would give anything to do the same! And every single one of them would be overjoyed to take your place!
I didn’t come to the Zone to push pencils. I wanted to achieve something great!
Look who’s coming to save the day!
sid_phrase_E08_SQ01_Dialog_SherbaRepeatA: Why don’t you collect them yourself?
When I was ten, I was bitten by a dog. It hurt so much, I still remember it to this day. All I did was ask it to shake shake a paw.
So I can only get close to these creatures when they’re securely caged.
A trip into the Zone would be a death sentence for me. But for a stalker like you, it’s a piece of cake.
Got it. I’ll do my best.
Good to hear it. I’ll be waiting for the entire batch!
Do you still need my help with those collars?
Absolutely! I’ve gone through the papers you brought, and… well, I told you before — there was one detail everyone at Malachite overlooked, but I caught it. And I’d like to test that theory.
To do so, I’ll need collars. Can you help me gather some?
Remind me again — how can I help with those collars?
I thought you'd already gone to retrieve them. You’ve dealt with these samples before, haven’t you?
You’ve got the coordinates. Keep in mind that they’re approximate, so you’ll need to locate them on-site. And make sure to bring all the collars — don’t bother bringing them here one at a time.
sid_phrase_E08_SQ01_Dialog_SherbaShowsHi: I shouldn’t have helped you with those collars.
If you hadn't, someone else would have. It’s all in a day's work for a stalker, yes?
Many others have tried their hand at it, but I’m the only one who's been able to get the science down. And this is just the beginning.
Regardless, I’ve got a little token of my gratitude for you. Head to the right-hand section of the lab, all the way to the back room.
You may collect your reward for now. Proceed to the right-hand section of the lab, all the way to the back room.
The scientists at Malachite and SIRCAA never even dreamed of such a thing. Some were probably too scared to even walk past the cages, while others probably blew all their funding on the cages alone.
But give the right kind of man a firm spot on which to stand, and he'll turn the Zone upside down, all by himself!
Of course, this is only the beginning — the toughest challenges still lie ahead. But everything will work out, you’ll see!
So what are you going to do now?
Itching to see it, are you? I’m right there with you. But we’ll get to the real heart of my research a bit later.
Aren’t you worried they might turn on you?
I’m absolutely certain I have them under control with the collars! But, as a safety measure, I’ve got a button that can instantly cut their existence short.
But that’s not all! We’ll get to the really exciting part soon enough.
sid_phrase_E08_SQ01_Dialog_SherbaWantsCo: All right. Tell me where to find them.
I’ve detected a few collar signals, but the locations are approximate. I’ll forward you the coordinates, but you’ll have to pin them down once you’re on-site.
Just make sure to bring me the entire batch. I don’t want my work interrupted by one sample at a time.
Stalker, this is Shcherba. While the professor was busy getting plastered, I went through the papers you brought back from X5.
And guess what? Leaving Malachite was the right call! I think I’ve stumbled upon something they all overlooked. Even Dvupalov himself missed it! But it’s just a theory — I need to put it to the test.
To do so, I’ll need collars. Can you help me gather some?
I hope you can spare some time for my request. Aftrer all, you’ve handled similar tasks for Dvupalov before.
If you happen to be nearby, stop by the lab. I’ll explain how you can help me.
I’ve got other things to do.
sid_phrase_E08_SQ01_Dialog_SherbaWhenDes: I’ve got fantastic news, Skif! The signal’s coming through — my collars are up and running!
Now I can finally get back to work. There’s so much left to test, and I can’t wait to dive in!
I’ll reach out if I need your help again. Oh, by the way, here’s a little something for your help.
sid_phrase_E08_SQ01_Serba_callUsBackAfte: Come back sometime. I may require your assistance once more.
sid_phrase_E08_SQ01_aspirant_serba_0_Def: I need a medic! I’m hurt!
sid_phrase_E08_SQ01_aspirant_serba_0_Inf: The Zone is an immense field ripe for exploration. And while some may throw in the towel and cease digging, others will persevere and ultimately unearth the treasure it holds.
The former end up drowning their sorrows in alcohol, while the names of the latter are etched into history.
It’s like they say: be careful what you wish for.
My wish was to follow in Dvupalov's footsteps… and now look where I've ended up.
I wanted to make a difference, to change something about the Zone… in the end, I couldn’t even get promoted.
My career is over. That enough news for you?
sid_phrase_E08_SQ01_aspirant_serba_0_Res: All right. Let’s get back to it.
sid_phrase_E08_SQ01_keps_0_InfoTopic_1_1: You know, the biggest puzzle in the Zone is those eggheads. What are they up to? Who’s their guinea pig? And what’s the whole point of it all? It’d be nice if they’d spill the damn beans already.
sid_phrase_E08_SQ01_professor_dvupalov_0: That grad student was always pestering me: “Why did you give it all up, Professor Dvupalov?” “You could have reached the very summit of scientific achievement, Professor Dvupalov!”
But I’m not fond of oxygen deprivation. Lack of hydration isn’t my thing either.
I never leave this place, so I don’t know any news.
I hope you didn’t bring any either.
Everything you do in the Zone has a price. And you’re fortunate if it’s measured in coupons alone.
sid_phrase_E09_EQ01_Comment_BothSurvived: I don’t think what's left of Tsyba was even aware of your “friendship.”
I… I don’t know. Here, stalker, take this. It’s not much, but it’s all I have. Because you cared.
So, you had your fucking fun playing Mother Theresa, huh?! Now, let’s head to Malachite. Should we set up a hospital for the Monolithians while we’re at it?!
sid_phrase_E09_EQ01_Comment_CD_Shmi_meds: You'd better stay away from us, stalker.
sid_phrase_E09_EQ01_Comment_CK_Laz_medla: You could’ve helped him, you bastard!
sid_phrase_E09_EQ01_Comment_CK_Shmi_meds: Thanks for staying out of it.
sid_phrase_E09_EQ01_Comment_FarTalk_fart: You’re right, we can’t. We have to finish him off.
We can’t just leave him here.
Are you out of your fucking mind?!
sid_phrase_E09_EQ01_Comment_LCD_Shmi_med: Leave me alone, stalker. You’ve “helped” enough already…
sid_phrase_E09_EQ01_Comment_LazarAliveAl: Guys? What’s wrong with you?! Get up!
sid_phrase_E09_EQ01_Comment_LazarenkoCal: Hey, stalker! We need some help over here.
Hit the road, stalker. We’ve got this under control.
sid_phrase_E09_EQ01_Comment_NO_LazShmi_d: We should have helped Tsyba before he went missing. As for this… thing... all I have to offer is a bullet through the head.
Fuck! Tsyba saved his ass so many times, and this is how he repays him?!
sid_phrase_E09_EQ01_Comment_SkifKilledTs: He hadn’t been one of us since the assault on the Duga, Sarge. Even then, there was no way we could’ve helped him.
You should be grateful he spared us from having to do it. Here, stalker. Take this as a token of my appreciation.
Fuck! What the hell?! There must've been some way to help him… you fucking butcher!
sid_phrase_E09_EQ01_Comment_SmidtCantWai: I can’t waste any more of our time on this. I’m sorry, buddy.
sid_phrase_E09_EQ01_Comment_SmidtKilledT: We should’ve “tried something” back at the Duga, before we lost contact with Tsyba. It was too late.
Now pull yourself together — we’ve gotta catch up with the rest at Malachite. And keep your cool next time, okay?
Captain, no! What the hell?! There must've been some way to help him! SIRCAA, the scientists… we could’ve tried something…
sid_phrase_E09_EQ01_Comment_SmitdAttack_: I warned you! You fucking idiots!
sid_phrase_E09_EQ01_Comment_TsibaMonolit: What? You want some water, Tsyba?
sid_phrase_E09_EQ01_Comment_YESNO_Shmidt: Don’t waste your time on him, stalker. It’s better to just finish him off right away.
sid_phrase_E09_EQ01_Comment_YES_LazInter: You gotta help Tsyba, stalker. He’s gonna bleed to death!
sid_phrase_E09_EQ01_Dialog_LazarenkoExpl: Hey, you got a medkit? Our buddy’s bleeding out here, and we ran out during the battle.
It’s Tsyba, Captain. Tsyba! He’s just wounded and exhausted, that’s it. We can’t let a brother-in-arms die, can we?
Hold on, stalker. He used to be one of us, but now he’s turned Monolithian.
Let me see what I can do.
Help me save Tsyba, stalker. We can’t leave him like this!
You should have rescued Tsyba back at the Duga, before he went missing. Now his brain is so scrambled, the only thing that can help him is a bullet.
So what? We’ll bring him back to the base. Our scientists are smart enough to figure it out!
No, guys. You’re on your own here.
Fuck… what’s wrong with you people?!
It’s for the best. The only way we can honor Tsyba’s memory now is… by ending the suffering of whatever’s left of him.
How can he be a Monolithian? He’s got the same uniform as you.
He went missing after the assault on the Duga. None of us had seen him since. And now here he is, like a little present in the middle of the battle.
Don’t waste your supplies on him, stalker. He hasn’t been one of us for a long time.
He was one of us, and he still is. Sure, he’s a little shaken up, but if we help him now, the medics can get him back on his feet. And if the medics can't, the scientists will!
sid_phrase_E09_EQ01_Dialog_SmitdAliveAlo: I was just doing what he asked me to.
He asked, did he? Fuck! I should’ve done Tsyba a favor and shot him dead back at the Duga…
I could tell he was going nuts right away, but I felt sorry for him, so I let him crawl over to Monolith… they didn’t even flinch.
And now I’ve lost both of them 'cuz I showed mercy…
What are you gonna do next?
Those assholes have fallen back toward Malachite, so I'll head that way. As for you — I'd stay out of it. You don't wanna end up rushing to the rescue of every brainwashed zombie you see.
I wonder how many more guys like Tsyba are out there in the Zone now…
You goddamn idiots, both of you… Lazarenko, I can understand, but you? Why the fuck did you get involved?
sid_phrase_E09_EQ01_ciba_0_BusyTopic_1_6: For the glory of the Monolith!
sid_phrase_E09_EQ01_kapitan_smidt_0_Busy: Move along. I’m pretty busy right now.
sid_phrase_E09_EQ01_kapitan_smidt_0_Info: New? Ain’t nothing new under the sun — least of all in the Zone. Blood, shit, death... it’s all the same story, day in, day out.
sid_phrase_E09_EQ01_serzant_lazarenko_0_: No way, I’m not fucking done here!
We kicked their Monolithian asses real good. They’ll think twice before messing with us again!
sid_phrase_E09_EQ02_Comment_EndOfTheRoun: Well? Where the hell did he go? There's only two left. I’d rather not stick around here for another hour.
sid_phrase_E09_EQ02_Comment_HollywoodAft: Thanks for not being a dick about the loot, stalker. I can respect that. Don’t hold it against Smidge — he lost a buddy in this mess. That’s why he’s getting all riled up.
Take it easy, stalker. He lost a good friend in this battle, and he’s taking it out on everyone around him.
sid_phrase_E09_EQ02_Comment_HollywoodAgr: What the fuck is wrong with you?!
sid_phrase_E09_EQ02_Comment_HollywoodGre: Well, well, look who the cat dragged in!
sid_phrase_E09_EQ02_Comment_HollywoodNOP: Too many of us have already died for the Shining Zone… but are we any closer to it?
sid_phrase_E09_EQ02_Comment_SkifRefusedZ: Where the hell were you, bud?
Damn… you should grab his bag too... in memory of our comrade.
I was out looking for Sashko. I wanted to make sure he ain’t coming back.
sid_phrase_E09_EQ02_Comment_SparkNPCgk_N: I don’t get it. Why the hell did Smidge get two bags at once?
That’s one for his buddy. No one’s seen him since the assault…
We've all lost people in the Zone, ya know. We should’ve shared it, or at least given it to Powerplug to hold onto.
sid_phrase_E09_EQ02_Comment_SparkNPCgk_Y: You should’ve cut Smidge some slack, man…
He had his reasons, man. We’ve got rules…
When it comes to this stuff, Hollywood’s the boss. If he says it’s cool, it’s cool. And that stalker’s been fighting with us for a while now.
Gimme a break! Smidge was the one who started it.
You did the right thing, stalker, letting one of ours take his pick. You gotta respect the rules, after all.
No way! This stalker’s been through a ton of shit with us, and you guys still treat him like crap. Like, “You ain’t one of us! Get lost!” Keep it up, and your own guys are gonna start bailing.
sid_phrase_E09_EQ02_Comment_SparkNPCsvh_: Too bad it didn’t come to a fistfight. That would’ve been a hell of a show…
Hey, we'll divvy it up fair and square. Smidge ain’t gonna have nothin' to bitch about now, and you still got your share. Well done, bud!
sid_phrase_E09_EQ02_Comment_Warning_Warn: Hands off, got it? It ain’t yours. This is your first and only warning.
sid_phrase_E09_EQ02_Comment_ZmenyaLeftUn: What more do you want from me? You got your handout, so go fuck yourself.
sid_phrase_E09_EQ02_Comment_ZmenyaPick_H: How do ya like your loot? Mine’s better, right?
sid_phrase_E09_EQ02_Comment_ZmenyaSkifGa: You can keep the one you were gonna offer me and choke on it.
sid_phrase_E09_EQ02_Comment_ZmenyaSkifRe: I feel so bad about Sashko… we spent years together in the Zone. No loot’s gonna bring him back.
sid_phrase_E09_EQ02_Comment_ZmenyaSkifTa: Well, what else would you expect from some random schmuck?
sid_phrase_E09_EQ02_Dialog_HollywoodGame: “On the left” is relative. You mean the one on your left, or the one on my left?
Hey, calm your tits, I’m just kidding.
Hey there. You need a written invitation?
I’d love to know what for.
It’s simple enough. If you’re lucky, you get to pick a bag with some sweet loot.
That wasn't very nice, stalker. But it’s your call.
What’s this show of unrequited generosity all about?
After a big fight, we usually divvy up the loot blind. Helps avoid any squabbles.
And you’ve basically been earnin’ your keep lately, stalker. Besides, we seem to be a couple of men short right now.
Okay. So, which one’s speakin' to ya?
sid_phrase_E09_EQ02_Dialog_ZmenyaWantsTo: There are two of you, and there are two bags. What’s your problem?
What, I can’t go take a leak without the rules changing? We’re letting outsiders in now too?
What the fuck is this guy doing here?
You’d be still stuck fighting Faust if it weren’t for my help. I pitched in and earned my share of the loot.
Sashko didn’t take a backseat either, and in the end, he got a bullet instead. That’s just not fair, stalker.
We had a common enemy, and we beat them together.
Together, huh? A lot of our guys lost their lives for that, and you just happened to show up at the right moment.
Cool off. I didn’t ask for this, all right?
Here in the Zone, it’s just a matter of time until we all cool off, if you catch my drift.
Take it easy, Smidge. I’m the one who brought the stalker into this. Each of you gets a bag. Pick one and get over it.
sid_phrase_E09_EQ02_gollivud_0_HelloTopi: Hey, stalker. What brings you here?
sid_phrase_E09_EQ02_gollivud_0_InfoTopic: There’s always somethin' new just around the corner. Maybe it'll take a bite out of you from some unexpected angle, or maybe it’ll find a peculiar way to cripple your ass. Stay sharp!
sid_phrase_E09_EQ02_gril_0_InfoTopic_1_1: Another day down, another battle won. In the Zone, that counts for something, believe me!
sid_phrase_E09_EQ02_gril_0_ResumeComment: So where were we? Oh, right.
sid_phrase_E09_EQ02_kostan_sovuha_0_ByeT: So long, stalker. See you around!
sid_phrase_E09_EQ02_kostan_sovuha_0_Hell: Hey there! Wanna hang for a bit?
sid_phrase_E09_EQ02_kostan_sovuha_0_Info: A while back, I heard about someone in the Zone cooking up a special, damn near magical vodka. Man, I'd love to have a taste of that.
sid_phrase_E09_EQ02_kostan_sovuha_0_Resu: Yeah, all right. Where were we?
sid_phrase_E09_EQ02_vlad_kastan_0_InfoTo: Ugh! I ain’t no fan of anything new, least of all in the Zone. Surprises can suck it.
sid_phrase_E09_EQ02_zmena_0_InfoTopic_1_: Well, there’s this weirdo who just popped in, and he won’t stop talkin' my ear off. Do me a favor and beat it.
sid_phrase_E09_MQ01_Comment_DalinAfterIn: Sorry, I need some time to collect my thoughts.
sid_phrase_E09_MQ01_Comment_DalinAsksFor: May I ask you a favor, Skif?
If you happen to come across new data concerning the Representative’s statements, anything at all, please pass it along to me. Any bit of information could be important.
sid_phrase_E09_MQ01_Comment_DalinInShock: Quiet. Obey my orders. Follow me.
sid_phrase_E09_MQ01_Comment_Dalin_Assaul: You have your own mission, Skif. You shouldn’t be here.
sid_phrase_E09_MQ01_Comment_Dalin_StartQ: Skif? It’s Dalin. I heard you dealt with Faust personally. Is that true? Are you all right?
Great to hear! Skif, I have a favor to ask. Please search Faust’s body and collect everything you find on him. It’s extremely important.
I’ll be waiting for you at Lab X17. The colonel’s squad paid a visit to the Clear Sky base, and they didn’t come back empty-handed. You’ll get all the details when next we meet.
sid_phrase_E09_MQ01_Comment_InteractMara: Scar’s... well, he hasn’t really been himself lately. He was always a little nuts, but never like this.
sid_phrase_E09_MQ01_Comment_InteractOzer: All of my work, my Malachite… Scar made a promise to me… we need to go right now!
I’m sorry, I really don’t have time to talk right now.
sid_phrase_E09_MQ01_Comment_InteractScar: I can’t get the professor out of here while you’re still hanging around. You have to distract the Ward.
Come on, use the key already.
sid_phrase_E09_MQ01_Comment_Iskra_QuestC: And then there were none… exceptional work, my friend. Outstanding.
But you know what matters even more? I was right all along! How was that for a miracle?
Skif! There’s no denying I was right now, is there? What we witnessed was truly a miracle.
What do you know about Strelok?
All there is to know in the Zone. He did what no one has ever done before: he reached the very center of the Zone and revealed Prypiat’s secrets.
I’ve been looking for him for as long as I can remember, but so far all my efforts have been in vain.
Lucky for us, he wasn’t alone on his journey. He had Fang, Ghost, and a Doctor with him. And everyone save for the Doctor is either missing or dead.
Sure, it was pretty impressive. But I’m having a hard time believing it all.
That’s because we were so rudely interrupted. Faith always begins with doubt, but it also culminates in knowledge.
Doesn’t matter. We’ll find another way to contact the Representative. We’ll need to locate the X-Network center where all the visiographs are linked.
The problem is, we don’t know where it is or how to get there. It’s time to go legend hunting, Skif. We need Strelok. If anyone knows about that center, it’s him.
So what are you gonna do next?
You should be asking what you're going to do. To find Strelok, we’ll need to track down the Doctor. He’s the only one from Strelok’s group whose whereabouts aren't a total mystery.
He’s a legend in his own right — he materializes out of thin air, heals everyone he encounters, even mutants, and then vanishes without a trace.
Okay. Where do I find him?
I have no idea, but he’s often sighted in the Red Forest. Listen to the local tall tales; there’s always more truth to them than meets the eye. At the very least, you’ll get a break.
I have a feeling you could use one, hmm?
sid_phrase_E09_MQ01_Comment_KorshunovAft: Go ahead and get the Visiograph going like Dalin told you. I’m eager to know what we were betrayed for.
sid_phrase_E09_MQ01_Comment_Korshunov_As: Every minute you waste here means a soldier’s life lost, Skif. Get it done. That’s an order.
sid_phrase_E09_MQ01_Comment_Korshunov_Wa: What did you do to this one?
My men did a thorough search of Promin and found the Emitter. Do you know what that means?
Promin is a link between the Duga and Malachite. The schematics confirm that there’s no other location where the Duga can be activated.
The Duga was launched from Malachite.
sid_phrase_E09_MQ01_Comment_MalahitScien: We’ve been spotted. Stick to the wall. Get down, get down!
Tell me, is it… is it over out there?
It’s a nightmare… I need to report this to “Plain and Simple”...
sid_phrase_E09_MQ01_Comment_MaratGoing_1: Why the fuck did I have to join up? I shoulda stuck to being a loner…
sid_phrase_E09_MQ01_Comment_RadioIskra_r: Eliminate Dalin and Korshunov. If you spot Skif, take him out too. Over and out!
sid_phrase_E09_MQ01_Comment_RadioVarta_r: Alpha Team has located the target.
Eliminating Scar and the stalker known as Skif is our top priority. Delta Team is en route to your location. Over and out.
sid_phrase_E09_MQ01_Comment_ScarWaiting_: Are we digging in here or not? Make up your mind!
What if Faust doesn’t have it? What if…
Honestly, not really. Whether we dig in or not, everything is going to change very soon. Do you get it? You will.
sid_phrase_E09_MQ01_Comment_Scar_StartQu: Come in. Do you copy? Is anyone still alive out there?
That’s the kind of news I like to hear! And what about Faust? Is he in the “still alive” category as well?
I like that kind of news even better! Believe it or not, I’m a little sorry he’s gone. He was a true believer. That’s rare.
But enough poetry. Search his body — he’s got to have something on him that can help us. You’ll know it when you see it. Meet me at Malachite.
sid_phrase_E09_MQ01_Comment_SparkAssault: Colonel, Spark’s here! They’ve opened fire! We’re holding our ground and fortifying building A. Awaiting your orders!
Shoot to kill. Take no prisoners.
We have to sweep and clear the hallways, Skif. I’ll escort our luminary here upstairs to keep the Sparker gunmen away from him. I’ll meet you at the entrance.
sid_phrase_E09_MQ01_Comment_Varta_QuestC: Dalin, your father was a part of C-Consciousness. Did the Representative remind you of him in any way?
I can’t say… I’m not sure. It all happened so quickly. If I had a bit more time…
No. You won’t spend a moment on this nonsense until we reach the Generators. We have more important matters to attend to than dealing with C-Consciousness hocus-pocus.
But what if it isn’t just hocus-pocus?
You just got to Malachite, and you’re already diving into pseudoscience? I didn’t expect you to question your own expertise so quickly, Doctor.
Great job, Skif. The last few stragglers won’t cause any more trouble here.
What do you think of that Visiograph projection? The Representative... was he real? Or just some kind of hologram?
It’s all smoke and mirrors. Just another C-Consciousness trick. Our focus should remain on the real threat: Scar and his gang.
As for me, I’m not certain of anything anymore. Strelok’s report indicates that he spoke to C-Consciousness in the Monolith control bunker, but at that point, those scientists were still alive.
Today’s events, though... I can’t explain them. But I’m not ready to agree with the colonel that it’s all just a trick.
All right. What’s the next move?
We fucked up our chance to establish a remote connection to the Generators from X11. Now we have to find a way to get there directly through Dead Valley.
There’s only one man who’s ever made it to the center of the Zone and come back with his mind intact: Strelok.
This Strelok seems to be the Zone legend. Is he really that tough?
He did what no one else could. There are rumors that he’s recently returned to the Zone. I tried to track him down shortly after we moved to the Chemical Plant, but to no avail.
He used to lead this group: Fang, Ghost, and the Doctor. The first two are dead, but they say the Doctor’s hiding in the Red Forest. He’s something of a legend too. A very elusive guy.
But it’s all just hearsay. The Doctor is a man like any other. If you find him, we might have a shot at finding a safe route to the Generators. Best of luck.
sid_phrase_E09_MQ01_Dialog_Korshunov_Int: They’re all dead because of him!
Scar sent me here as a precaution, to make sure that Ozersky doesn’t throw in the towel and gets the installation online in time.
I’ve already said it several times.
That must be a mistake… I can’t believe it…
A mistake?! Your Ozersky is a traitor! My men went mad and died in droves at the Duga, not knowing how to protect themselves!
Sergeant, take the prisoner to the Chemical Plant. Now.
I said repeat it! Word for word!
So what are we gonna do now?
A device resembling the Visiograph was found in the basement of the Clear Sky base. It’s in bad shape, but if Faust managed to activate it, that means he had the key.
It’s the key used to activate the Visiograph. Go ahead and insert it. We’ll join you once the Visiograph goes live.
You searched his body, so you must have come across something — maybe some kind of a pendant or necklace.
Repeat what you told me before.
You killed Ozersky, but not the Sparker. Why?
He might have more information about Spark. And he didn’t betray me.
I can’t afford to doubt my men, Skif. There’s no time to speculate about who might stab me in the back next.
That’s why everyone needs to understand that traitors to the Ward will choke on their own blood.
sid_phrase_E09_MQ01_Dialog_MalahitScient: Please, don’t shoot! We’re working at the Institute…
Calm down. Just stay out of my way, and there won’t be any trouble.
Y-yes, of course. We’ll stay quiet.
We’ve been here the whole time.
Calm down, I’m not your enemy. Just tell me what you saw.
We were at our workplaces as usual when that bald officer showed up — stomping around in his exoskeleton, swearing at everyone, stationing his men on every floor… but they didn’t hurt us.
Then the Sparkers arrived... that’s when the bloodshed began. The man who sounded the alarm was shot. I’m not sure which side did it. We took cover here.
How did Spark manage to take over Malachite so fast?
They probably used hidden passages. This isn’t their first visit here — their leader used to have some dealings with Ozersky. They provided him with some components or something.
I see. Stay put. This place should be safe for now.
Yes, of course. If you say so. And here, take this. It might come in handy. Thank you for deciding not to shoot first and ask questions later.
sid_phrase_E09_MQ01_Dialog_ShramCutscene: Scar, the Ward is at the gates. It’s just a matter of time before they break through.
But of course… fuck! Take care of them, and fast!
Remember, I don’t work for you.
You’re working with me! Over and out.
Skif, I need you to help distract the Ward while I get the professor out. I’ll meet you outside.
sid_phrase_E09_MQ01_Dialog_ShramDirectio: There are Wardens near the railroad tunnel too, Skif. You know the drill.
We’re almost clear, but we’ve got snipers here. We need you to distract them.
All right, Skif. Get out of there! Ozersky’s safe. I’ll meet you at the designated coordinates.
sid_phrase_E09_MQ01_Dialog_Shram_Viziogr: All right. What do I have to do?
Place the key on those controls over there, and hold your breath.
Skif! At last! Did you find it?
It’s the key to something incredibly important, something that’s going to change us all. And it’s all thanks to you and me.
Come. The time of miracles is nigh.
You seem a little on edge. What’s going on?
It’s something I’ve been working towards for years. And now, with your help, I’ve finally reached it! Come on, Skif, let’s not keep everyone waiting.
Terrifying ones. Astonishing ones. And, above all, real ones. Those are the only kinds of miracles we have here in the Zone.
sid_phrase_E09_MQ01_Dialog_Soznanie_Dali: How can we be certain that you’re a real living mind, and not some sort of program or copy of someone’s memory?
You’re correct, Dmytro. A scientist should never take anything on blind faith. In the Zone, there’s a laboratory with a device that can be used to contact the image of a specific individual.
Where can we find this laboratory?
sid_phrase_E09_MQ01_Dialog_Soznanie_Shra: So this is what the Shining Zone really is! I knew it in my heart, I believed in it all along! Seek and ye shall find!
The Shining can rid us of the darkness that’s holding us back, preventing us from hearing each other. It will grant us eternal life. No pain, no regret, no suffering.
How can we make this new age come sooner?
sid_phrase_E09_MQ01_Dialog_Soznanie_WhoA: Who are you? Or… what are you?
We are C-Consciousness. We were once seven scientists, seven distinct personalities. Now, we exist as one mind.
By merging our consciousnesses, we’ve achieved a direct connection with the Noosphere. I am the Representative of C-Consciousness.
sid_phrase_E09_MQ01_Dialog_Soznanie_YouK: We’re aware of everything that happens in the Zone, but we’re not always capable of influencing it.
After SIRCAA tried to replicate the Caribbean Experiment, some of the Anomalous energy seeped into the Mainland. It affected a number of people, but you were the only one who sought the truth.
sid_phrase_E09_MQ01_Dialog_Soznanie_dest: Everyone in the Zone thinks Strelok killed you all. How did you manage to survive?
When Strelok went to Lab X7 with the intent of destroying our pods, we were certain that our end was near.
As it turned out, even after our physical demise, our existence persisted within the Noosphere.
Regardless of Strelok’s intentions, any efforts to revive us would have been futile because the merging of our minds is irreversible.
sid_phrase_E09_MQ01_Dialog_Soznanie_hell: Greetings, Skif. I know you have a lot of questions for me. Ask them, and then we’ll decide what to do with you.
sid_phrase_E09_MQ01_Dialog_Soznanie_immo: Since you survived, does that mean the Noosphere is an extension of life?
The Noosphere is much more than just the Earth’s information field.
Thanks to the Zone, sentient beings can achieve eternal life in the form of Subtle Matter — pure consciousness, free from the constraints and limitations of a physical body.
However, should anyone attempt to harness the Anomalous energy, it will inevitably result in the annihilation of all Subtle Matter.
sid_phrase_E09_MQ01_Dialog_Soznanie_what: What are you trying to achieve?
After the reckless intervention by SIRCAA, the structure of the Noosphere was damaged, isolating individual Subtle Matter images from one another.
The Shining will reinstate the right to immortality for all sentient beings in the Zone — and, eventually, for all of mankind.

From then on, our liberated consciousnesses will gain the ability to communicate directly with one another, facilitating the free exchange of knowledge and experience. It will be the dawn of a new age.
sid_phrase_E09_MQ01_docent_leonid_hoffma: Your sense of timing appears to be lacking, I must say.
Well, what can I say? All this chaos within the confines of the temple of science is something new indeed.
We got a little distracted there…
sid_phrase_E09_MQ01_professor_vsevolod_m: What? No, I can't talk to you right now.
I warned Ozersky that chumming around with those Sparkers would lead to no good. And look where we are now! We could easily end up catching a stray bullet in these hallways.
sid_phrase_E09_MQ01_starsij_laborant_gri: Oh, no. I didn’t sign up for this. Help!
Are you sure I’m the one you're looking for?
I’m sorry, but it’s hard for me to think clearly under these circumstances.
sid_phrase_E10_MQ01_Audiolog_DeadSivyj_1: Arkhyp, can you hear me? Arkhyp! It’s Gray! Fucking anomaly!
Tundra got shot! We'd just set up shop right where the Doctor was spotted. I figured if we saw him, we’d hand him over to the mercs. Now Tundra’s guts are hanging out!
I couldn’t even see where the shot came from! Could've been anywhere. Tundra’s a dead man walking! You gotta tell... Please, don’t…
sid_phrase_E10_MQ01_Audiolog_DeadTundra_: Gray abandoned me... The Anomaly blocks the signal.
Don't come out, Doctor. Wherever you... don't come out... Those mercenaries are searching for you... I saw them near the village...
sid_phrase_E10_MQ01_Comment_ArhipAfterQu: Oh, it’s you, stalker. Found what you were looking for?
Whatever. Hornet’s gone. Gray’s dead and buried. There ain’t a soul left to hunt in this place. I’m just gonna sell off the last of my stuff, and that’s it.
This forest got the best of us. It’s no place for ordinary folks.
sid_phrase_E10_MQ01_Comment_ArhipMeetBef: Welcome, loner! We don't have much here, but we’re always glad to welcome guests.
If you wanna hunt, I’ve got a stockpile of ammo and guns for any game you’re after, deer or boar, you name it.
Of course, I’ve got a stash of medkits, too, in case the hunt goes sideways. If you need anything, just give me a holler.
sid_phrase_E10_MQ01_Comment_ArhipOnInter: I already told you everything, stalker. You’ve got the coordinates for the clearing on your PDA. Now let me concentrate.
sid_phrase_E10_MQ01_Comment_Cemetery_1_5: We got that scumbag! Ha ha! Did anyone catch where he went? I’m forwarding the coordinates to the client. Watch my back!
sid_phrase_E10_MQ01_Comment_Cemetery_1_6: Commander, there’s someone else trying to flank us!
Take ’em all down! Must be a grateful patient coming to the rescue!
sid_phrase_E10_MQ01_Comment_DocOnInterac: Hurry up. He doesn’t have much time. I’ll take him inside once he's stable.
sid_phrase_E10_MQ01_Comment_HuntersFirst: Taking him here was a mistake! We risked our necks just lugging him back, and you wanted to search the clearing too!
Calm down. He saved your life six months ago. We couldn’t just leave him behind.
Dead folks don’t give a fuck where they rest. But I do. I mean, who could do this to a human being?!
I know who: it’s those mercenaries. They were looking for the Doctor, and they found Gray instead.
Bullshit! Mercs wouldn’t do something like this.
People ain’t mutants — they’re capable of anything. Now hush. I’ve gotta figure out what mauled Gray like that. Maybe then we can hunt the fuckers down.
sid_phrase_E10_MQ01_Comment_SivyjBodyInt: Stay away from him, loner. If you’ve got something to say, spit it out.
sid_phrase_E10_MQ01_Comment_SkifIdle_doi: Why are you poking around here? If you’ve got something to say, make it snappy. I’m already on edge.
sid_phrase_E10_MQ01_Comment_VolkodavCall: Alpha Team – we’ve tracked him down to the cemetery. We need backup ASAP. I repeat: everyone, get your asses over here!
sid_phrase_E10_MQ01_Comment_YarlSaveThem: Help ’em, stalker. They might not be angels, but they sure as hell don’t deserve this. I’m gonna take a breather, maybe catch a few winks.
He’s about to off ’em all! You gotta help ’em, stalker. He’s gonna wipe ’em all out!
sid_phrase_E10_MQ01_Dialog_ArhipAboutSit: Sorry, loner, not a good time. We’re not open for business right now.
So why don’t you get the fuck outta here? And shut the door on your way out.
We’re not open for business, but we can chat. Keep it short, you can see what’s going on here.
If those mercs were behind it, watch your step out there. And if it wasn’t them, be even more careful.
Better yet, get out of here while you still can — before it gets worse.
I’m looking for the Doctor. I heard you might know where to find him.
You too?! Are you working with the mercs, you son of a bitch?! Answer me!
Put the gun down. Now. And you, loner — why are you so interested in the Doctor?
What are you guys doing here, anyway?
Breeding Fleshes for a stew! We’re hunting, what else? We can’t all go anomaly diving and playing detective.
Sometimes scientists cut us a check for some mutants, or Freedom tosses us a job. Life’s pretty decent here. Or at least it used to be.
I used to feel at home in these woods. But now… it’s like I’m locked up all over again, like that one time back in Garbage — enemies everywhere, nowhere to run.
We’ll find them.
Or they’ll find us first, just like Gray.
So where should I start looking for him?
Gray was gonna stake out a place where the Doctor was spotted before. It was a while ago, but I don't have any other leads. If it was those mercs, maybe they left something behind.
So that’s why you’ve been chattering away for two hours straight, huh? You’re thinking of sending a loner to snoop around the clearing?
You’re after the Doctor, stalker, and I’m looking for the bastards who did Gray in. Why don’t we lend each other a hand?
Don’t listen to him, loner. Two fellas took on Myklukha's job: Gray and a new guy. You can see what’s left of ’em. Something's fishy. If you go after them, you might not make it back either.
I’ll give it a thought.
Are these mercs looking for the Doctor too?
They swung by the other day, asking where the Doctor was at, where he was last seen. Throwing money around, so I told 'em as much as I could.
If I’d known what would come of it… I'd have shut my fucking trap, as Hornet would say.
No one knows where the Doctor lives. All we know is he helps the wounded, both human and mutant. He saved my life some five years ago.
Why didn’t your buddy the doctor step in for Gray, huh? Too busy stitching a tentacle back to a Bloodsucker’s ass? Or are we hunters worse than mutants?
If he didn’t help, he couldn’t. Or didn’t want to. Or maybe the mercs got to him first.
None of your business.
Fine by me. But tracking him down ain’t gonna be a piece of cake.
So you’ve seen the Doctor?
Just like I see you right now. Except I had a hole in my gut, so the memory’s a little hazy. I saw a shadowy figure, and then I woke up in the clearing.
My wound was all cleaned and patched up, and my radio was even charged. The guys said I was missing for a week.
So don’t pay attention to Hornet. The Doctor never hurt anyone — he's all about helping folks.
I’m looking for Strelok. The Doctor knows where to find him.
I don’t know why you need Strelok, but finding the Doctor ain’t exactly a walk in the park either.
Tell me more about the Doctor.
Want me to do a little dance for you while I'm at it? Everybody’s got questions about him — first the mercs, now the loners…
It can’t get much worse. The Doctor is a legend around here. Nobody really knows where he’s holed up, but if you’re injured, he’ll track you down and lend a hand. Well, at least he used to.
I’m telling you, it was the Doctor. We’ve been hunting his pets. That’s why he came after us.
You’re an idiot, Hornet. The Doctor saved my ass once, and I’ve been in the hunting business around here for a solid decade.
Thanks for the help — and the heads-up.
Who did him in?
I wish I knew. Two of our guys went on a job for Myklukha, and we found one of ’em close by… like this.
It might be those mercs that just showed up here. Hell knows what they're up to.
Nah, mercs are all right. They get their pay, they do their thing. But Gray… Someone went out of their way to make him suffer – cutting him up real slow.
sid_phrase_E10_MQ01_Dialog_Doctor_DidYou: Is he going to make it?
I’ve treated worse injuries – even on Monolithians. Especially on Monolithians.
He spilled a lot of blood to get here – his own, and other people’s.
And so did you, I imagine. I’m aware of what he’s done, but he’s injured and needs help. That’s all that matters to me.
sid_phrase_E10_MQ01_Dialog_Doctor_HeKill: So, it was him who killed the mercenaries and hunters.
They were a bait. A way to catch my attention.
sid_phrase_E10_MQ01_Dialog_Doctor_HelpHu: Why didn’t you help the hunters? Or the mercs?
How many lives can a dead doctor save? Sometimes you need to make small sacrifices so you can save more lives later.
sid_phrase_E10_MQ01_Dialog_Doctor_INeedD: So this is what the real Doctor looks like.
Do you know him? This Monolithian?
That’s a good name. An honest name. You were looking for the Doctor? Well, you’ve found him. Now what do you want from me?
sid_phrase_E10_MQ01_Dialog_Doctor_KillYo: Why are you so concerned about his life?
Because it’s the only thing we truly possess, no matter how bad we believe it to be.
Now go. Don’t waste any more time.
sid_phrase_E10_MQ01_Dialog_Doctor_Regene: A creation of C-Consciousness. It helps the Monolithians to heal even the most grievous injuries and get radiation out of their bodies faster.
Unfortunately, it only works on those who lack resistance to external control after being completely stripped of psi-immunity.
Trading one’s freedom for the chance to wander through the CNPP during an Emission is a devil’s bargain, but no one asked their opinion on the matter.
sid_phrase_E10_MQ01_Dialog_Doctor_Strelo: I’m looking for any information about Strelok. I know you two have some history.
I doubt Strelok’s figured out how to bring the dead back to life. So it’s up to us to help Strider. Everything else can wait.
You think there’s still a chance to help him?
In a way, Strider’s fortunate to be a Monolithian. There’s something that can help men like him, but not us.
I’ve marked a former Monolithian hospital on your PDA. If they’ve tapped into the old stashes, you might find a regenerator there.
Along with the Monolithians themselves, of course. I’d ask you to spare the wounded and keep them alive, but it’s your call, stalker.
sid_phrase_E10_MQ01_Dialog_Doctor_WhyHel: I’ve heard that you treat both humans and mutants. Why?
Because none of them are responsible for what’s going on here – not the stalkers, not the mutants, not even the Monolithians.
And also because everyone has to pay their dues someday.
sid_phrase_E10_MQ01_Dialog_Doctor_hi2_70: He didn't kill them by choice. The Monolith guided him to hunt me with live bait
And I won't let you finish him. Enough deaths.
sid_phrase_E10_MQ01_Dialog_Doctor_hi_580: If you’ve come to finish him off, it’s not gonna happen. No more bloodshed.
sid_phrase_E10_MQ01_Dialog_MercCamp_1_58: Stalker? What… what are you doing here? Ouch, it hurts…
sid_phrase_E10_MQ01_Dialog_MercCamp_Clie: I have no idea. Nobody’s ever seen the guy. And his jobs always turn out bad. He sent my pal to Wild Island after SIRCAA went kaboom.
Never heard from him again. We should never have taken this job. Bad call…
sid_phrase_E10_MQ01_Dialog_MercCamp_Doct: We were looking for him too. But he got to us first.
sid_phrase_E10_MQ01_Dialog_MercCamp_Find: Don’t. He’s not even human. Those eyes of his…
sid_phrase_E10_MQ01_Dialog_MercCamp_Him_: The Doctor! It was him! He’s the one who took out those hunters, then came gunning for us. He knew we were onto him.
I couldn’t do anything. He dropped two of our guys with a sniper rifle – bang, bang – gone! Then he walked right through the radiation.
I put two rounds in him, and the guy didn’t even blink! Next thing I knew, he was right up in my face, point blank. I’ve used up all my medkits…
I’m not gonna make it, am I?
sid_phrase_E10_MQ01_Dialog_MercCamp_Medk: Thanks, loner. I hope the guys get back soon.
sid_phrase_E10_MQ01_Dialog_MercCamp_Merc: I’m looking for the mercs who killed the hunters.
We’re the mercs, but we didn’t kill anybody.
sid_phrase_E10_MQ01_Dialog_MercCamp_No_6: I knew it. I should’ve gone with Wolfhound…
sid_phrase_E10_MQ01_Dialog_MercCamp_Nois: That’s our signal. When it’s blasting at full volume, it means trouble: make a run for it and don’t look back.
But what if someone follows the sound back here?
Bring ’em on. I’m as good as gone already, as you can see. At least I can give the guys a fighting chance.
sid_phrase_E10_MQ01_Dialog_MercCamp_Orde: We were sent here to track down the Doctor. I told Wolfhound not to take the job. We don’t even know the client!
I figured we’d be safe here, with all these anomalies and radiation around… but he just plowed right through, you know?! The Geiger’s off the charts, and he’s just strolling around…
sid_phrase_E10_MQ01_Dialog_ShershenjAfte: You really think the Doctor killed Gray?
You saw the body. He was killed slowly. They wanted him to suffer. Why the heck would mercs do something like that?
The Doctor's always helped mutants. I remember this one time I shot a boar that damn near tore me apart. A month later, I stumbled across the same boar sporting a nasty scar on its snout.
Lucky for me, I had my brand-new shotgun. Had to put that boar down for good. That Doctor of yours is off his rocker. Totally fucked up.
I guess Freedom can handle this. I wanted to ask about something else.
Five years in the Zone, and I never had to bury a single soul. Can you believe it? Must’ve been one lucky guy.
Now I guess my luck’s run dry.
What kind of job was Gray doing out there?
A bunch of Freedom guys set up a secret stash in a cave. Well, not that secret, really, but no one here wants to mess with Myklukha.
But the mutants don’t give a damn about Myklukha and his crew. One of those freaks took over the cave, and Freedom lost their stash.
They won’t risk going in themselves, so they hired us. But I swear, I ain’t settin' foot in that forest ever again — not even if all of Freedom goes bankrupt.
I’m not gonna try to talk you out of it. I just hope it goes better for you than it did for Gray.
What are you gonna do now?
Why do you care? Poking around, asking questions. I’ll live. But if you keep wandering around these woods, you might not.
Eh, whatever. Maybe the Zone's trying to talk some sense into me. I used to be the hunter, now I'm the prey. Guess it’s time to head to Rostok.
If I’m gonna buy the farm, I’d rather do it making some coupons than chasing after deer tails and old fairy tales.
sid_phrase_E10_MQ01_arhip_lisajnik_0_Bus: Can’t you see I’m busy? Later, okay?
sid_phrase_E10_MQ01_arhip_lisajnik_0_Bye: Make sure you don't get taken for a mutant in these woods.
Feel free to drop by anytime!
sid_phrase_E10_MQ01_arhip_lisajnik_0_Hel: I thought you were a mutant or something. But now I see it’s just you.
Glad you stopped by! What can I do for you?
Looking to buy or sell? Or are you just here to shoot the breeze?
sid_phrase_E10_MQ01_arhip_lisajnik_0_Inf: The Red Forest has been through a lot: the First Disaster, then the Second… and it’ll outlive us all, stalker. Every last one of us.
sid_phrase_E10_MQ01_arhip_lisajnik_0_Tra: Be quick about it. Take what you need and get lost.
If anything goes south, just swing by, and I’ll find a replacement!
Well, that’s what I’ve got. The prices are fair, and the stuff is good.
sid_phrase_E10_MQ01_arl_0_InfoTopic_1_1_: Ever since the Ward showed up in the Zone, going back to the Mainland’s been off the table. We know too much. My only ambition is to outlast that damn colonel, loner.
sid_phrase_E10_MQ01_bolotnyj_doktor_0_In: People who I find and treat often condemn their destiny and the Zone. But action always causes a proportionate reaction. What if stalkers themselves are to blame for the Zone's cruelty?
sid_phrase_E10_MQ01_bolotnyj_doktor_0_Re: We've been interrupted. Let's continue, it's important.
sid_phrase_E10_MQ01_doktor_kajmanov_0_In: People who I find and treat often condemn their destiny and the Zone. But action always causes a proportionate reaction. What if stalkers themselves are to blame for the Zone's cruelty?
sid_phrase_E10_MQ01_doktor_kajmanov_0_NP: He doesn't belong here anymore. There's nothing I can do to help.
sid_phrase_E10_MQ01_doktor_kajmanov_0_Re: We've been interrupted. Let's continue, it's important.
sid_phrase_E10_MQ01_sersen_0_HelloTopic_: What? You got nothing else to do?
sid_phrase_E10_MQ01_sersen_0_InfoTopic_1: I don’t buy these “legends.” Strelok, the Doctor… I heard all sorts of stories about the Zone back on the Mainland. And what’s it really like? Your guts on a tree — there’s your happy ending.
sid_phrase_E10_MQ01_sersen_0_InfoTopic_2: I was a park ranger back on the Mainland, protecting the four-legged beasts from the two-legged ones. Then I came to the Zone, but the critters here are pretty good at protecting themselves.
sid_phrase_E10_MQ02_Comment_KaimanovInte: I need to clean this place up… myself too.
Please hurry. If Valentyn was right… it could change everything.
sid_phrase_E10_MQ02_Comment_Monolith_Hel: Monolith, help our brethren in their recovery!
Your enemies are many, and every brother is invaluable.
sid_phrase_E10_MQ02_Comment_Monolith_Los: What’s my name? I keep forgetting it… maybe I never even knew it...
sid_phrase_E10_MQ02_Comment_Monolith_Rep: Skirmish at the Boundary. Casualties among the brothers. The wounded will be delivered in time.
Copy that. I will make sure they get the opportunity to avenge their injuries.
Skirmish at the Boundary. Casualties among the brothers. The wounded will be delivered in time.
Copy that. I will make sure they get the opportunity to avenge their injuries.
sid_phrase_E10_MQ02_Comment_Monolith_SoC: I'm so cold… why am I always so cold?
sid_phrase_E10_MQ02_Comment_Monolith_Why: What am I doing here? Why am I…
It does not matter. The Monolith will show me the way.
sid_phrase_E10_MQ02_Comment_Monolith_Wou: I saw it again! As if I was someone else…
It was just a nightmare. We have no other life beyond our devotion to Him. The Monolith is testing your faith. Do not avert your gaze from His radiance.
What if the treatment fails? How can I serve the Monolith without my arm?
If you have faith, it will succeed. And if your faith isn’t strong enough… I will help you find redemption for that sin. Without needless suffering.
sid_phrase_E10_MQ02_Comment_PortnoyPray_: May your light shine down on the souls of the brave, who gave their lives in service to your will.
Reveal the cunning plans of your enemies to us.
Give us the power to avenge our fallen brethren.
Those in eternal union with the Monolith can never be stopped!
sid_phrase_E10_MQ02_Dialog_DrKaimanov_Ab: A Zone open to… everyone? That sounds kinda dangerous.

That’s because it’s the only way you see it — frightened and caged, born amid evil and violence. What else could the Zone have evolved into in a place where both humans and nature are nothing more than test subjects?
If it had been born into freedom, like Tiger, it might have evolved differently – into a Zone of creation rather than destruction.
And then we might have become different too. We always had the ability to overcome the circumstances. Access to the Noosphere would’ve made us better, even if it meant a rough start.
Understanding and forgiving each other is a difficult endeavor.
Why did you refuse to get in the pod?
Because I’ve always held on to my belief in humanity. My “colleagues,” on the other hand, advocated for “fixing” it through the Noosphere, purging it of “darkness.”
How could I consent to such a notion? How could I permit them to rob all of humanity not just of its ability to act freely, but even to think independently?
Everything “dark” and “light” about us is a result of our own choices. The Zone was born in a place of tragedy and horrific experiments, but I believe in a different Zone – a Zone open to everyone.
What do you know about Subtle Matter?
It was a theory developed by Valentyn Dalin, the leader of C-Consciousness. Apparently, he’s now become the image of the Representative.
Back in the day, he ridiculed my calculations regarding the existence of the Noosphere, while I firmly believed that Subtle Matter was unscientific.
In the end, I turned out to be right. But if he was also close to the truth, that changes everything.
According to his theory, Subtle Matter can preserve a reflection of a person’s mind, their personality. If you’re implying the Representative is real, that means they actually succeeded.
Before me, Strelok was the only one who’d spoken to the Representative. But that was back in 2012, before C-Consciousness died. Strelok managed to reach the center of the Zone and return unscathed.
Then Strelok probably knows more about Subtle Matter than I do — along with the psi-technologies that C-Consciousness has mastered during their six years of frenetic activity.
Chances are, he’s still researching it to this day.
So you were the one who created the Zone?
Me? No, certainly not. The Zone created itself. I merely facilitated its emergence. Although my colleagues and I had different expectations for the outcome of our experiment…
You see these blind dogs? I’ve been trying to tame them for years, but they were born in a lab, where they’ve known nothing but pain and fear.
But Tiger here found me on his own. He just came out of the woods one day and tried to steal a sausage. He was born free and had the freedom to make his own choices, you see?
The Zone is our collective offspring, but all we’ve imparted to it is suffering and sorrow. It doesn’t know any other way. Maybe we’re the same.
sid_phrase_E10_MQ02_Dialog_DrKaimanov_CC: The scientists you mentioned, C-Consciousness. They’re still alive.
No. That can’t be… Strelok dealt with them back in 2012!
I spoke to the Representative through the Visiograph in Malachite. They still exist in some kind of form he called “Subtle Matter.”
Subtle Matter? But that’s impossible! That means… it’s still going on.
sid_phrase_E10_MQ02_Dialog_DrKaimanov_Do: Thanks for your help. Whatever happened to “life is the only thing we truly possess”?
Frankly, choosing between the two of you wasn’t easy. Wasn’t my first time, but it only gets harder over the years.
But if you don’t make the choice, someone else will make it for you. And it usually ends up being worse.
I’m not fond of thinking for others or giving advice, but I get the feeling Strider would approve of the choice I made – however strange it might sound.
sid_phrase_E10_MQ02_Dialog_DrKaimanov_Ei: The human brain is too frail to handle a direct connection to the Noosphere on its own. In order for it to work, we required eight participants in pods filled with a specific solution.
I was supposed to be in one of those pods, but I just couldn’t bring myself to do it. Valentyn reproached me for my lack of courage, but given how things turned out, I was actually too daring.
In the end, what we got wasn’t control over the Noosphere, but rather the Zone itself – the lesser of two evils, if you ask me.
sid_phrase_E10_MQ02_Dialog_DrKaimanov_No: Immortality in the Noosphere? No, it’s gotta be something else.
Dealing with “something else” is what science in the Zone is all about. So let’s take a more critical approach and apply the method of exclusion.
But keep in mind that this isn’t just about science, stalker. It’s about the fate of the Zone… and your own role in it.
sid_phrase_E10_MQ02_Dialog_DrKaimanov_Ok: I want to find out if Subtle Matter is real. Things like this shouldn’t be left to blind faith.
The scientific method isn’t just about the experiment, it’s about being able to replicate it. And I happen to know how to test a few of my hunches.
If only Valentyn could see me now… Well, perhaps he can.
sid_phrase_E10_MQ02_Dialog_DrKaimanov_St: That’s why I’m looking for Strelok. Word is, he’s back in the Zone.
I haven’t seen him in quite some time. Maybe that’s for the best. Our perspectives on the Zone have probably developed quite differently over the years.
If your information is correct, I doubt he’s ventured too far from the Cordon. Men like him tend to develop a deep attachment to a place they regard as their own and refuse to let go.
sid_phrase_E10_MQ02_Dialog_DrKaimanov_go: We tried to connect to the Noosphere, as an experiment, in the Orbita underground lab. Back then, I saw nothing like the afterlife, but now, with the Zone…
I think the truth is worth taking a risk. But first, you need to get the cartridge from the Signature station in a different lab – X3 near the Cordon.
It’s been twenty years and the entrances I knew of are long destroyed. But stalkers always find a way. Talk to Sidorovich, he’s in charge of the Cordon and could’ve heard such stories of X3.
