I’ll send you the code to the cartridge slot. Take it and head to Orbita. Good luck.
sid_phrase_E10_MQ02_Dialog_DrKaimanov_se: And so you’ve solved yet another mystery of the Zone, stalker. Perhaps the most unsettling one.
sid_phrase_E10_MQ02_Dialog_DrKaimanov_tr: Dr. Kaymanov. Pleased to meet you.
sid_phrase_E10_MQ02_Dialog_KaimanovCall_: Get under the dome and head to the basement. Waiting for you there.
sid_phrase_E10_MQ02_Dialog_Kaimanov_Asks: Tell me… did you kill them there, at the hospital?
I did what I had to do.
Of course. We all do what we must.
Thank you. I understand it wasn’t easy, but… thank you. It’s important to remember that not all of us are like these caged dogs. We can make our own choices.
sid_phrase_E10_SQ01_Comment_Arhip_RadioQ: You’d have made a fine hunter, loner. Too bad it didn’t work out that way.
You know, Gray would’ve appreciated what you did. You finished his last job. Now he can rest in peace.
sid_phrase_E10_SQ01_Dialog_Arhip_StartSe: What about Gray’s job? Is it still good?
Yeah, but there’s no one left to do it. Freedom stashed their loot in a boar cave to keep stalkers out. But then another mutant moved in and chased off both the boars and Freedom.
If you can take it out, the reward is yours. But watch out, Freedom’s stingy with their money. If they’re willing to pay and risk their stash, that means that the critter is a serious threat.
I guess Freedom can handle it. Let’s talk about something else.
I’m not gonna try to talk you out of it. I just hope it goes better for you than it did for us.
sid_phrase_E10_SQ01_Dialog_Arhip_StartWi: You said Gray was doing some kind of job. Is the job still good?
sid_phrase_E11_MQ01_Comment_Kajmanov_Orb: Skif, it’s Kaymanov. Did you make it into X3?
What about Signature station? Is the data still there?
I’ve got it. It wasn’t quite what I expected.
Yes, it’s just a cartridge. Take it to Orbita. I’ve sent you the coordinates. We’re getting close to the truth, Skif. So very close…
sid_phrase_E11_MQ01_Comment_Merc2_NeedTo: I need a rope and a clip, guys. Quick!
The fucker got Butterball real bad. We gotta get him out of here before he passes out.
Copy that! I’ll be there in two minutes, Vulture.
sid_phrase_E11_MQ01_Comment_Merc_Ambush_: You’ve got company, Vulture. A loner.
Okay. We’ll give him a warm welcome.
sid_phrase_E11_MQ01_Comment_Merc_Correct: I told you, Dark’s at his place. Down the hall and to the right.
sid_phrase_E11_MQ01_Comment_Merc_Looting: Check out the one in the corner.
He’s after the PDAs of everyone who works with Dark. Why disappoint him?
sid_phrase_E11_MQ01_Comment_Merc_Warning: Well lookee here, Screamer! Quite the curious stalker we got here, huh?
Curiosity killed the stalker, but not this one…yet.
sid_phrase_E11_MQ01_Comment_Roosevelt_Di: What the fuck is that? Where’d you dig this thing up?
You’ll end up with a bullet in your dome if this thing jams in a fight!
Clean it up! You got the whole arsenal tonight. And if I spot even one speck of dust… Ugh, what a dumbass!
sid_phrase_E11_MQ01_Comment_RuzveltRadio: We’ve got you in our sights, stalker.
You want to talk? Keep your cool, and the guys will let you in. You have my word.
You weren't so quiet this time, Skif. My men have had you in their crosshairs for a while.
You want to talk? Back at the Commissary, we managed to part on good terms. Maybe we can do that again. Come on in. Keep your cool, and the guys will let you pass. You have my word.
sid_phrase_E11_MQ01_Comment_RuzveltThank: Sidorovich put quite a price tag on our fuckup. But hey, we’re all still breathing.
I keep my word too. Take these coordinates. Anything you find, it’s all yours. Appreciate it.
sid_phrase_E11_MQ01_Comment_RuzveltWelco: Zero discipline. And I can’t say I’m thrilled to see you either, Skif.
I know your type. Wherever you go, death follows.
sid_phrase_E11_MQ01_Comment_Temnyj_CantO: Tsk-tsk, stalker. That’s wasn't our deal.
Cleanup first. The rest can wait.
I know you’re getting off on this. There's only a few left, so enjoy it while you can.
sid_phrase_E11_MQ01_Comment_Temnyj_Doors: Just one more push, and we’re there!
sid_phrase_E11_MQ01_Comment_Temnyj_IfSki: See? I told you, you can't open it without me. It’s got a secret lock.
sid_phrase_E11_MQ01_Comment_Temnyj_Pseud: Get that fucker closer to me. I’m up here. I’ll cover you.
sid_phrase_E11_MQ01_Comment_Temnyj_SaveM: Hey, you’re pretty good at this. We make a hell of a team.
Keep going. I’m almost there. If you make it, I’ll owe you one.
sid_phrase_E11_MQ01_Comment_Temnyj_There: It’s no good, Skif. It’s rusted shut.
All right. I’ll take the main entrance, and you find the back door. It’s gotta be somewhere near you. I’ll be in touch. Once I reach the door, I’ll need you. We can’t get it open without the decoder.
sid_phrase_E11_MQ01_Comment_Thugs_AboutH: Looks like we can kiss the dough goodbye, bro. I told ya – never take a gig unless they pay up front!
Don’t worry, the clock’s tickin’. We’re over here fuckin’ around, while his debt’s gettin’ bigger. If this creep doesn’t show up, we’ll just sell his package to someone else.
Must be a pretty decent gizmo if he’s payin’ that much for it.
sid_phrase_E11_MQ01_Dialog_Kit_AboutRedM: I’m interested in the old lab here at the Cordon. Maybe one of your patients said something about it?
They say all sorts of things, especially when the strong painkillers kick in.
I’ve heard about the lab too. Just a few months back, Red got woozy after a shot and started babbling about embryos, test tubes, Snorks… “Stay away from it,” he kept saying.
Man, that was quite a side effect. I even had to double the dose in his IV bag the next day. Messed him up pretty bad.
Oh, he’s a legend around here. I’m surprised you haven’t heard of him.
He saved Strelok himself back in the day — yanked him out of a death truck, barely alive, and dragged him over to Sidorovich.
He can spin that tale for you himself... when he gets back, that is.
He’s got a schedule. Two weeks out in the Zone, then a couple days here. You won’t believe the stories he has to tell about his adventures.
He’s running late this time, though. I haven’t seen him in about three weeks.
Do you think the lab story was just gibberish?
I’m pretty sure. Trust me, I’m a doctor. Given the state Red was in, I had to pump him full of painkillers. Textbook side effect.
Besides, I asked him about the lab after he came to.
He was quite surprised to find out he told me about it earlier. Red’s a terrible liar, mind you. Blushes right away. It’s just his nature.
sid_phrase_E11_MQ01_Dialog_Merc_WhoAreYo: Who the hell are you, anyway?
The cat in the fucking hat.
It’s pretty simple, loner. We ask, and you answer. What are you doin’ here?
Looks like an interesting place. I was hoping to find some loot.
Whatever loot we have, it definitely ain’t yours.
Now scram, loner. Go look for trinkets somewhere else.
Is Dark around? I need him.
Hey, Screamer! Have you seen Dark?
He’s probably at his place. Come on, let him in. He’s a big boy, he can find his way around.
Where do you think you’re going, loner?
sid_phrase_E11_MQ01_Dialog_Multik_AboutS: Have you heard anything about the old lab at the Cordon? Gossip? Rumors?
Well, a couple of years back, I got a weird order from a rookie. Wanted an electronic lockpick.
He’d just gotten to the Cordon and stumbled across an unusual door. He said the lock was based on some kinda old electronics. Wanted my help unlocking the place, or whatever.
So you made a lockpick for him? Something like this one?
Uh, nope. That’s way out of my league.
Mine was way simpler, more like a stun gun. Zaps the circuit boards, and bam! You’re in.
Did he mention the location of this door?
Why would I risk my neck? Here in the Zone, all you get behind doors like that is teeth and claws. Tried and tested.
Do you know where that stalker is now?
Nope. He took the lockpick, and that was the last I saw of him.
I reckon he’s lying somewhere just on the other side of that door. When things go well, my customers usually come back.
sid_phrase_E11_MQ01_Dialog_Roosevelt_Non: Sidorovich sent me. You’ve got his package.
So that was Sidorovich’s courier? Fuck! I had a feeling the job was fishy.
Fair enough. I’ll pass your apology on to Sidorovich.
Then it’s all yours. You sort it out with him, and I’ll owe you one. You have my word.
You’re in trouble. You shouldn’t have messed with someone else’s courier.
Are you trying to lecture me? Here in the Zone, whatever isn’t nailed down is mine. That’s how it’s always been.
You’re not in a position to make demands. Give me the package.
So that’s how it is, eh? But the package is worth something, and your life ain’t.
Hand over the package, and we won’t have a problem.
Yeah, of course... on one condition.

If you’re here, that means we’re on Sidorovich's naughty list. The last thing I need now is a war with him. I gotta protect my guys — they’re all I’ve got. The Monolith took the rest of 'em out at Garbage.
That’s why I’d rather have a sit-down with Sidorovich. If you put in a good word for us, I’m sure we can come to an arrangement.
We’ve got our eyes on you, kid. Keep your finger off the trigger. Who are you? What’s your business here?
Who tipped you off about the courier?
I’m just trying to make ends meet after the Monolith slaughtered my men at Garbage. No questions asked.
If somebody pays, I’ll take the job. All I care about is coupons, not names.
sid_phrase_E11_MQ01_Dialog_Ruzvelt_About: We’ve got no reason to shoot at each other right now. Make sure Sidorovich knows that.
And add something of your own. Something eloquent.
sid_phrase_E11_MQ01_Dialog_Ruzvelt_MyDam: Well, whose pretty little toes have I stepped on this time?
I doubt you’re here to catch up with an old friend.
You crossed Sidorovich. And he’s not happy.
Fuck! I had a hunch that courier job was fishy.
But, you know, in times like these, you gotta do what you gotta do to survive.
Fair enough. Hand me the package, and I’ll pass your apology on to Sidorovich.
Here. You sort it out with him, and I’ll owe you one. You have my word.
You’re not in a position to make demands, Roosevelt. Just give me the cargo.
Not in a position, ha? The Zone is my witness, I tried to settle this quietly. But I guess it just wasn’t meant to be.
Who tipped you off about the courier?
I couldn’t care less about names these days, Skif. Only coupons. And that was one sweet gig.
Yeah, I shoved my ethics up my ass, imagine that. And I don’t regret it one bit.
Besides, I owe it to my guys. They didn’t ditch me at the Commissary when I was down. They might be no different from Rodents, but I’m just like them now. At least I’m alive.
How did you end up here? A stickup? Sounds too petty for someone like you.
I had to move here. It was the will of the fucking Monolith.
You have no idea what those whackjobs did at Garbage. We ain’t even human beings to them, Skif. We’re just filthy rodents that need a bullet or a boot to get out of their way.
I need the cargo that Sidorovich’s courier had with him, Roosevelt.
Sure, I’ll hand it over… on one condition.
The last thing I need right now is a war with Sidorovich, but after this, he’ll probably shoot me on sight. However, he might be willing to talk about our fate with you.
You’re in trouble, Roosevelt. You shouldn’t have messed with someone else’s courier.
Are you trying to lecture me? Here in the Zone, whatever isn’t nailed down is mine. That’s how it’s always been.
No deal, then.
sid_phrase_E11_MQ01_Dialog_SidorovichAbo: The bandits don’t want to start a war with you. They said they didn’t know the courier was yours, and they’re hoping you’ll understand.
They realized whose pie they stuck their fingers in, eh?
Fine, they can live. I'll find some use for those idiots.
Guess I shouldn’t ask what happened to the bandits, huh?
You were looking for a package? I got it. Now tell me everything you know about X3.
I thought you had everything under control here.
I do. You did the job in the end, didn’t you?
And those couriers… if they stopped getting' into trouble, I wouldn’t be able to sleep at night. ’Cuz it just wouldn’t feel like the Zone anymore.
The bandits at the station have been taken care of. They won’t bother you anymore.
Looks like you’re not afraid to get your hands dirty. I’ll remember that. It’s for the best, though. I ain’t gonna shed a tear for ’em.
So, you wanted to hear about X3. All right. Here’s the scoop.
Not long ago, a little birdie told me the Swamps were swarming with folk looking for artifacts. All because someone found a way into an old base, proving the rumors right.

The bald forest prick kept bragging about how business was booming. I was intrigued. So I started digging into our local goss. And the most interesting rumor I heard was the Oasis. Right here at the Cordon.
You’re a crafty guy, Skif. This is the second time you’ve jumped into a risky deal, and yet you’re still in one piece. A rare talent. Folks like you are where Zone legends come from.
I knew a stalker once, called the Marked One. Much like you, he jumped into all kinds of risky gigs, no questions asked. A valuable and rare talent. Folks like that are where Zone legends come from.
Are you suggesting I find the entrance myself? Where?
Not just suggesting. If we’re the only ones who know the way to the Oasis, that information could be worth a pretty penny.
As for where… ask the locals. Someone might’ve heard more than I. Sometimes even campfire gossip ain’t all bullshit.
The Oasis? What’s that?
A stalker’s dream come true. A Klondike with more artifacts than you can shake a stick at.
Imagine the opportunities if this legend happens to be the real deal.
Somebody who's got “full of shit” written all over his face.
He was just messing with me. And when the time came to back it up, he vanished. Poof! Gone for good.
Who told you about the Oasis, anyway?
What is the link between the Oasis and X3?
I've seen some documents where it was listed under the number three. Oasis - three, your X - also three. See where I'm going?
We're looking for the same thing. Or, at least, for something closely connected. Because, according to those documents. the only way to the Oasis is through the abandoned laboratory.
But here's the catch. We have the key to the door — the decoder you took from the bandits. But where is this door? Hell knows.
sid_phrase_E11_MQ01_Dialog_SidorovichInf: I need to get to X3. Do you know where it is?
I am  busting my ass trying to find it, and you waltz in here expecting me to serve it to you on a silver platter?
How the hell did you get word of it?
Okay, I’ll get your delivery back. Where did your people find the courier again?
Not far from here, near the old vehicle station.
Just make sure the package gets here in one piece. As for the bandits, do whatever you want. If you take ’em out, I might even add a tip.
All right, let’s cut to the chase. I don’t give a damn why the hell you want X3. But since we’re both in the know now, how about a little swap? I’ll tell you what I know, and you do me a favor. Deal?
The Doctor told me about it.
You’re quite a well-connected stalker. If it’s the Doctor himself, those rumors must be true after all.
It doesn’t matter. You've got your sources, and I've got mine.
I know all about your sources. "Stalker-FM". You think you’re the first guy at the Cordon to yap about this stuff?
Who are these thugs? How many, and what guns are they carrying?
Some douchebags from Garbage, I guess. Looks like they figured they could wait Monolith out at my Cordon.
But they forgot one thing: in the Zone, you can get killed anywhere, especially if you rain on your host’s parade.
What do you want me to do?
I need you to retrieve a package for me.
Some thugs offed the courier who was supposed to bring it to me. My men found him dead, and the package was missing.
I really don’t like folks sticking their ugly noses in my business. Do you catch my drift?
Is there something of value in that package?
I’m not interested in cheap junk.
Return the package, and I’ll make sure it’s worth your while.
You got any gossip about X3? I’m all ears.
Let’s put it this way: if these stories pan out, it’s a win for both of us. The Cordon and me.
That’s all you need to know for now.
sid_phrase_E11_MQ01_Dialog_Strelok_After: The Doctor told me about X3. It seems like the two of you have some history. He sent me here to test a theory.
Kaymanov? Yeah, he helped me get to the center of the Zone to take down C-Consciousness.

Now he’s using other people to fix his own mistakes... How convenient. He used to be part of C-Consciousness. He would’ve gotten into one of those pods himself if he hadn’t escaped at the last minute.
What kind of nonsense did he feed you? Something about the old experiments of the dearly departed, and the data that might still be here?
Induced illusion? Like Faust’s phantoms?
Like the phantoms, the Wish Granter, the Visiographs, the Scorcher…
Or the Shining Zone. Do you want a peek? It’s right here. You can grab the data the Doctor wants along the way. Just be sure to shut it down on your way out.
Why are you here, Skif?
Dark had no choice, unlike you. Who are you working for? If you lie, I’ll know it.
Have you ever heard of the Subtle Matter theory? Kaymanov wants to test it.
Immortality in the Noosphere? The C-Consciousness scientists are dead. I killed them. All of them. And took a piece of glass from one of their pods as a keepsake.
It’s real. And so were the bullets. Subtle Matter is an induced illusion, a psi-radiation technology C-Consciousness used to manipulate people.
So the mercs are your friends, then? You’re playing dirty. You put a hit on Dark… and maybe others, too?
When you’re fighting against everyone, you use what you’ve got. They were my backup plan. Since you’re here, it failed. The Zone is no stranger to death.
I talked to the Representative of C-Consciousness. In Malachite.
They’re dead. I killed them... all of them. I even kept a piece of glass from one of their pods. It was real.
What you witnessed was an induced illusion, a psi-radiation technology C-Consciousness used to manipulate people.
I’m with Spark. They know what’s really going on in the Zone. Maybe they can help me find a new place to call home, too.
You’re like a moth drawn to a flame, ready to get burned to ashes.
Scar’s trained to follow orders. Clear Sky used to call the shots. That's when he almost got me.
But who’s he answering to now? I don’t think you bothered to ask.
At least with Scar, I know what he stands for. But you? I have no idea what you want.
I want the Zone to be left alone.
All that common good Scar and Korshunov talk about is just a smokescreen. What they really want is to control the Zone, each in his own way.
No. The Zone doesn’t owe anyone a thing. Realizing this made me an enemy to both the Ward and Spark. And how exactly did you find out about X3?
What about Dark? What did you do to him?
I didn't do anything. It was the “TVs” – they're neurovisual programmers.
Those previously affected by them can’t survive another exposure.
What do you mean, Dark had no choice?
He’s a C-Consciousness Agent. Killing me was just a program planted in his head.
Those who made it to the center of the Zone got the short end of the stick. Their “prize” was either to join Monolith and protect C-Consciousness or become an Agent and do their bidding.
The STALKER Program. That’s what C-Consciousness called it.
I’m with the Ward. I want to deal with the Zone once and for all… and make sure my new home is safe.
So you want to keep the Zone in check? Along with everything that comes with it?
The Ward are just SIRCAA’s lapdogs. And I know exactly what the Institute’s up to.
Want me to show you my SIRCAA advisor ID?
At least with the Ward, I know what they stand for. But you? I have no idea what you want.
I’ll get in touch when I figure out if you’re worth it. Not if, but when.
And remember, either you’re against everyone now, or I’m against you. The choice is yours. That’s the price of my candor.
So the Shining Zone is a lie?
I saw it when I took down C-Consciousness. It seemed… better. So bright, so real… or so I believed.
I spent years wandering this Zone in search of its Shining counterpart. I suppose that’s when Scar caught wind of it.
But my Shining Zone turned out to be… well, you saw what it was. Just another illusion. Another lie. And the one he's looking for — it never existed in the first place.
Is there anything I can do to help?
Not now, I got this covered. Just focus on what you need to do.
But keep in mind that the Ward and Spark are my enemies.
If they end up being yours too, we’ll get along just fine.
Dr. Kaymanov reached out to me. He asked me to take the cartridge to Orbita Station.
Kaymanov is an old asshole. He’s always had a mind of his own. He knew the C-Consciousness experiments were fishy all along. But instead of stopping them, he decided to see how things played out.
Still, the Zone ended up teaching him something after all: the only way to understand it is by taking action.
So, Orbita it is. Maybe you’ll find the kind of truth that suits you best there.
There can only be one truth.
Then you don’t know the Zone well enough.
So what’s your next move, then?
Keep searching. Right now I’ve got to find a way into the STALKER Program Control Center. The neuroprogrammers in X3 should be connected to it.
I just need to figure out how.
The STALKER Program? What’s that?
It lets you manipulate people’s minds. A C-Consciousness invention.
Those who made it to the center of the Zone got the short end of the stick. Their “prize” was either to join Monolith and protect C-Consciousness or become an Agent and do their bidding.
The programming only malfunctioned once — with me. But I paid a price for it: my own memory.
When I found this place, I realized the Zone can’t be true to herself as long as someone keeps trying to use her to fucking brainwash us.
Whether through “TVs” like these, or the STALKER Program before them.
I just wish I knew who that “someone” is right now.
Not everyone can handle what you’re proposing.
This is the only way to the truth I can offer you.
sid_phrase_E11_MQ01_Dialog_TakeBack_take: I found a way into Lab X3. Marker coordinates sent.
You are oddly cooperative. No haggling, no fuss... I take it you didn’t find the Klondike then?
Depends. Whatever you’re looking for, it might be there. But taking it with you is a whole different story.
Unless you grab the psi installation too. Your Oasis is nothing but a mirage. And I shut it down anyway.
Oh, I see. But let’s keep it quiet. No need for anyone else to know, you dig?
Like I told you before, the way to the Oasis is worth a pretty kopiyka. You have earned your reward.
sid_phrase_E11_MQ01_Dialog_Temnyj_AboutO: Wow, that was fun. That smell really takes me back – I had a cap gun when I was a kid. A Mauser.
So who might you be, unknown gunslinger?
I’m Skif. And you must be the guy who knows where to find the entrance to the old lab at the Cordon.
Wow. Not much for foreplay. You must be from the Oasis cult. So skilled, and so stupid. Who actually trusts Sidorovich?
How do you know Sidorovich never got his package?
If I ask someone and insist, that’s usually how it goes.
I gotta pick it up, by the way. It nearly slipped my mind, with these mercs and all.
You mean the decoder? I've got it right here.
You’re a very resourceful guy, Skif. And you’re alive, unlike all my men.
Here’s the deal: the lab’s gonna be our joint venture. It’s a real maze, but I’ve got a map of it. Right here, in my head. In case you’re thinking about shooting me and taking it.
Besides, there’s one lock down there you can’t open without me, not even with a decoder. I guarantee you won’t get through it alone. Deal?
Any idea who paid the mercs to kill you? The thing is, you’re not the first person we’ve both been chasing.
You’re one intriguing guy. Where did our paths cross before?
Last time was in the Red Forest. I was looking for the Doctor.
Whoa! Can you introduce me later? I’m wicked eager to meet his friend, but he’s been ghosting me. Whenever I ask him out, he always sends someone else instead. Like these guys.
All right. I’m in.
You bet you are. Don’t expect it to be a walk in the park, though.
Head to these coordinates. There’s something you need to pick up. It was meant for the bandits, but we need it more now.
I’ll be waiting for you at the lab entrance. There’s a tracker in the stash. When you grab the stuff, you’ll get the location.
Where’d you find the Oasis documents?
I snagged them from an old gig. But I was sick of it anyway. Playing guerrilla with the Ward bored me to tears.
The documents were just lying around.
There’s a lot of info there about the Oasis – three Oases, in fact. These are just code names, though. Stalkers made up the rest of the story.
You think Sidorovich is lying?
Only when he opens his mouth. How do you think he found out about the Oasis? Stalker gossip? Maybe. That, and the documents I showed him.
But as soon as our deal was all worked out, I was suddenly no longer welcome in his little village.
But the old fox’s tricks are wearing thin. That’s why the lab entrance I told him about was bullshit — unlike the key in that package that never made it to his bunker.
Why do you want to get into the lab?
“Why” questions are of a more personal nature, and our relationship is strictly business. That’s why I’m not asking you why.
sid_phrase_E11_MQ01_Dialog_Temnyj_AlarmO: Yes! The gate's opening! What’s that sound over there?
I’ve got company, Dark. And the door won’t open. Fuck…
sid_phrase_E11_MQ01_Dialog_Temnyj_Connec: Where are you freaks coming from?!
Open up… I said open up!
sid_phrase_E11_MQ01_Dialog_Temnyj_DoorCo: Fuck whoever designed this whole damn place.
Your door has a double security system. I had to confirm from this end that I see you out there. It's a good thing we split up.
Quiet now? Give the decoder another shot. It has to work this time.
sid_phrase_E11_MQ01_Dialog_Temnyj_OpenSk: I'm looking at what's gotta be the biggest blast door in the whole Zone, Skif. If it's rusted too, we’re shit out of luck.
You okay? Is the decoder ready?
Yeah, I’m at the entrance. Let’s get this done.
sid_phrase_E11_MQ01_Dialog_Temnyj_Pseudo: You can’t get in… can’t get in without me…
I’m here, Dark. You all right?
Finally! I’m pinned down, Skif. There’s too many of them. But we’ve got a shot.
We need to unlock all the doors. Look around for the security console — it’s on the lower levels. After you use it, I’ll make my move. Remember, you won’t make it without me.
sid_phrase_E11_MQ01_Dialog_Temnyj_Reunit: I don’t believe in luck, but I’ll be damned if you aren’t a lucky charm.
This is just what I need right now. Open it. I’ve got your back.
sid_phrase_E11_MQ01_Dialog_Temnyj_TurnOn: No rush – we can’t take the elevator anyway. The power’s out.
According to the map, the breaker is just behind this wall. Get there through the vent, and then you can go back through the door. It’s connected to the same circuit as the elevator.
sid_phrase_E11_MQ01_Dialog_Volk_AboutTem: I hear there was an old lab at the Cordon. I want to know more about it.
What’s so special about that place? First that Dark guy, now you…
I’ll give you the same advice I gave him: stay away.
So the lab does exist. What do you know about it?
Everyone who thought they could strike it rich there didn’t even need a burial.
There was nothing left to bury.
Anyway, do you know where the entrance is?
From what I know, it’s a pretty huge facility. Part of it might even extend beyond the Cordon.
But all the entrances we used to know are closed off now. Some of them got wrecked by anomalies, and others just caved in over time.
It’s for the best, honestly. I sleep better when there are fewer jackasses going in and fewer freaks coming out.
A seasoned stalker, by the looks of it. But there was something rotten about him, if you ask me. He roped in some rookies with big promises, but never said a word about the actual deal.
Then he had a long chat with Sidorovich. Judging from their sour mugs, they clearly didn’t see eye to eye.
In the end, I made it clear he’d overstayed his welcome. But if you’re looking for this Dark guy, here’s the coordinates. You can figure out if he’s a rat or my gut’s off.
sid_phrase_E11_MQ01_bora_koleso_0_Defeat: You piece of shit! I’m gonna bury you!
sid_phrase_E11_MQ01_bora_koleso_0_HelloT: Why, hello there. How are you?
sid_phrase_E11_MQ01_bora_koleso_0_InfoTo: Man, this place is boring. We had it sweet over at Garbage. Back then, if you wanted to play cards, you’d slap your AK mag on the table and let the good times roll.
Now it only costs you a single round! That’s how strapped we are. But hey, at least we’re still kicking. Can’t say the same for a lot of folks over at the Commissary.
sid_phrase_E11_MQ01_bora_koleso_0_Resume: We weren’t done, huh? All right. Go on.
sid_phrase_E11_MQ01_dzim_0_DefeatComment: I’m gonna gut you for this.
sid_phrase_E11_MQ01_dzim_0_InfoTopic_1_I: The less you know, the better my chances of sticking around a bit longer. That’s how it goes with stalkers. I don't wanna be your next campfire story.
sid_phrase_E11_MQ01_dzim_0_ResumeComment: Okay, let’s get back to it…
sid_phrase_E11_MQ01_fima_semafor_0_BusyT: Back off, huh? I’m askin’ you nicely.
sid_phrase_E11_MQ01_fima_semafor_0_InfoT: It just kind of all came crashing down. My buddy took a Monolithian bullet, and I ended up with Roosevelt. And his rules are worse than ten years in maximum security.
sid_phrase_E11_MQ01_fima_semafor_0_Resum: So, what were you sayin’ before?
sid_phrase_E11_MQ01_krikun_0_InfoTopic_1: No good news. And there won’t be, not as long as Korshunov’s still alive. I got a real score to settle with him, and I hope to square it someday. Seems like that’s where things are headed now.
sid_phrase_E11_MQ01_krikun_0_ResumeComme: Let me finish so I won’t forget it.
sid_phrase_E11_MQ01_mona_0_BusyTopic_Bus: Later, man. Don’t bother me now.
sid_phrase_E11_MQ01_mona_0_DefeatComment: Spare a bro a medkit, guys!
sid_phrase_E11_MQ01_mona_0_HelloTopic_He: You wanna chat? Sure, why not?
sid_phrase_E11_MQ01_mona_0_InfoTopic_1_I: I’m all right, bro. These days, being alive and kickin’ is good news.
sid_phrase_E11_MQ01_mona_0_ResumeComment: Go ahead. Now where were we?
sid_phrase_E11_MQ01_ruzvelt_0_InfoTopic_: It's all gonna be new now, Skif. I gotta rebuild from the ground up. Honestly, when you're fifty, the prospect of going from rags to riches all over again feels less than thrilling.
sid_phrase_E11_MQ01_ruzvelt_0_ResumeComm: We got interrupted. Let’s get back to it.
sid_phrase_E11_MQ01_strelok_0_InfoTopic_: Freedom, Duty, the Ward, Spark… there’s always someone trying to twist the Zone to their own ends. Their reasons may vary, but the end is the same. And I’ve got a bullet for each one of ’em.
sid_phrase_E11_MQ01_strelok_0_NPC_Combat: Where the hell are you shooting at?
I'll smoke him out with a grenade!
They're falling back! It's all good.
If I find you, it’s gonna get worse.
All clear. You can take it easy now.
sid_phrase_E11_MQ01_temnyj_0_BusyTopic_B: Some other time. When things settle down.
sid_phrase_E11_MQ01_temnyj_0_ByeTopic_By: All right, that's it. No need for long farewells.
sid_phrase_E11_MQ01_temnyj_0_DefeatComme: You hit me, but didn't kill me. Why don’t you up your game, you mutt?
sid_phrase_E11_MQ01_temnyj_0_InfoTopic_1: I swung by an old buddy’s place not too long ago. Needed advice on an old gadget. So there we were, shooting the breeze, and their head honcho walks in. Scar, I reckon.
His mug looked real familiar, but I just couldn’t place it. He even gave me a nod like we were old pals. Strange, eh?
sid_phrase_E11_MQ01_temnyj_0_ResumeComme: Right when things were getting interesting…
sid_phrase_E11_MQ02_Audiolog_InstAnt_1_7: In the event of my absence, follow these instructions. Reminder: the antenna is a powerful psi conductor. Being in its immediate vicinity while it is in operation is strictly prohibited.
Activate the antenna on the rooftop. Ensure that there is no biological activity nearby — any interference could damage the antenna.
If the equipment detects such activity, the fuses will blow, cutting off the power supply.
Stand by. The settings are configured to activate the antenna automatically if the aforementioned conditions are met. No manual input required!
sid_phrase_E11_MQ02_Comment_FirstScarGua: If you've got business, take it to Scar. He speaks for all of Spark.
sid_phrase_E11_MQ02_Comment_IskraFight_k: He’s not walking out of here alive!
sid_phrase_E11_MQ02_Comment_KaymanovGive: This is Dr. Kaymanov. Have you managed to get the cartridge?
Before you begin, you’ll need to calibrate the central antenna. It’s nothing too complex, but you’ll need precise instructions.
I used to work at this station, and my old notes might still be around. All the information you need should be in there.
All right. Where do I find them?
They should be in one of the offices. If it’s locked, try the code 1995. Hopefully I remembered it correctly.
sid_phrase_E11_MQ02_Comment_ScarRE_1_639: Get all the info you can out of Strelok. That’s our priority right now.
sid_phrase_E11_MQ02_Comment_SecondScarGu: Scar’s a sharp guy. If you wanna be one of us, you gotta listen to him.
sid_phrase_E11_MQ02_Dialog_AfterTurnOff_: I've tracked the antenna signals. All good. Just a little more to go.
In X15, there's an experimental unit. You need to place the cartridge in the control room, and then - get into the capsule. I'll take care of the connection.
To enter the pod? But why?
Don't worry about the capsule: unlike the C-Conciousness ones, it was designed for one user. I'll take care of the rest.
sid_phrase_E11_MQ02_Dialog_ScarAfterTaki: Now we can go back to our search for a Visiograph. There’s bound to be another one out there in the Zone. Which means we still need Strelok.
Have you been here before?
Old habits die hard, don’t they? Especially those rooted in a lack of faith.
Yes, I’ve been here before — with Ozersky. He couldn’t believe the truth about Subtle Matter either.
Care to tell me how you ended up on Orbita?
I found the Doctor. He said I might find some answers here.
Remember, Skif, everything hinges on how you handle your relationship with the Ward. You’ve seen the truth. Are you ready to embrace it?
I found Strelok. He thinks that all psi-devices are controlled by the STALKER Program.
If that’s true, there could be another Visiograph at the Program Control Center.
Get him to spill everything he knows. He’ll help us reestablish contact with the Representative.
Strelok himself doesn’t need to know that, of course.
Faith requires no answers, Skif. Especially not from these gizmos.
But I’m glad you stayed on the right path. The Shining — that’s the only answer we need.
And those answers have finally steered you towards the right path! Better late than never, right?
It’s a shame it took all those gizmos to sway you. Faith is about believing in spite of something, not because of it.
What did you see while I was in that pod, Scar? Did you hear Strider?
I don’t need to see to believe. Now you believe too. That’s all that matters.
What can you tell me about Strelok?
Just about anyone in the Zone can tell you more about him than I can.
I combed half the Zone looking for him once. That’s all I have to say.
sid_phrase_E11_MQ02_Dialog_ScarAndOzersk: Why do you scientists always have to make things so complicated? Faith is about believing in spite of something, Mr. Ozersky, not because of it. Otherwise, what’s the point?
I need to know for sure.
All right. If you prefer knowing to believing, see for yourself.
Ha! I didn't expect to see a familiar face here. How's it going?
I want to make sure. Tell me this: during our time in the bunker, someone saved you and your crew from a Controller. Who was it?
I’d never forget something like that. It was Degtyarev.
You really are still alive! Unbelievable…
As you can see, Professor, faith is always rewarded. Are you ready to take action?
sid_phrase_E11_MQ02_Dialog_StrelokCallAf: Skif, I've examined the neuroprogrammers, and I have an idea how to find the STALKER Program Control Center.
They are connected to the same device at the Tropospheric Communications Point. If you get there and activate it, I'll get the data I need.
Get going. I'll contact you later.
sid_phrase_E11_MQ03_Comment_KorVartRE_Sp: Take any questions to the colonel.
sid_phrase_E11_MQ03_Comment_StrelokCallB: Judging by the atmospherics, you’ve made it to the TCP.
Listen up. You need to find and activate the neuroprogrammer control panel. Look for a panel with lots of switches.
As soon as you launch them, I’ll be able to calculate the approximate location of the STALKER Program Control Center, the heart of X-Network.
sid_phrase_E11_MQ03_Comment_StrelokCallP: You can pass through an abandoned loading terminal. There is a large anomalous area, patrols stay out of it.
You could use Dark's decoder to open the back door to the terminal.
Got it. Beats going in guns blazing.
When you get to the facility, try to get the Project FIBER files.
I’ve got a Warden pass. I’ll try the front door.
In that case, you can ask your Ward friends to show you the Project FIBER files.
Are you at the TCP yet? Nice and easy. As soon as they see a gun, they'll surround you. You just need to get inside and launch the neuroprogrammers.
sid_phrase_E11_MQ03_Comment_StrelokCallS: I've managed to track the signal down to the Prypiat catacombs. The STALKER Program Control Center must be somewhere there. We need to find and destroy it.
It's Degtyarev's territory. I guess it's time for you to meet him.
But we need to act fast. You've made so much noise that all the Ward dogs will run to the TCP now.
sid_phrase_E11_MQ03_Comment_VartaATK_ATK: No one gets in or out of the facility until the intruder is eliminated!
Intruder alert at the Troposphere Comm Station. Repeat, we have an intruder!
Don’t let him reach the control panel! Eliminate him at all costs!
sid_phrase_E11_MQ03_CutScene_PTS_NOvarta: Skif doesn’t side with the Ward.
sid_phrase_E11_MQ03_Dialog_KorshunovACS_: Strelok mentioned Agents. Who are they?
Stalkers. The cream of the crop. Once they got to the center of the Zone, they ended up in the neuroprogramming lab at the Generators.
That’s where they were programmed by C-Consciousness... and got a “S.T.A.L.K.E.R.” tattoo on their right forearm.
After C-Consciousness died and their psi-installations were shut down, some of the agents woke up and had their "brands" removed. That’s why we can’t catch them anymore.
I want to know more about the Agents.
That’s all I wanted to know about the Agents.
The Ward badge didn't protect me when I asked a “simple question” about Project FIBER. Your people tried to kill me.
But they didn't. Believe me, I'm not happy about what happened here either.
There are Agents outside the Zone? Who are they, and how are they dangerous?
I’m left with more questions than answers myself. Two of them, Chornozem and Nestor, never made it out of the Zone. Had to chill out. In a fridge at the Chemical Plant.
They seem like a new breed tailored for missions on the Mainland. Hell knows what those are. Could be a typical C-Consciousness imperative, like “find and eliminate, whatever it takes.”
We’re working on it. Those stiffs' PDAs are the only ones we managed to hack so far.
Anyway, Agatha is convinced that a man of your skill is essential to the success of our mission. And our mission is the only thing that matters right now.
For the time being, we'll settle for cooperation instead of the gory revenge I would otherwise guarantee you.
Ask away, soldier.
So, Colonel, would you forget that I had to shoot a bunch of your men?
In your dreams, stalker.
Strelok’s putting together a new crew. He's planning to take on both you and Scar for the sake of his Zone.
Alexander the fucking Great! This'll be the end of him, then.
I always knew he was trouble. Even back when he showed up at SIRCAA with that C-Consciousness pod piece of glass and the X7 papers.
And I was right. Six months later, an explosion at X11. Strelok was barely mentioned in the report. New hires have had to deal with extra background checks ever since.
Strelok told me he’s located the STALKER Program Control Center somewhere underground in Prypiat.
That facility manages all of Project X's infrastructure and controls the induced illusions. We have to get there before Strelok does.
Time’s of the essence, Skif.
You think Strelok and Scar are Agents too? But why?
Strelok’s got the tattoo below his elbow, and he’s fixated on an agenda that no one else can understand.
I kept pointing that out to Dalin, but in vain. After some pressure from the brass, he gave him a job at SIRCAA.
Oh, and I appreciate what you did with Scar. Thanks to you, my men found documents at the Clear Sky base that confirm the group’s ties to C-Consciousness.
Anything else, soldier? Make it quick.
What’s our next move, Colonel?
For now, our main focus should be controlling the Anomalous energy. Based on our intel, it looks like the Control Center might give us clues.
But first, we need to get to Prypiat. The only way there goes through Yaniv. Head out right away and contact Degtyarev if you can.
Our forward units are already in Prypiat, but maintaining communication has proven to be... challenging. Locate them, and they’ll help you find the Control Center.
And Skif... in case the Corps starts acting up, I’ve heard of a stalker named Spirit who’s known to help people cross the Boundary. Keep that in mind.
sid_phrase_E11_MQ03_Dialog_KorshunovRE_G: Enough chatter, soldier. Time to focus on the mission.
sid_phrase_E11_MQ03_Dialog_VartaIntercom: Calm down, I’ve got a pass.
Get the fuck outta here already!
You’re trespassing. Leave the facility immediately.
What kind of facility is this?
This is your final warning, stalker. Get out of here!
sid_phrase_E11_MQ03_Dialog_VartaOhr_IDan: All good. What’s the purpose of your visit?
I want to know more about Project FIBER.
sid_phrase_E11_MQ03_lotvin_0_InfoTopic_1: I’m only concerned about the security of this facility. As for the Zone… Let others worry about that.
sid_phrase_E11_MQ04_Comment_AlertOnHimza: Warning! Security protocols breached. Red alert!
sid_phrase_E11_MQ04_Comment_DalinCallsSk: We need to talk. We don’t have much time.
sid_phrase_E11_MQ04_Comment_DalinLeaves_: And Skif? If our paths ever cross again, remember, I’m on your side.
sid_phrase_E11_MQ04_Comment_PrisonerCall: Hey, Skif! Come on over. I promise I won’t bite! They knocked all my teeth out anyway.
sid_phrase_E11_MQ04_Comment_PrisonerHurr: Get moving while the hallway’s clear!
sid_phrase_E11_MQ04_Comment_ScarCallsAbo: I got word from our mutual friends that you’ve found yourself in the Ward’s clutches. That’s good — means you’ve made the right choice.
I hope you don't overstay your welcome and you're gonna catch up soon. We’re in Prypiat, camped out at the Hospital, northern part of the first district.
Head to Yaniv. Deggy'll let you through. We’re waiting for you, and the intel you got from Strelok.
Oh, and if Degtyarev refuses to cooperate, find Spirit. He’s an old mate of mine. He’ll help in any way he can, I’m sure of it.
sid_phrase_E11_MQ04_Comment_SkifGivesKey: I’ll give you a head start. You’ll probably go quietly, but I want the Ward to know I was here. Thanks for not leaving me in the lurch. Zone willing, we’ll meet again.
sid_phrase_E11_MQ04_Comment_SkifGivesNoK: I’m serious, Skif! You know what Korshunov'll do to me! Skif!
sid_phrase_E11_MQ04_Comment_SkifTakesToo: Whatcha starin’ at? This ain’t a fucking museum. Come on over, I said!
sid_phrase_E11_MQ04_Comment_StrelokCalls: Where have you been? Degtyarev's tired of waiting.
Go to Prypiat through Yaniv — the Corps will let you through.
If not, find the stalker called Spirit. He'll help.
sid_phrase_E11_MQ04_Comment_VartaGuardBe: I wonder what Dalin saw at Malachite... he’s like a different person now.
sid_phrase_E11_MQ04_Comment_VartaGuardSt: I gotta renew my contract. I’ve been here for so long, I’m starting to wonder how it’s all gonna end…
sid_phrase_E11_MQ04_Comment_VartaGuardsI: Don’t hold last night against me, okay? I guess my brain got fried too. I bet they’re gonna discharge me now…
The Doc’s been humming lullabies while performing autopsies. Your commander won’t take his body armor off — all stuffed with wire and C4. And look at the colonel…
The only way we're getting discharged is into an open grave.
sid_phrase_E11_MQ04_Comment_VartaGuardsO: Well, I'm relieved. It's too bad about the guys at the Duga, but at least the treaties are over. No more bootlicking.
You’re either with us, or you're dead. Plain and simple. We should’ve done it right away.
Zotov’s dead. Senkevych too. But that bastard Dvoryankov will outlive us both…
He saved my life in the second assault. If it weren’t for his orders, I’d be buried under the Duga. Maybe if you quit swiping rations, they won't have to cut your paycheck.
He was trying to save our equipment, not your ass…
sid_phrase_E11_MQ04_Dialog_DalinHelpsSki: They’re alive too.
And now?
If that’s immortality, it’s frightening. An eternal prison where the mind remains static. I’d rather die and be done with it.
No. That’s not enough. I have to see for myself.
You know, when I was a kid, my father once told me, “For a true scientist, impossible simply means unexplored.” Whenever I faced a problem I couldn’t solve, he’d say, “Dig deeper, Dalin.”
I’ve been to Orbita. Using the prototype pod, I was able to make contact with the Noosphere. While I was there, I talked to Strider. He’s alive, Dalin – alive with no physical body.
Strelok can confirm what I’m saying. The Representative is either an illusion or a psi-imprint of someone who was once alive.
Skif, I know we’ve had different agendas so far, and it might be hard for you to trust me, but I genuinely want to help.
Are you all right? The colonel’s completely lost it. He’s been difficult before, but now he’s gone off the rails completely.
Tossing you in here after all you did for the Ward…
Where am I? Is this the Chemical Plant?
Yes. The isolation unit. The colonel keeps prisoners here.
He had his reasons to throw me in here. I found out about… some things, and I can't keep working with the Ward knowing them.
Oh… What did you find out?
But that’s impossible. Unthinkable. And yet…
I was looking for the Doctor in the Red Forest. Turns out, the man posing as the savior is Dr. Kaymanov — the same guy who refused to go into the eighth pod in 2006.
He told me about your father... and his Subtle Matter theory.
And now I’m beginning to think there are more important things than science.
Richter told me Ozersky and Scar have managed to build a Visiograph in lab X17 at Malachite. He said you had a personal conversation with the Representative. Is that true? Did you speak with him?
It all adds up now… the head of C-Consciousness was Valentyn Dalin. Are you his son?
How did you know that?
It doesn’t matter. I’ve got my sources. I know about C-Consciousness and your father, along with his Subtle Matter theory.
I went to Orbita and ran an experiment with a prototype pod. While I was there, I saw a projection of Strider. It was just a copy, not the man I once knew. Strider’s dead.
How can I be sure of what you’re saying? Do you have any proof?
Are you trying to recruit me again? Don’t bother. I can see right through your Ward bullshit.
I’m here because we have a mutual acquaintance: Richter. He was once a field consultant for SIRCAA. We parted ways because his priority was the Zone, while mine was science.
Which means the Representative…
You’re doing the right thing, Dalin.
I hope so.
Just be careful. There are patrols everywhere. Wait until I’m at a safe distance, then get out of here.
I’m sorry.
I appreciate your help. Like me, you got mixed up with the wrong crowd. But it’s never too late to change things.
I’ll think about it. I promise.
Just don’t get caught, or it will end badly for both of us. Get out once I’m at a safe distance. But stay quiet – there are patrols everywhere. Be careful.
What you shared wasn’t what I had hoped to hear. But I appreciate your candor.
I’ve tried to live by that rule my whole life. And that’s why I want to thank you. You didn’t settle for superficial answers. You dug deeper. Thank you for that, Skif.
Yes. What’s left in the Noosphere is just an imprint of consciousness. A copy. A collection of a person’s memories.
Yes. Everything the Representative said is true. Subtle Matter, the Shining Zone, immortality within the Noosphere – it’s all real.
Have you learned anything new about Subtle Matter?
You’re taking a great risk. Do you realize that?
On a scale from day-to-day work at SIRCAA to the Alpha Artifact experiment, I’ve been through worse.
Just be careful. There are patrols everywhere. Wait until I’m at a safe distance, then get out of here.
And the Ward wants to use these imprints to spy on every single one of us.
What is this place?
The isolation unit at the Chemical Plant. The colonel keeps prisoners here.
Where’s all my stuff?
I don’t know. Sorry. They must have taken it when they searched you.
If my words aren’t enough to convince you, you can reach out to Scar. He figured it out long before I did.
Also, thanks for rescuing me at X11. I haven’t forgotten it.
I’ll give you the key to the cell. You need to get out of here – the sooner, the better.
sid_phrase_E11_MQ04_Dialog_PrisonerAsksF: Don’t scare me like that, eh? I thought you left for real. So, we got a deal? The key for info on your stuff.
I don’t wanna know whatever’s going on between you and that brainiac, turning against his own... fuck, I’m done with all that “mysteries of the Zone” crap.
Here’s the deal: gimme the key, and I’ll tell you where they stashed your stuff.
How long have you been here?
Ever since Malachite. I stayed behind to cover Scar and ended up… sticking around. Eh, whatever. If it helps us find the Shining Zone, then it was worth it.
No, I might still need the key. See you.
Why?! You’re already out! For the Zone’s sake, Skif, don’t just leave me here! They’re gonna kill me!
Here you go. How do you know where my stuff is?
The guards mentioned it. You can overhear a lot if you play dead — including a little chat between a Skif and a Dalin…
They didn’t take your PDA? Then again, who would you even call — Strelok? Here, this is where they stash their stuff. Don't get caught out there.
Hey, I remember you. You’re the one who activated the Duga.
It wasn’t me, it was Ozersky. I was literally just standing there! I knew it wouldn’t end well. But hey, nice job telling Korshunov to fuck off. I can respect that.
sid_phrase_E11_MQ04_zora_mamont_0_InfoTo: The Shining Zone's time is coming, and then the Ward’ll be done for!
sid_phrase_E12_MQ01_Audiolog_Duh_1_70269: The key card to the bunker is in my pocket. The scanner I used to get to Prypiat is inside. It’s not too hard, you’ll figure it out. I left the manual there, too.
sid_phrase_E12_MQ01_Audiolog_Duh_1_70270: Sorry for delivering the message like this. We can’t be sure who's listening in. A delayed one is safer.
sid_phrase_E12_MQ01_Audiolog_Duh_1_70271: When you search my body, don’t be shy, take everything you need. Just make sure you bury me deep, okay? I think I deserve some rest.
sid_phrase_E12_MQ01_Audiolog_Instruction: Got your scanner thing tweaked. You can turn anomalous fields off now. Not all of them, so don't get too excited, or I’d be richer than Myklukha by now. Should do the trick for your Offloading Terminal tunnel.
Pretty easy to use: put the scanner next to a stable anomaly, smash the button, get going. Don't drag your feet: the anomaly isn't gonna stay off for freaking ever. Throw in a couple of bolts, too.
sid_phrase_E12_MQ01_Comment_DuhVarta_hom: It's working, I can feel it. Maybe you’re onto something. Maybe I’ll find my home after all…
sid_phrase_E12_MQ01_Comment_IskraBunker_: Scar used to roll with Topol, right? Why didn’t he just hand us the key card?
“The road to the goal is as important as the goal itself…” Ugh! How much longer till Trigger gets back with the explosives?
sid_phrase_E12_MQ01_Comment_NorHana_fluf: Well? I know you’re part of this. Now spill it, or you’ll have to answer to Degtyarev when he comes back.
Did I stutter? Can't you see how deep in shit we are? And Pripyat even more so. We gotta be ready for the worst.
What you need to do is shut your trap!
sid_phrase_E12_MQ01_Comment_RihterCall_c: One, two, three… urgent transmission from Richter Press: Monolith on the horizon! All hands on deck!
Or not. You might draw a bad card.
sid_phrase_E12_MQ01_Comment_RihterComing: Over here, you Monolithian son of a bitch!
Your ugly mug ain’t any better. All right. Come on up here.
sid_phrase_E12_MQ01_Comment_RihterIntera: Give me a minute. Losing your faith is easy, but rediscovering it takes effort. And time. And a little bit of liquid courage…
I need to clear my head before I hit the road again, so to speak. Here, take this. You’ll need it more than I will.
Get lost before I sober up. I don’t miss when I’m sober.
sid_phrase_E12_MQ01_Comment_SanaBackOff_: You’re a shitty helper. Get lost!
sid_phrase_E12_MQ01_Comment_SanaComeOn_1: Looks like we got reinforcements! Stalker, help!
sid_phrase_E12_MQ01_Comment_SanaHelpedDu: I didn't think there was a way to help Spirit. You're a gift from the Zone itself.
sid_phrase_E12_MQ01_Comment_SanaStrelok_: Don’t worry, we’ll give him a proper farewell. Thanks again for your help.
sid_phrase_E12_MQ01_Comment_SkifIskra_se: See you in the Shining Zone, my friend.
sid_phrase_E12_MQ01_Comment_SkifVarta_by: The Zone shouldn't exist at all. We need to get rid of it.
sid_phrase_E12_MQ01_Comment_VartaBunker_: Where the fuck are those explosives? We’re sitting ducks out here in the open..
Dvoryankov must’ve been stingy with the C4 again. When I get back to the Chemical Plant, I’m heading straight to the colonel.
sid_phrase_E12_MQ01_Dialog_DuhShram_1_65: I told you, Spirit, Scar doesn’t lie. In the Zone, death is not the end. We just leave all the darkness behind.
sid_phrase_E12_MQ01_Dialog_DuhShram_2_65: I told you, Spirit, Scar doesn’t lie. In the Zone, death is not the end. We just leave all the darkness behind.
sid_phrase_E12_MQ01_Dialog_DuhShram_hedo: Scar doesn’t lie. He skirts the truth, he leaves things out, but he never lies outright. Especially not about what matters most: the Shining Zone.
I’ve got even less reason to lie to you. I’m on my last legs here. I don’t have anything left to fear anymore... except the unknown.
sid_phrase_E12_MQ01_Dialog_DuhShram_noos: But there’s no unknown. Only the Shining Zone. Immortality within the Noosphere.
sid_phrase_E12_MQ01_Dialog_DuhShram_nowy: I trust Scar, not you. There’s no use talking to you.
A fanatic, huh? Watch out, guys!
sid_phrase_E12_MQ01_Dialog_DuhShram_rusu: Are you absolutely certain about that?
I’m a Monolithian. My entire life as I remember it was devoted to Topol and the original Spark. I still see him in my dreams. He keeps asking me why I left him there.
But it doesn't matter. It'll all be over soon.
sid_phrase_E12_MQ01_Dialog_DuhShram_shra: I have no idea why Scar needs to get to Prypiat, but my secrets will die with me.
sid_phrase_E12_MQ01_Dialog_DuhShram_sorr: Then I can… I can apologize to him! I can… the bunker, you need the bunker! There’s a scanner in there, tweaked by some smartass from Garbage.
It doesn’t create anomalies; it disables them, get it? Here are the coordinates. The instructions are there too. Scar'll figure it out, I’m sure of it!
Thank you. Thank you for showing this to me. Maybe there really is a Shining Zone after all. Maybe that’s where I’ll find my home. For the first time since the Signal…
sid_phrase_E12_MQ01_Dialog_DuhShram_will: No one dies for real here. Scar proved it.
Scar, huh? Do you know what happened at the Chemical Plant when the Ward showed up?
They attacked us out of the blue. I saw Topol die in an instant. Boom, and he was gone. And if it weren’t for Marshal… I’m still not sure how I made it.
Scar was never there. He was never part of Topol’s original Spark. If he claimed otherwise, he was lying through his teeth.
And if he lied about that, just imagine what else he might be lying about.
sid_phrase_E12_MQ01_Dialog_DuhStart_hiyo: Those mutants are tricky, eh? One distracts you while another one flanks you out… almost like humans. It's about to be one less human in the equation, so whatever, I guess.
sid_phrase_E12_MQ01_Dialog_DuhStart_howd: How do you know who I am?
I don’t have a fucking clue. But I can sure as hell see who you've been hanging out with.
sid_phrase_E12_MQ01_Dialog_DuhStart_hurt: Not wounded — killed. One of those guys you put down out front. I guess I gotta thank you for avenging me.
But I’ll tell you what: vengeance might bring peace to the dead, but it ain’t no fucking comfort to the dying.
sid_phrase_E12_MQ01_Dialog_DuhStart_peop: Why won’t your men help you?
Because they can't save me. I hired them to help me get to Lymansk. But, as you can see, there’s no way to get to Lymansk from here.
So they’re stuck here with me till I snuff it. Honest folk, good stalkers. Not many left in the Zone these days.
sid_phrase_E12_MQ01_Dialog_DuhStart_pryp: I need to get to Prypiat. I know you’ve been taking stalkers around Yaniv.
You think I was doing it for the money? Hell, I barely covered my expenses. Degtyarev is an idiot.
You can’t take away someone's dream, you know? It’s like the seaside you’ve never visited… always sunny, always peaceful…
Or at least it used to be, until Monolith showed up.
sid_phrase_E12_MQ01_Dialog_DuhStart_shra: Whatever Scar’s men are looking for around here, I don’t have it. Fuck off and let me die in peace.
It’s all in your eyes. You Sparkers are just like Monolithians — blind fanatics, the whole lot of you. Now spill it. What did your buddy Scar want?
sid_phrase_E12_MQ01_Dialog_DuhStart_stre: At least I’ll meet my end in the presence of a true stalker. Could be worse.
You can always spot a lone stalker. Now spill it. Why won’t you let me die in peace?
sid_phrase_E12_MQ01_Dialog_DuhStart_vart: So the last face I’m gonna see is Korshunov’s lapdog? Why thank you, Zone. What a nice parting gift.
I can always spot a Warden pig. So much for not working with stalkers. Why were you looking for me?
sid_phrase_E12_MQ01_Dialog_DuhStrelok_bu: The instructions are in the bunker, too. You’re sharp — you’ll figure it out. Tell Strelok I said hi, okay? I had two dreams: find a home in the Zone, and meet Strelok. Neither came true.
sid_phrase_E12_MQ01_Dialog_DuhStrelok_he: Thanks for finding me, stalker. And for the advice too. Good… hunting…
sid_phrase_E12_MQ01_Dialog_DuhStrelok_it: Dreams coming true don't always make you happy. Strelok would know.
Now I can tell you’re rolling with him. They say he ain't much of a shoulder to cry on, either.
You found your way into Prypiat, and you’ll get to Lymansk too. I’m sure you’ll find your place there.
Now I can tell you’re rolling with Strelok. They say he's a bad liar too.
sid_phrase_E12_MQ01_Dialog_DuhStrelok_oh: Secrets never really die, no matter how deep we bury them. Strelok taught me that much.
You know him? Strelok? No way… yes, you’re telling the truth. I can see it in your eyes. Wow, I never thought I’d have the chance to help Strelok himself.
sid_phrase_E12_MQ01_Dialog_DuhStrelok_ok: Your call. In the Zone, the dead can speak as loud as the living, you know.
sid_phrase_E12_MQ01_Dialog_DuhStrelok_st: I have no idea why you need to get to Prypiat, stalker, but my secrets will die with me.
sid_phrase_E12_MQ01_Dialog_DuhVarta_bein: That’s how you stay alive. Here, take it. Now tell me what you know.
sid_phrase_E12_MQ01_Dialog_DuhVarta_bunk: The instructions are in the bunker too. You Wardens are sharp — you’ll figure it out. And thanks, I guess. But whatever your plan is, I hope it crashes and burns.
The Zone needs to stay free. Don't you get it?!
sid_phrase_E12_MQ01_Dialog_DuhVarta_dont: But you don’t have to die for it. I’m still looking for a home too, just like you. We’re not as different as you might think.
Besides, our secrets don’t really die with us. Only our dreams do.
That’s how you become a Warden, huh?
sid_phrase_E12_MQ01_Dialog_DuhVarta_goto: That’s how you go all in to achieve your goals. Here, take it. Now tell me what you know.
sid_phrase_E12_MQ01_Dialog_DuhVarta_ican: The Colonel and I don’t want anyone to die needlessly. I’ve got a Monolithian regenerator. I can heal you. In return, you’ll tell me what I need to know.
It’s always the same story: you give me something, I give you something, we do a little dance with our conscience… nah. Fuck you and your Ward.
I was heading to Lymansk to find a home of my own, not make all the stalkers homeless. The Zone has to be free!
sid_phrase_E12_MQ01_Dialog_DuhVarta_koor: Hold on... there. These are the coordinates to a bunker where I left a scanner. It's been tweaked by a digger from Garbage — Diode, I think… unlike the regular scanners, this one disables anomalies.
sid_phrase_E12_MQ01_Dialog_DuhVarta_ok_6: Your call. In the Zone, the dead can speak as loud as the living.
sid_phrase_E12_MQ01_Dialog_DuhVarta_sign: How did you make it through the Signal?
A Spark bunker. Not the Spark that's out there right now — the original one. My Spark. It was shielded against any psi-effects. Ozersky really outdid himself there.
I wonder what’s become of him. I hope he’s doing okay.
sid_phrase_E12_MQ01_Dialog_DuhVarta_vart: I have no idea why the Ward needs to get to Prypiat, but my secrets will die with me.
sid_phrase_E12_MQ01_Dialog_DuhVarta_what: What, Richter didn’t fill you in? Don’t give me that look. Who else could’ve told you how to track me down?
I don’t know anything but the Zone. When the Monolith went silent, I started looking to fit in here somehow. Find a place of my own. A home, as weird as it might sound.
When the Monolith came back, there was nothing left for me in Prypiat. I figured maybe I’d get luckier in Lymansk. But no matter how hard I tried, I just couldn’t get there.
sid_phrase_E12_MQ01_Dialog_Kaimanov_5_65: Thank you for sharing, Skif. Here, this is all I’ve got. Where you’re headed, every little thing could come in handy.
sid_phrase_E12_MQ01_Dialog_Kaimanov_answ: My home was destroyed by an anomaly. I came here to find out how that could have happened.
sid_phrase_E12_MQ01_Dialog_Kaimanov_brod: I saw exactly what you thought I would: Strider.
sid_phrase_E12_MQ01_Dialog_Kaimanov_fini: And remember, it’s never too late to make up your own mind. Trust me, I'd know.
sid_phrase_E12_MQ01_Dialog_Kaimanov_golo: Or, rather, a holographic projection of him.
Strelok thinks there’s a machine deep within Prypiat controlling these illusions. Sounds way more plausible than some kind of Noosphere "subtle matter".
sid_phrase_E12_MQ01_Dialog_Kaimanov_hell: Good to see you here. Tell me all about where you’ve been and what you’ve seen.
sid_phrase_E12_MQ01_Dialog_Kaimanov_nodo: Not a shadow of doubt... I genuinely envy you, Skif, that unwavering confidence of yours.
sid_phrase_E12_MQ01_Dialog_Kaimanov_rewe: My home was destroyed by an anomaly. I came to find those responsible for it.
sid_phrase_E12_MQ01_Dialog_Kaimanov_shra: A one-way ticket. A miracle. A new world the Shining is about to reveal to us, free from pain and regret… and guilt.
I need to get to Prypiat and reach out to the Representative. C-Consciousness knows what to do next. They can control the Anomalous energy.
Control it? They’ve tried to control it before, and we both know how that went.
A miracle they never truly comprehended. And neither do you — or the people you take orders from.
sid_phrase_E12_MQ01_Dialog_Kaimanov_so_6: I see… stalkers often say, “Everyone has a different path to the Zone,” but I think it's more like, “The Zone calls us all in different voices.”
Its call was hard to ignore, but the journey has given me more than I was looking for. I’m getting close to what I really want.
sid_phrase_E12_MQ01_Dialog_Kaimanov_sram: It was him. He’s alive in the Noosphere — alone and trapped, but alive. And so are the others!
The Shining will set them free, Scar and I will do whatever it takes to make it happen! I need to talk to the Representative again, and then the miracle will finally unfold.
I didn’t dare hope for Dalin to be right. Is this joy or fear? What should I be feeling right now?
There’s no point in being afraid of the new world.
But you can still mourn the old one.
sid_phrase_E12_MQ01_Dialog_Kaimanov_stre: He intends to destroy whatever’s inducing these illusions, and he’s right. People deserve to be free of illusions, and so does the Zone.
So Dalin was mistaken, then? I was prepared for that, though I had hoped otherwise. As you can see, this man of science has already freed himself from illusions.
I met Strelok in X3. He’s aware that you were part of C-Consciousness, and he’s not exactly itching to meet you.
He was always smarter than he let on, and he always preferred solitude — especially when those around him ceased to be useful in his quest for the mysteries of the Zone. Is that why you’re here too?
A home. Not just an apartment — the entire Zone, in all its natural splendor. And a purpose: to defend it from any attempts to alter it, from the outside or from within.
I need to get to Prypiat and destroy the STALKER Program Center. Strelok can set the Zone free from those who want to enslave it, both now and in the future.
To a little fish, a big tank might feel like an entire ocean. But we both know the truth.
The Zone may grant stalkers freedom, but that alone doesn’t mean it's free — and neither are you, Skif, as long as you’re doing the bidding of others.
sid_phrase_E12_MQ01_Dialog_Kaimanov_vart: He intends to destroy whatever’s inducing these illusions, but that’s where he’s wrong. This technology can be a great asset to humanity.
So Dalin was mistaken, then? I was prepared for that, though I had hoped otherwise. The only miracles that exist are the ones we create ourselves.
A new home. And not just an apartment to replace the one that went up in flames! A new world where I can take my rightful place. A new life, one I never got to experience.
I need to get to Prypiat. The Ward believes they can bring the Anomalous energy under control.
How did that “control” work out at SIRCAA? The Zone is creating new laws of physics, an entirely new world, and they’re having trouble even grasping the old one.
We must seek to understand the Zone, not enslave it. We keep trying over and over again, and we fail every time.
We need to rise to its challenge, not try to bring it down to our level.
sid_phrase_E12_MQ01_Dialog_Kaimanov_what: I know what I’m going to do. And I’m not turning back.
sid_phrase_E12_MQ01_Dialog_RihterShram_b: You two share something — both searching for a home, both refusing to give up no matter what. Whatever's waiting for you in Prypiat… thank you for not giving up on your faith.
Something better and brighter. Something greater than yourself.
sid_phrase_E12_MQ01_Dialog_RihterShram_f: Faust was wrong about his means, but his aim was noble.
sid_phrase_E12_MQ01_Dialog_RihterShram_h: Scar wasn’t lying. C-Consciousness is just a step toward the Shining Zone.
You're hearing a man who believes in what he’s doing — just like you once did.
sid_phrase_E12_MQ01_Dialog_RihterShram_i: I saw them. The Representative, Strider… they’re alive in the Shining Zone! Scar's methods may be misguided, but his goal is noble.
Help me track down Spirit. I promise you, it’s all true. It's all worth it.
I’m not even sure who you sound like: Scar… or Faust. Who am I hearing?
sid_phrase_E12_MQ01_Dialog_RihterShram_l: I’m looking for Spirit. I need to get to Prypiat.
What does Scar want with Prypiat? It’s sunny there, sure, but I doubt it’s shiny enough for him.
Prypiat holds the key to the Shining Zone. See, I’m not holding anything back.
Just because you believe in it doesn’t make it real. You know how many years I trusted Scar?
sid_phrase_E12_MQ01_Dialog_RihterShram_m: A miracle. Something greater than yourself.
sid_phrase_E12_MQ01_Dialog_RihterShram_s: I figured your ugly mug would make an appearance here sooner or later. Another one of Scar’s “miracles,” I suppose.
You know what it means to make a choice?
Holy shit, you even sound like him now.
It’s not about picking between right and wrong, or yourself and other people. It’s about choosing whether to believe or not.
sid_phrase_E12_MQ01_Dialog_RihterShram_t: That’s why the Ward and Strelok are both going to lose. They don't have faith in themselves, let alone the Zone. And you're the same way.
Maybe it really was a miracle that I stumbled across you in Zalissya back then. I used to regret saving you, but maybe the Zone really does work in mysterious ways.
If you don’t trust him, trust me.
sid_phrase_E12_MQ01_Dialog_RihterShram_z: The Zone. Something greater than yourself.
sid_phrase_E12_MQ01_Dialog_RihterStrelok: Spirit is a bit of a dreamer. He wanted to find a place in the Zone to call his own — first in Prypiat, and after the Signal, in Lymansk.
I trust you, Skif, but I don’t trust Strelok. He’s keeping something from us.
We all have our secrets. But at least he wants to protect the Zone, not take it away or give it a makeover.
There are times when I feel like helping you in Zalissya was the only good call I ever made. It’s the only thing I don’t regret.
Then push forward, and don’t give up your faith — in the Zone, and, most importantly, in yourself. That’s the only way.
That’s it. It’s more than enough.
No matter what you do, as long as you keep pushing forward, you’ll get to where you want to be. The Zone tests people. Those who give up on their dreams… the Zone will never love them.
Are you a Zone expert now too?
Not at all. But I’m not a quitter either. That’s why I came here. I’m looking for Spirit to take me to Prypiat.
What are you hoping to find there? The Heart? The Heart of Chornobyl? Is that what Strelok’s after?
You two share something — both searching for a home, both refusing to give up no matter what. Good luck, Skif. And thank you.
The Heart of Chornobyl? What’s that?
An artifact. Whoever finds it can learn to understand the Zone and become almost one with it. That’s what they say, anyway.
Strelok found it once. Now you’re after it too, and something tells me you might actually find it. But it should’ve been me! I have to understand the Zone, I have to earn its forgiveness!
Then help us protect it. Scar and Korshunov couldn’t stop me, and they never will.
We are who we are, and you’re the kind of person who wouldn’t leave another man in the lurch... for better or worse.
I appreciate that. But I suspect Scar and Korshunov may not share that sentiment.
So, how’s our favorite Zone Legend doing?
I’ll never understand why the Zone loves him more than anybody else. What'd he do?
He always comes through, no matter what goes down.
That’s it?
You worked with Spirit. What can you tell me about him?
You’ve been to Prypiat before. Maybe you can help me get there?
It was a long time ago. I had a deal with Degtyarev, but now he's gone, and Nor doesn’t like me very much. The folks I failed to take to their destination… he knew them.
So the deal’s off now. The Zone’s not big on honoring deals these days, eh?
What can you tell me about Strelok?
Everything and nothing. Legends, rumors, myths shared around the campfire… Strelok the person and Strelok the legend have had very little in common for a long time.
Word is, the legend wants to save the Zone. As for what the person wants... well, nobody’s got a clue.
sid_phrase_E12_MQ01_Dialog_RihterVarta_1: Ha! Today’s your lucky day, Skif! Here are the coordinates to Spirit’s stash.
I’d wish you good luck, but I hope you never make it to Prypiat.
sid_phrase_E12_MQ01_Dialog_RihterVarta_2: All right. if you say so. Who am I to argue?
sid_phrase_E12_MQ01_Dialog_RihterVarta_a: Spirit is a bit of a dreamer. He wanted to find a place in the Zone to call his own — first in Prypiat, and after the Signal, in Lymansk.
I suppose you two are a little alike, as insulting as that might sound — for Spirit, of course, even though he used to be a Monolithian.
I trusted you, Skif. I thought you could see what the Zone was really like. But you still don’t understand.
Too bad it doesn’t love you back.
Fuck you. Or do you think I’m too sloshed to kick your ass?
You won’t, and you know it. You also know that the Zone, despite all its beauty, is still a mistake.
Or the only thing you ever did right. SIRCAA pushed science forward, and the Ward brought peace and order. It wasn't bad for anyone, other than the bandits and Spark terrorists.
Let me show you that this wasn’t all in vain, that we can make the world a better place! Help me find Spirit. Help me… set everything right.
sid_phrase_E12_MQ01_Dialog_RihterVarta_c: That’s why I want to turn it all around. We deserve better.
Who’s “we?” You and the Ward? Korshunov? That smartass clown Dalin?
I should’ve let those anomalies have you. What did I get by saving you? Putting the only thing I love — the Zone — at risk?
You know what? Remember that coin I gave you at the Slag Heap? The one from Prypiat?
Let the Zone decide your fate.
sid_phrase_E12_MQ01_Dialog_RihterVarta_d: He was part of the original Spark – the real Spark, not whatever Scar had turned it into.
And before that, he was a Monolithian.
Then he’s back with them now.
When the Signal hit, he happened to be in a place shielded from psi-effects. He’s still himself, miserable as ever.
sid_phrase_E12_MQ01_Dialog_RihterVarta_f: I’m not at liberty to say.
Of course. How quickly the Ward changes people…
sid_phrase_E12_MQ01_Dialog_RihterVarta_i: There’s nothing to understand, Richter. The Zone is a mistake.
Quite the opposite. I understand everything. The Zone was a mistake from the very beginning.
I hope you never make it to Prypiat. You’re just not worthy.
sid_phrase_E12_MQ01_Dialog_RihterVarta_n: It’s not all bad. You saved my life back then.
And where did that get me? I put the only thing I love — the Zone — at risk.
sid_phrase_E12_MQ01_Dialog_RihterVarta_t: What can you tell me about him?
I’m looking for a way into Prypiat, and I know he’s been helping stalkers get there.
And what is the Ward looking for in Prypiat? No, don’t tell me, let me guess: “it’s classified?”
When Spirit and I went our separate ways, he was heading down the tunnel to Lymansk. He’s probably still out there, looking for a way into the town.
sid_phrase_E12_MQ01_Dialog_RihterVarta_v: So what did Korshunov lure you in with? Money? Power? What’s the going rate for freedom and self-respect these days?
Right up to the last minute, I clung to the hope that you were just fucking with them. But you… I’m a lousy guide. Quite a shitty guardian angel, too.
sid_phrase_E12_MQ01_Dialog_RihterVarta_w: What are you doing here, anyway?
I’m having a great time hanging out with my best buddies: misanthropy and self-loathing.
Same thing as you: chasing my dreams. It’s just too bad I haven’t gotten there yet. Silver lining: neither have you.
sid_phrase_E12_MQ01_Dialog_Sana_goaway_6: I said, thank you, but hit the road already.
sid_phrase_E12_MQ01_Dialog_Sana_letmesee: I just need to talk to him. Maybe I can help.
I don’t think you can. It’s too late.
If you make it worse, you’re dead, man. I’ll see to it.
sid_phrase_E12_MQ01_Dialog_Sana_needtota: I’m looking for Spirit. Do you know him?
Where did you even... Oh, well. I guess, it’s no time for secrets now.
He hired us to guide him to Lymansk. But now it seems we can only guide him to the other side.
So you're free to go. There's nothing to see here.
sid_phrase_E12_MQ01_Dialog_Sana_notyourb: I need to get inside. Open it. Or I will.
Zone knows I didn't want to fight. But you're too much of a hothead.
sid_phrase_E12_MQ01_Dialog_Sana_opentheg: Open up! He wants to talk to Spirit.
sid_phrase_E12_MQ01_Dialog_Sana_talktome: Will you tell what happened here?
The Zone happened! In all its fucking glory!
We went into the tunnels, thought we'd find a way through. Nope. Nothing but rubble and mutants.
So we turned back, and the Monolithians came outta nowhere. Those bitches tracked us like fucking bloodhounds!
sid_phrase_E12_MQ01_Dialog_Sana_tunnel_6: What kind of tunnel is this?
The road to Lymansk. Or so we thought.
But it’s a dead end, too. I guess, Nor himself ordered to collapse it. Fucking gloryhound.
sid_phrase_E12_MQ01_Dialog_Tunnel_thanxb: Thanks for helping us with those critters, but you better get outta here, and quick. I can’t deal with you right now.
sid_phrase_E12_MQ01_Dialog_YanovDegot_de: Tell me about Degtyarev. What’s he like?
Ever seen a chimera? Like, a real mean one that's been lurking around since the Brain Scorcher days?
That’s Degtyarev for you. If I were a loner, I’d make sure not to run into him. But you’re not just a regular loner anymore, are you?
sid_phrase_E12_MQ01_Dialog_YanovSkaner_t: I’ve got the device Spirit used to guide stalkers across the Boundary.
The device? What the fuck are you talking about?
Spirit modified a SIRCAA Scanner into something that could disable certain anomalies.
You’ve gotta be fucking kidding me! It's bad enough you folks are willing to scale the wall to get to Prypiat, now you're waltzing through anomalies!
Hand it over. I’m gonna destroy it before any more guides pop up.
You wanted to cross the Boundary, stalker. Fine, the way is clear. I’ll tell them to let you through.
sid_phrase_E12_MQ01_Dialog_YanovStart_ab: He knows his way around. Hooked me up with folks at Garbage and with the IPSF. But I haven't seen him since the Monolith returned.
Probably because he figured his business guiding stalkers across the Boundary went kaput.
His business, not mine. I didn't know what he was up to.
You have no idea how little I care about this at the moment.
If you want to get to Prypiat, find me that Spirit guy. As you can see, my crew's a little thin, so if you’re set on crossing the Boundary, you’ll need to do a bit of legwork.
You can find a guy who worked with Spirit at the Water Tower. He might be able to tell you something. If he's not too drunk to talk.
See, it’s not that hard to cut to the fucking chase. You’ve got your lead, stalker. Dismissed.
sid_phrase_E12_MQ01_Dialog_YanovStart_an: Same old. The Monolith keeps moving forward round the clock. They've taken the Bridge, but won't stop there.
And we both know how they took the Bridge, Wreck. Don’t even think the loner's gonna distract me from our conversation.
sid_phrase_E12_MQ01_Dialog_YanovStart_du: I’ve heard that a guy called Spirit might be able to lend me a hand.
Holy fucking shit... well, would you look at that! Care to offer our guest a discount, Wreck? Or should we ask Telegraph to give him a proper tour?
I haven’t seen Spirit since the Signal! Yeah, we did business together. But you didn’t make a fuss about my connections when I brought you two boxes of ammo, did ya?
sid_phrase_E12_MQ01_Dialog_YanovStart_ho: Seems like things are going south here, huh?
We've always been short on manpower. And now… I’ll put it this way: I still have enough resources to keep you out of Prypiat.
But not enough to push the Monolith back.
None of your business. But you’re well aware of that, or you wouldn’t have gotten this far in the Zone. Is that all you wanted to know?
sid_phrase_E12_MQ01_Dialog_YanovStart_ic: And what good are your coupons? Ammo's worth more to me lately, as I can at least hold that in my hand.
And what good are your coupons? Ammo's worth more to me lately, as I can at least hold that in my hand.
sid_phrase_E12_MQ01_Dialog_YanovStart_ko: Why would I care? Korshunov can kiss my ass. What’s left of the Ward would be better off limping back to the Mainland, not trying to take on Prypiat.
sid_phrase_E12_MQ01_Dialog_YanovStart_pr: I need to get to Prypiat.
Sure, no problem. Come on in, make yourself at home.
