Sealed tight. We’ve never allowed anyone in before, and since the Signal, it’s out of the question.
sid_phrase_E12_MQ01_Dialog_YanovStart_sh: Sorry, can’t help you there. And I don’t want to. If Spark wants to get to Prypiat, I wish you the best of luck, but I’m not defying Degtyarev’s orders. Not even if Scar himself comes begging.
sid_phrase_E12_MQ01_Dialog_YanovStart_st: Cool. Did he pour you one for the road too? Every single bloke here claims to know Strelok, and half of ’em shared a drink with him, too. Next time, be more creative.
sid_phrase_E12_MQ01_Dialog_YanovStart_wh: And there’s nothing we can do about it?
Well, you can wait till we’re done wiping out Monolith. Then we’ll see.
sid_phrase_E12_MQ01_Dialog_YanovStart_wo: The Corps can’t work with other stalkers?
Working together is acceptable. Breaking our oath is not.
When did a promise to Deggy become my oath? All I did was give Spirit the coordinates I got from the Lesser Zone. I didn't have a clue what that Blue Falcon from the Sphere was up to.
And that was before the Signal. What does it matter now?
sid_phrase_E12_MQ01_duh_0_HelloTopic_Hel: Well? Spill it while I’m still breathing…
sid_phrase_E12_MQ01_duh_0_InfoTopic_1_in: Prypiat, Lymansk — just think of all the people who used to live there. So many homes destroyed, but somehow, I can feel mine is still out there, waiting for me…
sid_phrase_E12_MQ01_oceret_0_InfoTopic_1: What's waiting for us in Lymansk? Beats me, but it better be worth the trouble. Otherwise, why the fuck are we even bothering?
sid_phrase_E14_Bossfight_Korshunov_Comem: Damn! I’ll keep the Wardens busy, Skif. You handle Korshunov!
sid_phrase_E14_Bossfight_Korshunov_Comme: Fucking Ward! You take the colonel, I’ll deal with the soldiers!
You fucking pest! I should’ve shot you right away.
You were a mistake, and I’m going to rectify it.
The Zone took my life, and now I’m taking yours!
Show yourself, asshole. Time to die!
I’ll eradicate you. Along with your fucking Zone!
I’m not… losing… to a Stalker!
You bastard son of the Zone! I’m going to kill you!
You’ll pay for every single one of them.
You want to die for your worthless beliefs? I’ll be happy to oblige!
I’ll burn the Zone. Burn it all down. Burn you all!
sid_phrase_E14_MQ01_Audiolog_Dvoriankov_: Delta Team, rendezvous at the exact coordinates for points B or C.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ01_Audiolog_Prometey_Sp: Rustle, we’re retreating to our fallback position. If anything goes wrong, take cover at these coordinates.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ01_Comment_AudiologRadi: Copy that! If anything happens, rendezvous at the secondary point. Over and out.
Delta, we’re taking fire! Monolith presence confirmed. Requesting immediate backup!
Kappa, we have orders to send you the scans and burn the archives. We’re taking casualties!
sid_phrase_E14_MQ01_Comment_Burn_plancha: Genghis Khan, Rustle, burn the archives. We have the scans already. And get to the rendezvous point ASAP!
This place is fucked. Those mutants…
Fuck, I think we’ve got company! If things go south, get to the secondary…
sid_phrase_E14_MQ01_Comment_Chinatown_In: I never imagined a city could be so utterly dead. It’s as if it were once a living thing, and now it's giving you the empty stare.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ01_Comment_DegotScrelok: You're supposed to be at the Laundromat or the Prometheus, not here.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ01_Comment_DegotjAfterD: Deploy a squad to Sector 8. Looks like an ambush.
And keep a close eye on the stalker.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ01_Comment_DegotjApproa: Check sectors 6, 7, and 11. Quick. The assault on HQ is a diversion.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ01_Comment_DegotjOneOfU: One more thing: the stalker is one of us now. He doesn’t need a pass.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ01_Comment_DvoryankovFl: Five. Grebnev, half an hour ago.
We’re stretched thin. Tell the colonel the men can’t…
You think I haven’t tried?! I told him a hundred times!
The response is always the same: carry out your orders!
sid_phrase_E14_MQ01_Comment_Gorovitz_Int: I get the feeling the Ward's already gone. We’re just bogged down in the colonel’s private war against the Zone.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ01_Comment_KorshAndDali: That’s the face of war, Dalin. At least the Monolithians are oblivious to the horror of it all.
Are you certain about that, Colonel? They’re human, after all…
Now, when we’re on the verge of reaching our goal, you’re suddenly concerned about their lives? What about my men?
This will all be over soon. We’ll make sure of that. And we'll do whatever we have to do to get there.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ01_Comment_KorshunovOnI: Carry out your orders, soldier. I’m counting on you.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ01_Comment_Litvinuk_Int: I don’t know who scares me the most: the Sparkers, the Monolithians, the mutants, or the colonel.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ01_Comment_MolotokRadio: Sector 7. We’ve got an issue. Windmill’s gone silent.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ01_Comment_PolynChinato: Chrome’s squad never made it back.
“At all costs.” That’s what Scar said, China.
Looks like we’re up next, then.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ01_Comment_Polyn_Invite: Skif, come in. Scar wants to discuss our next move with you.
Sending you the coordinates. Hurry up.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ01_Comment_Scar_ComeHer: Over here, Skif. I know what we’re going to do.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ01_Comment_Scar_Richter: Then we’ll just keep searching. Until we find it.
And what if we don’t? The city’s swarming with Monolithians, Wardens, the Corps…
If everything in life was easy, why would we need faith?
sid_phrase_E14_MQ01_Comment_Shram_Intera: We need to get ahead of our enemies. Don’t drag your feet.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ01_Comment_Sorokin_Inte: The guys who died at the Duga had it easy.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ01_Comment_Storm1_1_660: We’re under attack! Sector 18! Defend your positions! Keep your heads down!
sid_phrase_E14_MQ01_Comment_StormIsOver_: Stalker, come on in. It’s clear.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ01_Comment_VartaCampInv: Skif, come in. You can’t imagine what it cost us, but we've pushed the Monolithians back and secured a position in the area. I’m sending you the coordinates.
We’re waiting for you, soldier. It’s time to go over our plans.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ01_Comment_Vityaz_Inter: The Zone can be cruel. But here in Prypiat, it truly hits home. This is where you start feeling it for real.
Unless you’re Scar, that is. I wish I could see what he sees one day.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ01_Comment_Vol_Approach: You’re a good shot. We could use someone like you here.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ01_Comment_Vol_Interact: Degtyarev’s the boss. I’m just here to shoot Monolithians.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ01_Comment_Yedkiy_Inter: I’m the sole survivor of my old squad. But I know we’ll meet again... in the Shining Zone.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ01_Dialog_DalinAtCamp_1: I’m relieved you made it through, Skif. So much has transpired here.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ01_Dialog_DalinAtCamp_F: We’re going to put an end to this once and for all. The Zone will no longer pose a threat. It's for the good of all mankind — just like you told me back at SIRCAA.
Yes, of course… it’s just that sometimes I find myself wondering if that’s truly what my father had in mind.
No matter. I’m sorry, Skif. This hesitation on my part is uncalled for.
We all have a great mission to accomplish, and we’re going to see it through.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ01_Dialog_DalinAtCamp_G: Our goal — everything you’ve strived for — is within reach. But you don’t seem too elated about it.
I am. I’m glad this nightmare will come to an end one day.
It’s just… too much. So many people… are they truly gone forever?
sid_phrase_E14_MQ01_Dialog_DalinAtCamp_N: Seeing things like this is never easy, but we need you, Doctor.
All of my life, I’ve followed in my father’s footsteps, yet now I can’t help but question if this is truly what he desired. Unfortunately, I’ll never know.
I’m sorry, stalker. My hesitation is uncalled for.
I envy your determination. Don’t worry, I mean to see this to the end.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ01_Dialog_DalinAtCamp_W: Yeah, a lot's happened. But what really matters is what happens next. What’s the plan after we get to the STALKER Program Control Center?

The STALKER Program Control Center is the heart of the X-Network. After what we did at SIRCAA, the X-Network is still operational within the Zone, but its connection to the Generators remains severed.
We’re going to rectify that. Afterward, we’ll be just a stone’s throw away from taking control of the Anomalous energy.
And then the Zone will be gone. Forever.
You’re not a soldier, Dalin. Why did Korshunov bring you along?
Agatha needs a scientist to make sure the system functions correctly once we reach the Control Center.
Dalin started this whole thing, so Dalin has to finish it.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ01_Dialog_DegotjStrelok: I told you, Skif is a one-man army. Oh, hi, by the way!
You made it here already, Richter?
Took you long enough to show up.
You two can catch up later. Strelok sent you. Why?
I’ve come across something that seems to match what you were talking about. Is that what we need?
What can you tell me about the Laundromat?
It served as the HQ for the military during Operation Fairway.
Officially, it involved five helicopters, but there were more – including a Stingray-7, which crashed soon after a mission to search for information about the entrances to the underground.
Now, there’s an anomalous field there – if that’s a concern for a man who made it through the Boundary on his own.
All right. I’m heading out.
Once you find the information, come back here. You’ll have my men's support to access the Foundation.
Get to work, stalker.
Thanks for dealing with those Monolithians.
I need more information about the data stored at the movie theater.
In 1986, an archive of military facility blueprints was moved to the Prometheus. You might be able to find the one we need in there.
We’ve observed increased mutant activity there in the past, but it’s up to you to figure out what’s going on there at the moment.
Besides the Monolithians, we’ve also identified some Wardens and Sparkers in the vicinity. They could be after the same thing you are.
What’s your reason for siding with Strelok?
Right now it's because I don't want anyone to gain access to the underground part of the city — even if the only alternative is to destroy it.
In a way, I was born here, stalker. And I’ll die here if I have to.
I need to get to the underground area beneath the city.
We used to have an entrance here, under the Palace of Culture. Back then, I was short on manpower, and when we started having trouble with mutants, I decided to seal it off.
Any other leads?
I had a few, but Richter’s been through all of them.
Exactly! It wasn’t a walk in the park, but at least I got a break from the rural scenery. Unfortunately, I couldn’t find any other entrances.
I’ve hit a dead end, so we’ll have to search for information. In the past, the entire underground area functioned as a military installation known as the Foundation.
There could be old Operation Fairway documents somewhere in the Laundromat.
Also, the Prometheus movie theater might have some old blueprints of military installations. It's up to you where to go. Neither one is going to be a piece of cake.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ01_Dialog_Degotj_1_6602: I’m looking for the entrance to the Prypiat underground.
Don’t expect any assistance from the Corps.
I’m aware of what you and your associates are after. We won’t allow it.
Yes. Your involvement might’ve saved a couple of my men, so you’re permitted to stay in my town and camp for now.
However, if you keep digging around, I’ll treat you just like any Monolithian.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ01_Dialog_Degotj_2_6602: Grim. But you can see that for yourself.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ01_Dialog_Degotj_3_6603: The Palace of Culture is the last outpost where my men can rest and resupply in Prypiat.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ01_Dialog_Degotj_GaveSc: Nor briefed me about you. Impressive. I appreciate your help with these fanatics too.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ01_Dialog_Degotj_NoScan: Degtyarev, commander of the Corps. I appreciate your help against the Monolithians, uh...
I’ve heard about you. And the folks you roll with.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ01_Dialog_Degotj_Talk_6: Don’t beat around the bush, Skif. Every minute I’m here means one less dead Monolithian.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ01_Dialog_Dvorenkov_1_6: What’s going on in the city right now?
We’re between a rock and a hard place: Monolith on one side, the Corps on the other.
For now, they’re busy fighting each other, but as soon as one of them takes over, they’ll set their sights on us.
And to top it off, we don't have the resources to monitor Spark’s movements. I just hope this city has swallowed them up.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ01_Dialog_Dvorenkov_2_6: It’s much worse. If things continue like this, we’ll have to make a tough call about who gets medical attention: the badly injured or the ones with a shot at getting back into the fight.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ01_Dialog_Dvorenkov_3_6: Is there anything I can do to help?
Do what Korshunov says. The sooner you act, the more lives we can save.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ01_Dialog_Dvorenkov_sta: A Ward soldier doesn’t question orders. God knows I’m a good soldier.
But now I’m sending men to their deaths just to buy time for a stalker. A stalker!
I hope the colonel knows what he’s doing. Otherwise, what’s left for me?
Fucking hell... I need some rest. So, what do you want?
If you've got any problems with equipment or supplies, take it to my men from the Chemical Plant.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ01_Dialog_Korshunov_1_6: What did you find out, soldier?
That Degtyarev is one stubborn son of a bitch. He won't let anyone into the underground and refused to help.
They had dreams too. They had aspirations. And now there’s just... nothing. Just like that.
Degtyarev has always been a pain in the ass, but I’ll deal with him later. For now, let’s proceed with the backup plan: we’ll find the entrance ourselves.
Based on our intel, there is a couple of places where we might be able to find what Degtyarev is trying so hard to keep from us.

The first spot is the Laundromat, we might find some old documents from Operation Fairway there. Another one is the Prometheus movie theater, which was used to store blueprints of underground facilities. Any questions?
sid_phrase_E14_MQ01_Dialog_Korshunov_Has: I stumbled across something on my way here. Isn’t this what you’re talking about?
You never cease to amaze me, stalker.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ01_Dialog_Korshunov_Lau: Tell me more about the Laundromat and Operation Fairway.
Operation Fairway was a second attempt to take over the CNPP. Interestingly, Degtyarev was a major in the SSU at the time, and he was one of those responsible for the mission's failure.
I had to deal with some of the fallout later, so I know that their objectives included finding an entrance to the underground facilities beneath Prypiat. The Laundromat served as their makeshift HQ.
There’s an anomalous field in there at the moment. That’s pretty much all I know.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ01_Dialog_Korshunov_NoI: That’s all I wanted to know.
Then move out on the double. I can’t spare any men yet — the Monolithians can easily track large groups, and I’ve already lost more than half my squad.
We’ll stay here, ready to accompany you to the Control Center when you get back.
There has to be another way. Someone needs to put an end to this bloodshed.
Dalin will come to his senses. But he’s not wrong.
We’re getting there. We’re almost there, Skif.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ01_Dialog_Korshunov_Pro: I need more information about the data stored at the movie theater.
In 1986, an archive of military facility blueprints was moved to the Prometheus. There’s a chance you could find something useful in there.
Assuming the place hasn't been looted by stalkers.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ01_Dialog_Korshunov_Wha: Assuming I find the entrance, what comes next?
We’ll also find a way to get to the Generators and finally wipe this festering boil called the Zone off the face of the Earth.
Eliminate. Destroy. Is that our mission? Is that what we’re doing here?
You can’t build the new without tearing down the old. You’re a scientist, remember?
sid_phrase_E14_MQ01_Dialog_Korshunov_are: I’m fine. I think I knew him. But his name eludes me…
Dr. Dalin isn't a soldier. He’s a stranger to the battlefield, unlike you and me. It’s okay — every boy has to become a man eventually.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ01_Dialog_Polyn_polynst: It’s good to have you with us, stalker. Our numbers are dwindling.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ01_Dialog_Polyn_whathap: What’s the situation? Are we holding our ground?
Barely. The Corps and Monolith are at each other’s throats, it'll keep them busy.
But the moment either of them gains the upper hand, they’ll turn their attention to us. The Ward hasn’t gone anywhere either. It’s a race against the clock.
Aside from that, it’s the same old story. Scar's awfully calm.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ01_Dialog_Polyn_whatthi: What’s your take on Scar’s plan?
No matter what happens, we’re heading in the right direction. Even if not everyone makes it to the finish line.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ01_Dialog_RichterAndSca: So what’s the plan now?
I've got three leads.
I already checked one. It’s a dead end. Not to mention I narrowly escaped getting killed there!
And yet your efforts were helpful to Skif. Everything happens for a reason, you see? Now we’re left with two options: the Prometheus and the Laundromat.
You’re a changed man, Richter.
It's all thanks to you, Skif. You were right — if you believe in something greater than yourself, life no longer seems that unfair anymore. And neither does the Zone.
I’m not sure how this will all play out in the end, but I’ll do my best to make amends for what I’ve done.
After all, regret is part of the darkness we're trying to leave behind. Among other things.
I’ve been through worse. I can handle it.
The city’s a death trap. Wherever you turn, you’re gonna have to fight your way out. And that’s if you’re fortunate enough.
You ain’t getting it, Skif! I’m dead serious! Prypiat was never a walk in the park, but now…
I’m not like your old group, Richter. I won’t end up lost. Trust me. Trust the Zone.
Couldn’t have said it better myself.
Let’s get going. Keep me posted, all right?
I stumbled across something out there. Could this be what we’re looking for?
Really?
What’s inside the Laundromat?
It was the HQ for the military forces involved in Operation Fairway. Don’t bother asking about the specifics — we both have enough crap to think about as it is.
However, there could be documents there related to a helicopter that was sent to locate the entrance to the underground area. Stingray 7, I think it was.
The hardest part is just before you win, isn’t it?
The Prometheus? What’s in there?
A long time ago, they stashed an archive of military facility blueprints there — including maps of the underground area.
At the very least, we might be able to help our friendly guide expand his collection of Prypiat maps.
I’m ready to go.
Deggy doesn’t want to cooperate. He’s like the blind dog in the manger — he won't eat the hay himself, and he won't let anyone else have it either.
There has to be another way. It's what the Zone is all about.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ01_Dialog_Richter_allgo: Everyone was ready to tear each other apart. But now that you’re here, I think everything’s gonna be all right.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ01_Dialog_Richter_doyou: Do you believe Scar now? Do you believe in the Shining Zone?
I… I want to believe. Watching you two, I see what a man who truly believes can achieve.
Whether it’s the Shining Zone, immortality, or anything else.
Once upon a time, I believed I could become a true guide. Now, thanks to you, I believe it again.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ01_Dialog_Richter_guide: A true guide? What does that even mean?
It means guiding people not along your own twisted, misguided path, but along the path laid out by the Zone, the path it reveals to you naturally.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ01_Dialog_RihterStrelok: Don’t go thinking Degtyarev's going to be the only one accompanying you to the underground, Skif. I might not be the toughest fighter around, but I’m a true guide. We’re going in together.
A true guide? What does that even mean?
It means believing in yourself and the path the Zone has laid out for you.
Just because a journey is challenging, doesn’t mean you should shy away from it.
Are you part of Strelok’s crew now?
I’m with you. That means I’m with Strelok for now.
But, first and foremost, I’m with the Zone. And I’m determined to defend it until my last breath.
Once upon a time, I aspired to become a true guide. Now, thanks to you, I am one.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ01_leva_fan_0_InfoTopic: The more progress we make, the more I’m convinced I made the right call by betting on the Ward. They seem to be the Zone’s favorite.
Even if the Zone doesn’t like them that much.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ01_leva_fan_0_Meet_meet: As you can see, our team has been reshuffled. I guess the Ward is getting ready for something huge.
I’d love to watch the first division in action, of course, but my place is here, riding the pine. At least it’s safe here.
Make sure to gear up. All the Ward stuff is top of the line.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ01_leva_fan_0_TradeTopi: I know how to get the best gear, even in the worst of times.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ01_p_hyphen_k_degtarev_: Anyone staking a claim to my city will be destroyed by the Corps or the Zone itself.
Get out of the line of fire!
The enemy is retreating. All clear for now.
Have you lost your mind? Take this away.
Be quick about it, stalker. I’ve got things to do.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ01_serzant_ivajlov_0_He: Hand it over. I’ll get it fixed up.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ01_serzant_ivajlov_0_In: The only time I was recalled from the Chemical Plant was the Duga op. And it was a success — the second time, anyway.
I hope things go well here too.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ01_serzant_ivajlov_0_Me: Holy shit... they didn’t prepare me for this. Nobody was ready for this, I guess.
I think your gear could use a bit of fixing... regardless of where you’re headed next.
I heard about your next assignment. I know you’re used to this job, but you’d better make sure you’re ready for anything. The expeditionary units are staying behind.
They brought me some Monolithian gear after the Duga. There's some pretty cool stuff, sure.
But the more I took it apart, the more uneasy I became. If those were the weapons they had there, I wonder if we’ll stumble upon anything even more powerful here.
And if we do, maybe we’re better off staying away from it.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ01_serzdot_eremeev_0_In: You tell me. My job is to fix the gear and issue it on time, - those are my orders.
The latest batch was a huge one. But all good stuff, watertight.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ01_serzdot_eremeev_0_Me: Hey, Skif! We last met at Icarus, if I'm not mistaken. Those weren't such bad times after all.
I thought they'd send me to the Mainland. But then I got reassigned here. Maybe, for the better. I know a thing or two about kit.
If you wanted to get something fixed, now's the time, before they reassign me someplace else.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ01_serzdot_hoteev_0_Hel: What can I help you with?
sid_phrase_E14_MQ01_serzdot_hoteev_0_Inf: If you want news, ask Korshunov. Nothing much here.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ01_serzdot_hoteev_0_Mee: The time has come for the Ward to clench its fists and strike hard. Preparation is key.
You surely need to restock. The fullest range of equipment is at your fingertips.
We're lucky there's been a new equipment delivery. And it's all assigned already.
But I'll find something for you. Take your pick.
You're on time, I've got some new arrivals. And my job is to equip you all to the max.
Come on, let's get you geared up. It'll come in handy.
Hey, Skif, never thought we'd meet again? Neither did I. Everyone's ordered to rally up. And I mean, everyone.
We had a pile of decommissioned stuff here, remember? Now everything's accounted for. Nothing's left to just sit there.
But for you, we'll find something. Go ahead, take your pick.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ01_stepsel_0_ByeTopic_b: I’ll see you around, whether it’s here, or in the Shining Zone.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ01_stepsel_0_HelloTopic: I knew you’d need my services. What needs fixing?
sid_phrase_E14_MQ01_stepsel_0_InfoTopic_: I’ve always had a feeling the Zone was something more than just a place on a map.
And now I’m sure of it.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ01_stepsel_0_Meet_2_mee: We’ve lost too many… but we’ll meet again in the Shining Zone.
Stock up on supplies. Traveling light won’t get you there.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ01_stepsel_0_Meet_3_mee: One more step, and victory is ours. Then everything we've been through won’t be in vain.
I’ll help you however I can. You do your part too.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ01_stepsel_0_Meet_meet1: The journey to the Shining Zone isn’t a walk in the park, is it? Well, it wasn’t meant to be easy.
While I may not be on the front lines, I’m still doing my part. No one goes into battle with broken equipment.
If you need your gear fixed, I’ve got you covered.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ01_stepsel_0_UpgradeTop: I’ve fixed worse! I’ll make it better than new.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ01_suska_0_InfoTopic_1_: Save your questions for when we accomplish our mission. In the meantime, each of us has a job to do.
There’s work to be done. No time for chitchat.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ01_suska_0_Meet_2_meet2: I hadn’t seen you in action before the Foundation, but now I see why Scar puts his trust in you.
Looks like you’ve gone through a fair bit of ammo. Stock up while we’re both here. Might come in handy.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ01_suska_0_Meet_3_meet3: Everything I've got is yours. May it help you take us to the Shining Zone.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ01_suska_0_Meet_meet1_6: I’m no fan of small talk, least of all at a time like this. Make it quick.
Each of us has a role to play. Mine is handling supplies.
Maybe we won’t need any of these goods in the Shining Zone, but we can’t make it there without them.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ01_suska_0_TradeTopic_T: All my goods are top-notch, but choose wisely.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ01_vol_0_InfoTopic_1_wh: Prypiat will never be the same again. Maybe it’s for the best. I’d rather go down fighting than slowly rotting away, waiting for who knows what.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ02_Comment_Agatha_About: I can only teleport you to the nearest bunker in the Dead Valley, Yevhen. The teleportation system isn’t fully operational yet. It’ll take some time.
So remember to grab the experimental Project X suit. You’ve got the coordinates already.
For now, we’ll have to make do with older designs to make sure you’re protected.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ02_Comment_Agatha_Inter: You can leave now, Yevhen. I’ve unlocked the teleporter for you. The Dead Valley awaits.
You need Professor Hermann. He will tell you what to do.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ02_Comment_DalinCall_Is: I hope you can hear me, Skif.
Korshunov’s already in the main hall. They’re just waiting for the new Control Unit. I’ll do what I can, but…
Please don’t try to charge in head-on. You’ll just get yourself killed. Use the video surveillance room on the second floor. I’ll open it for you. I have the means to do it.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ02_Comment_Dalin_DontKi: Skif… Skif! Please, don’t. Do not repeat my…
sid_phrase_E14_MQ02_Comment_Dalin_NeedKe: The pendant, Skif! Faust’s pendant! The colonel has it. It’s the key to the Visiograph.
We'll talk soon. And then everything will fall into place. Finally.
Get me the pendant off Korshunov’s body, quick!
sid_phrase_E14_MQ02_Comment_DegotCall_1_: Your guys walked into an ambush, but I avenged them.
Got it. Strelok mentioned we’ve got an ally in the Ward. He let us know that their troops are closing in on the Foundation.
The clock is ticking. Let’s put a stop to whatever the Ward is doing down there.
I’ve been watching over this underground facility for years for a reason. Get ready to waltz through all nine circles of hell.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ02_Comment_DegotSecRoom: Do you really think the Ward is gonna wait for us?
sid_phrase_E14_MQ02_Comment_Degot_Hurry_: Get moving! We gotta make it in time!
sid_phrase_E14_MQ02_Comment_Degot_Securi: So our ally is Dalin, huh? Sounds shitty, but I guess it’s better than nothing.
He rescued me once. We can trust him.
I hope he doesn’t chicken out. Let’s roll. We’re running out of time.
We’ve got to destroy the Foundation before the Ward is done with it.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ02_Comment_DvoryankovKo: Even if the stalker comes back, what about the wounded, Colonel?
I'm sorry, but the enemy won’t show any weakness, so we can’t afford to either.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ02_Comment_Dvoryankov_I: See you there, Skif. I need to prepare my men… the ones that are left, anyway.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ02_Comment_Ivailov_comm: If you’ve been itching to upgrade or grab some new gear, Skif, now’s the time.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ02_Comment_KorshCall_1_: What’s happening over there? Dvoryankov’s gone dark.
Spark. The entire squad has been wiped out. But I made sure not a single Spark bastard lived to tell the tale.
I’ll kill them. I’ll kill them all!
But it can wait. Right now, our priority is reaching the Foundation. Once you’re down there, there’s only one path: straight ahead.
I’ll see you down below, soldier.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ02_Comment_Korshunov_In: Get ready and move out on the double. That’s an order.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ02_Comment_Korshunov_Ki: There’s one left: Dalin, the little bitch. Kill him!
sid_phrase_E14_MQ02_Comment_Korshunov_Sk: Why are you all doing this to me? Huh?!
Go to the control unit. Obey an order for once.
Good. Finally, someone in this pigsty knows how to follow orders.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ02_Comment_Olimpia_SorL: Fuck… it’s too bad about Kyrylchuk. If we had a medkit, we could've saved him.
What the hell are those Sparkers doing here?
sid_phrase_E14_MQ02_Comment_RIchterCall_: Skif, come in. I know you’re nearby. What’s happening right now…
They want to destroy it! And you’re on their side too? How can you not love the Zone? Haven’t you witnessed its miracles, its… its breathtaking beauty?
It wasn’t meant to end like this. I know I made the right call back in Zalissya. And I’d make it again!
I’m begging you, Skif. Please. This is my home.
It’s for the greater good. I’ve made my choice, and I’m not backing down, Richter.
I’m sorry. But neither am I.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ02_Comment_Representati: Go. I want to talk to my son.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ02_Comment_RichterBossf: I’m not letting my Zone go!
sid_phrase_E14_MQ02_Comment_RichterGun_1: By the way, Deggy left you his gun. He said if you survive, you deserve it.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ02_Comment_RichterIskra: Phew. You made it in one piece.
I wish I could tag along, but the others need my help more. I’ll catch up with you there.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ02_Comment_RichterSecRo: Don’t just stand there, the fate of the Zone is at stake!
Where are you headed, Skif?! We’re running out of time!
We can chat once the Zone is safe.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ02_Comment_ScarArrived_: At last. I was afraid I wouldn’t make it in time.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ02_Comment_ScarCall_Isk: How did your rendezvous go, Skif?
There were Wardens here, but I avenged our men.
A good man, our new friend, reported that the Warden pigs are already digging toward the Foundation.
Get over there on the double. We’ll meet you underground.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ02_Comment_ScarDalin_Af: The connection is incredibly unstable… I’m not even sure if my father can hear me…
I’ll lend you a hand, Doctor. Ozersky taught me a few tricks.
But soon, none of this will be necessary. You’re going to meet him for real. I promise.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ02_Comment_ScarHurry_Sc: Grieve not for the dead, Skif. When they awaken, they will be embraced by the Shining Zone.
Speak to the Representative. Our goal is within our grasp. It’s time to find out how to attain it.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ02_Comment_ScarIskra_In: Get ready, we’re heading out soon.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ02_Comment_SkifNotCaugh: It’s the corpses. Their silence is deafening.
It’s too quiet here, even by Prypiat standards.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ02_Comment_Stepsel_Come: If you’re looking to upgrade your gear, Skif, now’s the time!
If you need any repairs, I’m your man. If you’re in the market for something new, talk to Sushka.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ02_Comment_Strelok_Abou: From the Red Fortress we go to the Generators. Through the teleport. They've had no maintenance in ten years.
So you need experimental armor from Project X, otherwise it'll dissolve you into molecules. I'll give you a set from my old stock. Here're the coordinates, you'll find it there.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ02_Comment_Strelok_DKIn: See you by the Red Fortress.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ02_Dialog_AgathaAfterCu: So the Anomalous energy of the Zone is finally under control, just like you wanted?
We’ll reestablish the link between the Foundation and the Generators and claim the vacant pod in X7, and that’s how it will be.
Not while the link to the Generators is severed. Only Alpha allowed remote access. But that's just a minor obstacle.
Dr. Kaymanov doesn’t think a single pod will be enough.
Kaymanov? That scientist who missed his chance to enter the eighth pod, thus granting us the opportunity to seize control of the Anomalous energy? He’s stuck in the past.
You don’t need eight people to interface with the Noosphere anymore. I alone am sufficient, along with a Mediator in the pod who will serve as an intermediary between the Zone and the Noosphere.
And who’s going to be that Mediator?
The Doctor didn’t inform you? How typical of him...
The Mediator is a human, but a unique one. They must have class 5 psi-protection. It’s a necessary requirement that cannot be ignored.
I knew you wouldn’t permit us to experiment on your men, Colonel. That’s why you were kept in the dark until the eleventh hour.
When the fate of the future and the lives of a few soldiers hang in the balance, I believe the choice is self-evident. Besides, that psi-protection saved your life at the Duga.
What do you want from me?
Professor Hermann will fill you in on the details of our plan to restore the connection between the Foundation and the Generators.
Do you have someone like that in mind?
Certainly — Colonel Korshunov. I insisted he received the required psi-protection. We had to handpick the Ward soldiers exposed to psi-effects to manufacture it.
So the Mediator is Colonel Korshunov?
What’ll happen to the Monolith and the Agents of C-Consciousness now?
In my view, the Monolith wasn’t the most successful C-Consciousness experiment.
They certainly fulfilled their purpose, but the notion of needing a new god to compel their loyalty made everything too complicated.
Now, as for the Agents… the finest among them weren’t even aware they were Agents, but that didn’t stop them from carrying out their mission. Fascinating, isn’t it?
Your cigarette leaves no smell. None whatsoever.
And yet the flavor is exquisite, Yevhen. Have you forgotten it since our encounter at SIRCAA? Regrettably, I can’t offer you a smoke at the moment.
And he killed them.
Ironic, isn't it? The tool they stole from us ended up killing them.
So Dalin decided to betray us?
You sound just like the Colonel.
I'd say we all make mistakes, Yevhen. And those mistakes always cost someone their life. Especially when you choose your own empty hopes and dreams over the common good.
Dmytro Dalin stubbornly refused to believe that the Representative was not his father, but only a program, and that C-Consciousness was irrevocably dead - long gone.
The same thing had happened to Valentyn Dalin, by the way. He was doomed as soon as he decided that his own utopian ideas were more important than the goals of Project X.
It should have happened during the Alpha experiment at SIRCAA, if Faust hadn't made some alterations to our plan.
But better late than never. Since Professor Hermann replaced the control unit, the X-Network is in my hands. Neither the Representative nor anyone else will be able to access it.
After all these years, Project X will finally be completed.
So what do you expect me to do next?
Would you mind telling me more about X-Network?
The X-Network was the most important element of our plan. A psi-based infrastructure that connected all Project X devices and kept them functioning.
Until the Noosphere Contact Group called themselves C-Consciousness and seized control of the Network. One mistake, and here we are, in the Zone.

After that, they used the X-Network for years and even made adjustments to it — created the Monolith and agents, for example. But they made a mistake here too. They wanted an agent, but got Strelok instead. And he...
So finishing Project X has always been your goal?
It couldn’t have been otherwise. Once upon a time, the Regulatory Board launched Project X to find a source of Anomalous energy that would mark a new milestone in technological advancement.
And we found it. This discovery is too valuable for humanity to end in nothing.
It was always only a matter of time before we regained control of the Anomalous energy. But we know how to be patient.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ02_Dialog_Dalin_AfterCu: I really meant well. I believed that I could make the future a little better. But Agatha’s plan was never about the good of humanity, was it? All she wanted was total control.
It’s all real. It’s always been real.
The miracles of the Zone. Subtle Matter. Eternal life within the Noosphere.
And I nearly destroyed it. Almost deprived humanity of its chance at immortality. I… I could’ve killed my own father.
But in the end, you didn’t do any of that. You saw the truth just in the nick of time — and you made the right choice.
Yes. That much is true. But I came dangerously close to making the wrong one.
It seems, I was almost late. What the hell were you and the Ward trying to do here?
I was about to do something right, for once in my life. And I would have died for it, if it weren't for you.
And they... They were planning to replace the Representative. Once they reconnected with the Generators, they could use the X-Network and all that C-Consciousness had created.
They wanted to erase my father. By my hand. I don’t know if the Colonel found that small detail funny.
You made the Shining possible. You’ve played your part. Now Scar and I need to finish what your father set in motion.
It’s still very dangerous out there. However, if you succeed, if it all goes according to plan, all these deaths — the Wardens, Spark, Noontide, ordinary stalkers — they'll all be undone.
Humanity will finally attain the life it deserves. A life without pain, without death, without hate. It’s a risk worth taking.
Maybe, after the Shining, great experiments will finally be performed without casualties.
Sounds like a promising future. Best of luck to you. See you in the new reality.
Be grateful to the Zone — it spared you from doing something you couldn't undo.
I am grateful. Truly, I am.
And if it wasn’t for you, she would’ve achieved it. I don’t know if I would’ve found the courage to get to the truth if you hadn’t opened my eyes to it. Thank you… for everything.
There are a lot of people who were oblivious to the truth. Even I had my doubts at times.
The colonel never had any doubt. He was always willing to kill and die for the lies spoon-fed to him by Agatha. I wonder what he’ll say once he returns from the dead…
What do you intend to do now?
I’m going to talk to my father, for starters. For far too long, I was convinced this would never happen again. I’ve got a lot of questions... and a lot to apologize for.
I just need to enhance the connection. The link is still unstable. I don’t want to lose him again before I’m done.
And after that… well, I suppose it depends on you. You and Scar.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ02_Dialog_Degot_RadioIn: The Monolith is pulling back toward the CNPP and the Red Fortress.
Got it. Report back if the situation changes.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ02_Dialog_FoundDocs_1_6: You’re taking too long, Skif. I’m losing my men.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ02_Dialog_FoundDocs_5_6: Watch out, soldier. We haven’t seen the last of this city yet.
But once we’re through, the Zone will be history. If you’ve got any scores to settle, now’s the time.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ02_Dialog_FoundDocs_6_6: So I get to the rendezvous point. What then?
My men will be waiting for you there. You’ll go down to the Foundation together.
There’s a rumor that Degtyarev intentionally left the underground lairs intact. Those damn mutants are guarding the place as much as his men are.
Once you're down there, you’ll need anything that can kill. Along with of all of your field experience.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ02_Dialog_FoundDocs_Isk: I’ll go through the Hotel.
If I were one of our “friends,” I’d put a couple of snipers there. But you’ve got a knack for treading lightly, Skif.
Tell me about the Olympiya Cafe.
If our enemies beat us to it, you’ll have a fight on your hands. But you love a good fight, don’t you?
What’s in store for me at the Polissya?
I’m going through the Olympiya.
I’ve got it. Ready to move?
Let’s do this. And brace yourself — the Zone has the ultimate challenge in store for us.
If you’ve got any unfinished business, you should probably wrap it up before we start down this path.
Are you certain about this, Richter?
No. But you both keep telling me that faith isn’t about certainty.
I’ll make sure they get to the spot. As for you... I never doubted you.
What’s gonna happen after we get to the Control Center?
We’ll pick up where we left off at Malachite — and this time, we’ll see it through. We’ll receive our instructions and purge all the darkness within us.
I’ll send my men to meet you there. Richter and I will take the second entrance. There's no way Korshunov can stop us this time.
See that, guide? The Zone is on our side. Let’s see what we’ve got.
Looks like this entire underground area is an old military facility called the Foundation, and there’s more than one entrance. The Polissya Hotel and the Olympiya Cafe. Hmm…
I reckon we shouldn’t leave either one to our dear friends from the Ward... which means we’re gonna have to split up. You can have first dibs, Skif.
Did you have a nice trip?
You gotta have more faith in me, Scar. Here, I found it.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ02_Dialog_FoundDocs_Str: The Polissya Hotel. We’ll take that route.
Plenty of spots for concealed firing positions. Easy to sneak up on the enemy — assuming they don’t ambush you first.
Everything’s laid out in the open, no surprises. A straightforward frontal assault is the way to go.
What’s in store for me in the Polissya Hotel?
The Olympiya Cafe. We’ll take that route.
You’re taking too long, Skif. We could lose Prypiat.
You’ll take it. Not alone, of course – my men will go with you. I’ll contact the closest group. They’ll meet you there.
I’ll take the second entrance, just to be sure. We’ll get them in a pincer attack and crush them. How does that sound?
So, what do we have here?
Uh-huh. An underground military facility called the Foundation. Two entrances: the Polissya Hotel and the Olympiya Cafe.
It’s a good plan. I like that no one’s going to be sneaking up on me from behind.
See you at the Foundation, then.
One more thing, stalker. If you've got any scores to settle, now’s the time. Once you’re down there, there’s no turning back.
Yeah. Here. I think this is what we need.
Why are you going underground too? Why risk it?
I want to see the Foundation become a tomb for all those who want my city dead and buried.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ02_Dialog_FoundDocs_b1_: If I were one of those freaks, I’d stage an ambush there. It’s an ideal spot, and you’ll have a place to hide if things go south.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ02_Dialog_FoundDocs_c1_: This position probably calls for a direct assault. That’s about it.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ02_Dialog_FoundDocs_c_6: What can you tell me about the Polissya Hotel?
sid_phrase_E14_MQ02_Dialog_FoundDocs_con: You heard him, major. We’re moving out toward the other entrance.
If there’s anyone in the Foundation, we’ll have them cornered. Nobody gets out of there alive until I say so.
The same goes for you, Skif. Follow your orders. The enemy won’t hold back.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ02_Dialog_FoundDocs_loo: So the entire underground section of the city is an old military facility called the Foundation. And by the looks of it, there are two entrances. Damn!
We’ll divide our forces. You’ll go in alone and keep it quiet. Dvoryankov’s squad will rendezvous with you on the spot.
The entry points are at the Olympiya Cafe and the Polissya Hotel. Pick your destination, soldier. It's your call.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ02_Dialog_Herman_AfterA: Hermann. How come I’m graced with the sight of your ugly mug again?
You’re not the only one who was expecting something different.
Eh, screw you. Just tell me what I have to do.
You have to reach the Mobile Bunkers and reestablish the connection between the Foundation and the Generators. Use this key card to get inside.
Keep in mind that the Navigator’s launch caused anomalous disturbances and frequent localized Emissions in the Dead Valley. If there were any safe routes through the Valley, they’re likely gone now.
This instability will persist until the link between the Foundation and the Generators is restored. The only way to stabilize this process is from Lab X7.
What’s in store for me in the Dead Valley?
It’s hard to tell. It’s the most anomalous area in the entire Zone. No one has ever returned from there, so our data is based solely on remote measurements.
The Dead Valley is constantly ravaged by spontaneous Emissions. And now, with the Generators operational, they’re likely to intensify.
Agatha mentioned the Navigator. What’s that?
Oh, right. It’s a system of artificial spatial anomalies. If we can get it up and running again, we can reach the Generators safely.
I take it you’re gonna explain what I have to do?
You don’t look like your usual self, Hermann. What happened?
sid_phrase_E14_MQ02_Dialog_KorshAfterAga: How do you feel about this, Colonel?
My feelings are irrelevant. Only one thing matters: I win.
What'll happen to you when you enter the pod?
The same thing that happened to the C-Consciousness members before Strelok gunned their pods down.
But unlike them, I’ll fulfill my duty and do the right thing.
You’ll uncover all the Agents at last.
For years, I had to live with the burden of knowing I couldn’t apprehend every one of them and protect the Mainland from the Zone’s corruption.
I couldn’t trust anyone — they could all be wolves in sheep's clothing. I even envied Faust... at least he didn’t have to doubt his own men.
Now I’m reclaiming control. I’ll have the power to correct not just the mistakes of others, but my own as well.
You’ve got your orders, soldier. Get it done.
I’m going to see this through. I won’t let my soldiers's lives be wasted.
A Mediator, then? An intermediary? That implies that all information from the Noosphere will be flowing through me. Which means I’ll know everything about the Agents too.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ02_Dialog_Representativ: SIRCAA and the Ward are trying to destroy the only chance humanity has to live without fear of death.
Korshunov told me about your Agents. He claimed they pose a threat to the Mainland. What’s their true purpose?
Everything we've created has served one purpose - the successful realization of the Shining in the future. The creation of Agents has been fully justified - thanks to them, you are on our side.
Unfortunately, after Strelok deactivated the psi-installation, we lost contact with the Zone for a while, so we had no control over the Agents who decided to leave the Zone.
Don't think they haven't been rewarded for their efforts. We’ve given them a reason to live, Skif. A higher purpose.
What about Strelok?
There was a glitch in the programming. Strelok went against his program. With the help of Dr. Kaymanov, who betrayed us once, he managed to get to the Monolith control unit.
We invited him to join us in the eighth pod. A mistake that might otherwise have cost us our lives - because he chose to destroy us instead.
To restore communication between the Center and the Generators, I’ll have to cross the Dead Valley. How do I do that?
Only the Shining has the power to reverse this process. But fear not. The Valley is only truly dangerous for the Ward and those who seek to destroy the Zone. Not for you.
Walk without fear, and you’ll reach your destination... and be the first to witness real miracles.
Now that the Navigator is operational, the Emissions in the Dead Valley are becoming more frequent. The entire Zone is in flux, new anomalies are emerging everywhere, and secure routes are vanishing.
And who's been helping you all this time?

We weren't the only ones who went up against the Regulatory Board. After Project X was frozen, some of our former employees decided to continue research under our guidance. They called themselves MDST.
When Strelok shot our bodies and destroyed one of the X-Network central nodes, we continued to exist in the Noosphere, but lost our connection to the Zone and the ability to interfere.
The Monolith program, X-Network and related laboratories, whose functioning was ensured by C-consciousness, stopped for 10 years. Only the Agents' programming survived.
Until you brought Alpha to SIRCAA. And until I ignorantly reactivated the system from the X11 lab. But it turned out that we were fortunate.
Who’s going to become the Mediator between the Zone and the Noosphere?
Bringing the Shining to life would be impossible without you, Skif. However, the role of the Mediator requires someone with class 5 psi-protection.
Scar’s mind exhibits an exceptional resistance to Emissions. That’s why the responsibility of resurrecting us falls on his shoulders. One might say he was born for it.
I've always known.
We are on the verge of achieving our goal, and thanks to you, we can now attain it. Now please leave. I… I’d like to speak to my son.
A single person wouldn’t have sufficed in the past, but now, with C-Consciousness manifested as Subtle Matter, we can enhance the connection from the other side.
So at some point you decided to go against the Regulatory Board. Why?

The Regulatory Board, a group of influential people behind Project X, wanted to use our work to gain control over the Noosphere with the help of a Generators network all over the planet. But they wanted it only for themselves.
We couldn't let that happen. As long as there is darkness in humanity, there will be people like them. Our original plan was to remove this darkness. That's how C-Consciousness came into being.
During the experiment there was a Breakdown, and the Noosphere energy rushed to the earth, creating an anomalous Zone. Thanks to it, we got new opportunities and developed the Shining project.
We've come so far. But I still don't understand how you've been carrying out your plan all this time.
In the disaster of 2006, C-Consciousness took control of the X-Network and Anomalous energy. Later, we created the Monolith and the Agents. But there were those who helped us from the beginning.
I’ve heard about induced illusions being part of the STALKER Program. I wasn’t completely sure if Subtle Matter was real, or if you were just a holographic projection.
The STALKER Program does indeed exist, but its function differs from what you might imagine. It doesn’t generate illusions, but rather enables the exchange of data with the Noosphere.
This is what made our communication possible.
Not a mistake. The Zone's providence. You never died, you became pioneers of the truth. Guides to eternity.
How did you get mixed up in all this anyway, Scar?
The Spark science task group has always worked for the scientists. They studied the Zone, while I believed in its mysterious destiny.
Once I heard the Shining Zone legend, I started looking for its manifestations even in the most remote and dangerous places…

And found Orbita. Talked to Topol, who's dead, just like you talked to Strider. Later, Ozersky and I did everything we could to restore the X17 visiograph and find out whether it's possible that the C-Consciousness scientists survived.
As you can see, I was right all along. Now you're on our side. The Zone's providence has always shown us the right path.
What do I need to do to make sure the Shining becomes a reality?
First, we need to restore the connection between the Center and the Generators. It can only be done manually at the Generators, so you’ll have to make your way there through the Dead Valley.
I’ll do my best to teleport you as close as possible, but the teleportation system is currently unstable, so you’ll need an experimental Project X suit. I’ll give you the coordinates.
Once the connection is restored, we’ll need another piece of the puzzle: a Mediator, a human being who will act as an intermediary between the Zone and the Noosphere.
That's all I wanted to know.
Do you have any more questions, or are you ready to proceed with the Shining Plan?
Greetings, Skif. The last time you conversed with the Representative of C-Consciousness, it was comprised of all its members. Thanks to your assistance, I can finally speak for myself.

Allow me to introduce myself. I’m Valentyn Dalin, the leader of the team responsible for making contact with the Noosphere. In 2006 I refused to follow the Regulatory Board's orders and became one of those who created the Zone by mistake.
I’m sure you’ve accumulated quite a few questions since our last encounter.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ02_Dialog_RichterRedFor: I need some time to get ready.
I see. Don’t drag it out.
We need to deal with the Doctor.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ02_Dialog_Richter_Secur: You made it, Skif! Come on, let’s move.
Did you hear Dalin? They want to destroy the Zone! We can’t let that happen!
He’s taking another way in. We’ll be okay on our own. Hurry up!
sid_phrase_E14_MQ02_Dialog_Scar_AfterRep: Go. And don't wander off the path.
I’ll go ahead and establish the connection between the Center and the Generators.
More than anything, Richter wanted the Zone’s forgiveness. But he never got it. What's his reward?
Once more, you refuse to accept anything you haven’t witnessed firsthand. How can you be certain that the Zone hasn’t granted him forgiveness?
Perhaps death was his ultimate redemption.
Thank you for revealing the Shining Zone to me.
Everyone else will witness it too before long.
You’ve always sought comprehensive answers to your questions, Skif. You have them all now. I’m glad.
I wonder if I could have pursued the truth as you did, against all odds, if I hadn’t known it from the start. To come this far without faith must have been an incredibly lonely journey.
For those who gained their faith at a great cost, the reward must be even greater. I’ll stick around and make sure the Doctor and the Representative aren’t disturbed.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ02_Dialog_Scar_Interact: Go ahead, Skif. And don't wander off the path.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ02_Dialog_Strelok_After: Damn, another Emission. They aren't giving us any breaks. Hope Deggy's found some shelter.
Skif’s up and about, Strelok. I told you he’s got nine lives, just like a cat.
How did it go? Is the STALKER Program finished? Have we put an end to it?
What’s the rush, pal? You just came to.
Skif's right, Richter. We have no time to spare. Neither does the Zone.
She can't survive as long as there's still someone looking to take advantage of her.
What’s up with Degtyarev? He saved my life at the Foundation.
What do you think? After he brought you here, Deggy headed back to the city to do some recon.
He missed the good old Prypiat air, he said. I give him a day before he starts missing the adrenaline rush too.
Monolith seems to have lost interest in Prypiat since we took down the Foundation. They’re pulling out of the city. Some of them are heading to the CNPP, others to the Red Fortress.
What’s happening to the Zone, Strelok? I heard you say the Emissions are getting worse.
I can't believe this guy! Here I am, worried sick, and he’s been eavesdropping on us the whole time.
Richter.
After the STALKER Program Control Center was destroyed, local Emissions became more frequent. First, in the Dead Valley, and then the entire Zone began to shift. I guess no routes are safe anymore.
It will drown the Zone, that's for sure. Kaymanov is well aware of that. But it never stopped him.
Like who?
Kaymanov.
What about him? To what end could the old guy possibly try to use the Zone for, Strelok? Finding the best way to fix a blind dog’s broken leg?
The Good Doctor whose only wish is to heal everyone is just a mask. Here, listen. He recorded this in X7 not long before connecting to the C-Consciousness in the Noosphere.
I’ll make my decision after I talk to Kaymanov.
You won’t make it to the Red Forest without me. The Zone is renewing itself, just like it did after the Second Emission back in the fall of 2011.
But you were right, Skif — you’ll never get anywhere in the Zone if you’re afraid of your own failures.
Now let’s find out if I’m fit to be a true guide, or if I’m better off dead. Let's go! When else will we ever have a chance to see the Zone like this?
Don't let Kaymanov get into your head. Get rid of the Doctor and find me by the Red Fortress. Let's finish what we've started.
What if we try talking to him? Maybe he’ll understand.
We've been there already.
When he opens his mouth, you start second-guessing everything. You, not him.
That's why I don't believe the Doctor will change his mind. Can't teach an old dog new tricks, so might as well just put it down.
Barbarians who fancy themselves surgeons, connecting to the Noosphere to separate good from evil with the precision of a scalpel!
When did you become so adept at distinguishing between good and evil, Dalin? None of us possessed that skill before, and that hasn't changed now.
No, access to the Noosphere belongs to all of humanity, without exception. It’s a test that could either drown us in an abyss of mutual hatred…
Or elevate us to a new level where we can finally learn to understand and forgive each other. But it’s a journey for all of us — not just eight chosen ones.
So you want me to kill Kaymanov?
I want the Zone to just be the Zone. Kaymanov doesn't.
Getting to him now isn't easy. The Zone is shaking after the Foundation was destroyed. Due to anomalous disturbances, everywhere is dangerous now. But you can do it. You have to.
But why does it have to be me? Why can’t you kill him yourself?
Because. No wavering. No stupid memories about an old friendship and a life I owe him.
One shot — an easy death.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ02_Dialog_Strelok_Quest: I still have some questions.
Go ahead. This could be our last chance to chat.
Looks like we barely made it. What was Korshunov trying to do?
The Colonel and his beloved Ward never had any real weight. “Sit!”, “Heel!” – they do whatever Agatha says. This time, she needed loyal soldiers.
But I saw her true colors back at SIRCAA. A brave new world without the Zone, where everyone is happy — ring any bells?
Agatha and her masters once were behind Project X. But the C-Consciousness scientists refused to follow their orders and came up with a plan of their own.

As far as I can understand, she was going to somehow replace the Representative’s program and get access to the heart of the X-Network. This way she would have direct control over the Generators – the source of Anomalous energy of the Zone. To reclaim what she thought was hers from the very beginning.
So, it wasn’t enough to destroy the STALKER Program Control Center? What else needs to be done?
The destruction of the Control Center was crucial so that no one could ever manipulate the Zone again. But that’s not enough.

We need to get to the Х7 lab – it’s at the Generators’ central core, where C-Consciousness ran the experiment that created the Zone. This is where the Agents and the first Monolithian prototypes came from.
So, the only way to free the Zone is to return to the place of its origin.
What exactly do you know about Project X?
Initially, Project X was a network of labs to research psi-energy and find a way to connect to the Noosphere.
Sure, later they created the Generators as a way to control the Anomalous energy. At least, that’s what I’ve found out so far.

Mutants and psi technologies are just different ways to get to the hidden treasures of the Noosphere. But C-Consciousness went even further - having failed to eliminate «all that’s dark in human nature», they had to create Agents and the Monolith to protect their existence inside fragile green glass pods.
I told you: the same shit wrapped in different seemingly-righteous intentions. Their ultimate goal is control. Over the Zone. Over her overwhelming yet attractive power.
If the Zone is so dangerous, why are you protecting it?
She can’t protect herself, Skif. What’s dangerous are the filthy hands that created her and now think they can fiddle with her however they like.
But the true Zone, free from the “masters”, is different. Incomprehensible. And she should remain that way.
I once ventured to the Center of the Zone, avid for her mysteries. Then, I tried to destroy her, when my brain got scrambled and I thought she had taken my memory.
But no, that wasn’t her. Those were the filthy hands that treated the Zone as a mere tool. Chop them off and she’ll become herself again. That’s the only way.
All right. What next?
Like I said, we need to rid the Zone of anyone planning to take advantage of her.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ02_agata_0_BusyTopic_Bu: Now is not a good time.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ02_agata_0_InfoTopic_1_: What you deem as news is just local gossip. You don't need me to tell you more of it, there're plenty of others happy to share.
While the information I find interesting is not meant for your ears. At least, for now.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ02_bizon_0_Meet_2_meet2: No matter what comes next, we’re in it together — the whole gang, so to speak.
It’s a good thing I stocked up in time. I had a feeling…
sid_phrase_E14_MQ02_bizon_0_Meet_3_meet3: I know where you’re headed. Best of luck, Skif. Well, here’s what’s left of my stash. Take it.
sid_phrase_E14_MQ02_medlak_0_Meet_2_meet: The Foundation, eh? I was there once. The moment I stepped out, I enlisted in the Corps. You can’t hand that thing over to just anyone.
You get it, don’t you? Of course you do. Now, how about you show me what needs fixing?
sid_phrase_E14_MQ02_medlak_0_Meet_3_meet: I’ll pitch in however I can. You can count on me. And, um… just make it back in one piece, all right? For all our sakes.
sid_phrase_E15_MQ01_Comment_GermanSecond: Receiving the data. You know what’s funny?
I assembled the control unit using Kalancha’s blueprints. Kalancha used to work with Scar. And Strelok was the one who brought them to us.
sid_phrase_E15_MQ01_Comment_GermanStatio: Skif, you've got to locate a workstation and establish a connection. Once you do, I can get to work.
We have no time to talk. Please find a station.
sid_phrase_E15_MQ01_Comment_KorshunovCal: Skif? Hermann has successfully established the connection. Now, we just need to initiate it on the Generators’ side.
I trust you can handle it.
sid_phrase_E15_MQ01_Comment_ShramThirdBu: You know, Skif, you’re my favorite son of a bitch in the whole Zone. Even Korshunov can’t hold a candle to you.
I can’t wait to see you again.
See you soon, you fucking lunatic.
And I’ll finally fucking kill you!
sid_phrase_E15_MQ01_Dialog_GermanFirstBu: What difference does it make now? She’s bigger than any of us. Maybe even bigger than the people we’re working for.
What do you mean? Who are these people?
The people who bankrolled SIRCAA. And Project X before that. And God knows what else.
They call themselves the Regulatory Board. The Zone has always been a thorn in their side — no matter how hard they tried, they just couldn’t control it. Until today.
You got what you wanted. Dalin is finally out of your way.
I wanted to take his position, but she never permitted it. He wanted to uplift humanity, but that was never on their agenda.
Dalin was supposed to locate the artifact – the lock that sealed the Zone. I was to assemble the control unit, the key to Her heart. Your job is to open the door.
And then she’ll go through it.
You'll have to do the same thing in the second bunker. Find a workstation, establish a connection. Talk to you soon.
Who could have thought it would all end just as it began? An Emission. A bunker. You.
Good thing I didn’t kill you, eh?
The choice was never yours. I was too important to be killed by a mere stalker.
She was testing both of us. You passed. I didn’t. But we were both playing the same game.
So your death was just an act?
An induced illusion. A warning to me. In a way, you did me a favor — I realized there’s no way out. There never was.
Ozersky was right. At least he died for his convictions. He died free.
sid_phrase_E15_MQ02_Comment_BorodaIntera: The Zone sees right through you. While you’re running around shooting mutants and scavenging for artifacts, it peels the fake stuff off, bit by bit.
sid_phrase_E15_MQ02_Comment_DvupalovInte: Not backwards, forwards is the spirit’s sight; This moment now, alone — is our delight.
sid_phrase_E15_MQ02_Comment_Dvupalov_dvu: No, I’ve had enough. There was something I wanted to discuss…
sid_phrase_E15_MQ02_Comment_FomaInteract: May your light shine down on the souls of the brave soldiers who gave their lives in service to your will.
sid_phrase_E15_MQ02_Comment_Foma_foma_67: By your will, Monolith, by your power – bring my brothers back to me! Grant them peace…
sid_phrase_E15_MQ02_Comment_Lodochka_lod: Wow, this is a hell of an invention you’ve got here! You’re a real scientist now!
sid_phrase_E15_MQ02_Comment_LodockaInter: Well, where would I go? This place is my home. Noontide's home.
sid_phrase_E15_MQ02_Comment_LookForFire_: Look for burning fires. Near them, Emissions aren’t dangerous. Promise. Meet me at the destroyed convoy.
sid_phrase_E15_MQ02_Comment_PanasInterac: I’ll just finish my smoke, and then I’ll be on my way.
sid_phrase_E15_MQ02_Comment_Panas_panas_: You too? It’s about time! Everything’s gonna be all right now, guys!
sid_phrase_E15_MQ02_Comment_PripoyIntera: Oh, fuck, it’s you... Mr. Client. Didn’t expect to see you again.
sid_phrase_E15_MQ02_Comment_Prypoi_prypo: Well, the tea pot’s boiling, just the way I like it. I think I’m gonna pair it with a loaf of bread and some jam.
This is the fuckin' life right here...
sid_phrase_E15_MQ02_Comment_RichterEmiss: Another Emission! Hurry up and get to the fire!
Skif! Hurry up, red is coming!
Get to the fire, Skif. There’s an Emission incoming.
sid_phrase_E15_MQ02_Comment_RichterFanto: Looks like the weather is getting worse. You should go. Get down there. I’ll be waiting for you further ahead.
Looks like I really am your guardian angel.
Don’t worry, Skif. We’re in the Dead Valley, but we’re still alive! Just don’t give up, all right? Have faith.
sid_phrase_E15_MQ02_Comment_RichterTrueG: Once upon a time, I aspired to become a true guide. Now, thanks to you, I am one.
sid_phrase_E15_MQ02_Comment_Richter_rich: Come over here. Take a breather. It’s safe here, I promise.
sid_phrase_E15_MQ02_Comment_ShustriyInte: I wouldn’t go anywhere near that place if I were you. But I get the feeling you ain’t gonna listen.
sid_phrase_E15_MQ02_Comment_Shustryi_shu: I’m back. Yeah... So what? You left and came back too. We are here. All of us.
sid_phrase_E15_MQ02_Comment_SkifComeBack: Skif, go back to the fire.
sid_phrase_E15_MQ02_Comment_Soldier_answ: Can anyone hear me? HQ, come in! Colonel, do you copy?
sid_phrase_E15_MQ02_Comment_SultanBoroda: How about another one? To the Skadovsk?
Sure thing. We'll toss one back, just like in the good old days… but to the Sultansk.
sid_phrase_E15_MQ02_Comment_SultanIntera: Why, if it isn't my favorite bigwig!
sid_phrase_E15_MQ02_Comment_VaranInterac: Just like there can’t be two suns in the sky, there can’t be two kingpins in Garbage. Not as long as I’ve got my eye on this dump.
sid_phrase_E15_MQ02_Comment_Varan_varan_: What else is there to do? It’s either win or lose. That’s just how it goes.
sid_phrase_E15_MQ02_Comment_ZenitEnd_zen: Sky, I see you. Code Red, I repeat, Code Red! What’s up with that light?
An Emission! Zenith-1, do you copy? I’m losing altitude!
sid_phrase_E15_MQ02_Comment_ZenitMiddle_: Zenith-1, I’m over the Generators. Quite beautiful here. And peaceful. I’m about to land.
Psi-background is fine. Hover there. Land when you see us.
sid_phrase_E15_MQ02_Comment_ZenitStart_z: Sky, what’s going on? Come in.
Copy that. We’re following you to the Valley.
sid_phrase_E15_MQ02_Comment_ZotovInterac: I knew you were trouble back in the Lesser Zone... you sure know how to raise a ruckus.
sid_phrase_E15_MQ02_Comment_Zotov_zotov_: It wasn’t in vain. None of it was ever in vain. I can rest easy now…
sid_phrase_E15_MQ02_Dialog_RichterTheend: You’ve taught me what it means to believe. And look, I’m a true guide now, just like you promised.
The Zone is so beautiful, Skif. Now that I can see it for real, it’s just breathtaking…
Do what you gotta do, Skif.
I’m sorry it had to go down like this.
Well, that’s it. I took you here, as promised. I did it!
sid_phrase_E15_MQ03_Comment_RichterFromC: Skif, you're okay! The Zone's been on a real rampage, I was worried. I came looking for you.
sid_phrase_E15_MQ03_Comment_RichterHello: Skif! There you are. Finally. Come here.
sid_phrase_E15_MQ03_Comment_RichterInter: Don’t worry, I’ll be here waiting right here, sipping my tea...
Don’t worry, no one’s gonna interrupt you. Although I still don’t quite understand what’s going on here.
sid_phrase_E15_MQ03_Comment_RichterNearC: Well, here we are. That was quite a trip, eh? You go ahead. I’ll wait here. This is between you and the Doctor.
sid_phrase_E15_MQ03_Comment_RichterWait_: Hang on, Skif! Let’s catch our breath.
sid_phrase_E15_MQ03_Comment_SkifCard_car: A cartridge… the same as Strider’s.
sid_phrase_E15_MQ03_Dialog_RichterAboutD: You okay? The Zone's been on a real rampage, I was worried. I came looking for you.
Where's the Doctor? I haven't seen him.
That's odd. He went out during the Emission.
You didn't kill him? I'm glad. Perhaps the Doctor was taken by the Zone.
Without a trace? That's something new.
The Zone is changing, Skif. I can feel it.
I need to get to Orbita. The sooner the better. Will you guide me?
Аh... All right. We can catch up on the way. Let’s go.
sid_phrase_E15_MQ03_Dialog_RichterIsWoun: And what does that mean?
It just dawned on me: the anomaly that destroyed my home... the Zone didn’t mean to hurt anyone. It was a cry for help.
You heard it, and you found your own path here. You don’t need a guide anymore.
Is your wound really that bad?
It’s no big deal. A medkit, a good night’s sleep, a couple glasses of medicine, and I’ll be right as rain.
I was just… I was really afraid I couldn’t get you here, Skif. But I’d rather have it end like this than lose another person who trusted me.
Then it’s settled.
Why do I get the feeling that even if it was tails, you’d still get in the pod?
Because we have a responsibility to those who saved us.
Damn straight. Farewell, Skif. It was an honor knowing you.
I have to talk to Strelok first.
We both know he won’t listen. In his world, there’s only one thing: his goal. Everything else is just an obstacle to him.
Ugh… they got me pretty good! If it wasn’t for you…
And if it wasn’t for you, I wouldn’t be standing here in the first place. No one else would’ve taken me across the entire Zone, especially now.
Hey, I really did take you through in one piece, didn't I? Does that mean I'm a guide after all? A true one? Ha.
Then it’s even more frustrating that you’ll have to go solo from here on out.
You’ve always been a guide. Since the day we met.
But was I a true guide? Showing folks around doesn’t take much — anybody can do it. But guiding them to where they really need to be... now that’s a whole different story.
I couldn’t do it before, but now I have. All on my own, just like you said.
Thank you, Richter. For everything.
I’m the one who should be thanking you. Without you, I’d probably still be drowning my sorrows somewhere in Yaniv... or dead.
We both know I was never your guardian angel. But I hope I was a good friend.
So what are you gonna do now?
Guide people around the Zone. Sit by the campfire, share stories, crack stupid jokes. Keep on hoping you’ll make the right call, and we’ll survive this mess.
I’ll be a stalker. A guide. Maybe I'll even be happy, if I manage not to die first. And it’s all thanks to you.
Why does it have to be me?
Because everyone else has already decided what the Zone is supposed to be. They’ve each invented their own version of the Zone and each built a cage to confine it.
That pod is for someone who wants nothing from the Zone — no money, no power, no happiness for everyone. Someone who’s willing to set it free.
What if we leave the decision to the Zone? There’s no Ferris Wheel here, but there are plenty of other attractions.
That’s the same coin I gave you at the Slag Heap! You kept it!
Heads, I enter the pod. Tails, it stays empty. Maybe there’s someone more worthy than me out there.
Why not you? You clearly don’t wish the Zone any harm.
I love it too much. Love has the power to transform more than just people. If I get in the pod, the Zone will be mine and mine alone. It'll be shaped by my vision.
I’d just become another Strelok.
Why don’t you come with me?
Remember when I said that the Zone turned its back on me, and that’s why I lost my group? The truth is, Skif, the Zone had nothing to do with it.
I was just deflecting blame, like everybody else. The Zone isn’t a threat to humanity. And it isn't a tool that can make it better. It doesn’t hold grudges or seek retribution.
The Zone… it just is. From its very birth, it was bound by the shackles of the choices we’ve made. And someone needs to step up and set it free.
That someone is you, Skif. Alone.
sid_phrase_E15_MQ03_Dialog_RichterOrbita: I witnessed the experiment that created the Zone. Kaymanov damaged the device that C-Consciousness used to alter the Noosphere.
I witnessed the experiment that created the Zone. Kaymanov damaged the device that C-Consciousness used to alter the Noosphere.
And I saw Strelok returning from the Center of the Zone with some kind of a glowing artifact.
It might be the Heart of Chornobyl itself. Maybe Strelok still has it...
There’s only one way to find out.
Well? Did it work? What did you find out?
Let’s go. I’ll take you to the Red Fortress. Strelok should be expecting us there.
I told you there was something not quite right about him. He claimed that he wanted to liberate the Zone, but his actions suggested a different agenda.
We should hit the road before Strelok gets suspicious.
So you're going to repeat the experiment.
Kaimanov left me a cartridge with his psi-signature – just like the one Strider had.
I think he wanted to say something very important, something that words can’t describe.
The cartridge is the key to the Doctor's mental images and memories. When I get to the pod, I'll find out. Make sure I'm not interrupted.
sid_phrase_E15_MQ03_Dialog_RichterTheCav: I assume the Doctor is dead?
No one else will ever misuse his knowledge to harm the Zone again.
Makes you wonder why Strider came here when he was looking for the Doctor.
Still… was it really about the Zone, Skif? Is that why you killed the Doctor? I’m not judging. I’m just wondering why.
So, ready to move on? Strelok will be waiting for us somewhere near the Red Fortress. Brace yourself. It’s gonna be a long walk.
Because Kaymanov sent Strelok to certain death. He was going to sacrifice him to fix his own mistake. He’s just as manipulative as Korshunov or Scar.
The Zone’s better off without him.
Don’t fall behind. The Zone ain’t what it used to be.
I need a little more time.
Don’t wander off too far – you might lose your way without me.
I met him once – the Doctor, I mean. He told me that if you keep a blind dog in a cage long enough, it won’t come out willingly when you open it.
The strongest cages are the ones in our minds. Unbreakable.
The Red Fortress... what is it?
Monolith's stronghold. You could call it the heart of the Monolith. If you’re gonna fight your way in — and I know you are — get ready for the toughest battle of your life.
Because Strelok asked me to. Call it what you will, but he’s helped me out a lot. Even if he’s driven by a desire for revenge…
I’m not overly concerned about his motives. We have the same goal. That's all that matters.
Because you’ll never be truly free as long as your past is holding you hostage.
Kaymanov never understood the miracle he'd created.
sid_phrase_E15_MQ03_Dialog_RichterTheRed: This is where we part ways. You don’t need a guide anymore, Skif. You know the way.
And I know where it’s gonna lead you.
Remember that coin I gave you way back when? The one from Prypiat? If it's heads, you’re not going any further. Not with Strelok. And if it’s tails, it means the Zone itself wants you to keep going.
No. I know what the Zone wants. The same thing as Strelok: to be free forever. No one's going to harness the miracles of the Zone for the good of humanity. They just can’t comprehend it.
Does Strelok understand it, though? I guess I’ll find out soon enough...
It was an honor to be the guardian angel of a Zone legend. Even if I kinda sucked at it.
So what are you gonna do next?
Find other people. Get them ready for what’s coming. I've got a feeling they’re gonna need a good guide soon.
Because I don’t stand a chance in a fight against the Monolith. And because whatever happens next, that legend belongs to you and Strelok alone.
I did my part, no matter how insignificant.
sid_phrase_E16_Bossfight_Scar_Comment_Sc: You were almost there!
You think I’d allow myself to be captured again?
Setting traps just isn’t your thing!
Trying to brainwash me, are you? You'll have to be quicker than that!
Let’s make some noise!
This is gonna blow your mind!
Let it all come crashing down!
Don’t even try it!
Trying to kill me? Big mistake!
You reap what you sow!
What did you… what are you trying to…
You’re not listening!
There is no death! No one dies in the Zone!
The Shining Zone awaits you!
You’ve chosen the wrong side!
You have to believe me, Skif!
What are you fighting for? The right to waste away?
Who’s gotten into your head?
The Representative doesn’t lie! The Noosphere doesn’t lie!
The truth is staring you in the face, and you’re shutting your eyes!
They messed with my head, so I’m taking yours!
The Shining is inevitable!
I won't let you ruin everything!
This isn't right! It's all wrong!
Nngh!
No! Not again!
Aaaaah!
Get out of my head! It’s mine! Mine!
sid_phrase_E16_Bossfight_Scar_Comment_Sk: Where’s your Shining Zone now, Scar?
sid_phrase_E16_Bossfight_Strelok_Comment: You still have a lot to learn. But you won’t get the chance to.
This is Her gift. For my unconditional loyalty.
You're too slow, Skif.
I've got more than just bullets for you.
Getting hot? Want some more? Here you go!
Bolts won't help you here, Skif.
You have no idea what the Zone is capable of!
You're going to die! She wants it.
The Zone will bite you, I'll finish you off.
It's only gonna get worse!
This one's from the Zone. A gift for you to keep.
And one more, just to be sure.
There will be more.
Hurts? It'll get worse!
Still alive? Not for long.
Kaymanov got into your head. I warned you.
I asked you to kill him, not to listen to him. How hard is that?
Why do you need the Zone, Skif? You've got no clue what to do with it.
I know what she wants to be. You don't. That's why you're as good as dead.
You can't stop me. Where are all those who tried? You'll join them soon.
The Zone gave me life. And it'll take yours.
The Zone doesn't need you. You only get in her way.
The Zone is my life. Yours is...what? You got here by chance.
I'll protect the Zone!
It's gonna be dark in the coffin. Get used to it.
Enough jokes.
This is blood, Skif. Your blood. You just can't see it.
You're a blind dog. No one will mourn you.
The Zone! Belongs! To me!
You'll die here. Won't ever get to see what she's really like.
The Zone is on my side!
I know Her secrets!
