Loners are afraid of Emissions, but Emissions are something you can hide from. People, on the other hand... there’s no hiding from them. They’ll get you anywhere.
When I got here, I thought life in the Zone was gonna be simple. No laws, you’re your own boss, just you against nature… man, was I stupid.
I was studying to be an archaeologist, and then I ended up here. Bet the other guys in my class wish they had an internship like this!
The Zone’s taught me two things. One: always keep your gun with you, and make sure it's loaded.
And two: always keep the goddamn thing loaded! You never know what kinda shitfucker is gonna jump out at you.
Me? I’m small potatoes. A little trade here, a little side gig there... let Duty play heroes if they want to!
I've always felt like Duty was living in the past, but now I’m certain of it. We’re making good money here, and they keep running around chasing monsters.
The loners are starting shit with the Ward. I don’t get it. If they don’t like their lot in life, that’s not the Wardens' fault. You've got brains - you figure shit out, not whine about everything.
Some stalkers are always whining — nowhere to sleep, no food, pants all worn out… learn to make some scratch, bud! Can't put the light of the Zone in your stomach.
Everybody wanted to get to the center of the Zone, and so did I… till I realized there’s no point. With a good head on your shoulders, you can even make it big at the Cordon.

Myklukha is more of a friend than a boss to us. Voronin won’t even sit by the fire with his men. That’s why those Duty guys always look like they ate a medium rare pile of shit cooked on that campfire.
Everyone in the Zone's got beef. We stay away from that crap. Just be chill, don’t show up empty-handed, and we’ll treat you like fam.
That goes about artifacts outside the Zone's true, totes. We started that shit, my bros and me. Now our baby’s all grown up.
As for me, I’m fine with Duty. They kill mutants, and with their own ammo. stalkers get the goods home faster if they don’t have to waste extra rounds.
Only morons believe that the Zone can be changed or controlled. It can bend anybody until they break.
SIRCAA this, SIRCAA that... but whose idea was it to repurpose those lab medkits? Ozersky's! And what did Dalin do for people? That’s right — zilch.
There’s no order in this place. Even less than at the Slag Heap. But what can you expect from those eggheads? They're not of this world.
So, you want to earn some coupons? You’re in the right place, pal. These lab rats always need something — bring them an artifact, guide them someplace... you’ll get plenty of work.
There was one loner here, Gosha Ping. He had a nasty cough, so the eggheads offered to treat him with some artifact. He lost all his hair after that. Now he's still got a cough, plus he's bald.
You should be careful here. It’s not Project X, of course, but they'll still try to talk you into some shit. Don't let yourself get involved in those crazy experiments of theirs.
Perimeter security kept trying to stop us from getting in... well, those scientists made a huge stink, and guess what — now we’re welcome anytime.
Sometimes I just want to quit and walk outside the Perimeter. But… what am I supposed to do on the Mainland? Who cares about me there? Well, even here… nah. We ain’t goin’ nowhere, loner.
I go into an Emission every once in a while when nobody’s looking. Not for long, of course. There’s just something familiar about it. I feel…home, feel welcome.
They say we should leave, the Ward’s gonna drive us off the island, the IPSF is gonna come, there’s no point living in a swamp anyway…
I say let ’em come. This is my swamp. I finally got a place to call my own after all these years, and I’d rather die here than roam the Zone again.
You’re aware that drinking is prohibited here, right? If you can’t live without vodka, go ahead, but never offer it to others. It’s dangerous for us to get drunk.
I hope the scientists can develop a cure for people like me. To get my memory back. Even for one day…
It’s funny, really. Sometimes I remember a word, but I don’t have a clue what it means. I end up walking all over Wild Island asking if anybody knows it.
I’d rather not know about my past, 'cuz then I’d have to live with it. I’m not sure I want that…
I ain’t complainin’. I went through hell before Strider found me… here, I get to live in peace and quiet and stay close to my own kind.
Maybe we should just go loner and live normal lives… did you hear that Quiet left to become a trader? I guess that’s kind of a way out, too.
There’s no end to these barrels. Sometimes I think the only thing left in the Zone is waste... and us.
The Ward is worse than any anomaly. You can at least guess where an anomaly might be, but nobody expects to run into the Ward... and once you’ve run into them, you’re done for.

I’m out there doing hunting jobs, running petty errands for Sidorovich. He says I don't have the right experience. So how am I supposed to get the right experience if you don’t give me the right jobs?
"Get yourself a partner," they say. How am I supposed to find one? A run in the Zone isn’t the same as going out for drinks with your buddies. You gotta be sure they've got your back.
As it stands, I’m fully confident that everyone here would shoot me in the back without a second thought just to save his own sorry ass.
Digging a grave is dangerous in the Zone. You’ll be sure to dig up something nasty... so nasty you might end up dead too. Burning bodies is no good either. Too conspicuous.
So what are we supposed to do, carry the dead to the Mainland on our backs? No way. We all knew what it meant when we came here. If I drop dead, just leave me on the spot.
The Wardens asked me to snitch for them. Promised me a lot of money — enough to retire and quit stalking. Like that’s a good thing, right? Who says I wanna quit?
No idea, bro. Some shit with Duty, some shit with a Treaty... I’m as clueless as you are, so ask someone else. I’m here to take it easy.
We’re all adrenaline junkies here. Some guys loot corpses, others hunt mutants for money or rummage around in anomalies. Me, I'm a bookie. I get the same rush, but I get to keep all my limbs.
There was this one smartass. Whenever he got drunk, he's start yappin' about how Myklukha stole the whole coupon idea from him. Why would anybody care about some loser with nothing to his name?
Here in the Zone, your rep matters way more than coupons.
Freedom used to have a real goal. We wanted to make the Zone free from any authority. And where have we ended up? We’re the Ward’s bitch now. Fucking capitalism always wins.
Freedom will be good either way, with or without the Treaty. That’s because we don’t fight the Zone, we find ways to coexist with it.
Colonel Korshunov would've cleared all the stalkers out of the Zone a long time ago if not for Dmytro Dalin’s deliberate humanism. If you see a young guy in a suit, remember to say thank you.
Dr. Dalin this, Dr. Dalin that… he’s not working alone, you know! It's the Institute for Research of the Anomalous Area, not the Institute of Dmytro Valentynovich Dalin!
I’ll never understand the people who are willing to do field work. All that rain, cold, and mud… ugh! Not to mention mutants, anomalies, and, well, stalkers. Sounds suicidal to me!
The Zone is key to the future of humanity! It has to be studied gently and with great care. You stalkers are just vandalizing the place!
Dalin may be a genius, but I’d rather wait out the Emissions in the lab. The Absorber might work today, but what about tomorrow? Science is good, but reinforced concrete is better.
How can you not tell an acid artifact from a flame one? What they do is completely different! They don’t even look alike!
They send you idiots to work with, and then you're back to square one looking for that variable you’ve been chasing for six months. A damn zombie could do a better job.
Forgive me for being blunt, but my life is far more valuable than yours.
The ability to scout, shoot straight, and find things — frankly, that's not a particularly unique skill set. Besides, you guys have a lot of internal competition.
Me, on the other hand… being able to transmute the Zone’s mercury into the gold of earthly delights — that's an invaluable skill.
Those stalkers see the Zone every day, but they can't see the forest for the trees. It's a unique, living mechanism! It needs to be studied, not milked dry, pardon the metaphor.
All right, I get the mutants, they don’t know what they’re doing. But human beings... Why are they killing each other? The Zone has enough for everyone!
Stalkers deal with Emissions, mutants, and artifacts on a daily basis. They see it all, and we analyze what they've seen. In a way, you're the Zone's eyes, and we're its brain.
Skadovsk… I hid from my first Emission there. Now it's turned into a pigsty for bandits.
I’d forgotten Skadovsk even existed. Sultansk is doing so well, it seems like it’s always been called that.
I’m doing fine. Beard’s got some decent booze, the barge keeps me safe from the Emissions… what more do you need?
Skadovsk ain’t what it used to be. In fact, it's been Sultansk for a while. Beard ain’t what he used to be, if he’s putting up with this crap.
Beard’s taking too long to retaliate against the Sultan. He’s been making plans for a good seven years now, and he still won’t give us the go-ahead.
The damn Ward keeps snooping around anomalies. We have to dive into the mud twice as often now — it’s either them, or the Sultan’s thugs.
Some rookies thought it was a good idea to take a shot at Clear Sky. Two of 'em actually managed to get out, stunned but still sane. The third one lost it. We had a hard time pullin’ him out.
I can't get any sleep here. Something's been walking circles around me in the Swamps. I keep hearing it go "splat, splat, splat..." and it's calling me, calling me into the reeds...
That old stalker San lost it. Kept sayin’ there was some train still runnin’ underground. Then he took off for the Swamps down the old tracks. He never returned, and his fate is still unknown.
The Sultan’s patrols are useless. They can scare off Fleshes, shoot a half-dead dog, and that’s it. If they as much as hear a bloodsucker, they shit their pants and run home to papa.
The Wardens think they’ve got everybody under their thumb. Those they can’t control, they pay off. You know what? I’m glad they’re thinking that way. I love surprising people.
Scar’s keeping us in the dark. The other day he came back alone, with none of the guys he left with. “Where are the boys?”, I ask him. He shrugs, “It’s the Zone. You can’t protect everybody…”
I know the Zone takes its toll. But with Scar around, the toll’s gotten too high, if you ask me.
Even a rusty piece of metal you find in an old cave could be treasure to someone. You find something - clean it up, plug it in, see what it can do. If it doesn’t make your head explode, good.
If it does, well, not too bad either. Plenty of people in the Zone are better off without heads.
The best thing about the Zone is that it promises you nothing — no riches, no discoveries, not even that you’ll live to see another day. And that’s why you live every day to the fullest.
Sure, Ozersky is a weirdo, but he’s been around longer than lots of stalkers. I’d listen to him.
People keep saying that the Zone’s not the same anymore. Like they got any clue what it's supposed to be! Do any of them know the true nature of the Zone, its inner essence?
I wish we could get our hands on SIRCAA's research on psi-protection. I wonder how far they’ve come. How do they test it, anyway?
Scar is obsessed with these old labs. Digging around all the time like a blind dog.
Of course, sometimes the stuff he digs up is amazing. Turns out after all these years, you don’t know shit about the Zone!
The factions keep struggling for control over the Zone. But the Zone doesn’t give a damn about their claims to authority. It's up to the Zone to decide who gets to stay here.
What are they studying at SIRCAA, never even poking their nose out of their labs? How can you understand something if you've never seen it? Stupid bunch, the whole lot of them.
If you want to understand the Zone, you gotta cross it on foot, look under every rock. It’s not a walk in the park, that much is true. But it gives you a different perspective.
The Wardens are turning the Zone upside down looking for our base — a base that doesn’t even exist, ha! The Zone is no place for anything permanent. If you stand still, it’ll eat you up.
SIRCAA would love to pick the Zone apart and sort it into their folders. Ha! Looks like it's gonna be the other way around. Bit by bit.
Word is, the Ward’s got a separate list for “attrition due to anomalous Zone exposure.” And the list is pretty long.
Pretty handy if you ask me. If anybody asks us if we did that Warden in, we can just say, “Nah, it wasn’t us, man, it was the Zone.”
You can bet the Zone’s got some favorites among us. Take Scar, for example. He’s one weird dude, sure, but he’s been through some shit, and survived to tell the tale.
I hope he’ll take me along on his next outing. I’d like to see the Zone for real.
So many of us diggers have given up over the years… some went back to the Mainland, others went to Prypiat looking for artifacts…
I’d head over to Jupiter too, but it’s too risky for my taste. At least this place is kinda safe.
A buddy of mine said he saw a Burer in the neighborhood. It was a foggy day, and he thought it was a bandit stashing his loot.
So he tiptoes up to the freak, and suddenly a barrel comes flying straight at his face! The poor bastard ran off so fast, he dropped his gun and almost got himself drowned in the creek.
I try to avoid places with anything new. I check my detector after every Emission, but I can’t help but think… what if there’s something our detectors can’t detect?
I’ve been here in the Lesser Zone for a while now, and I’ve never seen a mess like this before. Duty and Freedom never went this far.
The Zone’s not the same anymore. Remember the factions we used to have around? The rookies would shit themselves at a mere mention of Monolith. But tell them now, and they’re, like, “Who is that?”
The Ward wants to bring the Zone back to the Mainland. Well, fuck that. The Zone can never be tamed, and neither can we!
Anomalies and mutants — at least those are things you can see. But radiation... if your Geiger counter is dead, you might as well not leave the bar. And you should drink too! They say it helps.
I get where the blind dogs and Fleshes come from... but Snorks and Bloodsuckers?! I can’t even begin to guess what those abominations are. The more I think about 'em, the scarier they get...
sid_phrase_General_freestalkers_0_Mutant: I'll sure get a ton of coupons for this!
Now that's one hell of a prize…
Wish I could capture this… in the name of science!
I bet I wouldn't find any of you here. Guess I lost!
Hope the slugs didn't ruin the hide…
So that's what biocoupons look like!
Shame I can't bring the whole thing in.
If you didn't kill it, you ain't chopping it up.
We don't like sassy folks like you here.
Oi! Did you hunt it down? No.
Are you mad? This is mine!
Aren't there enough mutants in the Zone?
Get your hands off my loot!
Not so fast. That's my catch.
sid_phrase_General_freestalkers_0_NPC_Ch: Have you moved your stash?
Ask again louder so the whole Zone can hear you!
This jacket looks like it’s my size, but it kinda doesn’t fit…
You gotta lay off the bread and sausages.
There was that guy who got sucked into the whirligig, and…
Stop it. I’m still feeling sick from the last Emission.
How come there are so many rookies here?
’Cuz the Zone is expanding, that’s why. And more space means more people.
Still so many monsters creeping around!
Yeah. Not to mention the mutants!
Think you could tame a mutant?
I’ve heard stories of a stalker who walks around with a pseudodog, but I’ve never met him myself.
Have you heard of the chimera?
Yeah. A lot of people talk about it, but no one’s ever killed it. Or seen it, for that matter.
The cats here shriek all night long. Just like on the Mainland…
Yeah. But these cats prefer human flesh to catnip.
This one time I went to check on my stash, and there was a fresh anomaly on top of it!
Good. Guess it was more secure that way.
There’s an artifact I want real bad. It’s called the Night Star. I’d give all my coupons to get one!
Get your head outta your ass and look up at night. Sky’s full of them, all for free!
When I get back to the Mainland, I’m gonna get wasted for at least a week!
Isn't that what you do here?
Every Emission gives me a splitting headache .
You get used to it. I barely notice it anymore.
I wish I had an artifact to protect me from all the trouble here.
Yeah, I know one. It’s called a brain.
Any plans for tomorrow?
Just surviving...
I’m sick of canned meat. Can't shove it down my throat.
Spend a week on bread and water, - you'll be begging for it.
Do you hear that?
What?
The wind. It's whispering, “let’s tie one on...”
You're lookin’ pretty rough.
I haven’t slept in days. Not even vodka helps.
What day of the week is it, guys?
Who the hell cares? Not like it changes anything.
My PDA’s on the fritz again!
Just sell it to some rookie and get yourself a better one.
I found three artifacts in an anomaly today.
Oh yeah? Was one of those called “bull-fucking-shit?”
What a day, huh? No coupons, zero loot, and an empty stomach.
I’m so over today.
If I made some coupons, then today wasn’t wasted.
This is the first break I’ve taken today!
I’ll never get used to those Bloodsuckers. Been here for a year, and they still make me sick to my stomach!
I’m almost outta coupons…
Why get scared, though? Fate is fate. Whatever needs to happen, will happen.
How did I manage to break the detector? What a bummer…
We had some real artifacts back in the day! Now it’s a shitpile of junk…
I could sure go for a nice steam bath right about now…
I’ve been in the Zone for a long time now, and I’m still afraid of the Emissions.
Another day, another coupon in your pocket!
I just washed my pants yesterday, and now they're all dirty again.
Checked my stash, cleaned my gun, bought some shit… I'm calling it a day!
I gotta figure out how many coupons I need to buy a new rifle.
I had a dream about a Bloodsucker last night. It looked so real! I almost shot someone, half-awake.
They say there are tons of mutants underground. But there's a lot of loot down there too...
The Duga is a hell of a place. The howling wind, the tunes of clinging metal… beautiful.
I shoulda kept my first artifact as a memento.
I wish I had a decent guide to take me to Prypiat. Maybe then I’d risk the trip.
I’m one raid away from leaving the fucking Zone behind me for good.
What do you expect from a place called Garbage? It’s full of trash.
I can’t even remember what made me come to the Zone in the first place. And now I can’t see the point of going back to the Mainland.
I’m so craving some baked apples…
Man, this sucks. No loot, no profits… we’re gonna starve here!
Turning into a zombie is the worst. I’d rather die fast.
My knees are killing me. Guess it’s gonna rain. Either that, or there's an Emission coming.
Oh, my head hurts so bad! That’s it, no more vodka!
Yesterday was my birthday... I think. Or was it the day before? Whatever, at least I’m still alive.
Guess what, I had that dream again. The poppies.
It's been forever since I got back… still can't dream about anything other than poppies. And the smell, every morning, as if I'm lying in a field of those damn flowers, not in my sleeping bag.
And I'm so drowsy every day, blew all my coupons on energy drinks…
Sometimes I wonder if I'm still in that field…and I never woke up?
You know why Prypiat was closed down? No one cares about the anomalies, Deggy can handle just about anything. Folks go cuckoo down there.
They go out on a run, come back in an hour or so, looking…as if they'd been gone a whole year. Can't remember a thing. Other than wandering the empty streets for months.
You can't let them go back, they won't return the second time. But you can't force them to stay, either…
Remember that Stalker fellow who won’t shut up about how the Controller let him go? He went ballistic on the Shevchenko, trashed the place.
Tried to cut up another guy in his sleep. Boys rounded him up, he started crying like a baby, can't remember nothing, don't know nothing.
Rumor has it, they never let him go. Who knows what that Controller might have whispered in his ear.
How many of them never got back? Three? Five?
All-you-can-fucking-eat anomaly buffet, all the artifacts in the world for ya. And they still wandered off to that dump.
I've been there too, you know. Dropped a flashlight down there, it was on, - took forever before I lost sight of it.
And they keep going there…as if something is luring them in.
Beware of campfires at night. Especially the ones deep in the Zone. Especially if there's no one around but you.
Folks would find corpses, cold already. And weirdly, the safety on their guns would be on, and their faces were just… anywho, don't sit at them campfires.
Better to stay in the dark.
Have you seen the Prypiat Ferris Wheel?
This one guy, had a scar across half of his head, told me that the number of spins you took on it is the number of years the Zone will grant you.
So, this other guy thought he'd try it out. Took off to Prypiat, slipped past Degtyarev who knows how! Climbed onto the wheel… and that's when an Emission wiped him right out.
Still rolling.
Believe it or not, here's what went down...
I can't wrap my head around it…
Looks like everyone's here. So as the story goes...
Alright, alright, I said I'd tell you. Here it goes.
I don't even know where to begin. Well...
Maybe you will understand me...
So, here's what crossed my mind the other day…
Okay, guys, cut the crap now. This is serious…
Well, so it's now or never…Ptooey
I think I heard that one before...
Gave me the creepy-crawlies.
Folks, anyone got a smoke?
Yeah, better stay on the Zone's good list. Anything can happen here.
Phew! Like I didn't have enough shit on my bloody mind!
Better knock on wood after listening to all that. So you don't jinx it.
Right-y, and here I was, thinking nothing could scare me anymore.
Wish I hadn't just listened to all that…
Yeah, even I got the heebie-jeebies…
A Bloodsucker comes to an old pal, a military commissar. “Don’t send away all the folks with diabetes who wanna enlist, okay? Save a couple for dessert.”
There’s a legend among the Freedom old-timers: if you cut off a Duty guy's head, he’ll keep on marching and scrubbing toilets for another half hour.
An old-timer tells a new stalker, “Get ready, rookie. We’re hitting the circus tonight.” The newbie readies himself, grabbing lots of pouches for artifacts. As night falls, they set out.
The rookie realizes they’re going the wrong way. “Is this the right way?” he asks. “The Circus anomaly is the other way.” “What anomaly?” the old-timer grins, “we’re going to the Cement Factory!”

Two stalkers are at a bar. “Tomorrow,” one exclaims, “I'm gonna quit drinking.” “What for?” says the other. The first stalker grins, “I wanna surprise the Bloodsucker I’ve been feeding all this time.”
Two stalkers are out fishing. Suddenly, a Burer comes over. They get all jumpy, when the Burer goes, “Take it easy, I’m not looking for trouble. I’m just here to fish. Caught anything yet?”
Surprised, the stalkers put their guns away. One says, “I’ve got a perch, and my friend here, he caught a trout. You?” “Ah,” the Burer smiles, “I just caught two rookie losers.”
An old stalker asks: “Ever wonder why they built the Lesser Duga?” “Why?”, a young lad leans in, intrigued. The old man chuckles, “They figured the Zone would make it grow up.”
Noah decides to gather two of every kind of animal. Ten Wardens walk up to him, and he says, “I only need two of you brutes.”
Two stalkers are riding the Ferris Wheel in Prypiat, munching on cotton candy and sipping beer. One says, “Just look at this lush scenery. And you didn't think we could handle that Controller!”
Myklukha discovers a stash and cracks it open, only to find a million dollars inside. Frustrated, he mutters, “What the hell is this? Where's the coupons?”

A veteran stalker quizzes a rookie: “A Bloodsucker, a Poltergeist, and a Burer are sitting at the table. How many feet are on the ground?” The rookie starts musing, “well, the Poltergeist’s got none…”
“The Bloodsucker’s feet are invisible, and the Burer can’t reach the floor. Zero!” The veteran grins. “Nope. The answer is two, and both of them yours! You should’ve been shooting, not counting!”
A stalker comes across a Controller and starts shooting. The Controller shouts, “Don’t kill me! I’m no ordinary Controller! I can grant you a wish!”
The stalker pauses, then says, “Okay. I wish IPSF would stop demanding bribes from stalkers entering the Zone.” The Controller goes, “Are you nuts, man? I said Controller, not a damn genie!”
A stalker says to a trader, “I’ll take five cans of rotten meat, a loaf of moldy bread, and some cheese that's been gnawed by mice. Put it all in a backpack that'll fall apart an hour later.”
The trader is insulted. “I don’t sell stuff like that at my establishment!” The stalker replies, “That's funny, you did last week!”
So a new scientist starts working at SIRCAA. He gets assigned to a lab to study mutants and whatnot. A week later, he goes to his boss to complain.
“That’s it, I can’t work like this! Those mutants gobble up whatever they see, destroy whatever they touch, and shit wherever they feel like! And your office is right above us! How can you stand it?”
“Oh”, his boss says, “You must have gotten lost, son. The mutant lab is in the next building. The Ward’s barracks are below my office.”
A guide is lecturing some rookie stalkers. “A good stalker is hard to find. You don’t just stumble across one every day. And if you do stumble across one, that means he wasn't any good.”
A stalker wakes up with a bad hangover. He opens one eye and sees something scary. stalker goes, "Shit, gotta be some kinda Zone waste". "That's mean, man, you're the one wasted", says the psi-dog.
A young stalker who had a burr came into the Zone thinking he was ready to r-r-ro-o-o-ll! Wait until you see his face when he gets to deal with a BlodsuckeRRR, a BureRRR, and a ControllerRR!
A Bloodsucker walks into a bar and says, “I’ll take a bloody Mary.” The bartender says, “I don’t have any Maries. Bloody Harry okay?”
A veteran stalker lecturing a rookie. “Now, remember, always partner up with guys who can't shoot or run. They'll keep the mutants busy, and there's no chance they'll shoot you in the back.”
A stalker is driving through the Zone, gets pulled over by a patrol. “Do you have any weapons?” the officers ask. “Of course I do,” he says. “There’s a Glock 19 on my hip,
A Colt 45 in the glove box, and a sniper rifle and an RPG in the trunk.” “Who are you so afraid of?” the Wardens ask, surprised. And the stalker says, “Pretty much nobody.”
Scientists wanted to find out which of the Zone’s creatures roars the loudest. Turns out, the top three are fleshes, burers, and Sidorovich when he hears something good about the Gaffer.
It's the Zone's first year. A young scientist is making a list of known mutants. “What should I name the one that sucks blood?” he asks his colleague. “Just write down whatever the stalkers call it.”
“How do I choose? The ordinary ones call it a 'bloodsucker,’ the veterans call it ‘a sucker,’ and the rookies just call it ‘aaargh!’”
A vet stalker takes a walk with rookies, “Never rely on a guess in the Zone. You have to know for sure.” Then he sees they’ve just walked into a Bloodsucker nest. “Now I know for sure we’re screwed.”
One stalker asks another, “How’s Alex doing? I haven’t seen him in ages.” “Oh, you know, he's been all around the place... I mean, he got into a Whirligig.”
I was wondering why that mutant kept getting bigger and bigger, and then it hit me.
Why does Degtyarev regret banning vodka? 'Cuz now he can't call all the shots anymore!
A stalker boasts by the fire, “One little rainstorm, and now I don’t have to waste money on razors and haircuts anymore! My mom always said being outside was good for ya!”
“You should wash your mouth out with some of the local water,” another one tells him. “You’ll save a fortune on dentist bills too.”
Wardens walk out of the Chemical Plant and see a stalker dying in an anomaly by the gate. “Finish me off, guys!” he begs. The merciful Wardens shoot him and throw grenades generously.
As they walk away, the stalker sighs, “Thanks for trying, guys.”
A Bloodsucker is lecturing his little son: “Never trust Poltergeists, kiddo. They're always pulling your leg.”
A veteran stalker asks some rookies, “When does a mutant have four legs and one arm?” Kids sit there, all confused. “When he's eating some fresh-caught rookies!” the veteran says.
So a Warden goes to a shrink and says, “Doctor, I’ve been feeling like I’m an idiot, a cheapskate, and a jerk. Do I have a split personality or something?” “No, young man. You've had an epiphany.”
Stalkers are swapping stories at a bar. “I took down a Bloodsucker with just a handgun!”, one goes. “I fought one bare-handed,” another one brags. “I tackled a Bloodsucker naked!” says the third.
Okay, okay! You win. Put some pants on already, will you?
So, a stalker is crawling down a pipe and sees a Bloodsucker dead ahead. He turns back and sees another Bloodsucker behind him. “Well,” the stalker says, “looks like I’m lucky Pierre.”
Two stalkers are standing by a stash. “Why didn’t those blind dogs eat our sausage?” asks the first. “They’re blind,” the other replies, “not stupid.”
What does a stalker say after seeing something that blows his mind? “Monolith be praised!”
A loner comes up to a Warden: “I want to join Ward.” “Piss off,” the Warden says. “Please let me in!”. “Are you stupid?” the Warden scoffs. The loner's like, “Is that an enlistment requirement?”
A Freedom old spice gets caught in an anomaly. He’s about to snuff it. The kids try to bring him some joy in his final moments, running around with whiskey and cigars.
“I don’t need a thing, folks,” he says. “Just sell off my stuff and give my coupons to Duty, tell ’em to sign me up.” Everyone’s like, “What the fuck?!”
“When I kick the bucket,” he explains, “there’ll be one less Duty prick in the Zone!”
An old-timer asks a rookie, “Which would you rather lose, your armor or your loot?” The rookie ain’t half stupid, so he says, “I’ll just keep out of the Slag Heap, thanks.”
Two Bloodsuckers are feasting on the bodies of Duty members. “What did they say when they got here again?” one asks. The other replies, “Remember, our mission is to rid the Zone of mutants.”
You guys are hysterical. Okay, I got one too.
Alrighty, a joke won’t tell itself…
Hey, why the long faces? Let me cheer you guys up.
That's so funny, I forgot to laugh!
Well, thanks for trying.
That’s hilarious!
sid_phrase_General_freestalkers_0_NPC_Co: I need cover! Go at 'em hard!
Keep ‘em pinned! I’ll get to the cover!
Keep them in your sights! I’ll move to cover!
Keep them in your sights. I’m moving in from the rear!
Hold him! Coming in from the rear!
Keep them suppressed while I outflank them!
Cover me while I flank him!
Cover me while I outflank them.
Flanking the enemy! I've got you now!
Here’s a little present for you!
We got a man down, guys!
Give me fire support, I need to move!
Cover me, man! I’m going that way!
Won't go easy on you next time!
We’re out of our league here!
Well, that’s it. Looks like we're clear.
Come out while you still can!
Come out now, I'm warning ya!
sid_phrase_General_freestalkers_0_NPC_Em: Phew, we can come out now.
Here we go! We gotta find cover.
Get outta here! There's an Emission coming!
sid_phrase_General_freestalkers_0_NPC_In: Learn to watch where you're going before you head out into the Zone!
Sure, I can spare a minute for you.
What the fuck are you doing here?
I sure hope you're here to talk business.
Speak to the man in charge.
sid_phrase_General_freestalkers_0_NPC_Pe: Are you nuts? This ain't a graveyard.
Now show us what you’ve got…
You don't need it anymore, sorry.
sid_phrase_General_freestalkers_0_TradeT: No refunds. I’m warning you in advance.
Well, here we are... do you need anything?
sid_phrase_General_monolith_0_BusyTopic_: This is not the time for idle chatter.
sid_phrase_General_monolith_0_HelloTopic: What is it that you seek?
sid_phrase_General_monolith_0_InfoTopic_: Monolith, steel my flesh so that the unbelievers may perish. Guide my hand. May thy radiance shield me against their bullets. Thou art my path and my world. Lead me into battle. For the Monolith!
sid_phrase_General_monolith_0_NPC_Chatte: Whatever the Monolith blesses us with.
Where do you think our enemies go when they die?
Nowhere. Eternal oblivion is all that awaits them.
How many enemies of the Monolith have you destroyed?
The only ones I remember are the brothers who are no more.
The Monolith will send you new clothes soon enough.
Worry not. Prayer shall give me the strength.
Guide me along the true path, o Monolith.
May the word of the faithful multiply the power of the Monolith. And may the word of the unbelievers be turned against their own kind.
Keep my faith strong, o Monolith. Do not let me stray from your path.
May it not be my will, but yours alone. So be it, Monolith!
Darkness shall take the unbelievers. The gleaming of the Monolith shall light the way for the faithful and show the truth to those who abide in doubt.
sid_phrase_General_monolith_0_NPC_Combat: I’m moving to cover, brothers. Keep the enemy pinned!
I’m moving to cover! Brothers, support me with your fire!
Brothers, help me approach from the rear!
Brothers, I’ll take them from the other side!
Cover me, I’m going around the foe!
By the Monolith, he is dead!
I have struck an enemy of the Monolith!
Rejoice, brothers! The enemy is wounded!
Kill them! For the glory of the Monolith!
Death to the enemies of the Monolith!
Cover me, brothers! I’ll attack their flank!
I’m breaking through! Cover me, brothers!
A son of the Monolith is no more!
A brother has ascended to the Monolith!
He is one with the Monolith now!
I've been hit by a brother!
Take this grenade, enemy of the Monolith!
Cover me, brothers! I’m on the move!
Bless me with safe passage, Monolith!
Brothers, I see the enemy of the Monolith!
Rejoice, brothers, for the enemy has fled!
No one can hide from us!
I can sense you, enemy of the Monolith!
Rest easy, brothers. The enemy has fled!
The enemy feared the power of the Monolith.
Brothers, fall back! We will finish this later.
The Monolith commands us to retreat.
Death to the enemy of the Monolith!
All is well. Monolith be praised.
The Monolith has scared him away.
Brothers, he must not get away!
I know you’re close. And I will find you!
I hear the enemy. He cannot hide from me!
You’ll find no shelter from the Monolith!
sid_phrase_General_monolith_0_NPC_Emissi: The Emission won’t bother us any longer.
sid_phrase_General_monolith_0_NPC_Intera: What is it that you seek?
Put down your weapon. You won’t need it.
You should not have come here.
Beware the wrath of the Monolith.
Our leader will hear you... if he wills it.
sid_phrase_General_monolith_0_NPC_Peacef: The Monolith does not accept this offering.
I have no need of this body.
What do we stand to gain here?
Our brother is one with the Monolith. He requires nothing else.
sid_phrase_General_zombie_0_NPC_Contextu: I see… I see you… radiant… so radiant…
I can’t see… who’s there… can’t see… it’s so dark…
Wrong… it’s all wrong… let me go… Mommy…
Of course I’ll be back… Just one more time, and then I’ll be back… I promise…
Almost there… just a little bit more, and then I’m out…
It hurts… it hurts so bad… make it stop… please…
When I get back, everything's gonna be all right… We’d get our daughter the medicine she needs… It’s gonna be all right…
It’s so cold… why is it always so cold…
Don’t worry… we’ll make it… we’ve always made it…
I can hear them… so many of them… make them stop…
That’s it… I found you… found you…
You promised… you promised me… all alone… why…
Wow… you’re so… Shining… and I imagined…
Good… it’s all good now… so quiet…
So many of them… so many… where are you all coming from…
sid_phrase_Himzavod_Hub_GCA_1_GCA_1_6172: Hey man, got anything newer than this? You promised, remember?
So did you, and how’d that turn out? The Ward patrol waited a good while at that stash of yours, and left empty-handed. They were really pissed. Honestly, so am I.
What’s that got to do with me, man? The intel came from Myklukha. I was just the messenger!
Well, that makes Myklukha my client, not you. So it’s only fair.
sid_phrase_Himzavod_Hub_GCA_2_GCA_2_6205: Hey, Shtybor! What day of the week is it?
Yesterday was Saturday, right? Saturday, Thursday, Monday, Friday, Wednesday… what happened to the other two days? I must have lost track... again.
sid_phrase_Himzavod_Hub_GCA_2_GCA_2_6206: Fuck… what did they expose you guys to at Icarus?
sid_phrase_Himzavod_Hub_GCA_3_GCA_3_6212: Kramar and Beloholovets. We had to leave them behind. And now Jura, too. Burakhovsky couldn’t save him. He told me when he went out for a smoke after surgery.
sid_phrase_Himzavod_Hub_GCA_3_GCA_3_FF_6: Hey, Ed. Gimme Dvoryankov’s standard issue shot.
sid_phrase_Himzavod_Hub_GCA_4_GCA_4_6213: What about the helicopters, doc? My friend's there…
Everyone had friends there, private. I told you before: the major will announce the final list later.
Keep waiting. I hope your friend is alive.
sid_phrase_Himzavod_Hub_GCA_5_GCA_5_6221: What about the Monolith snipers, Sarge? Last time they trashed us like a blind dog tearing up a dead boar.
They did, and they’ll do it again. Their tactics are time-tested and effective.
sid_phrase_Himzavod_Hub_GCA_5_GCA_5_6222: Here’s an idea I’ve used before — I call it the “frantic minute” tactic.
When you run into a sniper, have your entire squad fire at him. While the bastard is pinned down, one of you sneaks up on him and takes him down. Anything goes, but a grenade works best.
sid_phrase_Himzavod_Hub_PropTopic_10_Pro: A stalker is not your friend, but being polite and attentive is the best strategy when communicating with them.
sid_phrase_Himzavod_Hub_PropTopic_11_Pro: To all those who have completed the advanced tactical medicine courses: Dr. Burakhovsky requires your assistance. We need everyone to pitch in right now.
sid_phrase_Himzavod_Hub_PropTopic_12_Pro: The medical block’s blood donation center is now operating 24/7. We currently have a critical need for blood types AB+ and A-.
Any additional needs will be communicated later.
sid_phrase_Himzavod_Hub_PropTopic_13_Pro: All Duga assault mission personnel must attend an emergency briefing by Sergeant Nedzelya as soon as possible. No exceptions!
sid_phrase_Himzavod_Hub_PropTopic_14_Pro: To all officers and NCOs, please conduct an additional survey at your earliest convenience.
Any additional skills or experience your personnel may have will help us restaff to the appropriate level.
sid_phrase_Himzavod_Hub_PropTopic_15_Pro: Remember that a sleep-deprived soldier can a pose danger to everyone. Thus, the mandatory sleep requirements will remain unchanged.
However, in light of the current events, we are allowing schedules that include brief breaks. Please consider when scheduling duty and patrols.
sid_phrase_Himzavod_Hub_PropTopic_1_Prop: Ward soldiers, remember that it is extremely dangerous to explore anomalies on your own.
sid_phrase_Himzavod_Hub_PropTopic_2_Prop: Your life and well-being are our top priority. Therefore, any health issue requires a visit to a medical professional.
Remember, neglecting any symptoms while in the Zone may result in the early termination of your contract.
sid_phrase_Himzavod_Hub_PropTopic_3_Prop: Personnel whose contracts are about to end should contact Major Dvoryankov as soon as possible. It is your responsibility to ensure timely contract renewal.
sid_phrase_Himzavod_Hub_PropTopic_4_Prop: Any requests for new uniforms, equipment, and weapons can be submitted to the technician. They will be processed and fulfilled within three days.
sid_phrase_Himzavod_Hub_PropTopic_5_Prop: SIRCAA shifts are a privilege that demand a high level of skill and dedication.
sid_phrase_Himzavod_Hub_PropTopic_6_Prop: Reports of Anomalous psi-activity should be promptly documented and submitted to Dr. Kryvenko. You can find the form and a sample report in the medical block.
sid_phrase_Himzavod_Hub_PropTopic_7_Prop: This Saturday is local fauna defense tactics practice. The instructor for this exercise is Sergeant Nedzelya.
sid_phrase_Himzavod_Hub_PropTopic_8_Prop: Attention! The recommendations regarding sleep requirements have been updated. The standard daily sleep duration is now six hours.
This rule is mandatory for everyone. In the Zone, fatigue can lead to irreversible damage.
A reminder to those participating in deeper Zone raids: new sleeping bags can be obtained from Sergeant Ivaylov.
sid_phrase_Himzavod_Hub_PropTopic_9_Prop: All artifacts that come into your possession for any reason must be promptly delivered to an on-duty SIRCAA employee. There are no exceptions.
sid_phrase_Himzavod_Hub_aleks_fraj_0_Inf: I scored some sweet gear here, man. This spot is one of the few places where you can grab Warden stuff. And the prices are pretty groovy.
Sure, it’s not exactly straight from the factory, but there’s a certain peace of mind that comes with a Warden piece, you dig?
sid_phrase_Himzavod_Hub_aleks_fraj_SCAct: I've smelled some rank shit in my day, but the Chemical Plant… the Ward may have cleaned it up, but you can still catch a whiff of that stench from time to time.
sid_phrase_Himzavod_Hub_closing_HubClosi: Attention! The Ward base is under attack. Barbican Protocol is now in effect. Leaving the base is prohibited until further notice.
sid_phrase_Himzavod_Hub_ed_2_BusyTopic_B: I’ve got enough on my plate. This can wait.
sid_phrase_Himzavod_Hub_ed_2_HelloTopic_: Make it quick. Talking ain’t gonna fill your stomach.
You need to grab a bite? Or do you just wanna chat?
sid_phrase_Himzavod_Hub_ed_2_InfoTopic_1: I’ve always wondered what it is about you stalkers that makes you so fond of vodka and canned food. Got no taste at all?
You lot should publish a cookbook. Something like “To Serve the Zone.”
sid_phrase_Himzavod_Hub_ed_2_InfoTopic_2: The guys who came back from Icarus are acting real weird. Just yesterday, one of them took a bowl of soup and started stirring it with a spoon, real quick-like.
I ask him, “What are you doing?” And he says, “It’s so deep and quiet down there.”
sid_phrase_Himzavod_Hub_ed_2_InfoTopic_3: After the Duga, Dvoryankov let us have a daily vodka shot for stress relief — only after hours, of course. It helps some of us to have a drink and quietly remember the fallen.
sid_phrase_Himzavod_Hub_ed_2_InfoTopic_4: When you’re in good spirits, everything just seems better. Your plate feels bigger, your bread softer, and your glass frostier. I wish we had more occasions to celebrate!
sid_phrase_Himzavod_Hub_ed_2_Meet_2_Meet: I’m swamped with work these days, Skif. I barely have time to eat.
Luckily, Dvoryankov sent me some extra hands in the kitchen. They're all recovering from Icarus and SIRCAA and aren’t quite fit for regular duty yet.
At least they’ll get some decent food.
sid_phrase_Himzavod_Hub_ed_2_Meet_3_Meet: We’re all doctors now, Skif... in a way.
I've been doctoring up some broth, for example.
With the doctor’s care and my nutritious grub, I’m sure we’ll have the boys back on their feet before you know it.
sid_phrase_Himzavod_Hub_ed_2_Meet_4_Meet: I’ve saved something from the previous shipments. For a rainy day, you know? To sugar-coat it, literally.
Turns out we've got a reason to celebrate, and something to celebrate with!
As soon as brass gives me the green light, I’m throwing a five-star party!
sid_phrase_Himzavod_Hub_ed_2_Meet_Meet_6: The Zone has a challenge for everyone, even someone like me, here in the kitchen.
I mean, before I joined the Ward, I used to work with caviar and lobsters. It’s almost impossible to mess that stuff up.
Now all I've got is army rations. Turning this stuff into something that resembles a decent meal is harder than turning water into wine.
sid_phrase_Himzavod_Hub_ed_2_TradeTopic_: This ain't no restaurant. Take it or leave it.
I saved something special for you.
Take a look around and pick what you want. Nothing fancy, but I got everything you need in the Zone.
sid_phrase_Himzavod_Hub_leva_fan_0_BusyT: Man, that’s some real bad timing.
sid_phrase_Himzavod_Hub_leva_fan_0_Hello: Now that you’re here, let’s get down to business.
I’m glad to see you, brother.
Man, we two loners are bound to find some common ground.
sid_phrase_Himzavod_Hub_leva_fan_0_InfoT: Don’t fool yourself, man. The Zone won’t ever let you go. There’s always one more anomaly, one more artifact... “one last job, and then I’m going home.”
Did you hear that Monolith attacked the Western checkpoint? Those crafty bastards tried to pose as stalkers, but you ain't fooling the Ward like that. Took them all out.
Looks like it’s a good time to be in this business. Ammo and guns are selling like freakin' hotcakes.
But now that those Monolith fanatics managed to get Korshunov himself… I kinda feel like if they make a move on us, the Ward will just nod and let 'em.
I’m going through my PDA contacts, getting in touch with folks after the Monolith thing. It's sad some of them ain't ever texting back.
But it’ll get better, I guess. It might cost me, but during my time in Rostok, I learned to set a little something aside for a rainy day.
sid_phrase_Himzavod_Hub_leva_fan_0_Meet_: Are you immortal or something, Skif? The Ward took quite a hit at SIRCAA, and here you are, not a single scratch.
By the way, got anything interesting from there? My gut's telling me that even bricks from SIRCAA could be worth a small fortune now.
Nah, don’t gimme that look. I know you’re an honest stalker, man. Or you wouldn’t be here.
Looks like Noon called in a substitution, bud. Monolith is on the field now.
One hell of a game. So who should I bet on? It’s a tough call… nah, just kidding. Ward's always the champion.
They’ll figure out the other team's new tactics and win the second half. Won't be the first time.
If I were you, I’d double up on my stock. I’m telling you this because I genuinely care, not because I'm lookin' for extra dough.
The Duga hit the Ward hard, and I doubt it went unnoticed. You catch my drift?
Plenty of folks will be looking to get a piece of the action. You don’t want to get caught with your pants down, do you?
That Monolith fight was pretty intense, eh? But in the end, that roller coaster was a huge win for me.
The Ward’s guns were the real MVPs on the field.
My customers are coming back. Even the old junk sells, whatever's got the “Property of the Ward” label.
Surprised to find a loner here? Well, here in the Zone, we’re all living in the shadow of the Ward.
And I’d rather be on the winning team, even if it means riding the pine.
I like it here, man. You get to secure your future, and I get to hear some cool stalker stories. It’s a win-win.
sid_phrase_Himzavod_Hub_leva_fan_0_Trade: The stuff I’ve got, you won’t find anywhere else.
It's worth the coupons. You never know what might come in handy in the Zone.
sid_phrase_Himzavod_Hub_major_dvorenkov_: Not right now.
So that’s all? About-face!
Anytime.
You can go.
Get to the point.
What’s on your mind? Speak up.
I’m listening, stalker.
What can you tell me about the Chemical Plant?
First: security is a priority here, and we intend to keep it that way. Is that clear? If not, a cell might be more suitable for you.
Second: the Ward follows established protocols. If you have a specific request, direct it to the appropriate specialist. Don’t bother others needlessly.
And third, on a personal note: the colonel trusts you. Don’t let him down. Or me.
All the occult nonsense in the Zone comes down to stupidity. There was this one outpost, a real nasty place. Our patrols would hear strange voices, like children crying. It was driving them crazy.
But then an acoustician from SIRCAA took some measurements and pinpointed the cause. Turns out it was an old air vent acting like an acoustic funnel. So much for ghosts.
Monolith is only half the problem. We can handle them. What bothers me more is how the others might see our failure at SIRCAA. I’m not keen on having the entire Zone turn against us.
To put it bluntly, the reinforcements from Icarus are no soldiers. I’d dismiss the lot of them, but my orders are to get everyone armed. If you can tell the barrel from the butt, you’re good to go.
The worst part is that we don't have any reserves left. We’ve got some men back on the Mainland, but how could they become real Wardens without any special training?
Drop them in the Zone, and the first blind dog will chew them up, let alone those Monolith bastards.
I’ve heard that Duty and Freedom are at it again, despite the Treaty. Now that we’ve dealt with Monolith, it’s time to sort out that mess too.
Heard about Icarus yet? Damn, we lost a lot of good men there.
Senkevych is an idiot. An officer’s supposed to use his wits, not just wave his gun around.
It’s like he’s gone brain dead out there in the Swamps.
It was high time we evacuated Icarus. If Monolith had decided to attack the camp, we wouldn’t have stood a chance.
It’s better to abandon the camp for a while than to lose everyone who served there.
I have to inform Senkevych’s family. And Tachenko’s. It used to be the colonel’s job, but now it's mine.
I don't even want to open the filing cabinet. Half of the files have the same stamp: killed in action.
I just don’t know what I’m supposed to tell their families…
Monolith finally got some real payback!
You did real good in there, Skif. Took down that son of a bitch Faust! Our boys have been avenged.
I hope we’ve seen the last of those scumbags. May whatever’s left of them rot away in Prypiat.
Okay, Skif, let me be clear. I don’t have time to deal with you. Right now, my main concern is the well-being of my men.
So I’m only going to tell you this once: here, discipline comes first.
We appreciate initiative, but it’s best carried out in accordance with orders and without resorting to typical stalker antics. Any questions?
All right, the Emission is over! Get back to your posts as per your duty schedules.
Attention, everyone! There’s an Emission outside. Extreme caution strongly advised. Remain in cover. I’ll inform you when it’s safe to come out.
An Emission is approaching, soldiers. Seek shelter immediately. I repeat, we have an incoming Emission. Seek shelter.
Am I on the Ward’s naughty list, Major? Is there anything I can do to make amends?
I warned you that going against the Ward would have consequences, Skif.
So my men will continue to keep you in their sights.
This is a direct order from the colonel. And it’s up to him to decide when and how to change it.
sid_phrase_Himzavod_Hub_muhomor_0_ByeTop: May enemies and mutants never cross your path.
sid_phrase_Himzavod_Hub_muhomor_0_HelloT: I wish you’d forget the way to this place.
Say the word, and we're off.
Where do you want to go, stalker?
sid_phrase_Himzavod_Hub_muhomor_0_InfoTo: “This place isn’t what it used to be,” “The Zone’s not the same anymore…" you hear that a lot, don’t you? As for me, I like it better this way. The Ward’s finally cleaned things up around here.
The routes through the Zone have gotten longer. Another mile here, five extra miles there. You end up packing more ammo than food. And the ruck ain't gonna carry itself.
The Monolithians are no mutants. A single shot ain’t gonna scare them off — it'll just make them angry.
The worst part is that sometimes you can’t tell friend from foe anymore. The other day, I ran into some Wardens. Looked like a regular patrol, familiar faces.
I waved at them, like always, and they opened fire. I barely managed to dive into the bushes. Turns out they were among the guys who got their brains fried at the Duga.
I’ve heard enough news. We’ve already got plenty of yesterday's shit to deal with.
Nothing I want to know about, thanks very much. We've got more important things to deal with... like giving our dead a proper burial.
sid_phrase_Himzavod_Hub_muhomor_0_Meet_2: Everybody asks me where the Klondike of artifacts is. They all want a glimpse of it.
I used to take them to SIRCAA for an answer. “Look,” I’d say, “there it is, the real deal. Everything else is just tall tales.”
It's too bad the Institute itself is now just another tall tale.
sid_phrase_Himzavod_Hub_muhomor_0_Meet_3: I’ve always had an ironclad rule: if I cross paths with a Noontider on the road, next time, I choose a different route.
You could never tell what they were up to. No wonder, if their brains are cooked and sprinkled with shit.
sid_phrase_Himzavod_Hub_muhomor_0_Meet_4: Duty just reached out to me, you know, for old times’ sake. Rumors in the Zone travel faster than any guide.
They asked how the colonel's doing, but I had a feeling they had something else in mind. Like a Treaty with Freedom.
I assured them that Korshunov’s doing just fine. Just a scratch. Old grudges are the last thing we need right now.
sid_phrase_Himzavod_Hub_muhomor_0_Meet_5: Don't get me wrong, it’s good that Monolith went back to Prypiat with their tails between their legs.
But that means Prypiat is now off limits to everyone.
I was guiding a group there the other day, and they started shooting right at us. Friend, foe, mutant, all the same to them.
sid_phrase_Himzavod_Hub_muhomor_0_Meet_M: I joined the Ward with a big dream. It’s hard to believe, but I wanted a world where people wouldn’t need folks like me anymore.
Imagine: strolling from the Perimeter all the way to Prypiat, and there’s not a single blind dog or a damn Bloodsucker anywhere. Doesn't that sound amazing?
Duty couldn't make it happen, but I think the Ward can. So, where are you off to?
sid_phrase_Himzavod_Hub_muhomor_0_Travel: If you weren’t paying, I wouldn’t even bother to move.
Shortcuts don’t come cheap, but good company is worth a discount.
I’ll take you wherever you want, but you gotta pay up front.
sid_phrase_Himzavod_Hub_nikolaj_burahovk: Hold on. I'm too busy.
That’s about it. The rest can heal on its own.
Keep some bandages and a medkit close. Might come in handy.
Just try not to get shot.
We’re not quite ready to bury you, although the idea has crossed my mind.
You’re fit enough to be an astronaut.
I’ll have you up and about in no time — better than ever.
Whatever it is, I'll take care of it.
Interesting. Guys like you don’t normally last this long.
Nothing too serious, I hope?
How can I help you?
I got a bit too comfortable with SIRCAA nearby. They could work wonders, even with the worst injuries.
Now I’m back to good old combat medicine. I suppose it’s time to see if I’m still a doctor, or if retirement is calling.
The timing of those SIRCAA experiments couldn’t have been worse.
With the Monolith causing trouble, our need for sutures and bandages has already tripled. And we’ve got no one to look after the Icarus survivors.
I hate triage, ever since med school. Black, red, yellow, green… we were dealing with mannequins, but now I have to do it with living, breathing people.
You look at them, try to console them... but you've already marked them for dead, in your head. You have to. They’re not going to make it anyway, while others still have a chance, at least.
I finally found some time to organize my notes. After all, I’m not the worst doctor in the Zone. My records just might come in handy.
Trust me, if someone had handed me a manual like this when I first got to the Zone a few years ago, I would have avoided a lot of bumps and bruises.
Batalov did his best at Icarus, but unfortunately…
Nightmares, anxiety, memory problems… that’s a lot to deal with.
It’s a good thing those damn Swamps didn’t hit you nearly as hard.
If I sit down, I’ll doze off, and those energy drinks make my hands all jittery. What kind of surgeon operates with his hands shaking?
So many bad injuries, Skif! I wish we could send them all back to the Mainland.
But it’s just too dangerous. They’re not stable enough. I don’t want to add their names to my own personal graveyard.
I hope they’ll consider restoring SIRCAA now. When it comes to Zone healthcare, the Institute is truly irreplaceable.
You know how it goes... the Zone’s got quite the imagination — always manages to come up with new ways to make you suffer.
If there's anything that should be available to everyone in the Zone, it's healthcare.
The colonel might think stalkers self-heal, but physiology tends to disagree.
Besides, it’s pretty fascinating to treat folks like you. You tend to venture into places the Ward would never set foot in.
Be glad I’m not offering you a laxative.
Our cutting-edge healthcare is at your disposal.
Anything the Ward has, you can have too.
sid_phrase_Himzavod_Hub_opening_HubOpeni: Attention! Barbican Protocol lifted. Security has been restored in the Chemical Plant area.
sid_phrase_Himzavod_Hub_serz_nedzela_0_I: I’ve heard Monolith has this preacher guy, their commander-in-chief, kinda like our Korshunov. It's too bad he won’t get to see us kick their butts... 'cuz he's blind, right?
My platoon just got reinforcements, but what’s the use? I'll have to train them all from scratch. My guys know the drill, but those Icarus boys? They can’t tell right from left.
SIRCAA was supposed to be working for us, but it’s Monolith that ends up with a gun that can blow right through a chopper. Gets under my skin. No way those eggheads didn’t know about it.
They could’ve come up with something to counter it, but no, why bother? It’s more fun to watch Wardens in tissue-grade armor.
What news could a sergeant have? When it’s quiet, I share my combat experience — teaching small-unit tactics to the guys who haven't faced Monolith .
I want their muscle memory to kick in. Finally doing what I enjoy.
sid_phrase_Himzavod_Hub_serz_nedzela_SCA: It’s like Monolith is our problem and no one else’s. Where are IPSF and Duty? Playing it safe? I’d love to get them all together and do some recon. Might make a little difference, at least.
sid_phrase_Himzavod_Hub_serz_nedzhela_SC: Those Icarus guys are real dumb. Looks like sergeant Pickman forgot all about his duties. They barely remember how to hold a gun anymore.
Some of the bullets the Doc pulled out of our guys were our own. I saw it myself. I don't get how that's possible.
They started the party early. Monolith might have retreated, but they’re still a threat. The local troublemakers aren't sitting around either.
sid_phrase_Himzavod_Hub_serzant_ivajlov_: You’ll have to wait. I’ve got more important stuff.
Get lost, you're wasting my time.
Take care of yourself. The rest, we can fix.
Take good care of your gun, and it’ll work like a charm.
You can't stay outta trouble, and then I have to put everything back together again.
You've been through some tough times, huh? That’s okay, we can fix everything.
What have you got? Come on, be quick about it.
The Monolith has certainly kept us busy. Guns are wearing down faster, and so are the soldiers. We’re getting tons of replacement requests. Fortunately, our supply chain seems to be holding up.
I checked out some of the vests they had to cut off the wounded at the hospital. Every single one was shot right through, and the entry and exit holes were almost identical, perfectly round.
What kinda bullet speed penetrates both Kevlar and human flesh like that? What guns were their snipers using?
Heh... I thought the entire network would go down with the Duga! But no, the connection's smooth, and the PDA messaging speed's cool. I guess they never gave us the whole story.
I always knew those Noontiders were trouble. You can always tell what a stalker's up to, but those bastards…
We should’ve put them on the target list as dangerous mutants right from the get-go.
But the colonel won’t be so diplomatic now. After what they did at SIRCAA, it’s gonna be an eye for an eye.
I got everyone kitted out personally before the Duga. Made sure each soldier got exactly what he’s best with. But it still ended up…like this.
No way my guns could've just failed our snipers. But somehow, they say the Monolith preacher didn’t even get a scratch. I just don’t get it.
They brought some nice trophies from the Duga. Fascinating things. I'd like to see what that stuff's made of.
I could maybe whip up an upgrade for the choppers. A Gauss is no Stinger, 'course, but there might be a way around it.
I’ll figure out how to make that Monolith crap useful.
You stalkers are all about showing off. You think the whole Zone needs to see how geared up you are - yeah, real soldier.
You’ve got it all wrong. You're the weapon, not your gun.
I can take care of your gun, sure. But remember, a good gun isn't enough. You gotta know how to use it.
Turning good stuff into scrap... is that all you can do?
The usual? Good as new, even better?
The stuff I can do, you could only dream of, stalker.
sid_phrase_Himzavod_Hub_soldat_kunovskij: I don’t give a damn about the news. Let my superiors worry about it. All I need from them is to identify the enemy — they can sort the rest out later.

Korshunov’s office has been humming non-stop — officers coming and going… Looks like brass wants to come up with a master plan. Like those nutcases from Wild Island know the first thing about warfare!
All I hear on smoke breaks is Korshunov goss. “Shipped off to the Mainland”, “here, in cold storage…” I’m so fed up with this crap, and the major’s not saying a freaking word.
I’m waiting for my new stripes. After we took over the Duga, Dvoryankov submitted a whole list of promotion recommendations, and I’m on it. That’s what my friends at HQ say, anyway.
Okay, let’s get back to it.
I met my share of idiots with empty stares in my counter-terrorism unit back on the Mainland. I thought I’d get a break from them in the Zone. Nope – we’ve got plenty here too.
We got used to being comfortable, and now it feels like a submarine. Cramped, smells like dirty socks, and there’s always a line for the latrine. Then again, an angry soldier fights harder…
sid_phrase_Himzavod_Hub_soldat_kunovsky_: I need to get that Dvoryankov’s vodka shot at Ed's. Might help me get some sleep.
This victory sure came at a high price. Too many of our men killed, too many medals postmortem.
sid_phrase_Himzavod_Hub_soldat_stibor_0_: You want to hear the news? I’ve got a long list, all starring scumbags. And after SIRCAA, that list is only getting longer.
I was supposed to go to Icarus too. Had my duffle bag all packed and ready to go. But Dvoryankov decided to keep me here last minute. I guess I owe him a drink, seeing how things turned out.
I won’t be renewing my contract. The money's good, but I don’t want to be shipped home in pieces. Just a couple more months, and I’ll be back to civilian life on the Mainland. If I make it that far.
I got grazed at the Duga this time. Just a scratch. I got a little scared, sure, but I knew exactly what to do — thanks to Burakhovsky and his tactical medicine course.
By the way, I signed a new contract while I was in the hospital. Figured I’d get bored to death on the Mainland.
sid_phrase_Himzavod_Hub_soldat_stibor_SC: I don’t remember a clause in my contract saying that all kinds of creeps could shoot at me.
We’ve never had this many people at the Chemical Plant. I only hope they’re not gonna cut our rations. Too many mouths to feed…
Lucky me, I guess. First I dodged Icarus, and now I missed out on Duga, too. Standing watch here is a breeze.
Things are getting better. We’ll be back to our regular patrols soon, no more special ops or assaults.
sid_phrase_IKAR_Hub_Audiolog_Radiochat_1: Metlov, come in. How’d things go down at the Slag Heap?
We’re good. Put the local thugs in their place. What’s the situation on your end? Over.
Hasn't changed. Still watching the Sultansk and the Shevchenko, hands off.
We used to play this game when Nestor ran the Slag Heap, Pick. Maybe there’s somebody on those ships playing for our team, too...
Fucking politics, man. We could have Zaton wrapped up in no time. Let’s hope Senkevych knows what he’s doing and doesn’t fuck this up. Keep in touch, Metlov. Over and out.
sid_phrase_IKAR_Hub_Closing_1_62558: Attention, everyone! Icarus is under attack. I repeat, Icarus is under attack. Leaving the camp is prohibited until the threat is neutralized.
sid_phrase_IKAR_Hub_GCA_1_1_62595: Yesterday Khoteyev hands me a rag and says, “Quick, to the latrine!” I rush in and see the doc, hands covered in gore like a butcher and blood smeared all over the mirror.
sid_phrase_IKAR_Hub_GCA_1_1_62596: “Shaving incident,” he says. “It was Scherban, not me. Fortunately, I was nearby and took the razor away.”
sid_phrase_IKAR_Hub_GCA_1_1_62597: Scherban? Dammit! He begged me to help him shave, said he couldn’t see his reflection in the mirror anymore... I thought he was fooling around, so I told him to scram. Is he okay?
sid_phrase_IKAR_Hub_GCA_1_1_62598: Yeah, the doc patched him up. He looks like a mummy now, with all those bandages.
sid_phrase_IKAR_Hub_GCA_2_1_62637: Sarge! Hey, Sarge! Tell the captain to get someone to replace me.
sid_phrase_IKAR_Hub_GCA_2_2_68958: I’ve been here for three years, and I can’t take it anymore.
sid_phrase_IKAR_Hub_GCA_2_3_68959: I'll tell him. You drink this and I'll tell him.
sid_phrase_IKAR_Hub_GCA_2_3_68960: I’ll drink it once he relieves me of my watch. Don’t you remember regulations? The honor guard can’t drink on duty.
sid_phrase_IKAR_Hub_GCA_3_2_68961: Don’t trust anyone around here. They’re not real!
sid_phrase_IKAR_Hub_GCA_3_3_68962: The real Captain Senkevych is lying over there. He hasn’t budged in three days.
sid_phrase_IKAR_Hub_GCA_3_3_68963: And they drowned Batalov in the swamp to make sure nobody could stop them from taking over. I saw it with my own eyes.
sid_phrase_IKAR_Hub_Opening_1_62559: The emergency is over, Wardens. The threat has been eliminated.
sid_phrase_IKAR_Hub_bronevoj_0_HelloTopi: Well? Don’t beat around the bush.
Permission to speak freely. What’s on your mind?
sid_phrase_IKAR_Hub_bronevoj_0_HubInfo_1: Icarus is a SIRCAA outpost. It's our defense against threats from the Swamps. Our garrison is a modest one, but it's enough to maintain control of the area.
However, as soon as I get my men in line, I’m going to ask Korshunov for more boots on the ground.
Those Zaton punks are getting cocky. Besides, there are plenty of places around here we need to explore. We’ve only heard about them from stalkers, and that’s just plain wrong.
sid_phrase_IKAR_Hub_bronevoj_0_InfoTopic: Oh, we've got all sorts of stuff going on! Would you like to hear about a soldier after patrol meowing like a cat? Or maybe about Winnie the Pooh speaking from a wall?
That’s some fucking great news, isn’t it? I hear a dozen stories like that every day, each crazier than the last.
sid_phrase_IKAR_Hub_bronevoj_0_Meet_1_62: Let’s set the record straight: I’m not thrilled to have you here. This isn't the version of Icarus a stalker should ever see.
However, since the Colonel trusts you, I’ll give you the benefit of the doubt and assume you’ve got brains. Here and now, you could be useful.
We only have one rule here, and it’s as clear as it gets: orders are not up for debate. They’re to be followed. Got it? Good. Then we’ll get along just fine.
sid_phrase_IKAR_Hub_bronevoj_0_NPC_Emiss: The Emission is over, Icarus. Resume your duties.
Attention, soldiers! We've got an Emission! Keep your heads down!
Listen up, soldiers! An Emission is headed our way. Follow standard procedure to the letter.
sid_phrase_IKAR_Hub_bronevoj_0_RestoreRe: Your men don't seem happy to see me, Captain. Can you put in a good word for me?
What did you expect? You’ve stirred up so much shit, I’d rather take you outside and drown you in the nearest puddle myself.
But I don’t want to get my hands dirty.
Consider yourself lucky Korshunov hasn’t ordered us to take you out immediately. If you want to make things right, you need to take it up with him.
sid_phrase_IKAR_Hub_medik_s_ikara_0_Busy: I’ve got enough on my plate already.
sid_phrase_IKAR_Hub_medik_s_ikara_0_ByeT: Be careful out there. I can’t handle any more patients.
sid_phrase_IKAR_Hub_medik_s_ikara_0_Heal: Anesthesia is reserved for Wardens. You’ll have to tough it out.
Everyone here thinks there’s something wrong with them, but rest assured, you’re perfectly fine.
Just a sec, and you’ll be as good as new.
sid_phrase_IKAR_Hub_medik_s_ikara_0_Hell: You’re the last thing I need right now. What is it?
Are you okay? Anything can happen here in the swamp.
Let’s keep this brief. I don’t even have time for a smoke.
sid_phrase_IKAR_Hub_medik_s_ikara_0_Info: SIRCAA doesn’t give a damn about the Ward. The future of all mankind is at stake! The fact that our brains are melting here is no biggie.
Here, soldier, take this pill, it’ll keep your hands steady. You don’t want to drop your rifle, do you? Fucking eggheads!
sid_phrase_IKAR_Hub_medik_s_ikara_0_Meet: When I left for Icarus, I thought it’d be like a vacation. Small, remote garrison. What could go wrong? An encounter with local fauna or food poisoning, at worst.
Boy, was I wrong! There isn’t a single empty bed left in the infirmary. We bring a new patient in every day. I can’t remember the last time I got a good night’s sleep — definitely before I got here.
So, for the time being, you seem to be the most mentally stable one here. But trust me, it won’t last long.
sid_phrase_IKAR_Hub_medik_s_ikara_0_Trad: To be honest, I wouldn’t even waste peroxide on you.
I was saving it for myself, but I think you need it more.
It’s not much, but we have all our basic needs covered.
sid_phrase_IKAR_Hub_radovoj_kotc_0_Hello: What do you want from me?
sid_phrase_IKAR_Hub_radovoj_kotc_0_InfoT: Let’s see… the mold keeps on spreading, and our brains are slowly turning to mush. That’s about all the “news” we've got around here. But that's okay, 'cuz we've got plenty of godawful food to eat.
If you’re looking for news, head over to SIRCAA, they've got something exciting going on every day. They’ll have to shoot you dead for trespassing, though.
sid_phrase_IKAR_Hub_radovoj_kotc_0_SCAct: What the hell are we even doing in here? Guarding a bunch of muck? Command says it’s some kind of important facility. I wish I had a clue what it is. Maybe then I’d have something to be proud of, huh?
sid_phrase_IKAR_Hub_radovoj_smerdenko_0_: Well? Is there something you need?
I really don’t want to be on the next patrol. Casualties every time. I gotta talk to Batalov and see if I can be excused.
But what do I tell him? I should eavesdrop on the guys who went out last time, figure out what they're screaming about at night. Yeah, that might convince the doc.
I feel like I’m about to grow some fins over here. How come my boots are always soaked? Seriously, I just dried 'em, and I haven’t stepped in a single puddle since this morning.
sid_phrase_IKAR_Hub_serz_pikman_0_BusyTo: Stand down with the chitchat, stalker!
sid_phrase_IKAR_Hub_serz_pikman_0_HelloT: Speak up already. I’m busy enough as it is.
sid_phrase_IKAR_Hub_serz_pikman_0_InfoTo: You are, for one. And it just keeps getting worse.
The captain might see things differently, but to me, if the Ward needs help from a stalker, that can only mean one thing: we’re fucked.
sid_phrase_IKAR_Hub_serz_pikman_0_Resume: All right. We can talk again.
sid_phrase_IKAR_Hub_serz_pikman_0_SCActi: The air here… it’s like breathing in jelly. No filter can clean this stuff up. Just trying to breathe it feels like I’ve been on a loaded march the whole time.
sid_phrase_IKAR_Hub_serzdot_eremeev_0_Bu: I’m busy. I can’t deal with you right now.
sid_phrase_IKAR_Hub_serzdot_eremeev_0_By: You keep bugging me for nothing.
Come on in whenever you need something.
Watch your head. I can’t fix that.
sid_phrase_IKAR_Hub_serzdot_eremeev_0_He: Now I gotta waste my parts on you.
Great. I had nothing better to do anyway.
sid_phrase_IKAR_Hub_serzdot_eremeev_0_In: We keep wasting ammo like there’s no tomorrow. I have no idea what they’re shooting at. Every time I ask, all I get is “There was something creepy out there.”
sid_phrase_IKAR_Hub_serzdot_eremeev_0_Me: Stalker, eh? Haven’t seen you around here before.
Though, to be fair, I just got assigned here myself. The previous tech ended up under Batalov, so now I’m filling his shoes. I just hope I don’t end up like him.
Let me know if you need anything.
sid_phrase_IKAR_Hub_serzdot_eremeev_0_Up: All right. Let’s see your scrap.
What do you want to upgrade?
Show me what you need tweaked.
sid_phrase_IKAR_Hub_serzdot_hoteev_0_Bus: You've got a decent shelf life — you can wait.
sid_phrase_IKAR_Hub_serzdot_hoteev_0_Bye: That’s it, I’ve seen enough of you for today.
All my merchandise is yours — within reason, of course.
sid_phrase_IKAR_Hub_serzdot_hoteev_0_Hel: If it was up to me, you’d be locked up. So what do you want?
You’ve got a nose for new arrivals, eh?
sid_phrase_IKAR_Hub_serzdot_hoteev_0_Mee: A soldier’s happiness boils down to a few things. Good grub, for one. Then there's comfortable gear, from his armor down to his skivvies. And enough ammo.
And let me tell you, I’ve got all those bases covered.
Whatever’s going on inside their heads... that’s the commander’s problem. I’m just here to make sure their helmets fit.
sid_phrase_IKAR_Hub_serzdot_hoteev_0_Tra: You can have a look, but keep your expectations in check.
We’ll make sure you’re equipped to the highest Ward standards.
This ain’t the Chemical Plant, but we do have a few things to offer.
sid_phrase_Malahit_Hub_Audiolog_1_Audiol: Are you certain no mutant approaches were detected in the last 24 hours? Did you make sure the sensor is on?
Well, the green light looks on to me.
No, I’m sure you’ve got something wrong. Get back to Malachite, and I’ll go over everything with you in detail again.
Maybe the Chunk of Meat just doesn’t work as bait. I’ve never seen a psi-dog eat an artifact before.
That’s because you haven’t closely observed their behavior. Fine, I'll join you in the field next time!
sid_phrase_Malahit_Hub_Closing_2_Hub_Clo: Due to adverse external circumstances, Malachite is temporarily closed. I apologize for the inconvenience.
sid_phrase_Malahit_Hub_Closing_Hub_Closi: Due to an external threat, access to Malachite is temporarily restricted. Please remain calm. Our guards are more than capable of handling the situation.
sid_phrase_Malahit_Hub_DialogueOnEntranc: I might be new around here, but I’ve been in the Zone long enough to know that coupons are usually as good as a permit.
Keep it down! You want Ozersky to hear us?
All right. I can tell you’re not exactly a newbie. Hand me your… “permit” and come on in.
Does Korshunov’s badge count as a special permit?
You’re from the Ward? Well, why didn’t you say so? Come on in.
All right. I’ll come back some other time.
I hope my special permit is still valid.
Have you heard about what went down at the Duga? I was there.
Yeah, sure. So you’re telling me the Monolithians wiped out all the soldiers at the Duga, and you somehow got away without a scratch? Bullshit!
I got lucky. That blind preacher of theirs almost got me.
Faust? I hear he’s calling the shots for the whole Monolith now. You must have a lucky charm if you ran into him and made it out alive.
All right, you’re in. After the Duga, our scientists’ jobs will be a walk in the park for you.
Freeze! This is a secure facility. No rookie stalkers allowed. Limited access, special permit required.
sid_phrase_Malahit_Hub_GCA_1_GCA_1_62763: Darn bureaucracy! What kind of paper do you want now?!
sid_phrase_Malahit_Hub_GCA_1_GCA_1_62764: No, these are the rules. Show me evidence that this experiment makes any sense, and I’ll provide the equipment.
sid_phrase_Malahit_Hub_GCA_1_GCA_1_62765: But Professor Ozersky said it’s a promising experiment!
sid_phrase_Malahit_Hub_GCA_1_GCA_1_62766: Professor Ozersky doesn’t handle equipment allocation; I do. Papers, Kuprienko. I need grounds.
sid_phrase_Malahit_Hub_GCA_2_GCA_2_62768: Getting us research materials under Professor Ozersky’s direct orders. But that's need-to-know.
sid_phrase_Malahit_Hub_GCA_2_GCA_2_62769: You guys just can’t resist adding a touch of mystery, eh?
sid_phrase_Malahit_Hub_GCA_3_GCA_3_62770: Well, we don’t have much to lose. Kryvenko is a well-known scientist, maybe he’ll take us under his wing.
sid_phrase_Malahit_Hub_GCA_3_GCA_3_62771: After we worked directly under Ozersky? I don’t think so.
sid_phrase_Malahit_Hub_GCA_3_GCA_3_62772: The Sircaans think we're a joke. And the Ward is still looking for anyone who used to work with Spark.
sid_phrase_Malahit_Hub_GCA_3_GCA_3_62773: Yeah, it’s been tough. Might as well just leave the Zone for good.
sid_phrase_Malahit_Hub_GCA_4_GCA_4_62774: You think I could become a stalker?
sid_phrase_Malahit_Hub_GCA_4_GCA_4_62775: Why not? Degtyarev was in the secret police, and look where he ended up. Come on, let's go for a walk. We'll take a look at the old Scorcher!
sid_phrase_Malahit_Hub_GCA_4_GCA_4_62776: I need to think it over. Grab some bolts, too. I gotta ask Semyonych…
sid_phrase_Malahit_Hub_Opening_2_Hub_Ope: The threat has passed. We are delighted to once again welcome everyone to the true temple of science!
sid_phrase_Malahit_Hub_Opening_Ozerskij_: The threat has been eliminated. Malachite is now open again.
sid_phrase_Malahit_Hub_PropTopic_10_Prop: Dear colleagues, let’s respect each other’s time. Assignments with the guide are pre-scheduled only.
sid_phrase_Malahit_Hub_PropTopic_11_Prop: Reminder to all research personnel: everyone has to fill out the safety training form by the end of the month.
sid_phrase_Malahit_Hub_PropTopic_12_Prop: Discipline and ethics are the two fundamentals of scientific progress!
sid_phrase_Malahit_Hub_PropTopic_13_Prop: In accordance with the new regulations, discussion of the incident involving the former head of STC Malachite is not permitted.
sid_phrase_Malahit_Hub_PropTopic_14_Prop: In accordance with the new regulations, scientists are prohibited from leaving the Malachite facility without personal permission from Victoria Dorozhnyuk.
sid_phrase_Malahit_Hub_PropTopic_1_PropT: All staff, please note that STC Malachite does not provide funding for experiments without clearly defined objectives.
sid_phrase_Malahit_Hub_PropTopic_2_PropT: In the case of an unsuccessful experiment, I strongly advise seeking immediate medical attention. Our doctor’s services are available at any time.
sid_phrase_Malahit_Hub_PropTopic_3_PropT: Please be aware that alcohol consumption does not enhance cognitive functions. Visiting the bar during work hours is strongly discouraged.
sid_phrase_Malahit_Hub_PropTopic_4_PropT: Whoever left their protective suit at the entrance, please contact security.
sid_phrase_Malahit_Hub_PropTopic_5_PropT: Our security personnel have reported unauthorized attempts to bring live Rodents into the STC. Please remember that field experiments should be conducted exclusively in the field.
sid_phrase_Malahit_Hub_PropTopic_6_PropT: When arranging field experiments, please remember that assignments with the guide are scheduled on a first-come, first-served basis.
sid_phrase_Malahit_Hub_PropTopic_7_PropT: Please remember that all equipment must be returned upon the conclusion of an experiment.
sid_phrase_Malahit_Hub_PropTopic_8_PropT: Remember that not all research requires hand-on specimen collection. Our guide and allied loners are ready to bring anything you might need.
sid_phrase_Malahit_Hub_PropTopic_9_PropT: In accordance with the new regulations, refusal to cooperate with the Ward may result in immediate termination of employment and expulsion beyond the Perimeter.
