sid_phrase_Malahit_Hub_docent_ilahov_0_I: Professor Ozersky is about as much a scientist as he is a professor. He hasn’t taught anyone anything in twenty years, and his last scientific discovery is even more ancient.
I shouldn’t have spoken ill of Ozersky. The man paid for his research with his life, like a true scientist!
sid_phrase_Malahit_Hub_docent_ilahov_0_N: I can’t concentrate! They won’t leave me alone. No time to myself even in the exclusion zone!
This place used to be a total mess, now it feels like an army barracks.
I never thought I’d be against a change in leadership. But now, I’d choose a bumbling genius over someone with a stick up their ass.
sid_phrase_Malahit_Hub_mns_kuprienko_0_B: Good luck in… wherever you're going.
sid_phrase_Malahit_Hub_mns_kuprienko_0_H: Another stalker? What can I do for you?
sid_phrase_Malahit_Hub_mns_kuprienko_0_I: I’m pretty new here. Professor Ozersky himself invited me. He said I've got a promising future!
And SIRCAA rejected my application. They’re pencil pushers, nowhere near as passionate about science as us, that much is obvious!
Good gracious! Have you heard about the, uh… incident? I can’t begin to fathom what he must have done to deserve that. I’m scared to work here now.
And these new protocols... my goodness. The bureaucracy here has become unbelievable. Should I consider becoming a loner scientist? Is that even a thing?
sid_phrase_Malahit_Hub_mns_kuprienko_0_N: The institute didn’t prepare me for this! And all those Emissions…
I’d love to hit Pythagoras's place for a drink and some inspiration. But with the new management, I might end up getting sacked.
Gotta avoid the Wardens. You never know with those guys... give them the wrong look and they'll reach for their guns.
sid_phrase_Malahit_Hub_pifagor_0_BusyTop: Look, I’m kinda busy right now.
sid_phrase_Malahit_Hub_pifagor_0_ByeTopi: Come by, I’ll always have a cold one for you!
sid_phrase_Malahit_Hub_pifagor_0_HelloTo: I wish I had more customers like you. We’d be more popular than Rostok.
sid_phrase_Malahit_Hub_pifagor_0_InfoTop: I know everyone who comes to Malachite. Everyone stops by the bar. We’re the local landmark!
Every now and then you run into folks who really shouldn’t be here at Malachite. But you didn't hear it from me, obviously. If it’s fine by the professor, it’s fine by me.
Ozersky promised that I’d become a lab technician, that I’d advance science. And now it seems like I’m about to advance myself… to an early grave.
Our security is all worked up after the Duga. Monolith brought down a whole Warden army! If they advance to Malachite, we’re sitting ducks here…
sid_phrase_Malahit_Hub_pifagor_0_Meet_2_: Take it easy on the drinks. Did you hear what they did to Ozersky? Don’t let your guard down. The Malachite we knew is done and dusted, loner.
sid_phrase_Malahit_Hub_pifagor_0_Meet_Me: If you want a drink, don’t be shy, pick your poison. I’ve got something for everyone, whether you’re a soldier or a scientist.
It sure helps that their tastes are ind… identical. Truth be told, the only thing you can get here is vodka.
I’ve heard they have quite the selection over at SIRCAA. Well, that's okay... I’ll hang around here a bit longer, learn a thing or two, and then… who knows?
sid_phrase_Malahit_Hub_pifagor_0_TradeTo: It’s all good, I’ve sampled everything myself.
sid_phrase_Malahit_Hub_professor_ozerski: You’re interrupting me.
Leaving already? What a pleasant surprise!
Good luck with your stalker career!
Goodbye!
This is a science facility, not your personal base camp.
I see the Zone's been kind to you!
I’m all ears.
STC Malachite is the only place where we study the Zone as it is.
Here, no theory is labeled eccentric, and no field experiment is considered unnecessary. Even the smallest detail may hold the key to comprehending the fundamental patterns of the Zone.
Admittedly, those snobs at SIRCAA mock our research, call it pseudoscience. But why listen to shallow pricks whose narrow-mindedness obstructs their own quest for truth?
Tell me about Malachite.
They were actually thinking of making me the head of SIRCAA, you know? Luckily, it didn’t work out. I’d rather go back to the Mainland than deal with that bunch of show-offs and hypocrites.
Allow me to introduce myself. I am Professor Ozersky, the Director of STC Malachite.
If you intend to remain at the Center, please avoid interfering with our work, and treat all visitors with courtesy and respect.
My colleagues frequently require stalker assistance to retrieve artifacts or conduct field experiments. Should you prove yourself capable, you may be entrusted with such assignments.
The Emission is over. Staff involved in field research may now resume activities outside the facility.
Ladies and gentlemen, please remain calm. Do not leave the premises. There’s an Emission outside!
To all STC Malachite staff: an Emission is approaching! It is dangerous to remain outside.
Can you help me make amends with Spark?
You should ask their leader, not me. But I’m not sure he'll be interested anyway.
You shouldn’t have been so quick to show off your weapon. You stalkers have grown so wild in those wastelands of yours, the very notion of civility appears to be foreign to you.
sid_phrase_Malahit_Hub_semenyc_0_ByeTopi: If you need anything, stop by!
sid_phrase_Malahit_Hub_semenyc_0_HelloTo: You ain’t in a rush, are you?
sid_phrase_Malahit_Hub_semenyc_0_InfoTop: I like it here at Malachite. Got everything I need, and the people are nice. But everyone’s always in such a hurry, like their science is gonna up and vanish. You gotta take it easy, loner, just slow down.
I just wanted to tinker with Ozersky’s gadgets, and almost got shot! And then they claim I lack initiative!
sid_phrase_Malahit_Hub_semenyc_0_Meet_2_: Honestly, Wardens or no Wardens, I couldn’t care less.
Warden gun, loner gun - jams the same, and I'm stuck here fixing 'em, eh…
Unlike some folks, I’m not whining. What’s the point? Just a waste of time.
sid_phrase_Malahit_Hub_semenyc_0_Meet_Me: When I left Garbage, I was heading for Prypiat, just like everyone else. But then I stopped just shy of it, as you can see. And I think I made the right call.
Tech likes peace and quiet. And it's always messy in Prypiat.
So, if you need something fixed or adjusted, give me a shout. Just don’t rush me, okay?
sid_phrase_Malahit_Hub_semenyc_0_Upgrade: Oh, as if I didn't have enough on my plate…
All right. I'll make it priority number one.
No rush, I’ll do it nice and slow…
sid_phrase_Malahit_Hub_soldat_debrev_0_I: I wanted to serve at the Duga so badly. The view out there is incredible! Here, there’s nothing worth seeing besides Pythagoras’s mini-bar.
Fuck it, I’ve filed a transfer request. I was lucky at the Duga, but now the Ward came in and started tightening the screws.
Just three more weeks, and I’ll get back to the Perimeter.
sid_phrase_Malahit_Hub_soldat_debrev_0_N: This sucks. I used to take a nap on duty whenever I felt like it. Now, I can’t blink for more than a second. The Ward is watching my every yawn.
I shouldn’t have been complaining that it's boring here. First, one group takes over Malachite, then another… what’s next? A third coup?
sid_phrase_Malahit_Hub_spica_0_ByeTopic_: I won’t be upset if you never come back.
Don’t go overkill with those energy drinks.
If you need anything, I’m here.
sid_phrase_Malahit_Hub_spica_0_HealTopic: An adequately immobilized patient requires no anesthesia, ha!
Physically, you’re in perfect condition. As for your mental health... that’s not for me to assess.
I've gotten pretty good at this thanks to you stalkers. It won’t hurt.
sid_phrase_Malahit_Hub_spica_0_HelloTopi: What seems to be the problem?
sid_phrase_Malahit_Hub_spica_0_InfoTopic: It's a shame they shut down the Scorcher. While I’m glad it’s no longer a threat, the changes it induced in the brain were… truly unique.
I wonder if we could turn it back on for a few minutes...
I’ve requested a transfer to SIRCAA — once it gets rebuilt, of course. There’s still some time for me to make a breakthrough.
sid_phrase_Malahit_Hub_spica_0_Meet_2_Me: Shame about the professor. However, I’ve heard that Kryvenko is looking for neurobiologists…
Say what you will about the Ward, but they do recognize that progress often requires a certain flexibility, particularly when it comes to science.
As for this mess, in the end, Dorozhnyuk got what she wanted, even though the means hardly justified the ends. Like I said, science demands a certain degree of flexibility.
sid_phrase_Malahit_Hub_spica_0_Meet_Meet: Your eyes react to the light. Freeze, don’t move! Cognitive function seems intact.
Preliminary conclusion: you’ll live. Providing, of course, that you stay out of Prypiat, and the Scorcher remains off.
This place has plenty of ways to end you, which is actually helpful for my research. No offense.
sid_phrase_Malahit_Hub_spica_0_TradeTopi: No need to worry, all the samples have been tested.
Malachite still has some in stock.
sid_phrase_Malahit_Hub_viktor_koska_0_He: At least I’ll have some decent company!
sid_phrase_Malahit_Hub_viktor_koska_0_In: You wouldn’t believe some of the places they wanted to send me to. To the Generators — the very shithole of Prypiat. I know, right?
That smartass got real mad when I refused to take him there. I should’ve dropped him off at the nearest old power plant and let him generate whatever he wanted there, ha ha!
The Wardens spend a lot of time in the Zone, but still need to be rescued from every other anomaly. Might as well write ten percent off each group before they go out on a mission.
sid_phrase_Malahit_Hub_viktor_koska_0_Me: It actually turned out pretty good! Sure, the Wardens are a shady bunch, but they drew me up a schedule, down to the minute.
It’s almost like a regular job now. I make a run there and back, and once my shift’s done, I can relax. So far, so good!
Another field trip? Look, at least let me… wait, you’re not one of them scientists. Phew.
Listen, buddy. If you need to go places, I can help you out, but we gotta stop and take a break along the way, all right?
These eggheads have been up my ass all around the clock. Take me here, set my equipment up there… man, I should’ve stuck to the Swamps.
sid_phrase_Malahit_Hub_viktor_koska_0_Tr: We’ll take it slow, all right?
Where are we going this time?
sid_phrase_Malahit_Hub_viktoria_doroznuk: Later!
Yeah.
See you around.
Have a nice day.
What have you got there?
It's good to see you.
Hello. Looking for some equipment, are you?
Malachite is going to see some real changes now! Sure, I’ll keep some things the way they were to honor the professor, but if we don’t act swiftly, the whole facility could be shut down for good.
With SIRCAA in ruins, the responsibility to study the Zone and push the boundaries of science is now ours alone.
I have to admit, though, I’m a little scared. Who knows what else Professor Ozersky could have been hiding from us all? What other skeletons did he keep in his closet?
Tell me about Malachite.
If I were in charge, we’d be respected like SIRCAA. There’s so much potential here, and it’s being wasted. It’s disorganized!
Can you believe it? They just funded a study to determine whether pseudo-dogs have a fifth limb. This is a circus, not a scientific facility!
I proposed that the Ward establish a temporary research camp here at Malachite. It’s so refreshing to work with real scientists for once!
What happened at SIRCAA is outrageous. But what else would you expect from Dalin and his experiments? Now the whole Zone has to deal with the consequences.
It’s all happening so fast! I wanted to take the reins at Malachite, sure, but… not like this. This is horrible!
I need to speak to Dr. Dalin. As for the colonel… I’d rather not. Maybe some other time.
Right now I need to get everything in order and prove that the professor’s… that my team is making a great contribution to scientific progress. I have to ensure their future. I’m sure you can relate.
Professor Ozersky says that my commitment to maintaining order “stifles the flight of imagination.” Well, flights are for airplanes. And in science, order is paramount.
That’s why I won’t tolerate mess. No discounts, no “let’s make a deal,” and certainly no “I’ll pay you next time.” You pay, you get your goods. Not the other way around.
Is that understood? Good.
Attention, everyone. The Emission is over. Please return to your work stations in fifteen minutes.
Do not leave the shelters until the Emission has passed! Your safety is your responsibility!
Colleagues, an Emission is underway! Move to the shelters and follow safety protocols!
Don't even think about hustling me.
It’s been a while.
Here’s what we’ve got.
sid_phrase_NoonBase_Hub_Audiolog_ZavhozA: Nah, I got this. Just tell me what to do about the gas masks. The junk Khors fixed last month isn't cutting it anymore.
Fuck Quiet! We charge his generators, find him gear, and he goes off and sells it to whoever he damn well pleases?! That’s not why I showed him the ropes!
I think that’s exactly why, brother…
Want me to pay him a visit?
No way in hell. Quiet’ll try to scam you. I’ll handle it myself.
sid_phrase_NoonBase_Hub_Brodyaga_Meet2_1: People have been leaving Noon for as long as it’s existed. And just as many of them come back.
Did you know that even Dubny ran away at first? He didn’t like the ban on fighting and drinking.
Don’t worry about us. Noontide is more than just the people who are here at the moment.
sid_phrase_NoonBase_Hub_Comment_GCA_1_1_: Hey, stalker! Need to sell some gear or grab something new?
You can forget about doing it here on the island. Trading’s a no-go. You’ll have to head over to Quiet; he’s not too far from here.
But you didn’t hear it from me, okay?
sid_phrase_NoonBase_Hub_Comment_GCA_2_1_: I went to scavenge some artifacts nearby and got ambushed by Snorks. Right after I gave you my last medkit, damn it.
So then I stumbled across some stalkers, thought I’d ask for a bandage. What do they do? Point their guns at me and tell me to get lost. “All Monolithians are murderers”.
Do you remember it? No, you don’t, and neither do they. Just heard some campfire stories. Those bastards weren’t even around in the Monolith days.
sid_phrase_NoonBase_Hub_Comment_GCA_3_1_: I’ve been thinking: the guys who went with Faust did the right thing.
I just hope they won’t regret it.
Why would they? We’re not exactly begging for a return ticket ourselves.
We might be sticking with Quiet, but we’re our own masters here. Can the folks who left with Faust say the same?
sid_phrase_NoonBase_Hub_Comment_GCA_4_1_: I step out of the infirmary, and nothing’s the same anymore…
It’s for the best. The brothers who went away... they didn’t want to be here.
I guess… but it stings that even Stone didn’t drop by to say goodbye.
sid_phrase_NoonBase_Hub_Comment_GCA_5_1_: From what I can remember, he’s the first loner who’s stuck around the island this long. I hope it won’t turn out to be a mistake.
When it comes to people, Strider’s never been wrong.
He was dead wrong about Faust, for one.
sid_phrase_NoonBase_Hub_Comment_HubClosi: We’ve got a situation here, brothers. Lock everything up! We’ll hunker down until the shooting’s over.
sid_phrase_NoonBase_Hub_Comment_HubOpeni: Seems quiet out there. Get back to your posts.
sid_phrase_NoonBase_Hub_Hors_Meet2_1_573: It’s too bad the brothers left. Things were better when we were all together.
I’m gonna miss Faust. He used to tell these stories and kept us so intrigued to the very end.
Anyway, I have faith they’ll return. In the meantime, what can I do for you?
sid_phrase_NoonBase_Hub_Infotopic_Forgot: I feel so empty. The Monolith’s voice is gone, my memories, too. Sometimes I dream about laughter, voices, faces… but who are those people? I wish I knew…
sid_phrase_NoonBase_Hub_Masina_Meet2_1_5: I know the ways of the Zone. The one Faust chose won’t lead brothers to happiness.
So, where do you want to go?
sid_phrase_NoonBase_Hub_PropTopic_10_1_5: I lost my PDA on my shift. If someone finds it, bring it to me. And please don’t read what’s on it.
sid_phrase_NoonBase_Hub_PropTopic_11_1_5: Brothers! The professor and I, we’ve made… tomorrow, we’re going to show… that will change our home forever! Is this supposed to not make real sense?
sid_phrase_NoonBase_Hub_PropTopic_12_1_5: Good news! All… Birdy and Rocket are back!
…brothers… enough gas masks for five more…
sid_phrase_NoonBase_Hub_PropTopic_13_1_6: Brothers! The storage coordina-… for the Mono…
sid_phrase_NoonBase_Hub_PropTopic_1_1_57: If I see any empty bottles, I’ll track down whoever drank from them.
sid_phrase_NoonBase_Hub_PropTopic_2_1_57: Always look for shelter right away during an Emission — even if you don’t feel like it. Remember, mutants are also resistant to its effects, and they enjoy a good meal too. Take care of yourselves.
sid_phrase_NoonBase_Hub_PropTopic_3_1_57: The converters are getting overloaded. We’ll need to conserve energy for the time being, but I’m working on it.
sid_phrase_NoonBase_Hub_PropTopic_4_1_57: The converters are operating at maximum capacity! You don't need to conserve energy at this time.
sid_phrase_NoonBase_Hub_PropTopic_5_1_57: Or you’re not coming with me.
sid_phrase_NoonBase_Hub_PropTopic_6_1_57: Remember to let us know if you decide to leave the island. We’re all very concerned.
sid_phrase_NoonBase_Hub_PropTopic_7_1_57: The new safety rules apply to everyone. This is particularly important after what went down at the WPS.
sid_phrase_NoonBase_Hub_PropTopic_8_1_60: If you come across any artifacts, don’t take them to Quiet. Bring them to me. They could save your life — or the lives of your brothers.
sid_phrase_NoonBase_Hub_PropTopic_9_1_57: If you haven’t played “bet you can’t fix it” with me yet, swing by my workshop.
sid_phrase_NoonBase_Hub_Tihij_Meet2_1_57: Hey, stalker! Have you heard about what happened with Noontide?
Faust had a fight with Strider and took half the people off the island. That dude sure knows how to talk folks into stuff. I wish I had that kind of charisma.
So, what can I do for you?
sid_phrase_NoonBase_Hub_brodaga_0_ByeTop: I wouldn't stay on this island too long if I were you.
Come back anytime. You’re always welcome here.
sid_phrase_NoonBase_Hub_brodaga_0_HubInf: What can you tell me about Wild Island?
Wild Island is home to some very capable people. We wouldn’t have made it this far without them.
For anything tech-related, ask Khors. He can fix it all. Machine is the best guide I’ve ever met. Our doc can work wonders too, given our limited resources.
But if you’re looking for trade or a drink, you’ll have to go somewhere else. I’m trying to keep this place independent — from money and alcohol, among other things.
sid_phrase_NoonBase_Hub_brodaga_0_InfoTo: The Zone is changing, and this place can’t stay the same either.
We’ve already found ourselves a home, and we can change how others see us. I’m sure of it.
sid_phrase_NoonBase_Hub_brodaga_0_Meet_1: We don’t usually let stalkers in. Or any strangers, really.
Not because we’re afraid or hate them. I made this decision to ensure everyone's safety.
But you helped us. Noontiders won't forget it. You’re free to leave and return to the island whenever you wish.
sid_phrase_NoonBase_Hub_brodaga_0_NPC_Em: An Emission is coming, brothers. Find shelter!
sid_phrase_NoonBase_Hub_brodaga_0_Restor: We got off on the wrong foot. I’d like to make things right.
You let us down, but we all make mistakes. I believe that everyone deserves a second chance.
But you’ll have to earn our trust, loner.
Every mistake has a price. I know that all too well. But let’s put it behind us and move on.
I need to think it over.
Until then, try to keep a low profile.
sid_phrase_NoonBase_Hub_foma_0_HealTopic: I’d rather spend my time on folks who are actually sick.
sid_phrase_NoonBase_Hub_foma_0_InfoTopic: We used to have no problems with radiation, but lately I’ve been noticing more and more signs of radiation exposure, all because of radioactive waste.
We had to bury two of my patients just last week.
I joined Strider because he promised us a future. These days, it feels like the only future we'll see is a slow death.
sid_phrase_NoonBase_Hub_foma_0_Meet_1_57: A loner, huh? I normally only treat my brothers, but since they let you in, I won’t turn you away either.
I’ll do what I can with what I’ve got, but don’t expect much. Supplies are running thin, and we’re short on just about everything. If you've got anti-rad, I’m willing to buy it. We always need it.
So, you need some patching up, or do you wanna take a look at my meds?
sid_phrase_NoonBase_Hub_hors_0_ByeTopicG: You have a good one too.
sid_phrase_NoonBase_Hub_hors_0_HelloTopi: What did you break this time?
It’s a lovely day. The gadgets are practically fixing themselves, ready for some action!
Huh? How long have you been here?
sid_phrase_NoonBase_Hub_hors_0_InfoTopic: If everything works out — and I’ve triple-checked it — the generator should run smoothly. That means the ventilation is up and running, too.
So feel free to take off your gas mask when you sleep; the air is clean for now. Until the next emergency, that is.
Steward’s always whining about how we don't have enough meds, equipment, food… but I don’t think it’s that bad.
And it’s gonna get even better! Professor Lodochka and I are working on it.
sid_phrase_NoonBase_Hub_hors_0_Meet_1_57: A stalker, here? Well, if the brothers are okay with it… they are, right? You can come on in, then.
Professor Lodochka noticed my knack for tech stuff and taught me everything. Your gadgets are safe with me.
Show me what you need fixed.
sid_phrase_NoonBase_Hub_hors_0_TradeTopi: My selection is limited, but you’ll still find it worthwhile.
sid_phrase_NoonBase_Hub_hors_0_UpgradeTo: We’ll get it fixed in no time.
sid_phrase_NoonBase_Hub_kodak_0_BusyTopi: What’s wrong with you?! Not now!
sid_phrase_NoonBase_Hub_kodak_0_InfoTopi: We were at the trader’s the other day, Quiet and me. Cost us a lot of artifacts, but totally worth it — got a huge pile of magazines from the Mainland!
Thankfully, only a handful of folks left with Faust. They can’t do much harm, and they’ll probably come to their senses.
sid_phrase_NoonBase_Hub_kodak_0_ResumeCo: Do we need to continue this?
sid_phrase_NoonBase_Hub_kodak_Whining_1_: When I see my brothers collecting waste, I can’t help but feel ashamed. We’re just sitting around here, and they…
sid_phrase_NoonBase_Hub_kodak_Whining_2_: What the hell's up with this place? I had no idea we had so many fools here pining for the old days…
sid_phrase_NoonBase_Hub_masina_0_InfoTop: I used to be on the run all the time. Then I learned to defend myself.
sid_phrase_NoonBase_Hub_masina_0_Meet_1_: I’ve been all over the Zone. Nobody was able to stop me. And they tried hard.
You’re in good hands. Just don’t point a gun at me.
sid_phrase_NoonBase_Hub_masina_0_TravelT: I’ll charge you less than other stalkers.
You name the place, I name the price.
sid_phrase_NoonBase_Hub_patno_0_InfoTopi: I don’t know where Quiet got those apples. Never tasted anything so delicious in my whole life!
Wild Island’s getting less crowded, true. But I’m all for change, as long as it’s for the better.
sid_phrase_NoonBase_Hub_patno_0_ResumeCo: All right. What did you want to know again?
sid_phrase_NoonBase_Hub_patno_Whining_1_: I keep dreaming of a train rolling through an endless field of golden wheat. It’s quiet, except for the birds singing… it’s silly. Is there really a place like that?
sid_phrase_NoonBase_Hub_patno_Whining_2_: Everything’s just slipping through my fingers. I don't see blooming fields in my dreams anymore, only nasty swamps… what could that mean?
sid_phrase_NoonBase_Hub_poldenec_0_InfoT: Faust and his Monolith obsession sure messed things up. We were just starting to get accepted in the Zone…
I want something more, ya know? Not the stuff Faust is talking about — just a normal human life.
But is there anything human left in the Zone?
Finally, I can get some sleep. I’m fed up with these late-night sermons.
sid_phrase_NoonBase_Hub_portnoj_0_BusyTo: I don’t have time for this, loner!
sid_phrase_NoonBase_Hub_portnoj_0_ByeTop: Try not to get sick. I can’t do much for you.
sid_phrase_NoonBase_Hub_portnoj_0_HealTo: You seem fine to me. Is there anything else?
I’ll take a look and patch you up, best I can.
sid_phrase_NoonBase_Hub_portnoj_0_HelloT: You sure you can’t handle it on your own?
sid_phrase_NoonBase_Hub_portnoj_0_InfoTo: A while ago, they brought one of our guys in, asked for help. Some stalkers had pumped him full of lead and left him to die.
His belly looked like a sieve. I was scared shitless, but somehow I managed to patch him up. A month later, he was back on his feet. It just happened. Foma told me the same thing happened to him.
But what would I do with the common cold or food poisoning? Guess I’d have to rely on artifacts — at least I know a thing or two about them. And there’s no shortage of ‘em in the Zone, right?
sid_phrase_NoonBase_Hub_portnoj_0_Meet_1: A loner? How did you… never mind, you wouldn’t be here without Strider’s approval. I guess it’s fine, then.
I’m just filling in for the medic here — temporarily, until they find a replacement. For your own sake, I hope it’s nothing too serious.
sid_phrase_NoonBase_Hub_portnoj_0_TradeT: Take what you need and get lost.
sid_phrase_NoonBase_Hub_tihij_0_ByeTopic: Get lost. The air’s thick enough as it is.
It was great talking to you. Let's do this again some time.
If you happen to come back, feel free to stop by.
sid_phrase_NoonBase_Hub_tihij_0_HelloTop: I thought you were a decent guy. Turns out you’re just like everyone else. Just another stalker.
You might be a loner, but there’s something about you that feels just right.
A visitor to Wild Island? Don’t worry, I won’t bite.
sid_phrase_NoonBase_Hub_tihij_0_InfoTopi: Noontiders gave me a hard time. Faust was obsessed with yesterday, and Strider only saw tomorrow.
But life is all about today! That’s why I decided to leave, and, honestly, I’ve never looked back.
I want to ask about something else.
Ask away!
What can you tell me about Wild Island?
This place used to be empty. Hazardous, toxic, and just plain unfriendly. But Lodochka was always in his bunker, tinkering with all kinds of gizmos, researching who knows what.
But where else could Strider and his bunch of former Monolithians go? So he brought the first five of them to Wild Island. He struck a deal with Lodochka, and they seemed to get along.
The Ward didn’t seem to mind, Duty and Freedom didn’t have any interest in these parts. So Wild Island became the Noon base and my very first store!
I’ve got it easier than the rest. They’re all trying to figure out who they were before the Monolith. Me? I don’t care. Living in the moment — that’s how I roll.
What made you quit Noon?
The real question is: how did I end up there in the first place? Strider promised me a shop at the base, and I fell for it.
I thought I could make a difference for myself and my brothers. But then Strider started fighting with Faust, and the trading restrictions stayed in place.
That’s when I knew it just wasn’t gonna work out.
The Zone offers plenty of opportunities. Why trading, of all things?
Well, for one, it’s a peaceful gig. And I get to meet all sorts of folks, make connections, you know. I like that kind of thing.
As soon as Monolith fell, I started working as a courier for some merchants. Picked up some handy business tricks on those runs.
As you can see, it worked out pretty well.
I’m building up my customer base. After Faust took his men, I had no choice.
Surprised? That’s why I’ve stayed close to Wild Island. Noontiders sneak in here all the time.
No worries, though. I’m already in touch with folks from Zalissya and the lady from the Swamps. Pretty soon, the entire Zone will be buzzing about Quiet’s shop!
sid_phrase_NoonBase_Hub_tihij_0_Meet_1_5: I keep it simple: honest work for a fair price. That’s how I do things, and I think others should do the same.
You know the saying, “keep it simple, and people will come to you?” It looks like it’s paying off. You’re here, right?
So, what can I help you with today?
sid_phrase_NoonBase_Hub_tihij_0_TradeTop: You ain’t gettin’ no discounts from me.
You’ll always get the best deals, my friend!
Feel free to pick whatever you like.
sid_phrase_NoonBase_Hub_vektor_0_InfoTop: Strider was right to ban vodka… a drunk stalker is dumber than a mutant, and twice as mean.
sid_phrase_NoonBase_Hub_vektor_0_ResumeC: Sounded like you had more to say.
sid_phrase_NoonBase_Hub_vektor_Whining_1: I’d take an Emission over more rain any day. My boots are like a goddamn sieve!
sid_phrase_OCTE_DialogSkifChoice_alive_3: You still have a lot to learn. You’ll live.
sid_phrase_OCTE_DialogSkifChoice_dead_34: Sorry. Only the strongest survive in the Zone.
sid_phrase_PC_Comment_not_alone_here_1_P: Looks like I'm not alone here…
sid_phrase_PC_Comment_not_alone_here_Exp: Looks like I'm not alone here…
sid_phrase_Pripyat_Hub_Closing_Hub_Closi: Okay, guys, we’re closing up! Best of luck to whoever's left outside!
sid_phrase_Pripyat_Hub_GCA_1_GCA_1_62914: We shouldn’t have left. It’s our town!
sid_phrase_Pripyat_Hub_GCA_1_GCA_1_62915: It’s no one’s town. It belongs to the dead, and I’m not gonna join them. Leaving for Yaniv was a rational decision.
sid_phrase_Pripyat_Hub_GCA_1_GCA_1_62916: Oh yeah? And where are we gonna run when they kick us out of Yaniv? To the Cordon? Or maybe straight to the Mainland? I didn’t join the Corps to be on the run all the time.
sid_phrase_Pripyat_Hub_GCA_1_GCA_1_62917: And I didn’t join up to get killed by the Monolith! We've got orders from Deggy, and they don't say anything about dying for Prypiat.
sid_phrase_Pripyat_Hub_GCA_2_GCA_2_62921: Monolith iced a whole Warden squad yesterday. They're still there, just lying in the street. It's so weird...
sid_phrase_Pripyat_Hub_GCA_2_GCA_2_62922: It’s a good thing, if you ask me.
sid_phrase_Pripyat_Hub_GCA_2_GCA_2_62923: Are we that different, though? They go to the center just like us, they die in Prypiat for no fucking reason. The Zone swallowed them up, and now it's spitting them back out.
sid_phrase_Pripyat_Hub_Opening_Hub_Openi: All right, that’s it. Anyone who’s still alive can come back in.
sid_phrase_Pripyat_Hub_bizon_0_ByeTopic_: You know where to find me.
Come by if you need anything.
sid_phrase_Pripyat_Hub_bizon_0_HubInfo_H: Tell me about Enerhetyk Palace of Culture.
We're the vanguard here. Sounds nice, right? Well, it ain't as pretty as what we gotta do here. But someone has to keep an eye on Prypiat while Deggy is away.
Besides, now that we’re trying to take the town back, we need to know how many Monolithians we've got on our hands, where they're hiding, and what they’re up to.
So we’re kinda scouts, kinda engineers, but most of the time we’re just sorry-ass bastards who were dumb enough to take the job. But Deggy’s right: who else, if not us?
sid_phrase_Pripyat_Hub_bizon_0_InfoTopic: Sometimes I think the Zone is resistant to change. We've been guarding Prypiat for so many years now, and look at it — Monolith and the stalkers, all over again.
And we’re hiding from them in some shithole just like before. Feels like some kinda creepy deja-vu.
sid_phrase_Pripyat_Hub_bizon_0_Meet_Meet: I don’t have much stuff, as you can probably guess. We took all we could to Yaniv, and the rest... well, see for yourself.
Just don’t mention it to Deggy. He wouldn’t approve selling our stuff to a stalker.
But you know every bullet counts in Prypiat. So I’m just saving the life of a fellow human being — for a little donation, of course.
sid_phrase_Pripyat_Hub_bizon_0_NPC_Emiss: It’s over. You can come out now. I hope someone's still alive out there.
It’s started. Everyone but the Monolith, take shelter!
There's an Emission coming. Run and hide.
sid_phrase_Pripyat_Hub_bizon_0_TradeTopi: I don’t have much, but you’re welcome to take a look.
Take what you want and beat it.
I won’t bullshit you with fake discounts, and I'll never sell you any crap.
sid_phrase_Pripyat_Hub_medlak_0_ByeTopic: Say hi to the Monolithians for me.
sid_phrase_Pripyat_Hub_medlak_0_HelloTop: I was hoping you were dead, but here you are...
I’m glad Prypiat hasn’t chewed you up just yet, loner.
sid_phrase_Pripyat_Hub_medlak_0_InfoTopi: Even after all these years, Prypiat is still a mystery. Maybe it’s for the best.
sid_phrase_Pripyat_Hub_medlak_0_Meet_Mee: You know, I’m actually glad you’re here, loner. I dreamed about Prypiat for a long time while I was stuck over in Garbage.
I thought I could dig up something mind-blowing! And I did. Fuck it, man... I was there with Deggy when we found you-know-what.
And now I can’t study it, I can’t do anything with it. But you can. You never made Deggy any promises. You’re one lucky son of a bitch.
sid_phrase_Pripyat_Hub_medlak_0_UpgradeT: By the way, I got my hands on a pretty interesting thing for you...
sid_phrase_Pripyat_Hub_valera_telegraf_0: Prypiat used to be different. Not safer, but calmer. And now the whole town is all edgy, you know? Something’s coming. Something bad.
I deliver messages. Radios don't work great here, and Deggy always wants to be in the loop.
But I don’t like it here. The town's dead, the streets are quiet... too quiet, if you know what I mean.
If you're gonna come with me, you gotta move fast. And don't look back.
Keep up, or you're on your own.
You’re in luck — I found a couple of new safe routes!
sid_phrase_RSQ01_Dialog_Warlock_C01_Fini: Now that’s what I call service! Thank you. I mean it.
sid_phrase_RSQ01_Dialog_Warlock_C02_Fini: It’s done. I culled some Fleshes for you.
Good to hear it. Here’s your reward.
sid_phrase_RSQ01_Dialog_Warlock_C03_Fini: About that artifact you asked for...
I can’t help you with this.
Then why are ya wasting my time? You sure about this?
Then what was that all about? All right. Get a move on.
Well, what good are ya then? Get outta my sight. I’ll pay someone else.
You’re a dependable guy. I respect that.
sid_phrase_RSQ01_Dialog_Warlock_C04_Fini: Those thugs won’t bother you anymore.
Thank you. Good riddance. They’re the worst kind of scum in the Zone. Here’s your reward, plus a little extra.
sid_phrase_RSQ01_Dialog_Warlock_C05_Fini: I took care of those mutants. They weren’t so scary after all.
Well, what did you expect? This is the Lesser Zone, not Prypiat. But even here, you can still get mauled by mutant dogs. So Zalissya thanks you.
sid_phrase_RSQ01_Dialog_Warlock_C06_Fini: I found your rookie. Here’s his PDA.
Damn... I know it sounds stupid, but I kinda hoped you’d bring him back alive... thank you. I’ll pass it along to his people.
sid_phrase_RSQ01_Dialog_Warlock_NoJob_No: Believe it or not, rookies have already snatched all the jobs I had. But don’t you worry, I’ll have something for you soon enough. Our work is never done here.
sid_phrase_RSQ01_Dialog_Warlock_RSQ_Ques: This one’s quick and easy. You just need to pick up a package from the Mainland. The servicemen aren’t too keen to run into me, and the feeling is mutual.
So they left the package in a stash. I sent you the coordinates. Bring it to me, and I’ll pay you well.
The Gaffer says Fleshes are harmless... well, the last time he actually left Zalissya was when Strelok was going after the CNPP.
He doesn’t remember what it feels like when one of those piles of meat rushes right at you. Feel like shooting a few before they try to get to Zalissya?
You know artifacts fade quickly outside of the Zone. So they’re perishable goods, always in high demand.
Nab one for me lickety-split, and I’ll give you a decent amount of coupons. Just don’t screw it up.
There’ve been some people sneaking into Zalissya from Garbage — real nasty people. I won’t let ‘em start shit here.
Will you take care of them for me? Forget about subtlety — we want to send a message.
The Zone is teeming with mutants. Rookies have been getting eaten as soon as they set foot outside!
Who’s gonna buy my drinks if there’s no one left? Eh, I’m joshin’ ya! Or am I?
You never know when your luck might run out in the Zone. I guess you’re well aware of that. But that doesn’t mean we should just leave people twisting in the wind. You feel me?
So I’ve got the last coordinates of this rookie. That’s where he went off radar. His first time in the Zone. Can you go find him? At least bring back his PDA.
sid_phrase_RSQ01_Dialog_Warlock_RSQ_canc: I can’t help you with this.
Then what was that all about? All right. Get a move on.
Well, what good are ya then? Get outta my sight. I’ll pay someone else.
sid_phrase_RSQ01_Dialog_Warlock_RSQ_decl: Do you have anything else for me?
Suit yourself. I’ll pay someone else.
sid_phrase_RSQ01_Dialog_Warlock_RSQ_have: Sure do! I was starting to think I’d have to go out there myself. Here you go, pick something.
sid_phrase_RSQ04_Dialog_Drabadan_C01_Fin: I dealt with your freelancers. They won’t be causing any more trouble.
And guess what? You’re not broke anymore.
sid_phrase_RSQ04_Dialog_Drabadan_C02_Fin: Your clients’ stashes are safe now. I helped those dumb thieves take a little dirt nap, just like you asked.
You’re good at your job, loner. Here’s your reward. You earned it.
sid_phrase_RSQ04_Dialog_Drabadan_C03_Fin: I dealt with that freelancer problem of yours.
Okay! Now any of their pals will think twice before crossing us. Here are your coupons.
sid_phrase_RSQ04_Dialog_Drabadan_C04_Fin: I culled those mutants, just like you asked.
sid_phrase_RSQ04_Dialog_Drabadan_C05_Fin: You did good, loner. And you kept silent about it. Here’s your reward.
sid_phrase_RSQ04_Dialog_Drabadan_C06_Fin: No one’s gonna try to steal your clients from you — or poison them, for that matter.
You did good. Those bastards had it coming.
sid_phrase_RSQ04_Dialog_Drabadan_C07_Fin: You remember the artifact you asked me to get?
You bring me the artifact, and then you get the coupons.
I don’t have it just yet.
So what are you doing here wasting my time?
sid_phrase_RSQ04_Dialog_Drabadan_C10_Fin: Clean job. Nice and silent. I respect that.
As I promised, you get a few extra for the PDA.
sid_phrase_RSQ04_Dialog_Drabadan_NoJob_P: At the moment, I don’t. But come back in a couple of days. Don’t be a stranger.
sid_phrase_RSQ04_Dialog_Drabadan_RSQ_Not: Suit yourself. How about a drink then?
sid_phrase_RSQ04_Dialog_Drabadan_RSQ_can: Nah, I gave it some thought, and I’m not doing it.
Suit yourself, but this is your last chance. Are you sure?
You know what? How about you stop messing with my head and come back when the job is done, huh?
You should’ve said that from the get-go. It’s always somethin’ with you...
sid_phrase_RSQ04_Dialog_Drabadan_RSQ_con: Deal. Now have one for the road.
sid_phrase_RSQ04_Dialog_Drabadan_RSQ_dec: Do you have any other options?
sid_phrase_RSQ04_Dialog_Drabadan_RSQ_hav: I always have work for decent folks. Take your pick.
sid_phrase_RSQ04_Dialog_Drabadan_RSQ_job: Do you have any work for me?
sid_phrase_RSQ04_Dialog_Drabadan_RSQ_no_: At the moment, I don’t. But come back in a couple days. Don’t be a stranger.
sid_phrase_RSQ04_Dialog_Drabadan_RSQ_que: I’ve got this problem with some tourists. A bunch of crooks from outside think they’re allowed to shake down diggers at the Slag Heap, or even stab ‘em. Thing is, those diggers are my clients.
So I want you to find these tourists and deal with them. I promise I’ll pay you good.
Some idiots got themselves into debt playing cards, or maybe they missed a payment to the bandits... I dunno. Thing is, they’ve been going through my clients’ stashes.
No one with half a brain would ever get themselves into a mess like that, and in the Zone, idiots all end up in the same place: the ground. How ‘bout you show ‘em the way?
We’ve got some freelancers hanging around Garbage. They like shaking down loners, but they don’t like paying their cut. That ain’t how it’s done around here.
So I’d like you to take your favorite gun and go teach ‘em some manners. You in?
Mutants have been running rampant here at Garbage. It ain’t the tidiest place as it is, but the damn mutants are just makin’ our image even worse.
Anyway, it’s a fine job for a loner. Go clear those fuckers out and get a load of coupons. Easy-peasy.
Some serious people left a package for me — the kind that should be kept secret.
So deliver it discreetly, and I’ll make you rich. Well, richer than you are now, at least.
One chump was boasting in the bar that he and his pals are gonna sell moonshine to my clients. And not the good kind of moonshine. “Dead men are easier to rip off” — that’s how the fucker put it.
So I want you to put him and his buddies down. No one sells poison to my clients. That’s my prerogative.
A certain notorious kingpin — I won’t tell you his name — instructed me to get a curious artifact for him.
No idea how he’s gonna use it — maybe as a fancy paperweight or somethin’. I didn’t ask, and neither should you.
You haven’t sold your scanner and drunk the coupons, have ya? I’m just kidding, don’t look at me like that. I just happen to have a job that’s perfect for a loner.
We’ll split the profits fair and square. You’re the one who’s risking his ass, after all. So? We got a deal?
I don’t care much for trinkets, but I happen to know a guy who’s willing to buy artifacts. I can be your middleman... for a modest commission, of course.
He won’t buy junk, mind you. But I guess you know your shit.
sid_phrase_RSQ04_Dialog_Drabadan_RSQ_spe: Here’s the deal, loner. Some guys rely too much on their luck. They think they’re on fire, they’re unstoppable.
But luck is a fickle bitch — you never know when she’s gonna dump your ass. Take this one kingpin, for example... he doesn’t realize that his luck ran out a long time ago.
And that he’s become a thorn in the side of some pretty influential people. Get rid of him. I’ll pay extra for his PDA.
sid_phrase_RSQ05_Dialog_Sich_C01_Finish_: Is this what you asked me to bring you?
Yes. Now take your coupons and forget this ever happened.
sid_phrase_RSQ05_Dialog_Sich_C02_Finish_: I had a little chat with those thugs. There were no survivors.
Okay, that’s one less problem to deal with. Take your coupons, and you’re free to go.
sid_phrase_RSQ05_Dialog_Sich_C04_Finish_: I took care of the mutants.
sid_phrase_RSQ05_Dialog_Sich_C05_Finish_: I took care of those stalkers for you.
Yeah. The dumb bastards should’ve left while they still could.
sid_phrase_RSQ05_Dialog_Sich_C10_Finish_: I found out what happened to that man of yours. He’s dead.
Whoever did it’s gonna pay. Here’s your reward. You’re free to go.
sid_phrase_RSQ05_Dialog_Sich_C11_Finish_: You remember that artifact you asked me to get?
Don’t you worry about my memory, loner. It’s just fine. So?
Hey, thanks for the valuable intel. Now go get it.
Here it is. I’d like my payment now.
There you go. You can even spend it right here if you want.
sid_phrase_RSQ05_Dialog_Sich_No_Job_Post: I’ve got nothing for you at the moment. Go make yourself useful elsewhere.
sid_phrase_RSQ05_Dialog_Sich_RSQ_NotGet_: Oh yeah? Aren’t you a picky one?
sid_phrase_RSQ05_Dialog_Sich_RSQ_cancel_: I can’t do what you asked.
You disappoint me. Are you sure?
Okay, okay, fine. I’ll do it.
Then what the hell are you waiting for?
sid_phrase_RSQ05_Dialog_Sich_RSQ_job_363: I’m looking for work. Got any?
sid_phrase_RSQ05_Dialog_Sich_RSQ_quest_1: I want you to deliver me a package. It’s a souvenir for an old friend of mine. You go where I say, you get it, you bring it to me. You don’t ask questions.
As simple as that. Whaddaya say?
sid_phrase_RSQ05_Dialog_Sich_RSQ_quest_2: Some of the stench from Garbage has been wafting our way. Apparently they had some kind of feud over there — a local kingpin got pushed from his turf and had to move towards Zaton.
Now his petty thugs are licking their wounds right on my couriers’ route — too close for comfort, if you ask me. I don’t need any turf wars around here, so they need to get cleared out.
sid_phrase_RSQ05_Dialog_Sich_RSQ_quest_4: I’ve got an easy job for you: get rid of some mutants. There’s a couple of nice, quiet places nearby, good for important sit-downs. I don’t want mutants getting in my way.
The Sultan’s boys are useless. They’re good at shooting loners in the knee, but they can’t handle mutants. Will you do it for me?
sid_phrase_RSQ05_Dialog_Sich_RSQ_quest_5: I need a little pest control around here. See, this bunch of rats were given enough time to get the fuck out of here through the Perimeter, but they’re either too slow or too cocky.
I’m sure they’re expecting me to send someone for them, so don’t give them too much time to get ready.
sid_phrase_RSQ05_Dialog_Sich_RSQ_quest_7: An awfully peculiar artifact is suddenly in high demand on the Mainland. I don’t know why it’s such a bestseller, but I’ve been hearing some pretty disturbing rumors...
Anyhow, we can raise a nice stack of coupons from all the hype. So think fast.
sid_phrase_RSQ05_Dialog_Sich_RSQ_quest_8: I need a specific artifact, and the loner who’s taken the job is drunk as a skunk.
The job’s urgent, and my customer’s out of patience. If you can get me the artifact before the bastard sobers up, you’ll get the reward.
sid_phrase_RSQ05_Dialog_Sich_RSQ_quest_9: I’ve got one regular client: a very serious organization from outside the Perimeter. They always get served first, no matter how long my waiting list is, and even the bandits have no objections.
So if you’re quick enough to get what this client wants, you’ll get a top-notch reward too.
sid_phrase_RSQ05_Dialog_Sich_RSQ_special: One of my most trusted men has gone off the radar, and I’ve been hearing some troubling rumors. You don’t need to know what he’s been up to. Your job is just to find out what happened to him.
My informants have been busy, and they left some clues for us. Get these clues, follow them, and report your findings.
sid_phrase_RSQ06_Dialog_Sidorovich_C01_F: Let's hope they stay that way. Here’s your payment.
sid_phrase_RSQ06_Dialog_Sidorovich_C02_F: The mutants causing trouble on the stalker routes have evolved into their ultimate form. In other words, they’re dead.
As long as I’ve been here, there's never been an end to those freaks. At least you stalkers can always get a side gig.
sid_phrase_RSQ06_Dialog_Sidorovich_C03_F: You can forget about the raiders who were harassing your men.
That settles it. Those scumbags don’t belong at the Cordon. Thanks for your help, loner.
sid_phrase_RSQ06_Dialog_Sidorovich_C04_F: Those stalkers trying to ride your coattails won’t be causing you any more trouble.
sid_phrase_RSQ06_Dialog_Sidorovich_C05_F: I've got your shipment with me.
Nice work! And here’s your pay.
sid_phrase_RSQ06_Dialog_Sidorovich_C09_F: I found a gun for that golden boy of yours.
You sure know your way around, loner. Okay, you’ll get your pay.
sid_phrase_RSQ06_Dialog_Sidorovich_C10_F: Then don’t bother me. If you haven’t got the artifact, scram!
Now you’re talkin’. And here’s your coupons.
sid_phrase_RSQ06_Dialog_Sidorovich_No_Jo: I got no gigs at the moment, but swing by later. Work tends to show up, just not always right when you need it.
sid_phrase_RSQ06_Dialog_Sidorovich_RSQ_N: None of these gigs are my style.
Then hit the road, and stop wastin’ my damn time.
sid_phrase_RSQ06_Dialog_Sidorovich_RSQ_c: I can’t help you with this. Sorry.
Then why are you wastin’ my time? You sure about this?
Okay, I changed my mind. I’ll do it.
I changed my mind too. You’re only gettin’ half the reward. Just kiddin’, ha, ha! The deal remains the same, stalker.
Well, whatever. Now scram. Get out of my hair.
Deal. And no funny business, all right?
sid_phrase_RSQ06_Dialog_Sidorovich_RSQ_d: Do you have anything else in mind?
sid_phrase_RSQ06_Dialog_Sidorovich_RSQ_h: I can toss a few jobs your way, sure. Take your pick. Well, whatcha waitin’ for?
sid_phrase_RSQ06_Dialog_Sidorovich_RSQ_j: Do you have any work for me?
sid_phrase_RSQ06_Dialog_Sidorovich_RSQ_q: Here’s the scoop: we’ve got a zombie problem in this neck of the woods. They’ve offed a few greenhorns and sent the rest packin’.
So head out there and cull the herd, loner. I’ll owe you big time.
All sorts of nasty critters have been crawlin’ outta holes of late. Beats me why. They’re prowlin’ along the stalker routes, making a meal out of the rookies.
Mind you, half of those greenhorns are on my payroll, and the other half owe me cash. In other words, those mutants are fuckin’ with my business. Go take ’em out. I’ll make it worth your while.
Here’s the deal: some pricks keep tryin’ to rob my men. They’re nothing serious, just a bunch of lowlife punks. More a nuisance than anything else.
Still, they gotta be punished — to send a message to the others, ya know. I’ll give you the coordinates, and you can say hello to those wannabe raiders for me.
I’ve heard some assholes have been posin’ as my “sales representatives,” peddlin’ garbage to the rookies using my name.
I wanna spook these “representatives” and make ’em scram so they don’t mess up my rep. They need a real wake-up call. You catch my drift?
I’m gettin’ a shipment from my friends on the Mainland, Skif. My men in the IPSF hid it away in a secret stash.
Now hurry up and grab it before someone else catches wind of it.
So, here’s the deal: I’ve got this order from some folks on the Mainland. They’re lookin’ for an artifact. I dunno why the hell they need it, but they’re offering some serious cash for it.
I mean, we’re talkin’ big hrivna here. Ahem. So, anyway, get me the artifact, and I’ll make sure you’ll be rewarded.
I’ve got an interesting trade offer. The customer doesn’t care about money; he’s after a specific artifact. And I’m mighty keen to get what he’s offerin’ me in exchange.
Here’s the kicker: you ain’t the only one in on this gig. Whoever moves fastest gets a special treat. You game?
One of my stalkers is in a bind. The idiot owes the bandits some cash. Maybe he lost at a card game, maybe he lost a bet... beats me.
Anyway, now they want either their money or his head. I managed to persuade them to settle for a rare artifact instead. It ain't easy to come by, but I’m willin’ to pay you good money.
sid_phrase_RSQ06_Dialog_Sidorovich_RSQ_s: Some of the newbies in the Rookie Village are more gold than green, if you catch my drift. Some rich guy’s kid is comin’ to the Zone for a thrill, and he wants me to make sure everything’s top-notch.
I dunno how long he’ll last here, but we’ll make some cash either way. He wants to shoot a specific kind of gun. Can you get it?
sid_phrase_RSQ07_Dialog_Barmen_C01_Finis: Those zombies won’t bother you anymore.
This calls for a toast! It's on the house. I remember about your reward too. Here you go!
sid_phrase_RSQ07_Dialog_Barmen_C02_Finis: Looks like you’re at the top of your game. Here’s your reward — you earned it.
sid_phrase_RSQ07_Dialog_Barmen_C03_Finis: It’s done. The mutants are dead.
Fantastic news! Your reward’s been patiently waiting for you. Here you go!
sid_phrase_RSQ07_Dialog_Barmen_C04_Finis: Those thugs won’t bother you anymore. Your provider can relax now.
sid_phrase_RSQ07_Dialog_Barmen_C05_Finis: I’ve got what you asked for. Here, take it.
Thanks a ton! You’ve seriously taken a load off my shoulders, and I can’t thank you enough. Here’s your cut.
sid_phrase_RSQ07_Dialog_Barmen_C08_Finis: Well, keep your eyes peeled. But don’t take any crazy chances — no artifact is worth losing your life over.
Here’s the artifact you asked me to get.
I knew you had it in you! And here’s your reward.
sid_phrase_RSQ07_Dialog_Barmen_C09_Finis: And I take it there were no Freedom folks left to spin a yarn about what happened? Pleasure doing business with you! I’ve already sent you the coupons.
sid_phrase_RSQ07_Dialog_Barmen_No_Job_Po: It doesn’t happen too often, but there’s no work here right now. Swing by later.
sid_phrase_RSQ07_Dialog_Barmen_RSQ_NotGe: I’ve got other things to do.
Your call. Just a heads-up — these gigs get snatched up real quick, so don’t drag your feet. There ain’t much entertainment around here.
sid_phrase_RSQ07_Dialog_Barmen_RSQ_cance: Oh… that's a damn shame. Are you sure?
You’re pretty quick to shift gears, huh? Well, whatever floats your boat.
Too bad. I bet you would’ve done a great job. Feel free to swing by if you have a change of heart.
sid_phrase_RSQ07_Dialog_Barmen_RSQ_confi: Alrighty then! I’ll be here when you’re done.
sid_phrase_RSQ07_Dialog_Barmen_RSQ_job_5: I need money. Got any jobs?
sid_phrase_RSQ07_Dialog_Barmen_RSQ_quest: Those zombies have been getting way too close to our base lately. I’d rather not see them bust into the factory.
How about I toss you some cash to take out a few of those fuckers? As a prophylactic measure, like the doc says.
We’re right in the middle of a contest, and there’s some sweet cash up for grabs. Maybe you’d be up for joining us?
The Mutant of the Week is a blind draw, so everything’s fair and square. If you manage to bag more mutants than the current champ, you can snatch his crown.
We’ve got a couple of routes that are crawling with mutants. They’re like mutant magnets or something. The only one who's thrilled about it all is Marshal.
How about this: help me get rid of the critters, and I’ll fork over some cash. It'll go a whole lot smoother and quicker if we team up.
Look, some thugs have been seriously messing up my supply chain. My provider’s too scared to make a move with those jerks on the loose, and we’re dangerously low on supplies.
Help me fix this mess, and I’ll make sure it’s worth your while.
I’ve got a job lined up. I need a courier to pick up some merchandise.
I’ll give you the coordinates. You grab the goods, and I’ll slip you a cut of the profits. That work for you?
We’ve got a fun thing going on here: an artifact hunt. Anyone can get in on the action. The first one to bring it in pockets a tidy sum of money.
Sure, it’s not exactly a job, but as long as you end up with some coupons, who cares? What do you say? Got a competitive streak in you?
I obtained a certain something for Komissarov, and my partners are eyeing a particular artifact in return. Fetch me that artifact, and I’ll make sure you’re paid a fair price.
I’d hate to lose my supplier, and it’ll be a win-win if we keep Komissarov stocked up, don’t ya think?
I’ve got a sweet deal where we can both rake in some decent dough. Get me an artifact, and we’ll go 50-50 on those coupons.
sid_phrase_RSQ07_Dialog_Barmen_RSQ_speci: I’ve caught wind that Freedom is moving a valuable artifact. My source is as solid as they come — I've even got exact coordinates.
I’ll shell out some serious cash if you send them to their maker and bring the artifact to me. Those hippies have had it good for too long. It’s time to serve them a heaping helping of justice.
sid_phrase_RSQ08_Dialog_Barmen_C01_Finis: Your shipments are safe now. I’ve taken care of those mutants.
Well done. You just earned yourself a payday.
sid_phrase_RSQ08_Dialog_Barmen_C02_Finis: Those punks are gone. Things should be quieter around here from now on.
My customers will be glad to hear that. Here’s your reward.
sid_phrase_RSQ08_Dialog_Barmen_C03_Finis: The freeloaders you told me about have paid their dues in full.
It’s always a pleasure doing business with you.
sid_phrase_RSQ08_Dialog_Barmen_C04_Finis: Here’s your package, safe and sound.
Yep, everything’s in good order. Your payment's good to go.
sid_phrase_RSQ08_Dialog_Barmen_C05_Finis: Here’s that gift. You can have it.
A big shout-out from a satisfied customer!
sid_phrase_RSQ08_Dialog_Barmen_C06_Finis: About that artifact you wanted me to find…
So why are you bothering me?
Take a look at this one.
Perfect. The customer will be delighted.
sid_phrase_RSQ08_Dialog_Barmen_C09_Finis: That “old acquaintance” of yours won’t ever bother you again.
Fantastic news! You’ve earned yourself a generous reward, just as I promised.
sid_phrase_RSQ08_Dialog_Barmen_No_Job_Po: Jobs are going fast. You need coupons to have a good time in Rostok, you see. But swing by again sometime — there’s always something fresh on the horizon.
sid_phrase_RSQ08_Dialog_Barmen_RSQ_NotGe: I'm not interested in this kind of job for now.
Flush with coupons, are you? Well, whatever.
sid_phrase_RSQ08_Dialog_Barmen_RSQ_cance: I can’t help you with this. Sorry.
I’ve got another man for the job. Are you sure about this?
Okay, I changed my mind. I’ll do it.
All right. Go take a break, then. There’ll be other folks willing to step up.
sid_phrase_RSQ08_Dialog_Barmen_RSQ_confi: I'll wait for some news then.
sid_phrase_RSQ08_Dialog_Barmen_RSQ_decli: Do you have anything else in mind?
sid_phrase_RSQ08_Dialog_Barmen_RSQ_haveJ: You’re in the market for some new gear, and the old coupon stash is running low, eh? I get it. Here’s what I can offer you.
sid_phrase_RSQ08_Dialog_Barmen_RSQ_job_5: Do you have any work for me?
sid_phrase_RSQ08_Dialog_Barmen_RSQ_no_jo: Jobs are going fast. You need coupons to have a good time in Rostok, you see. But swing by again sometime — there’s always something fresh on the horizon.
sid_phrase_RSQ08_Dialog_Barmen_RSQ_quest: I dunno what Duty’s up to, but the mutants are getting real rowdy. They’ve taken to jumping our couriers. We’ve lost a couple of vital shipments, and it’s really ticking Myklukha off.
It’s not good for business to have mindless beasts messing with our deliveries. If you clear them out, I’ll slide a few coupons your way.
My customers have been griping about some punks in the area ruining their good time. The jingle of coin draws in all sorts of scum, and Rostok’s been jingling enough to wake the whole Zone up.
We gotta teach those pricks some manners and set an example.
Some jerks had a wild time at my bar. They ate, they drank, they raised hell… but when it came time to pay up, they decided to get into a shootout with security instead of ponying up the coupons.
Freedom ain’t an excuse for lawlessness. I need you to deal with them and make sure nobody else ever thinks about pulling a stunt like that again.
I’ve got some exclusive goods on the way. The request came from the pickiest customers at my bar. It's the kind of stuff you won’t find anywhere else in the Zone.
I need someone trustworthy to deliver the package in one piece.
A wealthy customer got a birthday gift from the Mainland. The courier stashed it nearby.
It’s kinda urgent, but this customer’s a real big tipper. If you can handle the delivery, you’ll get a sweet bonus.
I need an artifact delivered pronto. The customer wants to keep a low profile, but, judging by the sound of his voice, he’s got a wallet full of coupons, I can tell you that much.
If you’re up to it, make it snappy.
Some guy who was tying one on here said he’s in the market for a specific artifact. And wouldn’t you believe it, Freedom just shipped off the last batch of 'em to the Mainland.
He’s throwing down some serious cash, way more than the usual market rate. It’d be a shame to let this opportunity slide. You might as well give it a shot.

There’s this weirdo, maybe a collector or an artist like our pal Screw, and he's on the hunt for an artifact. It's gotta be a real specific one, found by a lone stalker under a full moon or some shit.
Don’t sweat the details, though. Just snag me that artifact, and I’ll weave a captivating tale for our customer.
sid_phrase_RSQ08_Dialog_Barmen_RSQ_speci: I overheard something about a Duty officer and his squad setting up shop near here. He’s an old acquaintance of mine, by the sound of it.
I’ve got a hunch they might be gunning for me. Even if they’re not, their motives ain’t likely to be friendly, now that the Treaty went south. Take care of them, and I’ll make it worth your while.
sid_phrase_RSQ09_Dialog_Spica_C01_Finish: The neighborhood has fewer mutants now.
No serious injuries, I hope? Your payment should cover any expenses.
sid_phrase_RSQ09_Dialog_Spica_C02_Finish: I dealt with those mutants that were ruining your equipment.
Spare me the details. Here’s your reward.
sid_phrase_RSQ09_Dialog_Spica_C03_Finish: The mutants are less likely to harass your patients now.
Thank you! At last, I’ll have more time for my research.
sid_phrase_RSQ09_Dialog_Spica_C04_Finish: I tracked those runaway patients of yours down.
Let’s keep this between us. Here’s your bonus.
sid_phrase_RSQ09_Dialog_Spica_C05_Finish: Your wait is over. I’ve got your package right here.
At last! Here, a little extra for your trouble.
sid_phrase_RSQ09_Dialog_Spica_C09_Finish: Yes, it did the trick! Thank you.
sid_phrase_RSQ09_Dialog_Spica_C10_Finish: About that artifact you asked me about…
No, I’m still working on it.
Well, I’ll be waiting. Try not to take too long.
A fine specimen! Thank you kindly.
sid_phrase_RSQ09_Dialog_Spica_No_Job_Pos: I’m out of jobs for now. Swing by later.
sid_phrase_RSQ09_Dialog_Spica_RSQ_NotGet: I think I’ll try my luck elsewhere.
Well, if you change your mind, I’ll be here.
sid_phrase_RSQ09_Dialog_Spica_RSQ_cancel: This job of yours — it’s not working out.
Too bad. Is this your final decision?
sid_phrase_RSQ09_Dialog_Spica_RSQ_confir: I’m so glad to hear that!
sid_phrase_RSQ09_Dialog_Spica_RSQ_job_60: Do you have any work for me?
sid_phrase_RSQ09_Dialog_Spica_RSQ_quest_: As a committed scientist, I recognize the significance of field research. Regrettably, venturing into the Zone is out of the question for me, what with all the mutants around.
I’d be truly grateful if you could help thin out their population.
I’ve had this idea I’ve been itching to test for quite a while now. It’s almost guaranteed to work… in theory. However, the local mutants have wrecked my equipment three times already.
In order to test my idea in the field, I don’t really have any other choice but to ask for your help in ending their lives — humanely if you can, but, above all, swiftly and efficiently.
We’ve been dealing with a surge in mutant encounters lately. If this keeps up, I’ll be stuck pulling double shifts instead of getting ready for my job at SIRCAA.
I thought it would be more expedient to request your help in eliminating the root cause of the issue than to continue tending to all the casualties.
Some of my patients managed to escape from my care. They’ve undergone certain… procedures and now pose a threat not only to themselves, but also to those around them.
I’m afraid I must request your assistance in resolving this issue. The research in question is classified, so this needs to remain strictly between us.
You wouldn’t believe how tough it is to get anything around here. I've waited a whole six months for a package from the Mainland.
Your task is to move it from a secure location to Malachite as swiftly as you can. I’d be extremely grateful for your assistance.
I’m currently on the hunt for a particular artifact. If you manage to secure it for me, I’ll be more than generous.
Our research staff loves to complain about funding, that much is true. As for myself, I’m ready to dip into my own stash if I have to. Science requires a degree of flexibility, after all.
A graduate student I know managed to get into a lecture with Dr. Kryvenko, the renowned scientist from the Institute! He’s willing to share his notes, but in return, he wants a specific artifact.
I think having access to SIRCAA’s data would greatly enhance my understanding of the Scorcher’s effect on the brain. Thus, I’m willing to pay handsomely for this artifact.
A colleague reached out to me. He's trying to obtain an artifact that’s officially prohibited in his research.
If you ask me, bureaucracy can only hinder scientific advancement. Let’s lend him a hand — he’s willing to offer us a tidy sum.
sid_phrase_RSQ09_Dialog_Spica_RSQ_specia: Even with the Scorcher offline, I’m pressing ahead with my research. I’m making good progress, but every now and then, issues crop up.
Recently, the equipment I’ve set up around the area has started acting up. I’d appreciate it if you could pitch in. It’s nothing too fancy, a simple reboot of the devices should do the job.
sid_phrase_RSQ10_Dialog_Harpy_C01_Finish: I gunned down all the mutants.
Damn, I took a smoke break and lost track of time. I’m not sure if you beat my record, but you’ve earned the reward anyway.
sid_phrase_RSQ10_Dialog_Harpy_C02_Finish: It took a bit of work, but I got rid of the mutants in the area.
You make it sound like it’s a huge accomplishment. I would’ve handled it myself if I… Anyway, I’ve transferred the coupons.
sid_phrase_RSQ10_Dialog_Harpy_C03_Finish: I took care of those loners who ambushed your patrol.
We’re good at keeping secrets, and we’re willing to pay handsomely to make sure they stay kept.
sid_phrase_RSQ10_Dialog_Harpy_C04_Finish: Those assholes won’t be using people as lockpicks anymore. Ever again.
I’m gonna sleep like a baby tonight! Enjoy your coupons, loner.
sid_phrase_RSQ10_Dialog_Harpy_C05_Finish: And now you’ve got my coupons too.
sid_phrase_RSQ10_Dialog_Harpy_C09_Finish: The perpetrators have been dealt with.
Few things make me happier than seeing my comrades avenged. Here’s your reward.
sid_phrase_RSQ10_Dialog_Harpy_C10_Finish: I want to talk about that artifact deal.
Then make it quick. I was about to take a nap.
Wow, thanks for keeping me in the loop. That was truly crucial information.
You really found it? Whoa. Here’s your pay.
sid_phrase_RSQ10_Dialog_Harpy_No_Job_Pos: Take a stroll. There aren’t any gigs for you at the moment.
sid_phrase_RSQ10_Dialog_Harpy_RSQ_NoGet_: None of these gigs are my style.
Suit yourself. I’ll pay someone else.
sid_phrase_RSQ10_Dialog_Harpy_RSQ_after1: Some assholes killed two of our guys on their way to Prypiat. Normally, we’d handle it ourselves, but right now, we’re stretched thin. So get to Prypiat, find the perpetrators, and put them down.
sid_phrase_RSQ10_Dialog_Harpy_RSQ_before: Some assholes killed two of our officers and somehow made it out of Prypiat. Take out everyone involved, and the Corps will give you plenty of coupons.
sid_phrase_RSQ10_Dialog_Harpy_RSQ_cancel: I can’t help you with this. It’s too much trouble.
Chickened out, have you? Of course you have. All right, this is your last shot, and there won’t be another. Are you absolutely sure about this?
I guess I’ll give it a shot.
Yes. I’m not doing it, and that’s final.
I don’t give a fuck. Someone else’ll come along.
sid_phrase_RSQ10_Dialog_Harpy_RSQ_confir: No big deal, I’ll do it.
sid_phrase_RSQ10_Dialog_Harpy_RSQ_declin: Do you have anything else for me?
sid_phrase_RSQ10_Dialog_Harpy_RSQ_haveJo: Lemme take a good look at you… ha. Okay, here’s what I’ve got lined up for you.
sid_phrase_RSQ10_Dialog_Harpy_RSQ_job_61: You said you had a job for me?
sid_phrase_RSQ10_Dialog_Harpy_RSQ_quest_: Before I got into the junk repair business here, I used to be the top shot in my squad.
If you’ve got the time and ammo to spare, why not show me what you’re made of? Your job is to clear the perimeter of mutants. Let’s see if you can outdo my personal best.
I fix stalkers’ guns every day, but it seems like the mutant population around here just keeps growing. Is that why you’re here too? ‘Cuz the other places don’t offer enough of a challenge?
We’re sentries here, not mutant hunters. Still, we’re willing to pay folks to cull their numbers. This place could use some discipline, after all.
There’s a reason why Deggy’s the one who decides who gets a pass and who doesn’t. This place has its share of scumbags, you know.

There were these guys... I did some repairs on their guns. They stuck around for a couple of weeks, and then they thought it’d be a good idea to ambush our patrol. You know, to figure out our secrets.
Anyway, I need you to deal with those pricks. The pay’s decent.
There was this band of stalkers... they went out, then came back a few men short and loaded with artifacts. That’s the norm around these parts, but these fellas looked a little too smug.
And then… let’s say I was tinkering with a radio and accidentally tuned into their frequency. Turns out they’d been sending folks into anomalies instead of using bolts. “Lockpicks,” they called ’em.
I’m offering a reward for those fuckers’ heads — preferably with a bullet in ’em.
Sure, I can cobble together a gun from various odds and ends, but sometimes I crave something a little different.
Some old buddies from the military sent a couple of brand new gadgets my way, and I’m itching to give ‘em a spin. Fetch me the package, and I’ll owe you one.
Myklukha might be a greedy son of a bitch, but he’s not eager to risk his own people. Instead, he sources some of his artifacts right here in Yaniv.
They’re offering me some brand new tools straight from the Mainland in exchange for a particular artifact. That’s why I’m willing to pay a tidy sum if you can bring it here.
I’ve heard the artifacts in Prypiat come with all sorts of fancy gimmicks. Personally, I don’t care much for those lousy rocks. Compared to them, even nuts and bolts are more interesting.
But a buddy of mine has a thing for ‘em, and I want to give him a present. If you find me an artifact, I’ll pay you good money for it. If not, well... don’t waste my time.
Now and then, passionate collectors try to make their way into Prypiat in search of a particularly rare artifact. Deggy almost always turns ‘em away, though.
He’s got a point, of course. It’s better for us to earn some cash than to let the dumbass die for a piece of rock in the first anomaly he stumbles into, right?
sid_phrase_RSQ10_Dialog_Harpy_RSQ_specia: Sometimes we have break-ins in the sealed-off areas. It always ends up a bloody mess, but this time it was especially nasty business.
sid_phrase_RookieVillage_Hub_Audiolog_1_: Did you see those containers, Wolf? What the fuck was in there? Embryos? What are they growing in those things?
If you ask me, those... things they’ve been growing should definitely stay in the catacombs.
That’s why I blew up the entrance. Wouldn’t want to come to the Cordon one day and find you all ripped to shreds, now would I?
sid_phrase_RookieVillage_Hub_Audiolog_2_: Eh, just my luck! Right after I finally talked the Gaffer into approving my transfer!
Don't sweat it. I’m telling you, they’ll get rid of those Monolithians soon enough. And then you might be not so eager to leave the Cordon after all.
Well, I heard Sidorovich talking to that guy... I think they call him Dark. He thinks he’s on the verge of finding the Oasis. Right here, in our backyard!
Oasis my ass... Sidorovich knew you were snooping, and he said exactly what you wanted to hear. He doesn’t want to die, so he feeds bullshit like that to suckers like us.
sid_phrase_RookieVillage_Hub_Closing_Hub: Listen up, stalkers! Some scumbag has been shooting people around here. So lay low and wait for us to put a bullet in his brain.
sid_phrase_RookieVillage_Hub_GCA_1_GCA_1: You should really stop fixing your gear with duct tape. It won’t protect you from radiation... or a bandit’s bullet.
Have you seen the prices for repairs? To say nothing of new gear! Nah, I’m not getting myself drowned in debt.
What are you, stupid? If you don’t want to borrow for gear, you’ll be borrowing for meds soon enough. Or should I patch you up with duct tape too?
sid_phrase_RookieVillage_Hub_GCA_2_GCA_2: Hey, Wolf. They say you know Strelok, huh?
What’s he like? Can he really, like… sense anomalies? And drive mutants away with the power of his mind?
He’s a normal guy. A decent, hard-working guy. Not a blabbermouth like you.
sid_phrase_RookieVillage_Hub_GCA_3_GCA_3: So I’m like, “You okay?” And he’s like, “The Monolith...” Eyes empty and all. Then he grabbed his rifle, so I had to shoot.
Sanya the Brick, huh... I heard a similar story in Zalissya, but it was about Uncle Lyonya.
It’s so fucked up... I mean, I'd know who I was talking to! Right?
sid_phrase_RookieVillage_Hub_GCA_4_GCA_4: So, what’s with Zalissya? Is it done for?
Not as far as I know. They've got pretty good defenses, so no need to worry about them.
They’re lucky to have the Gaffer. Now there's a real leader! Always fights with his men.
Not like our guy. He just seals himself up in his bunker as soon as the Monolith shows up on the horizon, leaving us all to die on his doorstep.
sid_phrase_RookieVillage_Hub_Opening_Hub: Okay, show's over. Someone neutralized the shooter... I guess. All's quiet in our little village.
sid_phrase_RookieVillage_Hub_kit_0_BusyT: Come on, you’re not dying. Suck it up and wait.
sid_phrase_RookieVillage_Hub_kit_0_ByeTo: As your doctor, I hope I don’t see you soon.
Just be smart, okay? I’m getting tired of patching you all up…
sid_phrase_RookieVillage_Hub_kit_0_HealT: If it wasn’t for the Hippocratic Oath, I wouldn’t lift a damn finger.
Nothing critical. Just a couple of scratches.
You'll be as good as new, badass scars are on the house.
sid_phrase_RookieVillage_Hub_kit_0_Hello: I told you not to bother me with nonsense.
So, how are you? How’s your health?
sid_phrase_RookieVillage_Hub_kit_0_InfoT: You know, gunshot wounds, blind dog bites... same old, same old. Oh yeah, and I’m alternating my surgical knots every day. To keep me on my toes. This isn't Prypiat — life is pretty dull here.
I keep hearing new tall tales about magic vodka. Miraculous stuff. One shot gives you the power to kill a Controller. Folks actually believe it, you know! And I have to deal with the consequences.
sid_phrase_RookieVillage_Hub_kit_0_Meet_: The Zone seems to have run out of mutants and anomalies. You know what I get in the infirmary every day now? Gunshot wounds.
All my patients say they got shot by Monolithians.
Which is strange. It’s like they’re everywhere all at once...
I’ve been here way too long. But how can I leave them?
They’re all wet behind the ears. Without a doctor, they’ll be dropping like flies. And I don't have a replacement.
Or decent gear, to tell you the truth. What I have is no good for the deep Zone, and I’m planning to head out there one day. I filed a request with Sidorovich. He’s looking into it.
sid_phrase_RookieVillage_Hub_kit_0_Trade: I’m not SIRCAA, you know. I don’t get meds helo'ed in.
I can’t promise you any miracles, but I think you’ll find what you need.
I’ve only got the bare essentials.
sid_phrase_RookieVillage_Hub_kosta_lomov: You picked a really bad time…
They have it easy in Zalissya, with Warlock and the Gaffer to wipe their asses for 'em. Back in the day, everybody used to come here, to Sidorovich and Wolf. And we ended up with men like Strelok.
They say SIRCAA’s already been scattered by the Monolithians. Well, the bigger they are, the harder they fall. They aren't the first bugbears in the Zone, and they won’t be the last.
I think I’ll ask Wolf how to get there as soon as the dust settles. Maybe I’ll get myself some coupons.
When you’ve seen enough shit in the Zone, you learn to appreciate simple comforts — like a warm bed.
The prices here have always been ridiculous, but now that the Monolith is around, they've really skyrocketed.
sid_phrase_RookieVillage_Hub_multik_0_By: Okay, good luck, and have fun.
sid_phrase_RookieVillage_Hub_multik_0_He: Okay, what is it? You won’t piss off anyway.
Good to see you. Looking for some more firepower?
You need repairs? That, I can do.
sid_phrase_RookieVillage_Hub_multik_0_In: The Zone used to begin here, at the Cordon. Sidorovich never had a shortage of customers. Now all the rookies go straight to Zalissya. The loot's better there, and they got a half-decent bar.
But Zalissya’s also closer to the Ward, and they’re like a drunk neighbor — you never know when they're gonna start a fight or burn your house down.
sid_phrase_RookieVillage_Hub_multik_0_Me: What if there wasn’t any Signal? What if Noontide was just pretending to be a bunch of hapless idiots?
While waiting for a chance to seize control of the whole Zone?
I don’t have many customers, but all of them are loyal.
They say I've got the magic touch and can fix anything.
But it’s not the magic touch, it’s the magic gear Sidorovich managed to get me. With tools like that, anyone would be a magician.
sid_phrase_RookieVillage_Hub_multik_0_Up: You idiots can’t take care of your shit, and I’m the one who has to put it back together.
Not much work here, to be honest.
Well, well, let’s see what I can do.
sid_phrase_RookieVillage_Hub_serega_dubc: Be careful out there, all right?
I have to be really careful with my gear. Fix it with duct tape where I can, use old shock absorbers for my armored vest...
What else am I supposed to do? Sidorovich's prices are a real bitch.
So, the Emissions weren’t enough, were they? Now we got this Signal in the middle of the night. Is it gonna keep happening, just like the Emissions?
My boots are killing me... but it’s either new kicks, or more ammo. Not much of a choice, really...
Meds are getting to be pricier than artifacts. You gotta think twice before heading out there nowadays.
sid_phrase_RookieVillage_Hub_sidorovic_0: Will you gimme a break?
Now get out of my hair.
Good hunting, stalker.
We’ll meet again, Zone willing.
So, not dead yet, huh? Okay, tell me what you want. Keep it short.
You’re alive! You lucky dog!
Come on. I don’t bite.
Well, it’s a village – nice and quiet.
Everyone knows each other inside-out. We know who’s good at what, who’s up for the job, and who’s useless.
Everyone keeps an eye on strangers, mind you. If you steal something or hurt someone, you’ll get your ass kicked on the spot by our warm, supportive community.
And no one’s going hungry here, you know. This place ain’t exactly Tiffany's, but it ain’t the Prypiat Grocery Store either. Go see for yourself. I won’t charge you for the first half-hour.
Tell me about the Rookie Village.
What do you think I am, “The Chornobyl Fucking Herald”? If you need to hear the latest bullshit, go sit by the campfire. The boys’ll be happy to give you the 411.
So it's like this: any kind of psi-protection sells like hotcakes. Artifacts, meds, you name it. And I’m always ready to help. And I've got a huge selection.
Well, well. Just like old times.
The Zone is freaking out, the Monolithians shoot whatever they see, and stalkers are jumping at their own shadows.
But none of it affects business. It just keeps getting better and better.
I’m a respected man. Every single dog around these parts knows my name. I was doing business with the Zone bigshots when you were still in diapers.
I run a tight ship here at the Cordon. So, if you need to restock or sell something at a decent price, this is where you want to be.
We can help you out with other stuff too. But for that kind of help, you're gonna owe us. And we’ll ask you to return the favor soon enough.
Okay, the Emission’s done. If you think you need medical assistance, go talk to Whale, he’ll patch you up. Or put you outta your misery.
And the shit-show begins! I hope you’re all holed up deep enough. If not, it’s been a pleasure knowing you. Now sit tight until further notice.
Hey you, wannabe Robin Hoods, there's an Emission coming! Now hide your asses if you wanna live. I won’t tell you twice.
No amends, then. You’re asking too much.
Are you messing with me, Skif? You wanna be looking over your shoulder for the rest of your life? If so, be my guest.
My life is definitely worth more.
Now we’re talking business. Okay. No one’s putting a bullet in your head, that I can promise you. Now scram.
How about we mend fences with you and your loners?
I see… So you want to make amends, eh, stalker?
You hope I’ll put in a good word for you with my men? Why would I do that? I agree with them – after all the stunts you’ve pulled, your life is worth less than an empty tin can. Or am I wrong?
Why don’t you think about what’s good for you, and I’ll do the same?
Straight to the point. I respect that.
Okay, now let’s talk business.
sid_phrase_RookieVillage_Hub_vita_ded_0_: Is this really the time for a chat?
I won't say goodbye. It’s bad luck.
I went on a run with some friends once and got plenty of loot. So we celebrated, of course, and I passed out. Woke up — no loot, no “friends.” They even took my mug! Since then, I always go alone.
You know, I’m scared to even look at my PDA 'cuz of this thing with Monolith. Every time I ping my friends, I get nothing but crickets.
