Maybe. But it doesn't matter anymore. Now we've all got a chance to survive, understand?
We got bigger problems now. So what's your plan, lifesaver?
It looks like you sent me off to chase the rat, but it turns out the rat is right here.
Risk what? Your life, mine, the whole camp?
You said you wanted to save the group. I wasn't part of it, so you sold me out for it. You didn't even lie about it.
No! You're one of us! Skif, I'm a bastard, but it saved us. Better this than a bunch of honest corpses!
I made it so the whole network could hear us talking about Volkhov. I thought they'd send thugs! If I knew, I'd never have…
You better tell me. Facts, now.
You sent me to look for a fake rat so I wouldn't get in your way?
What the hell?! Turn it off!
What the hell are you doing? You almost lost it with that transmission!
I got it under control. It was tough without Medulin, but I managed. I installed the amp and recorded the whole thing.
I really did trust you.
You can think whatever of me, but I'll prove to you it was worth trusting me.
Scoutmaster left at the right moment as part of your plan, too?
Not exactly. He… doesn't show it, but Scoutmaster's scared of the transmission. I turned it on near him, and he was just gone.
I was lucky there. Medulin and Rascal can't decide anything without him, or without you. I can.
Remember when you said you trusted me? You were right, I pulled it off… but I had to do this one nasty thing.
sid_phrase_supack_SQ101_QD_MercOnRadio_s: Not a lot of campsites in the Burnt Forest. Sweep them all.
No prisoners this time. If you find any radio equipment, destroy it.
Damn. We don't have much time. Our only hope now is Medulin and his interference signal.
Good luck to us – we're gonna need it. And I'm not going anywhere, so chill.
sid_phrase_supack_SQ101_QD_VazCallAboutB: Thank Banzai for that. He screwed both of us over, although he did record the transmission.
What?! Okay, you can explain later, after you've dealt with the guests. I'll go to Medulin.
Banzai recorded the whole transmission. We need Medulin to decipher it ASAP, but he's drunk as a skunk.
You picked the perfect time to get him drunk… Okay, I got it. I'll sober him up, you deal with that squad.
Skif, Scoutmaster here. We're being watched. I spotted a crew on my way back.
sid_phrase_supack_SQ101_QD_WithShelmaAbo: Stop looking for excuses and help your group figure all this out.
How am I supposed to help? I can't shoot the broad side of a barn! Fine. I'll try to think of something… I'm at least creative.
And thanks for being so… understanding.
Not so fast. You traded with Skull. What else did you sell him? Did you sell me out at Volkhov?
What?! No! Hell no! I'm an honest merchant, I'm not like that! Think about it – why would I do that?
You got us into this mess, you figure out what to do now.
"Up to me"… So, it's up to me which side I get a slug from, the front or the back?
Okay. This isn't your problem, and I didn't pay for consulting services. It's up to me…
I thought you were a traitor, but it turns out you're just dumb. You got people killed.
I found your notes. It looks like you and Skull go way back.
Fuck… Don't do anything rash now, let me explain!
I didn't know! I didn't! What do you want me to do, shoot myself? Or go to a gunfight with Skull all alone? What do you want from me?!
You're a fuckwit, Rascal. But not a traitor, at least.
I'm no traitor. Traitors know what they're doing, and I…
I know everything. Better come clean.
About what? Skif, what the hell? I bought scrap from Banzai below market price, but he agreed!
Got Medulin medkits, you know he's paranoid… Wait. You went through my notes? Fuck…
Stay quiet from now on. Or this camp will make sure you won't live long.
Figured as much. I'll stay quiet, then…
It sucks, but you're right about that. I made a fool of myself.
Sorry, pal. I closed up shop. I'm getting outta here.
I thought it'd be my USP – complex radio equipment! I was just looking for a partner who'd transport it into the Zone for a fee.
When some mercs contacted me, it felt like I hit the jackpot. I'd give them a list of orders, they'd deliver a box of spares. Clean, fast, safe.
How was I supposed to know they wanted names, not a profit share?! Or know what they'd do… to customers. Or to me later on!
Why didn't you come clean earlier? Like when they put a bounty on our heads?
What was I supposed to say? "Hey, I'm the reason they wanna kill us"?!
sid_phrase_supack_SQ101_QD_WithVozAfterS: You never said you knew Skull. Or that you were a mercenary.
Because I wanted to live, and the only way to quit is to go six feet under. So Surgeon is dead, now long live Scoutmaster.
I need Kazkovyi. My future quiet place for my golden years. Skull wants to take that away from me – so I'll have to defend myself with the people I have here.
You won't slaughter another group after listening to that transmission?
There's no way to be certain. But I can feel it when it's about to happen.
That's why I came out here. To hold some old stuff, and let out my anger on the mutants. To remind myself who I am. It helps.
Don't you worry about me. Get back to Kazkovyi.
I worked with Skull for many years. You heard of Surgeon? I guess not, I stepped back long ago.
There he was, gathering the ones still alive for one last gig, promising them a quiet house on the Mainland. "In the Zone together, out of the Zone together".
I fell for it. Bloody romantic… You heard how he abandoned me to listen to Zombie FM. So now Skull and I are in a conflict of interest.
I'll get a grip and go back to Kazkovyi. You should check if Medulin or Rascal happen to have a little rat's tail.
I'll do just that.
Remember the "perfect storm" and NK-3? I'm almost certain their complete lack of luck is Skull's handiwork. So is the disappearance of the previous groups.
It's hard to trust you, after what happened.
I can understand that, but I need your trust now more than ever.
Once Skull gets to the camp, and he will, we'll have to watch each other's backs.
Let's say you didn't rat me out, but somebody else sure did.
I'd check Medulin and Rascal. I have my suspicions.
It's quite easy with Medulin. He doesn't drink usually, but if you get him to, he won't be able to hold his tongue.
Rascal is a tough one. But Medulin did say he had a secret warehouse in the camp, so he definitely has something to hide.
If you did work with Skull before – spill it. Who's behind the transmission?
One of ours contacted Skull, promised him a ticket out and a new life. A tale as old as time, but this one was convincing.
Someone on the Mainland really wants this shit to be broadcast, and really doesn't want any scientists paying attention to it.
I'm almost sure the previous groups got into similar 'accidents', orchestrated by someone.
sid_phrase_supack_SQ101_QD_WithVozNearSt: You hid this stash for so long, and now you just agree to show it?
I have things to hide, but not now, and not from you. No need to be scared, crack it open.
Fine. But I wouldn't do anything stupid if I were you.
Here. Open the safe yourself, for the sake of the investigation.
sid_phrase_supack_SQ101_QD_WithVozatijAf: He said only a fool trusts words alone. I underestimated the lad. What did he send you here for?
Someone tipped off the mercs about my visit to Volkhov. Everything points to you.
Fair enough. If I were you, I'd have figured as much.
I'm no saint myself, but nothing like that. Come on, I'll show you the stash.
Who told you about this place? And, since you're here, what for?
Good job dealing with those mutants. Almost too good for an old guide like yourself.
Good eye. I didn't become a guide right away, and spent my whole life shooting. What's your point?
Why did you leave the camp right when Skull ambushed me at the Volkhov site?
To clear my head, take a breather. But it's my understanding you assumed I sold you out?
sid_phrase_supack_SQ102_QC_AfterPlanInte: Don't you worry, if anything happens, we've got your backs!
Get some sleep, you'll need your strength. We'll take care of security and fortifications.
sid_phrase_supack_SQ102_QC_BanByeDurinDe: I know you can pull it off.
sid_phrase_supack_SQ102_QC_CherepAnger1_: Weighed your options and decided to quit? Smart.
Run. Raise a glass to your fallen friends. And you'd better never come back here.
sid_phrase_supack_SQ102_QC_CherepAnger2_: Skif..? No?! Not you? Decided to hide? I will… find you, bastard!
If I don't, your friends will tell me everything. I got all the time in the world… to have a chat with them.
sid_phrase_supack_SQ102_QC_CherepCallSki: Skif, where are you? Come to Banzai's! We got quite the crowd here.
sid_phrase_supack_SQ102_QC_MedByeDuringD: Good luck. To you, and to us.
sid_phrase_supack_SQ102_QC_MedHelloDurin: I'm busy deciphering! We don't have much time.
sid_phrase_supack_SQ102_QC_MedulinAboutG: Can anyone hear me? I can't work without electricity. Turn the generator on!
The generator! Someone turn it on! I'm almost done with the deciphering!
sid_phrase_supack_SQ102_QC_MedulinIntera: The worst possible timing… We're once again catastrophically unprepared!
sid_phrase_supack_SQ102_QC_MedulinLastIn: I know the moral aspect of Banzai's actions is questionable, but we're alive, and now he owes us.
sid_phrase_supack_SQ102_QC_ShelmaFinalCo: He's dead… Fuck me. And you guys are all alive. I did it…
sid_phrase_supack_SQ102_QC_ShelmaHiDurin: Your gun's almost ready. I'll get it!
sid_phrase_supack_SQ102_QC_ShelmaInterac: I heard you and Banzai. I don't know how you managed not to blow his brains out!
sid_phrase_supack_SQ102_QC_VozatijAfterE: Fuck, it's Skull! Run to Medulin!
sid_phrase_supack_SQ102_QC_VozatijIntera: Leave me here! Run to the medic!
sid_phrase_supack_SQ102_QC_VozatijLastIn: So this is it – my new home. I earned it honestly, with my blood. Who would've thought…
sid_phrase_supack_SQ102_QC_VozatijToShel: Hey, soldier, don't forget to breathe. You did everything you needed to.
sid_phrase_supack_SQ102_QC_VozatyjCallSk: We fought them off, and I'm gathering our men. Come on over. We'll discuss the latest news.
sid_phrase_supack_SQ102_QC_VozatyjDontLe: Skif? Damn it, where are you off to?!
sid_phrase_supack_SQ102_QC_VozatyjIntera: We'll discuss it in front of everyone. No more secrets.
sid_phrase_supack_SQ102_QC_VozatyjWakeUp: Rise and shine. It's on. Only bandits so far, no mercs in sight.

sid_phrase_supack_SQ102_QD_AboutPlan_abr: Since you're my VIP client today, would you prefer the local option or the foreign one?
sid_phrase_supack_SQ102_QD_AboutPlan_cha: Changed your mind? You'll help us defend the camp?
sid_phrase_supack_SQ102_QD_AboutPlan_dea: Roger. I'll cover from the rooftop. I'm a better shot than I am a runner. If everything goes to hell, climb up to the roof.
Grab a gun and go cover Medulin. And get some decent guns for us. Skif, what type of weapon do you need?
sid_phrase_supack_SQ102_QD_AboutPlan_dec: Let's deal with the northeast first.
Let's start reinforcing from the south.
sid_phrase_supack_SQ102_QD_AboutPlan_eas: And this is my gift to you. If they storm us at night, an NVG is way better than a flashlight.
Fuck. I leave the camp for a single day, and what've they done?
We'll deal with the fortifications, you get some rest. You won't sleep through an assault op, anyway.
Don't panic! I'll get you your guns. I'll sort it all out…
sid_phrase_supack_SQ102_QD_AboutPlan_ide: I'm ready to take my pick.
sid_phrase_supack_SQ102_QD_AboutPlan_lon: Get me a long-range weapon. The more of them I can take out from far away, the better.
sid_phrase_supack_SQ102_QD_AboutPlan_mid: I need a medium-range gun. And the more ammo, the better.
sid_phrase_supack_SQ102_QD_AboutPlan_no_: You’re on your own this time.
How come? You saved our asses just to leave us to die now?!
Rascal, he isn't on the list. Forgotten already? Skif, thanks for everything. I do hope you change your mind.
sid_phrase_supack_SQ102_QD_AboutPlan_nor: The northeast. What do you think of the fortifications there?
If I were Skull, I'd have the main squad strike from there. They'd find cover by the building and the stage. Easy to sneak up on us.
Why should we focus on the northwest part?
It's our weak spot. In the south and northeast, the landscape is to our advantage. In the northwest, we have the weakest fortifications.
sid_phrase_supack_SQ102_QD_AboutPlan_sho: Something short range and heavy duty.
sid_phrase_supack_SQ102_QD_AboutPlan_sou: What if we prioritize the south part?
I doubt the main force will come from there, but it's a good place to set up trip wires. We'll cover Banzai and Medulin.
sid_phrase_supack_SQ102_QD_AboutPlan_sta: Skull now knows where we are. The first squad is a recce, the main squad will be here soon. Banzai hasn't pulled any more stunts?
I paid him a visit. He won't pull anything, or it's on him. Do you need me? If not, I'll get back to work.
Go. You're in charge of the interference signal, we're on the defenses. Skif, you're with us, right?
sid_phrase_supack_SQ102_QD_AboutPlan_yes: Reinforce the perimeter, organize fortifications. We don't have much time, so we'll have to prioritize.
They won't strike from the West as the field is a kill zone. I bet they'll come from the northwest or northeast. They could sneak in along the buildings.
Or the south, from the ruins or near Banzai. Knowing Skull, he's coming in hot. So pick one to make it easier.
sid_phrase_supack_SQ102_QD_AfterRadioWav: Did it work?! Somebody answer! Come in!
Damn it. A camp full of zombies…
Skif, help me finish off these poor bastards. We need to find out if Skull's here anywhere.
sid_phrase_supack_SQ102_QD_FinalWithCher: I see. So, you wanna drag everyone down with you…
Look at that. He finally grew a pair.
You know what, Rascal? Aren't you "just a merchant" who didn't know anything? If you get outta here right now, you'll live.
Thought I wouldn't shoot ya?! Thought I'd run?!
No Surgeon? Or did he leave without saying goodbye, like Rascal?
Here's the deal: you're going to give me the... "interfering signal". Did I get that right, doctor?
Or you take turns listening to Zombie FM until one of you changes your mind.
I got it under control. Run.
If you won't listen to me, listen to Skif.
Just think: is it really worth it? Your employers won't appreciate the sacrifice!
I got nothing to lose. Either we all die, or I make it out of this hellhole.
I won't wait long. Run while you still can.
Rascal! Chill out! He's dead, do you hear me?!
You let me leave back at Volkhov. Now it's my turn: put the gun down and leave.
We're even, right? Go fuck yourself.
sid_phrase_supack_SQ102_QD_FinalWithVozM: None of my business. You deal with him.

If I ever write a memoir, it'll be impossible to read. Mayhem, farce, tragedy – in only thirty minutes!

What do we do with him? Let him stay at the camp after all? What do you think, Skif?
I can't believe Banzai's crazy idea worked. I was right on time. And we pulled through…

Humankind turns everything into a weapon… Thank you, Skif. Here, what's ours is yours.

Skif? Scoutmaster? You good? I think I got it all…

To each their own. Listen up: you know how they pump the water out of the mine so it doesn't flood? Same shit here.
Find a pump and turn it off. They'll come running and open the door, follow them. And then comes the best part!
What a surprise! Here it goes: you know how they pump the water out of the mine so it doesn't flood? Same shit here.
We studied this strange radio signal. If the bandits listen to it, then… I don't know what's gonna happen, but nothing good.
And if they learn to use it, it's game over. So I'm sorry, but I can't leave without them.
I never counted, but a lot. They're all sitting down there. I hope they didn't hear all the noise you've made.

Wait. I'll deal with the bandits and get you out.
Keep it simple.
Better to think on it first than waste bullets.

So? Will you help me get out of here?
And my stuff! That's most important – the drive and the tools.

What was your plan again?
That's everything I found on Pickaxe. There were no tools.

Kill the bandits, get the drive. Now I'm supposed to fetch your tools?

You said something about tools. Still need them?

Sorry, I got carried away. Here, for your troubles. After I try the tools out, feel free to stop by for repairs!

sid_phrase_supack_SQ96_QD_C02_Start_Befo: There's this tech who crafts custom tool sets. A massive asshole who always underpaid me for soldering. But he forgot I was the one who set up the trackers for his stashes.

No time for this now. Maybe later.

Scoutmaster didn't chain me up here, if that's what you thought. I locked myself in. I hate being distracted.
sid_phrase_supack_SQ97_QD_BanzaiRoom_Nee: Do you need anything else so you can work?
Just time, and no distractions. Maybe you can give Scoutmaster a hand while I'm stuck here? He doesn't show it, but I can see he got pretty messed up.
sid_phrase_supack_SQ97_QD_BanzaiRoom_Too: Atta boy! Thanks a lot. I don't know what scared me more: that Skull was gonna get them, or that I'm totally useless without them.

sid_phrase_supack_SQ97_QD_BanzaiRoom_Wha: What is it you're working so much on?

That's okay. I'll figure it out and get all the answers – about the frequency, the bounty, all of it.
sid_phrase_supack_SQ97_QD_NewBanzaiRoom_: And, after you help Scoutmaster, stop by. I'll see what I can do for you.
Dang it, Scoutmaster, I asked not to be disturbed!
Ah, Skif? Well… I understand. You're smart. And curious.
Sorry. Thought you were my friend… I mean, my other friend. And I hate to be disturbed.
Scoutmaster's got your back, no matter what. He's truly a good friend.
Yeah. Good man. Used to guide our NK-5. If he hadn't been wounded, he would've gotten all of us out.
sid_phrase_supack_SQ97_QD_NewNK3Group_Ne: You said group NK-3 was here before you. I found their bodies and their notes. Looks like the transmission made them go crazy.

Good thing you found them. Give me a minute, then Banzai and I will take care of them. We were colleagues, after all.
sid_phrase_supack_SQ97_QD_NewQuestGive_N: Leave them with me, I'll pass them along. Banzai is at his place. A rare luxury in the Zone – his own place. But it's too dangerous for him outside.
I'm wounded, and we're in the middle of nowhere. Had to kill the generator so the noise didn't attract mutants, and been hoping for a miracle since. Looks like you're the miracle.
And don't rush it – this place has a way of cooling down hotheads.
And don't storm into Banzai's place, or it'll take him a whole day to concentrate again. The sooner he figures that frequency out, the better our chances are.
Turn on the generator in the main hall, but be careful – critters from outside could be attracted by the sound.
The generator's up, lights are on. Cheers! It's got a lot cozier.
I'm the guide for NK-5, his group. His bodyguard after they put a bounty on our heads – and possibly the last person who cares if Banzai is still alive.
Mind your own business.
It is my business, unfortunately. And yours too, since you came here. Are you sure you've not been added as the fifth person on the target list?
First, Banzai doesn't want to run without his equipment, and now you're willing to risk both your lives. What's the deal with this transmission?
Not a clue. But at least we have the right to know why it's open season on us, and I suppose the previous group, too.
I don’t have time for this right now.
I understand, but we don't have much time, either.
It's the reason they put a bounty on your heads. You need to run. Now's not the time for science.
If you run from a bloke like Skull, you'll die tired.
Maybe we can trade the results of this research for our lives? Maybe we can use the transmission to our advantage? Our best chance is to keep working.
Why are you and Banzai here? What is this place, anyway?
Research groups used to operate here, on Malachite's orders. Studied this radio transmission. We're NK-5. NK-3 was here before us.
I couldn't find out what happened to them, or the others. Maybe the answer's somewhere at the camp.
Do you need help? How can we defend the camp?
You're injured, Banzai's no good, and it isn't safe here. I wouldn't have picked it as a shelter.
Didn't have much choice, being chased down. Plus, this place has the equipment we need to figure out the transmission.
sid_phrase_supack_SQ97_QD_ReturnBanzaiRo: Keep it short and sweet. What's up?
sid_phrase_supack_SQ97_QD_ScoutmasterGro: The offer to work with us still stands. What do you think?
The offer to work with us still stands. What do you think?
No. Count me out.
Suit yourself. It's a pity, though. I hope you change your mind.
I still have some questions.
Ask away.
Any clue where to find them?
Banzai overheard Pickaxe looking for the "lil one" by the Army Warehouses. If anyone is "lil", that's Rascal.
As for Medulin, I only know he's got an emergency hideout in the Malachite depot. If he's alive, he's likely to try and make it there.
You said you needed me. But what do I get out of it?
If you didn't ask, I would've seriously thought that we recruited the wrong man.
I'll pay for the work. Plus, Banzai has been getting side gigs for gear repairs – he'll be happy to help. As a guide, I'll be at your service, too.
Fine, count me in. Now, give me the details – what's happening here?
We were a team of researchers. Four of us, as you know. I'm Scoutmaster, Banzai's the tech, Medulin is a scientist, and Rascal, our quartermaster.
Our mission was to figure out the nature of a strange radio signal. As it turned out, one that makes stalkers lose their minds.
When we got ambushed by Pickaxe, Rascal and Medulin escaped. I never found their bodies, so they might be alive. We need to find them and get the group back together.
I already know everything I need to.
Fine. I'll let you know as soon as we find out more. I sure hope I can count on you doing the same.
What is that research group?
The order came from Malachite, and Medulin was in charge. The group identifier was NK-5. You saw the corpses of NK-3 already, and I don't know what happened to the others.
That Skull guy hunting you down. Know more about him?
I know his kind. He's got enough resources, and he won't back down.
What do you know about that transmission anyway?
Not much. Random stalkers intercepted it a couple of times. The ones who only heard parts of it stayed alive to tell the story.
The ones who listened to the whole thing lost it. Shot themselves or turned into zombies. It may be that Pickaxe's ambush saved us from that fate.
sid_phrase_supack_SQ97_QD_ScoutmasterQue: I did what you asked.
Scoutmaster, how did you get that generator to rise from the dead?
Wasn't me. Skif started it. He cleaned the camp up, too.
Tell me.
Working on it.
No rush. Knowing Banzai, we'll be here for a while.
Scoutmaster, I mean it. We need a guy like him here! He kicked the bandits' asses and sorted the camp out, so maybe he'd be able to take care of our bounty?
All in good time. Pay up first, then negotiate a new contract.
He's right, you know. I propose you work with us. If you agree, we'll tell you all about the transmission and the research. Think about it.
What do I need to do again?
Generator, building sweep, summer stage, got it. Anything else?
That's it for now.
Building sweep, summer stage, got it. Anything else?
You started the generator, didn't you? Cheers. It's a lot cozier.
Building sweep, got it. Anything else?
Apart from that, Banzai and I heard voices on the summer stage, and now they've gone quiet. Thanks to you, I guess. Cheers for that.
Banzai is at his place. A rare luxury in the Zone – his own place. He asked not to be disturbed, so you and I will have to wait.
While we're at it, maybe you can help us settle in? We don't have lots of money, but I'll pay a fair price for your help.
And don't rush it – this place has a way of cooling down hotheads.
And don't barge into Banzai's place. He doesn't like to be distracted, and I don't like him whining about it.
The offer still stands. Anyway, I won't refuse a conversation with a smart man like yourself.
Turn on the generator – it's locked up in the hall. You can try getting there from the roof. I saw a vent there.
Take a look around the summer stage. I can't tell you what's out there. Banzai and I heard voices, but we can't say for sure with that damn radio.
Take a look around the summer stage. I can't tell you what's out there. Banzai and I heard voices, but we can't say for sure with that damn radio.
Greetings. I'd shake your hand, but mine isn't moving that well yet.
Bandit ambushes are bad for your health. You're the one who saved our radio genius, right?
Mind your own business.
This is my business. And yours, if you came out here. I want to know who I'm in the same boat with.
Yes, I got him his tools.
That's great. Banzai is as good without them as we are with no guns – useless, and he knows it. It makes me uneasy.
Maybe. Depends on who you are and why you're asking.
I'm his group's guide. His bodyguard after they put a bounty on our heads. And, possibly, the last person who cares if Banzai is still alive.
I don’t have time for this right now.
I understand. But I'll be glad if you change your mind.
Settle down where? What is this place, anyway?
Base camp for groups who studied this strange radio transmission before us. We're the last one.
I don't know what happened to the previous group, but they never came back.
What kind of help do you need?
Take a look at the building on the left. The last group tried to stand their ground there, and we inherited their trip wires. Maybe something else, too.
Here's the key. And do be careful.
You're injured, Banzai's no good, and it isn't safe here. I wouldn't have picked it as a shelter.
Well, I like it. A sturdy roof, strong walls, and given its reputation, no one will come looking for us here.
sid_phrase_supack_SQ98_QC_AllOver_Safe_8: Is it all over? There's no more danger outside?
sid_phrase_supack_SQ98_QC_ToScary_NotMov: I won't take another step until I know what's outside!
sid_phrase_supack_SQ98_QD_AfterFight_ARG: The research protocols? The data packages you sent from Malachite?
Yes. My little scheduled message program for emergencies. As you can see, it's all been one huge emergency.
sid_phrase_supack_SQ98_QD_AfterFight_Abo: I came across this radio transmission that literally makes you lose your mind. Sound waves that impact your mental state!
sid_phrase_supack_SQ98_QD_AfterFight_Acc: I'll wait for you. We'll go together.
Great! Nothing can scare the three of us. Let's continue this talk at Kazkovyi?
sid_phrase_supack_SQ98_QD_AfterFight_GoB: I just went out with the group to personally conduct an experiment when we were attacked. Wait a minute…
Medulin, come in. Banzai intercepted your transmission. Stay there, I'm on my way.
Scoutmaster, this is Medulin! Your friend found me and helped. Wait… Was it so easy to intercept?
It was for Banzai, but he's a genius. Skif, if you're there – thank you. You coming with us or not?
sid_phrase_supack_SQ98_QD_AfterFight_NoO: Don't be so dramatic. No one's going to die.
Easy for you to say! You haven't been holed up here like a rat for hell knows how long.
sid_phrase_supack_SQ98_QD_AfterFight_Ref: I have things to do. I'll find my own way.
It's a shame. It would be safer to travel together. Well, see you at Kazkovyi!
sid_phrase_supack_SQ98_QD_AfterFight_Ser: Mercenaries were sent to hunt down your group. You crossed someone powerful.
It seems so, but I fail to understand how. We were simply studying radio waves!
sid_phrase_supack_SQ98_QD_AfterFight_Too: So many shots fired… They're late anyway. I sent the research protocols a long time ago.
Even if they silence me by force, my work won't die with me.
sid_phrase_supack_SQ98_QD_AfterFight_Wha: What could make these studies so important?
sid_phrase_supack_SQ98_QD_FindMedic_DidH: He's alive? Thank god… Just him, or the others, too?
sid_phrase_supack_SQ98_QD_FindMedic_DoNo: Stop right there! I'm armed, and I'll shoot!
sid_phrase_supack_SQ98_QD_FindMedic_Enou: Three kits for one? And that's not enough?
It sure isn't! Since when can fieldwork boast one problem at a time? Bandits – one. Anomalies – two. Radiation – three. And these are just examples!
sid_phrase_supack_SQ98_QD_FindMedic_Find: The others are alive and in a safe place.
That's great news. You've put my mind at ease! I wouldn't forgive myself.
If I find a new suit for you, will you manage to get there?
I think so. Well, almost sure. If it's a high-quality suit.
sid_phrase_supack_SQ98_QD_FindMedic_Give: Now you got four. Feeling any better?
Much better. Here. Take this, please. For being so understanding.
sid_phrase_supack_SQ98_QD_FindMedic_Good: I have one. You could go to Prypiat and back wearing it.
They'd make me sign three logs to get one of these in Malachite! Never thought I'd get to try one on. Thanks!
sid_phrase_supack_SQ98_QD_FindMedic_GotY: Gotcha! Another step, and you're dead!
sid_phrase_supack_SQ98_QD_FindMedic_Hard: Put the gun down. You Medulin?
sid_phrase_supack_SQ98_QD_FindMedic_Look: Scoutmaster. Asked me to find you.
sid_phrase_supack_SQ98_QD_FindMedic_Next: I'm in no position to demand, but please think about it again!
sid_phrase_supack_SQ98_QD_FindMedic_Norm: I have a spare suit. A nice and sturdy one, too.
Decent, indeed. It gives me hope to get to the camp unscathed. Thanks.
sid_phrase_supack_SQ98_QD_FindMedic_NotF: Scoutmaster and Banzai are alive and safe. Don't know about Rascal yet.
Poor Rascal. He wasn't even aware of the expedition's goal! Try to find him. I'd never forgive myself if he's dead.
sid_phrase_supack_SQ98_QD_FindMedic_Poor: I got something here. Not much protection, though.
Yeah… That'll be my burial shroud, but it's still better than these rags. Thank you.
sid_phrase_supack_SQ98_QD_FindMedic_Show: The one saving your group's asses. You've got targets on your backs.
Saving our asses? Please… tell me I'm not the only member of the group who's still alive.
sid_phrase_supack_SQ98_QD_FindMedic_Stas: I don't want to be in your debt. So, in return, here are the coordinates to a stash with a gift.
sid_phrase_supack_SQ98_QD_FindMedic_They: Shhh! Hear that? They've found us! They were probably tailing you. If someone's out there, I'm not coming out!
sid_phrase_supack_SQ98_QD_FindMedic_TryF: I'll try to figure something out. Wait here.
I will. Got no other options.
But hurry up, I'm begging you! If it was so easy for you to find me, others can too.
sid_phrase_supack_SQ98_QD_FindMedic_WeNe: Questions later. You need to get to your group.
I would, but how? Some goons are after me, and they've even asked around Malachite about me already. This isn't safe!
My protective suit is torn into pieces, and I've only got three medkits. I'm catastrophically unprepared!
sid_phrase_supack_SQ98_QD_FindMedic_YouB: You're back! Help me, please! Without a protective suit, there's no way I can get out of here!
sid_phrase_supack_SQ98_QD_FindSuit_DoYou: Did you find a suit? Did anyone follow you?
sid_phrase_supack_SQ98_QD_FindSuit_GoodS: I did. Be careful with it. I won't be able to get you another one.
They'd make me sign three logs to get one of these in Malachite! Never thought I'd get to try one on. Thanks!
sid_phrase_supack_SQ98_QD_FindSuit_Leave: I will. Got no other options.
sid_phrase_supack_SQ98_QD_FindSuit_Norma: Decent, indeed. It gives me hope to get to the camp unscathed. Thanks.
sid_phrase_supack_SQ98_QD_FindSuit_PoorS: It's not the best quality, but yes.
Yeah… That'll be my burial shroud, but it's still better than these rags. Thank you.
sid_phrase_supack_SQ98_QD_FindSuit_Stash: I don't want to be in your debt. So, in return, here are the coordinates to a stash with a gift.
sid_phrase_supack_SQ98_QD_FindSuit_TheyC: Shhh! Hear that? They've found us! They were probably tailing you. If someone's out there, I'm not coming out!
sid_phrase_supack_SQ99_QC_BanditsRandom_: Are you gonna answer or not, you piece of… Fuck! Hello?! Hello, boss?! Just static…
Boss? Come in! We've been calling for an hour.
sid_phrase_supack_SQ99_QC_ShelmaBusy_Bus: I get it. Not need to escort me.
sid_phrase_supack_SQ99_QC_ShelmaDontLeav: Hey! Don't go! I haven't paid you for my rescue!
sid_phrase_supack_SQ99_QD_ReturnToVozhat: The group's back together. Thanks to you. I had no doubt you'd pull it off.
While you were gone, I found something valuable, left by our predecessors. Part of the sale profit is yours.
Speaking of valuables – Banzai found something to show you. Now it's your turn to listen to him.
sid_phrase_supack_SQ99_QD_ShelmaAftermat: Mercs didn't send me, Scoutmaster did. Your group is waiting for you in the Burnt Forest.
They're alive? That's great news! I saw Scoutmaster fighting like a lion, covering the others as they fell back. Not bad for an old guy like him.
Give them my best regards, and tell them that I'll meet them in Rostok. I'll make sure they get the best personal discounts. We've been through some tough times together.
But... Considering the alternatives, if I stay — I'm fucked, even if I go to Rostok — I'm fucked. Burnt Forest, though? Maybe not.
Whatever… I'll go there. Don't forget about the stash.
Oh. Oh, fuck! Hold on, let's discuss this like two honest businessmen! I'm not the one you need, I'm telling you!
You know what? It's your lucky day! I have an excellent stash nearby. I give you the coordinates, you pretend you never saw me. Deal?
And this stash has enough to cover your ransom?
Maybe… It depends on the exchange rate. This was force-majeure! My last chance. What was I supposed to tell them?!
I get it, the situation is unpleasant. Take some more coupons, maybe? For the collateral?
All of you are in danger. Your group needs you, and you need them.
We're both grown-ups. In the Zone, it's every man for himself. I'm sorry they have to go through this, but I was nearly fucking killed. Twice!
They can do whatever, but I'm off to Rostok. They've got good security, and no freaks get further than the Arena.
If you're separated, you'll get killed. Together, you have a chance.
Chances are we'll all get killed together. One hell of a lose-lose situation.
Scoutmaster and Banzai are trying to get rid of the threat. For good.
The ones who created the problem are heroically solving it. Idealists…
Tough times have just begun. There's a bounty on your heads.
How come? What for? I didn't sign up for this! Medulin's contract only had two pages, no fine print, and definitely no mercenaries!
What do I do now? I'm no Sidorovich – I don't have a bunker! What are the others saying, if they sent you to get me?
That this is because of the radio transmission they studied.
What've I got to do with it? I was in charge of supplies. It was none of my business what transmissions they listened to.
What am I gonna show the mercs? Ration papers? They'd be better off looking for Medulin and Banzai. They say anything else?
What happened to you?
I'm still trying to figure that out! I signed a contract with this scientist – grub, spares, expedition supplies – then we got jumped! Chased! I'm a businessman, not a marathon runner.
They trapped me here and wouldn't take my money! Get it? Bandits, refusing money?! That's when I knew I was screwed.
They said there was this merc interested in me, and I'd never done any business with them before. I am quite a notable figure, of course, but not like that!
sid_phrase_supack_SQ99_QD_ShelmaBanditsR: Once I get paid, I'll bolt to the mainland. Buy myself a tire shop. And get married, fo sho!
Smart investment! But I'll give you more to let me go – enough to buy a whole car shop!
Mercs and Pickaxe chewed it over, and where's he at? Only payment we'll get is in lead.
Shut up, damn it! Quit whining. You, call Pickaxe. And you, smartass, open your mouth again and I'll close it for good.
sid_phrase_supack_SQ99_QD_ShelmaBandits_: Cool your jets, eh. If Pickaxe ain't answering, he's busy. Let's wait a bit, finish the chump off, then bounce.
Something's not right. Pickaxe sleeps and shits with that radio. No way he's not picking up.
Gents, gents. I'm no mercenary, of course, but I am a businessman. How much did he say I was worth?
My dead body is worth less, I guarantee. Dragging a prisoner with you? Think about security, the logistics… How about the same amount, here and now?
Oh yeah? Go ahead. Bargain for it. Surprise me.
What the hell?! You think you can screw over Pickaxe and the merc he's working for?!
Here's the money. On the PDA. Real money, right now!
Shut your trap. And you, call Pickaxe again.
Pickaxe. Come in. Come in, damn it…
sid_phrase_supack_SQ99_QD_TradeDialog_Ca: I'm here for Rascal. But I'll take care of you first.
Are you fucking superman?! Alone against us? Get him, boys!
Mercs sent me. To collect the package.
A-fucking-mazing! Got the dough, like we discussed?
sid_phrase_supack_SQ99_QD_TradeDialog_Co: Pickaxe – a merc. Chump thinks he's funny! Get him!
Maybe you need to cool your jets, huh?
All right. Who'd Skull shoot the shit with?
I told you he's a fraud! Fucking told you! Get him!
A-okay. We got the dough, go get your chump.
Looks about right. He's all yours.
Nah, nah, nah! No one told us about no couriers! Smells like bullshit… Lemme ask you some questions.
Who's the merc that sent you?
sid_phrase_supack_SQ99_QD_TradeDialog_Hi: The job's good. Pays well, too.
Why's he worth so much?! Smartass knows sumthin' important, like where the Monlith's buried?
For this much, I don't care what he knows. Gimme the coupons – he's your problem now. Let's get outta here.
sid_phrase_supack_SQ99_QD_TradeDialog_Ho: Look what the cat dragged in. Be on your freaking way, we're busy here.
sid_phrase_supack_SQ99_QD_TradeDialog_Im: Ask Pickaxe, and how he royally fucked up and lost twenty per cent.
Ask Pickaxe. He asked the customer stupid questions, too.
So you're… one of them? Those… gentlemen? And Pickaxe ain't picking up…
If Pickaxe fucked up, ain't got nuthin' to do with us. We were told to catch this one – here he is. Take 'im. We don't need no trouble.
sid_phrase_supack_SQ99_QD_TradeDialog_Ju: I'm just passing by. I don't want trouble.
That's me! Didn't see me, eh? You're right on time there, buddy. Got the ransom? I'll pay you back, with interest!
sid_phrase_supack_SQ99_QD_TradeDialog_Lo: I'll throw in an extra bullet for you.
Now that's better! You better not sit on other folks' money. It might end badly. Get your chump.
I'd take what I'm offered if I were you.
You're shitting me. This is bullshit! You wanna screw us over?!
sid_phrase_supack_SQ99_QD_TradeDialog_Pa: What ransom? I've never seen you before.
Whatcha bitching about? Finish him off now, think about it later.
I got it, yes. Take the coupons and let the man go.
Fuck a duck… I've only seen this much money in the movies!
Dump the chump and let's go. Real fucking quick.
sid_phrase_supack_SQ99_QD_VozhatyNotFoun: Hello. Found any of our missing persons yet?
Where should I look for them?
Banzai thinks Rascal might be at the Army Warehouses. Bandits were chasing him, so keep your eyes peeled.
As for Medulin, I only know he had a hideout in the Malachite depot. Check both places. I hope they're alive.
Hurry up. I don't think those two have much time if they've split up.
sid_questItemprototypes_ANCQ01_VadaHvoro: A PDA from the body of the rookie Vadya Kindling, slain by mutants. The guy had a hunch that the Monitoring Station was attracting dogs for a reason. He had a keen sense for loot, but the mutants proved to have an even keener sense for him.
sid_questItemprototypes_ANCQ04_LokotPDA_: Fueled by an overpowering desire to regain his authority, Elbow lost his marbles and decided to face the challenge alone, without any support. It could only have ended badly.
sid_questItemprototypes_ANCQ06_VadyaPDA_: A stalker’s PDA with an interrupted audio log. Startled by an unexpected encounter, the rookie inadvertently dropped his primary means of communication.
sid_questItemprototypes_ANCQ07_RabinaPDA: Apparently Rowan had a meeting with a dealer, but things didn’t go the way he planned.
sid_questItemprototypes_ANCQ08_NoteToHom: A note found in the Fisherman’s House. Upbeat and jovial as ever, Nightingale shows genuine concern for his friend – a sight as uncommon in the Zone as fish in the local streams.
sid_questItemprototypes_ANCQ10_NoteMedic: Notes written by a medic, found at the Levitating Silo in the Lesser Zone. They express his hopes for expanding the Zone’s perimeter – for the good of mankind, of course.
sid_questItemprototypes_ANCQ12_Note_desc: A report from the Perimeter decontamination station condemning Cpt. Sukhin. No one could have expected it to end up in the hands of the very person it criticized rather than Garmata.
sid_questItemprototypes_ANCQ13_PDAVladAs: A PDA belonging to Vlad Vivid, found by the soldier statue in the Dire Thicket. He must have been so terrified, he dropped his PDA without realizing it.
sid_questItemprototypes_ANCQ15_NoteRat_d: A PDA taken from the body of a bandit punished by his own gang, found near the Railway Bridge in Garbage. Mitya seems to have set his buddies up, leading to a swift and brutal reprisal.
sid_questItemprototypes_ANCQ16_PDALetyn_: The owner of this PDA must have been either really gutsy or really naive, dissing Roosevelt like that. When you’re working for a big shot, you gotta keep quiet or be real careful who you’re talking to.
sid_questItemprototypes_ANCQ17_AboutVara: A lost PDA from a Garbage bandit with a warning about Varan’s gang. One of the rules this kingpin lives by is, “if your own people are scared of you, everyone else will be terrified.”
sid_questItemprototypes_ANCQ20_Lost_PDA_: Yet another stalker who wanted to go back to the Mainland, but couldn’t. It’s difficult to find a way out when there’s nowhere to go. For folks like him, the Zone is a one-way ticket.
sid_questItemprototypes_ANCQ21_MemoryDia: The note contains memories of what this place used to be like before the Disaster. The paper is visibly worn – it seems to have been carried by the wind over the bridge for quite some time.
sid_questItemprototypes_ANCQ22_ClearSkyN: An old sheet of paper ripped from a diary containing a scientist’s notes from his expedition with Scar. “September 2011” is faintly discernible on the surviving portion of the page.
sid_questItemprototypes_ANCQ23_AtasPDA_d: A PDA found near the Hydroelectric Station. The conversation inside discusses a stash prepared for some bandits by a Warden. Looks like even the group known for imposing its law on the rest of the Zone has its own issues.
sid_questItemprototypes_ANCQ24_VenyaNote: A crumpled note discovered in a Prypiat apartment. The author wrote down his last words, and yet his handwriting is still surprisingly steady and neat.
sid_questItemprototypes_ANCQ_19_BanditDi: An old diary of a thug employed by the Shah, discovered in the Brood’s barracks. The worn cover implies that the owner was dealing with considerable anxiety.
sid_questItemprototypes_ANC_18_IOUNote_d: Sounds like the author of this note screwed up big time in front of his own buddies. Luckily, his partners agreed to wait for the debt to be repaid. In the Zone, issues like this are usually settled on the spot.
sid_questItemprototypes_AzimutControlRoo: A key card granting access to the control room of Azimuth Station. Found in the pocket of the late Ward sergeant Nikitin.
sid_questItemprototypes_BasePDA_Bandits_: A pocket personal computer, version 2.0. In the Zone, it functions as a navigation tool, notebook, communication device, and voice recorder. This one was owned by a bandit.
sid_questItemprototypes_BasePDA_Duty_des: A pocket personal computer, version 2.0. In the Zone, it functions as a navigation tool, notebook, communication device, and voice recorder. This one was owned by a Duty member.
sid_questItemprototypes_BasePDA_Freedom_: A pocket personal computer, version 2.0. In the Zone, it functions as a navigation tool, notebook, communication device, and voice recorder. This one was owned by a Freedom member.
sid_questItemprototypes_BasePDA_Mercs_de: A pocket personal computer, version 2.0. In the Zone, it functions as a navigation tool, notebook, communication device, and voice recorder. This one was used by a mercenary.
sid_questItemprototypes_BasePDA_Military: A pocket personal computer, version 2.0. In the Zone, it functions as a navigation tool, notebook, communication device, and voice recorder. This one was owned by an IPSF soldier.
sid_questItemprototypes_BasePDA_Monolith: A PDA formerly used by Monolith soldiers, now broken beyond repair.

A pocket personal computer, version 2.0. In the Zone, it functions as a navigation tool, notebook, communication device, and voice recorder. This particular device was modified to run on the Monolith network.
sid_questItemprototypes_BasePDA_Neutrals: A pocket personal computer, version 2.0. In the Zone, it functions as a navigation tool, notebook, communication device, and voice recorder. This one was owned by a lone stalker.
sid_questItemprototypes_BasePDA_Spark_de: A pocket personal computer, version 2.0. In the Zone, it functions as a navigation tool, notebook, communication device, and voice recorder. This one was owned by a Sparker.
sid_questItemprototypes_BasePDA_Ward_des: A pocket personal computer, version 2.0. In the Zone, it functions as a navigation tool, notebook, communication device, and voice recorder. This one was owned by a Warden.
sid_questItemprototypes_BasePDA_descript: A pocket personal computer, version 2.0. In the Zone, it functions as a navigation tool, notebook, communication device, and voice recorder.
sid_questItemprototypes_BordulinPDA_desc: A PDA belonging to Borodulin, a man who presents himself as a Duty colonel. If he ever had three stars, though, they probably came from a brandy label, not a military rank.
sid_questItemprototypes_DLC00_Deluxe_Fla: Flash drive with the Journalist's last message.
sid_questItemprototypes_DLC00_Journalist: It looks like a regular PDA. But this one keeps the secret of the man who found what he was looking for in the Zone, and left on his own terms.
sid_questItemprototypes_DLC00_PreOrder_F: Another secret message from the Journalist.
A flash drive with a new message from the Journalist.
A flash drive with a message from a mysterious stalker called the Journalist.
sid_questItemprototypes_DevatyjPDA_descr: The PDA of a former Monolithian who resided in the Lesser Zone. It’s difficult to picture this calm, composed man among the ranks of Monolith.
sid_questItemprototypes_DocPDA_SQ103_des: PDA of a scientist who worked in X18. An old "R&D" prototype with an inventory number on the side.
sid_questItemprototypes_DogmasterDairy_d: A small worn-out leather notebook. Inside it are notes about trying to tame a blind dog.
sid_questItemprototypes_DogmasterPDA_des: PDA of a hunter who died trying to tame a blind dog.
sid_questItemprototypes_DvupalovVodka_de: Zone-made vodka, distilled by a skilled chemist. It smells like strong moonshine.
sid_questItemprototypes_E02_MQ02_BS_PDA_: A PDA owned by Mug, the leader of the gang holed up at the warehouse with the radiation hazard signs. It sports a skull keychain.
sid_questItemprototypes_E02_MQ03_RebraKe: A little old key, clearly meant for a door.
sid_questItemprototypes_E02_MQ03_RebraPD: Despite showing signs of damage, the device is still operational.
sid_questItemprototypes_E02_MQ03_RebraSp: This key card is slightly worn from frequent use.
Key Card to the Sphere’s Cable Tunnels
sid_questItemprototypes_E02_MQ03_RihterN: A key that can be used to leave the Lesser Zone.
sid_questItemprototypes_E02_MQ03_VartaPD: A device Squint pilfered from a group of dead Wardens.
sid_questItemprototypes_E02_MQ03_VartaSe: These devices are designed to measure and record environmental readings, anomalous energy fluctuations, and the wearer’s health data.
sid_questItemprototypes_E02_SQ01_HmuriyP: This PDA belonged to a stalker who was taken captive by bandits. The device’s condition indicates that it suffered nearly as much as its owner.
sid_questItemprototypes_E02_SQ01_RabinaP: Apparently, Rowan had a meeting with a dealer – but things clearly didn’t go the way he planned.
sid_questItemprototypes_E02_SQ01_ZorikPD: This used to belong to the stalker from the Post Office.
sid_questItemprototypes_E03_01_Chernozem: A shabby PDA taken from Chornozem’s shoulder bag.
sid_questItemprototypes_E03_02_OldTechDo: Blueprints detailing a certain “Product 81,” yellowed with age and ruined by mold in places.
sid_questItemprototypes_E03_05_NestorNot: A worn-out notebook densely filled with Nestor’s scribbles. The former kingpin of Garbage didn’t trust electronic media.
sid_questItemprototypes_E03_05_NestorPas: A weathered piece of old plastic, stained with blood.
sid_questItemprototypes_E03_MQ01_GoshaMo: This PDA belonged to a bandit who bolted from the Depot out of fear of the Ward.
sid_questItemprototypes_E03_MQ02_IskraPD: A PDA belonging to a Sparker from Serpentine’s group. The name on the keychain is illegible.
sid_questItemprototypes_E03_MQ02_Isolato: A dull metal key with the barely visible number “-1” scratched into its surface.
sid_questItemprototypes_E03_MQ02_Zmeevik: This weathered PDA bears a scar-like knife mark on its back panel.
sid_questItemprototypes_E03_MQ04_Key_des: These keys are commonly used to unlock the doors of prison cells.
sid_questItemprototypes_E03_MQ05_BanditP: This PDA has seen some rough times. The case is all chipped and scratched, and there’s an old piece of chewed gum stuck to one of the buttons.
sid_questItemprototypes_E04_MQ02_Basemen: This worn, rusty key comes with a new plastic keychain labeled “Basement.”
sid_questItemprototypes_E04_MQ02_PDAWith: A PDA belonging to Maj. Hrygorchenko, commander of the Ward detachment during a mission at Azimut Station. He died in the line of duty.
sid_questItemprototypes_E04_MQ02_Recorde: This is a typical PDA of a SIRCAA scientist – a brand-new model in perfect condition, never taken outside. Regrettably, it broke after slipping out of its owner’s hands.
sid_questItemprototypes_E04_NoteDubnyjTo: A carefully folded sheet of paper containing handwritten text. The letters appear almost etched into its surface.
sid_questItemprototypes_E04_Note_UnityNo: A scrap of paper revealing the meticulously detailed thoughts and feelings of an unknown Noontider.
sid_questItemprototypes_E04_SQ01_Lodochk: This container stores the cargo the Noontiders failed to bring back to their base. They might still be expecting it on Wild Island.
sid_questItemprototypes_E04_SQ01_PDAStal: A PDA belonging to a bandit who was undone by his own greed. He thought he’d earn some extra coupons for killing more Noontiders, but all he got was a bullet.
sid_questItemprototypes_E04_SQ02_Control: A key to the control room in Lab X5.
sid_questItemprototypes_E04_SQ02_KvaskaP: A PDA recovered from the body of the late Noontider Kvashka. Though it doesn’t explain the reasons behind his self-sacrifice, it does capture the final moments of his existence.
sid_questItemprototypes_E05_MQ01_EchoRep: A monitoring report from a team of SIRCAA scientists at Echo Station. Evidently, they were researching sources of psi-radiation.
sid_questItemprototypes_E05_MQ01_Shterev: The personal diary of Lt. Shterev, who has since been exposed as an agent and fervent follower of Faust. It suggests that he found true happiness in carrying out his mission.
This PDA was found in the Claw, on the body of a dead Alpha Team commander. It looks like the traitor Shterev was there, but managed to escape.
sid_questItemprototypes_E05_MQ01_VartaSe: These devices are designed to measure and record environmental readings, anomalous energy fluctuations, and the wearer’s health data.
sid_questItemprototypes_E05_MQ02_Kalanch: A hand-drawn map of the Swamps, showing the beacons left by Prof. Kalancha to protect the Clear Sky base. It’s clear that a lot of effort went into it.
sid_questItemprototypes_E05_MQ02_Zverobo: A PDA belonging to a wandering hunter. It contains information about his latest job and mentions an encounter with a partner who nimbly exited the scene.
sid_questItemprototypes_E05_MQ03_Kalanch: These are protocols of communication sessions from the time when C-Consciousness was still active. They were recorded using the setup in Kalancha’s laboratory, which continued to operate in autonomous mode.
sid_questItemprototypes_E05_MQ03_Lebedev: The lab employee’s last entry sheds some light on the failure of the “Mission to Protect Scientists” and the factors leading to Clear Sky’s departure from the base.
sid_questItemprototypes_E05_MQ03_NimbleD: The note is addressed to one Prof. Kalancha. It indicates that Scar had a longstanding association with Clear Sky, much like the author of the message.
sid_questItemprototypes_E05_MQ03_NimbleP: The guide’s PDA contains an exchange with someone called Sickle, along with information regarding the operation of Kalancha’s beacons. Apparently, the only way to reach the Clear Sky base involves re-calibrating them.
sid_questItemprototypes_E05_SQ01_VartaPa: A document detailing the escaped squad’s recent patrols.
sid_questItemprototypes_E05_SQ03_DocsAbo: Printed documents outlining mission objectives for the Warden squad led by Sgt. Shavruk.
sid_questItemprototypes_E05_SQ03_PDASavr: This PDA bears numerous scratches and dents, evidence of a recent fight with a mutant – a stark contrast to its pristine condition just a little while ago.
sid_questItemprototypes_E05_SQ03_Parts_d: Parts of exceptional reliability, used to craft military gear. The SIRCAA label is barely visible on them.
sid_questItemprototypes_E05_SQ04_GunPart: Surplus gear, including standard maintenance components that someone was looking to sell under the radar.
sid_questItemprototypes_E05_SQ04_LapaPDA: This ordinary, worn-out PDA might offer insight into the mysterious customer who was supposed to receive the gear.
sid_questItemprototypes_E06_MQ01_AgathaN: This handwritten note from Dr. Dalin’s safe carries a faint scent of perfume.
sid_questItemprototypes_E06_MQ01_Card_de: This old green key card is a bit scratched, but the magnetic strip seems to be intact. It should probably still work.
sid_questItemprototypes_E06_MQ01_CryptoK: The key to the back room in the Cryptobiology Lab, kept by a lab technician who hid in a cage to escape rampaging mutants.
sid_questItemprototypes_E06_MQ01_SciMaga: A clipping from an article in a prominent scientific journal. Found in Dr. Dalin’s penthouse.
sid_questItemprototypes_E06_MQ01_ZombieN: A tattered tag, taken off a zombie at the Cryptobiology Lab.
sid_questItemprototypes_E06_MQ03_DalinDa: A high-tech flash drive that contains comprehensive data on recent SIRCAA research, including the experiment in Lab X11.
Flash Drive with the SIRCAA Archive
sid_questItemprototypes_E07_EQ01_Zelenyj: Damaged during the battle with Monolith, this PDA contains a recording that sheds light on the reasons behind the stalker’s desperate act.
sid_questItemprototypes_E07_MQ01_Fuse_de: An essential component that protects devices from the impact of voltage fluctuations.
sid_questItemprototypes_E07_MQ01_HorsPDA: A PDA belonging to the Noontide technician. It looks like it was just unboxed yesterday. Khors was meticulous about everything.
sid_questItemprototypes_E07_MQ01_SinajAu: The device contains a single recording, seemingly made right before the Signal.
sid_questItemprototypes_E07_MQ01_SkiperP: This old, but reliable PDA was owned by a mercenary who was working on Wild Island for an unknown client.
sid_questItemprototypes_E07_MQ01_TihiyDo: A regular graph paper notebook filled with Quiet’s sprawling handwriting, detailing his business transactions.
sid_questItemprototypes_E07_MQ02_PripoyK: A small metal key received from Solder.
sid_questItemprototypes_E07_MQ02_SyhinOf: An official document bearing the signature of the IPSF commander.
sid_questItemprototypes_E07_MQ02_SyhinPD: An ordinary military-grade PDA, although its owner turned out not to be the person he was pretending to be.
sid_questItemprototypes_E07_MQ02_SyhinSt: Captain Sukhin’s medical records, found in his office at the Sphere.
sid_questItemprototypes_E07_MQ03_Archive: A voice recorder with an illegible inventory number containing an archival recording by Dr. Orlov.
Voice Recorder Labeled with an Inventory Number
sid_questItemprototypes_E07_MQ03_Control: This device can be used to control and transmit data within the X-Network. When installed in a communication node, it grants limited control over network elements.
sid_questItemprototypes_E07_MQ03_SciDict: A nondescript voice recorder containing a report from an unidentified agent.
Recording of an Unknown Agent from Promin CMD Factory, pt. 1
A nondescript voice recorder containing a report from an unidentified agent.
Recording of an Unknown Agent from Promin CMD Factory, pt. 2
A nondescript voice recorder containing a report from an unidentified agent.
Recording of an Unknown Agent from Promin CMD Factory, pt. 3
sid_questItemprototypes_E07_PsyEmitter_d: The device used to transmit the Signal. Despite its compact size, the radiation it releases is so intense that conventional psi-defenses are unable to counteract it.
sid_questItemprototypes_E07_SQ01_LinzaPD: This PDA found in Lens’s workshop has a large crack across the screen. Its owner must have dropped it during the uneven battle against the Monolithians.
sid_questItemprototypes_E07_SQ01_MPDA_Da: The Monolithians under Uncle Lyonya’s command used this shabby M-PDA to receive their orders. The former guide and protector of rookies in Zalissya was apparently determined to destroy everyone who trusted him.
sid_questItemprototypes_E07_SQ01_NoteFor: A note on a scrap of paper, left by a grateful rookie on Uncle Lyonya’s desk. Little did the rookie know that the former Zalissya guide would turn out to be a Monolithian…
sid_questItemprototypes_E07_SQ01_Surgery: Carefully wrapped in a soft cloth, these scalpels and clamps look pristine and razor sharp.
sid_questItemprototypes_E08_DvupolovReco: The professor’s pocket voice recorder, containing Dvupalov’s sessions with his test subject. The device was left abandoned in the cell – more of a cage, really.
sid_questItemprototypes_E08_EQ01_CollarB: Stained with dried blood on the inside and scratched on the outside, this massive electronic collar must have been uncomfortably tight for an adult specimen, so the mutant tried to peel it off.
sid_questItemprototypes_E08_EQ02_CollarS: This collar appears brand new except for the partially destroyed tag. What little remains legible reads “Test sub…”
sid_questItemprototypes_E08_EQ02_DenyaBy: This PDA looks pretty battered, but it fared much better than its unfortunate owner.
sid_questItemprototypes_E08_EQ03_CollarB: A metal device showing minor damage, as if it had been grazed by a bullet fired at the mutant.
sid_questItemprototypes_E08_EQ03_KrikPDA: A PDA retrieved from the body of the hunter Yevhen Mamay’s body containing exchanges with Viktoria Dorozhnyuk, his client at Malachite. Looks like Mamay will never get to make his move after all.
sid_questItemprototypes_E08_MQ01_Activat: A small sheet with a memo on activating the Mirror VPC. Maybe its late owner wanted to see what would happen if he broke the rules.
sid_questItemprototypes_E08_MQ01_Desiato: A document containing Desyatov’s formal request for transfer. Evidently, it’s tough to work under someone you don’t get along with.
A voice recorder containing Desyatov’s notes. It appears that prior to his involvement, the staff at Mirror VPC was pretty lax about safety precautions.
sid_questItemprototypes_E08_MQ01_Phantom: The print on these instructions from the Mirror VPC has faded slightly over the years. Given its condition, it was probably a hit among the local scientists.
Protocol for Dealing with Induced Illusions
sid_questItemprototypes_E08_MQ01_Razumov: A voice recorder containing the notes of Prof. Razumovsky, reflecting his genuine passion for his work.
A voice recorder containing Prof. Razumovsky’s later notes. It’s impossible to say for sure whether his visions were merely residual induced illusions, or something more profound.
sid_questItemprototypes_E08_MQ01_TruePha: Written in a shaky hand, this crumpled note is abruptly cut off at the end. Evidently, the person who wrote it was suffering from residual induced illusions originating from the Mirror VPC.
Real Protocol for Dealing with Induced Illusions
sid_questItemprototypes_E08_MQ01_TuningI: A document outlining the protocols for communication within X-Network facilities. It looks like the scientists were exchanging a truly massive amount of data.
sid_questItemprototypes_E08_MQ03_Collars: This faded paper provides evidence of mutant experiments at Lab X5. The “Project Leash” label is barely legible.
sid_questItemprototypes_E08_MQ03_ConrolR: A key to the control room of Lab X5’s experimental area.
sid_questItemprototypes_E08_MQ03_Dvupalo: A worn-out leather-bound notebook. Someone frequently consulted these notes, even after the professor had left the lab.
One of the recordings is set on repeat. It's labeled “Prof. Dvupalov, 2006.”
sid_questItemprototypes_E08_MQ03_Keycard: The key card for Lab X5, located beneath Ahroprom. Miraculously, it’s still functional after all these years.
sid_questItemprototypes_E08_MQ03_LetterF: Classified documents found in Lab X5. The laboratory apparently continued to operate under the direction of MOST after it was officially closed.
sid_questItemprototypes_E08_MQ03_ListWar: A note from Dvupalov’s desk at X5.
sid_questItemprototypes_E08_MQ03_Recepti: A note from Lab X5, stained with dried blood. It’s unclear whether the recipient managed to carry out the instructions.
sid_questItemprototypes_E08_MQ03_SciArti: It’s hard to fathom which scientific journal would have accepted this kind of research for publication.
sid_questItemprototypes_E08_MQ03_SherbaT: A draft of a message addressed to Prof. Dvupalov, composed by a graduate student named Shcherba. It looks like someone is completely unable to let go.
sid_questItemprototypes_E08_MQ03_Strelok: Strelok’s old PDA. He went to his stash before making the decision to leave the Zone. Judging from the recording, the stalker had dealt a substantial blow to the Ward and intended to come back and finish the job.
sid_questItemprototypes_E08_MQ03_Suppres: The schematics for the Suppressor, a device designed to hinder the psi abilities of mutants.
sid_questItemprototypes_E08_MQ04_Explosi: A container packed with high-powered military explosives.
sid_questItemprototypes_E08_MQ04_Undergr: A small key card granting access to one of the engineering rooms in the underground section of the Duga.
sid_questItemprototypes_E08_SQ01_KardanD: An old PDA displaying an incoming late-night call. The timing of the call suggests that something important was going on.
Coated in a layer of dust, this PDA contains negotiations involving some kind of device.
sid_questItemprototypes_E08_SQ01_Kartezh: This worn-out PDA contains messages between a stalker and a scientist involved in something big.
sid_questItemprototypes_E10_MQ01_ArhipPD: The lack of concern shown by this PDA’s owner implies that buying a replacement was likely not a problem for him.
sid_questItemprototypes_E10_MQ01_LeaderP: Wolfhound left this PDA in an abandoned village. He clearly intended to come back for it, but it wasn't meant to be.
sid_questItemprototypes_E10_MQ01_SiviyPD: A bloodstained PDA belonging to a stalker called Gray, found in a clearing in the Red Forest. Recovering it was easier than expected.
sid_questItemprototypes_E10_MQ01_TundraP: Even as he was dying, he tried to warn others about the danger. He must’ve been a good man. Just goes to show you that good people don’t last long in the Zone.
sid_questItemprototypes_E10_MQ01_Volkoda: This PDA contains nothing but a set of coordinates recently transmitted to an undisclosed recipient.
sid_questItemprototypes_E10_MQ01_YarlPDA: A somewhat battered PDA. It must have endured some tough times during its owner’s last stand.
sid_questItemprototypes_E10_MQ02_Monolit: A small sheet of paper providing a summary of the current state of affairs at the Monolith infirmary.
sid_questItemprototypes_E10_MQ02_OldPDA_: A well-worn M-PDA, rarely used and frequently lost in various locations.
sid_questItemprototypes_E10_MQ02_PDABrod: An M-PDA retrieved from the body of the former leader of Noontide. He wanted to build a new future on Wild Island, but his Monolithian past caught up with him.
sid_questItemprototypes_E10_MQ02_Regener: A medical device used by Monolithians to treat injured soldiers.
sid_questItemprototypes_E11_MQ01_Cartrid: A small storage drive containing data from the signature station. It can only be read on a compatible device.
sid_questItemprototypes_E11_MQ01_Deciphe: This electronic lockpick seems capable of unlocking the door to the abandoned science lab in the Cordon.
sid_questItemprototypes_E11_MQ01_Stalker: This PDA’s deceased owner found the new Oasis after all. However, as is often the case in the Zone, it wasn’t what it seemed. Unfortunately, a malfunction has rendered the stalker’s name illegible.
PDA of a Stalker from X3
sid_questItemprototypes_E11_MQ01_Tracker: A tiny gadget containing the coordinates to the entrance to Lab X3 at the Cordon.
sid_questItemprototypes_E11_MQ02_2ndKaym: Notes from Dr. Kaymanov found at Orbita Station.
sid_questItemprototypes_E11_MQ02_3rdKaym: Notes from Dr. Kaymanov found at Orbita Station.
sid_questItemprototypes_E11_MQ02_Audiolo: This worn, dusty recorder contains a copy of a conversation between Scar and Prof. Ozersky.
Recording of a Conversation Between Scar and Ozersky
sid_questItemprototypes_E11_MQ02_CodeToB: A memo outlining guidelines for the station’s managerial staff.
sid_questItemprototypes_E11_MQ02_InstAnt: Handwritten instructions for restarting the antenna at Orbita Station.
sid_questItemprototypes_E11_MQ02_Kaymano: Notes from Dr. Kaymanov found at Orbita Station.
sid_questItemprototypes_E11_MQ02_KeyCard: A key card to the entrance of Lab X15.
sid_questItemprototypes_E11_MQ03_PTSthre: A yellowed and dusty report from the Troposphere Comm Station, filled out in neat handwriting. The author, whoever they were, took great care in their work.
sid_questItemprototypes_E11_MQ04_DoorKey: A key to the cell in the detention center, given by Dalin. Without the doctor’s aid, escaping the Chemical Plant seemed nearly impossible.
sid_questItemprototypes_E11_MQ04_PDA_Che: Chornozem’s PDA, found in a warehouse at the Chemical Plant. Apparently, there was more to the bandit than meets the eye.
sid_questItemprototypes_E11_MQ04_PDA_Nes: Nestor’s PDA, found in a warehouse at the Chemical Plant. The former Garbage kingpin apparently had the backing of someone even more important than the Ward.
sid_questItemprototypes_E11_MQ04_Protoco: At first glance, this file of documents found in the infirmary of the Chemical Plant appears totally unremarkable. However, the papers inside are clearly not meant for prying eyes.
sid_questItemprototypes_E12_MO01_DuhToRi: A short message on a crumpled piece of paper.
sid_questItemprototypes_E12_MO01_GroupsN: A sheet containing an annotated list of names, found in Spirit’s bunker. There were probably many others like it, but only this sheet has survived.
List of People Crossing the Boundary
sid_questItemprototypes_E12_MO01_Instruc: A sheet of paper with instructions for the device. Looks like it was used as a coaster.
sid_questItemprototypes_E12_MO01_PDAOzer: A surprisingly neat PDA in almost pristine condition that somehow found its way into Spirit’s belongings. It only contains a single exchange with a colleague.
sid_questItemprototypes_E12_MO01_PhotoIs: This crumpled photo must be a precious memento for its owner.
sid_questItemprototypes_E12_MQ01_DuhStas: The scuffed surface of this magnetic key card suggests it has seen frequent use.
sid_questItemprototypes_E12_MQ01_PDADuh_: This unassuming PDA contains a message with some crucial information.
sid_questItemprototypes_E12_MQ01_SkanerM: A modified scanner capable of temporarily disabling anomalies. It can be operated by anyone who has read the manual.
sid_questItemprototypes_E14_MQ01_Coordin: A PDA containing a scanned map of the Foundation underground military facility. One of the rooms has been circled three times with a ballpoint pen.
PDA with a Map of Prypiat Military Facilities
sid_questItemprototypes_E14_MQ01_Helicop: An aged and partially damaged flash drive. Despite its appearance, it still works, preserving confidential data on Operation Fairway.
sid_questItemprototypes_E14_MQ01_Laundro: A miraculously preserved fragment found in the Laundromat. The paper has faded over the years, but the names of the rescued Stingray group members are still legible.
Papers from Former HQ in Prypiat
sid_questItemprototypes_E14_MQ01_Promete: A PDA showing signs of damage after a pitched battle with mutants. It contains coordinates for the locations where Spark’s main force could be heading with the scanned archive in tow.
Dead Sparker’s PDA from the Prometheus
A PDA showing signs of damage from an encounter with mutants. It contains coordinates for the locations where the Ward’s main force could be heading with the recovered documents in tow.
Dead Warden’s PDA from the Prometheus
sid_questItemprototypes_E14_MQ01_SCAT7Fl: A flash drive recovered from the wreckage of Stingray-7. The group was apparently searching for an entrance to the Foundation underground facility, but the helicopter crashed before they could transmit their discoveries to HQ.
Flash Drive with Coordinates to Foundation Entrances
sid_questItemprototypes_E14_MQ02_MobileB: Grants entry to the Mobile Bunkers.
sid_questItemprototypes_E14_MQ02_Tunnels: Unlocks the entrance to the underground tunnels that lead to the Foundation.
sid_questItemprototypes_E15_MQ03_Cartrid: A small storage drive. It can only be read on a compatible device.
sid_questItemprototypes_E15_MQ03_Project: These scribbled drafts are full of revisions and crossed-out sections. Whose work is this, and who was it intended for?
sid_questItemprototypes_E16_AgentsFile_A: The archive encompasses hundreds of files detailing every agent recruited by C-Consciousness. For years, these people operated in the Zone and beyond, unknowingly carrying out instructions they were programmed to execute. The list includes a number of familiar names.
Archive of Files on C-Consciousness Agents
sid_questItemprototypes_E16_MQ01_PDA_Sca: A PDA taken from Scar’s corpse in the Neurolaboratory. He went to the Generators looking for the Shining Zone, but all he found there was death.
sid_questItemprototypes_E16_MQ02_Marshal: A PDA belonging to a long-dead member of the original Spark. C-Consciousness used its owner’s memories as the basis for a new identity.
sid_questItemprototypes_E16_MQ03_4thKaym: Notes from Dr. Kaymanov found in his office at Lab X7.
sid_questItemprototypes_E16_MQ03_5thKaym: Notes from Dr. Kaymanov found in his office at Lab X7.
sid_questItemprototypes_E16_MQ03_6thKaym: Notes from Dr. Kaymanov found in his office at Lab X7.
sid_questItemprototypes_E16_MQ03_Caribbe: Old scientific papers outlining the experiment that confirmed the Noosphere theory. These records paved the way to the Zone’s emergence.
sid_questItemprototypes_E16_MQ03_Cartrid: A small storage drive. It can only be read on a compatible device.
sid_questItemprototypes_E16_MQ03_Instruc: An old instruction sheet covered with handwritten notes in red pencil.
sid_questItemprototypes_E16_MQ03_KeyKard: An old key card, once the property of Lt. Col. Korshunov. It grants the highest level of clearance, which is necessary to enter Lab X7.
sid_questItemprototypes_E16_MQ03_Strelok: By the look of this PDA, it faithfully served Strelok for many a year. The most important entry deals with the procedure for recording a psi-signature.
sid_questItemprototypes_EQ08_FreshBread_: Soft and crispy, with the lingering smell of the oven. How on earth Myklukha gets fresh bread to the Zone is anyone’s guess, but it’s only available in Rostok.
sid_questItemprototypes_EQ109_MarikTopot: A PDA from a stalker’s corpse, found in a crashed helicopter at the edge of the Burnt Forest. The harsh reality of the stalker life – the very thing Marik Stomper sought so eagerly proved to be much tougher than he anticipated.
sid_questItemprototypes_EQ109_VitekLunaP: A PDA belonging to Vityok Moon, a bandit associated with the Sultan, who went on to start his own gang in the Burnt Forest. Just another wannabe king of the Zone who didn’t quite grasp that an imaginary crown won’t protect him from a bullet to the head.
sid_questItemprototypes_EQ10_PastuhPDA_d: This veteran stalker’s PDA is full of messages and personal notes. One entry designates a stash location, along with a reminder to rig it with booby traps.
sid_questItemprototypes_EQ110_DokaPDA_de: A PDA belonging to Dima Hotshot, a stalker who died near the school in the Burnt Forest. His search for the legendary Hermit’s stash was cut short by a mutant encounter. The Zone fiercely guards some of its most coveted secrets.
sid_questItemprototypes_EQ110_HermitPDA_: A crumpled note written by veteran stalker Hermit. The warning left by the Zone seems to have scared him out of his wits. Found in the school at the edge of the Burnt Forest.
sid_questItemprototypes_EQ111_KorkaPDA_d: This PDA belonged to a Freedom member who carelessly ignored the presence of mutants in his haste to recover some stolen goods. Found at the Krug Antenna Complex.
sid_questItemprototypes_EQ118_DeadIskraP: A PDA belonging to a dead Sparker found near the checkpoint at the bridge in the Red Forest. It contains information about a mission given by Ozersky.
sid_questItemprototypes_EQ11_NortonPDA_d: A PDA taken from Norton’s body. Based on his messages, it seems like he and his partner were arranging a mercenary hit on loners from the Cordon. Sidorovich might be interested in hearing about this.
sid_questItemprototypes_EQ13_FilimonPDA_: This PDA belonged to a Freedom member who used it to conduct business. It holds some recent correspondence with a bandit who purchased the coordinates of a stash. For some, there are no friends or foes in the Zone – only a bottom line after accounting for income and expenses.
sid_questItemprototypes_EQ13_MihaPDA_des: This PDA of a lone stalker stores a chat with a mercenary. The latter is ready to tell him who sold the weapon stash to the bandits and got Cowboy’s friends into trouble. Of course, in the Zone, any information has a price, but Mykha is ready to give up anything for a chance of revenge.
sid_questItemprototypes_EQ140_IluhaVorob: This PDA belonged to a lone stalker who was seemingly plagued by perpetual misfortune. Unfortunately, his luck didn’t change this time. In a desperate attempt to regain the Zone’s favor, the stalker embarked on a dangerous adventure, only to learn that, unlike luck, death had no plans to pass him by.
sid_questItemprototypes_EQ141_TurgaPDA_d: A PDA taken from the body of a stalker in the southern part of the Burnt Forest. It contains an audio recording that abruptly ends with gunshots and bloodcurdling screams.
sid_questItemprototypes_EQ143_KolubPDA_d: Columbus’s PDA, found near the Vehicle Graveyard. Given the high radiation levels, his nearby stash should have been almost inaccessible. However, his ploy worked even better than intended: the Stalker himself succumbed to radiation poisoning. The Zone proves once again to be a dangerous and fickle ally.
sid_questItemprototypes_EQ144_ArtemPodvo: A shattered PDA that belonged to Artyom Submariner. The screen is cracked, with notifications about unread messages still visible, but inaccessible. Someone with the right skills might be able to breathe life back into it or dismantle it for parts, but it’s of little use in its current state.
sid_questItemprototypes_EQ144_GlazgoPDA_: A PDA taken from Glasgow’s corpse. After locking himself up, the stalker continued calling for help, only to realize that the dead man behind the door was his friend. Stumbling upon a fat stash, getting wasted, turning away a man seeking help during an Emission – this story could only have ended up badly. The Zone has punished people for far less.
sid_questItemprototypes_EQ144_KucyjPDA_d: A PDA taken from Short's corpse. Locked in the basement by a stalker who died right on the other side of the door, he and his companions perished amid a mound of loot that proved worthless in their predicament. Short probably didn’t even have a chance to sober up.
sid_questItemprototypes_EQ145KostaKonusN: A crumpled piece of paper found on the body of the scout Kostya Dry. It provides detailed observations on IPSF patrol routes. It looks like Spark is actively planning to infiltrate the Duga.
sid_questItemprototypes_EQ146_MonolithPD: A Monolithian’s PDA containing a recording of Faust’s sermon. Even in death, the shepherd continues to lead his flock.
sid_questItemprototypes_EQ148_KeyFromCon: Unlocks the old assembly hall located on the school’s second floor.
sid_questItemprototypes_EQ148_KostasPDA_: A soldier’s PDA containing an audio recording. Seeking refuge in the school after Monolith seized the Duga, the group stumbled upon a deadly menace within. For Kostas, it was the last straw.
sid_questItemprototypes_EQ150_NoteOtshel: Found in a small village at Yaniv, this note narrates a journey through the Red Forest. If these accounts are true, the path through the forest is fraught with danger, even for a stalker of Hermit’s ability.
sid_questItemprototypes_EQ155_BromPDA_de: In his final exchange, Bromine boasts that he’s about to get his hands on the scientists’ stash as long as he can find the “corpse with the password.” It looks like the “corpse with the password” found him first.
sid_questItemprototypes_EQ155_CollarCont: The metal collar has a tag on it with a barely visible inscription: “Test subject #48.” Someone wrote the number 24 next to it.
sid_questItemprototypes_EQ156_CaseBones_: A small suitcase found on the body of Zhenya Blizzard in the swamps of Ahroprom. It’s filled with human bones. The Sparker’s PDA could reveal the motive behind this gruesome collection.
sid_questItemprototypes_EQ156_MorozPDA_d: A PDA belonging to a Sparker killed in the swamps near Ahroprom. In his final exchange, Zhenya Blizzard promises to deliver a suitcase full of bones to Viktoria Dorozhnyuk, a scientist from Malachite.
sid_questItemprototypes_EQ161_kudravyjPD: A PDA belonging to a Sparker who set up an ambush at the X5 exit. The stalker wanted to seize Korshunov’s badge in order to sabotage the Ward, but the attempt ended in failure.
sid_questItemprototypes_EQ163_PripoyFune: The writing on this sheet of notebook paper is unsteady, and the ink is partially smudged.
sid_questItemprototypes_EQ165_TrachukPDA: The scientist's PDA contains exchanges with a Sparker named Trickster. He apparently trusted the guy and was intending to hand his research over to the Sparker. Sadly, his attempt to protect the discovery from the Ward ended in tragedy.
sid_questItemprototypes_EQ165_USBTrachuk: A flash drive with the results of Trachuk’s research. The scientist went to great lengths to protect his data, even putting his own life at risk. This information must be very important indeed.
sid_questItemprototypes_EQ166_ValerianPD: A PDA recovered from Father Valerian’s corpse near the Cooling Towers. If the messages are of any indication, Valerian’s group was already falling apart when he met his end at the hands of mutants.
sid_questItemprototypes_EQ167_NoteOtshel: A note found on Hermit’s body in the Dead Valley. He was apparently ready for death, and embraced it without fear or regret.
sid_questItemprototypes_EQ16_FaraPDA_des: The PDA of a stalker with the soul of a poet who cherished the Zone’s unique beauty. But even here, death is still horrible – Headlight can attest to that.
