sid_questItemprototypes_EQ26_EmptyFlashD: A flash drive found on Lefty’s body, marked “Property of STC Malachite. Please return to Viktoria Dorozhnyuk.” Lefty was apparently assigned to record the psi-radiation measurements on the drive.
sid_questItemprototypes_EQ26_FlashDriveW: A flash drive found on Lefty’s body, marked “Property of STC Malachite. Please return to Viktoria Dorozhnyuk.” The drive contains psi-radiation measurements, so I can take it back to its owner.
sid_questItemprototypes_EQ26_LevsaPDA_Al: A Sparker’s PDA containing data on psi-radiation measurements near Lake Yantar, along with messages from Viktoria Dorozhnyuk, a scientist from Malachite. I should take this PDA to her.
sid_questItemprototypes_EQ26_LevsaPDA_de: A PDA belonging to Lefty a stalker exposed to psi-radiation near Lake Yantar. It contains correspondence with Viktoria Dorozhnyuk, a scientist from STC Malachite. Science demands sacrifices, and the Zone is always willing to oblige.
sid_questItemprototypes_EQ29_TradeNote_P: The military’s terms for trading with the stalkers are blunt, but concise. These soldiers are posing as enforcers of the law, yet they constantly violate it themselves.
sid_questItemprototypes_EQ30_DobryakPDA_: This PDA was retrieved by a stalker who found Jolly Azat’s corpse at the Ribs. It’s not much, but it’s better than disappearing into an anomaly forever.
sid_questItemprototypes_EQ30_ReshkaPDA_d: A PDA belonging to a stalker who died at the Ribs, found by a nearby slab of concrete. Apparently Lyova had a bolt he kept as a lucky charm. He should have kept it on him when he went into that anomaly.
sid_questItemprototypes_EQ30_TitunPDA_de: An old PDA found near the Ribs. It doesn't seem like anyone was especially broken up about the owner’s death.
sid_questItemprototypes_EQ35_LegendAdvic: A note from Lex the Legend's "generous" stash. A first life lesson, of some sort: don't trust the loudmouths promising dope loot.
sid_questItemprototypes_EQ35_LehaLegendP: A PDA belonging to Lex the Legend, AKA Snag. His correspondence reveals a well-prepared scam. Lex maneuvered into a position of influence while doing business directly with the kingpin. In the end, that didn’t help him very much.
sid_questItemprototypes_EQ35_VolanNoteSh: A note found at the Flea Market, addressed to Lex the Legend. His ardent disciples swear to follow their idol through hell and high water. He seems to have them firmly in his grip.
sid_questItemprototypes_EQ36_KucherPDA_d: A PDA belonging to a stalker who was paying his respects to his partner and mentor Tolik at the Cherevach village cemetery. Apparently this Tolik guy had some kind of history with the legendary Strelok. Unfortunately, anomalies don’t discriminate based on who you are or who you know.
sid_questItemprototypes_EQ37_GromNote_de: Found by the Cage at the Cement Factory, this note lists the names of Hromovyi’s comrades-in-arms who fell during the First Raid – a time when Duty had yet to be established, and the captain held a rank far beneath his current stature.
sid_questItemprototypes_EQ38_Instruction: Left in a backpack in the Poppy Field, this note asks the reader to leave spare energy drinks in the stash for anyone who might need them. An uncommon display of kindness in the Zone.
sid_questItemprototypes_EQ38_ThanksNote_: A note found in the Poppy Field stash offering thanks to the anonymous benefactor who stocked it with energy drinks, ultimately saving the author’s life. Energy drinks might not be the healthiest beverage out there, of course, but they’ve got nothing on anomalies.
Note from the Energy Drink Stash
sid_questItemprototypes_EQ48_GiverPaper_: The author of the note bequeathed his stash to whoever finds it first. In the Zone, an occasional act of kindness like this can be more surprising than the most wondrous of miracles.
sid_questItemprototypes_EQ51_TemaJetPDA_: A PDA retrieved from the body of a Duty member killed in Kopachi Village. The decision to split up to complete Zulu’s objectives cost the owner his life, and Duty lost yet another soldier, further depleting their already diminished ranks.
sid_questItemprototypes_EQ52_PuzirPDA_de: This PDA was owned by Bubbs. He owed money to the Shah, so he went into an anomaly to secure an artifact and died there. By the standards of those indebted to the Shah, he got off easy.
sid_questItemprototypes_EQ58_TimurMolcan: A PDA belonging to Goodwin, found in the Yantar underground. It contains records of a stalker’s unsuccessful bid to save a partner who turned into a zombie. As the saying goes, hope is the first step on the road to disappointment, but in this case, disappointment was just the beginning.
sid_questItemprototypes_EQ60_PDAVovchik_: A PDA belonging to a rookie who refused to tell bandits where his stash is. Sadly, it didn’t save his life.
sid_questItemprototypes_EQ64_MakisPDA_de: A PDA belonging to a stalker slain by mutants in the Lesser Zone. Like many rookies, he had big plans for the future. But, as the saying goes, “a stalker proposes, the Zone disposes.”
sid_questItemprototypes_EQ65AK74_descrip: A customized AKM-74U outfitted with a silencer and a collimator sight. Few of the loners haunting the Cordon can afford a gun like this.
sid_questItemprototypes_EQ65LosPDA_descr: Found on a dead body by the Elevator, this PDA mentions a job from Sidorovich that the owner will never be able to complete. Guess I could take advantage of this and make some coupons.
sid_questItemprototypes_EQ65PiavkaPDA_de: Several exchanges on this PDA suggest that dealing with Leech wasn’t a popular choice. Nevertheless, Stets always received prompt responses from his contacts within the IPSF.
sid_questItemprototypes_EQ67UraKotPDA_de: A PDA belonging to a Freedom member whose unit was slaughtered by Monolith. The fear instilled by this terrifying group is so intense that even veteran Freedom soldiers hesitate to come to the aid of their comrades who fall prey to the fanatics.
sid_questItemprototypes_EQ68_KubrakPDA_d: This PDA is pristine, without a single scratch on it. The device is either brand new, or its owner took exceptionally good care of it.
sid_questItemprototypes_EQ70_DeadBodyPda: A PDA belonging to a stalker who attempted to safeguard his stash using radiation signs. The ruse proved ineffective against the mutants, which mauled him to death.
PDA of a Stalker Killed by Mutants
sid_questItemprototypes_EQ73_EdikBaskaPD: A PDA belonging to a thug dressed as a Freedom member, probably affiliated with the Brood. He was in cahoots with Docent.
sid_questItemprototypes_EQ74_Jitnichenko: A PDA belonging to a Malachite scientist. Zhytnychenko’s theories didn’t sit right with his supervisor, which resulted in the troublesome employee being sent out on a dangerous expedition. I guess when there’s a power struggle, scientists can be as ruthless as the bandits in Zaton.
sid_questItemprototypes_EQ75AK74_descrip: An AKM-74S recovered from a trench near the Slag Heap.
sid_questItemprototypes_EQ82_konserva_de: A swollen can of food, expired long before the Zone came into being. Without a decent gas mask, the stench is unbearable.
sid_questItemprototypes_EQ84_LotPDA_desc: A PDA preserving the final words of Vasko Lot, who went missing on Wild Island. Guess he learned firsthand that the Zone’s gifts often come at a hefty price.
sid_questItemprototypes_EQ85_MelPDA_desc: A PDA belonging to Stefko Tactician, found at the Pontoon Warehouse, where he met his end while looking for a stash with his associates. The audio log implies that their partnership did not pass the Emission test.
sid_questItemprototypes_EQ92_TunnelPDA_d: A PDA belonging to a dead stalker containing a conversation with some unknown person. There’s a chance that this stranger might have had a role in the owner’s death.
sid_questItemprototypes_EchoE01_descript: With outdated electronic components and subpar usability, this basic artifact detector is only redeemed by its very modest price.
sid_questItemprototypes_Electrocollar_de: A collar-shaped electronic device with inner metal electrodes designed to make direct contact with the wearer’s neck.
sid_questItemprototypes_FaustData_descri: Taken from the body of Faust, the Voice of the Monolith. This object is made from an unknown substance and has a mysterious hi-tech device embedded within it.
sid_questItemprototypes_FaustPDA_descrip: Found on the Preacher’s dead body, this M-PDA exposes Faust’s years of scheming to bring the Monolith back. Through this device, the Monolithians received orders that ruined hundreds of lives.
sid_questItemprototypes_FreshBread_descr: Soft and crispy, with the lingering smell of the oven. How on earth Myklukha gets fresh bread to the Zone is anyone’s guess, but it’s only available in Rostok.
sid_questItemprototypes_GontaPDA_descrip: Honta's PDA – a hunter from Zaton who set up a school for rookies. Looks like he set up a job market for future hunters, too.
sid_questItemprototypes_GunTOZ_Gonta_des: A popular hunting shotgun. Very cheap and affordable, and much more effective against mutants than a pistol.
sid_questItemprototypes_Himzavod_Hub_Not: A report on a sheet of 8x12 paper. Given the illegible handwriting, its author was likely a medic.
A sheet of handwritten text. Even the letters themselves seem to stand at attention.
sid_questItemprototypes_IKAR_PikmanPDA_d: A standard military-grade PDA. The device is somewhat hard to activate – the button requires a slightly longer and firmer press than usual.
sid_questItemprototypes_Icon_description: An old family heirloom, likely left behind during the evacuation in 1986. This item has no place in the Zone.
sid_questItemprototypes_Inductor_descrip: A device for decrypting messages on old Monolith PDAs. Requires a connection to Azimuth Station. This model has been phased out.
sid_questItemprototypes_KozimkovPDA_desc: A PDA belonging to the commander of the Ward’s Bravo Team, containing a distress signal from Echo Station along with its coordinates.
sid_questItemprototypes_KuziaPDA_descrip: A PDA belonging to Kuzya, a bandit with a knack for fleecing “rich suckers” from Rostok. In the messages, he flaunts his lavish lifestyle and urges his buddy to join his venture, contributing to a shared stash at specified coordinates. In the Zone, survival comes first, but once you’ve survived, it’s time to turn a profit.
sid_questItemprototypes_LeshiyPDA_descri: A PDA belonging to a bandit called Leshy, who was hiding at the Supply Depot. Looks like he overestimated himself and turned from hunter to prey. Well, should’ve prepared better to take on the kingpin.
sid_questItemprototypes_MaksSubbotaPDA_d: The PDA of a stalker from the Lesser Zone, a member of Mastiff’s group who made his living by robbing the stashes of dead stalkers.
sid_questItemprototypes_MalahitElevatorK: A key that opens the elevator to X17.
sid_questItemprototypes_Malahit_Hub_Note: A crumpled printout found at Malachite. Its contents clearly caused great offense to the recipient.
Written Reply to a Request for Collaboration
sid_questItemprototypes_Malahit_KeyCard_: Presenting this access card guarantees easy entry to STC Malachite.
sid_questItemprototypes_Malahit_PDADoroj: A PDA belonging to the Malachite trader. Its pristine condition is a testament to the owner’s perfectionism.
sid_questItemprototypes_Malahit_PDAKupri: This brand-new PDA contains negotiations between an STC Malachite employee and a stalker. Obtaining coupons from scientists is clearly no easy feat for a loner.
sid_questItemprototypes_MuhaPDA_descript: A PDA belonging to a bandit who died during a ‘negotiation’ with a rival gang in the classic Garbage tradition.
sid_questItemprototypes_NegodaPDA_descri: This PDA didn’t get a single scratch during many years of heavy use, but now there’s a big crack across the device’s surface.
sid_questItemprototypes_NestorHenchmanPD: The PDA is perfectly clean, and the screen is covered with a protector. It seems that the owner kept it from getting scratched, even after he turned into a zombie.
sid_questItemprototypes_NestorUSB_descri: An ordinary device used for storing and transferring information, marked with the haphazardly carved word “Nest.”
sid_questItemprototypes_NoonBase_Hub_Pan: This misplaced PDA contains the Noontider’s correspondence, but it would be inappropriate to intrude into his private messages.
sid_questItemprototypes_NoonBase_Hub_Ros: A battered PDA belonging to a Noontider that contains an exchange filled with contemplations about the Mainland.
sid_questItemprototypes_NoonBase_Hub_Vec: An ordinary PDA containing correspondence on topics that concern everyone on Wild Island to varying degrees.
sid_questItemprototypes_NoonBase_Hub_Zav: Covered in small scratches and scuffs, this PDA seems to be ever-ready and functional, even when not intentionally in use.
sid_questItemprototypes_PomorPDA_descrip: This stalker’s PDA contains a map of the Poppy Field. Taking advantage of those who succumbed to sleep within the anomaly, he now finds himself trapped in a dream from which he will never awaken.
sid_questItemprototypes_PripoyPDA_descri: Solder’s PDA reveals the owner’s involvement in clandestine dealings with Nestor, the kingpin of the Slag Heap.
sid_questItemprototypes_ProjectControlle: These faded papers contain gruesome details about experiments conducted in X5. It looks like these are the documents Shcherba was after.
sid_questItemprototypes_Prypat_NoteItem1: An 8x12 sheet carrying a message from someone who knows Degtyarev well.
sid_questItemprototypes_Prypat_NoteItem2: A short note penned in confident handwriting.
sid_questItemprototypes_QuestArtifactCry: An artifact that came out of Professor Dvupalov’s moonshine still.
sid_questItemprototypes_QuestArtifactHea: An incredibly rare, almost mythical artifact that functions as a psi-radiation amplifier. The degree of amplification depends on the characteristics and individual capabilities of the source. Its properties are still not fully understood, with the comprehensive range of parameters yet unknown. Presumed to encapsulate the original structure of the Zone’s matter.
sid_questItemprototypes_QuestItem_Folder: A file containing important papers – a traditional office management essential.
sid_questItemprototypes_QuestItem_Fuse_L: A common fuse protecting electronic circuits from voltage fluctuations.
sid_questItemprototypes_QuestItem_Fuse_M: A device that protects electronic circuits from abrupt voltage fluctuations. This specific fuse is designed with medium characteristics in mind.
sid_questItemprototypes_QuestItem_KeyCar: This old blue key card is a bit scratched, but the magnetic strip seems to be intact. It should probably still work.
This old green key card is a bit scratched, but the magnetic strip seems to be intact. It should probably still work.
This old red key card is a bit scratched, but the magnetic strip seems to be intact. It should probably still work.
This key card grants Level B access.
sid_questItemprototypes_QuestItem_Key_Ch: A key to an old padlock. The kind used to secure barns and garages.
sid_questItemprototypes_QuestItem_Key_Do: A fairly modern key, probably for a door or a decent padlock.
A little old key, clearly meant for a door.
sid_questItemprototypes_QuestItem_Key_Pa: A massive old key, probably for some kind of old-timey lock.
sid_questItemprototypes_QuestItem_Notepa: The robust cover of this notebook ensures the safety of the notes within.
An officer’s field bag, used to store documents and notes. A staple for many servicemen prior to the digital revolution.
An aged, well-worn diary – an excellent repository for one’s thoughts and observations.
A simple, everyday notepad, prone to staining and water damage. Best stored in a dry place.
sid_questItemprototypes_QuestItem_Record: A commonplace voice recorder, often used in the Zone for note-taking and diary-keeping.
sid_questItemprototypes_QuestItem_USB_Da: This old flash drive has been through a lot. The case is held together with black duct tape.
This old flash drive has been through a lot. The case is held together with classic blue duct tape.
Evidently, this flash drive has been exposed to high temperatures. It’s nothing short of a miracle that the device still works.
This old flash drive has been through a lot. The case is held together with white duct tape.
sid_questItemprototypes_QuestItem_USB_Mo: A relatively up-to-date USB drive, capable of storing a large amount of data.
A relatively up-to-date USB drive capable of storing a large amount of data.
A relatively up-to-date USB drive capable of storing a large amount of data.
sid_questItemprototypes_QuestItem_USB_St: A traditional portable USB drive, widely used to store information – even in the Zone.
A traditional portable USB drive, widely used to store information – even in the Zone.
A traditional portable USB drive, widely used to store information – even in the Zone.
sid_questItemprototypes_QuestItem_USB_Up: This USB drive contains data related to equipment upgrades.
sid_questItemprototypes_RSQ01_NewbiePDA2: A PDA belonging to an unknown stalker. He thought he could make it back, but the Zone had different plans for him.
sid_questItemprototypes_RSQ01_NewbiePDA3: A PDA belonging to an unknown stalker. He shouldn’t have strayed from the fire.
sid_questItemprototypes_RSQ01_NewbiePDA4: A PDA belonging to an unknown stalker. Sometimes friendship and the drive for revenge can kill as swiftly as any anomaly.
sid_questItemprototypes_RSQ01_NewbiePDA_: A PDA belonging to an unknown stalker. He ventured into the Zone and never found his way back.
sid_questItemprototypes_RSQ01_WarlockPac: A small, neatly wrapped package ordered by Warlock. Whoever packed it has clearly done this before… and will do it again.
sid_questItemprototypes_RSQ04_C05_FuelCa: A tightly sealed gas can with a pungent aroma of industrial alcohol.
sid_questItemprototypes_RSQ04_C10_Glavar: A run-down, outdated PDA containing the ill-gotten coupons of one of the gang leaders.
sid_questItemprototypes_RSQ05_C01_FlashD: A securely locked briefcase requested by the merchant Owl from the Sultansk.
sid_questItemprototypes_RSQ05_C10_IItemP: A PDA belonging to one of Owl’s agents. Some of his operatives do more than just gather information. This one clearly found more than he bargained for.
sid_questItemprototypes_RSQ05_C10_ItemNo: A note on a piece of paper smeared with dried blood. Looks like whoever took down Owl’s agent isn’t stopping anytime soon.
A torn piece of a document that Owl’s agent was willing to die for… and probably did.
sid_questItemprototypes_RSQ05_C10_ItemPD: A PDA belonging to an agent who bit off more than he could chew. In a way, he was lucky, since Owl doesn’t take kindly to those who try to peddle his information on the side.
sid_questItemprototypes_RSQ06_C04_Goods_: The stolen goods Sidorovich wanted back.
sid_questItemprototypes_RSQ07_C09_Contai: The gaps in the container are securely insulated, but it’s still giving off a little radiation. There must be an artifact in there.
sid_questItemprototypes_RSQ07_Item_descr: A package containing unknown merchandise, possibly meant for Hera.
sid_questItemprototypes_RSQ08_Item2_desc: A brand-new flash drive in pristine condition. From the looks of it, the device was purchased for the sole purpose of delivering information to an important customer.
sid_questItemprototypes_RSQ08_Item_descr: A suitcase featuring a small sticker that says “Fragile!” By the sound of it, there are several bottles inside.
sid_questItemprototypes_RSQ09_Item_descr: A lightweight, tightly closed suitcase. If you sniff it, you catch a whiff of medicine.
sid_questItemprototypes_RSQ10_Item_descr: An ordinary suitcase. The sound suggests it contains some metal tools.
sid_questItemprototypes_RichtersPlayer_d: The old player now only plays one song on repeat. The worn-out button suggests the owner never went anywhere without it.
sid_questItemprototypes_RookieVillage_Ma: A brand-new PDA. The owner’s whereabouts are unknown.
sid_questItemprototypes_RookieVillage_Wo: The screen of this dusty PDA is covered in a spiderweb of cracks. If Wolf ever uses it, it’s only as a backup.
sid_questItemprototypes_RooseveltPDA_des: A PDA belonging to Roosevelt, kingpin of the Commissary. Formerly with Duty, he challenged fellow bandit bosses for leadership in Garbage – an unusual fate, even by Zone standards.
sid_questItemprototypes_Rostok_NoteAgree: One of Myklukha’s many contracts that just happened to be out in the open. Normally, he keeps them hidden from prying eyes.
sid_questItemprototypes_Rostok_NoteToShe: An ordinary sheet of paper with a list of goods on it. Each entry is meticulously written, indicating that the author thoroughly evaluated every item before adding it to the list.
sid_questItemprototypes_Rostok_NoteToSul: A sheet with a list written in tidy handwriting. Deliberate and unhurried, the author gave careful consideration to every entry.
sid_questItemprototypes_Rostok_PDAPolovi: This PDA contains the correspondence that set in motion the events that ultimately led to the sergeant’s death.
sid_questItemprototypes_Rostok_PDA_Docto: There isn’t a scratch or a speck to be found on this PDA. The owner meticulously wipes the device with alcohol on a daily basis.
sid_questItemprototypes_Rostok_PDA_Lost_: Trying to juggle numerous tasks at once, this device’s owner occasionally forgets about certain things. In this case, he left his PDA behind without even noticing.
sid_questItemprototypes_Rostok_YarikChil: Left unattended, this PDA contains a discussion about a guide known for never staying in one place.
sid_questItemprototypes_SEQ01_BanditPDA_: Stalkers aren’t the only ones willing to take risks to turn an easy profit. But it always ends the same way.
sid_questItemprototypes_SEQ02_StalkerPDA: The state of this PDA implies that its owner had some serious anger management issues. Or maybe someone really got under his skin.
sid_questItemprototypes_SEQ04_ScientistP: Typically, so-called “free scientists” opt for more reliable record-keeping devices than a stalker’s PDA. This poor guy must’ve grappled with some serious funding issues.
sid_questItemprototypes_SEQ05_StalkerPDA: Anomaly exposure has wiped out most of the information on this PDA, and what’s left looks rather grim.
sid_questItemprototypes_SEQ06_StalkerPDA: It looks like this PDA’s owner wasn’t too concerned about its condition. Hopefully, he was more diligent with his weapon.
sid_questItemprototypes_SEQ08_StalkerPDA: The dents and scratches on the screen suggest that this PDA was frequently dropped, sometimes from a considerable height.
sid_questItemprototypes_SEQ09_StalkerPDA: The fate of this PDA’s owner is probably best left a mystery. Sometimes a good night’s sleep without any nightmares is more valuable than any loot.
sid_questItemprototypes_SEQ10_StalkerPDA: This PDA clearly belonged to someone from the Corps, and from the looks of it, they prefer to stay under the radar.
sid_questItemprototypes_SQ01_GunAK74_ST_: A regular AKM-74S in perfect condition, stored in a hidden stash at the Water Tower. Valik Lummox never got the chance to make use of his assault rifle.
sid_questItemprototypes_SQ01_ValenokPDA_: A PDA belonging to a stalker who tried to escape his debts. He didn’t even make it to the Water Tower.
sid_questItemprototypes_SQ02_GunM16_ST_d: An AR416 assault rifle found in the Poppy Field. You could almost feel a faint smell of flowers on its metal.
sid_questItemprototypes_SQ02_PomorKey_de: A small room key taken from Pomor, a stalker from the Poppy Field.
sid_questItemprototypes_SQ04_SoloveiPDA_: A PDA belonging to a hunter from Zalissya. He was a jovial man, but, unfortunately, the Zone leaves little room for humor.
sid_questItemprototypes_SQ05_GuronsGoods: A tightly sealed crate containing Huron’s shipment for the Brood.
sid_questItemprototypes_SQ06_CargoNote_d: An old form. The text is still partially legible.
sid_questItemprototypes_SQ06_MSOPAudiolo: It looks like an ordinary voice recorder, but the information it contains is rather useful.
sid_questItemprototypes_SQ06_PDA_ZombieS: An old PDA belonging to a scientist. The owner’s bleak end leaves little room for imagination.
sid_questItemprototypes_SQ06_PsiLampDest: A device resembling a lamp. It was a powerful source of psi-radiation, so I had to break it.
sid_questItemprototypes_SQ06_PsiLamp_des: A device with an unknown purpose, somehow linked to Monolith. Once activated, it produces radiation that’s harmful to the human nervous system.
sid_questItemprototypes_SQ103_BrokenDash: Looks okay to be put up for sale, but only a merchant can tell whether it still works, or if I can only sell it for scrap.
sid_questItemprototypes_SQ103_CircuitBoa: A complex board from an unknown device. A stalker would have no use for it, but it could be worth a lot.
sid_questItemprototypes_SQ103_DashboardP: These haven't been around for about 40 years, but the scientists keep paying merchants to replace their worn-out ones.
sid_questItemprototypes_SQ103_DeadDigger: One of the first PDA models that has seen some haphazard repairs. Looks like its previous owner didn't have enough money to go to a technician.
sid_questItemprototypes_SQ103_DeaddropIn: A notebook page with instructions, written in the same handwriting as the markings on the map.
sid_questItemprototypes_SQ103_Emitter_de: The Emitter that Diode needed so desperately. An open case with thin-film PCBs inside. A device like this can't be manufactured or repaired anywhere in the Zone.
sid_questItemprototypes_SQ103_KeyCardX18: A reprogrammed magnetic card with X18 scratched into it. It takes a skilled technician to create a copy of the original key.
sid_questItemprototypes_SQ103_Key_descri: Standard key to a door lock with an X18 inventory tag
sid_questItemprototypes_SQ103_Key_name: Key to the door to X18
sid_questItemprototypes_SQ103_MapNote_de: A notebook page with the code to the safe where the Emitter needs to be placed, quickly written in shaky handwriting.
sid_questItemprototypes_SQ103_NestorMikh: A sheet of paper folded neatly into an envelope. A report on the operation of the Aggression Suppressor, written in tiny, sharp handwriting. On top of the text is Nestor's huge ‘resolution’.
sid_questItemprototypes_SQ103_OldMonitor: The CRTs from these sell pretty well. There are plenty of rumors about what they're used for, but no merchant will let you in on the secret.
sid_questItemprototypes_SQ103_Oscillosco: A Japanese wideband oscilloscope. Used to be a top-shelf model 30 years ago, but still sells well. "P.D. Stryzh" is written on the inventory tag.
sid_questItemprototypes_SQ103_Powerbank_: An ancient model, as reliable and simple as they come. Any merchant would want it.
sid_questItemprototypes_SQ103_Voltmeter_: A precise voltmeter – a great tool for any technician. This one looks operational, and could be worth quite a lot of coupons.
sid_questItemprototypes_SQ103_Voltpannel: This model is popular, and pretty expensive. A perfect match for sensitive equipment.
sid_questItemprototypes_SQ10_BunchOfKeys: A set of keys providing access to the waste processing station’s facilities. Two keys stand out: one for the basement, and one for the shift supervisor’s office.
sid_questItemprototypes_SQ10_DubniyPDA_d: A PDA belonging to Dubny, Strider’s right-hand man. Its owner is unlikely to appreciate the idea of someone else reading his correspondence.
sid_questItemprototypes_SQ10_HogPDA_desc: This PDA belonged to a mercenary known as Hog. Its owner died as a result of his unwavering commitment to the job.
sid_questItemprototypes_SQ10_JumperPDA_d: A PDA belonging to Jumper, a Noontider from the waste processing station. He put his life on the line to rescue his partner, but the Zone doesn’t always reward the brave.
sid_questItemprototypes_SQ10_SeverNoteDu: A sheet of grayish paper with a report written in big letters. Who was this report intended for? It hardly matters now.
sid_questItemprototypes_SQ10_TihijMonoli: A hastily scribbled message on a small piece of paper. The author must have been in a rush to make a speedy exit.
sid_questItemprototypes_SQ13_PDA_Budnik_: This PDA was retrieved from a dead Warden at the Chemical Tanks. He died while helping his comrades escape from mutants.
sid_questItemprototypes_SQ13_Soul_descri: An artifact found at the Chemical Tanks. According to some, a Soul is created when a human body is ensnared by an anomaly.
sid_questItemprototypes_SQ18_KamysPDA_de: One of the bullets fired at the bandit became lodged in his PDA. The Ringmaster’s death was swift and less agonizing than those of his victims.
sid_questItemprototypes_SQ18_KamysPaperN: A neatly folded piece of paper with a request from someone who wished to remain anonymous. Nevertheless, the recipient is well aware of the sender’s identity.
sid_questItemprototypes_SQ47_DavidAkopan: A PDA belonging to the stalker held captive by Zakhar Babay’s thugs. Seduced by the promise of easy coupons, he ended up getting his ass kicked.
sid_questItemprototypes_SQ47_LevaBorzoj_: A PDA belonging to a thug from Garbage, Zakhar Babay’s right-hand man and the scourge of the loners who walked into his ambush.
sid_questItemprototypes_SQ50_SultanSuitc: The briefcase from the Sultan’s hidden stash aboard the Lviv. It seems surprisingly lightweight for an item of such value.
sid_questItemprototypes_SQ86_GenijAK74_d: A customized variant of the standard model. The neatly etched name “Mitya” and a recent paint job suggest that the owner took excellent care of the weapon.
sid_questItemprototypes_SQ86_SashkaBitok: A PDA found on a dead loner in the underground area beneath the Concrete Forest. Few stand a chance of survival when trapped between stalker greed and mutant ferocity.
sid_questItemprototypes_SQ87_AdikPedalKe: This weathered metal key opens the door to the administrative building at the Fish Farm, where Adik Pedal held stalkers captive.
sid_questItemprototypes_SQ87_AdikPedalPD: The notes in this PDA leave no doubt about the owner’s desire to overthrow the Sultan. It was his own ambition that led to his downfall. Found near the Fish Farm in Zaton.
sid_questItemprototypes_SQ87_ZenaGlybaPD: A PDA found on the body of Zhenya Hulk at the Fish Farm. Even when confronted with a hopeless situation, the stalker refused to give up the fight for his life. Unfortunately, his struggle was futile.

A PDA found on the body of Zhenya Hulk at the Fish Farm. In spite of the warning, the stalker held onto his belief in the deal with the bandits. While they did come to his rescue in the end, it didn’t turn out well.

A PDA found on the body of Zhenya Hulk at the Fish Farm. The stalker paid Sultan for protection, but it didn’t save him from getting killed. Looks like the kingpin of Zaton needs to think long and hard about the quality of his services.
sid_questItemprototypes_SQ88_GrebenNote_: This PDA contains a diary in which Crest documents his plan for revenge. The stalker was racing against time, striving to realize his plan before he succumbed to radiation sickness. Found on the stalker’s corpse in the Kindergarten.
sid_questItemprototypes_SQ88_KuvaldaDiar: Sledgehammer’s diary chronicles his lucrative artifact trade with the IPSF, but the mysterious disappearance of his couriers alerted the veteran stalker. Found inside a store in Chornobyl-2.
sid_questItemprototypes_SQ88_MacekPDA_de: This PDA contains correspondence between Machek and an unidentified stalker. Looks like their deal went off the rails. Discovered on the soldier’s corpse at a school in Chornobyl-2.
sid_questItemprototypes_SQ88_NimbleToBea: Looks like Beard gave Nimble an errand to run - ask around where some fellow named Sledgehammer went. The result isn't promising - Sledgehammer took off to Chornobyl and won't be getting Beard any more artifacts.
sid_questItemprototypes_SQ89_ZhgutPDA_de: Retrieved from a stalker’s body at the Pig Feedlot. Wisp and his partner were trying to compete with Sidorovich by establishing a trading post at the Tunnel Camp. Regrettably, their ambitious business plan was thwarted by a pack of hungry mutants.
sid_questItemprototypes_SQ90_FreedomPDA_: This PDA details a plan for a Freedom assault on a bandit base. The carnage indicates that the Freedom squad didn’t expect to encounter any resistance there.
sid_questItemprototypes_SQ90_GribPDA_des: A PDA belonging to a bandit from the Red Forest. In his correspondence with the gang leader, Mushroom snitches on his comrades, reporting that they got drunk, lured a mutant, and locked it inside a container. In response, the leader vividly outlines the consequences awaiting those found drinking on duty.
sid_questItemprototypes_SQ90_KeyFromTunn: This key grants access to the tunnels beneath the bandit base.
sid_questItemprototypes_SQ90_ParikPDA_de: The messages on this PDA belonging to the Red Forest gang leader’s lieutenant reference an informant at the Cement Factory. I can only guess how Duty members stay in the loop about Freedom’s activities.
sid_questItemprototypes_SQ90_PseudoGiant: An oversized iron collar equipped with an unknown mechanism, retrieved from a pseudogiant in the Red Forest. The tag, somewhat preserved, identifies it as “Test Subject #19.”
sid_questItemprototypes_SQ90_SefPDA_desc: This PDA belonging to a gang leader contains messages exchanged with a Duty member who has been leaking information about Freedom’s plans. It seems someone has discovered a loophole in the treaty.
sid_questItemprototypes_SQ90_ToropygaPDA: The messages stored on this PDA discuss a scheme to mine artifacts using other people’s labor. A neglected anomaly beneath the base has ignited the bandits’ “entrepreneurial spirit.”
sid_questItemprototypes_SQ92_MangustPDA_: Recovered from a Sparker’s body at the silo near the Chemical Plant, this PDA contains a conversation with a scientist who asked the deceased to test some equipment for him. The experiment resulted in a tragic outcome for Mongoose.
sid_questItemprototypes_SQ92_VolkovPDA_d: In his exchange with a fellow soldier, the Warden’s anxiety about patrolling the silo was bordering on paranoia. But his fears turned out to be justified – payback for his past deeds had arrived in the shape of a pack of bloodsuckers.
sid_questItemprototypes_Scanner_descript: The TOPAZ anomaly detection scanner, given to me by Hermann. This modification was used in artifact recharging experiments, but was discontinued due to safety issues. But at this point, I’ve got nothing left to lose.
sid_questItemprototypes_ShahPDA_descript: A PDA belonging to the Shah, kingpin of the Brood. He aimed to take over the Slag Heap, but ended up choking on his own blood. Greed, in the end, fails even the greedy.
sid_questItemprototypes_Shevchenko_Camp_: Sporting numerous well-worn stickers on the back panel, this aged PDA apparently sees little downtime.
sid_questItemprototypes_Shevchenko_NoteB: A brief shopping list. Based on the items on it, someone either caused trouble for Beard, or he was getting ready to stir up some trouble himself.
List of Equipment for the Shevchenko
sid_questItemprototypes_Shevchenko_NoteD: A intact page from a stalker’s diary, found on the Shevchenko. It offers a glimpse into the author’s life over the course of several days.
sid_questItemprototypes_SkifGun_descript: Skif’s award PTM. A somewhat humiliating army reminder that became a comfortable, familiar weapon.
sid_questItemprototypes_SpoiledCannedFoo: A swollen can of food, expired long before the Zone came into being. Without a decent gas mask, the stench is unbearable.
sid_questItemprototypes_StalkerPDA_SQ103: A zombified stalker's PDA from the X18 laboratory. It's all covered in numbered gum wrappers. Missing just a few to complete the collection.
sid_questItemprototypes_StrangePDA_descr: An unusual PDA, distinct from the standard stalker equipment. Its last owner was part of the crew hunting for the Scanners and met his end at the hands of Varan’s bandits, much like the device itself.
sid_questItemprototypes_Sultansk_Hub_PDA: The device is coated in dirt and blood, much like its lifeless owner. Whatever this stalker was looking for at the Cordon, it certainly wasn’t a bullet.
Dead Stalker’s PDA from the Cordon
sid_questItemprototypes_Sultansk_NoteAgr: This document has been carefully preserved against dirt and damage. The Sultan turns to it for a quick pick-me-up whenever he’s feeling blue.
sid_questItemprototypes_Sultansk_NoteKal: The uneven handwriting on the paper betrays the author’s nervous state. On the other hand, the notation at the bottom looks like it was written in a steady, confident hand.
This sheet serves as Sonya Kalina’s appointment book. Her time is precious indeed – anyone who canceled a meeting with her would have a lot of explaining to do.
An ordinary 8x12 sheet outlining Kalina’s plans for the day. It’s packed with activities – there doesn’t seem to be a single moment of free time in her schedule.
A sheet of neatly laid-out text, leaving plenty of free space to accommodate the author’s ambitious plans.
sid_questItemprototypes_Sultansk_NotePar: The scuffs on this sheet of paper indicate that it was passed around, and the ink stains confirm that the grocery list must have been a group effort.
sid_questItemprototypes_Sultansk_NoteZlo: A small scrap of paper covered in scrawled handwriting. The author was clearly in a hurry.
sid_questItemprototypes_Sultansk_PDAChil: This PDA is in good shape, indicating that its owner steers clear of situations that could result in damage to the device.
sid_questItemprototypes_Sultansk_PDAKali: This device boasts abundant contacts, but very little correspondence. Its owner promptly deletes whatever she doesn’t want others to see.
sid_questItemprototypes_Sultansk_PDARyhl: This PDA is in quite a battered state, yet its owner fared far worse.
sid_questItemprototypes_Sultansk_PDASult: Outgoing messages are a rare sight on this PDA, and incoming messages are even rarer.
sid_questItemprototypes_Sultansk_PDASych: This PDA has been through a lot, much like its owner. Only recent correspondence is still there – the rest has been deleted.
sid_questItemprototypes_Terricon_Hub_Kis: Found in the catacombs beneath the Slag Heap, this PDA serves as a reminder to think twice before crossing its former owner.
sid_questItemprototypes_Terricon_Hub_Mer: Abandoned without much thought by its owner, this PDA is covered in a surprisingly thick and even layer of dust. It seems like no one has picked it up for a very long time.
sid_questItemprototypes_Terricon_Hub_Zor: Despite being chipped around the edges, this PDA still remains operational – unlike its former owner.
sid_questItemprototypes_Terricon_NoteLis: A tattered handwritten note revealing a concise plan devised by four comrades from the Slag Heap. What could have happened to them?
Piece of Paper with a List
sid_questItemprototypes_Terricon_PDAAbou: If the message on this PDA is any indication, the owner has more pressing concerns than the prospect of losing the device.
sid_questItemprototypes_TestEQ_MoosePDA_: Обычный сталкерский КПК, изрядно потертый и поцарапанный.
sid_questItemprototypes_VaranPDA_descrip: A PDA belonging to Varan, the bandit boss. He wanted to be the kingpin of Garbage, but he wound up just another corpse.
sid_questItemprototypes_VargaPDA_descrip: A PDA belonging to Varga, stalker , who was defending the body of his friend from bandits
sid_questItemprototypes_VartaDogtag_desc: A polished, handmade badge personally issued by Korshunov. The wearer of this badge holds the power to give orders as though they originated from the colonel himself.
sid_questItemprototypes_VortexDudePDA_de: Contains some poor bastard’s conversation with his buddy.
sid_questItemprototypes_VortexDudePDA_na: PDA of a Stalker Who Fell from the Sky
sid_questItemprototypes_Yanov_KostaBandu: A typical PDA, showing signs of use and containing plenty of contacts and messages. Unless the owner has left for Prypiat, there’s a chance he might come back for it.
sid_questItemprototypes_Yanov_NoteHana_d: A small sheet of paper that has been hastily filled out. Apparently, the author is no stranger to compiling such lists.
sid_questItemprototypes_Yanov_NoteOrder_: A sheet of paper in impeccable condition, carefully preserved for a special occasion.
sid_questItemprototypes_Yanov_Note_ToDoL: A crumpled sheet of paper full of corrections, annotations, and crossed-out sections.
sid_questItemprototypes_Yanov_PDA_Garpia: Despite having seen its share of repairs, this PDA is in excellent working condition. Its owner clearly has a knack for technology and is in no rush to retire the device.
sid_questItemprototypes_Yanov_PDA_Nor_de: While there’s no apparent damage, the back panel of this PDA bears significant scuff marks, hinting that its owner tends to tap it on the table when nervous.
sid_questItemprototypes_Yanov_SenaDymPDA: This PDA continues to receive messages, but there’s no one left to read them.
sid_questItemprototypes_Zalesie_BatyaPDA: An ordinary stalker PDA. The inscription scratched on the back panel of the device says “The Gaffer.”
sid_questItemprototypes_Zalesie_HomakNot: A small handwritten note. The messy, uneven handwriting aptly capture the sender’s emotional state.
sid_questItemprototypes_Zalesie_HomakPDA: This PDA is seldom quiet. Its owner is constantly barraged with job offers – or calls expressing a lack of interest.
sid_questItemprototypes_Zalesie_KoldunNo: A brief, succinct message written on a scrap of paper.
sid_questItemprototypes_Zalesie_KoldunPD: Typically, PDA owners either keep them hidden from prying eyes or erase all sensitive data on them. However, this particular device still contains an exchange that wasn’t deleted in time.
sid_questItemprototypes_Zalesie_LenaPDA_: A rather battered PDA. Its owner traversed many miles across the Zone before discovering safer routes.
sid_questItemprototypes_Zalesie_LinzaPDA: An ordinary piece of paper with a message for a buddy on it. The use of different inks in various sections suggests that the author didn’t write it all in one go.
sid_questItemprototypes_Zalesie_NoteFrom: A stack of hastily scribbled sheets. Only one note is actually legible amid the otherwise indecipherable chicken-scratchings.
sid_questItemprototypes_ZhukovskyPDA_des: A PDA belonging to a recruit from Zulu’s unit, discovered on a corpse in the Eastern Tunnel. The device’s screen is cracked and smeared with blood. A seemingly simple mission turned out to be its unfortunate owner’s last.
sid_questItemprototypes_e01_mq01_ssp_mem: A document found on the corpse of an International Perimeter Security Force soldier. Given the grease stains on it, it was probably not used as intended.
sid_questItemprototypes_krivenko_flashdr: A blank high-speed storage device designed exclusively for use on SIRCAA equipment.
sid_questItemprototypes_krivenko_full_fl: A high-speed storage device designed exclusively for use on SIRCAA equipment. Any attempt to connect it to third-party devices will result in the destruction of the data.
sid_questItemprototypes_loopen_clock_not: An ordinary wall clock from your grandma’s house. Looks like it’s been broken for a while now, and it’s missing the seconds hand.
sid_questItemprototypes_nvgpack_ANCQ27_B: A PDA with orders for a bandit, taken from his corpse at Yantar.
sid_questItemprototypes_nvgpack_supack_A: Personal file of a researcher from the dead group investigating a strange transmission
Just a regular plastic drive, with the letter M and a date scratched on the side. Contains a recording. I wonder what all this means.
sid_questItemprototypes_nvgpack_supack_B: A regular key with something strange written on the tag: "d33s41d3m".
A regular door key with a tag
A scribbled draft that never got sent.
sid_questItemprototypes_nvgpack_supack_K: The PDA of the killed researcher of the mysterious radio transmission.
sid_questItemprototypes_old_looper_clock: You thought it was an ordinary clock you’ve seen a dozen of times. It has no batteries inside, but it glows from the inside, and as you look closer, you hear it ticking, while the seconds hand turns counter clockwise.
sid_questItemprototypes_psilamp_part_05_: A fragment of the device responsible for subjecting Icarus personnel to psi-radiation.
sid_questItemprototypes_scanner_lighter_: The very same TOPAZ anomaly detection scanner given to me by Hermann, though much lighter than last time. Looks like someone has tinkered with it since.
sid_questItemprototypes_sid_items_E02_SQ: To add a personal touch, the owner of this PDA scratched his nickname onto the back panel.
sid_questItemprototypes_supack_FairyTale: A regular door key that opens one of the Kazkovyi camp buildings.
Key to the Kazkovyi camp building
A little key with "Vent" carved into the key head.
sid_questItemprototypes_supack_SQ100_Sku: A folded note, put into the technician's chest pocket in a way it would be easily noticed.
sid_questItemprototypes_supack_SQ95_Banz: A plastic drive with the name "Banzai" scratched onto it. Contains blueprints of a military-grade radio direction finder with strange modifications.
Drive with blueprints for a radio direction finder
sid_questItemprototypes_supack_SQ95_Kirk: A worn-out PDA with multiple cracks on the screen. Filled with contacts like "Shady", "Skull", and "Mutt". Barely holds a charge.
sid_questItemprototypes_supack_SQ96_C01_: Note from the Vehicle Graveyard. Should help find the tools stash. Looks like it requires specific technical knowledge to understand it.
Note from the Vehicle Graveyard. Should somehow help find the tools stash. Looks like it requires specific technical knowledge to figure out what it means.
Note from the Vehicle Graveyard. Should somehow help find the tools stash. Unlike the others, this one has words that are readable.
A worn-out set of technician's tools, specifically for radio electronics. They won't be much help for weapons or body armor repairs, but will definitely be useful for radio equipment.
sid_questItemprototypes_supack_SQ96_C02_: A set of tools suitable for weapon and body armor repairs under Zone conditions. With these tools, a technician can equip a variety of different modifications, although this set isn't suitable for calibration.
sid_questItemprototypes_supack_SQ96_C03_: A professional set of tools. They'd make any technician happy, even on the Mainland. Allows any technician skilled enough to equip any kind of modification available in the Zone.
sid_questItemprototypes_supack_SQ97_Expe: An old journal with writing all over the pages. The first page has the STC Malachite stamp on it.
Log from the Burnt Forest expedition
sid_questItemprototypes_supack_SQ97_Gene: Fuel voucher for a generator at Kazkovyi. Looks like the group of campers before us left it.
sid_questItemprototypes_supack_SQ97_Pele: A laminated memo on an STC Malachite slip. Judging by its state, it's been passed on from the first NK group to each one after.
sid_questItemprototypes_supack_SQ97_Sany: PDA of an NK-3 group member, found in a Kazkovyi camp building
sid_questItemprototypes_supack__SQ99_She: A metal key received from Rascal.
sid_questItemprototypes_supack_an_upm_se: A receiving signal antenna amplifier, military-grade. Old, but still operational.
sid_questItemprototypes_supack_supack_SQ: The PDA found on a stalker's body in the boiler house basement. Looks like the owner was also looking for the tools stash, but didn't succeed.
sid_questItemprototypes_supack_vojatij_k: A worn-out key to the safe belonging to Kazkovyi's guide
sid_questItemprototypes_supack_vozmercfo: A mercenary's suit with traces of skilled mending. There's a chest patch with the name "Surgeon".
sid_questItemprototypes_supack_vozmercpd: A military-grade PDA with reinforced glass. Half-scratched off but still readable, "Surgeon" is written on the side in marker.
sid_stashes_ANCQ23_AtasStash_description: The situation has changed. I left the cargo in the cave. Proceed with caution, preferably at night.
sid_stashes_DLC00_Stash_Deluxe_BurntFore: I led this gear's previous owner right into a Spark ambush. Do I feel guilty? No. This was no scientist, but a Mengele from SIRCAA. And he is not the only one. Now I know that.
sid_stashes_DLC00_Stash_Deluxe_Duga_desc: In the Zone, the situation can change within seconds. While I was trying to hide from an IPSF soldier, a chimera attacked him. We beat it together, but the guy's wounds were so bad he was a goner. I remember it like it was yesterday.
sid_stashes_DLC00_Stash_Deluxe_Garbage_d: Now I am a murderer. But after what the Brood did to Shell, I had no other choice. I hate them! Strider is not one for retaliation, but he would have approved this kind. I will leave all of Shell's things here.
sid_stashes_DLC00_Stash_Deluxe_Pripyat_d: I knew I would end up in Prypiat. What I didn't know was what I would become - one with no trust or faith. But I have no regrets. Apart from the fact I now have to admire Her beauty through a riflescope.
sid_stashes_DLC00_Stash_Deluxe_Rostok_de: The Zone teaches me quickly - I'm becoming picky. Even when it comes to weapons. Now I won't be carrying a piece if the ammo costs me an arm and a leg. The less my ruck weighs, the faster I can run, the longer I can live.
sid_stashes_DLC00_Stash_Deluxe_SmallZone: These trophies bring everyone closer. People cherish them and thank the Zone - for staying alive after meeting their first owners. However, I didn't get all this from the Monolithians themselves.

The Zone is no walk in the park. But it seems to me that if you learn to see the good in it, it will change the way it looks at you, too. I have no other explanation for how it treated me from the start. What does it see in me, I wonder?
sid_stashes_DLC00_Stash_Deluxe_Swamps_de: At last, I met the Monolithian I heard so many frightening things about. But Strider doesn't like talking about the past. He even gave me his kit, - out of sight, out of mind.
sid_stashes_DLC00_Stash_PreOrder_Garbage: Sidorovich is one suspicious guy, not a big fan of journalists. Didn't want to sell me anything at first. Had to run some errands for him to get a Sunrise.
sid_stashes_DLC00_Stash_PreOrder_Swamps_: I still can't get used to death being just another day in the Zone. So I can't take this rifle. If I make it out alive, I'll make sure to write about its owner. This man's life is a novel waiting to be published.
sid_stashes_DLC00_Stash_Ultimate_Promzon: Stalker weapon names are pure poetry. And the deadlier, the more metaphoric. This one, for instance, puts one to sleep, rather than kills. An escape from the truth? Isn't this exactly what we're doing?
sid_stashes_DLC00_Stash_Ultimate_SmallZo: Before the Zone, I couldn’t hit the broad side of a barn. Tried it once at a range, just for fun. This weapon makes it almost easy. My fingers start remembering something they were never trained for.
sid_stashes_DLC00_Stash_Ultimate_Swamps_: At last, I met the Monolithian I heard so many frightening things about. But Strider doesn't like talking about the past. He even gave me his kit, - out of sight, out of mind.
sid_stashes_DLC00_Stash_Ultimate_Yanov_d: Before I met the owner of this revolver, I never believed that Freedom organized mutant safaris for rich foreigners. Where Duty works hard, Freedom makes a pretty penny.
sid_stashes_E02_SQ01_HmuriyStash_descrip: I’ll leave some supplies here for an emergency. If it gets stolen… well, so be it.
sid_stashes_E02_SQ01_ZhorikStash_descrip: If you know about this stash, you’re one of us. Don't take more than you need. The Zone doesn’t like greedy folks, and you-know-who likes them even less.
sid_stashes_E03_MQ03_RuzveltStash_descri: Let those sanctimonious assholes think they got rid of me. There’s no such thing as ex-Duty. I’ll be back, and I’ll make good use of this stash.
sid_stashes_E03_MQ03_ShakhStash_descript: You can use it to settle petty squabbles and hassle chumps — or you could reshape the balance of power in the Zone. It's your choice.
sid_stashes_E07_MQ02_Suhin_Stash_descrip: Something feels off. Won't hurt to stash some stuff. Just in case.
sid_stashes_E07_MQ02_SyhinStash_Variable: Something feels off. Won't hurt to stash some stuff. Just in case.
sid_stashes_E11_MQ01_DarkStash_descripti: There’s a beacon inside. Meet me at the coordinates. Feel free to keep the rest of this stuff.
sid_stashes_E11_MQ01_RooseveltStash_desc: I’m stashing what’s left from the good old days in the Commissary. It’s not like Voronin’s gonna lend me a hand anytime soon, right?
sid_stashes_E11_MQ04_VartaStash_descript: This is a space for storing material evidence confiscated from enemies of the Ward, alive and deceased.
sid_stashes_EQ109_MarikTopotCache_descri: I've got a bad feeling about this meeting. I’d better hide some stuff just in case. There’s a house in the village with a stove and an intact root cellar. I’ll leave it there, in the corner.
sid_stashes_EQ10_Stash_description: I stashed my loot in a locker at the train station. I also left some nasty surprises for unwelcome guests, so watch your step.
sid_stashes_EQ111_KorkaCacheVariable_des: I hid the cargo in the basement of the Krug Antenna Complex. Stalkers never go there, so it’s safe. I tossed the bag on top of a pipe in the corner of the hallway, so it's gonna take some jumping.
sid_stashes_EQ141_TurgaCacheVariable_des: Good thing my partner told me to meet him at the dam! Who would ever expect to find such a good stash of food here in the sticks? If Rowboat doesn’t show up soon, there'll be nothing but breadcrumbs left.
sid_stashes_EQ143_Stash_RybakinStash_des: I hid some stuff on the bus roof. No one will find it unless they know exactly where to look. Plus, there’s the radiation.
sid_stashes_EQ14_KuzaCacheVariable_descr: I hid our loot under the trailer, behind the right wheel. There are some pallets piled up right next to it, you can’t miss it. Let the boys know where to bring their contributions. The stalkers won’t be nosing around while we’re hanging out in the village.
sid_stashes_EQ155_Stash_Laba_description: I left some supplies in the safe. You’ll find the combination on the collar of test subject #48.
sid_stashes_EQ164_Stash_description: How come no one's noticed that nice little spot under the pier? Am I the only smart one around here? It’s the perfect place to squirrel away some loot.
sid_stashes_EQ166_Stash_description: I’m leaving my loot at an abandoned checkpoint to the south of the cemetery. No one will go snooping around there. There's nothing but mutants there — mutants and us loners.
sid_stashes_EQ16_Stash_LootFara_descript: I left a stash at the Ribs, near the concrete fence. Stalkers barely make it out of there alive – if they make it at all. Hopefully those medkits can save someone’s life.
sid_stashes_EQ20_Stash_Balcony_Marker_de: The Eastern tunnel is a good place for a stash. No one will go looking there. It’s also a perfect opportunity to see what the new recruits can do.
sid_stashes_EQ35_LegendStash_description: I had to get to my rookies fast, the backpack was too heavy, so I left it behind. Tossed it on the roof of a caterpillar tractor west of the Flea Market. The artifacts inside are worth a fortune...
sid_stashes_EQ48_GiverStash_description: Zhenya the stalker’s stuff, found in a tunnel at the Cordon.
sid_stashes_EQ54_Stash_InAnomaly_descrip: Some crazy loners left a stash inside an anomaly. I don’t think there’s anything in there that’s actually worth the risk, but I wouldn’t mind offering the coordinates as a gift.
sid_stashes_EQ60_Stash_LootVovcika_descr: I left a stash behind some crates in one of the pigsties. They all look alike, those damn pigsties... every time I end up looking in the wrong one and start thinking my loot got ganked.
sid_stashes_EQ64_Stash_LootHolmy_descrip: I hid some supplies on the beams by the two-story building at the decontamination station in the Lesser Zone. I don’t think anyone will look there.
sid_stashes_EQ65_PiavkaClue_description: I hid the loot on the roof of the elevator. The place is safe — unless you fall to your death, that is.
sid_stashes_EQ70_Stash_LootDimona_descri: I've got a stash by a hut in the Poppy Field. The radiation warning signs around there ought to keep rookies away. The mutants will scare off all the rest.
sid_stashes_GDN_09_StashInCave_descripti: Had to leave some loot in the cave with the bulldozer. Those damn snorks won’t leave me alone. Just you wait — I’ll get me some slugs, and then I’ll take care of you bastards...
sid_stashes_GDN_104_StashOnRoofKPP_descr: If the sergeant doesn’t notice, I’m good. I didn’t let anyone in, Sarge, I swear! I’ll fix the fence and the log, and then everything’ll be peachy. The stuff has to get moved from the roof, though. Too much radiation.
sid_stashes_GDN_105_StashNearElectricfie: It’s a good hiding place. I’m surprised the electronics are still functional. Hopefully they still are... anyway, the code is stick, cobra, porthole, infinity.
sid_stashes_GDN_106_StashInGasStation_de: I stocked up on food and even found a bag that odors can't get through. The wild animals here are hungry.
sid_stashes_GDN_107_StashDuga_descriptio: I don’t accept returns. Just can’t afford it. Here’s an artifact to compensate you for the inconvenience. If you hear any strange noises, that's just the anomaly in the drill field messing around.
sid_stashes_GDN_10_StashUnderBridge_desc: Those mercenaries have good equipment and decent pay, but will they miss an opportunity to rob a simple stalker? Never! So I had to hide some loot under the bridge close to checkpoint.
sid_stashes_GDN_119_StashInCafePripyat_d: The stuff is in the structure next to Olympia Cafe. I’m keeping the key myself for safety reasons.
sid_stashes_GDN_11_StashInSafe_descripti: I played cards with the commander and lost my shirt. But I know he has a safe for his valuables. I’m gonna take an impression of the key next time the bastard goes up to the roof for a smoke.
sid_stashes_GDN_120_StashInDKEnergetik_d: I hid some loot in a nook on the first floor of Energetik Palace of Culture, under the balcony. It was too heavy to carry in one go. Hopefully I can pick it up next time.
sid_stashes_GDN_127_StashOnCentralArea_d: I buried some stuff by the central square, in plain sight. No one will even think to look there. Except maybe rats...
sid_stashes_GDN_129_StashAboutAutotunnel: [00:45] Mission complete, Commander. We lost two men, and one is heavily wounded. We’re on the move to Wild Territory. Restocking from our stash in the Automotive Tunnel on the way. I’ll report as soon as we get there.
sid_stashes_GDN_12_StashInGrave_descript: Stashed some loot in the grave under the wheel — and some nasty surprises in all the rest. Now any merc who follows me can rest in pieces. And that cat, too. There’s a cat there, I could smell it.
sid_stashes_GDN_13_StashOnCrane_descript: I saw a light on the very top of the highest crane yesterday. Someone was hiding loot there – that’s gotta be it! As soon as my watch ends, I’m climbing that thing. There must be something valuable up there!
sid_stashes_GDN_15_StashInPumpingStation: Found a nice box with a combination lock at the Hydroelectric Station. Had too much loot on me, so I stashed some there. The code is 0666. I’d better come back for it soon, before the Monolithians show up.
sid_stashes_GDN_16_StashOnPigfarm_descri: The padlock’s all rusty, I can see it through the window. And a padlock that big can only mean one thing: there’s something valuable inside.
sid_stashes_GDN_19_StashInBanditsATP_des: Just my luck! I brought that artifact to Sidorovich, got my pay — and got mugged by bandits! Good thing I know where they stashed my stuff. I need to find a friend to help me get it back.
sid_stashes_GDN_20_StashOnBanditsATP_des: It’s a good place to hide stuff, right next to the hut and visible from a distance. If anyone goes near my stash at the vehicle station, I’ll see it right away.
sid_stashes_GDN_21_StashOnElevatorTower_: The Gaffer’s right: loot loves quiet folks. My pals were busy squabbling about that stalkers’ stuff, so I just helped myself to a fair cut. Hid it at the elevator tower. No pig can fly all the way up there.
sid_stashes_GDN_22_StashOnMilitaryKPP_de: The sergeant got too cocky for his own good. He wants a cut. I’ll have to send the location of our checkpoint stash to the military police. It'll be hard to replace him, but not impossible.
sid_stashes_GDN_38_StashOnElevatorRoof_d: Mom always told me never to climb up so high I can’t get back down... I sprained my ankle on my way back. Well, at least my loot is safe now.
sid_stashes_GDN_44_StashInOldShop_descri: The Zone took all my pals — Salami, Ark, even Knife... good guys, that’s what they were... I hid their stuff at the Cooling Towers, in an old workshop. No one goes there anyway.
sid_stashes_GDN_45_StashInCementFactory_: I left the cargo in the anomaly field at the Cement Factory. Wouldn’t want to lose it to bandits again. I’m so fed up with running back and forth for it. Maybe it’s a good idea to pay for protection.
sid_stashes_GDN_48_StashInEasternTunnel_: So much treasure, I can’t even carry it all in one go. I'm sure I’ll be back for it.
sid_stashes_GDN_49_StashOnSecurityRoof_d: I saw a buddy of mine on that roof the other day, either looking for something or hiding something, I don’t know which. I wasn’t reckless enough to go check.
sid_stashes_GDN_51_StashOnRoof_descripti: They think I’m a rookie, so it’s safe to mess with me. They should think again. I’m making my own private stash up here on the roof. My fair share of the loot. They won’t find it in all the dust.
sid_stashes_GDN_61_StashOnBridgePillar_d: Okay, guess that wasn’t the best idea I've ever had. I left a stash, but there's no way I’m going in the water again. It can stay there for all I care.
sid_stashes_GDN_62_StashOnObservationAre: What a view! I’d love to sit here with some buddies, a guitar, and a bottle... but I have to haul my loot to the Sultan instead. All the more reason to come back later.
sid_stashes_GDN_69_StashInBoilerRoom_des: I had them hunting me all the way there, and that wasn’t part of our agreement. So I left your cargo inside the Boiler House. Good luck getting it yourself. I’m keeping the advance payment.
sid_stashes_GDN_76_StashHiddenBackpack_d: When you find your backpack, I suggest thinking long and hard about alcohol and the effect it's had on you. You’re lucky I've known you since we were kids, otherwise I wouldn’t be hiding your stuff.
sid_stashes_GDN_77_StashInCamp_descripti: If you find this stash, be a good stalker and don’t touch it. It’s for emergencies. You never know what could be lurking in these woods… So if you take something, be sure to leave a replacement later.
sid_stashes_GDN_79_StashInBarracks_descr: You were hoping to get some goodies here, huh? Fuck you. I hid everything in a much better place. You'll never find it. And when you’re all nice and dead, I’ll come back for my stuff.
sid_stashes_GDN_84_StashOnCrossHill_desc: Sure, I had some boars on my tail, but is that a good enough reason to shoot at me? I’m not a boar! Whatever... we’ll meet again, you'll see. I’d better stash the loot here in the hut, just in case.
sid_stashes_GDN_86_StashOnRoofKPP_descri: I’m sick of following orders. I’m not cut out for this. Stashed a couple of magazines in the bag. They won’t find it on the roof.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash100_description: This is for everyone, but don’t take what’s not yours!
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash101_description: Sometimes it's better to get the hell out than get a hell lotta loot.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash102_description: Here are all my Zone riches. Hope some other fool finds them useful.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash103_description: The supplier's solid. You know the prices.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash104_description: Yeah, fuckheads, I screwed you over. You're lucky I left ya anything at all.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash105_description: Stashed it when I was drunk, can't even remember what I dragged in here.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash106_description: Don't you tell me I did a shitty job getting ready for the run.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash107_description: I'm just a courier! Take it all and just leave me the heck alone!
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash108_description: We all chipped in and got you a lil something. Hang in there, bro.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash109_description: Happy first hunt. Here's a little present.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash110_description: Too dangerous to meet. I'm being followed. P.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash110_name: A stash made in a hurry
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash111_description: If the mutants chew me up, at least my stuff won't go to waste.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash112_description: I'll just dump my stuff here. Maybe this one good deed will count before I die.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash114_description: Something ain't right about this place. But I guess it beats leaving it all out in the open.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash115_description: Always lucky at cards, you shitface.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash116_description: I'll leave your loot here. You won't need it anymore.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash117_description: Thanks again. I tossed the loot into a couple stashes just to be safe.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash118_description: This is the advance payment for the collars. You will get the rest once you bring me at least three of them. Try not to drag any mutants along.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash120_description: I always pay what I owe. But I don't want to see your ugly mug.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash122_description: I hated your stupid face the least back at camp. So I leave my stuff to you. Hope you can get it before some random stalker does.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash123_description: One solid spot: nothing ever gets snatched from here!
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash124_description: What I took from the Zone, I return to the Zone.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash125_description: Sure hope this'll be enough for next time.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash126_description: Loot from the last asshole stalker snooping around my damn stash.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash127_description: Did I ever ask you to rescue me? Nope. I'm freaking done running around the Zone. No more stashes.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash128_description: Better ditch six pounds of loot than end up six feet under.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash129_description: If I can't carry all of it myself, at least I won't leave any for that scumbag.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash12_description: Those morons will never, ever find it.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash130_description: The Gaffer always told me: make more stashes, at least one of 'em won't get snatched.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash131_description: Stashed it at night, no tail. Let's see if it stays.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash132_description: The Zone shall punish anyone who takes this stash.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash133_description: Can't even keep track of these damn stashes. Screw the Zone, what's the point if it all gets nicked anyway.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash134_description: We agreed to a face-to-face. But this is better than nothing.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash135_description: Don't think I'll survive this one, but making a stash anyway. Old habits.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash136_description: Note to self: grab on the next rest stop.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash137_description: If not for someone's stash, I'd have bitten the dust right here last year. So here's mine, for those in need.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash138_description: I'll come back here, one way or another.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash139_description: Rumor has it, this stash belonged to a stalker who got eaten by snorks, and they dragged all his stuff here. I didn't have the guts to take it, so suit yourself.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash13_description: I really should find a better place to stash my loot next time.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash140_description: It's not a shakedown, it's natural selection. The Zone ain't for the weak.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash141_description: Bandits jumped me, thought I was some kinda chump. Stashed all their crap here.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash142_description: Grey, you shitwit! I figured you're fucking nicking my loot. Let's see if I'm right.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash143_description: The dead sure won't need it anymore.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash146_description: Never drink the professor's vodka again. Or at least, never make stashes while at it. It's supposed to be somewhere around here...
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash146_name: Lost at the bottom of a bottle
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash147_description: Here lies all that's left of a stalker who wandered into an anomaly.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash148_description: Take it if you need it. If you don't need it, leave it for those who do.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash149_description: Better watch your back if you dare nick my stash.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash14_description: I ganked this stuff while everybody was asleep. I’ll hide it here for now and come back when the dust settles.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash152_description: Damn it all! Time to head back home. Take my stash if you still got your hopes up about this place.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash154_description: Can't carry all this crap around, but can't dump it either - the Zone won't forgive me.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash156_description: I got no idea what's in there, but I sure as hell know who can pay a pretty coupon for it!
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash157_description: Making friends with bandits can be a good thing. Especially when they're drunk as skunks.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash158_description: The Zone offers an endless supply of chumps.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash15_description: If you take something, you have to put something back. Other people need this stuff too.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash161_description: If you ain't no Bear, better get outta here, nothing for you to see.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash163_description: Bet those potheads will screw me over. Better stash this away.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash164_description: Time to lay low. That loot won't go anywhere.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash167_description: All y'all think I'm dead, let's keep it that way. Got enough stashes to get back on my feet.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash168_description: That was one awesome hunt! If you found this stash, take it, and put in a good word to Lady Luck for me.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash169_description: Stay here, my goodies, while I try to sell ya for more moneys.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash16_description: Someone’s on my heels. Had to leave some of my loot. I’ll come back for it later.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash170_description: Take it, it won't bite. I'm done with this Zone anyway.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash171_description: I shouldn't have looked what was in that package. Now you'll kill me for sure. Take it back, and let me be on my merry way.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash172_description: Yet another Zone rule: everything you need is way more than you can carry.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash173_description: My gift to you, Zone. Have mercy on me.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash174_description: Stalker wisdom: whatever you found, stash it somewhere else.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash175_description: Sidorovich sold me this useless crap. I'll resell it to another chump later.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash176_description: I'll make sure to come back here tomorrow and reorganize my stuff.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash178_description: They say the Zone is generous. But I'm not expecting anything from it. Gathered all of this by myself.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash179_description: Got a fuckton of stuff to carry around. Again. Like a goddamn mule. I'll be smart this time, better leave it here and grab it on my way back.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash17_description: If you touch my loot, I’ll find you and rip your damn arms off.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash180_description: Gotta get out before the Emission gets here. I'll go back when it's over.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash181_description: All the guys got stashes, how come I never had one? Here's to my first stash!
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash182_description: Rest stop first, work later. The job can wait until tomorrow.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash183_description: No more teamwork for me, no more teammates to screw me over.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash184_description: Now I just gotta remember where I left it.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash186_description: I'll just sell it to a clueless rookie.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash187_description: I hope I won't ever need it.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash188_description: I'll stash my cut here, before someone nicks it.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash189_description: Well, brother, not much left of ya.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash18_description: What was I thinking, taking so much stuff? I’ll stash some of it here for now.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash190_description: Some booze and munchies make you happy bunches.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash191_description: Back when I was a lil rookie, a stash like this saved my arse. Here I am, Zone, returning the favor. May it help someone who needs it.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash192_description: Your protection's useless as shit. But if I don't pay up, you'll off me anyway.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash193_description: Stalker number one took one bottle of vodka. Number two only left a bandage. Number three took everything and the bandage, too, but left his own stuff.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash194_description: Saw a stalker off the whole camp before they even came round. Handy, but sure ain't sharp. Missed the stash, ha!
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash195_description: Well, Ciry, now you're only half-chump!
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash195_name: A lil something from the bros
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash196_description: Take if owner does not return in seven days.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash199_description: Someone's trying to mess with my head. It's all here, but scattered around randomly. Better robbed than crazy. Bastards.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash19_description: Grabbed some loot off some rookies. I'll come back for it tomorrow.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash1_description: If you touch my stuff, it’s your funeral.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash200_description: Quit messing with my stuff! You wanna snatch it - do it already, just stop driving me nuts!
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash201_description: I'm no cheater, I just got lucky!
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash202_description: If the job goes south, I'll beat it. This will do for the first few days.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash204_description: Can't even remember how many times I restashed it.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash205_description: Maybe all that bar goss is bullshit. But what if this really helps keep those monolith nutjobs away?
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash206_description: A stalker wandered off where he wasn't supposed to. I'll take it once the stench is gone.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash207_description: I offed my hundredth mutant here once. Lucky spot. Maybe this won't get snatched.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash208_description: I'll dump it here. Or Mykolaich will talk my ear off with his backache remedies.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash210_description: Lost it somewhere around here. Should find it when I get the chance.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash211_description: I'll grab it next time, for sure.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash212_description: Who knows when you end up with nothing in the Zone. This may help a little.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash213_description: Traded an artifact for this while I was wasted. I'm one legendary chump… Can't even look at this trash, I'll just leave it.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash214_description: Let's see which Rostok cheapskate offers a better price for this.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash215_description: Saving it for someone I'll owe for helping me out.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash216_description: Yup, I sold you a map to this stash. And you paid me more than you found here. Bye, loser.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash217_description: I made up my mind, folks. I'm off to join the Ward. They issue decent kit, so I won't need all this anymore.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash218_description: Guys, don't nick my stash. I'll save up for a new guitar and throw a hell of a concert by the bonfire for ya!
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash219_description: Thanks for patching me up when you could just kill me. I don't like being in anyone's debt, so here's something that might come in handy. But next time, save yourself.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash21_description: Wow! Did I really leave it here?
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash220_description: Ever since I looted a dead stalker, I've been in a dark place. So I'm returning everything I nicked that day.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash221_description: As for me, I left some decent shit in this stash. But I can't guarantee that's what you'll end up finding here.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash222_description: Here's to hoping my digging years taught me not just to find all sorts of crap, but to hide it well.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash223_description: I'll send the next chump meet location to your PDA. This time there'll be more to shake down, promise.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash223_name: A cut from a team effort
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash224_description: This dude didn't exactly leave anything to me… But it doesn’t seem like he had anyone else.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash225_description: Like we agreed, it's a blind exchange! I hope you didn't cheap out and left me something decent.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash226_description: You'd have been better off paying me back the easy way. Now one of your goons had to learn his lesson the hard way. You can tell which one, I left him here with all his stuff.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash227_description: You're a diehard, bastard! Alright, a deal's a deal. I lost, you won, here's your prize.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash228_description: I'll grab it on my way back from the bar. Otherwise I'll just trade it for booze.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash229_description: Like I promised, left some supplies for you, waiting at our spot. If you're not there in two days, I'm off on my own. Not safe to stay in one place for too long.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash22_description: That was definitely overkill — my shoulders are killing me. Better stash some of it here.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash230_description: Cheers, folks. I thought I wouldn't make it out alive. Here's a little something for you. My eternal gratitude.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash231_description: Damn, that stalker was easy to follow. Restashed all the stuff he had. No worries, I'll sell it back to him.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash232_description: Sold my beauty. I'll buy it back when my hands heal. It's hard enough to hold a pistol, let alone hit the strings.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash233_description: Come on, guys, I didn't kill him! He's just holding his breath. For 70 hours straight, like a champ! We'll just watch his stuff meanwhile. Here's your cut.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash233_name: A stash for partners in crime
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash234_description: A couple more of these unlucky hunts, and I'll have to join Gnome's petty thieves.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash235_description: It is way too little to repay you for your help, but it's all I've got.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash236_description: Time to make some noise. But first, I need to hide some stuff away.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash236_name: Loot that might come in handy
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash237_description: Dealing with Baldhead was a lousy idea. One vindictive son of a gun. It's okay though, he can't keep track of everything. Sharing is caring - that's for losers.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash238_description: Turns out, playing the victim can be quite useful.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash239_description: Damn emission screwed up my plans. Alrighty, I'll leave it here and restash it later.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash23_description: These goodies are perfect for my stash.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash240_description: Get all the goodies in here. I'll decide what's your cut, and what belongs to the boss.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash241_description: Open in case of expedition failure only.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash242_description: Stashes are the real shit! Can't stash away enough under the kingpin's nose.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash243_description: Saw a couple of stalkers hiding it. If that's all their savings combined, I feel kinda bad taking it.
sid_stashes_Gen_Stash244_description: For the rainiest of days. If it keeps going like this, though, I might need it right away. Or maybe tomorrow.
